close_reason: Fixed in f5da447: per-block active-rect state now backs the tabindex binding, updated from both the arrow-key handler and focus events (the dual path matters — focus events don't fire in unfocused automated documents, which the reporter correctly flagged), clamped when polls shrink a block. Verified with the reporter's exact repro: after two ArrowRight presses the focused rect carries tabindex=0, exactly one tab stop per block, six page-wide, and the stop follows focus

title: 90-day periodic backup reminders
description: Gentle reminder every 90 days: 'It's been a while since you downloaded your wallet backups. Want updated copies?' Catches config changes and new wallets. Dismissable, returns in 90 days. Server-side timestamps (localStorage windows lose across browsers).
close_reason: Server-tracked, dismissable 90-day backup reminder banner; markBackedUp now refreshes downloaded_at so re-download resets the window.

title: Post-Heartwood-launch fixes: unblock sync/nav, finish reskin, notification bugs
description: Alex's Umbrel field report after v0.1.9 (Heartwood redesign) shipped: sync screen blocks the whole app on startup and its skip button is broken; SSR load functions block every navigation on Electrum round-trips; Umbrel store listing still says Cairn; reskin has residual old-branding gaps; notification panel has truncation + z-index bugs. Sub-beads scope each independently-shippable track. See docs/HEARTWOOD-REDESIGN-PLAN.md and cairn-koy4 for the original epic this follows up on.
close_reason: All 9 direct children (2zxt.1-2zxt.9) closed and shipped across v0.2.1-v0.2.8: sync gate banner, SWR streaming/caching (2zxt.2/.3/.8 + its 8 sub-tracks 2zxt.8.1-.8), notification panel truncation/z-index (2zxt.4), rebrand string sweep (2zxt.5), email/PDF Heartwood-ify (2zxt.6), Umbrel store listing ship (2zxt.7), chart bar-mode retrofit (2zxt.9). Related SSE/back-button/timeout beads (sb2l, y7ac, vn48) also closed. Only open descendant is cairn-2zxt.8.9 (P3, child of cairn-2zxt.8), deliberately deferred out of scope as nontrivial additive SSE push-plumbing per its own rationale and cairn-2zxt.8's closing comment -- not a blocker for this epic.

title: Sync gate: never block the app -- banner instead of full-screen redirect
description:
P0. src/routes/(app)/+layout.server.ts:63-69 redirects EVERY route to /sync (a full-screen blocking page) whenever !isFirstSyncComplete() and the hw_skip_sync cookie is absent. Alex reports users get stuck on this screen and its "Continue without waiting" button (src/routes/sync/+page.svelte skipAhead(), sets hw_skip_sync cookie then goto("/")) does not reliably get them through.

Required fix: the app must NEVER hard-block navigation on first-sync/scan status. Replace the redirect gate with a non-blocking banner/indicator (e.g. in (app)/+layout.svelte, reusing FirstSyncGrowth/EpochDial styling) that shows progress (epochsKnown/epochsTotal, phase, ETA) while users freely browse every route immediately. Poll the existing /api/sync endpoint from the banner instead of a dedicated blocking page. Keep the detailed /sync view reachable as an optional secondary page (link from the banner) if useful, but it must not be the forced landing point. Remove the now-unnecessary hw_skip_sync cookie escape hatch entirely once the gate itself is gone (or repurpose it only for dismissing the banner). Diagnose why skipAhead() currently fails before ripping it out, note the root cause in the close comment even though the fix subsumes it.

Files: src/routes/(app)/+layout.server.ts, src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte, src/routes/sync/+page.svelte, src/routes/sync/+page.server.ts, src/routes/api/sync/+server.ts, likely a new src/lib/components/heartwood/*Banner*.svelte. Verify live via the preview tool: fresh DB (no epoch history cache), confirm dashboard/wallets/every route renders and is clickable immediately with the banner showing progress, not a blocking screen.
close_reason: First-sync gate replaced with a non-blocking banner (commit 5699211, merged). (app)/+layout.server.ts no longer redirects to /sync -- it threads a cheap memoized firstSyncComplete boolean instead; new SyncBanner.svelte polls /api/sync and renders atop every route without blocking clicks; /sync demoted to an optional 'view details' page (goToApp() replaces skipAhead()). Root cause of the broken skip button found: the server-side redirect fired BEFORE (app)/+layout.svelte mounted, so the HTTPS auto-hop in secureRedirect.ts (which lives in that layout's onMount) never ran while sync was incomplete; hw_skip_sync (no Secure/Domain attrs) then got set on the HTTP origin but the subsequent goto('/') triggered the auto-hop's location.replace to a different scheme+port, and the cookie didn't reliably carry across that cross-origin hard navigation -- users bounced back to /sync, matching Alex's 'doesn't reliably work' report. Verified live: fresh signup through disclosure/recovery renders the dashboard immediately; forced an unreachable-backend + cleared epoch cache and confirmed /, /wallets, /explorer all render fully clickable with the banner showing 'still trying' state; /sync's new 'Back to Heartwood' link returns cleanly. npm run check: 0 errors. layout.server.test.ts: 19/19 passing (rewritten for the new non-blocking behavior).

title: Stream Electrum-bound SSR loads: wallet/multisig detail + send pages
description:
Extends cairn-ybsv (commit ee83cc5), which streamed dashboard/wallets-list/explorer-index but explicitly left wallet/multisig detail and send pages blocking. These await full BIP44 gap-limit Electrum scans (scanWallet/scanMultisig, many sequential round-trips, 60s cache) directly in `load`, freezing navigation until the scan completes.

Apply the existing streaming pattern (see src/routes/(app)/+page.server.ts `chain: loadChainSnapshot()` unawaited + src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte consuming via `.then()`/`{#await}`, and src/routes/(app)/wallets/+page.server.ts `scans: (async () => {...})()` IIFE-promise pattern) to:
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts (line ~61 getWalletDetail/scanWallet, ~63 peekReceiveAddress, ~73-76 getWalletUtxos+getTip, ~91 detectWalletUnconfirmedInflows) + its +page.svelte
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts (line ~83 getMultisigDetail/scanMultisig, ~84 peekMultisigReceiveAddress, ~94-97, ~112) + its +page.svelte
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.server.ts (~45, ~53, ~84, ~95) + its +page.svelte
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/send/+page.server.ts (~38, ~60, ~82) + its +page.svelte
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/stateless/+page.server.ts (~17 getFeeEstimates) + its +page.svelte

Each +page.svelte needs a loading-skeleton state for the streamed slice (match the visual treatment already used on the dashboard/wallets list for their streamed sections) — page must render its static chrome immediately and fill in balances/UTXOs/scan results as the promise resolves, with a defensive .catch() so a slow/unreachable Electrum server never surfaces as an unhandled rejection. Verify live via preview tool: point at an unreachable Electrum server and confirm these 5 routes still render and are clickable instantly.
close_reason: Superseded by cairn-2zxt.8.1 (true cache-first SWR, not just streaming). The abandoned streaming-only attempt on this bead (commit edf261f, worktree-agent-a34ef67341f65b9ed) was never merged and is now moot.

title: Stream Electrum-bound SSR loads: explorer sub-routes + admin node info
description:
Same bug class and fix pattern as the sibling wallet-streaming bead, scoped to the explorer sub-routes and admin overview that cairn-ybsv (commit ee83cc5) did not touch (it only streamed the explorer INDEX page, not its sub-routes):

- src/routes/(app)/explorer/address/[address]/+page.server.ts (~16 getAddressInfo, ~25 getAddressTxs) + +page.svelte
- src/routes/(app)/explorer/block/[id]/+page.server.ts (~20 getBlock, ~26 getTip, ~32 getBlockTxs — note `strip:` at ~48 is ALREADY streamed correctly, leave that pattern as the template) + +page.svelte
- src/routes/(app)/explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.server.ts (~22 getTx, ~45 Promise.all fee/rbf/cpfp/rawHex, ~61-63 getTxRbfInfo on redirect path) + +page.svelte
- src/routes/(app)/explorer/mempool/+page.server.ts (~11-17 Promise.all of 5 calls) + +page.svelte
- src/routes/(app)/explorer/mempool/blocks/+page.server.ts (~8-13) + +page.svelte
- src/routes/(app)/explorer/difficulty/+page.server.ts (~8-11) + +page.svelte
- src/routes/(app)/admin/+page.server.ts (~44 getNodeInfo() — a live UNCACHED Electrum headersSubscribe+banner round-trip on every /admin nav, worst per-call cost outside a full wallet scan) + +page.svelte

Apply the same unawaited-promise + `{#await}`/`.then()` + loading-skeleton pattern used on src/routes/(app)/explorer/+page.server.ts `chain: loadChainData(before)`. Each page needs its static chrome to render immediately; only the chain-derived data streams in. Verify live via preview tool against each of these 7 routes.
close_reason: All 7 explorer sub-routes + admin overview now stream chain data instead of blocking SSR (commit f64fec2, merged). Applied the cairn-ybsv unawaited-promise pattern to: admin overview (getNodeInfo), explorer address/block/tx/mempool/mempool-blocks/difficulty. Judgment calls: tx/[txid]'s getTx() and the not-found-path getTxRbfInfo() stay synchronous since they drive a routing decision (404 vs redirect to a replacement tx) that must resolve before the response streams -- only the 4 supplementary display calls (fees/rbf/cpfp/rawHex) stream; backend not-found for a syntactically-valid block/address now degrades to an in-page notFound state rather than a route 404 since that can't be known before streaming starts. Verified live: pointed admin settings at a deliberately unreachable Electrum+esplora host and confirmed /admin and /explorer/block/[id] render chrome+skeletons instantly and stay fully interactive through the hang, then degrade to a graceful in-page error+retry state after timeout -- exactly the freeze this bead fixes. npm run check: 0 errors.

title: Notification panel: fix text truncation and popover z-index wiring
description:
Both bugs isolated to src/lib/components/NotificationPanel.svelte only (no other files need to change):

1. TRUNCATION: `.row-msg` (CSS ~line 445, markup ~line 244/257) has zero truncation/clamp rules — no white-space/overflow/text-overflow/-webkit-line-clamp. The parent `.row-body` correctly has `min-width: 0` (~440) but nothing consumes it. Fix: add either single-line ellipsis truncation (match the `.truncate` utility pattern in src/app.css:211-215, used by src/lib/components/portfolio/RecentActivity.svelte:119-133) or a 2-line clamp (display:-webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp:2; -webkit-box-orient:vertical; overflow:hidden;) — pick whichever reads better for notification-length text.

2. Z-INDEX: the panel gets covered by the sidebar. Root cause: the component ALREADY has a half-written fix for this using the Popover API (top-layer rendering, immune to ancestor stacking contexts) — canPopover detection, popEl/bellEl variable declarations, positionPanel(), and an $effect calling showPopover() all exist (~lines 34-66), explicitly referencing this exact bug (cairn-k391) in a comment at ~line 37-42. But it was never wired up: no bind:this={popEl} on the .panel div (~line 214), no bind:this={bellEl} on the .bell button (~line 198), and no popover="manual" attribute on the .panel div — so popEl/bellEl stay null forever, positionPanel() and the $effect early-return every time, and it silently falls back to the broken position:absolute; z-index:40 path nested inside the sidebar's sticky stacking context (HWRail.svelte .rail, position:sticky). Fix: add the three missing bindings/attribute so the already-written Popover code path actually activates; adjust :popover-open styling if the popover top-layer default (which removes normal document flow positioning) needs any CSS follow-up to keep the panel's position anchored under the bell button.

Verify live via preview tool: open the notification panel with a long-text notification and confirm it truncates cleanly and renders above the sidebar, not behind it.
close_reason: Both bugs fixed in NotificationPanel.svelte only (commit 217f2f0, merged). Truncation: added a 2-line -webkit-line-clamp (plus standard line-clamp property) to .row-msg -- picked 2-line clamp over single-line ellipsis since real messages are title+body and routinely 150+ chars. Z-index: wired the three missing pieces for the already-half-written Popover API fix -- bind:this on both bellEl and popEl, and popover=manual on the panel div -- plus a :popover-open CSS rule so positionPanel()'s getBoundingClientRect coordinates still apply once the element is in the browser top layer; old position:absolute fallback left untouched for browsers without Popover API support. Verified live in real Chrome (not just source diff): seeded a real 254-char notification, confirmed clientHeight 35px (2 lines) vs scrollHeight 123px (clamped, ends in ellipsis) while shorter messages render unclamped; confirmed elementFromPoint at a sidebar-overlapping coordinate returns a node INSIDE the panel (popover-open, position:fixed) versus the sidebar itself in the old absolute-positioned state. npm run check: 0 errors.

title: Rebrand string sweep: server-layer Cairn strings missed by the frontend-only reskin lanes
description:
Audit found the CSS/component reskin is essentially 100% complete (58/61 routes, all non-heartwood components already match Heartwood tokens) — the residual gaps are literal "Cairn" strings hardcoded in server-layer .ts files and two .svelte strings, all previously missed because cairn-koy4 sub-beads only covered src/routes/ + src/lib/components/. NOTE: the "spend-limit page" Alex mentioned as the cutoff point does not exist anywhere in the codebase (grepped exhaustively, checked git log and beads) — flag this back to Alex, it may refer to a different feature/session than this one; do not invent a spend-limit page to reskin.

Swap "Cairn" -> "Heartwood" (or a brand-neutral rephrase where "Cairn" reads as a proper noun/product name) in exactly these user-facing spots — do NOT touch env var names (CAIRN_DB, CAIRN_LOG_FILE), the invite-code format CAIRN-XXXX-XXXX in generateInviteCode()/src/lib/server/auth.ts:749 (deliberate, leave as-is unless Alex says otherwise), the X-Cairn-Signature webhook header name (real protocol header, leave as-is), the electrum client handshake CLIENT_NAME string, or any code comments/identifiers:

- src/lib/server/disclosures.ts:17 DEFAULT_OPERATOR, :25,27 DEFAULT_USER_AGREEMENT, :55,57,59,63,65 ADMIN_DISCLOSURE (renders on /agreement, /disclosure, and prefills the admin settings agreement editor)
- src/lib/server/backup.ts:120,123 (wallet-backup restore error strings)
- src/lib/server/multisigExport.ts:37,70,223,345 (ColdCard export name fallback, config file header, import-validation error, descriptor-backup text)
- src/lib/server/walletExport.ts:114 (descriptor-backup text)
- src/lib/hw/ledger.ts:522 sanitizeMultisigPolicyName fallback (literally shown ON the Ledger device screen)
- src/lib/server/webauthn.ts:39 RP_NAME (shown in the OS passkey creation/sign-in dialog)
- src/lib/server/channels/email.ts:310,351 (test-notification subject strings — NOT emailTemplate.ts, that is a separate bead)
- src/lib/server/channels/ntfy.ts:184,185
- src/lib/server/channels/telegram.ts:161
- src/lib/server/channels/nostr.ts:353
- src/lib/server/channels/webhook.ts:250 (title only, not the header name)
- src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/config/+server.ts:33 (downloaded wallet-config JSON note field)
- src/routes/api/auth/register/password/+server.ts:49 (signup invite-error banner — also fixes the visible inconsistency where signup/+page.svelte:120 already says Heartwood but this error says Cairn on the same screen)
- src/routes/api/notifications/pgp/+server.ts:48
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/_components/CoinControl.svelte:171,264 (only 2 remaining Cairn strings in any .svelte file)

Verify via grep after: grep -rn "Cairn" src/ should only turn up the deliberately-excluded items above plus code comments/tests.
close_reason: Rebrand string sweep done: 15 files swapped Cairn->Heartwood (commit 6c934c7) + follow-up commit a5e0783 catching chainEvents.ts backend up/down notification bodies, trezor.ts Connect manifest appName, and updating 6 stale test files whose assertions still expected old strings. Final grep -rn Cairn src/ (excluding tests/comments) only shows deliberately-excluded identifiers: CLIENT_NAME='Cairn 0.1' electrum handshake, X-Cairn-Signature header, CAIRN_* env vars, CAIRN-XXXX invite code format, admin@cairn.local bootstrap email domain, and multisigBackupPdf.ts (owned by cairn-2zxt.6, untouched here). npm run check: 0 errors. Confirmed spend-limit-page premise does not exist anywhere in repo (git log, beads, grep) -- flagged to Alex separately.

title: Heartwood-ify the notification email template and multisig backup PDF
description:
Two rendered UI surfaces the 6-lane Heartwood redesign never touched at all (not just a string miss — no color/branding treatment either):

1. src/lib/server/channels/emailTemplate.ts — the HTML+text notification email sent for every channel event. COLORS.accent = #f7931a ("Bitcoin orange") does not match the actual Heartwood copper accent --accent: #e8935a (check current value in src/app.css). Brand wordmark says "Cairn" (~line 95, plus ~79 "View in Cairn" CTA button, ~116 footer). Update the color palette to the Heartwood copper/hairline tones and swap the wordmark/copy to Heartwood. Keep it email-client-safe (inline styles / table layout, no CSS custom properties — hardcode the resolved hex values).

2. src/lib/server/multisigBackupPdf.ts — the printable multisig backup PDF. Header is a bold 22pt "Cairn" text (~line 92); whole doc is monochrome (setDrawColor(180), black-and-white QR, zero color calls). Restore instructions also say "Cairn" (~line 247). Add the Heartwood copper accent as a header/rule color and swap wordmark/copy — a simplified HeartwoodMark glyph rendered as vector shapes is a nice-to-have if the PDF lib supports it easily, but is not required; color + correct text is the mandatory bar.

Verify by actually generating one of each (trigger a test notification from Settings > Notifications, download a multisig backup PDF) and visually inspecting the output.
close_reason: Notification email template + multisig backup PDF reskinned to Heartwood (commit 4cd5953, merge 40c2e5e-range, test fix 071fa23). emailTemplate.ts: replaced Bitcoin-orange palette with the real Heartwood tokens pulled from app.css (accent #e8935a, on-accent #1a1210, surfaces #100d0b/#17120f, border #3a2f27, text #ede4db/#a99c90, attention #d8b27a, error #e0604c); wordmark/CTA/footer copy swapped to Heartwood; stayed email-client-safe (inline styles, no custom properties). multisigBackupPdf.ts: copper accent applied to wordmark/rules/headings via jsPDF setTextColor/setDrawColor/setFillColor, added a simplified vector HeartwoodMark glyph (concentric rings + pith dot), wordmark and restore-instructions copy updated, QR modules and body text deliberately left black for scannability/legibility. Verified by actually rendering both artifacts (vitest + playwright-core Chrome screenshot) and visually confirming colors/copy, not just source diff. npm run check: 0 errors. Both existing test suites pass (multisigBackupPdf.test.ts 2/2, emailTemplate.test.ts fixed 4/4 stale assertions -> all pass).

title: Umbrel store listing: rename to Heartwood + ship the release
description: Update C:\dev\umbrel-community-app-store\caravan-store-cairn\umbrel-app.yml: name/tagline/description currently still say Cairn (app now displays Heartwood everywhere per v0.1.9's releaseNotes, which already announced the in-app rebrand but the listing metadata itself was NOT updated). Alex is now explicitly asking for the store listing text itself to say Heartwood -- this is a scope change from cairn-koy4.13 (deliberately left open, app id/repo/env vars still do NOT change, only display name/tagline/description/releaseNotes prose). Bundle this yml edit with the next version release once cairn-2zxt.1-.6 land, following the anthropic-skills:umbrel-update-app checklist (verify amd64+arm64 manifest, git diff --check, real update-path test on a persistent volume, releaseNotes with upstream GitHub release link). Also worth a follow-up pass: docs/screenshots/*.png in the store gallery are still stale (show old pre-Heartwood UI) -- not blocking this release but flag to Alex.

Listing name was already 'Heartwood' — no rename needed, just the ship.

title: Stale-while-revalidate page loading (wallet/multisig/dashboard/explorer)
description: Product-owner priority override for v0.2.4: replace the epic-cairn-vknb streaming pattern (which still re-scans/re-fetches on every navigation, just non-blocking) with true cache-first SWR. load() becomes SQLite-only (persisted snapshot + lastSyncedAt); a throttled single-flight refresh endpoint does the real Electrum/chain work in the background; client invalidate() picks up fresh data. Send/PSBT flows explicitly excluded — must stay synchronous to avoid stale-UTXO coin selection. Modeled on BlueWallet/Sparrow cached-first UX.
close_reason: Both sub-tracks shipped and merged to single-sig-full-wallet. True SWR now covers wallet detail, multisig detail, wallet list, dashboard, and all explorer pages.

title: SWR: wallet/multisig detail + wallet-list cache-first loads
description: Persisted wallet_snapshots table (balance/UTXOs/receive-peek/unconfirmed-inflows/tip), walletSync.ts single-flight+20s-throttle refresh engine, POST /api/wallets/[id]/refresh + /api/wallets/multisig/[id]/refresh, retrofitted load() in wallets/[id], wallets/multisig/[id], wallets/+page to read cache-only, SyncIndicator.svelte component. Send/PSBT pages explicitly untouched.
close_reason: Merged 953d6a3: wallet_snapshots table, walletSync.ts single-flight+throttle, refresh endpoints, cache-first load() on wallets/[id], wallets/multisig/[id], wallets/+page, SyncIndicator.svelte. 1991 tests pass, npm run check clean.

title: SWR: dashboard/explorer cache-first loads
description: Persisted chain_snapshot table, chainSync.ts single-flight+throttle refresh engine reusing ChainService's existing TTL cache, POST /api/chain/refresh, retrofitted load() in dashboard/explorer/difficulty/mempool to read cache-only + lastSyncedAt, inline sync indicator per page.
close_reason: Merged 614bbc8: chain_snapshot table, chainSync.ts single-flight+throttle, /api/chain/refresh, cache-first load() on dashboard/explorer/difficulty/mempool. 1991 tests pass, npm run check clean.

title: Background-refresh scan storm monopolizes Electrum pool, causes zeroed-balance flash on cold start
description: Post-v0.2.4 diagnostic pass (Alex reported app still slow/unresponsive on Umbrel): the wallets-list page's onMount fired one POST /refresh per wallet AND per multisig -- on a real portfolio that's N concurrent full gap-limit Electrum scans against a 2-connection pool, monopolizing it and starving interactive requests (send build, tx lookup). Separately, a failed cold start rendered fake zeroed balances instead of a syncing/retry state.
close_reason: Merged 5b282df: global semaphore (createLimiter, cap=pool size 2) wraps all scan work; single coalesced POST /api/portfolio/refresh replaces the N-way fan-out, most-stale-first, aborts remaining queue on connect-class failure. Startup warm pass persists real snapshots. Three-state UI (ready/first-sync/unreachable) on wallets list -- never renders a fake zero. wallet_snapshots gets a <1KB summary column so list view stops parsing full tx/address arrays per wallet per nav. ElectrumPool priority lane (interactive vs background) intentionally deferred as a larger follow-up. 2013 tests pass, npm run check clean.

title: explorer/tx/[txid] blocks first paint on live Electrum getTx
description: Post-v0.2.4 diagnostic pass: the one route not covered by the SWR work. load() awaited chain.getTx(txid) synchronously (needed to decide RBF-replacement redirect vs 404), so a slow/unreachable Electrum hangs the whole page open.
close_reason: Merged 2b65e0e: hybrid cache (tx_snapshots table). Cache hit renders + decides redirect instantly with a background refresh; cache miss wraps getTx in a 4s timeout, rendering a loading shell with a bounded client poll instead of hanging. Redirect-safety holds because replacement status is monotonic -- a stale cache hit is at worst out of date, never wrong, and self-corrects via the existing live RBF-chain callout. 3 new tests, npm run check clean.

title: ElectrumPool has no priority lane -- background scans can still queue behind interactive requests
description: Deferred from cairn-2zxt.8.3: the new scan semaphore caps concurrent background scans at the pool size (2), which removes the N-way monopolization that caused the original unresponsiveness. But ElectrumPool itself is still plain round-robin with no request-priority concept -- a background portfolio-refresh scan and an interactive request (building a send, opening a tx) compete equally for the same 2 connections. A dedicated priority lane (reserve a connection for interactive requests, or preempt/deprioritize background scan requests) would fully close this, but needs real pool-scheduler rework, not a small change.
close_reason: Merged 191b457: lane-aware pick() implemented -- one socket reserved that the background scan lane never selects, interactive lane always picks least-pending eligible socket. SCAN_CONCURRENCY decoupled from raw pool size (pegged to background-lane-width = pool-1) so a future pool bump can't silently raise scan pressure. Pool default bumped 2->3 (both the pool.ts constant and the settings.ts default that actually drives production). 2020 tests pass, npm run check clean. Real contention relief under live load is unverified (flagged) -- lane logic is proven deterministically via pendingCount in tests.

title: getWalletUtxos/getMultisigUtxos fired N individual listUnspent calls instead of batching
description: Found during the Electrum pool lane-awareness audit: unlike the gap-limit scanner (which already batches history+balance lookups via batchRequest), getWalletUtxos (transactions.ts) and getMultisigUtxos (multisigScan.ts) did Promise.all(candidates.map(listUnspent)) -- N separate facade calls each independently pick()-ing a pool socket, not real batching. On a wallet/multisig with many used addresses this sprayed a scan's UTXO fetch across the whole pool instead of pipelining it onto one lane-appropriate socket.
close_reason: Merged 191b457: both converted to a single electrum.batchRequest([...listunspent]) call, matching the gap scanner's existing pattern. Added an empty-candidates fast path. New transactionsUtxo.test.ts proves batchRequest called exactly once with all sub-requests, listUnspent never called, response order preserved.

title: GET /api/portfolio (dashboard hero balance) ran live Electrum scans on cache miss -- never covered by SWR work
description: Alex QA on real Umbrel: Home dashboard still slow/laggy after wallet-page SWR+throttling+lane-pool fixes. Root cause: +page.svelte fetches GET /api/portfolio on mount, which called getPortfolioDetail() -- live scanWallet/scanMultisig Promise.all across every wallet, guarded only by a 60s in-memory ScanCache with a 10s per-wallet timeout. This was a completely separate, never-fixed code path that happened to render on the dashboard -- none of the chain_snapshot/wallet_snapshots SWR work touched it. Same request path also did unbounded synchronous SQLite work (backfillSnapshots/recordSnapshot/getBalanceSeries reading all balance_snapshots rows) inline on every GET.
close_reason: Merged 26ee4a5: GET /api/portfolio is now a synchronous read of a persisted portfolio_snapshot, never scans. The aggregate is computed inside the existing coalesced refreshPortfolio pass (from the per-wallet snapshots it just wrote, zero extra Electrum) instead of a second competing scan path. backfillSnapshots/recordSnapshot/getBalanceSeries moved entirely off the request path into the background refresh; getBalanceSeries bounded to a ~400-day window + downsampled to <=400 points. 2031 tests pass, npm run check clean.

title: Explorer chain-snapshot refresh refetched difficulty/hashrate every pass despite rarely changing
description: chainSync.ts's refreshChainSnapshot refetched everything (recent blocks, mempool, fees, difficulty history, hashrate) on every 20s throttled pass, even though difficulty/hashrate only meaningfully change on a new block (~10min) not every refresh cycle. Wasted Electrum round-trips and CPU on Umbrel/ARM for data that was almost always unchanged.
close_reason: Merged 26ee4a5: doRefresh split into volatile-every-pass fetches (blocks/tip/mempool/fees/projections) and epoch-scale fetches (hashrate, difficulty info/history) that only refetch when tip height has advanced since the last successful refresh; otherwise persisted values carry forward. Same chain_snapshot storage, only which live calls fire changed. Test proves two same-tip refreshes call the epoch-scale fetches once (not twice); tip advance re-triggers them.

title: Portfolio has no push-on-update path -- dashboard only refreshes on mount/manual trigger
description: Deferred from cairn-2zxt.8.7: an SSE 'portfolio-updated' event type (wired into walletSync after buildPortfolioAggregate, new event in liveBlocks.ts/api/events, client subscription) would let the dashboard refetch its now-cheap GET when a background refresh completes elsewhere, instead of only on mount + manual POST /refresh. The existing SSE channel already drives onNewBlock for the chain snapshot; new blocks are deliberately kept off the portfolio-invalidation path to avoid triggering rescans, so this needs its own emitter. Assessed as nontrivial additive plumbing, not a small addition -- correctly out of scope for the mount+refresh fix, but worth doing if push-freshness matters more than the current pull model.

title: Retrofit difficulty-history bars + mempool fee bands onto CairnChart
description: cairn-49wy built CairnChart.svelte (shared axes/gridlines/tooltip) but explicitly left the difficulty-page bar chart and mempool fee-distribution bands on the old bare CSS-width/height divs. Extended CairnChart with a bar mode (signed vertical bars + horizontal proportional bars) and retrofit both pages onto it.
close_reason: Merged 2d98cf7: CairnChart bar mode (kind='bar', ChartBar type, vertical signed + horizontal orientations) added; difficulty page and mempool fee bands now have real value axes and hover tooltips instead of native title attrs. npm run check clean.

title: Electrum unbounded buffer growth on unterminated payload
description: client.ts:359-374 — this.buffer += chunk has no size cap. A server that never sends newline grows buffer without limit (memory exhaustion DoS). The eventual JSON.parse on a huge buffer stalls the event loop synchronously. Fix: add MAX_BUFFER_SIZE constant, destroy socket if exceeded.
close_reason: Fixed with tests + MANUAL.md updates; see per-bead comments for root cause + evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Fixed in client.ts onData(): added MAX_BUFFER_SIZE = 32 MiB cap (+ overridable maxBufferBytes option for tests). After draining complete lines, if the incomplete residual exceeds the cap the socket is destroyed and the normal disconnect/backoff-reconnect path runs. Because chunks accumulate across onData() calls, even a single monstrous newline-terminated line trips the cap before its terminator arrives, so the JSON.parse-stalls-event-loop case is also covered. Tests: 'destroys the connection when an unterminated payload exceeds the buffer cap' + 'does not destroy ... for a large but newline-terminated response under the cap' (client.test.ts).

title: Whale-address pagination refetches full Electrum history on every page
description: getAddressTxs (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts:1137-1155) calls getScripthashHistory(scripthash) -- a full Electrum get_history RPC for that scripthash -- on EVERY call, including every 'Load more' page request, then sorts the entire result and slices out just 50 items (PAGE=50) for the requested page. For an address with a large history (a whale/exchange address with thousands of txs), this makes paging O(page_number * full_history_size) instead of O(page_size): page 1 refetches+resorts the whole history to return 50 items, page 2 does it again, etc. A short-TTL server-side cache of the raw history list per scripthash (keyed on scripthash, invalidated on new-block/mempool events or after a few seconds) would let subsequent pages reuse the already-fetched+sorted list, making paging cost independent of how deep the user has paged. Not user-visible as a correctness bug -- only a latency/resource-efficiency issue on large addresses. Found during cairn-obg6/cairn-uibo explorer QA pass, filing separately since it's out of scope for that fix.

title: webhook SSRF guard has a DNS-rebinding TOCTOU gap — validation and actual fetch resolve DNS independently
description: src/lib/server/channels/webhook.ts: checkTargetUrl() (~167-217) resolves the hostname via dns/promises lookup(host, {all:true}) purely to validate it against a private/loopback/link-local blocklist, then returns { ok: true, url } where url is the ORIGINAL URL object — the validated IP addresses are discarded, never pinned or reused. postWebhook() (~234-275) then calls fetch(check.url.toString(), {...}) with the hostname intact and no dispatcher/Agent/custom lookup override to pin the connection to the already-checked address. Node's built-in fetch (undici) performs its own independent DNS resolution when it opens the connection. An attacker who registers a domain and controls its DNS can serve a safe public IP (short TTL) for the validation lookup, then serve 169.254.169.254/127.0.0.1/a LAN address for the resolution undici performs microseconds later when actually connecting — bypassing the SSRF guard entirely via classic DNS rebinding. The file's own doc comment claims the guard is 'DNS-resolved' but never pins the validated address for the real request. Fix: resolve the hostname once, validate the result, then either (a) connect directly to the validated IP with the original Host header (e.g. via a custom dispatcher/agent or an explicit IP-based URL + Host header override), or (b) re-validate on every redirect/retry using the exact same resolution the actual TCP connection will use.
notes: Partially mitigated in fc8da90: SSRF gate now shared (ssrf.ts) and validates ALL resolved A/AAAA records. Remaining work: pin the socket to the validated IP (node:http transport) to fully close the TOCTOU — requires reworking webhook/ntfy delivery tests off global-fetch mocking.
close_reason: Closed the DNS-rebinding TOCTOU in commit cc66e42: safeFetch now pins the connection to the exact IP checkTargetUrl validated via a node:http(s) transport (host=validated IP, Host header + TLS servername = real hostname), so DNS is resolved once and never re-consulted at connect. webhook/ntfy/queue tests reworked onto a mockable _transport seam. check + 962 tests green.

title: [hash-strategy] No-payout leaderboard federation (bragging-rights meta-board) — design
description: Cross-instance best-share leaderboard with NO payouts: instances gossip self-certifying share evidence (verification recipe already specified in docs/COINBASE-PAYOUT-POOL-DESIGN.md §14.4) over the federation transport, rendering a meta-board that is pure bragging rights. Because no coinbase value moves, this is legally OUTSIDE gate cairn-l1zu.1 (same logic as the pot-free leaderboard, DIFFICULTY-RAFFLE-ANALYSIS §f) — but it IS blocked on federation infra epic cairn-cz3q (Tor transport, F3/F4 invite gate). Strategic keystone (docs/HASH-ATTRACTION-STRATEGY.md roadmap #6): builds the exact gossip/verify rail the future payout federation needs, money-free, and doubles as the opt-in fleet-hashrate KPI telemetry substrate. Get a legal sanity-check note before shipping anyway.

title: Missing regression test: Ledger/ColdCard/QR signer method-list wiring (cairn-5i3)
description: cairn-5i3 (P1, closed in 0f59954 + a092c0f) fixed Ledger, ColdCard, and QR signers being built and tested but never wired into the Sign step's method list (they showed as 'coming soon' or were absent). Fix confirmed present in send/+page.svelte's method grid, but there is no unit test for the method-list construction — it's pure Svelte component logic with no colocated test. Recommend extracting the method-list/capability-gating logic into a plain .ts function and adding a unit test asserting all four device methods (Ledger, Trezor, ColdCard, QR) appear with no 'coming soon' placeholders, and that a capability-guard-false case renders as disabled-with-reason rather than silently vanishing (this exact silent-vanishing failure mode is also cairn-8dup from the same audit).
close_reason: Committed in 81181db: Trezor warn-not-swallow tests, signPsbtWithTrezor + signPsbtWithJade (scripted device + PIN-relay fetch) coverage, sign-method list extracted to pure signMethods.ts with unit tests (capability-false renders disabled-with-reason, never vanishes).

title: Solo mode leaks copy/links pointing at team-gated admin pages
description: adminqa QA (2026-07-12/13): with instance_mode=solo, /admin/feature-flags copy says 'A per-user exception can be set from a user's page under Users' (src/routes/(app)/admin/feature-flags/+page.svelte) and the /admin/users/[id] detail page renders a '<- All users' back-link (src/routes/(app)/admin/users/[id]/+page.svelte) — both point at /admin/users, which 404s in solo mode (assertTeamMode, src/lib/server/api.ts:128). Confirmed via browser: /admin/users 404s while /admin/users/2 renders with the dead back-link and the feature-flags page keeps the 'under Users' copy. Low severity UX inconsistency, not a security issue.
close_reason: Fixed: admin/feature-flags/+page.svelte now gates the 'per-user exception... under Users' lead-copy sentence and each flag's override-count badge (link vs plain span) behind data.instanceMode === 'team'. The back-link half of this bead (dead '<- All users' link on admin/users/[id]) is resolved as a side effect of cairn-7xlf's fix: that whole route now 404s in solo mode, so the page (and its dead link) never renders there at all. Verified live via the shared dev preview (port 5344, currently solo mode): lead paragraph renders as 'Turn features on or off for the whole instance. Everything is on by default.' with no trailing Users sentence, no console errors. MANUAL.md updated.

title: Explorer search misclassifies valid txid as unknown when Electrum-only (verbose unsupported)
description:
Found at 42d34ad on qa1 (qa1.localhost:5191), pointed at electrum.blockstream.info:50002 TLS, no Bitcoin Core RPC configured (the recommended out-of-box public-server config).

REPRO:
1. Get any real, recent, confirmed mainnet txid that is NOT a coinbase-to-legacy-address tx, e.g. 68f71dcb03ce2f7c7034d89c86ea61dbdbc52f4c2b486aa78fe760a4072db3e0 (verified 64 lowercase-hex chars via `echo -n <txid> | wc -c`).
2. Navigate to /explorer?q=<txid> (or type it into the explorer search box and press Enter).

EXPECTED: search recognizes the 64-hex-char string as a txid and offers "View transaction" / routes to /explorer/tx/<txid>, same as navigating there directly (which correctly renders the honest "Transaction detail needs a Bitcoin Core node" notice -- see tx/[txid]/+page.svelte).

ACTUAL: "Couldn't classify '68f71dcb03ce2f...4072db3e0' -- searches match a block height (like 800000), a 64-character block hash or txid, or an address (1..., 3..., bc1...)." Reproduced with a second independent real txid (7665fb4fd25d752514d26a6b325cb302c5105507c9fd2f86e9178157267a08d4, also verified 64 chars) -- not a fluke. Block-hash search for the same block (00000000000000000001721fe695ec86e1bac9d2c06223457d3d9a671de7fe06) works fine ("Looks like a block hash / View block") because it short-circuits on the leading-zeros check before ever calling chain.getTx().

ROOT CAUSE (traced): src/lib/server/search.ts classifySearch(), the HEX64_RE branch (lines 72-97) calls `chain.getTx(hex).catch(() => TX)` at line 82 -- ANY error from getTx() (not just genuine not-found) is swallowed and treated as "not a tx", falling through to a block-hash lookup that also fails, yielding `unknown`. With no Core RPC configured, chain.getTx() (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts ~line 989) falls back to getTxViaElectrum() (~line 1019), which calls `this.electrum.getTransaction(txid, true)` -- VERBOSE=true. Blockstream's public server (the exact server this task pointed qa1 at) replies `{"error":{"code":-32603,"message":"verbose transactions are currently unsupported"}}` for any verbose call. That error doesn't match the /no such|not found/i check at line 1024, so it's rethrown as a generic Error -- which search.ts's blanket catch then misreports as "couldn't classify" instead of routing to the tx page (which already has a proper graceful notice for exactly this state) or surfacing the real reason.

Same root cause also produces "Couldn't load transactions -- Electrum error: verbose transactions are currently unsupported" on the address detail page's transaction history section (tested against bc1q8mnhpqwuvymt9q856n5r5dnnvvajwf7k4dv5uk, a real address with 0.68 BTC balance -- balance loads fine via non-verbose electrum calls, only the tx-list breaks).

IMPACT: any self-hoster running the documented default (public Electrum server, no Core RPC) gets a broken/misleading explorer search for the txid case specifically -- looks like the user typo'd or pasted something malformed when they didn't.

Context from prior QA pass: an earlier pass flagged invalid-checksum addresses hard-404ing in search; that specific case was NOT reproduced here (invalid input classifies to 'unknown' gracefully, no 404). This bead is a distinct, newly-confirmed defect on the txid path with root cause pinned.

NOT filed: the two dropdown-suggestion-link clicks that appeared not to navigate during this session -- inconclusive due to the explorer's live SSE tip updates causing rapid DOM churn; the primary search flow (type + Enter + View address/block) works reliably and was used for the rest of this session's testing.
close_reason: done

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Fixed. Root cause: src/lib/server/search.ts classifySearch() HEX64_RE branch blanket-caught ALL chain.getTx() errors as "not a tx" and fell through to a block-hash lookup, which also failed for a real txid -> "unknown". isNotFoundError() was already exported in this file (used by 6 other routes to distinguish genuine not-found from other backend errors) but classifySearch itself never called it.

Fix: the getTx() catch now branches on isNotFoundError(e). Genuine not-found -> falls through to block-hash lookup (unchanged prior behavior). Any OTHER error -- capability/transport errors from Electrum-only backends (e.g. Blockstream's public server's "verbose transactions are currently unsupported"), unconfigured Core RPC, etc. -- routes straight to /explorer/tx/<hex> instead of misreporting as unclassifiable; the tx page already renders its own honest "needs a Bitcoin Core node" notice for exactly this state. Budget-exhaustion/timeout still falls through to the block lookup (unchanged prior behavior).

This matches the required behavior: getTx() transport/capability errors are now treated as "plausibly a txid -- route optimistically", and only a definitive not-found classifies as a miss.

Verification:
- Added 4 unit tests to src/lib/server/search.test.ts covering: a non-not-found getTx error (simulated Electrum verbose-unsupported capability error) routing to tx; routing to tx even when getBlock would also resolve (guards against re-adding a blanket fallback); genuine not-found still falling through to block/unknown; and a literal regression test using the bead's exact repro txid (68f71dcb03ce2f7c7034d89c86ea61dbdbc52f4c2b486aa78fe760a4072db3e0) against the mocked verbose-unsupported error.
- Full suite: 286 files / 4036 tests passed, 1 documented skip (vaultRegtestE2E, needs live regtest stack -- unrelated). npm run check: 0 errors, 0 warnings.
- Browser QA on deve.localhost:5187 (isolated DB + Vite cache), desktop + mobile 375x812: searching the bead's repro txid now shows "Looks like a transaction ID / View transaction", and navigating directly to /explorer/tx/<txid> renders the same honest "Transaction detail needs a Bitcoin Core node" notice referenced in the bead's EXPECTED behavior -- confirming search and direct nav now agree. Block height (800000 -> "Looks like a block height / View block") and bech32 address (bc1qw508d6qejxtdg4y5r3zarvary0c5xw7kv8f3t4 -> "Looks like a Bitcoin address / View address") routing unaffected.

NOT fixed, separate code site -- follow-up needed: the bead also documents the address detail page's transaction-history section hitting the same underlying error-swallow on Electrum-only instances ("Couldn't load transactions -- Electrum error: verbose transactions are currently unsupported" against a real address). That is a different call site -- chain/index.ts's getTxViaElectrum / the address tx-history fetch path -- not search.ts's classifySearch, and not something this classifier fix touches. Recommend filing a separate follow-up bead scoped to chain/index.ts (or wherever the address-history fetch's error handling lives) to apply the same isNotFoundError-style distinction there.

Files changed: src/lib/server/search.ts, src/lib/server/search.test.ts (uncommitted, per worker brief).

title: Address-watcher baseline race causes false 'payment received'+'confirmed' notification floods for old transactions
description:
Observed live on the v0.1.3 Umbrel instance (http://umbrel.local:3211): the per-user Activity feed and Admin activity log show dozens of DISTINCT real, already-deeply-confirmed transactions (thousands of confirmations each, e.g. 3142, 9598, 11305, 18523...) each firing BOTH a "Payment received -- X BTC received to Legacy wallet" AND a "Transaction confirmed -- now has N confirmations" notification, all clustered within about one hour, for a wallet whose real transaction history predates this notification session by a long time. Matching txids appear in both notification types (e.g. txid 0ddc338f397fa21e... -> both "Payment received -- 0.00012 BTC" and "Transaction confirmed -- ... 9602 confirmations").

Root cause (src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts): baselineExisting() (line 637) is supposed to seed notified_txids with confirmed=1 for every pre-existing tx BEFORE state.baselined flips true, specifically to prevent this exact flood (see its own doc comment, line 631-636). It is a slow, fully sequential per-scripthash loop (await chain.electrum.getHistory(scripthash) one address at a time, up to WATCH_WINDOW*2 = 60 addresses per wallet), wrapped in a try/catch that silently skips any address whose history fetch fails (line 653-655) -- with no retry and no re-baseline pass.

handleNewBlock() (line 527) has NO state.baselined guard (unlike handleScripthashChange(), which correctly gates on it at line 390) -- it processes every notified_txids row with confirmed=0 on every single 'header' event, including headers re-emitted by reconnects.

Given cairn-u7bw, this instance's Electrum connection was reconnecting every ~90-120s throughout the observation window -- so baselineExisting()'s slow sequential scan is very likely to have individual getHistory() calls fail mid-pass when the socket drops, leaving those specific addresses' real history un-baselined (never inserted with confirmed=1). Once state.baselined flips true regardless, the next scripthash-change event for one of those addresses (e.g. triggered by a resubscribe after the next reconnect) runs handleScripthashChange() for real: the txid isn't in notified_txids yet, so it's treated as brand new, recordTxid() inserts it with the schema default confirmed=0, and it immediately fires "Payment received". The very next 'header' event's handleNewBlock() then picks up that same freshly-inserted confirmed=0 row, sees it already has thousands of real confirmations, and immediately fires "Transaction confirmed" for it too.

Impact: a real user reconnecting after any period of Electrum instability (or simply restarting the app on a wallet with substantial existing history) can see a confusing flood of "new payment" / "transaction confirmed" notifications for transactions that happened long ago and were never new -- this landed as 50+ notifications in a single admin instance in this session.

Fix shape: make baselineExisting() resilient to partial failure -- e.g. retry failed addresses on the next periodic refresh before allowing handleScripthashChange to treat them as live, or track baselined-state per-scripthash rather than one global state.baselined flag, so a mid-scan Electrum drop can't let stale history leak through as "new".
close_reason: Fixed in src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts: baselined-state is now tracked PER SCRIPTHASH (state.baselinedScripthashes) instead of one global flag. baselineScripthash() only marks an address baselined after a fully successful history fetch; failures stay quarantined and are retried by refreshWatches' 5-min sweep AND on-demand when a change event arrives (which silently baselines instead of notifying, so stale history can never leak out as 'payment received'). handleNewBlock() now has the same !state.baselined startup gate as handleScripthashChange(). Bonus: newly subscribed wallets (incl. imports with history) are baselined by the retry sweep before going live. addressWatcher tests pass; full suite green (1445).

title: [pool-ux] No guided per-device connect help - card assumes the miner knows where stratum fields live
description: Evidence: MiningConnectionCard.svelte shows three copyable fields (pool address, worker username, password x) with one intro sentence. There is no per-device guidance (Bitaxe/AxeOS field names, Antminer web UI path, NerdMiner), no troubleshooting (nothing appeared after 5 minutes - check X), and no link to any help panel; the projects own UX philosophy is guided wizards and plain language. Braiins Academy and F2Pool publish step-by-step per-device guides; F2Pools docs even warn about the offline-alert foot-gun from misnamed workers. First-connect success rate is the single most important funnel step for attracting hash. Proposed fix (plain language): add a How do I enter this on my miner expander with 2-3 device tabs (Bitaxe/AxeOS, generic ASIC web UI, CPU miner for testing) mapping each field to that devices UI, plus a not connecting? checklist (same network, LAN access enabled by operator, port, exact username with dot suffix).

comments.author: pool-ux-audit
comments.text: Overlap note: cairn-8tfek [hash-strategy] covers the Bitaxe onboarding guide + connection wizard at the strategy level. This bead is the concrete UI evidence/spec for the MiningConnectionCard surface - implement together with 8tfek, do not build twice. Also note cairn-bm7c2 (loopback-bind trap) is the P1 prerequisite: no wizard succeeds while the default bind makes the printed address unreachable.

title: Dashboard /api/portfolio takes ~20s to resolve, balance stuck on skeleton loader
description:
Reproduced on Heartwood v0.2.0 at umbrel.local:3211 (browser QA pass).

On a fresh load of / (dashboard), the TOTAL BALANCE skeleton loader sits indefinitely -- confirmed via network trace that GET /api/portfolio stays in 'pending' state for 15-20+ seconds before finally returning 200. During this window Send/Receive are effectively dead (they link to /wallets, but the dashboard itself looks broken/frozen). A second /api/portfolio request was also observed firing and hanging after the first one resolved (possibly a background refresh).

Root cause (from reading src/lib/server/portfolio.ts + walletScan.ts): getPortfolioDetail() does Promise.all() over scanWallet()/scanMultisig() for every wallet. Each Electrum RPC has a 15s per-request timeout (src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts), but Promise.all only resolves once EVERY wallet's scan settles -- so one slow/unreachable wallet (the account under test has a 'QA Trezor (regtest)' wallet, likely pointed at a regtest Electrum server) drags the whole dashboard load to the slowest wallet's timeout, even though scan failures are already caught per-wallet and excluded from totals.

This is adjacent to cairn-2zxt (Electrum-bound SSR loads blocking navigation) but distinct: this is a client-side fetch to a dedicated /api/portfolio endpoint, not an SSR load function, and it's the dashboard's primary balance display that's affected.

Suggested fix direction: either (a) give getPortfolioDetail/scanWallet a shorter per-wallet budget with Promise.allSettled + a race against a timeout so slow wallets degrade gracefully instead of blocking the whole response, or (b) stream the portfolio response per-wallet like the SSR loads in cairn-2zxt.2 so the dashboard can render wallets as they resolve.

Repro: log in, load http://umbrel.local:3211/, watch Network tab for /api/portfolio.
close_reason: Fixed in 62ba92b (merge of worktree-agent-a19a2a3d9b3a5e617), reconciled with the P0 hotfix already shipped in 94b5efc (cairn-juhe). getPortfolioDetail() races the tip lookup and every per-wallet scan against a 10s PORTFOLIO_SCAN_TIMEOUT_MS budget (withTimeout with proper clearTimeout/unref cleanup), so one slow/unreachable wallet can no longer drag the whole /api/portfolio response to its worst case -- it settles as excluded-from-totals through the existing per-wallet catch/log path. npm run check 0/0, full suite 1666 passed/1 skipped.

comments.author: qa-heartwood-v0.2.0
comments.text: Confirmed the same stall affects /wallets (client-side nav from Send/Receive buttons lands there and sits on skeleton loaders for ~8s too) -- same root cause, the wallet-list loader awaits the same slow per-wallet scan (streamed 'scans' promise in __data.json). Not a separate SPA-routing bug.

comments.author: qa-heartwood-v0.2.0
comments.text: Possible root cause found: Admin > Settings has a stale SOCKS5 proxy (192.168.50.134:9050) configured, pre-existing (not set by me), which times out on Esplora calls (confirmed via /explorer/address/<addr> showing 'Proxy connection timed out'). Since the proxy setting applies to ALL chain traffic (Electrum + explorer) per its own description, this may be inflating the portfolio-scan timing measured here rather than (or in addition to) the gap-limit-scan cost analysis above. Filed as cairn-<see new bead> -- worth re-measuring this bead's repro after the proxy is cleared/fixed.

comments.author: qa-heartwood-v0.2.0
comments.text: Correction to previous comment: the proxy finding is filed as cairn-hy8z (not a placeholder).

The cairn-vxbk jargon-gloss pass (fix(ux) commit af2b620) deliberately skipped admin/** surfaces -- Node label + eyebrow references, and the "ring" quorum/health metaphor wording -- because another worker (cairn-9wld) held those files (admin/settings) as an active concurrent edit at the time, and vxbk's file-ownership boundary avoided clobbering in-flight work. Do the same termGlosses.ts-based gloss pass (src/lib/termGlosses.ts, see PASSKEY_TIP / DESCRIPTOR_TIP_SINGLE / DESCRIPTOR_TIP_MULTISIG for the established pattern: shared exported tip constants, wrapped via the existing <Term> component, copy-only changes, no markup restructuring) on the remaining admin-surface jargon:

Cite cairn-vxbk (closed, comment has full scoping rationale + DECLINED section listing this exact gap) as the source of this carve-out.
close_reason: Admin-surface jargon gloss pass (follow-up to cairn-vxbk, which skipped src/routes/admin/** for a since-resolved concurrent edit). Fixed: (1) admin's in-page eyebrow/title said Node while the app-shell nav already read Health (cairn-vxbk) -- src/routes/(app)/admin/+layout.svelte EyebrowBreadcrumb path now ['Health'], src/routes/(app)/admin/+page.svelte <title> now 'Health -- Heartwood'. (2) the 'ring' difficulty-epoch metaphor on the admin overview hero, parallel to vxbk's ChainStrip aria-label rewrite -- 'ring N forming -- X of 2,016 laid' is now 'difficulty period N forming -- X of 2,016 blocks in' (+page.svelte, both live and skeleton states). (3) added ELECTRUM_TIP and CORE_RPC_TIP to src/lib/termGlosses.ts and wrapped the Electrum/RPC headings on admin/settings in <Term> (same pattern as PASSKEY_TIP/DESCRIPTOR_TIP_*). Verified: npm run check 0 errors; live-rendered on a fresh adminqa dev instance (port 5401) -- HEALTH eyebrow confirmed on /admin and /admin/settings, RPC and Electrum tooltips confirmed rendering their gloss text via read_page in both public and custom connection modes.

title: cosigner fingerprint metadata never cross-checked against xpub
description: multisig.ts resolveKey only regex-validates /^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}$/; a wrong fingerprint is silently baked into every PSBT bip32Derivation + exported descriptor. No funds-loss (doesn't affect address math); hardware-signer confusion risk. Pinned in hostileXpubs.test.ts on branch test/qa-wave-2026-07-12, worktree C:\dev\cairn-testqa.
close_reason: Fixed (partial, cryptographically bounded): added verifyCosignerFingerprint() in src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisig.ts, called from validateMultisigKeyPaths (the ACCEPTANCE gate used by createMultisig/import, never from resolveKey which also runs on every read/derive/export of an already-stored wallet -- mirrors cosignerXpubDepth's accept-time-only discipline, cairn-b9iv). Full verification of a declared ROOT fingerprint against a multi-level xpub (BIP-48's 4-level paths) is cryptographically impossible from public data alone -- BIP-32 only exposes a key's IMMEDIATE parent fingerprint, and standard multisig paths are hardened past the master -- so that remains a documented, inherent trust boundary, not a fixable gap. The one case that IS fully checkable, a single-level path (depth 1, exactly BIP-45's m/45', used throughout this codebase for collaborative-custody vaults), is now cross-checked: the xpub's own parent-fingerprint field is the master fingerprint, compared directly against the declared value. '00000000' (documented unknown-fingerprint placeholder) is exempted. New tests in multisig.test.ts (4 cases: spoofed depth-1 rejected, genuine depth-1 accepted, placeholder exempted, depth>1 correctly left unchecked) -- also fixed a pre-existing test fixture that mismatched path vs xpub. multisig.test.ts (65/65), hostileXpubs.test.ts, multisigExport.test.ts, wallets-multisig.test.ts all pass. Commit 80c52fe.

Plausible paste input. Pinned in src/lib/server/bitcoin/hostileAddresses.test.ts on branch test/qa-wave-2026-07-12, worktree C:\dev\cairn-testqa.

(2) On mobile, the shell network banner + sync-status chip + eyebrow push the tip-height hero down even in the healthy/happy-path state (beyond the error-state banner pile-up already tracked in cairn-7zjo/cairn-obg6) -- collapse shell network+sync status into one compact chip on mobile. title: Surface derivation path more visibly in multisig 'Add keys' wizard step
description: Soft UX finding from 2026-07-06 derivation-path audit (docs/MULTISIG-DERIVATION-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md): the multisig creation wizard's main 'Add keys' step (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte, key 'slot' UI ~lines 1073-1098) shows device name/category/fingerprint for each cosigner but not the BIP-48 derivation path itself. Path is correctly stored/exported (descriptor export, ColdCard registration file) and used correctly for PSBT construction — this is purely a visibility nicety, not a correctness gap. Consider showing the path (e.g. m/48'/0'/0'/2') inline in each key slot, consistent with Cairn's plain-language UX philosophy (could show as a collapsed/secondary detail rather than headline).

title: Wire log.error() into remaining single-sig API route catch-alls
description:
Follow-up to cairn-obb (closed -- logger/handleError/request-logging infra all landed solidly). ~30 routes already call log.error() in their catch blocks (multisig broadcast, stateless ops, auth, admin restore, etc.) but a handful of single-sig routes were missed, most importantly:
- src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/broadcast/+server.ts (~line 26-35) -- catches, returns 502, never logs
- src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/+server.ts (~line 29-31, wallet detail GET)
- src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/receive/+server.ts (~line 18-20)
- src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/psbt/+server.ts (~line 75-77)

Not silent (hooks.server.ts logs a generic 'METHOD path 502 Nms' line for every request unconditionally) but the actual error/stack trace is lost for these specific routes since the exception is fully handled locally and never reaches the handleError hook. Add the same log.error({err: e, ...context}, 'message') pattern already used elsewhere (e.g. the multisig broadcast endpoint) to these remaining routes -- single-sig send/broadcast is the single most common money-moving path in the app, it should have the best error visibility, not the worst.
close_reason: Verified in d5f9c73: all previously-bare catch-alls in the single-sig wallet routes (wallets/[id], addresses, psbt, receive, transactions, broadcast, bump, utxo-mass) now call log.error({err: e, ...context}, 'message') via the 'wallet' childLogger before responding, matching the pattern already used in multisig routes. Spot-checked the broadcast endpoint directly -- log.error({err: e, walletId, txId}, 'wallet broadcast failed') now fires in the unexpected-error branch, right before the generic 502 response.

title: Settings component: expose Core RPC fields mode-independently + drop useDetectedCoreNode connection_mode flip (follow-up to cairn-6uok)
description:
Server side of cairn-6uok is FIXED at commit 6a06e17 - the save action in src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts now persists core_rpc_url/user/pass mode-independently, and absent-from-payload means leave-unchanged. Remaining component work in src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte (UX orchestrator territory):
1. Core RPC inputs are still rendered only inside the {#if connectionMode === 'custom'} block (~L284-467), so public-mode admins can't reach them even though the server now accepts them.
2. useDetectedCoreNode() (~L60-65) force-flips connectionMode to 'custom' as a workaround for the old drop bug (its doc comment cites cairn-6uok) - remove the flip so assisted connect stops mutating the operator's Electrum connection_mode as a side effect.
3. Out of scope of the fix, still open for Wave B Unit B2: coreRpcAssisted=umbrel hidden-field convention, core_rpc_provisioned_by='umbrel-detect' stamping, testCoreRpc() pre-persist validation. See docs/HANDOFF-wave-b-unit-b2-settings-form.md.
close_reason: Component-side item 1 fixed: Bitcoin Core RPC subgroup in +page.svelte now renders mode-independently (outside connectionMode==='custom'). See comments for full evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Partially landed, commit 0427661 (merge; underlying work 2292a20) on single-sig-full-wallet:
- Item 2 (useDetectedCoreNode() force-flip to connectionMode='custom') -- DONE. Function removed entirely; replaced with a self-contained assisted-connect card that never touches connectionMode.
- Item 3 (coreRpcAssisted=umbrel hidden-field convention, core_rpc_provisioned_by='umbrel-detect' stamping, testCoreRpc() pre-persist validation) -- DONE. New early-return branch in the `save` action, gated on the marker, validates via testCoreRpc() before persisting and stamps provenance on success. New dismissCoreDetection action also added (core_rpc_detected='dismissed'), covering the design doc's Section 8 stale-marker mitigation.
- Item 1 (expose the manual Core RPC input fields mode-independently, i.e. outside the connectionMode==='custom' block, for hand-entry not going through the assisted-connect flow) -- NOT done. The manual custom-mode subgroup (+page.svelte ~L400-470) is unchanged and still only renders in custom mode. A public-mode admin who wants to hand-type a non-Umbrel Core RPC endpoint (no assisted-connect banner involved) still has to flip to custom mode first. Left open for whoever picks this up next -- it's a bigger UX change (restructuring where that subgroup renders) than the two items above.

Tests: 8 new (31/31 total in page.server.test.ts). Full suite: 2698 passed / 1 skipped, 0 failed. svelte-check clean on touched files.

Leaving this bead OPEN since item 1 remains.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Fixed (component-side, item 1 from the cairn-6uok follow-up). src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte: moved the Bitcoin Core RPC subgroup (RPC URL/username/password inputs + test-connection button, formerly nested inside the {#if connectionMode === 'custom'} block) OUT to its own always-rendered <div class="subgroup proxy-group"> right after that block, so the manual hand-entry fields now render regardless of connectionMode -- matching the server, which has persisted core_rpc_* mode-independently since 6a06e17/cairn-6uok. Electrum server + Esplora subgroups intentionally stay inside the custom-mode conditional (those genuinely are mode-dependent -- public mode uses hardcoded electrum.blockstream.info + mempool.space). Reused the existing .proxy-group CSS class (border-top separator) for visual consistency since the field now needs its own section break instead of inheriting one from the removed parent wrapper. Did NOT touch cairn-zoz8.16 (Core RPC hide/reveal + Esplora removal redesign) -- kept minimal and compatible per brief.

Also added a persistence-proof test file, src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/page.server.load.test.ts (3 tests, unmocked $lib/server/settings so it exercises the real DB path): setSetting(core_rpc_url/core_rpc_user) + setSecretSetting(core_rpc_pass) round-trip with the raw instance_secrets.value_enc row asserted non-plaintext + isSecretEnvelope; a fresh getInstanceSettings() read recovers the plaintext; and the actual admin/settings +page.server.ts load() is called directly and asserted to surface coreRpcUrl/coreRpcUser + hasCoreRpcPass while 'coreRpcPass' in data.settings is false and JSON.stringify(data) never contains the raw password.

Secret-leak verdict: NO LEAK. getPublicInstanceSettings() (src/lib/server/settings.ts:192-197) destructures coreRpcPass out of the object entirely (const { coreRpcPass, ...rest } = getInstanceSettings(); return { ...rest, hasCoreRpcPass: !!coreRpcPass }) before the admin settings load() (src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts:15-24) returns it as page data -- the raw password never reaches the client, only a boolean presence flag. Confirmed by the new load() test and by the pre-existing settings.test.ts redaction suite (both pass).

Test evidence: npx vitest run (full suite) -- 2792 passed / 1 skipped / 0 failed. npm run check -- svelte-check clean, 0 errors/warnings across 1881 files.

title: docs: MANUAL.md lacks the 5-tier quorum-risk classifier documentation
description: quorumRisk.ts implements a 5-tier classifier (red/salmon/yellow/green/lightgreen) for multisig quorum risk, but MANUAL.md has no section documenting it. Rule order (from quorumRisk.ts): m==1 -> red; m==n -> yellow; 2m<=n (including ties) -> salmon; m==2 -> green; else -> lightgreen. Add a MANUAL.md section covering the tiers, the rule-order/precedence, and the copy shown to users for each tier. NOT edited here: MANUAL.md currently has uncommitted changes from another concurrent session, so this bead is filed but the doc itself was deliberately left untouched.

title: Multisig wallet name concatenates imported config name with user-entered name
description: When creating a multisig wallet via 'Already have this wallet in another app? Import it' with a Caravan JSON that has a 'name' field (e.g. "P2WSH-M"), and then typing a different name in the Confirm step (e.g. "Caravan Test Vault"), the resulting wallet's stored name becomes the two strings concatenated with no separator: "P2WSH-MCaravan Test Vault". Confirmed via: wallet page title, /wallets list, and the raw Caravan export from GET /api/wallets/multisig/[id]/caravan (the exported name field itself contains the mashed-together string). Likely cause: the imported config's name pre-fills the name input as a starting value, and the wizard appends rather than replaces when the user edits it, or the create action concatenates the two sources server-side. Repro: import docs fixture src/lib/server/__fixtures__/caravan-2-of-2-P2WSH_MAINNET.json (name: P2WSH-M), then type any different name in step 4 and create.
close_reason:
RETRACTED: not a real bug. A deep code trace by a second audit agent (checked every site where an imported config's name meets a user-typed name: +page.svelte:506 pre-fill-if-empty, +page.server.ts create action, api/wallets/multisig/import/+server.ts's '|| parsed.name' fallback, and multisig.ts createMultisig) found no concatenation logic anywhere -- multisigName is a single  string with a plain bind:value (+page.svelte:1454), and line 506 only pre-fills it 'if (imported.name && !multisigName.trim())', i.e. only when the field is still empty.

Root cause of the original observation: during live browser testing, the Caravan import correctly pre-filled the name field with 'P2WSH-M' (matching the imported config's own name). At the Confirm step I then clicked into that already-populated field and typed 'Caravan Test Vault' WITHOUT first clearing the existing text -- standard browser input behavior inserts typed text at the cursor position rather than replacing existing content, which produced the literal string 'P2WSH-MCaravan Test Vault' that I then submitted. Every downstream artifact (UI title, wallets list, Caravan/ColdCard export, PDF backup) correctly reflects that literal string I typed -- they are not further evidence of an app bug, they're confirmation the app faithfully stored and echoed back exactly what was submitted.

This was a test-methodology error (should have select-all'd or cleared the pre-filled field before typing), not a Cairn defect. Filing a much lower-priority, clearly-separate UX suggestion (auto-select pre-filled name text on focus) as a follow-up since it would have prevented even my mistaken input, but retracting this issue as originally filed.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Confirmed the same bug propagates to two more export formats, tested via curl: (1) ColdCard export (GET /api/wallets/multisig/2/coldcard) shows 'Name: P2WSH-MCaravan Test' -- truncated even further (missing trailing ' Vault'), suggesting the ColdCard file format applies its own length cap on top of the already-concatenated name. (2) The printable PDF backup (GET /api/wallets/multisig/2/backup-pdf) bakes in the literal string 'P2WSH-MCaravan Test Vault' -- a user who prints this for physical/break-glass storage gets the mangled name permanently on paper. Raises severity: this is written into every durable backup artifact the wallet produces, not just a cosmetic UI issue.

title: Test gap still open: describe.runIf silent-skip trap door in multisigPsbt.test.ts
description: The 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit noted: 'describe.runIf(scriptTypeReady(...)) in multisigPsbt.test.ts:665-748 is a silent-skip trap door — if p2sh/p2sh-p2wsh multisig support regresses, ~50 lines of tests vanish with zero failures instead of failing loudly.' Re-verified 2026-07-06: describe.runIf is still used in src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisigPsbt.test.ts and src/lib/server/bitcoin/vaultRegtestE2E.test.ts. This means a regression in p2sh/p2sh-p2wsh multisig script-type support would silently reduce test coverage rather than fail CI. Replace the runtime-conditional skip with either an explicit CI-visible skip reason (e.g. it.skip with a loud comment reviewed regularly) or make scriptTypeReady's precondition always true in the test environment so the tests always run.
close_reason: Committed in 26af83d: multisig substitution guard tests, legacy p2pkh/p2sh-p2wpkh spending-source tests, all 6 SPV reason codes, describe.runIf trap doors removed (multisig suites unconditional; regtest E2E skips loudly), coinbaseScan.test.ts fail-closed coverage.
