Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: addressWatcher live-notification window (WATCH_WINDOW=30) is fixed, not gap-limit-adaptive
description: addressWatcher.ts:44 subscribes a FIXED WATCH_WINDOW=30 addresses from index 0 for live push notifications (tx_received/tx_confirmed/tx_large), independent of the wallet's gap-limit scan cursor. A wallet whose last-used receive index is >30 (or with >30 consecutive unused addresses ahead of last-used) has a live-notification BLIND SPOT beyond index 30 — a deposit to address 31+ fires no live notification. Balance/history stay CORRECT (the full gapLimitScanner GAP_LIMIT=20 scan on next portfolio load picks it up); this is a notification-timeliness gap only, not a funds bug. Fix: tie the watch window to the scan cursor (lastUsed + gap) instead of a hardcoded 30 from 0, or document the cap. Note: gap-limit scanning itself (GAP_LIMIT=20, receive+change parallel, tail trimmed to lastUsed+20) was reviewed and is standard/correct.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: SCHEDULING (Fable roadmap): ship this WITH cairn-gakd — same file (addressWatcher.ts), same subsystem. gakd adds the subscription-REMOVAL path (leak on delete); this bead fixes the watch-window SIZING (fixed 30-from-0 blind spot). Distinct fixes, one PR — don't touch addressWatcher.ts twice.

title: Feature flags system (global + per-user overrides)
description: Admin-toggleable feature flags, resolved global-setting -> per-user-override -> resolved value, with server-side enforcement (not just UI hiding). Full scoping doc: docs/FEATURE-FLAGS-PLAN.md. Covers: send/spend, multisig creation, per-HW-driver toggles, explorer, coin control, CSV export, per-channel notification toggles, address book, camera/QR scan, wallet config import/export, stateless signer, plus reserved flags for unbuilt features (batch transactions, CPFP/RBF fee bumping, private mempool/tx review). All flags default ON so no existing install regresses.

title: Schema + flag registry
description: feature_flags (global) + user_feature_flags (per-user override) tables added to db.ts inline migration block, following the notification_preferences/notification_channel_config precedent. src/lib/server/featureFlags/registry.ts with the FeatureFlagDef type (defaultEnabled typed as literal true) and the initial ~24-flag list from docs/FEATURE-FLAGS-PLAN.md §1.1/§1.3. No separate migration files, matching this codebase's convention.

title: Resolution engine + request wiring
description: src/lib/server/featureFlags/resolve.ts: isFeatureEnabled(key, userId), resolveAllFlags(userId) implementing the global-row -> user-row -> registry-default precedence in docs/FEATURE-FLAGS-PLAN.md §1.2/§2. Wire event.locals.flags = resolveAllFlags(...) into hooks.server.ts right after locals.user is set. Add flags to the root (app)/+layout.server.ts load return. Add requireFeature(event, key) next to requireUser/requireAdmin in src/lib/server/api.ts.

title: Admin: global feature flags page
description: New /admin/feature-flags route (+page.server.ts + +page.svelte), same shape as /admin/notifications. Toggle grid grouped by FeatureFlagDef.category, one form action per flag, override-count badge per flag (COUNT from user_feature_flags GROUP BY key). See docs/FEATURE-FLAGS-PLAN.md §3.1.

title: Admin: per-user flag overrides
description: New /admin/users/[id] detail route (doesn't exist today) with a tri-state Inherit/Force-on/Force-off control per flag. Add an override-count badge/link on the existing /admin/users list page (+page.svelte:39-88). See docs/FEATURE-FLAGS-PLAN.md §3.2.

title: End-user UX: hide/grey + disabled messaging
description: Hide nav entries/pages the user has no access to at all (matching the existing conditional Admin-nav-item pattern in (app)/+layout.svelte:15); grey + show FeatureFlagDef.userMessage for in-context actions. Users never see flag names/categories, only the effect. See docs/FEATURE-FLAGS-PLAN.md §4.

title: Enforcement sweep: wire requireFeature() into every gated route
description: Add requireFeature(event, key) calls at the real server-side call sites for every flag in docs/FEATURE-FLAGS-PLAN.md §1.1: send API, multisig-creation routes, coin-control UTXO picker, CSV export route, address-book routes, QR camera entry points, wallet-config import/export routes, explorer routes, each src/lib/hw/* driver's registration path, each notification channel's send path. Largest/most scattered unit -- split into per-feature-area child beads as the sweep starts rather than guessing exact boundaries up front. This is the actual security boundary; §5 UI hiding alone is not enforcement.

title: Tests: resolution precedence + migration safety + enforcement
description: Unit tests covering all 5 states in docs/FEATURE-FLAGS-PLAN.md §1.2's precedence table; a migration-safety test asserting every registered flag resolves true against an empty database (defaultEnabled contract); at least one integration test asserting a gated route 403s via requireFeature when its flag is off.

title: Webhook HMAC signing secret has no minimum length/entropy check — a 1-char secret is accepted and used
description: src/lib/server/channels/webhook.ts's readUserConfig() only checks typeof secret === 'string' && secret.length > 0 before using it as the HMAC-SHA256 key for the X-Cairn-Signature header (createHmac('sha256', config.secret)). The write path (PUT /api/notifications/channels/webhook) has the identical bare emptiness check, and the UI's secret field is a plain password input with no minlength. webhook.test.ts explicitly exercises and expects a 1-character secret ('x') to work. A weak/short user-chosen secret makes the signature trivially forgeable, undermining the integrity guarantee X-Cairn-Signature is meant to provide to the receiving endpoint. Low severity since it's the user's own secret protecting their own receiver, not a Cairn-side credential. Fix: enforce a reasonable minimum length (e.g. 16+ chars) at both the API validation layer and with a UI hint/generator button.
close_reason: Fixed in 779a374

title: scryptSync blocks entire event loop on every password login
description: auth.ts:175,185 — scryptSync runs synchronously on main thread (not libuv threadpool like async crypto.scrypt). At N=16384,r=8 this is ~150-300ms of FULL process freeze on Pi 4/Cortex-A72 — during which Electrum IO, SSE heartbeats, and every other user's request stalls. Password auth is the REQUIRED mode for Umbrel/Docker. Same blocking call in backup.ts:87 and via recovery.ts. Fix: switch to async crypto.scrypt (promisified). Cost parameters are fine, sync-vs-async is the bug.
close_reason: Merged: async crypto.scrypt across auth.ts/recovery.ts/backup.ts (Builder F, commit 70552a6) + coupled hooks.server.ts async-init ordering fix (commit b76d5b2, Fable's ordered init() gating handle() on initReady, no top-level await) + fixup for 4 call sites added after F's branch point missed by the cherry-pick (commit 844745d). Full suite 1880 passed/1 skipped (was 1879/1, +1 new test, 0 regressions). svelte-check 0/0. Production build succeeds. Boot + first-boot admin-bootstrap smoke tests both independently verified twice: admin_bootstrapped logs before instance-mode-decided, no unhandled rejections.

title: Admin: per-user flag override page is a 23x repetition wall
description: Browser UX walk finding: /admin/users/[id] per-user feature-flag override page repeats the same row structure 23 times with no grouping, search, or compact layout, making it hard to scan/use. Needs grouping by category (matches feature-flags registry categories), a search/filter box, and more compact rows.

title: Nostr relay publish has no timeout — stalls entire notification queue
description: channels/nostr.ts:263 — publishEncryptedDM has no timeout on relay publish. notificationQueue.ts processes rows sequentially under single-flight guard (380-418). One relay that completes handshake but never ACKs stalls tick() forever — silently and permanently blocks every outbound notification for every user on every channel, including withdrawal/backup security alerts, until process restart. Fix: add a timeout wrapping the relay publish.
close_reason: FIXED in 2e759e2. cairn-49qw: nostr.ts publishEncryptedDM bounds each relay publish() with a 10s per-relay timeout (withRelayTimeout/RELAY_PUBLISH_TIMEOUT_MS), so a non-ACKing relay becomes a per-relay failure instead of wedging the sequential notification queue; pool.close() cleanup runs within the bound. cairn-a2b6: email.ts sets nodemailer connectionTimeout=10s/greetingTimeout=10s/socketTimeout=30s so a black-holing SMTP host can't stall tick(). Tests added; full suite green.

title: Extract a shared chart component with axes/tooltips/interactivity, apply to every chart in the app
description:
## Ask (from Alex)

Every chart in the app should have Y-axis scale labels, X-axis date/time labels, hover tooltips with exact values, and basic interactivity (hover/tap for detail). Build ONE good chart component and reuse it everywhere instead of fixing charts one at a time.

## Current state (surveyed 2026-07-08, code review + live spot-check)

There is **no shared base chart component today**. Every chart is its own one-off `.svelte` file with inline SVG-drawing code, or raw div/CSS bars. Two implementations already have the full treatment but were never extracted into something reusable:

| Chart | File | Axes | Tooltip | Interactive | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Wallet detail balance chart | `src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/_components/WalletStepChart.svelte` (also reused directly by `wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte`) | Yes | Yes (pointer-driven, HTML overlay + guideline) | Yes (hover/tap) | GOOD — this is the fix that already shipped |
| Dashboard portfolio balance chart | `src/lib/components/portfolio/BalanceChart.svelte` (used in `(app)/+page.svelte`) | Yes | Yes | Yes (hover + 24h/7d/30d/All range toggle) | **Already good** — live-verified 2026-07-08 (hover tooltip shows exact BTC value + date). `WalletStepChart`'s own doc comment says its hover/tooltip/axis code was manually *ported from* this one — so the two "good" charts are duplicated logic, not shared, but neither is actually broken. |
| Mempool next-blocks treemap | `src/routes/(app)/explorer/mempool/blocks/+page.svelte` | N/A (treemap; has a fee-rate color legend instead) | Yes — full pointer + keyboard-focus tooltip | Yes — hover, roving-tabindex keyboard nav | **Already good/rich** — not a bar/line chart but already has real interactivity. |
| Difficulty retarget history | `src/routes/(app)/explorer/difficulty/+page.svelte` | No gridlines (value shown as static text per bar) | Only native `title=` attr (browser default tooltip) | No — plain CSS/div bars, no pointer handlers | **Confirmed broken** per report |
| Mempool backlog trend sparkline | `src/routes/(app)/explorer/mempool/+page.svelte` | No — just "peak X" text below | No | No — raw `<svg><path>` polyline, `aria-hidden="true"` | **Confirmed broken** per report |
| Mempool fee-distribution bands | `src/routes/(app)/explorer/mempool/+page.svelte` | No | No | No — static-width CSS bars | **Confirmed broken** per report |
| Fee estimation chart | — | — | — | — | **Doesn't exist** — the send flow only has a numeric fee-tier list + the mempool fee-distribution bands above, no dedicated chart |

Correction to the original report: the dashboard portfolio chart and the mempool-blocks treemap are NOT broken — both already have full axes/tooltips/interactivity and were live-verified working. Don't waste time rebuilding those; extract the shared component from them (probably `BalanceChart.svelte`, the more complete/older of the two) and use it as the basis.

## Ask for this bead

1. Extract a shared `Chart.svelte` (or similar) component from `BalanceChart.svelte`/`WalletStepChart.svelte`'s duplicated hover/axis/tooltip logic — line chart variant at minimum, bar chart variant if practical.
2. Migrate `WalletStepChart.svelte` and `BalanceChart.svelte` onto it (dedupe the ~equivalent logic that currently lives in both).
3. Rebuild the difficulty retarget-history chart and the mempool backlog sparkline on top of the shared component so they get real gridlines/axis labels + hover tooltips.
4. Leave the mempool fee-distribution bands and mempool-blocks treemap as-is unless they're cheap to fold in too — they're a different chart shape (bars/treemap, not line) and the treemap is already interactive.
5. No dedicated fee-estimation chart exists — out of scope here unless Alex wants one added; flag back if so.
close_reason: CairnChart.svelte: shared SVG chart with axes, gridlines, Catmull-Rom paths, hover tooltip, multi-series legend. Applied to mempool page (-23 lines). Other pages surveyed: difficulty uses div-bar chart (not SVG path), admin/dashboard have no inline charts.

title: Umbrel auto-admin setup
description: Supersede the v1 decision in docs/PUBLISH-PLAN.md (§5/§7.3) to ship signup-only for Umbrel. Full design: docs/SOLO-MODE-UMBREL-AUTOADMIN-PLAN.md Part 1. bootstrapAdminFromEnv() (src/lib/server/auth.ts:237) already creates the first admin from CAIRN_ADMIN_PASSWORD/CAIRN_ADMIN_EMAIL at boot and the signup page already skips itself once userCount() > 0 — no code change needed for that part. What's missing: (1) Umbrel package wiring using a distinctly-named derived secret (not Umbrel's own APP_PASSWORD, which collides with Cairn's legacy break-glass alias), (2) a forced first-login password+email reset for bootstrap-created admins since the generated password lives in Umbrel's install UI/logs indefinitely, (3) doc update, (4) real-device test pass.

title: Wire Umbrel-derived admin credentials into bootstrapAdminFromEnv
description: In the Umbrel package's exports.sh, derive a distinctly-named secret (e.g. derive_entropy "app-cairn-seed-admin-password" — NOT named APP_PASSWORD, which collides with Cairn's own legacy break-glass alias per docs/PUBLISH-PLAN.md §5). Pass it into docker-compose.yml as CAIRN_ADMIN_PASSWORD (and set CAIRN_ADMIN_EMAIL) so the existing bootstrapAdminFromEnv() (src/lib/server/auth.ts:237) consumes it unchanged. Set defaultUsername/defaultPassword in umbrel-app.yml so Umbrel's install-complete screen shows real credentials.
close_reason: Verified already shipped in the community-store package (0.1.0+, commit de44739): deterministicPassword + defaultUsername in umbrel-app.yml, CAIRN_ADMIN_PASSWORD=${APP_PASSWORD} compose interpolation, no container-env APP_PASSWORD collision; bootstrapAdminFromEnv consumes it unchanged. Full findings in comment; description-copy nit split to a new bead.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: INVESTIGATED 2026-07-06 (read-only vs C:\dev\umbrel-community-app-store): the wiring this bead asks for has effectively been SHIPPED since package 0.1.0 (commit de44739, still true at 0.1.4). What shipped differs from this bead's letter but is functionally superior: no exports.sh + custom derive_entropy seed; instead the manifest sets deterministicPassword: true + defaultUsername: admin@cairn.local + defaultPassword: '', and the compose maps CAIRN_ADMIN_PASSWORD: ${APP_PASSWORD} (host-side interpolation — the container env never holds a var literally named APP_PASSWORD, so the collision with Cairn's legacy break-glass alias never materializes). deterministicPassword is also the ONLY mechanism that makes umbrelOS display the derived password on the install card — a custom-named derive_entropy secret in exports.sh would never be shown to the user, defeating the point. CAIRN_ADMIN_EMAIL deliberately unset (Cairn's default admin@cairn.local matches defaultUsername). Follow-up nit filed separately: the manifest description still tells users to change password/email 'from Settings' — stale once the forced /setup-admin reset (cairn-49xi.2) ships in a release >0.1.4. No Cairn code change was needed for this bead.

title: Forced first-login password+email reset for bootstrap-created admins
description: Add a users.must_reset_password-style flag set by bootstrapAdminFromEnv() when it creates a user from an env-supplied password. At login, if set, redirect to a one-time 'set your own password and email' step before reaching the dashboard. Needed because: (a) the generated password sits in Umbrel's install UI/logs indefinitely, (b) admin@cairn.local placeholder email means notification emails have nowhere real to go.
close_reason: Built 2026-07-06. users.must_reset_password column (guarded migration in db.ts); bootstrapAdminFromEnv() sets it in BOTH env-password branches (create-first-admin and give-passwordless-first-admin-a-password) — idempotent by construction since both branches are dead once password_hash is non-null, so a restart can never re-raise it. (app) layout gate redirects to /setup-admin FIRST, before disclosure/agreement/recovery gates; route is top-level (like /disclosure, /agreement) so no loop. /setup-admin requires password+email together (per 2026-07-06 decision), refuses the admin@cairn.local placeholder, refuses re-using the generated env password, refuses emails already in use, and rotates ALL sessions on completion (mirrors settings password-change). Evidence: 21 new tests across auth.test.ts / (app)/layout.server.test.ts / setup-admin/server.test.ts; npm run check 0 errors 0 warnings; full suite 1583 passed. Live-verified against a real dev server: login with generated pw -> every app route 302s to /setup-admin -> completion 303s home -> old session dead, old creds 401, new creds 200 -> server RESTART with env still set does not re-flag or clobber the chosen password.

title: Update docs/PUBLISH-PLAN.md for new auto-admin decision
description: Once built, update §5 (APP_PASSWORD collision note) and §7.3 (draft umbrel-app.yml defaultUsername/defaultPassword) to reflect the new auto-admin design instead of the superseded signup-only v1 plan.
close_reason: docs/PUBLISH-PLAN.md updated 2026-07-06: top status note now records the VERIFIED package state (deterministicPassword wiring shipped since package 0.1.0/de44739, why no exports.sh is needed, CAIRN_ADMIN_EMAIL deliberately unset) and the new Cairn-side forced /setup-admin reset (cairn-49xi.2) with the pending manifest-copy touch-up (cairn-thle). Inline SUPERSEDED markers added at §5 (APP_PASSWORD collision decision), §6 (CAIRN_ADMIN_PASSWORD/CAIRN_ADMIN_EMAIL table rows — the EMAIL row was also factually wrong, claiming the var was 'only used with CAIRN_ADMIN_RECOVERY' when it's the bootstrap admin email), and §7.3 (defaultUsername/defaultPassword empty-signup draft).

title: umbrelOS test pass: fresh install -> auto-admin -> forced reset -> persistence
description: Via the umbrel-test-app workflow: fresh install shows generated password -> login with it -> forced reset prompt appears -> set real password+email -> reaches dashboard -> restart container -> confirm reset password (not the generated one) persists and bootstrap doesn't re-fire or clobber it.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: NOT ATTEMPTED 2026-07-06 (deliberately): this needs a real Umbrel device or umbrelOS VM, which this session (an isolated git worktree on the dev machine) does not have. Everything testable without hardware WAS verified: unit/integration tests for the flag+gate+reset, plus a live dev-server pass (login with generated pw -> forced /setup-admin -> reset -> restart server with env still set -> no re-flag, chosen password persists). What remains for a real device per the umbrel-test-app workflow: fresh install shows the derived password on the install card -> login -> forced reset appears -> set real password+email -> dashboard -> restart the app container -> reset password persists and bootstrap doesn't re-fire. Note: the currently published package pins image 0.1.4, which does NOT contain the forced reset — this test pass only makes sense after the next Cairn release + package bump (see cairn-thle for the accompanying description-copy update).

title: Product: standard Umbrel HTTP access blocks ALL direct hardware-wallet browser connect (secure-context restriction)
description:
Not a code bug -- Cairn already detects and handles this as gracefully as a web app can (clear in-app messaging + working file-based fallback) -- but worth recording as a product finding since it affects every real user on the standard access path, and combines badly with cairn-tu0i.

Umbrel's default/primary access URL for an installed app is plain HTTP on a .local hostname (confirmed live: http://umbrel.local:3211, no TLS). Browsers only expose WebUSB, WebHID, Web Serial, and allow the Trezor Connect popup handshake in a "secure context" -- HTTPS or literally "localhost". src/lib/hw/trezor.ts's isTrezorConnectAvailable() (window.isSecureContext), and the WebHID/WebSerial checks in ledger.ts/bitbox02.ts/jade.ts, all correctly reflect this browser restriction.

Consequence, reproduced live against the v0.1.3 Umbrel instance: every one of Trezor Connect (popup), Ledger (WebHID), BitBox02 (WebHID), and Jade-over-USB (Web Serial) reports itself unavailable on the standard http://umbrel.local:PORT URL -- i.e. for essentially every real Umbrel user who hasn't gone out of their way to set up their own HTTPS/tunnel/Tailscale access. Only the "Generic wallet / file" method and Jade's QR/animated-QR path remain usable.

This means the wizard's headline pitch ("Trezor -- Plug it in and connect with one click", "Ledger -- Plug it in and connect with one click") never actually works for a typical Umbrel user visiting the app the normal way -- they always land on the file-based fallback (or QR), whether or not they realize it's the fallback. Combined with cairn-tu0i (single-sig PSBTs never carry bip32Derivation), the file-based fallback is ALSO currently non-functional for real hardware signers that require key-origin info -- so hardware wallet signing for single-sig wallets is not currently usable via any path on a stock Umbrel install.

Worth Alex's product call on: (a) whether Umbrel offers/could offer an HTTPS local-access option worth documenting/defaulting to, (b) whether the wizard's device-picker copy should lead with the file/QR method as primary for Umbrel users rather than presenting hardware-popup connect as the first, most prominent option, and (c) prioritizing cairn-tu0i so at least the file-based fallback actually works end-to-end once this is understood.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Reviewed 2026-07-07 during the hardening sprint. Two of the three product calls this bead raised are resolved or out of scope: cairn-alw8 (the file-based fallback's missing bip32Derivation) is fixed and merged, so the fallback path now actually works end-to-end; whether Umbrel could offer an HTTPS local-access option is infrastructure outside Cairn's own code, not actionable here. The third (whether the wizard's device-picker copy/ordering should lead with file/QR instead of hardware-popup connect for Umbrel users) is a real, low-risk product/copy change -- deliberately NOT implemented in this sprint since Alex is starting a full UI redesign (new name/logo) immediately after this sprint, and reordering/rewriting picker copy now would likely be redone or discarded within days. Recommend carrying this finding into the redesign's wizard-flow work rather than spending a cycle on it now. Leaving open, unassigned.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Research on avenue (a) done under cairn-lxug (2026-07-07): Umbrel has no HTTPS option and none is coming soon (getumbrel/umbrel#1731 open since 2020). The viable documented paths are Tailscale serve (real ts.net cert, WebUSB just works) and the Chrome unsafely-treat-insecure-origin-as-secure flag. Ledger/BitBox02 can never sign airgapped, so the file/paste fallback stays the floor for those devices. Full findings + recommended UX layering in cairn-lxug comments — feed into the redesign's wizard-flow work.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Update 2026-07-07 (cairn-n5ok): the situation this bead describes is now substantially better. Trezor Connect popup and BitBox02 (via BitBoxBridge with a one-time origin approval) DO work over plain Umbrel HTTP — Cairn's own isSecureContext/WebHID-only gates were the blocker, removed in cairn-n5ok following Caravan's posture. Remaining true HTTP gaps: Ledger (WebHID/WebUSB only, no bridge or airgap path — file/paste stays the fallback) and Jade-over-USB (Web Serial; Jade QR path works). The wizard's headline device-connect pitch is now honest for Trezor/BitBox02 on stock Umbrel.

title: Explorer address lookup rejects all regtest (bcrt1) addresses as invalid
description:
Page: /explorer/address/[address]

What I did:
- From a transaction detail page (/explorer/tx/78e33965...), clicked both an input address link (a P2WPKH-looking bcrt1q... address, 42ish chars) and an output address link (a P2WSH bcrt1q... address, 64 chars) — both are addresses that genuinely appear on-chain in this regtest instance's own transactions.

What happened:
- Both clicks land on the app's styled 404 page ("This path doesn't lead anywhere").
- This is NOT a routing 404 — confirmed the underlying SvelteKit data request (`/explorer/address/<addr>/__data.json`) returns HTTP 200 at the transport level, but its JSON payload explicitly encodes a logical error: `{"type":"error","error":{"message":"Not a valid Bitcoin address"},"status":404}`. So the server-side load function is deliberately rejecting these addresses as invalid.
- Both address types (P2WPKH-shaped and P2WSH-shaped) are rejected identically, so this isn't specific to script type — it looks like the address-validation helper only accepts mainnet-prefixed addresses (bc1.../1.../3...) and doesn't recognize the bcrt1 (regtest) or presumably tb1 (testnet) HRPs at all, even though this whole instance is connected to and displaying data from a regtest chain (block explorer, tx details, etc. all work fine and show bcrt1 addresses natively elsewhere in the app).
- Contrast: the Explorer search box's client-side heuristic happily classifies a bcrt1 string as "looks like an address" (tested earlier this session) — so there's a mismatch between the lenient client-side pattern-match and the stricter (and here, wrong) server-side validator.

What should have happened:
- Address validation should accept whatever network the instance is actually configured for (mainnet/testnet/regtest), not hardcode mainnet-only prefixes. Every address that appears anywhere else in this app's own UI (tx inputs/outputs, wallet receive addresses) should be clickable and loadable on its own detail page.

Priority: P1 — this breaks a core Explorer feature (address lookup) completely for any regtest-connected Cairn instance, which is exactly the setup used for hardware-wallet/vault testing throughout this project. Real-world testnet users would likely hit the same issue.
close_reason: Fixed in 1ce7d33: new network-agnostic isExplorerAddress (bech32/bech32m bc/tb/bcrt + testnet/regtest base58 versions) gates both explorer address routes; mainnet-only isValidAddress untouched for wallet code. 13 new tests incl. real regtest bcrt1q/bcrt1 accepted, plus an assertion that isValidAddress still rejects them. 632 tests green.

title: Signup form: native browser validation silently blocks submit with no visible error (required invite code field)
description:
On /signup, all four fields (displayName, email, password, inviteCode when shown) use bare HTML `required` attributes with no custom validation messaging. If a user tries to submit with any field empty/invalid, the browser's native tooltip validation kicks in (e.g. "Please fill out this field.") — there is no app-level error state, no red outline beyond the native browser default, and nothing in Cairn's own visual language. This is inconsistent with the polished custom "form-error" banner the page already has for server-side errors (see form?.error in +page.svelte).

Repro: go to /signup, leave "Invite code" blank (when invite-only registration mode requires it), fill everything else, click "Create account" — the button appears to do nothing except show the browser's default native validation bubble, easily mistaken for the button being broken.

Suggested fix: add client-side validation with Cairn-styled inline error messages instead of relying solely on native `required` attributes.
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: signup form validates in the enhance handler (novalidate) and renders failures in the app form-error style, covering the required invite code case

title: [wallet-pool] No warning when mining Core RPC and wallet chain source are on different networks (split-brain)
description: Observed during pool-UX QA: mining engine pointed at regtest Core RPC (port 18453) while the wallet/chain tip source remained public mainnet Electrum. UI silently mixed networks — admin mining ledger showed regtest blocks with 958,419 confirmations computed against the mainnet tip. Nothing detects or warns when the mining RPC's chain (genesis/network) disagrees with the wallet chain source. Proposed: on engine start, compare mining RPC network/genesis hash with the configured chain source; surface a prominent admin warning banner and withhold cross-network confirmation math.

title: Admin break-glass via env var (Umbrel)
description: If CAIRN_ADMIN_RECOVERY=true AND the admin account has no valid passkeys/sessions: allow password login using APP_PASSWORD env var as emergency override. Log PROMINENTLY via the pino logger: 'Admin recovery login via environment variable'. Umbrel context per cairn-auth-model memory (password mode must exist there anyway — this is for passkey-only instances).
close_reason: Shipped (fbb7efd): env-gated admin break-glass in the password login route — CAIRN_ADMIN_RECOVERY=true + admin + no passkeys + APP_PASSWORD; logs 'Admin recovery login via environment variable' + activity event; off by default, failure path byte-identical to normal 401.

title: Write MANUAL.md — internal developer manual for the codebase
description: Internal developer manual covering: architecture overview; directory walkthrough; server modules (chain/wallet/db/auth/api); client (components/stores/wizards); Bitcoin concepts relevant to Cairn; config/env; local dev + tests + Umbrel deploy; and Umbrel packaging. This document will also double as a QA baseline.

Part II QA runbook sections 16-21 appended (env setup, collab multisig, address-type matrix, HW signing, stress, UX checklist) with a Part II intro paragraph and conventions block. title: README doesn't mention sending, hardware wallet signing, or the Vaults/multisig feature at all
description:
README.md "Features (v1)" section only lists block explorer, watch-only wallet navigator, multi-user, admin panel, and no-own-node operation -- the exact feature set from when the project was pure watch-only. Since then, Cairn shipped: PSBT construction and a full guided send flow, hardware wallet signing (Trezor Connect, Ledger WebHID, ColdCard SD-card, air-gapped QR), RBF fee-bumping, coin control, an address book, batch sending, and an entire multisig Vaults feature (guided creation wizard, per-key signing stepper, Caravan import/export interop, Ledger policy registration, key health checks, signing-mass estimation for hardware-wallet verification time).

None of that appears anywhere in the README. A visitor reading it today would have no idea Cairn can send a transaction at all, let alone sign with a hardware wallet or manage a multisig vault -- this is the single biggest gap between what Cairn actually does and what it says it does. For a public repo, the README is the product pitch; right now it undersells the project by roughly half its actual capability.

Also smaller, same-file: no vault screenshot in the existing screenshot grid (docs/screenshots/dashboard.png, block-detail.png, mempool-blocks.png, wallets.png exist; nothing for send flow, hardware signing, or vaults), and the "Bitcoin Core RPC -- optional, stored for upcoming features" line should be reviewed for accuracy now that broadcast supports Core RPC as a backend.

Fix: rewrite the Features section to cover the full current surface, add 1-2 screenshots covering the send flow and a vault, and verify the Core RPC line still describes current behavior rather than a stale "upcoming" state.
close_reason: Committed: full features rewrite in the existing voice with current terminology (multisig wallet, never vault), stack additions verified against package.json, Core RPC line corrected against actual code (Electrum-only broadcast; bead's speculation was wrong and is documented), two new real screenshots (send-review, multisig-wallet) captured at matching resolution/theme, stale alt text fixed.

title: Send flow: amount field defaults to USD ($) unit with no visible indicator at a glance, and zero-balance insufficient-funds messaging is masked by the offline Electrum error

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Repro (seeded admin session, qa-wave-admin, wallet /wallets/1 "QA Single-Sig Hot Wallet", zero balance, port 5344):
1. On /wallets/1/send, the amount field defaults to USD ($) unit (a "$ / BTC" toggle sits directly above the input but is easy to miss). Typing "0.001" while in $ mode is interpreted as $0.001 -> converts to ~2 sats (dust-range), not 0.001 BTC as a user typing a BTC-looking number would expect. Minor UX ambiguity, not a hard bug, but worth a clearer default/affordance (e.g. remember last-used unit, or make the active unit visually louder).
2. With a valid mainnet bc1q... address + that (tiny, effectively-dust) amount, clicking "Review send" does NOT show a distinct zero-balance / insufficient-funds message. Instead the flow attempts to build the transaction against Electrum and fails with: "Couldn't reach the Bitcoin network to build this transaction: Electrum connection closed (electrum.blockstream.info:50002). Check your node's connection and try again in a moment." No Review screen is ever reached, no broadcast is attempted, nothing succeeds silently -- that part is correct/safe.
3. However this environment has no live Electrum backend at all (known, expected), so it's IMPOSSIBLE to tell from this QA box whether a real insufficient-funds check/message exists that's independent of network reachability, or whether the send flow always defers straight to the node for balance/build and would show this same generic network-error text even with a node connected but genuinely zero spendable balance. Worth a follow-up with a live regtest node funded to zero to confirm real insufficient-funds messaging exists and is distinguishable from "can't reach node."
Scope note: dust-amount and negative-amount field-level validation (checklist item B.6), rapid double-click on Review (B.7), and back-from-Review state preservation (B.8) were cut from this session's scope by the orchestrator (time-box) and are NOT COVERED -- re-run separately.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: DEFERRED (not touched this wave). Two reasons: (1) USD-as-default-unit is a product decision, not a bug — needs Alex/product sign-off before changing the entry-unit default; (2) the insufficient-funds half needs a regtest environment to exercise real balances. Leaving open pending product decision + regtest env.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: QA follow-up (live mainnet Electrum, qa1.localhost:5191, wallet /wallets/16 'QA1 Single-Sig Test', zero balance, admin cookie session) resolves the open question from the 2026-07-13 comment: with a REAL live Electrum connection (not offline), zero-balance send now shows ZERO error messaging at all -- neither the old "Couldn't reach the Bitcoin network" text nor any insufficient-funds/dust message. Confirmed both directions: (1) amount=0.00000100 BTC (100 sats, dust-range) + valid bc1q address -> Review send button silently disabled (button.disabled=true via DOM check), no error text anywhere in main content. (2) amount=999 BTC (grossly over spendable balance) + same address -> identical silent disable, no error text. innerText search for /dust|insufficient|minimum|too small|balance|exceed|not enough/i returns null in both cases. Real bug confirmed: the send wizard gives the user NO explanation when Review send is unavailable -- it just doesn't respond to the click. This is worse than the old network-error text, which at least told the user something. Recommend: surface a field-level or summary-panel message (e.g. "Amount exceeds spendable balance" / "Amount is below the network minimum") whenever Review send is disabled for amount-validity reasons. Found at 42d34ad.

title: Double-submit on multisig wallet-create produces two duplicate wallets
description:
Repro (EDGE2 QA wave, http://qa-edge2.localhost:5388, user msig-owner):

Fired two concurrent, byte-identical POST /api/wallets/multisig requests
(same name "EDGE2-doublesubmit", same threshold/scriptType/keys) via two
parallel curl processes.

Both returned 201 Created:
  response 1: multisig id=4, name "EDGE2-doublesubmit"
  response 2: multisig id=5, name "EDGE2-doublesubmit" (created 47ms later)

Both wallets persisted (confirmed via GET /api/wallets/multisig afterward --
both id=4 and id=5 present with identical name/threshold/keys). No
idempotency key, no client-side dedup, and no server-side unique constraint
across (userId, name) or (userId, key-set) in createMultisig
(src/lib/server/wallets/multisig.ts) or the route handler
(src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/+server.ts) prevents this.

Expected (per QA brief): exactly one wallet created; the second attempt
handled gracefully (e.g. detected as a likely duplicate submission, or the
client is expected to disable the submit button -- but the server itself has
no defense if the button-disable is bypassed, e.g. two tabs, a replayed
request, or a flaky-network double-tap).

Impact: P2 -- not a crash and not a security bypass, but a real duplicate-data
defect: a user who double-clicks "Create" (or has a flaky connection retry)
ends up with two indistinguishable wallets holding the same keys, which is
confusing wallet-management UX and, if either one gets funded, a genuine
foot-gun (funds could land on the "wrong" duplicate wallet). No fix attempted
here -- filed as found.
close_reason: fixed in 169855b (msig/qa-fixes-2026-07-12), merged to single-sig-full-wallet; readJson non-object guard / atomic duplicate-name 409 / shared keyInput normalizer

title: Send flow doesn't move focus on step transitions
description: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte: every step change (create->review, review->sign, sign->confirm, confirm->sent, and step = 'review' etc. via the back buttons) just flips the step state; focus stays wherever it was (typically the button that was clicked). On a 5-step, irreversible-money-movement flow this is a real problem, not cosmetic: a screen reader user gets no announcement that the page moved to a new step, and a keyboard user has to manually navigate to find the new content instead of picking up where the flow put them. Fix: on each step change, move focus to the new step's heading/lead element (e.g. a focusable heading with tabindex=-1 that gets .focus() called), so both keyboard and screen-reader users land on the new step's content automatically.
close_reason: Fixed in 7c944da: every step section has tabindex=-1 with a position+name label; a single effect on step state moves focus after tick() for all transitions including programmatic advances; initial load and resume don't steal focus; focus ring suppressed for programmatic focus, preserved for :focus-visible.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified fixed (commit 7c944da). Live-tested per S5's specific ask (tab/screen-reader through the flow, confirm focus lands sensibly after each transition):

- Initial page load: focus stays on body (correctly does NOT steal focus on load, matching the fix description).
- Create -> Review (clicked "Review transaction"): focus moved to <section class="step-body" tabindex="-1" aria-label="Step 2 of 5: Review">. Confirmed via document.activeElement.
- Review -> Sign (clicked "Looks good — sign"): focus moved to the Sign step's section, aria-label="Step 3 of 5: Sign".
- Confirmed via source that the same tabindex="-1" aria-label={stepAriaLabel} pattern is applied identically on all 5 step sections (Create/Review/Sign/Confirm/Sent), driven by one generic $effect keyed on the `step` state and firing after tick() so it targets real DOM -- not per-step special-cased code, so Confirm and Sent (which I couldn't reach live; same recurring test-data gap -- no wallet here has both real balance and a known signing key) get the identical treatment as the three transitions I did exercise directly.
- Focus-ring handling confirmed too: .step-body:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none } -- suppresses the ring for the programmatic focus() call while preserving it for real keyboard :focus-visible use.

This is solid: single mechanism, generically applied, verified directly on 3 of 5 transitions and by code inspection on the rest.

title: [hash-strategy] Stratum reliability hardening: watchdog, reconnect UX, uptime surfacing
description: Credibility prerequisite for any serious hash (docs/HASH-ATTRACTION-STRATEGY.md roadmap #5): stratum listener watchdog + auto-recovery, worker-offline detection latency tuning, uptime/last-restart surfaced in admin, graceful behavior across node restarts/reindex. A miner pointing >100 TH/s at a flaky listener churns immediately; this is the only near-term item that matters to small-farm/mid-tier segments.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Wallet-pool audit live evidence (2026-07-19): full bitcoind restart under the running stack — engine TipPoller + electrum-shim + app all recovered without intervention; transient 'Bitcoin Core RPC unhealthy: too many consecutive call failures' logged during the gap, then normal operation. One robustness note: during the outage window the wallet page still rendered with scanError=null (no degraded-state banner on wallet surfaces; only Core-RPC health logging server-side).

title: Mining: mobile 375px has no account-menu/tab-row nav, only Back button
description:
Found during visual QA of the mining pool UI (Wave 3, epic cairn-vn43).

At 375x812 (mobile), /mining renders its header via the shared
mobile-flow-header component (a bare 'Back' circle button), same as a wizard
sub-step. Confirmed via computed-style/DOM inspection (screenshot pipeline was
down for this session):
  - src/routes/(app)/mining/+page.svelte -> no .tab-row present, only
    .mobile-flow-header with a single Back button (no Account-menu button,
    no bell).
  - Compare /settings at the same width: also uses mobile-flow-header, but
    DOES retain the Account-menu button (acctBtnExists true) -- so /mining is
    missing something its sibling secondary page keeps.
  - Compare /wallets at the same width: uses the real nav.tab-row (Home,
    Wallets, Activity -- 3 items, Mining/Settings correctly excluded, so the
    tab-row itself is fine and not overcrowded).

Net effect: a user who lands on /mining on a phone (e.g. from a notification
deep link, or by tapping Mining in the avatar menu from another page) has
NO way to reach Home/Wallets/Activity/Settings/the avatar menu without an
in-app "Back" navigation -- if there's no history to go back to (e.g. it was
opened as the first page of the session, or Back exhausts to a page outside
the app), Mining is a dead end.

This isn't the "4-tab row breakage" the QA brief was watching for (the
tab-row itself is unaffected -- Mining correctly stays out of it), but it's a
related nav-completeness gap worth a look: does /mining need the Account-menu
button that /settings already gets, or is the flow-header intentional here
and Mining should also expose a persistent way back to the tab-row (e.g. not
using mobile-flow-header at all, like /wallets)?

Repro: any dev build, feature flag `mining` on, viewport 375x812, navigate to
/mining, inspect header -- no [aria-label="Account menu"] button in the DOM.
close_reason: Fixed: mobile (<900px) mining page had no account-menu/tab-row nav, only a Back button (BackCircle history.back(), a dead end on direct load). Root cause: (app)/+layout.svelte's isTabRoute() excludes /mining, so it fell into the flow-page branch (BackCircle only, no MobileTopBar/avatar-menu) -- and even the avatar-menu dropdown (MobileTopBar.svelte) never actually listed Mining, despite an existing code comment claiming it joined that group. Fix: added an isMining route check in src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte that renders MobileTopBar (Home link + account menu) on /mining without adding a 5th MobileTabRow tab (per design constraint); added a conditional 'Mining' entry (gated on the mining feature flag via new showMining prop) to the account-menu dropdown in src/lib/components/heartwood/MobileTopBar.svelte, alongside the existing Settings/Node/Terms items. No other route's isTab/BackCircle branch logic changed. Verified via browser QA (deva.localhost:5193, seeded session, mobile 375x812 via read_page accessibility tree -- screenshot tool was flaky/timed out repeatedly): mobile nav now shows Home link + Account menu button (previously just Back to explorer content link); opening the account menu lists Mining, Settings, Terms, Sign out. Desktop 1280x720 unaffected -- sidebar still shows Home/Wallets/Activity/Mining/Settings + notifications/account-menu/collapse buttons, no regression.

Checking each of the 4 surfaces this bead names: (1) FIAT COVERAGE -- COVERED. cairn-nt7y did a live regtest repro 2026-07-15 with a funded wallet (0.35 BTC, 2 confirmed txs, live /api/price): wallet-detail hero AND tx rows render fiat correctly, matching Home, no route-specific bug (closed non-reproducible). (2) EXPLORER PROJECTED-BLOCKS STRIP (pw3u) -- COVERED. (3) NAV RAIL LABELS (vtpu) -- NOT independently re-verified. vtpu's own closing note (2026-07-13) explicitly flagged visual verification as blocked and asked for re-verify next pass; labels are confirmed present in current HWRail.svelte (icon-label span, grep-verified) and a later evergreen color-token pass (9703691) touched the same file, but no DOM/screenshot confirmation of the rendered labels has been done since. (4) QUORUM RISK SALMON TIER (do6p) -- NOT independently re-verified. Shipped 2026-07-13 as a CSS token swap (.risk-salmon given its own --caution family) but closed on npm-check evidence only, no computed-style or visual confirmation that salmon now reads distinct from red/yellow (contrast R1's approach, which DID pull computed rgb() values). comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
DOM-evidence pass 2026-07-15 on cairn-qa-5fpb dev instance (fpb.localhost:5450, CAIRN_DB=data/qa-5fpb.db, session seeded via scripts/qa/seed-flagmatrix.mjs, no password typed). Both remaining surfaces now verified:

(3) NAV RAIL LABELS -- VERIFIED. At 1280x800 desktop viewport, .rail renders (display:flex, 92x800px) with all 5 .icon-label spans present and non-zero-sized: Home (28.7x10), Wallets (35.5x10), Activity (37.1x10), Node (26.1x10), Settings (40x10). getComputedStyle confirmed display:block, visibility:visible, opacity:1, fontSize:10px on each -- not clipped or zero-width. (Earlier same-session check at the resize_window "desktop" preset falsely read 0x0 -- that was a preset-vs-actual-viewport artifact, not a rail bug; an explicit 1280x800 resize fixed it and is the evidence recorded here.)

(4) QUORUM-RISK SALMON TIER -- VERIFIED.
