title: explorer: latest-blocks list renders NaN/0 from imperfect snapshots — harden render guards
description:
Root cause (verified): render guards in the explorer latest-blocks list use strict '=== null' checks, which let 'undefined' (missing snapshot keys on imperfect/synthetic chain_snapshot rows) and false-0 values (txCount:0, size:0, weight:0, fullness:0) render as NaN or a bogus zero instead of the intended '-' unknown state. A real block always has >=1 tx and >0 bytes, so 0 in these fields means 'unknown', not 'zero'.

Fix plan:
- src/routes/(app)/explorer/+page.svelte movedBtc() (~L209): harden null check to catch non-finite/<=0 totalOut.
- same file block-row txCount label (~L676) and size label (~L679): switch from '=== null' ternaries to truthiness checks so 0/undefined both render em-dash.
- src/lib/components/heartwood/RingBar.svelte (~L25,31): fullness guard hardened against non-finite/undefined/<=0 values, not just strict null.
- Fix QA seed script(s) writing synthetic chain_snapshot rows with txCount:0/size:0/weight:0 and missing fullness/total_out keys -- seeded unknowns must be explicit null and include all BlockSummary keys.
- Add/extend a test covering the render-guard hardening.
close_reason: Root cause confirmed and fixed: explorer render guards used strict '=== null', letting 'undefined' (missing keys on imperfect/synthetic snapshots) and false-0 (txCount:0/size:0/weight:0/fullness:0) render as NaN/bogus-0 instead of the honest '-' unknown state. Fixed: movedBtc logic extracted to $lib/format.ts formatMovedBtc() with a hardened guard (== null || !Number.isFinite || <=0); explorer +page.svelte block-row txCount/size labels switched from '=== null' ternaries to truthiness checks; RingBar.svelte fullness guard extracted to ringBarGuard.ts (ringBarVisible/ringBarPct), hardened the same way. Tests added: src/lib/format.test.ts formatMovedBtc describe block, src/lib/components/heartwood/ringBarGuard.test.ts. QA seed-script check: grepped all scripts/qa/*.mjs (and git history across every branch) for chain_snapshot INSERT/seeding -- none exists; only seed-explorer-wallet.mjs touches explorer QA data and it seeds a wallet row, not chain_snapshot. The synthetic chain_snapshot seeding referenced in prior session memory was not found in any tracked script, so no seed-script fix was needed/possible here.

title: explorer: block detail lacks 'Yours' pip on owned transactions
description:
Block detail's paginated tx-row list (src/routes/(app)/explorer/block/[id]/+page.svelte, .tx-row loop) has no per-row indicator of which rows are the viewer's own transactions, unlike the explorer index which pips whole blocks via ownedBlockHeights(). The page already has a block-level 'Yours in this ring' callout section (yours/ownedTxsInBlock), but that callout only covers txs on the CURRENT page's block-height lookup, not a per-row badge on the tx list itself, and only shows CONFIRMED txs already known via wallet snapshot -- it doesn't badge which specific rows in the rendered tx table are the viewer's.

Plan (re-implemented against canonical signatures, referencing explorer/heartwood-wave2 commit b6f0922's ownedTxids() concept but not cherry-picked):
- Add ownedTxids(userId, txids): Set<string> to src/routes/(app)/explorer/ownership.server.ts, reusing the existing memoized txid index (zero extra chain calls).
- Wire it into block/[id]/+page.server.ts's loadBlockData: compute the owned set over the current page's fetched txs.
- Render a sage 'Yours' pip on matching .tx-row entries in +page.svelte, consistent with the index's pip visual language.
close_reason: Added ownedTxids(userId, txids): Set<string> to src/routes/(app)/explorer/ownership.server.ts, reusing the existing memoized txid index (zero extra chain calls, same viewer-scoped privacy boundary as ownedBlockHeights/ownedTxsInBlock). Wired into block/[id]/+page.server.ts's loadBlockData (new ownedTxids field on BlockPageData, computed over the current page's fetched txs). Rendered a sage 'Yours' pip on matching .tx-row entries in +page.svelte, consistent with the index's block-level pip visual language. Test added: src/routes/(app)/explorer/ownership.ownedTxids.test.ts (anonymous viewer, empty list, cross-user isolation, multi-wallet matching).

title: explorer: consider rounding long-precision BTC figures on mempool waiting-fees and block-detail fees/reward (currently 8dp)
description:
New scope beyond explorer/heartwood-wave2 commit b6f0922 (P1-d), which only rounded the explorer INDEX's '~N BTC moved' figure (formatBtcApprox there / formatMovedBtc here, cairn-6efi.11). Canonical's index moved-BTC figure already rounds appropriately (whole BTC + thousands separators >=100 BTC, 1dp 1-100 BTC, 3dp under 1 BTC -- see $lib/format.ts formatMovedBtc).

Still full 8-decimal-precision (via formatBtc's default trim-to-min-2dp, which still shows up to 8dp for small/odd amounts) in:
- mempool page fee-histogram / waiting-fees figures
- block-detail hero fees/reward stat-line (block.totalFees, block.reward) and value-flow bar

Worth a design pass on whether these should adopt the same compact-rounding treatment as the index figure, or whether full precision is intentional there (fees/reward are often small, exact amounts users may want to copy/verify). Not fixing blind -- scoping only.

title: Multisig wizard drops bitbox02/jade deviceType — keys stored with null device routing

The wizard's connect flow sends deviceType 'bitbox02'/'jade' for those live reads, the key action filters them to null, so the stored multisig key loses its device routing — the signing stepper falls back to generic file signing instead of the device's own connect flow. Likely an oversight from when the BitBox02/Jade drivers landed. Fix is one line (add both to the set) but touches signing-stepper routing behavior, so left out of the fdlf.4 change deliberately — verify the multisig signing stepper handles deviceType 'bitbox02'/'jade' before enabling.

title: Amount.svelte sats-first default flip (shared component)
description:
DESIGN-MANIFESTO.md §3 money-typesetting rule 3 (MUST): BTC/sats is the hero, fiat is a muted secondary line, BY DEFAULT. src/lib/components/Amount.svelte currently does the opposite whenever a price is available (fiatText != null -> fiat renders as .line.primary/hero, BTC demoted to .line.secondary), src/lib/components/Amount.svelte:65-70. This is fed by the shared auto-fetching $btcUsd store (src/lib/price.ts), which starts polling /api/price the moment any Amount subscribes -- with NO opt-in gate -- so every one of the 13 call sites (wallets/[id]/send, wallets/[id], wallets/multisig/stateless, activity, AllocationBar, BalanceChart, wallets/multisig/[id], RecentActivity, MiningRewards, explorer/address/[address], wallets, SendReviewCard) goes fiat-primary as soon as the price loads, with no per-surface way to opt out.

Home's own hero (src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte) already complies by construction: its privacy-gated `heroPrice` (gatedFiatPrice, OFF by default) is passed explicitly to override Amount's default price subscription, so Home is sats-first by default today (cairn-sdx5.3 additionally wired this same gate through to Home's own wallet-list/picker Amount rows, which had been silently bypassing it via the shared store). But every OTHER surface listed above still defaults to fiat-primary once the price loads, in direct contradiction of the MUST rule.

Fixing this at the root means changing Amount.svelte's own default (make BTC-primary the default display order when no explicit `price` is passed, or flip $btcUsd itself to be gated by a shared Settings-level "fiat primary" preference default-OFF) and touching/retesting all 13 call sites -- a genuine cross-cutting behavioral refactor spanning Send, wallet-detail, Activity, Explorer, and Mining surfaces that are each owned by their own gt05/sdx5 visual-pass beads (sdx5.4 wallet detail, sdx5.5 send flow). Out of scope for a Home/Nav-only visual pass (sdx5.3/sdx5.6) -- flagged per the manifesto's own default-flip carve-out (§0) rather than silently reworked inside an unrelated bead.

Suggested approach: add a module-level default (e.g. an exported `fiatPrimaryDefault` store seeded from a Settings->Display toggle, default off) that Amount.svelte consults when the caller does not pass an explicit `price`, OR simply swap the primary/secondary branch in Amount.svelte's template (lines ~65-70) so BTC is always primary unless the caller explicitly requests fiat-primary via a new prop -- then update whichever of the 13 call sites need an explicit override, plus tests.
close_reason:
Fixed at the root per the suggested approach: Amount.svelte's own default,
not per-call-site overrides. DESIGN-MANIFESTO.md §3 MUST rule now holds
app-wide.

Mechanism:
- New pure decision function isFiatPrimary(fiatPrimaryPref, fiatText) in
  src/lib/format.ts: returns false (BTC/sats primary) unless the user has
  explicitly opted into fiat-primary AND a fiat value actually rendered.
- New persisted preference store fiatPrimaryPref + setFiatPrimaryPref() in
  src/lib/price.ts, localStorage key cairn.fiatPrimary, default OFF (same
  pattern as the existing cairn.fiat show/hide toggle).
- src/lib/components/Amount.svelte now derives fiatPrimary from
  isFiatPrimary($fiatPrimaryPref, fiatText) and swaps which value (BTC vs
  fiat) fills the existing .line.primary / .line.secondary slots -- no CSS
  changes needed, same classes either way. All 13 call sites from the audit
  inherit the fix automatically since they all render through Amount.svelte.
- Added a minimal "Primary display: BTC/sats | Fiat" toggle to Settings ->
  Display (src/routes/(app)/settings/+page.svelte), gated behind the
  existing "Fiat display" toggle, wired to the new store.
- Privacy eye-toggle (Home's gatedFiatPrice/heroPrice) untouched -- Amount's
  price prop / effectivePrice logic is unchanged, so the sdx5.3 fix holds.

Tests: added 3 cases to src/lib/format.test.ts under isFiatPrimary --
default BTC-primary even with a loaded fiat value, explicit fiat-primary
preference still wins, and never-fiat-primary when no price loaded
regardless of preference.

Verification: npm test -- 3669 passed, 1 pre-existing skip, 0 failures.
npm run check -- 0 errors, 1981 files. Browser runtime check was blocked
by the known localhost cookie-jar collision hazard (concurrent QA server
held a stale cookie); relied on unit tests + code review instead.

Note: settings/+page.svelte was being concurrently edited by a sibling
agent (light-mode/theme work, cairn-sdx5.7) -- staged only this bead's

title: seed-qa2.mjs DELETE-before-insert wipes parallel agents' sessions
description:
REPRO: scripts/qa/seed-qa2.mjs (in the cairn-qa2 QA-instance worktree, C:\dev\cairn-qa2\scripts\qa\seed-qa2.mjs) seeded a single shared qa2-admin user and, before inserting a session, ran `DELETE FROM sessions WHERE user_id = ?` for that user. When two parallel QA agents each re-ran the script against the same shared DB (C:\dev\cairn-qa2\data\qa2-cairn.db) to mint their own session, the second agent's run deleted the first agent's still-in-use session row.

IMPACT: silent auth loss mid-run for whichever agent's session got deleted — its next API call gets a 401, and it burns dozens of wasted retry/re-auth calls figuring out why before realizing another agent's seed run stomped its cookie. Observed during the QA campaign coordinating multiple concurrent QA agents against the shared qa2 instance.

FIX APPLIED (C:\dev\cairn-qa2\scripts\qa\seed-qa2.mjs): script now takes a username argument (`node seed-qa2.mjs <username>`, defaults to qa2-admin for backward compat), derives a per-agent email from it, and only INSERTs — creates the user (same admin/role flags as qa2-admin) if missing via check-then-insert, otherwise reuses the existing user row untouched, then always INSERTs a brand-new session row. The `DELETE FROM sessions WHERE user_id = ?` call is gone entirely; no session is ever deleted by this script.

Smoke-tested against C:\dev\cairn-qa2\data\qa2-cairn.db: ran with two new usernames (qa-selftest-1, qa-selftest-2) — both new users created, both new sessions inserted, and all 5 pre-existing session rows (ids 9-13, spanning 4 other users) remained present and unmodified. Re-ran with qa-selftest-1 again to confirm idempotency: user id=5 was reused (not recreated), a fresh session was added, and no existing session was touched (session count went 5 -> 7 -> 8 across the three runs, monotonically increasing, no drops).

DURABLE RULE: QA seed scripts that mint sessions on a shared DB must be INSERT-only and per-agent (unique username/email per agent invocation). Never DELETE existing session rows keyed only on a shared/default user — that shared-user pattern is exactly what causes one agent's seed run to invalidate another's live session.
close_reason: Fixed in C:\dev\cairn-qa2\scripts\qa\seed-qa2.mjs: removed the DELETE FROM sessions WHERE user_id=? call, added a per-agent username argv arg (default qa2-admin for backward compat), check-then-insert user creation (INSERT-only, same admin flags as qa2-admin), and always-fresh session INSERT. Smoke-tested against C:\dev\cairn-qa2\data\qa2-cairn.db with two new usernames (qa-selftest-1, qa-selftest-2): both new sessions inserted, all 5 pre-existing sessions (ids 9-13) untouched; re-run confirmed idempotent user reuse. Session count 5 -> 7 -> 8, monotonic, no drops.

title: Batch sending (multiple recipients per transaction)

title: Auto-redirect returning users to the secure address once the cert is accepted
description: Alex's ask for the next release: on non-localhost plain-HTTP pages with an advertised HTTPS listener (page.data.httpsPort), stop requiring the manual 'Open the secure address' click. Client-side after mount: probe the https origin with fetch(mode:'no-cors') — it only resolves once the user has already clicked through the cert interstitial for that origin (subresource fetches to a non-bypassed self-signed origin reject; no interstitial is shown for them). On success, location.replace(same path+search on https). First-time users keep the guided SecureContextHelp flow (probe fails silently); passkey users are unaffected until they've accepted the cert once (and the login page carries the password-note). Session cookies ignore ports, so login state survives the hop. Needs httpsPort exposed outside the (app) layout too (login page).
close_reason: Built 605797e, shipped v0.1.8 (public 733ac25). E2E 15/15 in real Chromium — see release/0.1.8 branch bead notes.

title: Admin settings form action drops core_rpc_* outside custom mode
description:
src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts writes core_rpc_url/core_rpc_user/core_rpc_pass ONLY inside the `if (connectionMode === 'custom')` block (~L101-131, specifically L123-130), but Bitcoin Core RPC is mode-independent: getChainConfig() (settings.ts ~L216-224) returns coreRpc* in BOTH 'public' and 'custom' connection modes -- there is no public Core fallback, Core is "on" iff core_rpc_url is set, regardless of connection_mode. The parallel JSON endpoint src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts (map at L22-24) already writes core_rpc_* unconditionally, so the two save paths disagree today.

This blocks Wave B Unit B2 (docs/UMBREL-AUTOCONNECT-WAVE-B-DESIGN.md, epic cairn-ylz5): the planned assisted-connect flow (admin pastes the RPC password into a banner surfaced by Unit B1's detect probe) must persist core_rpc_* regardless of connection_mode and must NOT force-flip connection_mode to 'custom' as a side effect of connecting Core. Today's code structurally cannot do that -- a 'public'-mode admin who tries the assisted connect would have their core_rpc_* fields silently dropped on save.

Fix direction (see design doc Section 9 "Save-path wrinkle to fix"): lift the three core_rpc_* writes out of the custom-only block (they should run unconditionally, mirroring the JSON endpoint), or give the assisted-connect action its own handler that writes Core settings + stamps core_rpc_provisioned_by='umbrel-detect' without touching connection_mode. Should also run testCoreRpc() before persisting, matching existing validation conventions.

HANDOFF NOTE: this file is under src/routes/(app)/admin/**/+page.server.ts, which is outside this session's edit ownership boundary (infra/Wave-B-B1 session, cairn-ylz5). Filing for whichever session/orchestrator owns admin-settings form actions. See also docs/HANDOFF-wave-b-unit-b2-settings-form.md (untracked) for the same summary.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 6a06e17 (single-sig-full-wallet): core_rpc_* writes lifted out of the connectionMode==='custom' block, gated on form.has() so absent-from-payload means unchanged. 25/25 targeted vitest tests pass (8 new regression tests). See comments for full evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Root cause: `src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts`'s `save` action wrote `core_rpc_url`/`core_rpc_user`/`core_rpc_pass` only inside the `if (connectionMode === 'custom') { ... }` block (old L101-131, specifically L123-130). Bitcoin Core RPC is mode-independent (`getChainConfig()` returns `coreRpc*` in both `public` and `custom` modes), so a `'public'`-mode submission that included Core RPC fields (e.g. the Umbrel Wave B assisted-connect flow) had those fields silently dropped -- never persisted at all. A second, related bug lived in the same lines: `String(form.get('coreRpcUrl') ?? '').trim()` treated "field absent from the FormData" the same as "field present and empty," so any submission missing a `coreRpcUrl`/`coreRpcUser` input (not just non-custom-mode ones) would have cleared the stored value instead of leaving it untouched. The parallel JSON endpoint (`src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts`) already wrote these three keys unconditionally, gated only on `key in body`, so the two save paths disagreed.

Fix: moved the three `core_rpc_*` writes out of the `connectionMode === 'custom'` block so they run on every `save` submission, and gated each one on `form.has(...)` (not `form.get(...) ?? ''`) so "absent from the payload" now always means "leave the stored value unchanged" -- an explicit clear still works (submit the field present-but-empty, or `clearCoreRpcPass=on` for the password). This mirrors the JSON endpoint's existing `key in body` semantics and matches design doc `docs/UMBREL-AUTOCONNECT-WAVE-B-DESIGN.md` section 9's fix option 1. `connection_mode` itself is untouched by this change -- a `'public'`-mode submission that includes Core RPC fields now persists them while `connection_mode` stays `'public'`.

Files changed:
- src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts -- lifted core_rpc_* writes out of the custom-only block, gated on form.has()
- src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/page.server.test.ts -- added 8 regression tests (describe block "admin/settings save action -- core_rpc_* persistence (cairn-6uok)")
- MANUAL.md -- added a paragraph under "Settings stored in DB vs env" documenting the mode-independent Core RPC save semantics and the absent-vs-empty distinction

Tests added (src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/page.server.test.ts):
1. preserves existing core_rpc_* when payload omits them entirely (public mode)
2. preserves existing core_rpc_* when payload omits them entirely (custom mode)
3. persists core_rpc_* fields present in the payload while connectionMode is custom
4. persists core_rpc_* fields present in the payload even while connectionMode is public (assisted-connect), and never mutates connection_mode away from public
5. an explicit present-but-empty coreRpcUrl/coreRpcUser still clears them (distinct from "absent")
6. a blank-but-present coreRpcPass does not overwrite the stored secret (existing "blank means keep" convention)
7. clearCoreRpcPass="on" clears the stored secret regardless of connectionMode
8. does not regress existing custom-mode Electrum/Esplora behavior

Test evidence: `npx vitest run "src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/page.server.test.ts"` -- 1 file, 25 tests passed (17 pre-existing + 8 new), 0 failed.

Scope note: this fixes the server-side persistence bug only (the literal cairn-6uok bug). It does NOT implement the full Wave B Unit B2 assisted-connect action -- no `coreRpcAssisted=umbrel` hidden-field convention, no `core_rpc_provisioned_by='umbrel-detect'` stamping, and no `testCoreRpc()` pre-persist validation were added, since none of those were required to fix the drop bug and the JSON endpoint precedent doesn't validate before persisting either. Remaining component-side work (outside this session's src/lib/server + routes/api + +page.server.ts territory) for whoever owns +page.svelte: the Core RPC input fields in `src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte` are still rendered only inside its own `{#if connectionMode === 'custom'}` block (L284-467ish), so a real 'public'-mode admin still can't reach these fields in the UI even though the server now accepts them mode-independently; and `useDetectedCoreNode()` (L60-65) still force-flips `connectionMode` to `'custom'` as a workaround (see its own doc comment referencing this bead) -- now that the server fix has landed, that flip is no longer necessary and should be removed so assisted-connect stops touching the operator's Electrum connection_mode as a side effect.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Shipped in commit 6a06e17 on branch single-sig-full-wallet: fix(settings): preserve core_rpc_* when absent from non-custom-mode submissions (cairn-6uok). 3 files changed: src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts, src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/page.server.test.ts (+8 regression tests), MANUAL.md. Test evidence: npx vitest run "src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/page.server.test.ts" -- 25/25 passed (17 pre-existing + 8 new: preserve-on-omit x2 modes, persist-when-present-custom, persist-when-present-public-without-mutating-connection_mode, explicit-empty-still-clears, blank-password-keeps-secret, clearCoreRpcPass-works-any-mode, no-regression-of-existing-custom-mode-behavior). Closing -- server-side drop bug is fixed. Remaining component-side work (NOT done here, flagged for whoever owns +page.svelte): Core RPC input fields in src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte are still rendered only inside their own connectionMode==='custom' conditional, so a public-mode admin still can't reach them in the UI; and useDetectedCoreNode() still force-flips connectionMode to custom as a workaround that is no longer necessary now that the server accepts core_rpc_* mode-independently.

comments.text: Component-side follow-up filed as cairn-3p9z (Core RPC fields still gated behind connectionMode==='custom' in +page.svelte, and useDetectedCoreNode() still force-flips connection_mode as an old workaround for this bug's server-side symptom).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Independent verification (separate session, 2026-07-13): confirmed this bead's fix is exactly as described in the prior close comment -- present at commit 6a06e17 on single-sig-full-wallet, core_rpc_* writes lifted out of the connectionMode==='custom' block, gated on form.has(). No recovery from stash/dangling-commit was needed; the commit was already reachable in this branch's history when I started forensics (git stash show for stash@{0}'s admin/settings paths diffed empty against HEAD -- that stash's copy of these files was identical to what's already committed, just leftover noise from the day's git-filter-repo safety snapshot).

Landed the two pieces this bead's own close comment flagged as NOT done (also independently filed as cairn-3p9z): a validated assisted-connect save path and a Dismiss action. Commit 0427661 (merge; underlying work 2292a20) on single-sig-full-wallet:
- +page.server.ts `save` action: new early-return branch on hidden coreRpcAssisted=umbrel marker -- runs testCoreRpc() before persisting (fails closed), then persists core_rpc_url/user/pass and stamps core_rpc_provisioned_by='umbrel-detect'; returns before connection_mode/registration_mode/etc are read, so it can't mutate connection_mode as a side effect.
- new dismissCoreDetection action writes core_rpc_detected='dismissed'.
- +page.svelte: removed the old useDetectedCoreNode() force-flip-to-custom workaround, replaced with a self-contained assisted-connect card (password field + Connect/Dismiss) that works mode-independently.
- +8 tests (31/31 in the file). Full suite: 2698 passed / 1 skipped, 0 failed.

See cairn-3p9z for the one item still open (manual Core RPC input fields are still gated behind connectionMode==='custom' for hand-entry outside the assisted-connect flow).

title: [pool-ux] Block-found and worker-offline notifications default to in-app only - miners miss the two moments that matter
description: Evidence: src/lib/server/notifications.ts DEFAULT_PREFERENCES lines 73-75: mining_block_found, mining_worker_offline, mining_best_share all default to [inapp] only, even though external channels (per-user SMTP email etc) exist and the settings UI lists the events. A solo block find is a once-in-years, life-changing event - if the user is not logged in they may not learn for days that 3+ BTC landed; and a rig that dies silently at 3am mines nothing. Competitor research: worker-offline alerting is the single most-cited gap/strength divider across pools (Braiins auto-threshold email alerts praised; Ocean/public-pool lack of alerts is why the third-party DeepSea dashboard exists; F2Pool ships app+Telegram+email alerts). Proposed fix (plain language): default mining_block_found to every channel the user has configured (it is the flagship moment), default mining_worker_offline to email+inapp when email is configured, and surface a one-tap get-emailed-if-a-miner-goes-quiet nudge on the mining dashboard rather than burying it in settings.

comments.author: pool-ux-audit
comments.text: Lane note (coordinator relay 2026-07-19): this bead covers CHANNEL DEFAULTS for the existing mining_block_found / mining_worker_offline events only - a miner-facing UX defaults issue. Missing event TYPES (reward-now-spendable at 100 conf, block-rejected/orphaned notice) are the wallet-pool integration sessions lane and will be filed as [wallet-pool] beads; do not expand this bead to cover them.

BitboxSigner.svelte (419 lines) vs MultisigBitboxSigner.svelte (538) — both carry the pairing-code flow; the multisig variant adds script-config registration. Preserve all copy/flows/error handling exactly; svelte-check + vitest + preview load of both send pages; ideally hardware-test.

title: Perf re-architecture: dashboard/portfolio, derivation, sync engine (Fable audit 2026-07-09)
description: Umbrella epic for the perf re-architecture work identified in Fable's 2026-07-09 audit: dashboard/portfolio bypass of load caching, address-derivation cost, and the sync engine's full-rescan-every-poll behavior. Scope spans (1) sync engine dirty-tracking via Electrum status-hash to skip clean rescans, (2) portfolio cache-first reads from wallet_snapshots instead of bypassing scan-lane guardrails, (3) memoized address derivation (WeakMap for xpub chain nodes + multisig deriver factory), (4) worker-thread offload for background DB work (gated behind the above landing and measured first), (5) env-var seeding for ELECTRUM_HOST/ESPLORA_URL for non-Umbrel self-hosters, and (6) the Umbrel Electrum/esplora-defaults product decision. This epic tracks and reports on that work; it has no code of its own. Originally created empty with the 6 real work items pointing at it via inverted 'blocks' edges (making them permanently BLOCKED while this epic showed READY) -- fixed 2026-07-12 per the beads audit by converting those edges to parent-child, matching the zoz8/koy4/49xi epic convention.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Informal scope note (2026-07-12 beads audit): this epic's 6 real children are cairn-wcxw (sync engine Phase 1 dirty-tracking), cairn-2ldr (product decision: Umbrel Electrum/esplora defaults), cairn-uhg1 (replace esplora tx-detail fetch with local Electrum-only parsing), cairn-8ubd (memoize address derivation), cairn-1q4b (worker threads, measure first), cairn-5pov (env-var seeding for ELECTRUM_HOST/ESPLORA_URL). These previously pointed at this epic via inverted 'blocks' edges, which made all 6 show BLOCKED forever while this epic showed READY (proof the edges encoded nothing real: cairn-b7to closed 2026-07-11 while nominally 'blocked' by this epic, requiring --force). Edges converted to parent-child today so the epic is blocked-until-children-close like cairn-zoz8/cairn-koy4/cairn-49xi, and the 6 children are independently ready.

title: Raw driver/exception text leaks to clients on unrecognized error paths (SMTP test, wallets.ts, stateless routes)
description: Three related sites forward raw driver/exception text to clients on unrecognized error paths: (a) src/routes/api/notifications/channels/email/test-smtp/+server.ts:100-103 and src/routes/api/admin/notifications/test-smtp/+server.ts:23-26 return raw err.message from an uncaught SMTP-connection exception verbatim to the client (the user-facing route accepts unsaved, user-typed host/port with no SSRF guard, so this could be a mild network-reconnaissance oracle); (b) src/lib/server/wallets.ts:160-165 createWallet rethrows raw DB driver errors for any failure other than a UNIQUE constraint violation, which src/routes/api/wallets/+server.ts:19-24 forwards verbatim to the client at 400; (c) the four src/routes/api/stateless/* routes (accepting untrusted pasted PSBT/Caravan/descriptor text) fall back to raw e.message at 502 for any exception not matching one of four known domain-error classes. Fix: consolidate to one reviewed "safe-to-forward" error classifier instead of the current instanceof-Error-then-forward-message idiom repeated across roughly 50 call sites; at minimum fix these three concrete instances. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed the three concrete instances in 22ab66d: both test-smtp routes and the stateless 502 fallback now return a generic message (raw error only logged); createWallet throws a sanitized message on non-UNIQUE DB errors. A single shared classifier across all ~50 sites remains a larger refactor, out of scope here.

title: Banner consolidation batch 1: auth + wallet-creation + explorer pages adopt Banner.svelte
description: 40+ files hand-roll form-error/saved-note divs; only send flow + Toasts use Banner (built for cairn-ivae.5). Batch 1 = highest-traffic user pages.

title: No end-to-end test for M=1 or M=N (unanimous) multisig quorum boundary
description: All multisig tests (multisig.test.ts, multisigPsbt.test.ts, multisigTransactions.test.ts, stateless.test.ts) exclusively use strict M-less-than-N configs (2-of-3, 3-of-5) -- no test exercises a 1-of-N or N-of-N (unanimous) quorum. Off-by-one threshold bugs in signature-counting/finalization logic live exactly at these boundaries. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Added explicit quorum-boundary tests to multisigPsbt.test.ts: M=1 (1-of-3 — not complete at 0 sigs and refuses finalize; completes+finalizes on the first signature from ANY single cosigner, verified with the third key) and M=N unanimous (2-of-2 needs both; 3-of-3 only completes on the third, refusing finalize at 1 and 2). Exercises the off-by-one threshold edges every prior lifecycle test (2-of-3, 3-of-5) skipped. All pass.

title: [pool-ux] Home page never reflects mining - an active rig is invisible on the apps front door
description: Evidence: src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte contains zero references to mining or hashrate (grep confirms). A user whose Bitaxe is hashing 24/7 sees nothing about it on Home - no hashrate tile, no best-share, no worker-offline hint - so the daily-check habit that retains hash never forms around the apps landing surface. Competitor angle: pool dashboards ARE the home page for miners; F2Pool ships a whole mobile app for glanceability. Proposed fix (plain language): when the user has mining enabled and at least one worker, add a small Home tile: current hashrate, worker count with a quiet offline pip if any rig is silent, and best-share teaser, linking to /mining. Data is one getUserMiningView call or a trimmed variant; respect the existing calm-dashboard design doctrine.

title: webhook SSRF blocklist misses IPv4-mapped IPv6 in compressed-hex form (::ffff:7f00:1), only checks dotted-quad spelling
description: src/lib/server/channels/webhook.ts's isBlockedAddress() regex /^::ffff:(\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+)$/ only matches the dotted-quad textual spelling of an IPv4-mapped IPv6 address (e.g. ::ffff:127.0.0.1). But WHATWG URL parsing normalizes a bracketed literal to the canonical compressed-hex form: new URL('http://[::ffff:127.0.0.1]/').hostname === '[::ffff:7f00:1]' (verified). That form doesn't match the regex, doesn't match any other branch (::1, ::, fc00::/7, fe80::/10), so isBlockedAddress falls through to 'not blocked' — a live bypass letting a webhook target loopback/private addresses via a bracketed IPv6-mapped literal (or an AAAA record returning the same canonical form). This directly contradicts the file's own doc comment claiming ::ffff:a.b.c.d is 'unwrapped and re-checked as IPv4' — it only handles one textual spelling, not the one the code's own URL parsing (and DNS resolution) actually produces. The existing test suite (webhook.test.ts) only feeds the dotted-quad form directly to the internal function, never the canonical hex form through an end-to-end checkTargetUrl() call, so this has no failing test today. Fix: normalize any ::ffff:HHHH:HHHH hex-form IPv4-mapped address back to dotted-quad (or compare numerically) before/within isBlockedAddress, and add a test exercising checkTargetUrl() with the bracketed literal end-to-end.
close_reason: Fixed in fc8da90

title: Receive screen has no QR code for the address (mobile+desktop)
description:
Wave-2S mobile QA sweep (qa2 instance, port 5192, wallet id=4 QA Mobile Sweep Wallet, http://qa2s2.localhost:5192/wallets/4).

The wallet-detail "receive" section shows the address as plain text with "Copy" and "Rotate" buttons only -- no QR code image/SVG/canvas anywhere. Checked at both mobile (375x812) and desktop (1280x800) viewports; confirmed via DOM inspection (no svg >50px, no canvas, no img[data:] near the receive address; only two icon-only buttons on the page, both unrelated: "Collapse sidebar" and "Back"). There is also no dedicated /wallets/:id/receive route (404s) -- receive is an inline anchor section (#receive) on the wallet detail page, not a separate screen.

Impact: users on mobile who want to receive from another wallet/app typically expect to scan a QR code rather than copy/paste or read aloud a bech32 address. This is a standard Bitcoin wallet UX affordance that's currently entirely absent.

Repro: navigate to any wallet detail page, view the "A fresh address, every time" section -- text address + Copy + Rotate only, no QR.

Filed by Wave-2S mobile QA sweep, 2026-07-19.
close_reason: Investigated: QR generation for receive addresses already existed (qrcode npm dep, in package.json/node_modules since initial public release; wired into src/lib/server/walletSync.ts + both wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts and wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts via QRCode.toDataURL, rendered as on both single-sig and multisig receive panels). Live-verified on the exact repro URL (qa2s2.localhost:5192/wallets/4) via JS DOM query: img present, valid data URI, decoded 220x220 PNG -- QR is NOT missing today. Root-caused the real defect instead: QR_OPTS used light parchment modules (#E4D8CC) on a transparent background, which has fine contrast against the app's near-black dark-theme --bg but is nearly unreadable in light theme, where the card sits on --light-bg (#f3efe7) -- two light parchment tones a scanner can't distinguish. Fixed in all 3 QR_OPTS definitions (walletSync.ts shared const used by both single-sig+multisig scans, wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts, wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts) to opaque light background (#f3efe7) + evergreen-ink dark modules (#1f2623) -- self-contained, scans correctly regardless of theme or surrounding surface. npm run check: 0 errors. Verified server-rendered HTML via curl against a seeded instance (port 5711): img.hw-qr present with valid data:image/png;base64 payload, decoded PNG header confirms 220x220 dimensions. No new dependency -- qrcode was already a project dependency used elsewhere (QrSigner.svelte air-gapped signing flow).

title: Coinbase-maturity check can be silently bypassed on a chain-RPC hiccup
description: src/lib/server/bitcoin/coinbaseScan.ts:16-29, isCoinbaseTx/annotateCoinbase: catches any chain-fetch failure and returns false (not coinbase) with zero logging and no caching of the failure. A transient Electrum/Esplora hiccup can make an immature mining-reward UTXO look ordinary and spendable, silently bypassing the 100-block maturity check added in commit 1dcc5bd. Fix: on fetch failure, propagate an explicit "unknown/unverifiable" state (not a silent "safe to spend" false) and log a warning. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in c17328b

title: psbt.ts: inconsistent dust threshold — pre-flight uses correct per-type dustThreshold(), coin-selection uses flat legacy DUST_SATS=546
description:
src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts has two different dust checks:
- Line 399-408 (the user-facing pre-flight, "This amount is too small to send."): correctly calls
  dustThreshold(address) (defined :228-246), which computes the right per-script-type floor
  (P2WPKH 294, P2WSH/P2TR 330, P2PKH 546, P2SH 540) per its own doc comment at :232-236.
- Lines 698, 773, 856: use the flat legacy constant DUST_SATS = 546 (:170) instead, e.g.
  `if (amount <= DUST_SATS)` at :698 — this would wrongly treat a 300-sat P2WPKH output as
  dust deep in coin-selection/change-output logic even though the pre-flight already approved it
  as non-dust, and vice versa is inconsistent with the documented per-type intent.

Not confirmed as user-reproducible in isolation yet — QA hit an unrelated live blocker
(see cairn-amrn8) that prevented any small send from completing, so this couldn't be exercised
end-to-end. Filing as a source-level consistency/cleanup finding for whoever owns psbt.ts:
either the :698/:773/:856 call sites should call dustThreshold(address) too, or DUST_SATS should
be removed if it's genuinely dead/superseded by the newer per-type function (git blame /
cairn-ykk6 context suggests dustThreshold() was added later as the fix — DUST_SATS may just be
leftover).
close_reason: Fixed: replaced the flat legacy DUST_SATS=546 comparisons at psbt.ts's three coin-selection/change/send-max sites (originally :698/:773/:856, now inside the send-max, exactInputs/RBF-change, and normal-selection-change branches of constructPsbt) with dustThreshold(address) calls against the actual destination — matching the pre-flight check in validateRecipientsAndFeeRate (P2PKH 546, P2SH 540, P2WPKH 294, P2WSH/P2TR 330). Removed the now-dead DUST_SATS constant. Standard P2PKH behavior is unchanged (dustThreshold(p2pkh)==546); P2WPKH/P2WSH/P2TR sites now correctly use their own lower floor instead of being held to the P2PKH ceiling — this changed 4 sendBoundaryMatrix.test.ts boundary assertions from hardcoded 546 to the correct 294 for their p2wpkh fixtures (updated in the same commit), which is the intended consistency fix, not a regression. npm run check clean; psbt/multisigPsbt/sendBoundaryMatrix/xpub/malformedSendInputs vitest suites green (362 tests).
