description: src/lib/server/db.ts ~L606-616: CREATE TABLE notified_txids has user_id INTEGER NOT NULL and wallet_id INTEGER NOT NULL but NEITHER column has a REFERENCES/FK clause (every other user_id column in db.ts uses REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE). Grepped the whole codebase (addressWatcher.ts is the only writer) and there is no DELETE FROM notified_txids anywhere — not in deleteUser(), not in wallet/multisig deletion paths, not in admin.ts resetInstance(). Effect: every row this table has ever written for a deleted user, wallet, or multisig becomes a permanent orphan. resetInstance() (src/lib/server/admin.ts) explicitly documents itself as a full factory reset ('delete every user, session, wallet, invite and setting') and the Settings UI (admin/settings/+page.svelte) tells the admin 'nothing else survives' — but notified_txids rows survive indefinitely with no cleanup path at all, even outside of factory reset (normal single-user/wallet deletion leaks rows too). Fix: add ON DELETE CASCADE-capable cleanup — either an FK (tricky since wallet_id is polymorphic across wallet_kind) or explicit DELETE FROM notified_txids WHERE user_id = ? calls alongside deleteUser()/wallet deletion/resetInstance().
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Single-sig wallets never persist master_fingerprint/derivation_path — HW signing broken for all single-key wallets
description:
createWallet() (src/lib/server/wallets.ts:138-190) inserts only (user_id, name, type, xpub, script_type, device_type) — master_fingerprint and derivation_path (both declared in WalletRow, real DB columns) are never written, by ANY creation path, including the real hardware-connect flow: readSingleSigKeyFromTrezor() (src/lib/hw/trezor.ts:1346+) correctly reads {xpub, fingerprint, path} from the device, but src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte only captures d.xpub into validatedXpub — fingerprint/path are discarded, and the submitted form only sends xpub + deviceType. setWalletDevice() (wallets.ts:197) likewise only ever touches device_type.

Consequence: constructPsbt() (src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts:509) only emits bip32Derivation when params.origin is given, and src/lib/server/transactions.ts:299 only builds origin when wallet.master_fingerprint is non-null — which is NEVER true for a single-key wallet. Every exported/signed PSBT for a single-sig wallet therefore has no bip32Derivation. Cairn's own signPsbtWithTrezor() explicitly requires it and throws 'This transaction is missing the key-origin information the Trezor needs to sign. Re-create it from a wallet set up with its master fingerprint.' (trezor.ts ~line 176). The same requirement almost certainly applies to the Ledger/BitBox02/Jade signers (same PSBT, same guard pattern).

Reproduced live on the v0.1.3 Umbrel instance (http://umbrel.local:3211): confirmed via source read plus a live download+decode of a real 'QA Trezor (regtest)' wallet's unsigned PSBT (src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/file/+server.ts output) — the input has witnessUtxo/nonWitnessUtxo but no bip32Derivation field at all.

Note: multisig keys (multisig_keys table / MultisigSignKey) DO store and validate fingerprint correctly (resolveMultisigSignKeys in trezor.ts requires it) — this bug is specific to the single-key  table/createWallet() path.

This may be a very recent regression: today's (2026-07-06) wallet-dedup refactor (see project memory 'cairn-wallet-dedup-refactor') left 'Ledger/BitBox/Jade signer pairs still unmerged' per its own notes — worth checking whether single-sig fingerprint capture regressed in that refactor or predates it.

Fix shape: thread {fingerprint, path} from the device-read result through the wallet-creation form (hidden inputs) and into createWallet()'s INSERT (and the paste-xpub path should leave both null, as today, since there's no device to trust).
close_reason: Duplicate of cairn-alw8 -- identical root cause (single-key wallets never persist master_fingerprint/derivation_path, breaking all HW signing), filed independently in another concurrent QA session against the same live instance (same pattern as cairn-tu0i, already closed as a dup of alw8 for this exact issue). See cairn-alw8 for the canonical writeup.

title: Sanitize chain errors in history.csv and receive API routes (cairn-sgtr follow-up)
description: history.csv/+server.ts calls scanWallet directly and api/wallets/[id]/receive + api/wallets/multisig/[id]/receive still return raw error text; apply sanitizeChainError from chainErrors.ts, same pattern as 492845e.
close_reason: Fixed in ba9d026 (integrated): sanitizeChainError applied to history.csv, both receive routes, and both utxo-mass routes; other wallet API routes verified already-sanitized via getWalletDetail/getMultisigDetail. 3 new boundary tests.

title: Add Dockerfile, compose file, and deployment docs
description:
No containerization exists at all — no Dockerfile, no docker-compose.yml. Self-hosters (this app's entire audience) commonly expect a Docker path, especially for Umbrel-style one-click deployment. Needs:
- Multi-stage Dockerfile (Node 22.5+ base per package.json engines, adapter-node production build)
- docker-compose.yml with a documented volume mount for CAIRN_DB persistence (currently ./data/cairn.db; a container restart without a mounted volume silently loses all users/wallets/invites)
- 'start' script in package.json (currently missing — only dev/build/preview exist; production convention is npm start -> node build)
- README 'Deployment' section covering the Docker path, the CAIRN_DB volume requirement explicitly, and documenting the full set of environment variables adapter-node/the app respects (PORT, CAIRN_DB, and whatever adapter-node exposes — HOST, SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT etc. — currently undocumented beyond a vague mention of two of them).
close_reason: Implemented in 393e119: multi-stage node:22-alpine Dockerfile (non-root, CAIRN_DB=/data, PORT, ADDRESS_HEADER=x-forwarded-for for the rate limiter, HEALTHCHECK, VOLUME /data), docker-compose.yml with documented ./data bind mount and data-loss warning, npm start script, README Deployment section incl. env table, proxy header trust caveat, and SSE buffering note. Verified beyond inspection: docker build succeeded, container ran healthy as non-root, /api/health 200 from host, compose config validates

title: Missing regression test: removing a contact doesn't revoke their multisig wallet shares (cairn-2oex)
description: cairn-2oex (P1, closed in 016acf2) fixed contact removal not revoking a contact's existing multisig wallet shares — a real access-control gap (a removed contact could retain viewer/cosigner access). Fix confirmed present, no test exists. Add a test to src/lib/server/contacts.test.ts: create a contact, share a multisig wallet with them (viewer or cosigner role with an assigned key), call removeContact(), and assert the multisig_shares row is gone / assigned_user_id is cleared and the (former) contact's access role for that multisig is now null.
close_reason: Committed in 8603ac6: multisigShares.test.ts (first coverage), contacts.test.ts share-revocation, notifications.test.ts stage isolation, broadcast route malformed-JSON 400 + log.error tests, walletsDraft.test.ts buildDraft/coin-control coverage.

title: hw_* feature flags don't gate the wallet-creation wizard's device picker (Key step)
description:
QA pass for bead cairn-1ytc (feature-flag matrix, my batch: hw_trezor/hw_ledger/hw_coldcard/hw_bitbox02/hw_jade).

BUG: turning off any hw_* flag in /admin/feature-flags has NO effect on the primary hardware-wallet entry point: the single-sig wallet creation wizard's Key step (/wallets/new, step "KEY").

Root cause: src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte defines its own METHOD_CARDS array (lines ~258-266: trezor/ledger/coldcard/bitbox02/jade/qr/paste) which is rendered unconditionally at lines ~791-826 ({#each METHOD_CARDS as m}) with NO feature-flag field and NO filter against page.data.flags. This is a SEPARATE device list from the flag-gated one in src/lib/components/DevicePicker.svelte (which correctly filters via `!o.flag || page.data.flags?.[o.flag] !== false` and is only used later, on the Finish step's "change device" sub-flow).

There is also no server-side enforcement: src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.server.ts reads `deviceType` from the submitted form (line ~72) and persists it with no requireFeature(event, `hw_${deviceType}`) check, so even a hand-crafted POST bypassing the client entirely would still succeed.

Net effect: an admin can toggle e.g. hw_trezor OFF, and a user (including a non-admin) can still pick "Trezor" as their key source in step 1 of wallet creation, fully unobstructed — the flag is silently inert for this surface.

REPRO (verified live in browser, both admin qa-matrix@test.local and plain user qa-matrix-user@test.local sessions, server on data/qa-matrix-b.db):
1. /admin/feature-flags -> toggle "Trezor" (hw_trezor) to Disable.
2. Navigate to /wallets/new.
3. Step 1 "KEY" still shows a fully-enabled "Trezor" card with description "Plug it in and connect with one click." and a "Buy one ->" referral link, identical to the enabled state.
4. Confirmed identically for Ledger/Coldcard/BitBox02/Jade after toggling hw_ledger/hw_coldcard/hw_bitbox02/hw_jade off together.
5. No console errors either way — this is a pure missing-gate, not a crash.
6. Toggled all 5 flags back on, confirmed admin UI reflects "Disable X" (enabled) state again.

FIX: filter METHOD_CARDS the same way DevicePicker.svelte does (add a `flag` field per entry, filter against page.data.flags in the {#each}), and add a requireFeature(event, `hw_${deviceType}`) guard in +page.server.ts's create action (only for device-sourced deviceType values; 'file'/paste stays ungated per registry.ts's userMessage design).

Evidence: HEARTWOOD_DB=data/qa-matrix-b.db, seeded via scripts/qa/seed-flagmatrix.mjs, dev server on port 5315, browser session isolated via http://qamatrixb.localhost:5315 (distinct *.localhost subdomain) to avoid httpOnly cairn_session collisions with other concurrent QA sessions sharing the bare localhost cookie domain.
close_reason: done

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
FIXED. Root cause exactly as filed: +page.svelte had its own METHOD_CARDS array rendered unconditionally with no flag field/no filter (separate from flag-gated DevicePicker.svelte); +page.server.ts create action persisted deviceType with no requireFeature guard.

Fix: extracted card list + gating into src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/deviceMethods.ts (METHOD_CARDS with per-entry flag hw_trezor/hw_ledger/hw_coldcard/hw_bitbox02/hw_jade/qr_scan; paste ungated; visibleMethodCards(flags) mirrors DevicePicker predicate). +page.svelte renders methodCards=visibleMethodCards(page.data.flags). +page.server.ts create action takes full event and calls requireFeature(event, DEVICE_TYPE_FLAG[deviceType]) for device-sourced values, closing the hand-crafted-POST bypass; empty/unknown deviceType stays ungated.

Test: deviceMethods.test.ts (6 green): each flag off -> device absent, paste never gated with all flags off, fail-open when flags absent.

title: [wallet-pool] BlockContext/txFlow coinbase band not finder-aware — generic 'New coins', never 'your reward'
description: BlockContext.svelte:97 flags coinbase generically; txFlow.ts:157-177 renders a 'Newly minted coins' source band with no notion of instance/finder. Fix: thread a poolFound flag to relabel the coinbase output band ('-> your wallet') and tint as an instance win.

title: [wallet-pool] Home has zero mining presence — no status line, hashrate, or link even when actively mining
description: Grep of (app)/+page.svelte and +page.server.ts: 0 mining matches; no MiningHero import outside /mining. A user with an active miner sees nothing on the front door; Home never links /mining. Fix: one calm Health-style line when mining is enabled ('Mining · 1.2 TH/s · 1 worker' / amber when a worker is offline), linking /mining — consistent with the spec's one-calm-line grammar.

title: Missing regression test: agreement page reserved-action crash (cairn-z6i1)
description: cairn-z6i1 (P0, closed in 46dd16f) fixed /agreement crashing with a 500 because a Svelte form action was named 'default', a reserved name. Fix confirmed still present in current code. No test exists guarding against this class of regression. Add a test that POSTs to the agreement page's accept action (or a route-level test on /agreement) and asserts a 200/redirect rather than a 500 — this would catch a future accidental rename back to a reserved action name. Filed from the 2026-07-06 process retrospective (docs/PROCESS-RETROSPECTIVE-2026-07-06.md), which found only 6% of bug fixes added a regression test.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 56a1371 (security-logging.test.ts, auth.test.ts setSessionCookie block, logger.test.ts + exported REDACT_OPTIONS, agreement/server.test.ts, api/wallets/server.test.ts, (app)/layout.server.test.ts).

title: Wallet wizard Step-1 'Paste public key' CTA sits below the fold at common 1280x800 desktop size
description:
On /wallets/new (single-sig wallet creation wizard, Step 1), the method-card grid (Trezor / Ledger / ColdCard / BitBox02 / Jade / Air-gapped QR / Paste public key) extends past the viewport bottom at a common desktop size (1280x800, viewport content area ~1280x720 under the app chrome). Measured via getBoundingClientRect(): the "Paste public key" card -- the ONLY option that doesn't require owning a hardware signing device, and the one the page's own copy recommends for "No hardware wallet yet?" -- sits at y=785-873, fully below the 720px-tall viewport, with no visible scroll affordance/indicator in the initial view.

Why it matters: a first-time, non-technical user without a hardware wallet is the exact audience this card is for, and the page gives no hint that more options exist below the fold (no visible partial card, no scroll cue). They may conclude their only options are hardware devices they don't own and abandon wallet creation. This also made the button materially harder to interact with in this QA pass (see cairn-61bh for the separate, more severe finding that clicks on these buttons don't work at all once reached).

Repro: navigate to /wallets/new at 1280x800, do not scroll -- "Paste public key" card is not visible or only barely visible at the very bottom edge.

Suggested fix: reorder so "Paste public key" (the no-hardware-required option) appears first/above the fold, or reduce vertical spacing of the method grid, or add a visible scroll cue.
close_reason: Fixed 005fb23 (branch single-sig-full-wallet): the desktop card-order design decision is intentionally preserved (mobile already reorders Paste-first via cairn-sahi's order:-1), so instead of reordering desktop cards this adds an always-rendered .scroll-cue line above the method grid: '7 ways to add a key below, including Paste public key - no hardware device needed for that one.' Verified live at 1280x800 (qa-wave instance, port 5344): cue renders at y=479.9, well within the 800px viewport, above all 7 method cards which still start at y=723 as before. Also verified at 375x812 (cue at y=489; mobile already had Paste reordered first via the pre-existing breakpoint, unaffected).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: QA re-verify (2026-07-14, qa-wave instance, 1280x800 desktop viewport, real browser measurement): REPRO'D, unfixed. Navigated to /wallets/new and measured all method cards via getBoundingClientRect() without scrolling: Trezor/Ledger/ColdCard at y=687-775, BitBox02/Jade/Air-gapped-QR at y=807-918, and Paste public key (the no-hardware-required option, still last in the grid) at y=928-1016 -- fully below the 800px viewport, consistent with the bead's original finding (card position shifted slightly from the original 785-873 measurement but the below-the-fold problem and lack of any scroll cue are unchanged). No reorder and no scroll affordance has been added. Verdict: STILL OPEN, no fix has landed.

title: Backup restore + account-reclaim chain allows registration-lockdown bypass to full admin
description:
Security audit 2026-07-05 (docs/SECURITY-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md), finding F7. Verified CONFIRMED by 3/3 independent adversarial reviewers.

Two independently-reasonable pieces of code chain into a full privilege escalation:

1. src/lib/server/backup.ts restoreBackup() (~lines 182-199): imports each user row from the decrypted backup file and writes u.is_admin verbatim with NO cap check against adminCount() and no distinct callout in RestoreSummary or the admin/restore route's log line. Imported rows have zero credentials by design (backups never carry credentials).

2. src/routes/api/auth/register/options/+server.ts (~lines 26-40) and register/verify/+server.ts (~lines 61-79): the account-reclaim path (reclaimableUserId() finds a credential-less user row) completely bypasses assertCanRegister() -- i.e. skips registrationMode (closed/invite/open) and invite-code checks entirely, by design, so a legitimate account can be reclaimed via passkey registration without an invite even when the instance is locked down.

Chain: attacker crafts/tampers with a backup file containing a row {email: attacker-controlled, is_admin: 1, no credentials}. An admin is social-engineered into restoring it via the admin-gated /api/admin/restore endpoint (looks like normal maintenance -- no preview of which imported rows are privileged). The attacker then visits the normal signup screen with that email; reclaimableUserId finds the credential-less admin row and routes around assertCanRegister entirely, regardless of registrationMode='closed'. Attacker completes a WebAuthn passkey ceremony with their own device, register/verify attaches the credential and immediately opens a live admin session. No invite code, no confirmation step, no admin-count cap, and no distinct audit-log/notification callout anywhere in the path (the only signup-notification, admin_new_signup, is never called on the reclaim branch).

Recommended fix (per verifier consensus):
- restoreBackup should force imported users' is_admin to 0, OR cap/require explicit admin re-confirmation for any is_admin=true row on import.
- The reclaim path should still honor registrationMode/invite-code for any account carrying is_admin=1, or require an additional out-of-band confirmation for admin-account reclaim.
- Fire a distinct, non-suppressible notification/audit entry whenever a restore imports an is_admin=1 row, and whenever any reclaim (not just admin) completes.
close_reason: Fixed in security-audit remediation pass; see commit. All 897 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: Unconfirmed tx detail page has no explicit confirmation count or speed-up CTA
description: Block-explorer elevation audit (2026-07-12): src/routes/(app)/explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.svelte (lines ~184-251) shows a burial-ring label + a fee-outlook sentence for an unconfirmed tx, but no explicit 'N of 6 confirmations' count and no bump/accelerate CTA, despite a fee_bumping feature already existing elsewhere in the app. Best-in-class: mempool.space puts an Accelerate button right next to the confirmation ETA. Fix: for an unconfirmed tx the viewing user owns, add a 'Speed this up' link into the existing RBF/CPFP send flow, and add an explicit confirmations count next to the burial-ring glyph. Related to (but distinct from) the UX-Elevation Blueprint's P0-3/P1-10 (plain confirmation-status wording on BurialRings labels generally) -- that's the wording fix; this is the missing count + missing CTA specifically on the Explorer tx-detail page. Searched for overlap first: br search for 'speed up'/'accelerate'/'tx detail' found no bead covering this specific gap -- filing new.
close_reason: Fixed in 8c37619 (integrated): confirmationProgress() renders 'N of 6 confirmations' beside the burial-ring label on explorer tx detail; speed-up CTA reuses the persisted speedUp (detectUnconfirmedInflows) eligibility via txOwnership().speedUpWallet, viewer role excluded, ?speedup= one-shot handlers on both wallet pages re-validate eligibility. Full suite 3628 green in worktree.

title: wallet: Taproot SPEND support (tr() single-sig + multisig) — receive-only today reads as incomplete
description: From docs/COMPETITOR-ANALYSIS-R3-2026-07-19.md gap #1. Epic-sized: PSBT taproot fields, HW-wallet tr() signing paths (Trezor/Ledger/BitBox02), descriptor + Caravan round-trip implications, fee estimation. Scope a design doc first; do not start ad hoc. Competitors (Sparrow, Nunchuk, Liana) treat taproot spend as table stakes in 2026.

title: Wallet creation/import emits no activity event (user feed or admin log)
description: Creating or importing a wallet (single-sig wizard, multisig wizard) generates no event in the user activity feed (/activity) or the admin activity log. It's a significant account action and should be recorded in both, following the existing event-emission conventions used by other features (see notification/activity plumbing).
close_reason: Fixed: createWallet emits wallet_added and createMultisig emits wallet_created (with quorum + created/imported wording) via recordActivity — both types were already whitelisted in USER_FEED_TYPES and have icons in /activity + the bell, so they now appear in the user feed and the admin activity log. Detail carries wallet identity only, never the xpub. Tests added in wallets.test.ts and wallets-multisig.test.ts (feed + admin log + no-event-on-failure + privacy assertion).

title: [pool-ux] Odds copy degenerates to -once every less than a year- when expected years is under 1
description: Observed live (regtest, where user hashrate is large relative to network): MiningOddsPanel renders At your current speed, finding a block is a lottery - on average about once every less than a year. formatYears() returns the phrase -less than a year- which is grammatical only standing alone, not after -once every-. Session 2 (integration audit) independently flagged the same sentence as reading broken. This is the dashboards most important honesty surface. Fix (plain language): special-case years < 1 with its own sentence (e.g. at your current speed you would expect a block within the year - still luck, not schedule) instead of splicing the phrase into the -once every X- template. Keep anti-hype doctrine (docs/HASH-ATTRACTION-STRATEGY.md): duration framing only, no earnings projections.

title: Federation (instance-to-instance PSBT coordination) — scoping epic
description: Scoping epic for Heartwood federation: instance-to-instance PSBT coordination for collaborative multisig custody. One Heartwood sends a PSBT to another for signing; peer discovery (invite codes/manual/Tor TBD); signature collection + broadcast; real-time cosigner notifications. NON-CUSTODIAL: each instance holds only its own keys; no e-cash, no Fedimint, no Lightning gateway. Reference model: Bastion's federation protocol (C:\dev\bastion\src\server\federation.ts + docs/COLLABORATIVE-CUSTODY-AUDIT.md). DESIGN CONSTRAINT: UI-first, never API-only (Bastion's lesson). Deliverable: docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md. Child beads labeled federation-scope.

comments.author: Fable-orchestrator
comments.text: Full child bead set filed 2026-07-13 from docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md; re-sequencing: U0 spike (cairn-cz3q.4) -> Track P PSBT verification (cairn-cz3q.11, .12) + Track B peer-auth hardening (cairn-cz3q.5-.10) -> Track C manifest (cairn-cz3q.13-.15) -> Track D unification (cairn-cz3q.16-.19) -> Track E (cairn-cz3q.20-.22). The unification keystone (C1 / cairn-cz3q.13) must land AFTER P1 verification (cairn-cz3q.11) and U0 transport (cairn-cz3q.4) or it locks in vulnerabilities.

title: Federation feature flag + gated nav tab scaffold
description: Add a federation feature flag to src/lib/server/featureFlags/registry.ts (one-line append, category 'wallet', defaultEnabled: true per registry constraint), a 'Federation' nav entry in src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte (the $derived nav array, lines ~29-44, gated flags.federation !== false, plus an Icon.svelte name), and a stub route subtree src/routes/(app)/federation/ with requireFeature(event,'federation') enforcement (src/lib/server/api.ts:99). NOTE: MobileTabRow only shows 4 destinations -- decide whether Federation replaces one, folds under Wallets, or extends the strip; document the choice in the bead when implementing. DESIGN CONSTRAINT (epic cairn-cz3q): UI-first -- the tab ships with real UI, never API-only. Full context: docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md.

comments.author: Fable-orchestrator
comments.text: A3 scope note (from docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md, filed alongside the full federation bead set 2026-07-13): flag 'federation' governs UI only (must ship defaultEnabled:true); a SEPARATE admin setting federation_enabled (default OFF) gates ALL networking (routes 503 / onion unpublished / watcher not started / no outbound when OFF); acceptance criterion = a test asserting /api/federation/* returns 503 when the setting is OFF regardless of flag state (security finding F15). J0 = settings/federation transport-picker wizard + identity card (.onion + fingerprint QR).

title: B4: federation audit log + Tor-safe rate limiting
description: Audit every action (ts, direction, peer, action, result, detail; xfp redaction in console). Rate limits always-on (independent of regtest auto-disable). SECURITY: Tor collapses IP-based limiting (all requests from loopback) -- use invite-gated handshake + global handshake cap w/ backoff, don't rely on client IP (F13). See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §2, §7.

title: P1: cosigner-side independent PSBT verification (anti-blind-signing)
description: THE headline security fix (F1). Before showing 'Sign', the cosigner's instance MUST independently: match each PSBT input to a LOCALLY-held vault descriptor (not the coordinator-pushed one), classify outputs as vault-change vs external using its own keys, render amount+fee from decoded PSBT bytes only, cap fee. Reuse existing src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts summarizePsbt() + validateRecipientsAndFeeRate() + hostilePsbts.test.ts. Without this, a malicious coordinator drains a cosigner. Blocks Track D unification. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §5.3, §7.

title: P2: validated signature counting
description: countSignatures must count only partialSigs whose pubkey is a descriptor cosigner for that input (F9); prevents junk-sig false 'complete' -> failed broadcast and corrupts the authoritative count. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §5.3, §7.

title: C1: multisig_keys.holder_* columns + cosigner-source picker
description: The UNIFICATION KEYSTONE. Extend multisig_keys with holder_type ('self'|'local_user'|'remote_peer') + holder_local_user_id + holder_peer_id. Extend the existing multisig create wizard (wallets/multisig/) with a per-key-slot source picker: connected peer / my key / paste xpub. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §3, §5.2, §5.3, §6 (J3).

title: C2: vault-manifest push + auto-create/accept + descriptor verify
description: D3: coordinator pushes a manifest; cosigner's instance auto-creates the matching multisig row (origin='remote') + notification; cosigner must EXPLICITLY accept, only from an already-handshaked peer, byte-identical descriptor + BIP-380 checksum verified both sides. SECURITY: accepted descriptor immutable, differing manifest = fresh accept, identical = idempotent no-op (F7); accept UX shows M-of-N + all xfps, requires user's OWN xfp present, flags look-alike names (F8). See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §5.1, §6, §7.

title: C3: non-skippable Recovery Kit gate
description: Vault not 'ready to fund' until creator downloads Caravan JSON (must round-trip) + key-holder map + plain-language 'what to do if a cosigner disappears' doc. Directly mitigates the funds-locked-on-disappearance risk (raffle-debate precedent). See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §6 (J3), §9 Q5.

title: D1: multisig_transactions federation columns + psbtSession.ts
description: Add origin/origin_peer_id/origin_ref/federation_session_uuid + broadcast/cancelled statuses. Unified submit/sign/collect service, DIRECT STAR topology (no multi-hop relay -- D1 delta), byte-identical-unsigned-PSBT dedupe (Bastion's IDOR mitigation). SECURITY: authorization-gate /psbt/submit -- reject unless descriptor fingerprints intersect a key we hold; cap open sessions per peer; enforce session TTL (F14). Blocked by Track P (cairn-cz3q.11, cairn-cz3q.12). See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §5.1, §5.2, §5.3, §7.

title: D2: federation PSBT endpoints (submit / get / sign)
description: src/routes/api/federation/psbt/{submit,[id],[id]/sign}; session auth = origin peer OR peer holding inbound key for a descriptor fingerprint; all failures 404 (no existence leak). Peer-token auth. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §5.4, §7.

title: D3: cosigner status strip + plain-language remote-review screen
description: Status strip in the Send flow ('You checked-in - Bob waiting'); J5 remote review renders amount/destination/fee FROM PSBT bytes (per P1/F1), Sign or Decline. Reuse Amount.svelte. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §6 (J4/J5).

title: D4: broadcast/cancel status wiring + federation.* notify types + peer fan-out
description: Wire the dead broadcast/cancelled statuses (D4 delta); append notify event types federation.{invite_accepted,vault_invite,vault_ready,signature_requested,signature_received,declined,broadcast,peer_offline} to the union + DEFAULT_PREFERENCES; broadcast fan-out to peers via existing notify()->SSE. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §5.1, §5.4, §6 (J6).

title: DECISION: backup/export policy for federation e-cash secrets (bearer instruments)
description: Open product decision, blocks federation wallet implementation. Cairn's backup.ts (src/lib/server/backup.ts, BackupData lines 37-49) and accountData.ts deliberately exclude instance_secrets from backups -- safe for xpub-based wallets (nothing spendable is lost), but Fedimint e-cash notes/client seed ARE the funds (bearer instruments). Excluding them means backup != recoverable funds for federation balances, unlike every other wallet type. Options to evaluate: (a) include encrypted federation secrets in backup with explicit user consent + passphrase, (b) rely on Fedimint seed-based recovery from the federation itself (verify recovery guarantees first), (c) hybrid. Needs Alex's call. Also decide per-user/per-federation secrets storage: instance_secrets is a global k/v store and does not fit; likely new table federation_secrets(user_id, federation_id, key, value_enc) reusing encryptSecret/decryptSecret from src/lib/server/secretKey.ts with a new HKDF info label (structural template: src/lib/server/deviceKeys.ts).
close_reason: obsolete — scope changed 2026-07-12, Fedimint dropped; federation = inter-instance PSBT coordination (Bastion model)

title: E1: federationWatcher.ts
description: From hooks.server.ts init(), try/catch-isolated, unref'd, idempotent (template addressWatcher.ts): retry queued outbound pushes w/ backoff, bump last_seen, expire stale sessions (drives F14 TTL), raise federation.peer_offline. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §5.4, §7.

title: E2: sneakernet fallback + failure-state UIs
description: Every federated step offers 'send this manually instead' (PSBT file/QR export); J7 failure UIs: peer offline (queue+retry+sneakernet), cosigner declined (signed decline), cosigner disappeared (N-day banner -> Recovery Kit migration path). See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §6 (J7).

title: E3: backup/restore additions + re-handshake-on-restore
description: Add federation_peers/peer_keys/invites + new multisig columns to backup.ts BackupData; tokens are bearer secrets (encrypt + require re-handshake on restore); federation_seed stays excluded; honest restore messaging ('lost instance = re-handshake, funds safe'). See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §5.4, §7 (F16).

title: Phase 2: application-layer PSBT encryption (libsodium box to peer ed25519 keys)
description: Mandatory before ANY supported clearnet transport; the hard gate that lets Tailscale/clearnet graduate from 'advanced/unsupported'. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §5.1 (D2), §8.

title: Phase 2: federation identity export/recovery
description: Explicit encrypted federation-identity export so a restored instance keeps its instanceId/peerings. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §7 (F16), §8, §9 Q4.

title: Phase 2: broadcast self-verification (F11)
description: Peers verify the finalized txid equals what they signed via their OWN node, not the coordinator's claim. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §7, §8.

title: Phase 3+: relay/mesh, Nostr wakeup signaling, Iroh transport
description: Deferred transport/topology expansion; revisit if Tor UX proves painful. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §4, §8.

title: DECISION Q1/Q2: onion spike outcome -> does app-layer encryption move into MVP?
description: Tied to U0 (cairn-cz3q.4). If umbrelOS per-app onion is gone, D2's no-encryption justification collapses and Phase-2 encryption bead (cairn-cz3q.23) moves into MVP. Recommended default: verify first; if onion fails, pull encryption forward. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §9.

title: DECISION Q3: testnet-only MVP vs mainnet-with-warnings?
description: Recommended default: testnet-steered in-product, mainnet gated behind explicit acknowledgement. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §9.

title: DECISION Q6: sign-request encryption vs bearer tokens (F16) -- encrypt-at-rest, signed-requests, or both?
description: Recommended default: both. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §9.

title: DECISION: fedimint client packaging -- sidecar container vs embedded client
description: Open architecture decision, blocks most federation implementation beads. Cairn is a single Node 22 process with no child-process/sidecar precedent; its pattern for external services is probing sibling Umbrel apps over LAN (umbrelProbe.ts / umbrelCoreProbe.ts, hooks.server.ts init lines ~165-192). Options: (a) fedimint-clientd (REST/JSON-RPC daemon, github.com/fedimint/fedimint-clientd -- alive, default modules only) as a separate container -- either a second container in the heartwood-bitcoin Umbrel package or a dependency app; (b) @fedimint/core (official Fedimint SDK npm package, WASM -- formerly @fedimint/core-web) embedded in the SvelteKit server IF Node.js server-side support is verified (claimed 'multiple JS runtimes' since Oct 2025 rename, unproven -- needs a spike); (c) browser-side @fedimint/core (client state in IndexedDB per browser -- conflicts with Cairn's server-side multi-user model). Research verdict pending in docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md. Deciding this fixes where client lifecycle code lives (hooks.server.ts watcher vs container orchestration in heartwood-app-store repo).
close_reason: obsolete — scope changed 2026-07-12, Fedimint dropped; federation = inter-instance PSBT coordination (Bastion model)

title: DECISION Q8: max quorum/parties for MVP?
description: Recommended default: cap at small N (<=5) for family/friends. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §9.

title: U0: umbrelOS per-app onion verification + transport allowlist spike
description: Verify whether umbrelOS 1.x still exposes a per-app .onion address to an app container (historically APP_*_HIDDEN_SERVICE via exports.sh) -- UNVERIFIED and a hard ship-blocker for Tor-as-default transport. Deliverable: confirmed mechanism to obtain/publish the instance's .onion, OR a documented fallback (manual torrc / Tailscale interim default). Also implement the transport allowlist: MVP must HARD-REFUSE any peer baseUrl that is not .onion (reject http:// and clearnet; loopback only for tests) -- this lands security findings F12 (no-encryption bet rests on onion) and F17 (SSRF via attacker-controlled baseUrl; block RFC1918/loopback/link-local). Everything downstream depends on this outcome. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §4, §7, §10.

title: A1: ed25519 federation identity module
description: federation_seed via getOrCreateSecret + new HKDF label (instance_secrets), instanceId = sha256(pubkey) -- widen to [:32]/128-bit per security finding F6 rather than Bastion's [:16]. Reuse encryptSecret/decryptSecret; template deviceKeys.ts. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §2, §5.2.

title: A2: transport.ts URL abstraction + onion provisioning
description: Opaque peer baseUrl so the push model works over any URL, but enforce the U0 .onion-only allowlist. Onion provisioning/verification per U0. Blocked by U0 (cairn-cz3q.4). See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §4, §5.4.

title: B1: federation_peers + /handshake + per-direction token auth
description: Port Bastion handshake (ed25519 signed nonce, per-direction bearer tokens, re-handshake rotates both, constant-time compare, X-Federation-Instance/-Token headers). SECURITY DELTAS vs Bastion: inbound handshake lands 'pending' unless it matches an outstanding invite you issued -- never auto-active strangers (F4, P0); encrypt token columns at rest and/or sign requests with ed25519 identity (F16); eviction = block/tombstone not bare delete (F5). See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §2, §5.2, §7.

title: B2: invite engine + invite/accept UI + peer list
description: Hash-only single-use codes, atomic redemption, reciprocal key exchange, portable base64url blob. SECURITY DELTAS: bind inviter's ed25519 pubkey (or sha256) into the blob and pin on accept (F2); mandatory out-of-band xfp fingerprint confirmation in accept UX + short TTL (F3). UI = J1 invite wizard + J2 accept/add-peer + peer list w/ status. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §2, §6, §7.

title: B3: share-key endpoint + federation_peer_keys routing map
description: Inbound keys table (peer_id, xfp, xpub, label); 404-not-403 on unshared-equiv-nonexistent; the xfp-to-peer routing map that drives PSBT push. See docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md §2, §5.2.

title: multisigScan.ts 11 of 12 exports untested including spend-flow data
description: multisigScan.ts — only multisigAddressAt tested, 11 exports untested including getMultisigUtxos (coin+chain/index attribution feeding constructMultisigPsbt — wrong attribution = unsignable PSBT or mis-identified change), nextMultisigChangeIndex (change derivation index for every multisig spend), nextMultisigReceiveAddress/clampToGap (gap-window handout clamp). Fix: add comprehensive multisigScan tests for UTXO attribution, change index, and receive address clamping.
close_reason: Covered by src/lib/server/multisigScan.edge.test.ts (324 lines, merged this wave from qa/edge-btc-2026-07-12, commit d387d1e): UTXO attribution, change index, and receive-address gap clamping all now exercised -- matches the bead's prescribed fix.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: This wave (qa-2026-07-12b) merged src/lib/server/multisigScan.edge.test.ts (324 lines, from qa/edge-btc-2026-07-12, commit d387d1e): covers getMultisigUtxos attribution, nextMultisigChangeIndex, and nextMultisigReceiveAddress/clampToGap per this bead's prescribed fix. Closing with this evidence.

title: PSBT construction hardcodes mainnet NETWORK — regtest send fails after validation passes
description: Follow-up to cairn-xqnn7 (eaaf0b2): deriveAddress/validation are network-aware via networkParams(), but constructPsbt/constructMultisigPsbt still use the @scure mainnet NETWORK constant (tx.addOutputAddress, p2wpkh, Address(NETWORK).encode) — on regtest/testnet, validation accepts bcrt1 but construction fails downstream.
close_reason: Fixed: threaded ChainNetwork through constructPsbt (psbt.ts) and constructMultisigPsbt (multisigPsbt.ts) via networkParams(network ?? getDefaultNetwork()), replacing every hardcoded @scure/btc-signer NETWORK (mainnet) use in tx.addOutputAddress/p2wpkh/p2wsh/p2sh/Address(...).encode — construction now matches the network-aware validation from cairn-xqnn7 (eaaf0b2). Also added ConstructParams.network / MultisigConstructParams.network overrides, threaded network through deriveMultisigAddress calls, and made addressFromScript/summarizePsbt network-aware for correct bcrt1/tb1 rendering on resume. Regression tests added: psbt.test.ts + multisigPsbt.test.ts 'network threading' blocks build real regtest/testnet/mainnet PSBTs end-to-end via constructPsbt/constructMultisigPsbt and verify summarizePsbt round-trips the addresses. npm run check clean; full psbt/multisig/xpub/sendBoundary/malformedSendInputs/hostilePsbts/coinbaseScan vitest suites green (400+ tests).

title: Multi-horizon balance delta + lazy fiat repaint (R6)
description:
DESIGN-MANIFESTO.md sec 5 'Notification & delta-display rules (MUST)': No naked
point-deltas -- any value-change display must show 1d/30d/1yr/all-time TOGETHER
so a red/down day can't dominate the emotional read. Lead growth stories with
percent framing ('+8% this month'); absolute sats one layer down. Down is
neutral, never red -- only up gets --sage.

Also sec 5 motion rules + F1 (docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-RESEARCH-2026-07-15.md R6):
fiat secondary line must repaint on NAVIGATION only, never live-tick -- every
repaint is a fresh loss-aversion evaluation event (myopic loss aversion,
Benartzi & Thaler).

Gap: Home Phase-1 (cairn-gt05.1) shipped the balance hero without the
multi-horizon delta the manifesto already mandated. portfolio.ts computed
change.d1/d7/d30 but nothing in the UI ever rendered it. Wallet-detail's hero
Amount had no price override, so it defaulted to the live-ticking \$btcUsd
store (60s auto-refresh) -- a second manifesto violation on the same surface
list as R6 (Home + wallet detail).

Scope:
- Extend portfolio.ts's changesFromSeries to 1d/30d/1yr/all-time (was 1d/7d/30d)
- New shared pure module lib/horizonDelta.ts: HorizonChange type, the cutoff-walk
  computation, an honest per-wallet tx-based reconstruction (wallet detail has
  no balance_snapshots wired to its loader; reuse the same trusted delta-walk
  technique WalletStepChart/portfolio.ts backfill already use), and a shared
  buildHorizonRows renderer (percent-led, sats one layer down via title attr,
  sage-only-on-up, neutral on down/flat, honest '--' when a horizon has no data)
- New BalanceHorizons.svelte, wired into Home (portfolio.change) and wallet
  detail (client-derived from chainData.scan.txs)
- Home's fiat was already lazy (one-shot fetch per mount/nav) -- confirmed, no
  change needed. Wallet-detail's hero Amount gets the same one-shot
  privacy-gated snapshot pattern Home already uses, replacing the default live
  \$btcUsd subscription.
close_reason:
Shipped: multi-horizon balance delta (1d/30d/1yr/all-time, MUST) + lazy fiat
snapshot on Home and both wallet-detail surfaces.

Evidence (live QA against uxr6-dev on http://uxr6.localhost:5931, seeded via
scripts/qa/seed-r6-horizons.mjs):
- Home: GET /api/portfolio returned change={d1:0,d30:-6000000,d365:null,all:-6000000}
  computed by the REAL server pipeline (backfillSnapshots -> recordSnapshot ->
  getBalanceSeries -> changesFromHorizonSeries), not a stub. Rendered as
  "1D 0% / 30D -8.8% / 1YR -- / ALL TIME -8.8%", all four together, all in
  neutral --text-secondary (rgb(155,166,161)) since none were growth -- verified
  down/flat/unknown never render red or amber.
- Wallet detail (/wallets/1, client-derived from scan.txs via historyFromTxDeltas):
  "1D 0% / 30D -8.8% / 1YR +24.0% / ALL TIME +24.0%" -- the two positive horizons
  rendered in rgb(131,184,146) = --sage exactly, confirming the up-only-green rule
  end to end.
- hideBalance toggle correctly suppresses the whole horizon row (privacy: a delta
  leaks wealth-change magnitude even with the total masked).
- Mobile 375px: wallet-detail's .balance-horizons stayed at containerRight=290px,
  no overflow (shared component with Home).
- Fiat: confirmed via source inspection (no setInterval in either page's local
  fetchPrice) AND live network trace -- 0 requests to /api/price fired while
  cairn.fiat was off; the default $btcUsd live-ticking store is only reachable
  when a caller omits Amount's `price` prop, which neither hero does anymore.

Tests: 11 new horizonDelta.test.ts cases + updated portfolio.test.ts/
portfolioSnapshot.test.ts for the d1/d30/d365/all shape. Full suite: 3693 passed,
1 pre-existing skip, 0 failures. svelte-check: 0 errors.

Gap flagged (not a bug, pre-existing): BACKFILL_HORIZON_MS (396d) sits inside
dataRetention's ~13-month purge window, so a backfilled carry-in point dated
right at that edge can be swept before the next read -- observed live in this
QA run (Home's 1yr horizon fell back to "--" while wallet-detail's client-side
reconstruction, unaffected by retention, correctly showed +24.0%). Both are
honest given their data source; not fixed here, out of R6's scope.

title: [pool-ux] No block-found celebration moment in the dashboard UI
description: Evidence: scope item 8 in docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md promised a celebration UI for block_found; what shipped is a notification row (mining_block_found, title You found a block! in src/lib/server/mining/index.ts:393) and a new line in the earnings list. Grep for celebration/confetti/congrat in src/ finds nothing mining-related. The rarest, most valuable event the product can ever deliver renders identically to a routine list update. ckpools entire brand is built on the lucky-block moment; those stories recruit hash. Proposed fix (plain language): when a block lands, /mining (already refetched immediately via the block-found live nudge) should show an unmistakable hero moment - e.g. a one-time full-width You found block N - reward is on its way to <wallet> banner with the maturity countdown, persisted until dismissed, with tasteful motion within the manifestos celebrates-accretion motion rules. Consider a shareable summary (block height, reward, pool tag) since found blocks are the pools best marketing.

title: Chain health signal conflates all pool members
description: chainHealth.ts:59-70 + pool.ts:39-43 — recordChainOk/recordChainError called from every pooled client, not just primary. Transient secondary failure flips global 'cant reach Bitcoin network' banner even if primary is fine. Fix: only primary connection should drive the health banner, or use majority-vote logic.
close_reason: Fixed. Added ElectrumClientOptions.reportsHealth (default true, for backward compat) to electrum/client.ts, gating both recordChainOk() and recordChainError() call sites in ensureConnected(). ElectrumPool (pool.ts) now constructs every client but its primary with reportsHealth:false, so a transient secondary-socket blip can no longer flip the global 'cant reach Bitcoin network' banner while the primary (and the real network) are fine -- addresses the exact chainHealth.ts/pool.ts conflation described in the bead. Also extended the same fix to two related throwaway-probe call sites that shared the same root-cause bug (an admin/candidate-server probe polluting the REAL banner): chain/index.ts's testElectrum() (admin Test Connection button) and umbrelProbe.ts's probeOne() (Umbrel zero-config candidate-IP probe) now both pass reportsHealth:false too, mirroring the existing Core-RPC precedent where the admin Core test probe was already deliberately excluded from getCoreHealth(). Added 4 new tests in client.test.ts (reportsHealth (cairn-d8aa) describe: default-true records OK/error, false suppresses both) plus MANUAL.md doc update. client/pool/chainHealth/umbrelProbe/chain/nodeTrust test suites: 176/176 pass.

title: Parallelize getTx's outspends fetch + stream stateless-signer page's fees
description:
Scoped by Fable consultation as the actual remainder of cairn-xlrm after
auditing every page route (see cairn-xlrm comment). Two small, independent
fixes:

1. src/lib/server/chain/index.ts:316-330, ChainService.getTx(): tx+tipHeight
   already fetch in Promise.all, but getTxOutspends() is awaited SEQUENTIALLY
   afterward in its own try/catch. This is the only awaited-on-paint chain
   call left in the app (explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.server.ts:76, deliberately
   awaited per cairn-2zxt.3 since it drives a 302-vs-404 routing decision) --
   folding outspends into the same Promise.all (with the existing
   catch-to-undefined degradation preserved) cuts it from 2 sequential esplora
   HTTP round trips to 1, on a Pi where each hop to a co-resident mempool
   instance can be 100ms-1s+ under load. Also speeds up api/tx/[txid] and
   explorer's classifySearch (which calls full getTx just to test existence).
   Update chain.test.ts mocks if any assert call ordering.

2. src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/stateless/+page.server.ts:15-20: fees
   are awaited synchronously (`fees = await getChain().getFeeEstimates()`),
   the last load in the app gating paint on a chain call it doesn't need --
   every other page with a fee estimate already streams it. Copy the send
   page's existing pattern exactly (src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte
   has a local `fees = $state<FeeEstimates | null>(null)` populated from an
   $effect that awaits the streamed promise): return
   `fees: getChain().getFeeEstimates().catch(() => null)` unawaited from the
   server load, and adapt +page.svelte's `data.fees?.economy` /
   `data.fees?.halfHour` / `data.fees?.fastest` / `{#if !data.fees}` references
   (lines ~176-182, 662, 694) to read from a local $state var populated the
   same way the send page does it.

Verified non-issues, don't re-investigate: ElectrumPool size (round-robin
dispatch, no checkout/exhaustion possible), the 15s connection timeout
(abandoned requests don't block new writes), and every OTHER page route
(already fully covered by the cairn-vknb streaming pass).

After landing, close cairn-xlrm referencing this bead + the premise-drift
note already on it.
close_reason: done

title: Wallet-import finish step can 200 with no wallet created and no error shown
description: POST /wallets/new returned 200 with no wallet row and no error banner when the hidden `name` field (bound to Svelte state) was empty because the visible input's binding never fired. Likely automation-only trigger (real keystrokes bind fine), but the silent 200-with-no-creation and missing user-facing error on the unhappy path matches the known silent-failure theme (audit-2026-07-05). File: src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte finish step + its action.
close_reason: Fixed at 9628a70, root cause was different than the automation theory but real: src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte's Finish-step form had TWO name="name" inputs — a hidden one one-way-bound to the name state (line 1299, removed) and the visible text field, which had NO name attribute of its own (only id="name") and so never submitted directly; it relied entirely on Svelte reactivity re-syncing the hidden field whenever the bound state changed. Removed the redundant hidden input and added name="name" straight onto the visible field, so native FormData now reads the actual DOM input value on submit regardless of whether Svelte's binding fired. Verified createWallet() (src/lib/server/wallets.ts:330-335) already auto-names an empty submission ('Wallet N') rather than silently no-op'ing, so the exact '200 with literally no wallet row' symptom as described doesn't reproduce against current code (pre-dates the bead, part of the original release) — but the duplicate-name-attribute landmine that prompted the report was real and is now gone rather than relying on Svelte reactivity always holding. svelte-check 0 errors; page.server.test.ts (3 tests) still green; dev-server HMR compiled clean with no errors.

title: address_book flag OFF doesn't hide saved-address UI in send flow
description:
Found during cairn-1ytc flag-matrix QA pass (wallet-flags subset, port 5314, HEARTWOOD_DB=data/qa-matrix.db).

REPRO: Admin -> Feature flags -> toggle Address book OFF (address_book flag). As a plain user, visit /wallets/[id]/send. The RecipientCombobox still renders normally with no flag-off messaging; GET /api/address-book correctly 403s (server enforcement intact), but nothing on the client hides or disables the saved-address autocomplete/save-new-address UI.

ROOT CAUSE (code-read confirmed):
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.server.ts:167 calls `savedAddresses: listSavedAddresses(locals.user!.id)` unconditionally in load() -- no `requireFeature(event, 'address_book')` guard anywhere in this file (only `requireFeature(event, 'send')` at line 126).
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:162 sets `savedAddresses = data.savedAddresses` and passes it straight into <RecipientCombobox saved={savedAddresses} .../> (lines ~912, ~949) with no `data.flags?.address_book` check anywhere in the file.
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/_components/RecipientCombobox.svelte has a proper flag gate for qr_scan (`const flagEnabled = page.data.flags?.qr_scan !== false;` at line 144) but NO equivalent check for address_book anywhere -- the saved-address list and (per code at +page.svelte:650, `savedAddresses = [body.address as SavedAddress, ...savedAddresses]`) the save-new-address affordance are never gated client-side.

Same pattern likely affects the multisig send flow (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/send/+page.svelte) -- not yet checked, worth a look when fixing.

IMPACT: with address_book turned off instance-wide, a user with existing saved addresses would still see and be able to select them from the send-flow autocomplete (the data only isn't reachable via the API if they navigate to a dedicated address-book page/route). This is the same class of regression the parent bead cairn-1ytc calls out ("History shows this area regresses: cairn-8dup, cairn-1x3w, cairn-jyh7") -- UI not following the flag even though the API is correctly gated.

FIX (not done here -- flagged only, owned by another session per current branch ownership): add a `data.flags?.address_book` check in +page.svelte (and the +page.server.ts load, mirroring the coin_control pattern at +page.svelte:1003) to hide the saved-address list/autocomplete and any save-new-address control when the flag is off, plus check the equivalent multisig send flow file.

Filed by: OPS QA session (cairn-1ytc wallet-flags subset), 2026-07-12.
close_reason: Fixed at root cause: wallets/[id]/send/+page.server.ts load() now returns savedAddresses: [] (without calling listSavedAddresses) when locals.flags.address_book === false, instead of always handing the full list to the client. +page.svelte's post-broadcast 'save this address' offer (showSaveOffer) now also requires the new addressBookEnabled derived (data.flags?.address_book !== false). Checked the multisig send flow per the bead's suggestion: it passes saved={[]} unconditionally already (no address book there at all, per its own comment) — not affected, no fix needed. Regression test: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/page.server.test.ts (3 tests, vitest green). MANUAL.md updated.

title: 9 pre-existing vitest failures at 9098d92: deleteCascade trigger sweep, dust-boundary sweep rejection (546), wallet/multisig id-collision
description: Found by Fix&Build session 2026-07-19 while gating v0.2.42: at base 9098d92 (and unchanged by the v0.2.42 wave — none of the failing tests' subject files differ), 9 tests fail across 4 files: src/lib/server/deleteCascade.test.ts (3: 'every discovered non-exempt table is swept by a trigger', direct-delete orphans, sibling isolation), src/lib/server/sendBoundaryDraft.test.ts (1: sweep of exactly 546 sats should reject with PsbtError but no error is thrown), src/lib/server/bitcoin/sendBoundaryMatrix2.test.ts (4: same dust-ceiling rejection across input counts/fee rates), src/lib/server/walletMultisigIdCollision.test.ts (1: dual-kind seed/delete at colliding id). deleteCascade tests were green when cairn-97ui closed 2026-07-08 (1839 tests passing), so this is a later regression — plausibly the delete-cascade trigger set not covering newer tables (mining_*? notification tables?) and a dust-boundary behavior change in draft building. May alternatively be failing-first tests armed by the concurrent QA session — coordinate before fixing. Repro: npx vitest run --project node src/lib/server/deleteCascade.test.ts src/lib/server/sendBoundaryDraft.test.ts
close_reason: Triage complete, all 9 green. Two distinct causes, NEITHER a v0.2.42-wave regression: (1) 5 dust tests in sendBoundaryMatrix2/sendBoundaryDraft were STALE — cairn-7ld60/2be1902 (in v0.2.41) intentionally moved sweeps to per-script-type dustThreshold (P2WPKH 294) and updated only sendBoundaryMatrix.test.ts; the two sibling suites still asserted flat-546. Updated to 294/295 boundaries mirroring 2be1902's pattern. (2) 4 cascade/collision failures were REAL: backup_nudges shipped in v0.2.41 without trigger coverage — fixed as cairn-o0dhu. Note: full-suite runs concurrent with the sibling QA session's vitest runs flake on timing-sensitive tests (rate-limit etc.) from CPU contention; failing files pass in isolation — serialize full-suite gates across sessions.

title: Investigate raw-tsc false positive on chainEpochs.ts ChainEpoch import
description: npx tsc --noEmit reports TS2614 at src/lib/server/chainEpochs.ts:22 for 'import type { ChainEpoch } from $lib/components/heartwood/ChainStrip.svelte' (no exported member ChainEpoch). NOT reproduced by the project's actual gate (npm run check / svelte-check), which resolves the .svelte named type export correctly via its language-service plugin -- 0 errors on this file. Pre-existing: file unchanged since 37b5c6d (heartwood foundation), untouched by the 2026-07-14 wave. Likely a benign divergence between svelte-check's svelte-aware module resolution and vanilla tsc's ambient '*.svelte' module declaration. Confirm harmless (or add an explicit type re-export / .d.ts so plain tsc also resolves it) and close.
close_reason: Confirmed the tsc TS2614 false positive AND fixed it (not just documented): moved the ChainEpoch type out of ChainStrip.svelte's <script module> block into a new plain-.ts sibling, src/lib/components/heartwood/ChainStrip.types.ts. Root cause: vanilla tsc resolves .svelte imports via the generic ambient 'declare module *.svelte' shim, which only models a component's default export — it can't see a <script module> named export, hence TS2614 on ChainEpoch. svelte-check's language-service plugin processes each .svelte file directly and resolves it fine, which is why the project's real gate (npm run check / svelte-check) was always clean. Fix: chainEpochs.ts now imports ChainEpoch from ChainStrip.types.ts instead of ChainStrip.svelte; ChainStrip.svelte re-exports the same type from that file so its own import surface (anyone doing ) is unchanged. Zero runtime behavior change (type-only). Evidence: npx tsc --noEmit now reports ZERO errors (previously exactly this one). npx svelte-check --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json: 0 errors, 0 warnings (1995 files, same as before). npx vitest run: 3820 passed, 1 skipped (pre-existing, needs live regtest stack) — no change from the pre-fix baseline.

title: Notification registry/settings coverage gap: mining types unroutable, multisig_removed/cosigner_left invisible in settings UI
description:
Emitted-but-unregistered mining event types: `mining_block_found`, `mining_worker_offline`, `mining_best_share` are declared in the canonical event-type registry (src/lib/server/notifyTypes.ts:35-37) and are actively emitted via `notify()` from src/lib/server/mining/index.ts (lines 333, 390, 410, 435, 512 — confirmed via grep, e.g. the `mining_best_share` call at line 332-339). But none of the three have an entry in `DEFAULT_PREFERENCES` in src/lib/server/notifications.ts:50-73 (the map ends at `security_new_device`, no `mining_*` keys). `enabledExternalChannels()` (notifications.ts:92-124) builds its default set from `DEFAULT_PREFERENCES[eventType] ?? []` (line 113) — for an event type with no entry, `defaults` is always empty, so every external channel (email/telegram/ntfy/nostr/webhook) evaluates to disabled unless the user has an explicit saved preference row. Since these three types also have no row in the Settings UI (see below), a user has no UI path to ever create that saved row. Net effect: mining notifications are in-app only, permanently, with no way for a user to route them to email/ntfy/etc.

Routed-but-invisible-in-settings-UI types: `multisig_removed` and `cosigner_left` ARE registered in `DEFAULT_PREFERENCES` (notifications.ts:60-61, both `['inapp']`) and are actively fired from src/lib/server/userDeletion.ts (lines 198 and 262). But src/routes/(app)/settings/notifications/+page.svelte's `GROUPS` array (lines 293-330, the "Wallet activity" / "Security" / "Admin" event catalogue rendered as settings rows) has no entry for either type — grep across the file for `multisig_removed`/`cosigner_left` matches nothing in GROUPS. Users can never see, discover, or manage delivery preferences for these two events even though the backend fully wires them to the in-app feed and would honor any preference row if one existed.

Combined impact: 5 of 25 registered notification event types (mining_block_found, mining_worker_offline, mining_best_share, multisig_removed, cosigner_left) are invisible in Settings > Notifications. For the 2 multisig types this means no discoverability/no control (in-app delivery still happens, user just can't see the toggle or opt into external channels). For the 3 mining types this means in-app-only delivery is silently permanent — there is no code path today to route a personal-best share or a block-found event to email/ntfy/telegram/etc, which is a meaningful gap for events users likely want push/external delivery for (mining_block_found especially — "your miner found a block" is exactly the kind of thing you want a push notification for, not just an in-app feed item).

Origin: found during v0.2.38 QA campaign code recon (wave 1), 2026-07-19. Not verified against live UI screenshots — this is a static code-registry finding; a browser QA pass would corroborate but this bead only needs the code evidence to be actionable.

Suggested fix shape (not yet scoped): add mining_block_found/mining_worker_offline/mining_best_share to DEFAULT_PREFERENCES (likely inapp only by default, but let users opt into external), and add all 5 types (mining_* x3, multisig_removed, cosigner_left) as rows in the GROUPS catalogue in +page.svelte, probably as a new "Mining" group plus filling the existing "Wallet activity" group for the two multisig types.
close_reason: Added mining_block_found/mining_worker_offline/mining_best_share to DEFAULT_PREFERENCES (notifications.ts) so external delivery can be enabled; added Settings UI rows for multisig_removed, cosigner_left (Wallet activity group) and all 3 mining types (new Mining group) in +page.svelte; audited all notify() emit sites vs NOTIFICATION_EVENT_TYPES vs DEFAULT_PREFERENCES vs Settings GROUPS -- all 25 canonical types now covered in both, no other stragglers found; added regression test asserting NOTIFICATION_EVENT_TYPES == Object.keys(DEFAULT_PREFERENCES) in notifications.test.ts. npm run check clean (0 errors), vitest notifications.test.ts 14/14 pass, related mining/userDeletion/queue suites 111/111 pass.

title: Broadcast has a check-then-act race on the already-sent guard
description:
src/lib/server/transactions.ts broadcastTransaction(): reads the transaction row and checks status/txid (lines 263-266), then later writes the resulting txid back (lines 310-314) -- with a real await (the Electrum broadcast call) in between and no database transaction wrapping the read-check-write sequence. Two concurrent requests to POST /api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/broadcast (double-click bypassing the client-side disable, a retried request, or two tabs) can both read txid=null, both pass the already-sent check, and both broadcast.

Scoped correctly: this is NOT a double-spend-different-funds risk, since assertSameTransaction still guarantees both concurrent requests would be broadcasting the identical reviewed transaction (same inputs/outputs) -- worst case is submitting the same tx to the network twice (which Electrum/Core typically just reject as already-known) and a confusing DB-write race on which response the user's browser sees. Still worth closing: wrap the read-check-write in a single database transaction (or an UPDATE ... WHERE txid IS NULL returning affected-row-count as the atomic guard) so only one of two concurrent requests can ever proceed past the check.
close_reason: Fixed in 8717710: guarded UPDATE claim (txid IS NULL + no live claim, 60s stale expiry) before the Electrum await; loser gets already_sent; failed broadcast releases the claim. Concurrent-race test proves exactly one network call.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified fixed (commit 8717710, atomic guarded UPDATE claim before the network await). Per S5's specific ask (confirm it actually blocks a rapid double-click, not just narrows the window):

Read the dedicated concurrency test ("lets exactly one of two concurrent broadcasts through (atomic claim)") -- it's a rigorous simulation, not a token check: it parks the FIRST broadcast call's mocked Electrum send on an unresolved promise (so the first call has already run the atomic claim UPDATE and is now "in flight" exactly like a real slow network call), then fires the second call while the first is still pending. Second call correctly gets rejected with already_sent even though no txid has been written yet -- that's the actual race condition, not a simplified version of it. First call then completes normally when its network promise resolves.

Could not reproduce this via a genuine rapid double-click through the live browser UI -- reaching the real atomic-claim code path requires a fully-signed, finalizable PSBT, and (same recurring constraint) no wallet in this dev environment has both a real spendable balance and a known private key to produce one. The unit test's simulation is faithful to the real race though: claim-then-await-network is exactly the code path being exercised, with a genuinely unresolved promise standing in for the slow network call.

Also confirmed the failure-path claim release (UPDATE ... SET broadcast_started_at = NULL on a failed broadcast) has its own passing test ensuring a failed broadcast stays retryable, and the 60s stale-claim expiry is in the WHERE clause for crash recovery. Full suite: 301/301 passing.

title: OP_RETURN output labeled 'Unspent' — should be Unspendable/N-A
description:
On a transaction detail page, an OP_RETURN output is shown with status "Unspent", the same label used for genuinely spendable-but-not-yet-spent outputs. OP_RETURN outputs are provably unspendable by consensus rules, so labeling one "Unspent" is technically incorrect and actively misleading for an educational explorer — a user could reasonably conclude the funds there could still be spent.

Repro: visit /explorer/tx/caca5a48c62c196940b1236624e816475f8bafa582619ae25ff53d7a5c3d9731 (coinbase tx in block 956,591) and look at the second output (OP_RETURN, 0.00 BTC) — it shows "Unspent" like the first output.

Suggested fix: special-case OP_RETURN outputs to show "Unspendable" (or omit the spent/unspent badge entirely and show the OP_RETURN data instead).
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: OP_RETURN outputs now show an Unspendable badge with an explanation instead of Spent/Unspent
