title: stateless.ts descriptor-import path never calls validateMultisigKeyPaths
description: Pre-existing gap surfaced (not caused) during cairn-acft: the stateless multisig escape hatch (src/lib/server/stateless.ts) descriptor-import branch never runs validateMultisigKeyPaths at all, so a malformed/wrong-script-type cosigner path there is not caught at ingestion the way the wizard and Caravan-JSON import paths are. Unrelated to the p2sh:1 change (left as-is since fixing it would change unrelated behavior). Fix: run validateMultisigKeyPaths (mode:'import') on the parsed descriptor config in stateless.ts, matching parseCaravanImport.
close_reason: Fixed: parseStatelessSource's bare-descriptor branch (src/lib/server/stateless.ts) now calls validateMultisigKeyPaths(desc, {mode:'import'}) right after parseDescriptor, same import-mode rules parseCaravanImport already applied (legacy-P2SH 1' label warns instead of hard-stopping). Added 2 tests in stateless.test.ts: rejects a BIP-84 single-sig cosigner path pasted into a descriptor, accepts (with warning) the historical p2sh 1'-suffix label. vitest run src/lib/server/stateless.test.ts: 15 passed. MANUAL.md updated (the cairn-e8de gap note near line 1188 now documents the fix).

title: Route remaining plaintext secrets through secretKey.ts encryption
description:
docs/DATA-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md section 4 + section 9: notification_channel_config's ntfy access
token and webhook HMAC secret are stored in plaintext JSON, and the instance-wide `settings`
KV table stores smtp_pass/core_rpc_pass/telegram_bot_token in plaintext -- all four are
bearer-credential material that secretKey.ts's AES-256-GCM envelope (encryptSecret/decryptSecret,
src/lib/server/secretKey.ts) already exists to protect. Personal SMTP password (email.ts) and
nostr_sender_privkey (channels/nostr.ts) are already correctly encrypted with this same
primitive -- this epic makes the remaining four consistent with that existing pattern.
Audit calls this "the single highest-value fix" in the report. Children: A1-A4 below.
close_reason: All 4 children done: ntfy token, webhook secret, and the three settings secrets encrypted at rest via secretKey.ts envelopes; credential keys split into typed instance_secrets table with startup migration.

title: Encrypt ntfy access token at rest
description:
notification_channel_config stores the ntfy accessToken in plaintext JSON. Write path:
src/routes/api/notifications/channels/[channel]/+server.ts, the 'ntfy' case (lines 209-221) --
currently `if (accessToken) cfg.accessToken = accessToken;` with no encryption, unlike the
personal-SMTP password handled by buildEmailSmtp() 40 lines below (same file, lines 263-292),
which calls encryptSecret(rawPass) from src/lib/server/secretKey.ts and stores the result as
passEnc. Consumer: src/lib/server/channels/ntfy.ts reads cfg.accessToken directly (lines 80-81)
and uses it in the Authorization header (line 95).

Fix (mirror the SMTP pattern exactly):
1. In the ntfy case (+server.ts:209-221), import encryptSecret from '$lib/server/secretKey' and
   replace `cfg.accessToken = accessToken` with `cfg.accessTokenEnc = encryptSecret(accessToken)`,
   keeping the existing blank-means-keep logic against `prev.accessTokenEnc` instead of
   `prev.accessToken`.
2. In channels/ntfy.ts, decrypt via decryptSecret(cfg.accessTokenEnc) before building the
   Authorization header (replacing the direct cfg.accessToken read at lines 80-81/95).
3. Update src/lib/server/notifyConfig.ts's redactChannelConfig() so its hasAccessToken boolean
   resolves off the new accessTokenEnc field.
4. Migration: existing rows have a plaintext `accessToken` key, not `accessTokenEnc`. Add a
   one-time guarded migration in db.ts (following the codebase's existing guarded-ALTER
   migration convention) that walks notification_channel_config rows for channel='ntfy' and
   re-encrypts any plaintext accessToken it finds into accessTokenEnc, removing the plaintext key.

Acceptance criteria:
- A freshly-saved or migrated ntfy config has no plaintext accessToken anywhere in
  notification_channel_config (verify via a direct sqlite query in a test).
- ntfy notifications still send successfully end-to-end with a real accessToken; extend
  src/lib/server/channels/ntfy.test.ts to cover the encrypted round-trip.
- The settings UI still correctly shows "access token configured" for both freshly-encrypted
  and migrated rows.
close_reason: Encrypted at rest as accessTokenEnc (secretKey.ts envelope): save path encrypts, ntfy.ts decrypts (fail-closed), redaction off Enc field, startup migration re-encrypts legacy rows. Commit 8f7f955.

title: Encrypt webhook HMAC secret at rest
description:
notification_channel_config stores the webhook HMAC secret in plaintext JSON. Write path:
src/routes/api/notifications/channels/[channel]/+server.ts, the 'webhook' case (lines 233-247) --
`cfg.secret = secret` with no encryption. Consumer: src/lib/server/channels/webhook.ts reads
parsed.secret directly (lines 125-127) and uses it to sign outbound payloads with
createHmac('sha256', config.secret) (lines 159-160).

Fix (same pattern as the ntfy access token, see sibling bead):
1. In the webhook case (+server.ts:233-247), encrypt via encryptSecret() from
   '$lib/server/secretKey' and store as cfg.secretEnc instead of cfg.secret, keeping the
   existing blank-means-keep + minimum-length validation logic (the >=16 char check at
   lines 240-242 must run on the raw value BEFORE encrypting).
2. In channels/webhook.ts, decrypt via decryptSecret(config.secretEnc) before computing the
   HMAC signature.
3. Update notifyConfig.ts's redactChannelConfig() hasSecret boolean to check secretEnc.
4. Migration: guarded migration re-encrypting any legacy plaintext `secret` key found in
   notification_channel_config rows for channel='webhook' into secretEnc, following the same
   convention as the ntfy fix.

Acceptance criteria:
- No plaintext secret remains in notification_channel_config for webhook configs after
  migration or a fresh save.
- Outbound webhook HMAC signatures still verify correctly end-to-end; extend the webhook
  channel's test file to cover the encrypted round-trip.
- Settings UI still shows "signing secret configured" correctly.
close_reason: Encrypted at rest as secretEnc (secretKey.ts envelope): length validation on raw value pre-encryption, webhook.ts decrypts fail-closed, hasSecret redaction, startup migration covers legacy rows. Commit a81b416.

title: Encrypt instance-wide smtp_pass / core_rpc_pass / telegram_bot_token in settings table
description:
The loose `settings` KV table (src/lib/server/db.ts, CREATE TABLE at line 71) stores
smtp_pass, core_rpc_pass, and telegram_bot_token in plaintext, readable directly from a
copy of cairn.db. nostr_sender_privkey in this same table is already correctly wrapped with
encryptSecret() -- this bead makes the other three consistent with that existing pattern.

Write sites:
- core_rpc_pass: src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts:113-115
  (`setSetting('core_rpc_pass', rpcPass)`)
- smtp_pass: src/routes/api/admin/notifications/+server.ts:91-92
- telegram_bot_token: src/routes/api/admin/notifications/+server.ts:94-95

Read sites:
- core_rpc_pass: src/lib/server/settings.ts:69
- smtp_pass: src/lib/server/channels/email.ts:95
- telegram_bot_token: src/lib/server/channels/telegram.ts:129,146

Reference pattern already correct: src/lib/server/channels/nostr.ts writes
nostr_sender_privkey via encryptSecret() (~line 132) and reads+decrypts it (~line 146) --
copy this pattern exactly for the other three keys.

Fix:
1. Wrap each setSetting(key, value) write above with encryptSecret(value) before storage
   (skip encryption when value === '' , i.e. the explicit-clear case already handled at
   +page.server.ts:115).
2. Wrap each corresponding getSetting(key) read above with decryptSecret() before use.
3. Add `core_rpc_pass` to src/lib/server/logger.ts's REDACT_KEYS array (lines 180-205) --
   confirmed this is the one settings-table secret currently MISSING from that list
   (smtp_pass, telegram_bot_token, and nostr_sender_privkey are already present).
4. Migration: guarded migration in db.ts re-encrypting any plaintext values currently stored
   under these three keys, following the existing guarded-ALTER convention.

Acceptance criteria:
- `sqlite3 cairn.db "select value from settings where key in ('smtp_pass','core_rpc_pass','telegram_bot_token')"`
  shows only encryptSecret() envelopes (JSON with v/iv/tag/data fields), never raw text, after
  migration or a fresh save.
- Instance-wide SMTP send still works end-to-end (Telegram bot / Core RPC currently appear
  unwired to a live consumer per code search -- verify and note in the PR if still true).
- logger.ts REDACT_KEYS includes core_rpc_pass.
- Extend src/lib/server/settings.test.ts to cover the encrypted round-trip for all three keys.
close_reason: setSecretSetting/readSecretSetting wrap all write/read sites; core_rpc_pass added to REDACT_KEYS; startup migration re-encrypts legacy plaintext; settings.test.ts covers round-trip for all three keys. Verified: core_rpc_pass still unwired to a live consumer; telegram_bot_token IS consumed by telegram.ts. Commit b3610ed.

title: Split settings KV table into typed instance_secrets table
description:
src/lib/server/db.ts's `settings` table (CREATE TABLE at line 71) mixes plain configuration
(electrum URL, connection_mode, ntfy/nostr defaults) with credential material (smtp_pass,
core_rpc_pass, telegram_bot_token, nostr_sender_privkey) in one untyped key/value table --
"what's sensitive here" currently requires a full code audit to discover rather than being
obvious from the schema. Depends on the encryption fix (sibling bead: "Encrypt instance-wide
smtp_pass / core_rpc_pass / telegram_bot_token in settings table") landing first so the values
being migrated are already encrypted envelopes.

Fix: add a new `instance_secrets(key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value_enc TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at
TEXT NOT NULL)` table. Migrate smtp_pass/core_rpc_pass/telegram_bot_token/nostr_sender_privkey
rows out of `settings` and into `instance_secrets` (values already encrypted per the sibling
bead). Update src/lib/server/settings.ts's getSetting/setSetting call sites for these four
keys to read/write the new table instead. Update src/lib/server/backup.ts's buildBackup()
(lines 49-68) -- which currently filters `settings` via a SENSITIVE_SETTING regex -- to simply
exclude instance_secrets entirely from the backup export, by construction rather than by regex.

Acceptance criteria:
- `settings` table contains only plain configuration values after migration.
- `instance_secrets` holds only the four encrypted credential values.
- Admin full-instance backup export (backup.ts) never includes instance_secrets rows even if
  the SENSITIVE_SETTING regex were ever loosened -- verified by a test that adds a bogus key
  to instance_secrets and confirms buildBackup() output never contains it.
close_reason: instance_secrets(key,value_enc,updated_at) table added; setSecretSetting/readSecretSetting retargeted with legacy settings fallback; nostr identity key routed through shared helpers; startup migration moves all four keys; buildBackup excludes the table by construction (test-pinned). Commit f5d2a6e.

title: Admin Settings: Bitcoin Core RPC section missing from first paint, appears on reload
description: Browser UX walk finding: on /admin/settings, the Bitcoin Core RPC section is absent from the first page render (first get_page_text capture lacked the section) but present after a subsequent reload (second get_page_text had it). Suspected hydration/render-order timing bug -- an admin loading the page fresh could briefly conclude Core RPC isn't supported/configurable. Needs repro + root-cause (likely a conditional gated on client-only state that isn't ready at first paint) and a fix so the section is present (or shows a loading placeholder) on first paint.
close_reason: not-a-bug (see comment): concurrent HMR-applied fix during the walk, not a hydration timing bug

title: EPIC: Sub-1 & fractional sat/vB fee rates (Core 28+ / Ocean)
description:
Heartwood must support sub-1 sat/vB fee rates when the connected node relays them (Bitcoin Core 28+ lowered default minrelaytxfee capability; Ocean pool accepts sub-1 sat/vB). Today the whole pipeline hard-floors at 1 sat/vB and one path (single-sig normal coin selection) rounds any fractional rate UP to an integer.

AUDIT SUMMARY (file:line evidence):

FLOOR INVENTORY (hardcoded 1 sat/vB):
- src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts:290 validateRecipientsAndFeeRate throws 'Fee rate must be at least 1 sat/vB' for feeRate<1 (PRIMARY gate; shared by single-sig AND multisig via multisigPsbt.ts:262).
- src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts:716-729 single-sig normal selection passes selectUTXO feePerByte: toBigInt(Math.ceil(feeRate)) -> btc-signer feePerByte is bigint (integer sat/vB only), so 0.5->1, 1.5->2. LIVE rounding bug: a fractional rate >=1 posted via the API (readSpendRequest walletApi.ts:80 Number(body.feeRate), no floor) is accepted and OVERPAID (safe direction, never under-relay, but wrong vs requested/displayed).
- src/lib/server/chain/index.ts:1646 carry=1 and :1652 out[key]=Math.max(1, round2(v)) floors displayed estimates at 1 (defeats honest sub-1 display).
- src/lib/components/send/FeeSpeedPicker.svelte:50 (|| 1), :52-55 (Math.max(1, fallback)) client custom floor at 1; input is inputmode=decimal so decimals type fine, but effectiveRate clamps.
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:362 canBuild requires feeRate>=1; multisig send +page.svelte:204 same.
- src/lib/feeBump.ts:364 CPFP targetRate<1 reject, :420 childRate clamp max(.,1), :82 cpfpChildFee floors child at childVsize (=1 sat/vB).

FRACTION-SAFE ALREADY (Math.ceil(vsize*feeRate), whole sats, rounds UP correctly): psbt.ts send-max :605 and exactInputs :676; ALL of multisigPsbt.ts (:386,:453,:473,:481). So multisig fee MATH already handles fractions; only its shared validation floor blocks it.

MUST STAY (spec-correct, do NOT change): feeBump.ts:263-267 RBF BIP-125 rule-4 incremental min = original fee + vsize*1 sat/vB (incrementalrelayfee default 1 sat/vB is independent of base rate and stays 1 even for sub-1 base rates). psbt.ts DUST_RELAY_FEE_RATE=3 dust math is separate from relay fee and unaffected.

CAPABILITY DETECTION (none exists today): Core client already has getMempoolInfo() returning mempoolminfee (bitcoinCore/client.ts:372, used at chain/index.ts:1558) — extend type to also read minrelaytxfee. Electrum has no relayfee wired (electrum/client.ts has estimateFee :703 but no blockchain.relayfee). Model the cached probe on packageRelay.ts (cached verdict + reset-on-backend-change) or chainHealth. Effective floor sat/vB = max(mempoolminfee, minrelaytxfee) from Core (both BTC/kvB, *1e5) or Electrum blockchain.relayfee (BTC/kvB *1e5); fall back to 1 when neither answers.

INTERNAL REPRESENTATION RECOMMENDATION: keep feeRate as a float sat/vB end-to-end (already true in 3 of 4 build paths + API + DB fee_rate column), and compute every ABSOLUTE fee as Math.ceil(vsize * feeRate) whole sats (already the pattern in multisig + send-max + exact-inputs). No iterative accumulation exists (feeRate is multiplied by vsize exactly once per build), so float error is <1e-10 sat and ceil() absorbs it — under-min-relay is impossible as long as every path uses ceil. The ONLY integer-sat/vB assumption to remove is btc-signer selectUTXO's feePerByte on the single-sig normal path (E.1). A sat/kvB-integer refactor is NOT recommended: it would touch every feeRate field, the API contract, and the DB column for no correctness gain over disciplined ceil(). Compare against the node relay floor in the node's own units (BTC/kvB) before converting, to avoid boundary misclassification.

CHILDREN: E.1 (P1 single-sig fractional math), E.2 (remove floor + thread minFeeRate), E.3 (capability probe), E.4 (honest estimate display), E.5 (UX decimals), E.6 (P3 viz/format), E.7 (P3 CPFP sub-1), E.8 (regtest verification).

COLLISIONS with coinbase epic cairn-oae1: oae1.1/.5 edit psbt.ts + transactions.ts + multisigTransactions.ts (E.1/E.2 collide — serialize psbt.ts edits or land oae1 first). oae1.3/.4 edit send page/MiningRewards/walletSync (E.5 touches send +page.svelte — flag).
close_reason: All children (eacw.1-.8) closed. Sub-1 sat/vB fee pipeline complete: single-sig fractional math fixed (eacw.1), hard 1 sat/vB floor removed + node-derived minFeeRate threaded (eacw.2), relay-floor capability probe cached (eacw.3), honest sub-1 estimate display (eacw.4), decimal fee UI with fallback explanation (eacw.5), FeeWeather/formatFeeRate sub-1 representation (eacw.6), sub-1 CPFP targets with RBF +1 sat/vB increment preserved (eacw.7), regtest 0.5 sat/vB build/sign/broadcast e2e verified (eacw.8). Ship gate 2026-07-14: npm run check 0 errors, npx vitest run 3433 passed/1 skipped (known vault E2E, needs live regtest), commit range b498c6f..cbe3682 pushed to origin/single-sig-full-wallet.

title: P1: single-sig normal selection mis-prices fractional sat/vB (ceil to integer)
description:
src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts:716-729 constructPsbt's normal (non-max, non-exactInputs) coin-selection path calls @scure/btc-signer selectUTXO with feePerByte: toBigInt(Math.ceil(feeRate)). selectUTXO's feePerByte is a bigint — integer sat/vB only — so ANY fractional rate is ceil'd: 0.5->1, 1.5->2, 2.3->3.

LIVE BUG TODAY: readSpendRequest (src/lib/server/walletApi.ts:80) does Number(body.feeRate) with no floor, and validateRecipientsAndFeeRate only rejects <1. So a fractional rate >=1 (e.g. 1.5) posted via the API or a coin-control client is ACCEPTED and then OVERPAID (pays ~2). Direction is SAFE (overpay, never under-min-relay), but the built tx disagrees with the requested rate and with the returned ConstructedPsbt.feeRate = round2(fee/vsize) (psbt.ts:821). Contrast: send-max (psbt.ts:605) and exactInputs (psbt.ts:676) already use Math.ceil(vsize*feeRate) and are fraction-correct; ALL of multisigPsbt.ts (:386/:453/:473/:481) is fraction-correct too.

FIX: price the single-sig normal path in fractional sat/vB. Preferred: replace the selectUTXO fee reliance with manual largest-first accumulation mirroring multisigPsbt.ts:462-498 (compute fee = Math.ceil((baseVsize+changeVsize)*feeRate) / Math.ceil(baseVsize*feeRate)), which also unifies the two builders' selection logic. If selectUTXO is kept, it cannot express fractions — do not simply Math.ceil.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Built tx absolute fee == Math.ceil(finalVsize * feeRate) for fractional AND integer rates; fee/vsize round-trips to the requested rate within rounding.
- ROUND-UP PROVEN: for every path, effective sat/vB (fee/vsize) >= requested feeRate (never under). Add a property/unit test over fractional rates {0.1,0.5,0.9,1.1,1.5,2.7}.
- Single-sig vs multisig parity test at the same fractional rate (same inputs -> same fee).
- Existing chain.test.ts fee tests stay green.

COLLISION: same file as coinbase epic cairn-oae1.1 (selectSpendCandidates getTip/tipHeight) and oae1.5 — different functions in psbt.ts but serialize edits (land oae1 first or rebase).
close_reason: Replaced the single-sig NORMAL selection path's btc-signer selectUTXO (integer-only bigint feePerByte: toBigInt(Math.ceil(feeRate)) -> 1.5 built at 2 sat/vB) with manual largest-first accumulation mirroring multisigPsbt.ts: fee = Math.ceil((baseVsize+changeVsize)*feeRate) whole sats, changeless fold-in of sub-dust remainder unchanged. Fractional rates >=1 now price correctly and always round UP; selection reads witnessUtxo values only so coin choice is identical; confirmed-first + preferLowMassOrder tie-break preserved via stable value-desc sort; BIP-69 in/out ordering preserved. send-max (:605) and exactInputs (:676) were already fraction-safe -- left as-is and pinned with regression tests. Did NOT touch the feeRate<1 floor (cairn-eacw.2) or feeBump.ts. EVIDENCE: psbt.test.ts 108 passed incl 8 new fractional tests (1.5->212 not 282; ceil(vsize*rate) for {1,1.1,1.5,2.7,5,20,1.25,3.33}; round-up fee/vsize>=rate proven; integer 1/5/20 unchanged=141/705/2820; send-max + exactInputs + sub-dust-fold fractional pins). sendBoundaryMatrix/2, transactions, malformedSendInputs, hostilePsbts all green (256). svelte-check 0 errors. b498c6f coinbase guards still green. NOTE: 4 multisigTransactions.test.ts CPFP failures are the concurrent chain agent's getRelayFeeFloor (cairn-eacw.3) mock gap, unrelated to this file.

title: Remove hard feeRate<1 floor; thread node-derived minFeeRate through validation + both constructors
description:
src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts:290 validateRecipientsAndFeeRate throws 'Fee rate must be at least 1 sat/vB' for !Number.isFinite(feeRate)||feeRate<1. This is the PRIMARY gate blocking sub-1 for BOTH single-sig and multisig (multisigPsbt.ts:262 calls the same validator).

psbt.ts is deliberately chain-independent (see file header comment) — keep it pure. Add a minFeeRate parameter (default 1) to validateRecipientsAndFeeRate and to ConstructParams / ConstructMultisigParams; the SERVICE layer (transactions.ts buildDraft, multisigTransactions.ts) resolves the node floor from ChainService.getRelayFeeFloor() (see sibling bead E.3) and passes it in. Validation becomes feeRate < minFeeRate. Keep MAX_FEE_RATE=1000 ceiling (psbt.ts:166,293) unchanged.

Also handle the plain-number check: currently feeRate<1 doubles as the finite/positive guard — keep an explicit feeRate<=0 / !isFinite rejection independent of the floor.

FILES: src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts (validateRecipientsAndFeeRate, ConstructParams), src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisigPsbt.ts (ConstructMultisigParams pass-through), src/lib/server/transactions.ts + src/lib/server/multisigTransactions.ts (resolve+pass minFeeRate).

ACCEPTANCE:
- With node floor 0.001 sat/vB, a 0.5 sat/vB build succeeds; with node floor 1 (unsupported/unknown), a 0.5 build is rejected with plain-language 'Your node relays at a minimum of N sat/vB' copy.
- Error message interpolates the resolved floor, not a hardcoded 1.
- Depends on E.3 (getRelayFeeFloor). COLLISION: psbt.ts/transactions.ts/multisigTransactions.ts overlap coinbase epic oae1.1/.5 — serialize.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: CLOSED (19b7b09). validateRecipientsAndFeeRate(recipients, feeRate, minFeeRate=1) in psbt.ts: absolute sanity (<=0/NaN -> 'Enter a fee rate greater than zero.') then feeRate<floor -> floor-quoting 'This fee is below what your node will relay right now - the minimum is N sat/vB.'; MAX_FEE_RATE unchanged. minFeeRate added to ConstructParams + MultisigConstructParams. Service callers resolve ChainService.getMinFeeRate() (new: round2(getRelayFeeFloor()), identical to getFeeEstimates floor -> no client/server rounding mismatch): buildDraft, buildMultisigDraft, buildStatelessPsbt. RBF/CPFP threaded via feeBump.ts. Draft fee_rate float round-trip verified (0.5 persists+reads back <1, single-sig+multisig). Tests: accept 0.5@floor0.1, reject 0.5@floor1 with floor-aware copy, always reject 0/neg/NaN, multisig parity. Server suites green, svelte-check 0 errors.

title: Node relay-floor capability probe (Core getmempoolinfo + Electrum blockchain.relayfee), cached
description:
No relay-floor probe exists today. Add ChainService.getRelayFeeFloor(): Promise<number> returning the effective minimum relay rate in sat/vB (float), so every floor stops being a hardcoded 1.

SOURCES:
- Bitcoin Core: getmempoolinfo already wired (src/lib/server/bitcoinCore/client.ts:372, used at chain/index.ts:1558). Its return type declares mempoolminfee (BTC/kvB) — EXTEND the type to also read minrelaytxfee (BTC/kvB, Core>=0.19). Effective Core floor = max(mempoolminfee, minrelaytxfee) * 1e5 sat/vB. (mempoolminfee rises above minrelaytxfee when the mempool is full -> this is a DYNAMIC floor, re-probe periodically.)
- Electrum: no relayfee method yet. Add ElectrumClient.relayFee() calling 'blockchain.relayfee' (returns BTC/kvB) in src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts near estimateFee (:703). *1e5 -> sat/vB.
- Fallback: when neither backend answers (or returns <=0), floor = 1 sat/vB.

CACHING/RESET: model on src/lib/server/packageRelay.ts (cached verdict + resetPackageRelaySupport called on backend change) or chainHealth. Short TTL (~30-60s, like cachedFeeEstimates) because mempoolminfee is dynamic; reset alongside resetChainCaches/resetPackageRelaySupport in chain/index.ts:19-21 wiring. Prefer Core when configured (authoritative for THIS node's relay policy); use Electrum relayfee only when Core absent.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Core mempoolminfee=0.00000100 BTC/kvB (=0.1 sat/vB) -> getRelayFeeFloor()==0.1; minrelaytxfee higher -> returns the max.
- Electrum-only server without blockchain.relayfee (unknown-method) -> falls back to 1, cached like packageRelay's unsupported verdict.
- No backend -> 1.
- Unit tests mock core.getMempoolInfo / electrum.relayFee (pattern already in chain.test.ts:970/1270).
close_reason: Implemented ChainService.getRelayFeeFloor() (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts): Core path max(mempoolminfee, minrelaytxfee) compared in BTC/kvB then *1e5, minrelaytxfee added to CoreRpcClient.getMempoolInfo type (bitcoinCore/client.ts:372); Electrum fallback via new ElectrumClient.relayFee()/ElectrumPool.relayFee() (blockchain.relayfee, *1e5) used only when no Core RPC configured; falls back to 1 sat/vB on any failure/unsupported-method/<=0 answer. 30s TTL cache (cachedRelayFeeFloor, chain/cache.ts) wired into resetChainCaches(). 9 new unit tests in chain.test.ts (Core-higher, minrelaytxfee-higher, dynamic-mempoolminfee-under-load, Core-RPC-failure->1, Electrum-fallback, unsupported-method->1, no-usable-answer->1, TTL-cache, reset-clears-cache) all passing (99/99 in chain.test.ts). Committed b930003. svelte-check clean (0 errors/1961 files).

title: getFeeEstimates: display sub-1 estimates honestly (stop flooring at 1 sat/vB)
description:
src/lib/server/chain/index.ts:1619-1655 getFeeEstimates converts Electrum blockchain.estimatefee (BTC/kvB) to sat/vB then floors: line 1646 carry=1 (the -1 no-estimate seed) and line 1652 out[key]=Math.max(1, round2(v)). A genuine sub-1 economy estimate is rounded UP to 1 -> dishonest (requirement #2: display sub-1 estimates as-is).

FIX: floor at getRelayFeeFloor() (bead E.3) instead of literal 1. carry seed = the node floor; Math.max(floor, round2(v)). round2 already keeps 2 decimals (0.5, 0.12). There is an existing test chain.test.ts:645 'floors a real but sub-1-sat/vB estimate at 1 sat/vB' — UPDATE it: sub-1 estimates now floor at the node relay floor, only clamping to 1 when the floor is unknown/1.

ACCEPTANCE:
- With node floor 0.001, estimatefee 0.000001 BTC/kvB (0.1 sat/vB) -> economy: 0.1 (not 1).
- With node floor 1 (Electrum-only, no relayfee), behavior unchanged (floors at 1) — existing tests pass after the seam is threaded.
- FeeEstimates type + /api/mempool/fees consumers unaffected (still float sat/vB).
Depends E.3.
close_reason: Fixed: getFeeEstimates() (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts:1620-1663) now seeds/floors at getRelayFeeFloor() (round2'd) instead of a hardcoded 1 sat/vB. Sub-1 estimates pass through untouched when the node's relay floor allows it; clamp UP to the floor when below it; unchanged 1 sat/vB behavior when the floor is unknown; estimates above the floor are never rounded up; monotonic carry preserved. Tests: chain.test.ts describe('getFeeEstimates') -- 5 new/updated cases, all passing (121/121 in chain.test.ts + cpfp.test.ts, 3411/3412 full suite [1 skipped needs live regtest]). svelte-check: 0 errors. Committed e564c8c.

title: Fee UI accepts decimals below 1 down to the node floor; explain fallback-to-1
description:
Client floors block sub-1 even when the node supports it.

src/lib/components/send/FeeSpeedPicker.svelte:50 fallback=Number(customFee)||1; :52-55 effectiveRate custom branch returns Math.max(1, fallback). Input is inputmode=decimal (:154) so decimals type fine — only the clamp blocks them. Replace the literal 1 floor with a nodeFloor prop (plumbed from the send page's live estimates/capability); when nodeFloor==1 (unsupported) keep the clamp and show plain-language copy: 'Your node relays down to 1 sat/vB.' When sub-1 supported, allow the entered decimal.
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:362 canBuild feeRate>=1 -> feeRate>=nodeFloor.
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/send/+page.svelte:204 same.
- src/lib/components/send/sendCopy.ts FEE_SPEEDS (:9-13): the tier VALUES come from live estimates (already sub-1 after E.4), but consider an Economy eta note reflecting sub-1 e.g. 'Economy: 0.5 sat/vB — a few hours' per requirement #5. formatFeeRate (src/lib/format.ts:118) already renders one decimal for <10 so 0.5 shows correctly.

ACCEPTANCE:
- With sub-1 supported, typing 0.5 in Custom builds at 0.5; the feeWarning typo guard (FeeSpeedPicker:66-75) still fires only on HIGH rates, never on sub-1.
- With unsupported node, 0.5 is clamped/blocked with the plain-language reason.
- Mobile 375px + desktop visual QA of the custom input.
COLLISION: send +page.svelte overlaps coinbase epic oae1.3/.4 (balance/MiningRewards edits) — flag before editing the send pages.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: CLOSED (1f64785). Client contract: extended the existing getFeeEstimates payload additively with FeeEstimates.minFeeRate (optional, = round2(relay floor)) - no new endpoint. FeeSpeedPicker + both send pages read fees.minFeeRate as the floor. Clamp/copy logic extracted to pure feeChoice.ts (resolveFeeRate clamps custom to floor; belowFloorMessage: floor==1 -> 'Your Bitcoin node doesnt relay fees below 1 sat/vB', else names the sub-1 floor). canBuild gates on feeRate>=nodeFloor (single-sig +page.svelte, multisig +page.svelte). feeWarning typo guard unchanged (only fires on HIGH rates, never sub-1). Tier labels render sub-1 via formatFeeRate (eacw.6). Tests: feeChoice.test.ts 14 cases (floor read, sub-1 accept, below-floor clamp, empty-box=floor, elevated floor, copy variants). Visual check @:5344 (no node -> floor=1 fallback path): picker renders 3 tiers as '-', 'Live fee estimates unavailable', Advanced toggle present, custom input inputmode=decimal. svelte-check 0 errors.

title: P3: sub-1 fee-rate representation in FeeWeather ridge + formatFeeRate edge
description:
Minor honesty gaps for sub-1 rates in visualization/formatting.

- src/lib/feeWeather.ts:48 EDGES=[1,2,...] starts at 1; bucketFees (:66-72) lumps all sub-1 mass into the [1,2) band and feeRateToX (:105-107) clamps rate<=1 to x=0. A sub-1 next-block marker or sub-1 mempool mass is indistinguishable at the left edge. Consider a sub-1 leading edge (e.g. 0.1) only if sub-1 rates become common on the connected node; low priority.
- src/lib/format.ts:118 formatFeeRate: satPerVb<10 ? toFixed(1).replace(/\.0$/,'') : round. For 0.5->'0.5' OK, 0.1->'0.1' OK, but 0.04->'0.0'->'0 sat/vB' (dishonest zero) and 0.01 likewise. Add a floor so very-small-but-positive rates render with enough precision (e.g. toFixed(2) or a '<0.1' style) instead of collapsing to '0'.

ACCEPTANCE: formatFeeRate(0.04) does not render '0 sat/vB'; ridge/marker still degrade honestly (Cardinal rule). feeWeather.test.ts + format.test.ts updated.
close_reason: formatFeeRate (src/lib/format.ts) widened to try up to 4 decimals so a real sub-1 sat/vB rate never collapses to a dishonest '0 sat/vB' (0.04->'0.04', 0.01->'0.01'); unchanged for >=~0.05 sat/vB (0.5/1/1.5/9.9/10/26 all still render exactly as before -- verified against the full pre-existing formatFeeRate.test.ts table). feeWeather.ts ridge/bucket sub-1 handling left as designed per the audit's explicit low-priority note -- already honestly degrades (sub-1 mass counted into the [1,2) band, never dropped/invented, per existing tests at feeWeather.test.ts:39-44,98-103); added one explicit cairn-eacw.6 regression test pinning that as intentional. format.test.ts +5 cases, feeWeather.test.ts +1 case; 208/208 tests passing across format.test.ts+feeWeather.test.ts+chain.test.ts. Committed 9f27e63. svelte-check clean.

title: P3: allow sub-1 CPFP target down to node floor (keep RBF +1 sat/vB increment)
description:
src/lib/server/feeBump.ts CPFP path hard-floors at 1 sat/vB in three spots: :364 executeCpfpDraft rejects targetRate<1 ('target fee rate must be at least 1'); :420 childRate=Math.min(Math.max(childFee/childVsize,1),MAX); :82 cpfpChildFee returns Math.max(childFee, childVsize) (child's own 1 sat/vB relay floor). For a node relaying sub-1, a sub-1 CPFP TARGET and a sub-1 child rate should be allowed down to getRelayFeeFloor() (bead E.3).

DO NOT CHANGE (spec-correct per prior review): the RBF replacement minimum at :263-267 — minFee = tx.fee + details.vsize is BIP-125 rule 4 with incrementalrelayfee default 1 sat/vB; the incremental relay fee stays 1 sat/vB even when base rates go sub-1 (it is a per-replacement increment, not a rate floor). The >= comparison elsewhere is also spec-correct — leave it.

ACCEPTANCE: with node floor 0.1, a CPFP target of 0.5 sat/vB builds and the child prices at >=0.1 (never under the node relay floor); RBF rule-4 min unchanged. Niche — lowest priority in the epic. Depends E.3.
close_reason: Fixed: feeBump.ts executeCpfpDraft now rejects targetFeeRate below getRelayFeeFloor() (not hardcoded 1) with a floor-naming error message; cpfpChildFee takes a new floorRate param (defaults to 1) and floors the child fee at ceil(floorRate*childVsize); childRate clamps to [floor, MAX_FEE_RATE]. BIP-125 RBF rule-4 increment (feeBump.ts ~line 263, tx.fee + vsize*1) left untouched per epic's explicit DO NOT CHANGE. Tests: cpfp.test.ts -- cpfpChildFee floors to a node-provided sub-1 floor and still defaults to 1 sat/vB with no floor passed; buildCpfpDraft accepts sub-1 target when node floor allows, rejects target below floor, still rejects sub-1 when floor unknown (fallback 1). Also fixed multisigTransactions.test.ts's chain mock (missing getRelayFeeFloor stub broke 2 existing multisig CPFP tests via the shared executeCpfpDraft path) -- now passing. Full suite 3411/3412 passing (1 skipped, needs live regtest), svelte-check 0 errors. MANUAL.md fee-estimate + CPFP sections updated. Committed d7048ff.

title: Regtest verification: build/sign/broadcast a 0.5 sat/vB tx end-to-end
description:
End-to-end proof the fractional-fee pipeline relays on a node configured for sub-1, and that rounding never goes under min-relay.

REGTEST SETUP: start Core with a sub-1 relay floor so it actually relays fractional-rate txs — bitcoin.conf: minrelaytxfee=0.00000001 (and blockmintxfee=0.00000001). Verify getmempoolinfo reports mempoolminfee/minrelaytxfee at the low value so getRelayFeeFloor() (E.3) returns sub-1. Point electrs/Fulcrum at this Core (or run Core-only; single-sig build path is Core-independent for construction but broadcast + relay-floor probe need the node).

STEPS:
1. Fund a single-sig wallet; build a send at feeRate=0.5 via the psbt endpoint. Assert ConstructedPsbt.fee == Math.ceil(vsize*0.5) and fee/vsize rounds to ~0.5 (E.1).
2. Sign (external seed / regtest signer per existing QA recipes) and broadcast.
3. VERIFY relay/acceptance with getmempoolentry <txid> — assert it is present and fees.base == the built fee (proves the network accepted the sub-1 rate; a silent under-min-relay would be rejected at sendrawtransaction with 'min relay fee not met', mapped by broadcastRejection.ts:28).
4. Repeat for multisig (multisigPsbt already fraction-safe; exercises E.2 floor removal).
5. Negative: with Core at DEFAULT minrelaytxfee (0.00001), a 0.5 sat/vB broadcast must be rejected with the friendly 'fee rate is too low for the network to relay' copy (broadcastRejection.ts:28), and getRelayFeeFloor() must report ~1 so the UI blocks it up front.

ACCEPTANCE: documented recipe + green e2e; rounding-up invariant (fee/vsize >= requested) holds on real txs. Reuse regtest patterns from bitcoin/vaultRegtestE2E.test.ts and scripts/qa/. Depends E.1, E.2, E.3.
close_reason: All 5 acceptance criteria verified E2E on regtest for single-sig and personal multisig. Found + fixed a real bug along the way: getRelayFeeFloor()'s round2()'d-to-0 case was wrongly treated as 'unknown' and silently re-imposed the hardcoded 1 sat/vB floor, defeating the sub-1 feature on exactly the bead's own recommended node config. Fixed in psbt.ts + feeChoice.ts, regression tests added, targeted suite green.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
E2E VERIFIED on regtest, with ONE product bug found and fixed along the way.

STACK: fresh sub-1-relay-floor node stood up (docker network qa-sub1): qa-sub1-bitcoind (bitcoin/bitcoin:28.0, regtest, minrelaytxfee=0.00000001 blockmintxfee=0.00000001, RPC 127.0.0.1:18544 qasub1:qasub1) + qa-sub1-electrs (electrs, RPC 127.0.0.1:60403, REST 127.0.0.1:3003). App instance: CAIRN_DB=data/qa-sub1.db on :5902, settings connection_mode=custom + electrum_host=127.0.0.1 electrum_port=60403 (note: connection_mode must be 'custom' or getChainConfig() silently falls back to the public blockstream.info default even with electrum_host/port set — tripped over this mid-session, not a bug, just the documented admin-settings gate). Negative-test node reused the existing vault-e2e-bitcoind (default minrelaytxfee=0.00001 BTC/kvB = 1 sat/vB) on :5900.

1. SUB-1 NODE SETUP — confirmed via `bitcoin-cli getmempoolinfo`: mempoolminfee=minrelaytxfee=0.00000001 BTC/kvB (=0.001 sat/vB). Electrum `blockchain.relayfee` on qa-sub1-electrs returns the same (1e-8). PASS.

2. APP DETECTS THE FLOOR — BUG FOUND: GET /api/mempool/fees returned minFeeRate:0 (correct raw math: 0.001 sat/vB round2()'s to 0.00), but POST .../psbt at feeRate=0.5 was REJECTED with "the minimum is 1 sat/vB" — the historical hardcoded floor, not the real ~0 floor. Root cause: both `validateRecipientsAndFeeRate` (src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts:337, shared by single-sig AND multisig via multisigPsbt.ts:269) and the client's `nodeFloorFrom` (src/lib/components/send/feeChoice.ts:18) used `minFeeRate > 0 ? minFeeRate : 1`, treating an honestly-computed floor that rounds to exactly 0 the same as "floor unknown" (whose sentinel is also constructed to be exactly 1, never 0 — see ChainService.getRelayFeeFloor()). On the bead's own recommended regtest config (minrelaytxfee=0.00000001) this collapsed the entire sub-1 feature back to the pre-epic hardcoded 1 sat/vB minimum, for both single-sig and multisig sends. FIXED: changed both checks from `> 0` to `>= 0` (commit follows) since minFeeRate is never negative by construction and the real "unknown" sentinel is 1, not 0. Updated/added regression tests: src/lib/components/send/feeChoice.test.ts (split the old "0 treated as 1" case, added a new one asserting 0 passes through unchanged) and src/lib/server/bitcoin/sendBoundaryMatrix.test.ts (new case: minFeeRate:0 + feeRate:0.5 builds). Full targeted suite green (164+38 tests). Re-verified live after the fix: GET /api/mempool/fees still minFeeRate:0; POST .../psbt at feeRate=0.5 now succeeds (fee=71, vsize=141, ceil(141*0.5)=71 ✓).

3. SINGLE-SIG BUILD → SIGN → BROADCAST @ 0.5 sat/vB — wallet id=1 "QA Sub1 Fee Wallet" (zpub 2f7341f7/84'/0'/0'), funded 1 BTC via a regular (non-coinbase) send from the sub-1 node's miner wallet. PSBT built at feeRate=0.5 (fee=71, vsize=141). Signed externally (scripts/qa/sign-singlesig-psbt.mjs, seed byte 99). Broadcast -> txid 8433fe3498135869add18ad437e637f82bd6a57c6ae6d9fc745325a58323aa1f. `bitcoin-cli getmempoolentry` on qa-sub1-bitcoind: fees.base=0.00000071 BTC (71 sats), vsize=141 -> actual rate 71/141=0.5035 sat/vB ≈ 0.5. PASS.

4. MULTISIG REPEAT — personal 2-of-3 p2wsh (multisig id=1 "QA Sub1 Multisig", BIP-48, collaborative:false, created directly via POST /api/wallets/multisig — same shortcut as cairn-oae1.2, no team-mode/contacts flow needed for a personal vault). Funded 0.5 BTC via regular send. PSBT built at feeRate=0.5 (fee=95, vsize=190 est / 189 actual). 2-of-3 signed externally (scripts/qa/sign-multisig-psbt.mjs, seed bytes 201 and 203). Broadcast -> txid 1a7b810c14ca8a5383dcddcc96c33f2e304205c2643f77ca9550a2696e2eb649. getmempoolentry: fees.base=0.00000095 BTC (95 sats), vsize=189 -> 95/189=0.5026 sat/vB ≈ 0.5. PASS. Confirms E.2's floor removal is fraction-safe for multisig too (shared validateRecipientsAndFeeRate).

5. NEGATIVE TEST (default-floor node) — vault-e2e-bitcoind (unmodified default minrelaytxfee=0.00001 BTC/kvB = 1 sat/vB). GET /api/mempool/fees -> minFeeRate:1 (all tiers 1, correct — regtest has no real fee market so every tier carries the floor forward). POST .../psbt at feeRate=0.5 -> 400 {"error":"This fee is below what your node will relay right now — the minimum is 1 sat/vB.","code":"invalid_amount"}. Matches broadcastRejection.ts:28-style floor-aware copy, explains the 1 sat/vB minimum in plain language. PASS.

ACCEPTANCE: documented recipe above; rounding-up invariant (fee/vsize >= requested, fee == ceil(vsize*feeRate)) holds on both real single-sig and multisig broadcasts (71=ceil(141*0.5), 95=ceil(189.x*0.5)).

Bug fixed in this session (see commit): src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts, src/lib/components/send/feeChoice.ts, + regression tests in src/lib/components/send/feeChoice.test.ts and src/lib/server/bitcoin/sendBoundaryMatrix.test.ts.

title: CSP blocks SvelteKit's inline hydration bootstrap script on every page (breaks whole app)
description:
QA (P0, blocking Umbrel): CSP added in 25c9a8f (cairn-ia2y) breaks the app under
CAIRN_AUTH_MODE=password.

Root cause (verified directly: built prod bundle, curled /login, inspected
HTML): SvelteKit ALWAYS injects an inline <script> hydration-bootstrap block
into every rendered page (the __sveltekit_<hash> = {...}; kit.start(...) call
in the response body) -- this is separate from the external module chunks it
imports, and its content is NOT static (embeds per-response page data), so it
can't be allow-listed with unsafe-inline alone without reopening the exact
clickjacking/XSS hole cairn-ia2y was filed to close. 25c9a8f's commit message
claim ("app.html has no inline <script>... hydration ships as external 'self'
module chunks") is factually wrong -- confirmed by direct inspection of the
built HTML output.

This is NOT specific to password mode -- it breaks every deployment. Umbrel
pins CAIRN_AUTH_MODE=password, so that's just where it was first hit in live
QA.

Fix: SvelteKit's built-in CSP nonce integration (kit.csp, mode 'auto') --
the officially-supported mechanism for exactly this problem. SvelteKit
generates a per-request nonce, adds `nonce-xxx` to the script-src directive it
emits on the response, and stamps the matching nonce attribute onto its own
generated inline script automatically (no app.html changes needed since that
script is entirely SvelteKit's own output). No weakening to 'unsafe-inline',
no dropping CSP.
close_reason: Fixed in 0d6b354: added kit.csp (mode: 'auto') to the sveltekit() plugin call in vite.config.ts, and made hooks.server.ts's applySecurityHeaders only set its fallback CSP when resolve()'s response doesn't already carry one (it previously clobbered SvelteKit's own nonced header unconditionally). Verified end-to-end: npm run check (0/0), npx vitest run (132 files, 1882 passed + 1 skipped, incl. 2 new regression tests), npm run build, then curled the built prod server -- Content-Security-Policy header carries script-src 'self' 'nonce-jiNMm4QUfU8uCPGBS8oMvQ==' and the response HTML's inline hydration script is <script nonce="jiNMm4QUfU8uCPGBS8oMvQ==">, matching nonces confirm hydration now runs.

title: portfolio.ts silently drops failed wallet scans from dashboard totals, zero logging
description: src/lib/server/portfolio.ts:158-179: scanWallet(...).then(ok, () => null) and the multisig equivalent convert any scan rejection (e.g. Electrum failure) to null with zero logging, silently excluding that wallet from the portfolio dashboard's confirmed/unconfirmed totals. A user could see an incorrect (understated) total balance during a partial chain-data outage with no error indicator anywhere. Contrast with wallets.ts/multisigScan.ts's list endpoints, which populate an explicit errors map for the same failure class. Fix: at minimum add log.warn on scan failure; consider surfacing a "some wallets could not be scanned" indicator in the dashboard response. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in 7ebd68c

title: Vault components hardcode accent/status rgba opacity instead of shared tokens
description:
Design-system audit found the vault feature (newest, least-scrutinized surface) repeatedly hardcodes rgba() breakdowns of existing color tokens instead of reusing them, e.g.:
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/+page.svelte:293 .vault-card { border-color: rgba(232, 147, 90, 0.25) } -- that's --accent at 25% opacity, hand-typed instead of referencing --accent-muted (which is 15%) or a new shared token if 25% is genuinely a different intentional weight.
- src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/+page.svelte:906,1015 -- rgba(232, 147, 90, 0.35) and 0.3 -- two more one-off accent opacities in the same file.
- src/routes/(app)/vaults/new/+page.svelte:1567 -- another rgba(232, 147, 90, 0.25).
- src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1753,1762,1935,1998 -- hardcoded rgba(232, 201, 90, 0.3) / rgba(232, 90, 90, 0.35) alongside var(--warning-muted)/var(--error-muted) in the same rule, rather than a single token-based border color.

None of these look visually broken today, but every one is a spot where a future palette tweak in app.css won't propagate, and it is scattered enough (5+ files) that it is drift, not a one-off typo. Either add the additional opacity weights actually needed as named tokens in app.css (e.g. --accent-border or similar) or standardize on the existing --accent-muted/--warning-muted/--error-muted everywhere and stop hand-deriving new opacities per component.
close_reason: Fixed in 0072dc7: five shared border tokens (--accent-border, --accent-border-strong, --warning-border, --warning-border-strong, --error-border) replace all hand-typed rgba literals across the wallets tree (verified zero rgba(232,... stragglers there). Appearance preserved (<=0.05 opacity shift on muted-fill borders). Dashboard/settings stragglers noted as out-of-scope.

title: Announcements: user-side banner rendering + dismissal
description: In src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte (~line 90-132), render active/non-expired announcements sorted by display_order, styled by type, below nav and above {@render children()} -- same slot used by the existing backup/reminder banners. Non-dismissible banners always show while active. Dismissible banners show a dismiss (x) button; dismissal upserts into announcement_dismissals and the banner disappears for that user until re-shown (it isn't -- dismissal is permanent per announcement, matching backup_reminders semantics) or the announcement is edited/reactivated. Load list server-side in +layout.server.ts, filtered against locals.flags.announcement_banners.
close_reason: Built + committed: schema/flag/tabs in 024542f, full feature (server module, /admin/announcements CRUD with live preview + audit trail, layout banner rendering, dismiss API, 28 tests) in b674fbd. Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: Mobile: primary nav is a horizontal-scroll strip instead of a proper mobile nav pattern
description:
All pages, mobile viewport (tested 390x844)

What I did:
- Resized to a phone-width viewport and browsed the app.

What happened:
- The top navigation bar (Dashboard, Explorer, Wallets, Vaults, Settings, Admin) doesn't collapse into a hamburger menu or bottom tab bar on narrow screens — it just becomes a horizontally-scrolling strip of nav items, with a visible scrollbar underneath. It is fully functional (confirmed scrolling reveals every item, all clickable), just not a typical/expected mobile navigation pattern.

What should have happened:
- A more conventional mobile-first nav treatment (hamburger/drawer menu, or bottom tab bar) would be more discoverable and idiomatic. Right now a first-time mobile user might not realize the nav scrolls sideways since there's no obvious "there's more here" affordance beyond a thin scrollbar.

Priority: P3 — purely a polish/UX-pattern issue, not broken functionality. Noting for the design backlog.
close_reason: Addressed in 22ab66d by the same edge-fade scroll cue as cairn-peoj: the strip now visibly reads as horizontally scrollable. A full hamburger/bottom-tab redesign remains a larger design-backlog item, but the core 'no affordance' complaint is resolved.

title: Electrum dispatch() crashes on null JSON payload
description: client.ts:359-395 — JSON.parse succeeds on bare null, passed to dispatch() which does property access on null → TypeError. No try/catch in the data listener, no uncaughtException handler in the repo. One line from a malicious/buggy server kills the entire process for all users. Fix: wrap dispatch() call in try/catch, validate msg is a non-null object before property access.
close_reason: FIXED in 2e759e2 (electrum/client.ts). cairn-ek9s: onData validates JSON.parse yields a non-null non-array object before dispatch(), and dispatch() is wrapped in try/catch — a bare null / hostile line is logged+dropped, cannot throw unhandled and crash. cairn-jhj6: a failed keepalive ping now destroys the current socket (guarded by this.socket===socket to avoid racing a concurrent reconnect), so a zombie connection triggers the existing close->onDisconnect->backoff reconnect path; no second reconnect loop. 3 new tests; client + pool tests green.

title: Vault schema, creation wizard, detail page
description: vaults + vault_keys tables (key category Hardware/Mobile/Recovery, per-key xpub/fingerprint/path/name), server module + API, 5-step wizard (why+quorum presets 2of3/3of5/custom; add keys one at a time with per-device import sub-wizard; review + test address; confirm; done with fund/export-backup next steps), vault detail page (quorum display, key list, balance/addresses/history, receive QR), descriptor export/import UI.
close_reason: Shipped across three commits + API routes in e0f5a5e: vaultScan/vaultExport with exact-string ColdCard + Caravan-contract tests, 5-step wizard (all steering rounds: device-first key import, five-method picker, xprv refusal, Unchained/Casa copy, ColdCard registration step), detail page with backup prominence, nav/list presence. 525/525 tests, 0 check errors, full wizard walked live in the browser + 18/18 HTTP suite. Notes: Trezor/Ledger read seam degrades to paste until wired (drivers landed after its final run); Caravan golden fixture deferred to cairn-yxf; imported uuid/ledgerPolicyHmacs dropped (no storage).

title: Portfolio cache: persist scan results to SQLite for instant-but-stale reads across restart
description: Part (b) split out of cairn-fd56 (part (a), startup pre-warm, is done). Persist the per-wallet/per-multisig scan results (WalletScanResult / MultisigScanResult: balances, tx summary, scanned-address count) to a new SQLite table (e.g. wallet_scan_cache keyed by xpub / multisig descriptor, with updated_at) so a cold restart can serve a slightly-stale-but-instant result immediately, then refresh on the next 60s cache expiry / the warm pass. Requires: (1) new schema in db.ts; (2) a cheap single-upsert-per-scan write path in walletScan.ts doScan + multisigScan.ts doScan (NOT per-address — the load test flags synchronous DatabaseSync writes as the app's primary bottleneck, so keep it to one write per scan); (3) load persisted rows into the in-memory scanCache Maps at startup (in warmPortfolioCache/startPortfolioWarm, src/lib/server/portfolioWarm.ts) BEFORE the network warm pass, so early post-restart requests get instant data; (4) correct invalidation on wallet/multisig deletion + xpub change (invalidateWalletCache/invalidateMultisigCache must also delete the persisted row) so stale data for a removed wallet never leaks; (5) a UX decision on whether stale reads need a plain-language 'Updating…' indicator (consult Cairn UX philosophy — no raw timestamps/internals). Deferred from cairn-fd56 because it needs schema + a write-path change + a UX call, unlike the localized part (a).
close_reason: Built: wallet_scan_cache table + scanCachePersist.ts; one-upsert-per-scan write path in walletScan/multisigScan doScan; startup seeding in portfolioWarm.seedScanCachesFromDb before force-refreshing warm pass; invalidate deletes persisted rows; UX decision to omit stale badge documented. 6 tests pass, svelte-check clean.

title: addressWatcher SPV verification gate mocked to always-pass in tests
description: addressWatcher.test.ts:50-52 — verifyTxInclusion is stubbed to always return {ok:true}. The defense against a hostile Electrum server inventing txids to fire fake payment-received notifications (cairn-7zj6) has no positive or negative test. tx_confirmed and tx_large event types are also entirely untested. The baseline anti-flood system (cairn-3bt1/u7bw, real shipped flood bugs) has no coverage. Fix: add SPV-negative test, tx_confirmed/tx_large tests, baseline anti-flood regression test.
close_reason: Added addressWatcherConfirmFlood.test.ts: tx_confirmed happy path + idempotency + pending-gate ordering (handleNewBlock), tx_large threshold fire/no-fire/no-config cases, and cairn-3bt1/u7bw baseline anti-flood regression (fresh-address silent baseline + failed-fetch quarantine then silent retry). addressWatcherSpv.test.ts (already on main, c753fc2) independently covers the real SPV pass/fail paths this bead also called out, so that half was already satisfied.

title: SMTP credentials sent in cleartext when admin sets tls='none' with smtp_user/smtp_pass configured — no cross-field validation
description: src/lib/server/channels/email.ts's sendMail() builds the nodemailer transporter with requireTLS/ignoreTLS derived purely from smtp.tls, while auth:{user,pass} is set independently whenever smtp_user is populated — with no cross-check preventing tls='none' + credentials from coexisting. If an admin sets smtp_tls='none' (the file's own docstring even recommends this as a 'local dev tip' for Mailhog) while smtp_user/smtp_pass are also configured for a real relay, nodemailer issues the SMTP AUTH exchange — and the notification body itself, which can include tx-amount details when no PGP key is set — in cleartext over the network. POST /api/admin/notifications validates smtp_tls enum membership and other fields independently but performs zero cross-field validation rejecting tls='none' when a user/pass is present. Fix: reject (or warn strongly on) saving smtp_tls='none' when smtp_user/smtp_pass are non-empty, in the admin settings save action.
close_reason: Fixed in c110211

title: gapLimitScanner.ts entirely untested — GAP_LIMIT boundary at risk
description: gapLimitScanner.ts — the shared discovery/attribution/caching engine has no test file and is not exercised by any test. walletScan importers all vi.fn() mock it. Specifically uncovered: GAP_LIMIT=20 boundary and tail-trim (off-by-one here = miss funds), collectScanTxs delta attribution (computes every balance/history figure), ScanCache TTL/forceRefresh/failure-not-cached. Fix: add gapLimitScanner.test.ts with boundary tests, delta attribution tests, and cache behavior tests.
close_reason: Covered by src/lib/server/bitcoin/gapLimitScanner.edge.test.ts (302 lines, merged this wave from qa/edge-btc-2026-07-12, commit d387d1e): boundary tests, delta attribution tests, and cache behavior tests all now exist -- matches the bead's prescribed fix.

comments.text: This wave (qa-2026-07-12b) merged src/lib/server/bitcoin/gapLimitScanner.edge.test.ts (302 lines, from qa/edge-btc-2026-07-12, commit d387d1e): covers GAP_LIMIT=20 boundary/tail-trim, collectScanTxs delta attribution, and ScanCache TTL/forceRefresh/failure-not-cached per this bead's prescribed fix. Closing with this evidence.

Admin/users/[id] override page inherits the label from registry.ts so it shows the same inconsistency. Normalize registry.ts to 'ColdCard' to match the repo's dominant convention.

title: [pool-ux] Pool has no user-visible pool-wide stats, blocks trophy wall, or leaderboard - all the exciting numbers are admin-only
description: Evidence: pool-wide hashrate, connected miners, per-user breakdown with share pct, the 24h pool hashrate chart, and the all-blocks-found ledger exist ONLY in getAdminMiningView / /admin/mining (AdminPoolHero, AdminHashrateChart, AdminUserBreakdown, AdminBlocksLedger), and the live mining:pool SSE topic is admin-gated (aggregates.ts publishNudges: livePublish mining:pool {admin:true}). A regular miner on /mining sees only their own numbers and has no sense that a pool exists around them - nothing to watch, root for, or share. Competitor research: Ocean shows a public pool dashboard (pool hashrate, blocks found count, top-15 leaderboard refreshed every 10 min, recent-blocks table) and reviewers single out its watchability; ckpool solo culture is built on shared lucky-block stories. For a pool whose vision is attracting users AND hash, community visibility is the retention surface. Proposed fix (plain language): add a user-visible pool section (on /mining or a /mining/pool tab): pool hashrate + chart, miners online count, blocks found here trophy wall (already in mining_blocks, display-name or anonymized per a privacy toggle), and optionally an opt-in leaderboard by hashrate share. Reuse the admin read model with names filtered per privacy; open a non-admin live topic or fold pool figures into the existing user mining nudge refetch.
close_reason: Shipped: getPublicPoolView + GET /api/mining/pool (requireFeature('mining'), NOT admin) + /mining/pool page — pool hashrate hero + 24h chart (AdminHashrateChart reused), miners online/people/blocks-found counts, best-share leaderboard (cairn-192dr mechanism), blocks-found-here trophy wall with finder names + you-chips + honest 'Not counted' rejected rows. mining:pool live topic scope changed admin->broadcast at all 3 publish sites (nudge is data-free; endpoints stay gated). Admin-only remains: settings, per-connection difficulty, fatal errors, share pct.

This bead remains the umbrella for user-visible pool-wide stats (pool hashrate/chart, miners online, blocks trophy wall) with 192dr as the leaderboard mechanism. cairn-20k25 (best-share high score) supplies the per-user stat 192dr ranks.

title: [pool-ux] P1: /mining dashboard renders with hero, connection card, payout and workers sections EMPTY client-side - silent, no errors
description: Found during live visual QA on design/evergreen-identity (dev instance, regtest engine, 2 live workers, 60+ blocks). SSR HTML for /mining contains the FULL dashboard (hero, MiningConnectionCard, MiningPayoutWallet, MiningWorkersList, MiningEarnings rows, odds), but in the browser the page renders reduced: .mining-col-connection and .mining-col-payout exist with 0 children, no .hero element, workers column absent entirely, MiningEarnings shows only static labels (its two Amount components empty, 0 of 60+ block rows), while MiningOddsPanel and the Turn-off-mining form render fully. ZERO console errors, zero window error/unhandledrejection events (instrumented), no vite overlay. Deterministic across full reload (hydration), client-side nav from Home (fresh mount), and vite --force dep rebuild. Each broken component mounts PERFECTLY via svelte.mount() with props in the same page (verified MiningHero, MiningConnectionCard). /admin/mining renders completely; /mining onboarding branches (no-wallet) render fine - only the full-dashboard {:else} branch is affected. GET /api/mining/me returns the complete correct view throughout. Impact if it reaches prod builds: the entire connect-a-miner funnel and workers list are invisible - the pool is unusable from the UI. A deep-reasoner root-cause investigation was dispatched by the pool-ux audit session (2026-07-19); attach its findings here. Repro env: vite dev :5301, CAIRN_DB=data/poolux-qa.db, live mining nudges active (view replaced wholesale every ~15s via refetch - suspect interaction with untrack(() => data.view) + $effect view mirror in +page.svelte).
close_reason: Fixed: MiningEarnings.svelte keyed {#each} now keys on row.txid ?? height:vout:i (coinbase txid unique per block; index fallback for null-txid rejected rows). Root cause per opus analysis on this bead: duplicate height:vout keys (reorg churn, vout always 0) trigger Svelte 5 each_key_duplicate which aborts the dashboard subtree client render silently, dev AND prod. Regression test src/lib/components/mining/miningEarnings.dom.test.ts (new jsdom vitest project) mounts with 3x height-284 duplicate fixture: FAILS with 'duplicate key 284:0' on old key, PASSES rendering all rows on fixed key — verified both directions.

comments.author: pool-ux-audit
comments.text: Final audit-session evidence (root cause UNCONFIRMED - the deep-reasoner dispatched on this died without output): the SvelteKit-serialized load payload in the SSR HTML contains the COMPLETE view (workers array with hashrates, totals incl hashrateNow 379.4, 60+ blocksFound), so the client hydrates from full data and still renders hero/connection/payout/workers empty and earnings partially. Ruled out: module load failures (all 200 + import OK), component defects (manual svelte.mount renders perfectly), vite dep cache (--force rebuild, same result), console/window errors (none captured). Affects hydration AND fresh client-side mounts; only the full-dashboard branch. FIRST TRIAGE STEP: reproduce against a prod build (node server.mjs) to determine whether this is dev-only; if prod reproduces, this is the top pool blocker. Suspects to check: Svelte 5 fine-grained update interaction with the wholesale view replacement in refetch() + $effect mirror + untrack init in +page.svelte; and the duplicate flat copies of Mining*.svelte in src/lib/components/ vs src/lib/components/mining/ (two component trees - possible compiler/registry confusion in dev).

comments.author: opus-deep-reasoner
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (opus deep-reasoner, 2026-07-19): duplicate keys in MiningEarnings' keyed {#each} trigger Svelte 5's each_key_duplicate throw, which aborts the mining-dashboard subtree's client render.

EVIDENCE
- Live GET /api/mining/me (this user) now returns 210 blocksFound. Grouping by the exact each-key `${height}:${vout}`: only 189 DISTINCT -> 21 collisions (e.g. 284:0 x3; pairs at 154/158/162/216/220/238/242/246/250/254/258/262/266/270/274/278/327/331/335 : 0). Coinbase vout is always 0 and regtest reorgs record multiple blocksFound rows at the same height, so the key collides.
- MiningEarnings.svelte:71 keys on `(`${row.height}:${row.vout}`)`.
- svelte 5.56.4 each.js:351 `if (length > keys.size)` (210 > 189 -> true) throws each_key_duplicate. The guard runs in BOTH dev and prod: each.js:356 `e.each_key_duplicate('','','')` throws in prod too (only the message detail is stripped under DEV). => NOT dev-only. A prod build (node server.mjs) reproduces. This resolves the bead's FIRST TRIAGE STEP: prod IS affected; this is a top pool blocker.
- SSR renders {#each} by plain iteration (no keyed reconciliation, no dup check), so the SSR HTML / serialized payload is complete — matching the observed full SSR.
- svelte.mount() in isolation renders fine because hand-picked props don't carry duplicate-keyed data.
- The two prior suspects are RED HERRINGS: (1) the wholesale view replacement + untrack(() => data.view) + $effect mirror is NOT required to trigger it — the throw fires from the keyed each itself; (2) the flat vs /mining/ duplicate Mining*.svelte copies are irrelevant — +page.svelte imports explicitly from $lib/components/mining/.

FAILURE MECHANISM (why partial DOM + silent)
- Throw originates in the Earnings each. On the UPDATE path (refetch replacing view, or the $effect mirror), Batch.#process catches it (batch.js:327), calls reset_all(root) which recursively marks the whole effect tree CLEAN WITHOUT running the pending render effects that build the sibling child components (batch.js:1340), then re-throws. The page's static wrapper divs were created synchronously so they survive, while Hero/Connection/Payout/Workers and Earnings' inner children never get wired -> the observed skeletal DOM.
- On HYDRATION, Svelte's hydrate() explicitly RE-THROWS each_key_duplicate (render.js:126-130, because its message carries an https://svelte.dev/e/ URL) instead of doing its clear-and-remount recovery. So the error is swallowed one level up in SvelteKit's client navigation/hydration layer (multiple .catch(noop) paths in runtime/client/client.js), not by Svelte — consistent with the instrumented zero window.onerror / zero unhandledrejection / no vite overlay.
- HYPOTHESIS (not fully pinned without a live batch step-through): the exact spatial ordering that leaves MiningOddsPanel + the disable form rendered while the earlier siblings blank is a consequence of effect scheduling/reset order around the throw. The duplicate-key root cause is independently sufficient and is confirmed; the fix resolves the bug regardless of the precise unwinding order.

DATA-DEPENDENCY
- Triggers ONLY when >=2 blocksFound share height:vout. A mainnet solo user with unique block heights never hits it; regtest/QA reorg churn (which produced height 284 three times here) does. The 60+/210-block volume is incidental — a SINGLE duplicate pair is enough.
- NOT related to specific float/large values (hashrate 429.49672960000004, reward 2500000000, heights 30/145/151+): formatSats, formatNumber, timeAgo (format.ts) and formatHashrate (shared/hashrate.ts) are all null/NaN/overflow-safe and none throw on these inputs. Ruled out as the cause.

MINIMAL FIX (MiningEarnings.svelte:71) — make the each key unique:
  {#each rows as row, i (`${row.height}:${row.vout}:${i}`)}
or key on the coinbase txid with an index fallback for null-txid (rejected) rows:
  {#each rows as row, i (row.txid ?? `${row.height}:${row.vout}:${i}`)}
Optional hardening: give each blocksFound row a unique identity server-side in getUserMiningView (readModels.ts) so two rows never share height:vout — but reorged-out rows at a re-mined height are legitimately distinct ('Not counted' chip), so txid is the honest unique id, not dedup-by-height.

No source files were modified during this investigation (node_modules + live API reads only).

title: Admin recovery setup is documented as mandatory but not server-enforced
description:
src/routes/(app)/recovery-setup/+page.server.ts has a comment stating: "Admins CANNOT skip: the whole point is that the instance operator stays recoverable. If they somehow leave with recovery incomplete, ... re-entry lands them right back here." This is not actually true of the current code -- checked src/routes/(app)/+layout.server.ts and src/hooks.server.ts, neither has any recovery-completeness check or redirect. The ONLY enforcement is a "nagging banner" in Settings, which is a visual reminder, not a gate.

Concretely: an admin can complete first-passkey setup, get routed to /recovery-setup, and simply navigate to / or any other URL instead of finishing it -- nothing stops them, redirects them back, or blocks any action. This is a real, if narrow, gap since it is the ONE account this whole system exists to protect against lockout for (the instance operator, who has no one else to ask for access back).

Not a security vulnerability -- it does not let anyone bypass auth or reach data they should not -- but it is a real functional gap between what the code claims to guarantee and what it actually does, on a feature whose entire point is preventing an unrecoverable admin lockout. Fix: add the enforcement the comment already describes -- an admin with incomplete recovery setup should be redirected to /recovery-setup from other routes (a check in the (app) layout load function, or hooks.server.ts, gated on isAdmin && !hasRecoverySetup(user.id).phrase-and-codes-complete), not just nagged.
close_reason: done

comments.author: auditor
comments.text: Audit note: closed with reason 'done' (no detail). Backfilling: fixed in 72b91d1 — added a recovery-completeness gate to (app)/+layout.server.ts, redirecting an admin with incomplete recovery (no phrase or no codes left) to /recovery-setup from any other route, guarded against a redirect loop on the wizard itself and runs after the disclosure gate. Verified: correctly scoped to admin-only (matches the original design in 659e24c where only admins are blocked, other users get a banner), and /logout lives outside the (app) route group so an admin can't get trapped unable to sign out.

title: Wizard-bypass: create action still accepts scriptType=p2sh POST after Option-B UI removal
description: Follow-up from cairn-acft (Option B removed bare legacy P2SH from the multisig-creation WIZARD UI only). The server create action (wallets/multisig/new create + api/wallets/multisig create:true) still accepts a POST with scriptType='p2sh' and valid m/45'/Trezor-0' keys — a scripted client can still mint a bare-P2SH wallet, bypassing the UI removal. Low risk (needs already-valid non-1' paths). DECISION (consult Fable): add a server-side guard rejecting scriptType=p2sh on CREATE (not import), or is API-level bare-P2SH creation acceptable for advanced users given the UI hides it? If guarding, do it where createMultisig branches on source==='imported' so IMPORT of legacy m/45' P2SH still works.
close_reason: DECISION: guard added (bare-P2SH creation is a UI-removed path per cairn-acft; scripted-client bypass closed for consistency/defense-in-depth). Implemented as a server-side check at the route layer (not inside createMultisig, to avoid colliding with another in-flight edit to wallets/multisig.ts in this checkout): src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts 'create' action rejects scriptType=p2sh when source!=='imported'; src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/+server.ts POST rejects scriptType=p2sh outright (this endpoint never passes source, always a fresh build). Import path (/api/wallets/multisig/import, source:'imported') unaffected -- legacy P2SH wallets still restore. New tests: page.server.test.ts (2 cases) + api/wallets/multisig/server.test.ts (1 case), all passing. Commit 00e5ee9.

title: [wallet-pool] Duplicate finder notifications on block find: mining_block_found + coinbase tx_received both fire

Two bell nudges for one event. Fix: make addressWatcher coinbase-aware and suppress/merge (but see companion live-refresh bead before suppressing).
