title: All destination address types + P2SH/P2SH-P2WSH vault scripts
description: Two halves: (1) verify+test sends to all six destination types (P2PKH 1.., P2SH 3.., P2SH-P2WPKH 3.., P2WPKH bc1q.., P2WSH bc1q.., P2TR bc1p..) from single-sig constructPsbt — addressToScriptPubKey in xpub.ts already decodes bech32m; needs explicit per-type construction tests incl. taproot output + addressFromScript p2tr reverse mapping for summaries. (2) extend multisig.ts VaultConfig with scriptType 'p2wsh'|'p2sh-p2wsh'|'p2sh' (default p2wsh, the recommended type): sh(sortedmulti) and sh(wsh(sortedmulti)) derivation + descriptor build/parse, BIP-48 script suffix 1 for both P2SH forms (Bastion convention). Taproot multisig explicitly out of scope. Emulator bead cairn-a4k must include a bc1p send.
close_reason: Shipped in two commits: six-destination verification with per-type tests (found+fixed flat output-vsize fee undercount on sweeps to p2wsh/p2tr and uppercase-bech32 client rejection; witness-v1 32-byte rule was already present and is now pinned), and P2SH/P2SH-P2WSH vault script types with per-type descriptor round-trips. 36 new tests; 422/422 passing, check 0 errors.

title: Address watcher subscriptions leak — never removed, no unsubscribe RPC
description: addressWatcher.ts:645-700 — refreshWatches only adds scripthashes to state.byScripthash, never removes. deleteWallet/deleteMultisig clean up DB but never touch watcher. No blockchain.scripthash.unsubscribe method exists in electrum/client.ts. resubscribe() (client.ts:338-347) replays entire accumulated scripthashSubs set serially on every reconnect, so reconnect cost grows with cumulative wallet churn, not current wallet count. On flaky ARM/Umbrel connectivity this compounds badly. Fix: add removal path diffing desired vs byScripthash, wire to wallet/multisig delete, implement unsubscribe RPC.
close_reason: done

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Phase 1 landed in acc0b69 (shared with cairn-uzgu): the LOCAL-STATE leak is fixed — refreshWatches now diffs desired-vs-current and prunes byScripthash/baselinedScripthashes/inFlight, wired to deleteWallet/deleteMultisig via unwatchWallet/unwatchMultisig, plus a walletStillExists notify-guard. REMAINING (Phase 2, keep open): (1) no blockchain.scripthash.unsubscribe RPC in electrum/client.ts so the server-side subscription persists until the socket cycles; (2) resubscribe() still replays the entire accumulated scripthashSubs set serially on every reconnect, so reconnect cost grows with cumulative churn (the ARM/Umbrel pain point). Needs the unsubscribe RPC + pruning scripthashSubs on unwatch. Deferred to avoid colliding with cairn-vn48's client.ts change.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Phase 2 FIXED (Phase 1 landed earlier in acc0b69).

Root cause (remaining Phase 2, as filed): (1) no blockchain.scripthash.unsubscribe RPC existed in electrum/client.ts, so the server-side subscription persisted until the socket cycled; (2) ElectrumClient.resubscribe() replayed the entire accumulated scripthashSubs set on every reconnect, so reconnect cost grew with cumulative wallet churn (the ARM/Umbrel reconnect-storm pain point) rather than the current wallet count -- because scripthashSubs was add-only and never pruned on unwatch.

Fix:
- ElectrumClient.unsubscribeScripthash(scripthash) (client.ts): deletes from scripthashSubs (the set resubscribe() replays -- this alone bounds reconnect cost to the CURRENT watch set) and, when a live socket exists, sends blockchain.scripthash.unsubscribe best-effort (not every server implements it; a rejection is debug-logged and swallowed). Never forces a connect: with no socket there is nothing to tell the server (a sub never survives a socket cycle) and the prune already stopped the leak.
- ElectrumPool.unsubscribeScripthash -> primary.unsubscribeScripthash (subscriptions only ever live on the primary).
- addressWatcher.ts: new releaseSubscription(scripthash) helper (fire-and-forget) wired into all three removal sites -- forgetWatchesFor (deleteWallet/deleteMultisig via unwatchWallet/unwatchMultisig), refreshWatches' desired-vs-current prune (catches FK-cascade/account-deletion paths), and handleScripthashChange's walletStillExists-false self-prune.

Tests (all green):
- electrum/client.test.ts: unsubscribe sends the RPC + is NOT replayed on reconnect while a kept sub still is; no-op for an unsubscribed scripthash; prunes local state with no socket when disconnected.
- addressWatcher.test.ts: deleteWallet now asserts pool.unsubscribeScripthash was called with the watched scripthash; FakePool gained the unsubscribeScripthash mock.

title: Referrals: register referral_links feature flag

Existing categories are wallet|hardware|notifications|upcoming; add a new 'marketing' category (small type union change) since referral links and announcements don't fit existing categories. defaultEnabled: true per convention. Wire requireFeature('referral_links') in any new server routes/actions; check page.data.flags?.referral_links in UI touchpoints to hide all referral UI when off.

Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: Invite-gated signup offers no in-app path -- copy just says 'ask for a code'
description:
Evidence: dev instance 2026-07-12, when registration_mode is 'invite' and a user has no code, the signup page's guidance is just 'ask for a code' (per cairn-keo's fix: 'Invites come from whoever runs this Cairn instance -- ask them for a code.') with no in-app mechanism to actually request one -- no request-access form, no contact surfacing beyond the generic hint, no way to leave a message for the admin. Product decision needed on whether to build a request-access flow (e.g. a lightweight form that notifies the admin) or whether better static guidance (configurable admin contact info) is sufficient.

Searched for overlap first: cairn-keo (CLOSED) added the 'ask for a code' hint text itself but explicitly did not build a request/contact mechanism -- its close comment even floats 'consider surfacing an optional instance name/contact field' as a maybe, not built. cairn-wt0.2 (First-run experience, P2 feature) is a broader onboarding epic-adjacent bead but doesn't specifically address invite-request flow. Searched 'request access', 'ask for a code', 'request-access' -- no hits. Filing as a new idea/product-decision bead, not a bug, since this is a scope/design question rather than a defect.

title: RBF replacement-history link to a replaced tx dead-ends at a generic 404
description:
Page: /explorer/tx/[txid] — Replacement history section on an RBF-replacing transaction.

What I did: pulled a live RBF replacement pair from mempool.space's public /api/v1/replacements feed (a genuinely still-in-mempool transaction that had replaced an earlier version of itself). Viewed the current version in Cairn at /explorer/tx/5b0666d8d1b9b79cfc8300c16c838af13805578d94219e6e74d4b1414336c788 — its "REPLACEMENT HISTORY" section correctly showed both versions with timestamps: the old txid (e1e82ceb91375fbf4f6736458de83f37b052e2a924383b498b8d235efed14a23, "1m ago") and the current one ("you are here / current version", "41s ago"). Then clicked the old txid's link in that history list.

What happened: the link points to /explorer/tx/e1e82ceb91375fbf4f6736458de83f37b052e2a924383b498b8d235efed14a23, which renders Cairn's generic 404 page ("This path doesn't lead anywhere. Back to dashboard / Open the explorer") — because a replaced transaction is evicted from mempool.space's/Electrum's mempool the moment it's replaced, so there's genuinely nowhere left to fetch it from. Confirmed by navigating to that URL directly as well (same 404).

What should have happened: since the very page that renders this link already has all the context needed (the replaced txid, when it was replaced, and which tx replaced it), the replaced tx's own detail page — or the link itself — should acknowledge that specific situation instead of a generic dead-end. Two reasonable fixes: (a) render the replaced txid as plain text (not a link) in the history list, since it's not a destination that will ever resolve, or (b) have the /explorer/tx/[txid] route recognize a "replaced-away" txid (via the same replacement-history API data) and show a small explanatory page — "This transaction was replaced by RBF — view the current version" with a link forward — instead of the generic 404.

Priority: P3 — minor UX rough edge in an otherwise well-built feature; most users land on the current transaction and are unlikely to click through to a historical predecessor expecting it to render, but the current experience (rich context → dead-end 404) is jarring when they do.
close_reason: Fixed in 74eb35a: the tx not-found path consults the RBF index and 302-redirects evicted replaced txids to the current version with a banner explaining the replacement. Verified with the reporter's exact pair: e1e82ceb... redirected to 5b0666d8... with the banner rendered

title: Address watcher: yield between wallet/multisig derivation batches to stop event-loop stalls
description:
Load test (docs/LOAD-TEST-RESULTS-2026-07-05.md, Scenario 5) found periodic 1.1-4.3s latency spikes every 60-110s under sustained mixed load. Root cause confirmed by code read: src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts.

The watcher's setInterval tick (startAddressWatcher, addressWatcher.ts:653-681, REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS=5*60_000 at line 53) calls refreshWatches() (546-589) -> enumerateAll() (159-196), which:
- Runs a synchronous node:sqlite DatabaseSync query for ALL wallets and ALL multisigs, all users, no LIMIT (163-165, 172-174).
- Per wallet: walletAddresses() (103-123) does a nested `for change of [0,1] x for i in 0..WATCH_WINDOW(30)` = 60 synchronous EC-derivation calls with zero await/setImmediate/setTimeout(0).
- Per multisig: multisigAddresses() (126-146) does the same 60-derivation nested loop, PLUS an extra synchronous DB query per multisig for its keys (N+1 query pattern, 176-178).

This entire (W wallets + M multisigs) * 60-derivation pass runs back-to-back inside one setInterval callback with no yielding anywhere. It scales linearly and unboundedly with total wallet count across all users. This is the prime suspect for the periodic spikes (fires every 5 min, but Node's single-threaded event loop means any in-flight HTTP request during that tick queues behind it).

Note: src/lib/server/notificationQueue.ts's tick() (214-232) was also investigated and is NOT implicated — it already awaits per-row (await processRow(row), line 227) doing real async I/O, so it naturally yields between iterations.

Fix: insert a yield (await new Promise(resolve => setImmediate(resolve)) or equivalent) between each wallet's/multisig's derivation batch inside enumerateAll() (after each walletAddresses()/multisigAddresses() call, addressWatcher.ts ~163-196), so a queued HTTP request can interleave between wallets rather than waiting for the entire enumeration pass. Also consider batching the N+1 multisig-keys query into a single upfront query to remove that inefficiency while touching this code.

Complexity: small. This is a localized, mechanical change to one file - add a yield point in the existing loop structure, no new dependencies or architecture change.

Risks/gotchas:
- Must confirm `setImmediate` (Node-only) is fine here since this is server-only code (it is, per existing addressWatcher.ts patterns using .unref()).
- Yielding mid-enumeration means enumerateAll()'s snapshot of "desired" scripthashes is no longer atomic across the whole pass - verify downstream logic (subscribe/unsubscribe diffing in refreshWatches) tolerates wallets list being read across multiple event-loop turns (e.g. a wallet added mid-scan). Low risk given watchers already tolerate eventual consistency (5-min refresh cadence), but worth a sanity check.
- Don't over-yield: yielding per-address (60x per wallet) would be excessive; yield per-wallet/multisig (i.e., once per ~60-derivation batch) is the right granularity per the load test's observed spike duration.
close_reason: Made enumerateAll async, yielding to event loop (setImmediate) after each wallet/multisig derivation batch in addressWatcher.ts to stop 5-min-tick event-loop stalls. Also batched the N+1 multisig_keys query into a single upfront query. Typecheck clean.

title: Wallet-creation security-policy chooser (single-sig vs shared wallet), Green-style
description: From docs/COMPETITOR-ANALYSIS-R2-2026-07-19.md sec2 (Blockstream Green) + sec7 rec #2. Add a short plain-language decision-helper at the wallet-creation fork: two cards ('Just you, on your device' vs 'Shared - needs more than one approval to spend'), one marked 'Recommended for most people', each with a one-line honest 'protects you from / does not protect you from' note. Model the trust-minimization framing on Green's timelock-degrades-to-self-custody pattern for any future server-assisted recovery ('after N months you can recover with your key alone, even if Cairn is gone'). Do NOT replicate Green's under-communicated 2FA lockout cliff. Doctrine: spec sec4 (no jargon by default), sec6 (safety never hidden).

Client-side stale-while-revalidate: cache last-seen page data to avoid skeleton flash on revisit

Only the page svelte effect blocks that consume streamed promises need to change -- load functions untouched.

title: Build shared API-handler plumbing for wallet/multisig route pairs (walletApi.ts)
description: src/lib/server/walletApi.ts: readSpendRequest (body shaping + coin_control/batch gates), psbtBuildErrorResponse, classifyUtxoMasses, historyCsvResponse, backupFileResponse. Rewired the psbt, utxo-mass, history.csv, and descriptor route pairs; auth gates stayed in route files.
close_reason: Committed in 881ee90; svelte-check clean, full suite (1120) passes

title: Address derivation infers script type from SLIP-132 prefix, can contradict stored script_type
description:
src/lib/server/bitcoin/xpub.ts:220-247 (deriveAddress) switches on `parsed.scriptType`,
which parseXpub derives purely from the SLIP-132 version-byte prefix of the xpub
string (xpub->p2pkh, ypub->p2sh-p2wpkh, zpub->p2wpkh; xpub.ts:14-18,168). This value
is never cross-checked against the wallet's own `script_type` DB column.

Every single-sig receive/change address path calls deriveAddress(parseXpub(wallet.xpub), ...)
and ignores wallet.script_type entirely:
  - src/lib/server/wallets.ts:628 (nextReceiveAddress)
  - src/lib/server/wallets.ts:650 (peekReceiveAddress)
  - src/lib/server/transactions.ts:437-439, 1048-1050, 1200-1201 (change address) —
    note transactions.ts:412 separately reads `wallet.script_type` into a local
    `scriptType` var (used for fee/vsize estimation) while the actual change
    address a few lines later (line 437-439) is derived from the prefix-based
    parsed.scriptType instead — two different notions of "this wallet's script
    type" computed from two different sources in the same function.

At wallet creation (src/lib/server/wallets.ts:320, createWallet) the stored
script_type IS set from parseXpub(xpub).scriptType, so the two values start in
sync and there's a derivation-path/prefix consistency guard
(assertDerivationMatchesPrefix, wallets.ts:287-295) — normal wallet creation
can't produce a mismatch.

However backup restore has no such guard: src/lib/server/backup.ts:390-403
inserts `xpub` and `script_type` straight from the backup JSON's `w.xpub` /
w.script_type fields with no check that script_type actually matches the
xpub's own prefix. A hand-edited, corrupted, or cross-version backup file can
restore a wallet row where script_type='p2wpkh' but xpub is an 'xpub'-prefixed
(p2pkh) key. After restore, the wallet detail page displays/treats the wallet
as p2wpkh (its stored, "declared" script_type) while every receive and change
address actually generated by nextReceiveAddress/peekReceiveAddress/buildDraft
comes out as legacy p2pkh (1...) addresses, because those all re-derive
scriptType from the xpub prefix via parseXpub, not from the stored column.

Risk: a user can be shown/told their wallet is one script type (e.g. native
segwit, lower fees, modern) while actually receiving/spending change to a
different script type's addresses (e.g. legacy) — a real funds-affecting UX
inconsistency, not just cosmetic, since fee estimates, PSBT construction, and
what the user believes their receive address format is can all disagree with
what's actually derived and validated on-chain.

Suggested fix: either (a) always derive scriptType from wallet.script_type
(the stored column) rather than re-deriving from the xpub prefix at every call
site, treating it as the single source of truth, or (b) validate on backup
restore (and anywhere else a wallet row is inserted) that script_type matches
parseXpub(xpub).scriptType and reject/repair mismatches.
close_reason: Fixed in 7df5f53 (integrated): restore boundary now validates parseXpub().scriptType === stored script_type + assertDerivationMatchesPrefix per wallet, and validateCosignerKeyPath per multisig key ('import' mode); mismatched rows skipped per the file's per-row convention with warn logs. Also catches empty/unparseable xpubs (old cairn-90k8 gap) -- its KNOWN GAP tests now assert the fix. 6 new tests + fixture upgrades to real BIP32 vectors.

title: Missing Esplora test file and untested ChainService methods
description: No esplora.test.ts exists. Many ChainService methods untested: getBtcUsdPrice, getHashrate, getDifficultyInfo, getMempoolBlocks, getNodeInfo, testElectrum/testEsplora, getTxRbfInfo. No pool partial-failure tests. Fix: add esplora.test.ts, add tests for each untested method.
close_reason: Test-gap closed: getNodeInfo, testElectrum, and pool-identification partial-failure coverage added to chain.test.ts (esplora.test.ts item is moot post-Esplora-removal); see comment for detail

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Re-verified against current code (post cairn-zoz8.16 Esplora removal). esplora.test.ts is now moot -- Esplora is fully removed from the codebase (no esplora.ts/testEsplora anywhere). Of the originally-listed untested methods, most already gained thorough coverage in intervening work (chain.test.ts was already 2139+ lines before this fix): getBtcUsdPrice, getHashrate, getDifficultyInfo, getMempoolBlocks, getTxRbfInfo all have describe blocks.

Remaining real gaps closed here:
1. getNodeInfo had zero coverage -- added 4 tests (connected+banner success, banner-unsupported degrade, headersSubscribe failure -> disconnected, unhashable header -> tipHash null while staying connected).
2. testElectrum (chain/index.ts export) was only ever exercised via a vi.fn() mock in the admin-settings auth-bypass test, never its own logic -- added 4 tests against a stubbed ElectrumClient class (success, banner-unsupported degrade, connection failure, close-always-called).
3. "no pool partial-failure tests" was still true and real: identifyBlockPool in getRecentBlocks had ZERO coverage (core.getBlock was never even stubbed in any getRecentBlocks test) -- added a "pool identification" sub-describe with 4 tests covering success, pool-fails-stats-ok, stats-fail-pool-ok (proving the independent try/catch at index.ts ~694-713), and the immutable pool cache.

All 115 tests in src/lib/server/chain/chain.test.ts pass (was 96 before this change). Fixed in commit on single-sig-full-wallet.

title: Fraunces hero serif never loads — font-family name mismatch with @fontsource-variable/fraunces
description:
Confirmed via document.fonts + computed font-family inspection on branch design/evergreen-identity @ d0dce27.

src/app.css:109 defines: --font-serif: 'Fraunces', 'Source Serif 4', Georgia, serif;
But the imported webfont package (src/app.css:14 @import '@fontsource-variable/fraunces') registers its @font-face under the family name "Fraunces Variable" (fontsource-variable naming convention), NOT plain "Fraunces".

document.fonts on Home (authenticated) shows exactly one custom family, "Fraunces Variable", all 3 weight-variant FontFace entries status "unloaded" — nothing on any page ever requests that exact family string, so the font-face is never triggered to load. The BTC-amount hero element's computed font-family resolves to 'Fraunces, "Source Serif 4", Georgia, serif' — since "Fraunces" matches no installed/loaded font, it silently falls through to the "Source Serif 4" / Georgia / generic-serif fallback stack on every render, everywhere in the app.

This defeats the redesign's signature typographic identity (Fraunces hero serif, per docs/DESIGN-MANIFESTO.md) with no visible error — it just quietly renders serif-fallback everywhere the design calls for Fraunces.

Fix is a one-line token correction: --font-serif should reference 'Fraunces Variable' (or import a non-variable Fraunces build under the plain name).

Filed during cairn-sdx5.8 QA sweep (non-pixel pass).
close_reason: Confirmed: src/app.css:109 --font-serif referenced the plain family name 'Fraunces', but @fontsource-variable/fraunces (node_modules/@fontsource-variable/fraunces/index.css) registers all its @font-face rules under font-family: 'Fraunces Variable' (fontsource-variable naming convention), so no installed font ever matched 'Fraunces' and every hero balance silently fell back to 'Source Serif 4'/Georgia/serif. Fix: src/app.css:109 changed to --font-serif: 'Fraunces Variable', 'Source Serif 4', Georgia, serif; (fallback stack preserved). Verified against node_modules/@fontsource-variable/fraunces/index.css that 'Fraunces Variable' is the exact registered family string. npm test 3668 passed (1 pre-existing unrelated flaky timing test), npm run check 0 errors/0 warnings (svelte-check does not typecheck CSS custom-property string values, consistent with this bug being invisible to it). Duplicate of cairn-sdx5.11 (closed as dupe).

title: Full UX redesign — simple and confident
description: Alex directive 2026-07-14: app feels cluttered/not intuitive. Principles: less is more (first-week test for visibility), one primary action per screen, no jargon by default (Advanced/Details expanders), generous whitespace, clear hierarchy. Screens: Home, wallet detail, send flow (3 steps max, Venmo-feel), explorer (visual not data-wall), admin/settings (grouped), navigation. Spec at docs/UX-REDESIGN-SPEC.md, then implement Home + Send first.

title: UX Phase 1: Home simplification + deletion wins
description: Per docs/UX-REDESIGN-SPEC.md section 2.1 + Phase 1. Rebuild Home (src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte): zero-wallet State A (one 'Add your first wallet' button + collapsed 'What Cairn does' expander); funded State B (hero balance, Send+Receive, one calm Health line, compact wallet list ONLY when >1 wallet, RECENT omitted when empty, inline eye toggle). Pure-deletion wins day one (both proposals): kill doubled '0 sats' on Home AND wallet detail, kill empty-chart range picker, kill Explorer footer duplicate strip, move always-visible xpub fragment off wallet-detail surface. Ship a minimal Home Health line reading unbackedWallets + /api/chain-health (full Health page is Phase 3). Files: +page.svelte (primary), wallets/[id]/+page.svelte (doubled-balance + xpub-fragment only), explorer/+page.svelte (footer), a health derivation helper, +layout.svelte. Blast radius medium (front door). Verify: dev server + seed 1 funded/1 empty wallet, screenshot 1280x800 and 375x812, confirm all deletions and the three Home states; Home/portfolio tests green.
close_reason: Implemented in d54eee5 (integrated): Home rebuilt per docs/UX-REDESIGN-SPEC.md Phase 1 -- zero-wallet welcome state, hero balance + Send/Receive + Health line, wallet list only when >1 wallet, activity only when non-empty; chart/range-picker/duplicate-balance/welcome-card/synced-chip removed, fiat toggle relocated to Settings; new homeView.ts state logic with 12 tests; structural browser verification of zero + multi states.

title: Distribute self-custody risk education across teachable moments (not an onboarding wall)
description: Research bead from docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-RESEARCH-R3-2026-07-19.md (finding F25, improvement R21). Just-in-time / teachable-moment literature: risk education lands when delivered at the moment of relevance (just-in-time phishing feedback cut repeat susceptibility; contextual micro-training sustained 60-80% improvement) and the teachable window is brief. Front-loading all self-custody risk in onboarding causes numbing/overload and drives the seed-phrase dropoff. Restructure the risk 'curriculum' so each lesson fires at ITS teachable moment: 'no reset button' at the recovery-phrase step; 'back it up' when first real funds arrive (ties gt05.5/gt05.6); 'a lost cosigner key can lock funds' at multisig/shared-wallet creation; 'this address is only yours' at first receive. Each lesson short (one plain idea), paired with an efficacy action (see gt05.8). Removes the onboarding warning-wall. Touches: onboarding wizard, first-deposit/receive states, multisig creation flow. Larger — spans several surfaces. Effort M/L.

title: Semantic-transparency send review: make verifying the recipient the explicit act, own-node as trust anchor
description: Research bead from docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-RESEARCH-R3-2026-07-19.md (findings F20+F21, improvement R17). Crypto-wallet study ('What I Sign Is Not What I See', 2026): 64% of users trust a FAMILIAR interface over verifiable data; only 32% check the underlying data; interface-familiarity substitutes for verification. 'Semantic transparency' (reconstruct + restate signing intent before confirm) lifted correct-identification 67.9%->84.2% and cut cognitive load. The shipped ReviewDisplay already restates amount+recipient as the hero — this bead hardens it into a deliberate verification act: phrase the review as a first-person check the user OWNS ('Confirm this is going to the right place'), reuse R1 R2 recognition aids (grouped address, emphasized ends), and surface the own-node-as-authority cue at trust-fragile confirms ('your node confirmed this', the sovereignty payoff) — a warranted-trust competence cue, NOT a reassurance/badge banner (manifesto bans badge-wall theater; transparency has an inverted-U, so keep it to the one load-bearing check, don't over-explain). Complements R2 F11 (diffusion of responsibility in multi-party signing). Touches: ReviewDisplay, send review/sign step, first-deposit confirm. Buildable now, rides gt05.2, no doctrine change. Effort S/M.

title: UX Phase 2: Send (fee-line) + wallet detail
description: Per docs/UX-REDESIGN-SPEC.md sections 2.2/2.3/2.4 + Phase 2. SEND (wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte, 2846 lines - reorder within create/review state machine, do NOT rewrite build/sign/broadcast): strip create step to amount + recipient + one 'Review payment' button + quiet 'Send everything' link + collapsed 'Advanced' expander holding CoinControl; move FeeChoice/FeeEstimates onto the ReviewDisplay card as one expandable line with plain-language speeds (Priority ~10min / Standard ~30min default / Economy); delete AtTipPill from Send; remove create-step HowItWorks/PSBT block (quiet Term-glossed link on sign step); replace zero-balance bare '0' with empty-state + Receive CTA; 'Max' -> 'Send everything'. RECEIVE: formalize canonical /wallets/[id]/receive subpage, route Home + wallet-detail Receive buttons to it (keep panel verbatim - it is the disclosure model). WALLET DETAIL (wallets/[id]/+page.svelte, 2429): 'Single-key' chip, move xpub/type/signing-model/chart into 'Wallet details' expander, add /wallets/[id]/settings subpage (rename / 'Download backup file' / address list / red-gated remove), collapse tabs to Activity+Receive, drop 'Addresses 40' tab. Blast radius medium-high (Send correctness). Guard with send tests; fee already computes at review. Verify: drive create->review->sign->confirm->sent, two inputs before review, one fee line, no 'at tip'; zero-balance routes to Receive; xpub/chart only under Details; remove gated. Screenshots desktop+mobile.

title: UX Phase 3: Settings grouping + Admin to Health
description: Per docs/UX-REDESIGN-SPEC.md section 2.6 + Phase 3. SETTINGS (settings/+page.svelte, presentational regroup only): five groups Account / Display / Security / Advanced / Danger; merge Units+Fiat+Theme into Display; API tokens -> Advanced; Contacts hidden unless team mode on; Recovery phrase -> Security, ranked first; Danger-zone 4-bullet caveat wall -> one sentence + confirm. ADMIN->HEALTH (admin/+page.svelte + sub-nav): rename Node->Health everywhere; status headline + four monitored duties (Node/Backups/Storage/Users); promote backup nudge (amber + 'Back up now'); collapse 9 tabs behind 'Instance settings' + contextual links; Uptime/host:port -> Node Details; factory reset -> bottom, red, confirm-gated subpage. Render the full Health object (single derived object from unbackedWallets + /api/chain-health + storage + users) that Phase 1's Home line already reads; re-point layout backup/chain banners (ChainHealthBanner, +layout.svelte) at it so Home line + banner + Health page are one truth. Blast radius low-medium (Settings pure regroup; admin-only; Health is aggregation over existing data). Verify: five Settings groups, Contacts hidden solo, API tokens under Advanced; Health headline + working amber 'Back up now' + gated factory reset; Home line/banner/page agree; admin+destructive-ops tests green.

title: UX Phase 4: Navigation rewrite (last) + Explorer de-jargon
description: Per docs/UX-REDESIGN-SPEC.md sections 2.7/2.5 + Phase 4. DO LAST - after every destination has its new home (Health must exist before Node->Health rename; Receive/settings subpages before nav points at them). NAV (+layout.svelte, heartwood/MobileTabRow.svelte, HWRail.svelte, account-menu markup): reduce primary nav to Home / Wallets / Activity (same desktop+mobile); move Explorer ('Explore the blockchain') / Health / Settings / Notifications into the account menu; rename Node->Health in nav; one active accent nav element; FIX the duplicate <nav> landmark (remove/aria-hidden the second 'Sections' block - real a11y bug, 'Home' appears 3x); accessible label on notifications bell; keep unread badge on avatar. EXPLORER DE-JARGON (explorer/+page.svelte, mempool, block/[id], tx/[txid]) - independent, can run parallel: shared FeeRate component (raw sat/vB + plain time side by side), Term glosses for ring/timechain/vMB/SegWit/PSBT, keep the Heartwood ring metaphor glossed-once (not deleted), one honest node-status line owned by Health (no 'unreachable' next to full data), kill 'vMB'/'not one removed'. Blast radius highest (layout wraps every screen; a11y tree app-wide). Verify: all breakpoints (desktop rail, mobile tab row, mobile account menu), exactly one <nav> landmark, 3 primaries with one active accent, avatar reaches Explorer/Health/Settings/Notifications, screen-reader pass; Explorer FeeRate raw+plain everywhere, no footer dupe.

title: Backup nudge: decaying + polymorphic + state-driven cadence (no fixed timer)
description: Research bead from docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-RESEARCH-R2-2026-07-18.md (finding F16, improvement R14). The 'back up now' amber nudge must NOT re-surface on a fixed interval — habituation collapses response by the 2nd exposure (Anderson CHI 2015) and fixed cadences trend toward alarm fatigue. Implement: surface once at earned moment (first meaningful balance), then DECAY to widening intervals (illustrative: day 3, day 10, day 30, quarterly); ESCALATE only on stakes-raising state change (balance crosses threshold, second wallet unbacked) not on the clock; POLYMORPHISM — vary phrasing/illustration across re-surfacings, never byte-identical amber row twice; CAP so it can never become per-session. Stays in Health calm amber grammar, never modal, never red (un-actioned backup = attention, not error). Touches: Health nudge component + a small cadence/scheduler seam. Non-doctrine (implements the notification-throttling MUST + amber grammar). Effort M.
close_reason: Implemented Spec A (docs/UX-BACKUP-NUDGE-AND-FIRST-DEPOSIT-SPEC.md): new backup_nudges table (db.ts) + getDueBackupNudge/escalateBackupNudge (backups.ts) replace the old sessionStorage-only unbacked banner in (app)/+layout.server.ts and +layout.svelte with a server-persisted +3/+10/+30/+90-day decay ladder, a 72h hard cap, MULTI/FUNDED escalation (hooked from addressWatcher.ts's tx_received path), and 5 rotating calm copy variants + 2 escalated variants. 9 new unit tests (backups.test.ts) cover the decay-schedule pure function and escalation/cap interaction; npm run check clean (0 errors/warnings); browser verification deferred to QA post-release retest.

title: First-deposit confidence: agency-giving empty + pending states
description: Research bead from docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-RESEARCH-R2-2026-07-18.md (finding F17, improvement R15). Pre-first-funds 'is this really mine' and first-deposit-pending are intolerance-of-uncertainty states that drive compulsive checking (block-explorer refreshing). Answer with AGENCY + MECHANISM, not reassurance-theater. (1) Empty/receive state carries a mechanism-fact in the R5 register: 'This address is yours. Anything sent here is controlled only by your keys.' — not 'don't worry'. (2) First-deposit-pending state answers the check ONCE, self-updating from the user's own node: 'Seen your incoming payment. Spendable after the network confirms it (about an hour).' — removes the reason to leave for a third-party explorer (sovereignty payoff). Reuses round-1 empty-state doctrine ('This wallet is ready. Receive your first bitcoin'); adds the pending half. Touches: Receive, empty states, tx-pending display. Non-doctrine. Effort S.
close_reason: Implemented in 95ebd66: neverFunded mechanism-fact confidence line ('This address belongs to your wallet...') gated on scan/detail truthy + empty history + zero unconfirmed, and hasIncomingPending self-updating .hw-pending-note (hedged 'usually within an hour', points at activity feed, 'this is your own node telling you') in both src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte and src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte. Tx-row local 'confirming now' label for 0-conf inbound rows added without touching burialRingsLabel. npm run check clean (2096 files, 0 errors).

title: Retry-safety statement on send/broadcast failures
description: Research bead from docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-RESEARCH-R3-2026-07-19.md (finding F23, improvement R19). Error aversion in irreversible-money contexts makes users freeze between retry and abandon; the specific fear after a failed send is 'did it half-send / will retry double-spend my money?'. Every send/broadcast failure surface (broadcast rejected, node unreachable at broadcast, sign failed, timeout) must state the fund-state explicitly and whether retry is safe: e.g. 'This payment was not sent. Nothing left your wallet — you can safely try again.' Preserve the user's draft (don't dump them back to an empty create screen). Non-accusatory, plain-language (NN/g heuristic 9). This is the genuinely-new, NON-color part of failure copy; the degraded-vs-broken COLOR rule is R2 R13 / R2 section3.1 and stays PENDING-ALEX. Touches: send state machine confirm/broadcast error paths, ReviewDisplay/sign step. Buildable now, no doctrine change. Effort S/M.

title: Pair every self-custody risk line with an immediately-doable efficacy action (PMT)
description: Research bead from docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-RESEARCH-R3-2026-07-19.md (finding F24, improvement R20). Protection Motivation Theory / EPPM: fear WITHOUT efficacy produces defensive avoidance, denial, fatalism — the mechanism behind the ~70% seed-phrase-backup onboarding dropoff. Efficacy (can-I-do-it) is the STRONGEST PMT predictor, stronger than threat. Rule: never state a self-custody risk ('there is no reset button', 'lose this and the funds are gone') without an immediately-available, do-able action attached in the same view. Apply at: recovery-phrase step (risk + 'Save it now' action, verify-once), backup nudge (risk + 'Back up now'), any 'you are the bank' copy. Never a wall of risk bullets (numbing/overload — over-communication increases anxiety and avoidance). Aligns with manifesto no-caveat-wall + friction ladder; PMT is the WHY. Touches: onboarding/recovery-phrase wizard step, Health backup nudge copy. Buildable now, no doctrine change. Effort M.

title: Notification severity + actionability taxonomy (batch non-actionable, real-time only for actionable-urgent)
description: Research bead from docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-RESEARCH-R3-2026-07-19.md (findings F26+F27, improvement R22). Alarm-fatigue + interruption-cost literature: only ~5-13% of clinical alarms are actionable; tier by consequence x time-to-respond, NOT deviation magnitude; batching 3x/day beat constant delivery (constant raised workload + errors); deliver at breakpoints to cut resumption lag. Cairn emits tx / backup / cosigner / system-health notifications — currently undifferentiated. Build a severity+actionability matrix: (1) actionable AND time-critical (e.g. a cosigner action is genuinely needed) -> may notify near-real-time; (2) actionable not-urgent (backup due) -> nudge in-app, decay cadence per gt05.5, no push; (3) informational/non-actionable (tx confirmed, system-health blips) -> BATCH into a digest / fold into the Health object, never individual pushes. Price notifications already banned by manifesto — keep. System-health should aggregate into Health (spec 2.6b), not ping individually. Bell badge on avatar (spec 2.7) is already a pull model — endorse and route batched classes there. Touches: notification scheduler/emit layer, Health aggregation, notification panel. Buildable now, implements the notification-throttling MUST. Effort M.

title: P2: migrateMiningDefault only writes mining-off row when userCount===0, so UPGRADED installs expose mining UI (contradicts soft-launch)
description: src/lib/server/miningDefaultMigration.ts migrateMiningDefault() writes the mining-off flag row only when userCount()===0. Upgraded installs (0.2.33->0.2.35) already have >=1 user, so the row is never written; registry defaultEnabled:true resolves mining ON, exposing the mining UI to all upgraded Umbrel users against the soft-launch. FIX: write the off-row whenever the mining flag has NO row at all, regardless of user count; never touch an existing row (operators who explicitly enabled mining keep it).
close_reason: FIXED in v0.2.36 (commit 9331050). migrateMiningDefault() now writes the mining-off row whenever the 'mining' feature_flags row is ABSENT (fresh OR upgraded install), not only when userCount()===0; an existing row (admin-toggled either way) is never touched. EVIDENCE: booted prod server against a DB seeded with a user but no mining row -> log 'mining_defaulted_off: no mining flag row: solo-mining defaulted off'; browser sidebar (desktop+mobile) showed Home/Explorer/Wallets/Activity/Node/Settings with NO Mining item. New unit test 'defaults mining off on an UPGRADED install (users present, no mining row yet)'. Full suite 4029 passed.

title: Wallet Navigator
description: Watch-only wallet tracking: import an extended public key (xpub/ypub/zpub), view balances and per-address usage, browse transaction history, and generate receive addresses. Strictly observational in v1 — no transaction construction, PSBTs, or signing.
close_reason: All wallet navigator children complete in 5595b82

title: Wallet list page
description: Cards for each imported wallet: name, script type, confirmed balance, pending delta, and last activity. Empty state introduces watch-only wallets and routes into the import wizard.
close_reason: Implemented in 5595b82: wallet grid with balances, script badges, pending/unreachable indicators, onboarding empty state

title: Wallet import wizard
description: Guided five-step import: choose type (watch-only xpub), paste the extended public key with a plain-language explanation of what an xpub is and is not, preview derived addresses for verification against the source wallet, name the wallet, and land on the populated wallet view. Server-side validation with friendly errors.
close_reason: Implemented in 5595b82: five-step guided import (type, key with xpub explainer, derived-address preview, name, done banner) with server-side validation

title: Wallet detail page
description: Balance hero (confirmed + pending), transaction history with direction and net amounts, and an address inventory split into used/unused with derivation paths, per-address balances, and usage counts.
close_reason: Implemented in 5595b82: balance hero, transactions and used/unused address tabs with derivation paths, two-click delete

title: Receive address generation with QR
description: Serve the next unused receive address with a QR code, derivation path, and copy affordance. Advancing stays within the BIP44 gap limit so funds are always visible to recovering wallets.
close_reason: Implemented in 5595b82: QR receive card with gap-limit-clamped next-address generation, verified live (m/0/40 -> m/0/41)

title: Vault wizard Back navigation doesn't reset device-selection state
description:
Reported by Alex, found immediately after the Ledger Buffer-error bug (see sibling bead): after a failed Ledger connection attempt, clicking Back returns to the same failed Ledger connect view instead of the device-selection picker -- the only way out is a full page refresh.

Root cause: src/routes/(app)/vaults/new/+page.svelte holds `method` (which signing/import method is active: trezor/ledger/coldcard/qr/paste) as page-level $state, not scoped to a step. There IS a working reset path -- the 'Different source' button inside the active-method view calls resetKeyForm() (line ~184), which correctly clears method, error state, and the QR scanner -- but the WIZARD'S OWN step-level Back buttons (e.g. the Review step's back-to-Keys button at line ~1297: onclick={() => (step = 'keys')}, and similar step transitions elsewhere in the file) just flip `step` without ever calling resetKeyForm(). Whatever Back control Alex actually hit (likely a step-level Back rather than the in-form 'Different source' link, possibly compounded by the crash happening before 'Different source' was reachable) lands back on step 'keys' with the stale method/addError state still set, re-rendering the same failed device view.

Fix: make sure returning to the Keys step -- by whichever Back control the wizard actually offers at that point, including any path reachable right after a device-connect error -- always lands on the device-selection picker (method === null) with a clean addError, not whatever method/error state was left over from before. Reuse resetKeyForm() rather than inventing a second reset path.
close_reason: Fixed: goToKeys() runs resetKeyForm() on every entry into the Keys step (Step 1 Continue + Review Back). Live-verified: stale Ledger view cleared, keys preserved.

title: [pool-ux] Admin blocks ledger: rejected submissions show confirmation counts and duplicate same-height rows are unexplained
description: Observed live on /admin/mining after concurrent workers raced the same jobs on regtest: the Blocks-found ledger shows rows like height 284 three times (2 Rejected + 1 Mature) with the Rejected rows displaying the same reward AND a live confirmation count (e.g. 958,436) as if they had confirmed. Evidence: getAdminMiningView (readModels.ts) computes confirmations: coinbaseMaturity(row.height, tipHeight).confirmations for every row regardless of submit_result, and AdminBlocksLedger renders it unconditionally. Fix (plain language): for rejected rows show a dash in the confirmations column, and visually group duplicate-height submissions (or annotate: another miner submitted this block first) so the ledger reads as a trustworthy record rather than contradicting itself. Note: duplicate submissions are engine-truthful data - display grouping only, do not drop rows.

title: Session cookie missing secure flag (challenge cookies have it, sessions don't)
description: Security review of the new dual-auth/passkey system found: setSessionCookie() in src/lib/server/auth.ts (~lines 85-92) sets httpOnly + sameSite:lax but never sets secure. Compare to the WebAuthn challenge cookie (src/lib/server/webauthn.ts:147), which correctly sets secure: event.url.protocol === 'https:'. Session cookies should match that pattern. Impact: in a misconfigured deployment or over plain HTTP, session tokens would be transmitted unencrypted and sniffable on the network -- the exact class of thing httpOnly/sameSite are meant to help guard, undermined by the missing secure flag. Fix: add the same secure: event.url.protocol === 'https:' check to setSessionCookie, matching the already-correct challenge-cookie pattern in the same codebase.
close_reason: Fixed: setSessionCookie takes the request URL and sets secure: protocol==='https:' explicitly at all six call sites (login/register password+passkey verify, settings password rotation), matching webauthn.ts's challenge-cookie pattern. Deliberately protocol-following rather than always-true so Umbrel http-LAN logins keep working per the auth-model constraint.

title: Signup: invalid invite code fails silently (no error shown) most of the time
description:
Submitting the signup form with an invalid/wrong invite code returns HTTP 400 from POST /api/auth/register/password, but the UI only shows the 'That invite code is not valid.' banner intermittently. Reproduced 2 of 3 identical attempts (different bogus codes each time) where the request 400'd but NO error message appeared at all -- the form just sat there, and in one case the already-typed name/email/password fields were silently wiped while the invite code field was kept. A first-time non-technical user would have no idea why account creation isn't working and might retype their info repeatedly with no feedback.

Repro: go to /signup, fill in name/email/password + a bogus invite code, submit. Repeat 3x with different bogus codes. Error banner appears ~1/3 of the time.
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Repeatable load-test harness: concurrent sessions + rapid-fire API + latency/event-loop report
description: All prior load testing was ad-hoc (the 2026-07-05 session that produced cairn-ggvt/fd56/ynfp); nothing scripted/reusable exists. Build a scenario runner: N parallel authenticated sessions doing dashboard->wallet->send-draft->explorer loops plus rapid-fire hits on /api/portfolio and label/history endpoints, against a prod build seeded with the scale fixtures, emitting p50/p95 latency and monitorEventLoopDelay histograms (reuses cairn-1q4b's measurement approach). Becomes the standing regression gate for the cairn-6xxa perf work.
close_reason: Repeatable harness built + run: scripts/load-test/run.mjs, offline Electrum-free bootstrap, 200-user seed, p50/p95/p99 + server event-loop lag per scenario x tier, JSON artifacts, safety-guarded throwaway DB. Commit 8b7b110f384299bf11d1076de3dccadb8298ec82 on test/qa-wave-2026-07-12. Full results in docs/TEST-REPORT-2026-07-12.md.

title: Signup page doesn't prefill invite code from ?invite= URL query param
description:
Reproduced on Heartwood v0.2.0 at umbrel.local:3211 (browser QA).

Navigating to /signup?invite=CAIRN-XXXX-XXXX shows the signup form with the Invite code field completely empty -- the query param is silently ignored. The user has to manually copy-paste the code from wherever it was shared, even though the URL already carries it.

Root cause: src/routes/(auth)/signup/+page.svelte:9 initializes `let inviteCode = $state('')` with no read of the URL search params, and the load function in +page.server.ts (src/routes/(auth)/signup/+page.server.ts) never returns/reads an `invite` query value either.

This matters because the admin Invites page (/admin/invites) presents invite codes as bare strings to copy, but the natural way an operator shares one is a link (e.g. "join here: https://.../signup?invite=CAIRN-XXXX"). Right now that link degrades to "go to the signup page and then also separately paste this code."

Suggested fix: in +page.svelte, seed `inviteCode` from `page.url.searchParams.get('invite')` on mount (or thread it through the load function like registrationMode/needsInvite already are).

Repro: create any invite in /admin/invites, sign out, visit /signup?invite=<the code>, observe the Invite code field is blank.
close_reason: Fixed as part of the Track F merge (1a76aea). Signup's load function now reads ?invite= from the URL and threads it through the same way registrationMode/needsInvite already are, seeding the Invite code field instead of leaving it blank. npm run check 0/0.

title: Vault creation wizard doesn't warn when two keys share the same master fingerprint
description:
Page: /vaults/new (Add keys step)

What I did: created a 2-of-3 vault, pasting three extended public keys that
were all derived from the same master seed at different BIP-48 accounts
(m/48'/0'/0'/2', m/48'/0'/1'/2', m/48'/0'/2'/2') via the "Paste public key"
option, each with its origin annotation e.g. [73c5da0a/48'/0'/0'/2']xpub...
Named them Test Key 1/2/3 for clarity.

What happened: the wizard accepted all three with no warning, even though
all three share the identical master fingerprint (73c5da0a) -- clearly
visible right there in the key list next to each key's name. Nothing in the
"Add keys," "Review," or "Confirm" steps flagged this. The vault was created
successfully with all 3 keys effectively coming from the same underlying
seed, defeating the entire point of an M-of-N vault: compromising ONE device
(or seed backup) could compromise 2 or 3 of the "independent" keys at once.

What should happen: when two or more keys in the same vault share a master
fingerprint, the wizard should show a clear warning (e.g. "Test Key 1 and
Test Key 2 have the same master fingerprint — they likely come from the same
device or seed. For real protection, each key should live on a separate
device or in a separate place.") Whether this should be a hard block or just
a prominent, dismissible warning is a product call -- there could be
legitimate reasons (e.g. re-testing with different derivation paths off one
seed) -- but right now there's zero signal at all, and a user could build a
vault they believe is protecting them with 3 independent keys when it's
really 1.

Related: this exact scenario (duplicate fingerprints across vault keys) also
triggers a separate, more severe bug in the signing stepper -- see cairn-x54,
where it makes the vault impossible to fully sign at all once one key has
signed. That bug's impact is a direct consequence of this gap letting the
vault be created in the first place.

Priority: P2 -- doesn't corrupt anything by itself, but it's a real security-
transparency gap for a feature whose entire value proposition is "no single
point of failure," and it's the root enabler of cairn-x54's more serious bug.
close_reason: Fixed: sharedFingerprintGroups warning ('00000000' exempt) on Keys + Review steps, prominent but non-blocking, duplicated fingerprints highlighted on Review. Live-verified with exact wording.

title: Vault address list with paths and scripts
description: Caravan-parity: full receive+change address table with derivation path per address, and per-address script display (witnessScript / redeemScript hex) behind a 'verify the details' disclosure for cross-checking against devices/other tools. Extends the detail page's used/unused list (cairn-eo3). Scope refinements after the Caravan research report.
close_reason: Committed: path column with braid-suffix handling, change chips + education, per-address derive-on-demand script/pubkey details via GET address-detail. Live-verified against a fixture 2-of-3 (correct witness script, sorted pubkeys, full paths).

title: Missing regression test: notified_txids rows orphaned forever, no FK cleanup (cairn-zari / cairn-c92m)
description: cairn-zari and cairn-c92m (P1, duplicate beads for the identical bug, both closed in 46dd16f) fixed notified_txids rows never being cleaned up on wallet/multisig deletion or factory reset (no FK to users/wallets/multisigs). Fix confirmed present, no test exists. Add tests: in wallets.test.ts/wallets-multisig.test.ts, assert deleteWallet()/deleteMultisig() cascades away the wallet's/multisig's notified_txids rows; in admin.test.ts, assert resetInstance() clears the whole table.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 834945e (admin.test.ts, notifiedTxidsCleanup.test.ts, settings.test.ts, api/admin/{settings,users,notifications}/server.test.ts). All green in full suite.

title: Vault descriptor + multisig address library
description: wsh(sortedmulti(m, xpub1/../*, ...)) descriptor build/parse/export/import (Sparrow/Electrum-compatible, with checksum), M-of-N address derivation via @scure/btc-signer p2wsh+p2ms over sorted child pubkeys, test-address generation for wizard verification. Pure new files under src/lib/server/bitcoin/.

Byte-identical to Bastion's Core-verified format incl. #e2frtjsz pin; 4 checksum vectors; BIP-67 sort verified; SLIP-132 canonicalization; multi( and sh(wsh( rejected with clear messages. 366/366 suite, check 0 errors.

title: api/search bypasses the explorer feature-flag gate (requireFeature missing)
description:
SEVERITY: P2 (authz/feature-gate bypass; limited to public chain data + backend resource use).

SURFACE: api/search (cairn-jqss audit).

FINDING: src/routes/api/search/+server.ts:7 calls only requireUser(event) — it never calls requireFeature(event,'explorer'). Every explorer page is gated by src/routes/(app)/explorer/+layout.server.ts:9 (requireFeature(event,'explorer')). The 'explorer' flag now defaults OFF on fresh installs (registry.ts:112 + explorerDefaultMigration.ts), so on a default install the whole explorer UI 403s but /api/search stays fully live.

EXPLOIT: Any signed-in non-admin user on an instance where the admin disabled — or never enabled — the explorer flag can still GET /api/search?q=<64-hex|height|address>. classifySearch() then drives real backend chain RPC: getTip() (search.ts:63), getTx() (search.ts:83), getBlock() (search.ts:92). Effect: (1) a disabled feature's backend is reachable and consumes Electrum/Core resources; (2) existence of arbitrary txids/blocks is confirmable (public data, low sensitivity). This violates the exact invariant api.ts documents: 'the 403 here is what makes a disabled flag real against a direct API call.'

FIX: swap requireUser(event) for requireFeature(event,'explorer') in src/routes/api/search/+server.ts. Also audit other /api/* that reach chain data for the same gap.
close_reason: Fixed: /api/search now calls requireFeature(event,'explorer') instead of requireUser, matching the explorer layout gate (src/routes/(app)/explorer/+layout.server.ts:9). Flag-off now 403s before classifySearch runs. src/routes/api/search/+server.ts commit d76f8cb. Regression tests in src/routes/api/search/server.test.ts (flag off -> 403, classifier never called; flag on -> 200).

title: Residual unit-drift: send review card + multisig pending-balance note hardcode BTC
description: Follow-up to cairn-fbgl1 (c299fc3). Two surfaces bypass the shared Amount component and hardcode BTC, ignoring the sats unit pref: (1) src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte ~line 1489 — Send review card uses formatBtc + ' BTC'; (2) src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte ~566-568 — pending-balance note hardcodes ' BTC on its way', inconsistent with its single-sig sibling. Fix: use the formatUnitAmount helper added to format.ts by c299fc3. ~5-minute fix, batched with zltwz-b/7ld60 tail.
close_reason: Both surfaces now use the shared formatUnitAmount helper (added to $lib/format.ts by c299fc3, cairn-fbgl1) instead of hardcoding BTC. (1) send/+page.svelte's 'Verify on device' review card Amount row (was formatBtc(review.amount) + ' BTC') now calls the page's existing summaryUnitAmount(review.amount) helper -- the same $unitPref-aware wrapper around formatUnitAmount that c299fc3 already wired the summary rail through, so it can't drift again. (2) multisig/[id]/+page.svelte's pending-balance note (was manual +/- prefix + formatBtc(...) + ' BTC on its way') now renders <Amount sats={detail.balance.unconfirmed} size="inline" sign direction="in" /> on its way, matching its single-sig sibling (wallets/[id]/+page.svelte) verbatim -- Amount.svelte itself reads unitPref via formatUnitAmount since c299fc3, and its own sign/direction props replace the hand-rolled +/- prefix. Removed the now-unused formatBtc import from the multisig page. npm run check: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

title: notifications.test.ts never exercises notify()/processRow()/tick() integration — only pure helpers tested
description: commit c9483a6 (Notify Unit 1) shipped notifications.test.ts (139 lines) covering resolveRecipients() and the pure backoffMs()/takeToken() helpers, but never calls notify() itself, and never drives notificationQueue.ts's processRow()/tick() state machine — no test asserts a notification_queue row is actually inserted with expected columns, no test confirms the dead-letter transition at attempts>=5 flips status to 'dead' (notificationQueue.ts ~line 192-201), and no test covers the unknown-channel-marks-failed path (~line 143-151) or corrupt-payload-marks-failed path (~line 155-161). These functions aren't even exported for testing (_internals only exposes backoffMs/takeToken/buckets/BACKOFF_MS/MAX_ATTEMPTS) so the integration surface is currently untestable without additional exports or a DB-level integration test. A regression breaking the wiring between processRow and its DB updates (wrong column/status name) would ship undetected despite 139 lines of tests. Fix: export what's needed for testing (or add a DB-integration test using the existing test DB helpers) and cover notify()'s actual INSERT, plus the dead-letter and failed-path transitions in processRow.
close_reason: Resolved by src/lib/server/notificationQueue.test.ts (new file, this session): exercises notify() -> queue insert -> tick()/processRow() end-to-end via _internals.tick, covering sent/failed/dead status transitions, retry backoff progression to MAX_ATTEMPTS, per-channel rate-limit bucket interaction, and preference-gated dispatch (disabled channel never sent, enabled+configured channel is). Also added src/lib/server/channels/email.smtp-integration.test.ts which exercises the real SMTP wire protocol (unmocked nodemailer) against a local TCP stub, including a real PGP encrypt/decrypt round trip.

title: resetInstance has no ROLLBACK — FK violation leaves open transaction permanently
description: admin.ts:107 — Factory reset runs db.exec('BEGIN; DELETE...; COMMIT;') with no try/catch/ROLLBACK. feature_flags.updated_by references users(id) with no cascade and is never cleared, so DELETE FROM users throws FK violation. With no ROLLBACK, the shared DatabaseSync connection is left with an OPEN TRANSACTION, breaking every subsequent BEGIN (restore, account deletion, recovery-code save) until process restart. Fix: wrap in try/catch→ROLLBACK, clear updated_by columns before deleting users.
close_reason: FIXED in 2e759e2 (admin.ts resetInstance). Nulls feature_flags.updated_by + user_feature_flags.updated_by before DELETE FROM users (deleteOwnAccount idiom), and wraps the sequence in explicit BEGIN/try/COMMIT/catch-ROLLBACK instead of a bare db.exec BEGIN;...;COMMIT. An FK error now rolls back cleanly instead of leaving a permanently-open transaction. Test: reset with a toggled feature flag succeeds and a subsequent BEGIN works (verified it threw FK before the fix).

title: Surface multisig as first-class on /wallets; link orphaned stateless route; honest sub-progress in Add-keys step
description: Three multisig-discovery/orientation gaps (Fable UX ranking c > b > d, all below the wizard-resilience #1 = cairn-1u41). (c, #2) Multisig is NOT a first-class choice on /wallets — to create one a user clicks 'Add a wallet' (single-sig flow) then must notice a small hand-off link near the bottom of the Key step: wallets/new/+page.svelte:787-794 'Several keys guarding one wallet? Set up a multisig wallet instead.' The funnel loses users inside the COMPETING flow before they know multisig exists. Add a top-level multisig option on /wallets/+page.svelte (which today only links /wallets/new, lines 95-125). (d, #4) /wallets/multisig/stateless (Caravan-compatible, nothing persisted server-side) has NO inbound link anywhere in src — orphaned/undiscoverable; link it from the multisig hand-off or a footer/docs (confirm with Alex whether it's deliberately advanced-only). (b, #3) The 'Add your N keys' step is ONE dot on a 4-dot progress bar but is internally an N-deep sub-wizard (per key: method -> read/paste/import -> repeat); for 5-of-7 that's 7+ screens hidden in 'step 2 of 4'. Each sub-screen is well-labeled ('Add key 2 of 5') so orientation exists, but expected EFFORT is understated — add a secondary sub-progress element. Do c+d before more users are funneled in; b is polish.
close_reason:
Verified + finished all 3 symptoms:

(c, #2) Multisig first-class on /wallets — ALREADY SHIPPED prior to this pass, commit 979f9f6 "feat(ux): multisig creation discoverable from the wallets page (M1)" (2026-07-12): src/routes/(app)/wallets/+page.svelte has co-equal onboard choice cards (zero-wallet state) + a persistent "Create a multisig wallet" card between the hero header and wallet rows (populated state), both flag-aware. Verified still present and correct on design/evergreen-identity tip. No further code needed.

(d, #4) Orphaned /wallets/multisig/stateless — confirmed genuinely unlinked (grepped all of src/ for inbound references, found none besides the route's own directory). NOT dead code — it's a fully-built Caravan-compatible ephemeral signer (load config -> build tx -> collect signatures -> broadcast, nothing persisted server-side). Linked it from the multisig-creation wizard's existing "Already have this wallet in another app? Import it" disclosure (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte, learn step) as the complementary path: "Or don't save it here at all — work from it directly, nothing saved" -> /wallets/multisig/stateless. This sits exactly where a user importing a config decides between persisting it (existing "Read it" button) and using it ephemerally (new link), and mirrors the stateless page's own back-links to /wallets/multisig/new.

(b, #3) Add-keys sub-progress — added a secondary fraction indicator ("2/5") on the wizard's top-level step bar next to the "Add keys" label, visible from any step once the quorum is chosen (not just while already on that step). Implemented as a pure, unit-tested helper `keysStepSubLabel(quorumValid, keysLength, totalKeys)` in wizardProgress.ts (4 new tests in wizardProgress.test.ts, all passing), wired into +page.svelte's step list and folded into the existing stepAriaLabel for screen readers (the visual badge is aria-hidden to avoid double-announcing).

Evidence: 100/100 tests pass under src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig, 181/181 under src/routes/(app)/wallets; `npx svelte-check` 0 errors/0 warnings across 1990 files. Branch fix/multisig-discoverability, worktree C:\dev\cairn-hla1, base a203614 on design/evergreen-identity. Not pushed/merged.

title: Signup submit fails silently -- no POST fired, no user feedback (valid invite code present)
description:
Evidence: dev instance 2026-07-12, filled a valid signup form (all fields including a VALID invite code) and clicked submit -- no network POST fired at all, no visible error, button appears dead. A brand-new user has no way to tell why registration isn't working. Likely a client-side validation check silently blocking submit on some field even though the tester believed all fields were valid (e.g. password-confirm mismatch, email format, or a hidden required field). Repro: fresh browser, /signup, fill all fields incl. valid invite code, submit -- zero network activity.

Note: state-dependent -- a separate QA run ~1h earlier successfully registered on a DIFFERENT fresh DB with the same general flow, so this may depend on specific field content, invite-required mode nuance, or DB/session state rather than being universal.

Searched for overlap first: cairn-4lp (CLOSED, 'native browser validation silently blocks submit with no visible error, required invite code field') and cairn-1qv7 (CLOSED, 'empty invite code gives no error message, just silent focus') both addressed the EMPTY-field case and were fixed via novalidate + styled inline errors (commits 1f94da6, 22ab66d). cairn-gy79 (CLOSED) covers invalid invite CODE returning 400 with an intermittent banner -- also not a match since this repro has zero network activity, not a 400 response. None of the closed fixes account for a fully-filled valid-looking form still producing zero POST -- filing as a new bug since this looks like either a regression of the closed fixes or a distinct silent-validation path they didn't cover.
close_reason: Root cause: the '(auth) layout secureRedirect HTTPS-probe could location.replace() mid-fill and silently orphan a signup submit ('dead button', no POST, no feedback); the interaction veto (0ff5d53) was only wired to focusin/input on .auth-col. Browser-verified on dev 5173 (invite mode) that the client form is now correct on every path: empty submit renders 'Enter a display name.' banner (no silent block); filled+invalid-invite fires POST /api/auth/register/password -> 400 and shows plain-language 'That invite code is not valid...' banner. Hardened the veto: added a window-level capture-phase pointerdown listener in onMount (marks interacted at the very START of a click gesture, before focus/click/submit) so a pointer-driven submit can never be orphaned by a probe resolving in the focus->submit gap; window listener avoids the a11y_no_static_element_interactions warning a div handler triggers, and is torn down on unmount. npm run check: 0 new errors/warnings from this change (6 pre-existing errors in recoverySetupFlow.test.ts untouched).

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Root cause: (auth)/+layout.svelte maybeRedirectToSecure HTTPS-probe can location.replace() mid-submit when CAIRN_HTTPS_EXTERNAL_PORT is set, orphaning the signup POST silently. Fixed 0ff5d53 (merged): redirect vetoed once the user touches any form field; layout-local, secureRedirect.ts untouched. Race not live-triggered in sandbox -- verification via code path + typecheck; watch on next Umbrel/HTTPS QA.

title: HTTPS listener (3212): first dashboard load after idle takes 10-30s (cold cache)
description:
On v0.2.1, the HTTPS listener (port 3212) dashboard is no longer permanently empty (the previously known issue) — but the very first authenticated GET / after the process has been idle took 10s+ (timed out a 10s curl, completed within 30s), while a repeat request right after returned in 48ms. HTTP port 3211 didn't show this — it was already warm from prior testing in the same session.

Likely just a one-time cache-warm cost (tip/fee-estimate/mempool-blocks TTL cache, cairn-vknb.5) paid independently per listener/process. Not spending more time root-causing since it self-resolves and isn't a hang — flagging so someone checks whether HTTPS and HTTP share the same ChainService cache instance or warm independently (if independent, every HTTPS-only user pays this once per cache expiry).

Found during v0.2.1 perf-fix QA pass, 2026-07-07/08 UTC, against live umbrel.local instance.

title: Backfilled carry-in point for 1yr horizon can be swept by retention purge, degrading Home to "—"
description:
src/lib/server/portfolio.ts:261 defines BACKFILL_HORIZON_MS = 396 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 (396 days) to synthesize a carry-in snapshot point so the Home multi-horizon delta view (cairn-d326, BalanceHorizons.svelte) can show a 1yr change even when real snapshot history doesn't reach back that far.

However, dataRetention.ts's purgeBalanceSnapshots hard-deletes balance_snapshots rows older than ~13 months (see comment at portfolio.ts:171-174, 'Aligned with the retention horizon ... purgeBalanceSnapshots hard-deletes past ~13 months'). 396 days (~13.03 months) sits right at/inside that purge boundary, so a backfilled carry-in point synthesized near the edge of the horizon can itself be swept by the very next retention sweep.

Effect: Home's 1yr horizon can silently degrade to '—' (no data) after a retention sweep runs, even though wallets have long enough history — because the carry-in point it depended on got purged. Meanwhile wallet-detail's horizon view (src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte and multisig/[id]/+page.svelte, added in the same cairn-d326 change) computes its delta client-side from scan.txs rather than from balance_snapshots, so it is unaffected and keeps showing a real value — producing a visible inconsistency between Home and wallet-detail for the same wallet/horizon.

Found during R6 (cairn-d326) integration review, 2026-07-15.

Suggested directions (not yet scoped):
- Widen the retention purge window slightly beyond BACKFILL_HORIZON_MS so the carry-in point always survives one full backfill horizon, or
- Have the backfill logic re-synthesize/refresh the carry-in point on each read rather than relying on a single persisted row surviving, or
- Have Home's 1yr horizon fall back to the same client-derived scan.txs computation wallet-detail already uses when the snapshot-based carry-in is missing.

Acceptance criteria: Home's 1yr horizon should not regress to '—' purely due to retention timing when the wallet has sufficient real history, and/or Home and wallet-detail should agree on whether a 1yr horizon is available.
close_reason: fixed: portfolio.ts's changeWithTxFallback recomputes d365/all from live scanned-tx history (historyFromTxDeltas, same derivation wallet-detail already trusts) and prefers it over the persisted balance_snapshots series for those two long-horizon fields. Root cause confirmed: BACKFILL_HORIZON_MS (396d) backfills a carry-in anchor at a FIXED past instant, while dataRetention's hard purge cutoff is a MOVING now-396d window -- the anchor is guaranteed to fall out of the retention window on some later daily sweep, and backfillSnapshots never re-derives it (one-time guard). Discovered in the process: the bead undersold the bug -- 'all' doesn't degrade to null the way d365 does (it unconditionally reads series[0]), so post-purge it silently reports change vs the next-oldest SURVIVING row instead of the true earliest one, which can flip the sign of the reported change, not just blank it. Retention purge itself (dataRetention.ts) is UNTOUCHED -- its ~13-month privacy-driven cap on persisted balance_snapshots rows still applies exactly as documented; this fix only patches the READ side in assemblePortfolio so Home can't show a horizon that contradicts what wallet-detail's identical tx-derived math already proves is true. New regression tests in portfolio.test.ts use a fake clock to backfill then advance 10 days and run the real purgeBalanceSnapshots(), reproducing the purge before asserting the fix recovers correct values; a companion test confirms the fallback still honestly returns null/falls back rather than fabricating a number when tx history can't be trusted for any one wallet. Full suite: 3767 passed, 1 pre-existing skip, 0 failures; svelte-check 0 errors/0 warnings. Branch fix/backfill-retention-horizon in worktree C:\dev\cairn-ht11 (base 6677a10 v0.2.28).

title: package.json's jadets '^1.1.18' is a caret range, not the pin the in-file comment claims
description: src/lib/hw/jade.ts's header comment (~line 9) says 'pin the version and never auto-update (foundation: jadets@^1.1.18)', but package.json actually specifies "jadets": "^1.1.18" — a caret range permitting any 1.x release up to (excluding) 2.0.0. The lockfile currently resolves to exactly 1.1.18 so today's installs are consistent, but that's an artifact of the lockfile being present/honored, not an actual pin — any workflow that regenerates or bypasses the lockfile (npm update, a fresh install after lockfile deletion, dependabot-style automation) would silently pull a newer 1.x release with no version-string change to flag it for review. Given jade.ts's own comment explicitly flags jadets as an unofficial single-maintainer library requiring extra caution, this undermines the stated mitigation. Fix: change package.json to the exact version "1.1.18" (no caret) so a real version bump requires an explicit, reviewable package.json diff.
close_reason: Pinned jadets to exact 1.1.18 in package.json (was ^1.1.18) and synced package-lock.json's dependency spec. A future bump to this unofficial single-maintainer library now requires an explicit, reviewable package.json diff, matching jade.ts's own header comment ('pin the version and never auto-update').

title: [pool-ux] Admin miners table shows Difficulty 0 for sub-1 share difficulties
description: Observed live on /admin/mining with share difficulty 0.000001: the Miners table Difficulty column renders 0 for every worker (formatNumber rounds sub-1 values away), making the vardiff diagnostic useless exactly when difficulties are small (Bitaxe-class defaults are 0.5 per settings.ts DEFAULT_SHARE_DIFFICULTY). Fix: format small difficulties with adaptive precision (e.g. 0.5, 0.001, 1e-6) instead of integer rounding.

title: Vault key health check
description: Caravan-parity: per-key 'is this key accessible?' verification — for connected devices (Trezor/Ledger) re-read the key at its recorded path and compare fingerprint+xpub; for air-gapped/file keys, a guided address-verification prompt instead. Surface on the vault detail key list ('Last verified ...'). Scope after the Caravan research report.
close_reason: Committed: last_verified_at + markKeyVerified (ownership-checked), device re-read checks compared server-side with SLIP-132 canonicalization, guided manual checks for air-gapped keys, staleness rendering + half-year-windowed dismissible nudge (fresh vaults exempt 7 days). 12 new vaults tests; 581 total green. Caveat noted: device buttons not exercised against real hardware — emulator E2E covers that.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Per Alex's Unchained/Casa research: model health checks on Casa's pattern — PERIODIC prompts to verify keys are still accessible (not just an on-demand button). Detail-page nudge when a key hasn't been verified in N months; connected devices re-read the key at its path and compare fingerprint+xpub, air-gapped keys get a guided address-verification prompt. 'Last verified' per key row.

title: single-sig receive action: QRCode.toDataURL outside try/catch causes unhandled 500
description:
src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts receive action (~lines 74-91): nextReceiveAddress is guarded by try/catch -> fail(502), but the subsequent await QRCode.toDataURL(next.address, QR_OPTS) was OUTSIDE the try, so any QR-encode failure is an unhandled 500 instead of a graceful form-level error. The multisig sibling (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts, ~lines 138-150) already wraps both calls in one try -- mirror that pattern.

Caveat found while fixing: nextReceiveAddress (src/lib/server/wallets.ts:452) can return null, in which case the action calls error(404, 'Wallet not found') -- unlike the multisig sibling's nextMultisigReceiveAddress, which never returns null/throws. Folding the error(404) call inside the same try as QRCode.toDataURL would let the generic catch swallow the thrown SvelteKit HttpError and mis-report it as a 502 "Could not reach the Electrum server" instead of a real 404. Fixed by importing isHttpError from '@sveltejs/kit' and re-throwing when isHttpError(e) is true before falling through to fail(502).
close_reason: Fixed in 275f751 (worktree branch worktree-agent-a76bdb15881777d63). src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts receive action (lines 74-94): folded the QRCode.toDataURL() call into the same try as nextReceiveAddress(), mirroring the multisig sibling (wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts:138-150). Divergence from that sibling: nextReceiveAddress() (src/lib/server/wallets.ts:452) can return null, triggering error(404,'Wallet not found') inside the try -- added isHttpError(e) check (imported from '@sveltejs/kit') to re-throw that HttpError before falling through to fail(502), so a real 404 no longer gets mis-reported as a 502 Electrum failure. Full suite: 2032 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed (npx vitest run). svelte-check: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Fix landed on single-sig-full-wallet as 3fae3de (cherry-picked from worktree commit 275f751 referenced in the close comment above).

title: api/search has no rate limiting — authenticated chain-RPC amplification
description:
SEVERITY: P3 (authenticated DoS amplification against the shared chain backend).

SURFACE: api/search (cairn-jqss audit).

FINDING: src/routes/api/search/+server.ts applies no per-user/per-IP rate limit (unlike /api/auth/* and /api/contacts which use rateLimit.ts). A single GET /api/search?q=<64-hex> fans out to as many as three upstream chain calls: getTip (search.ts:63, for a height query) OR getTx then getBlock (search.ts:83, :92, for a 64-hex query that is not a known tx). CLASSIFY_BUDGET_MS (4s, search.ts:37) bounds per-request latency but NOT request rate or concurrency.

EXPLOIT: An authenticated user runs a tight loop of GET /api/search?q=<random 64-hex that is neither a real tx nor block>. Each request forces getTx()+getBlock() against the Electrum/Core pool and burns the full budget window. Enough concurrent requests exhaust the connection pool and stall chain-data reads (Home, Explorer, portfolio warm) for every other user on the instance — a low-cost self-DoS from any ordinary account.

FIX: add a per-user (or per-IP) fixed-window limiter around the search handler, mirroring rateLimit.ts's existing counters (e.g. the contacts-endpoint pattern). A modest ceiling (e.g. a few searches/sec) is invisible to the real type-ahead UI but caps the amplification. Note: fixing cairn-he4e (flag-gate) reduces but does not remove this — it still applies whenever the explorer flag is on.
close_reason: Fixed: added a per-user + per-IP fixed-window rate limiter (searchRetryAfter/noteSearchRequest, SEARCH_LIMITS = {user:120, ip:300} per the existing 15-min WINDOW_MS) in src/lib/server/rateLimit.ts, mirroring the contacts endpoint's every-request-counts pattern. /api/search now 429s with {code:'rate_limited'} once tripped, before classifySearch runs. commit d76f8cb. Regression tests in src/routes/api/search/server.test.ts (under limit -> 200 x5; at 120 -> 429, classifier not called; a fresh user/IP unaffected by another user's limit).

title: feat: Umbrel zero-config Electrum auto-detect via Docker-network probe (auto-connect Wave A)
description:
Wave A of the Umbrel zero-config Electrum auto-connect design: probeAndSeedUmbrelElectrum() in src/lib/server/umbrelProbe.ts. Strictly gated on CAIRN_PLATFORM==='umbrel' and connection_mode unset. Probes the well-known Umbrel Docker-network fixed IPs -- 10.21.21.10:50001 (electrs) then 10.21.21.200:50002 (fulcrum) -- with a real ElectrumClient.headersSubscribe() handshake, 2s timeout, credential-free (no manifest dependency declaration needed). On first successful handshake, seed-once writes electrum_host/electrum_port/electrum_tls, sets connection_mode='custom', and stamps chain_provisioned_by='umbrel-probe' for the settings-UI provenance card. Never throws; silent fall-through to the existing public-server default when nothing is reachable.

Builds on the already-closed cairn-loq7 (env-var seeding from Umbrel manifest dependencies, chainEnvSeed.ts). Wave A covers installs where the operator has electrs/Fulcrum running on Umbrel but Cairn's manifest doesn't declare a hard dependency on it (env vars therefore never arrive) -- the probe finds it anyway via the shared umbrel_main_network bridge.
