Wiring: chainEnvSeed.ts now also stamps chain_provisioned_by='umbrel-env' (only when the env-provided host was actually adopted); settings.ts/types.ts expose chainProvisionedBy on InstanceSettings; hooks.server.ts calls the probe in init() right after seedChainConfigFromEnv() so env always wins over probe.

Design doc: docs/UMBREL-AUTOCONNECT-DESIGN.md.
close_reason: Shipped in commits b16eea1/1e311c3 on single-sig-full-wallet, pushed to origin. npm run check 0 errors; full suite 2315 passed/1 skipped. See comment for full evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Shipped. npm run check: 0 errors. Full suite: 2315 passed, 1 skipped (VAULT_E2E-gated regtest test, expected without live infra) -- targeted umbrelProbe.test.ts + chainEnvSeed.test.ts pass was 32/32 pre-ship. Reconstructed docs/UMBREL-AUTOCONNECT-DESIGN.md (original scratchpad design doc lost to a reboot) and repointed all code comments (umbrelProbe.ts, umbrelProbe.test.ts, hooks.server.ts) at it. Added MANUAL.md coverage: init() step 4 (Part I), CAIRN_PLATFORM env var + auto-connect section (§12), Umbrel test-target note (§16.3), and QA scenario 20.14 (Part II).

Commits on single-sig-full-wallet (pushed to origin):
- b16eea1 docs: add internal dev manual + QA runbook (MANUAL.md)
- 1e311c3 feat(umbrel): zero-config Electrum auto-detect via Docker-network probe (Wave A)

Also commented on cairn-2ldr noting Wave A's local-first probe confirms option (c) from that bead's decision list is viable without a manifest dependency; that bead remains open (product-positioning decision).

title: Docker build fails: native usb module needs node-gyp/Python, unused at runtime
description:
Reproduced: `docker build .` fails during `npm ci` inside the build stage with:

  npm error gyp ERR! find Python - "" could not be run
  npm error gyp ERR! stack Error: Could not find any Python installation to use
  ... cwd /app/node_modules/usb

Root cause: `usb@2.18.0` is a transitive HARD dependency (not optional) of @trezor/transport <- @trezor/connect <- @trezor/connect-web@9.7.3 (confirmed via `npm ls usb`). It is a native Node addon requiring node-gyp + Python + a C++ toolchain to build. The Docker base image has none of these.

The kicker: Cairn never actually needs this native binding. @trezor/connect-web only runs client-side in the browser via the Trezor Connect popup (see src/lib/hw/trezor.ts) -- there is no server-side Node USB access anywhere in the codebase. This native dependency is dead weight for how Cairn actually uses Trezor Connect, and it currently breaks the one deployment path (Docker) this project explicitly built and documented for self-hosters.

This is a hard blocker for anyone following the README Docker instructions -- the image cannot be built at all right now, not "builds but is degraded."

Options to fix, roughly in order of preference:
1. Check if a lighter Trezor Connect build target exists that does not pull in @trezor/transport native USB (e.g. an npm override/resolution pinning usb to a no-op stub, since the real transport used is always the browser popup, never the Node native path).
2. Add build tooling (python3, make, g++) to the Dockerfile build stage as a fallback if (1) is not viable -- heavier image, but functionally correct.
Verify whichever fix is chosen by re-running `docker build .` to completion, not just by reasoning about it -- this exact failure was only caught by actually attempting the build.
close_reason: Fixed + verified. Added python3/make/g++/linux-headers/eudev-dev to the Dockerfile build stage so npm ci can compile the usb native addon (hard transitive dep of @trezor/connect-web). Verified by actually running docker build to completion AND starting the container: it boots and serves /api/health 200. Chose the build-tools approach over stubbing usb because it's reliable and the multi-stage build keeps the runtime image toolchain-free (usb is never loaded server-side; Trezor Connect is browser-only).

title: Stale/unreachable SOCKS5 proxy config (192.168.50.134:9050) breaks Esplora lookups and likely explains the widespread portfolio/wallet-scan slowness
description:
Reproduced on Heartwood v0.2.0 at umbrel.local:3211 (browser QA), discovered while testing explorer error states.

Admin > Settings has a "Privacy: SOCKS5 / Tor proxy" section (Proxy host / Port) already filled in with 192.168.50.134:9050 -- I did not set this, it was pre-existing on the instance when I started this QA pass. This proxy is unreachable/broken: visiting /explorer/address/<any address> eventually surfaces "Can't reach chain data sources -- Esplora GET /address/<addr> failed: Proxy connection timed out" and "Couldn't load transactions -- Esplora GET /address/<addr>/txs failed: Proxy connection timed out" (after several seconds of skeleton loading with no error shown in between).

Per the settings copy: "Route all chain traffic (Electrum + explorer) through a SOCKS5 proxy... Applies whether you use the public servers or your own node." -- meaning if this proxy is broken, it degrades ALL chain data on the instance, not just the explorer.

This is very likely the root cause (or a major contributing cause) of the widespread slowness already filed as cairn-3gvb (dashboard /api/portfolio taking ~20s to resolve) and cairn-2ic5 (Rotate button hanging 30-40s+): every Electrum/Esplora round-trip on this instance may be eating a full proxy-connect timeout before falling back or erroring, rather than those pages being simply "slow to scan." The portfolio/wallet-scan code has legitimate optimization opportunities per cairn-3gvb/cairn-2ic5, but the proxy misconfiguration is likely inflating every number observed during this QA pass.

This isn't necessarily an app bug (a broken proxy is an operator configuration issue), but it's flagged P0 because: (1) it's silently degrading the entire instance with zero visible warning outside the explorer address page's specific error banner -- the dashboard and wallet pages just show generic skeleton-then-eventually-loads with no indication a proxy is the reason, and (2) it made every other perf finding in this QA pass hard to diagnose correctly.

Suggested actions:
1. Alex: check whether 192.168.50.134:9050 is intentional (was this set deliberately for privacy testing, e.g. via cairn-wgr8/Tor work) or leftover from an earlier session and should be cleared.
2. Product: surface a clear, instance-wide warning (not just buried in the explorer address page) when the configured proxy is unreachable, so this doesn't read as generic "everything is slow."
3. Re-run the cairn-3gvb / cairn-2ic5 timing repros with the proxy cleared/fixed to see how much of that slowness was proxy-timeout vs genuine gap-limit-scan cost.
close_reason: Fixed in 62ba92b. Added src/lib/server/chainHealth.ts (cheap in-memory chain-transport health signal, 2-consecutive-failure threshold, derived from Electrum connection outcomes in electrum/client.ts's ensureConnected -- no fresh probe), a read-only /api/chain-health endpoint, an instance-wide ChainHealthBanner.svelte mounted in the (app) layout next to SyncBanner (warns balances may be stale, links admins to connection settings), and a live-ish proxy status line in admin > Settings under the SOCKS5/Tor config. A broken proxy is now visible instance-wide instead of only surfacing as an unexplained skeleton on the dashboard. npm run check 0/0, full suite green. Alex still needs to independently verify whether 192.168.50.134:9050 is intentional or leftover.

title: [pool-ux] Best-share context line + personal-best celebration moment (plain-language closeness, memoryless gloss)
description: From docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-NOTES.md proposal #1 (S effort, high impact). Show best share as plain-language closeness to the block target (e.g. 'your best share reached 0.4% of the way to a block') plus a celebrated personal-best moment when it's beaten. MUST carry the memoryless gloss (past shares never improve future odds — near-miss display without implied progress; Clark et al. 2009 ethical line, see notes §2/§8). Builds on cairn-20k25 (best-share display, v0.2.42) and the raw-hashValue precision fix required by cairn-192dr. Bitaxe owners screenshot best-difficulty — this is the stickiest number in home mining.

title: Invite creation silently clamps out-of-range count/maxUses/expiresDays with no feedback to the admin
description: src/lib/server/admin.ts createInvites() clamps count to [1,50] and maxUses to [1,1000] via Math.min(Math.max(...)) with no error/warning returned. The form at src/routes/(app)/admin/invites/+page.svelte has no client-side max/min enforcement feedback either (the number inputs have min/max attributes but a user can still type an out-of-range value and submit). If an admin requests count=200 expecting 200 invite codes, they silently get 50 with no indication anything was capped, and the returned form.created array (len 50) doesn't explain why it's short of the requested count. Fix: either reject out-of-range values with a form error (consistent with how registrationMode/connectionMode/electrumPort are validated elsewhere in this same admin area) or return a note in the action result explaining the count was clamped.
close_reason: Duplicate of cairn-sh5h (same invite-clamp issue, same description). Fixed under cairn-sh5h in commit 22ab66d.

title: [wallet-pool] Mining payouts render as generic 'Received' in every wallet feed — no 'Mining reward' identity
description: wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:1079 (delta>=0 ? 'Received' : 'Sent'), portfolio/RecentActivity.svelte:48, activity/+page.svelte:217 (no mining marker case) — zero coinbase detection in any tx-row renderer; only the explorer tx page knows. Fix: pass the scan's coinbase flag through tx rows and render 'Mining reward' + flame glyph.

title: Config-backup UX: Envoy-style 'auto, encrypted, we can't read it' framing
description: From docs/COMPETITOR-ANALYSIS-R2-2026-07-19.md sec3 (Envoy/Foundation Magic Backups) + sec7 rec #3. Turn Cairn's amber 'Back up now' Health nudge (spec sec2.6a) and wallet-detail 'Download backup file' (spec sec2.2) into a calmer 'already-handled' state. Cairn backs up wallet descriptors/labels/settings - NEVER seeds/keys (key-on-device self-custody) - which is exactly Envoy's non-sensitive encrypted class. Adopt the concrete honesty framing: 'this backup contains your wallet's public setup and labels, encrypted so only you can read it - never your keys'. Add auto-advancing screens for purely-mechanical wizard steps (config write) so users don't click through work they didn't ask to supervise. GUARD: never introduce a silent cloud/third-party dependency (Envoy's iCloud/Android reliance) - any such dependency needs plain disclosure + a fully-local alternative on the same screen. Doctrine: manifesto sec5 (active-affordance privacy).

title: Explorer landing block list + tip are Electrum-only — empty on Core-RPC-only deployments
description:
After the Esplora removal (cairn-zoz8.16, d632f64) and the per-source blockContext fix (cairn-zc4x, ed9d91c), the /explorer landing page's recent-block list and tip still source exclusively from Electrum:
- getTip() via headersSubscribe (Electrum-only).
- getRecentBlocks() is Electrum-first for base block headers; Core only enriches via getblockstats.

With Core up / Electrum down, the landing page shows "Verified by your Bitcoin Core node — not connected right now" alongside an EMPTY block list, while the block/tx/mempool/blockContext pages already work Core-only (per cairn-zc4x fix and cairn-zm7o's mempool-summary fix). This is the same class of gap as cairn-zm7o but for the landing tip + recent-block list specifically (zm7o intentionally left the Core-only recentBlocks fallback OUT of its fix, per its close note).

CANDIDATE FIX: add Core fallbacks mirroring the neighborHeader fallback pattern added in ed9d91c (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts):
- Tip: fall back to Core getblockcount/getbestblockhash when Electrum's headersSubscribe is unavailable.
- Recent blocks: fall back to a getblockhash + getblockheader loop (walking back from tip) when Electrum can't supply base headers.

Priority P3 — Core-only deployments are an edge case today since Electrum is always configured by default; this is a completeness/honesty gap, not a live outage risk.

Related: cairn-zc4x (tx block-context Core fallback, same fix pattern), cairn-zm7o (mempool/home snapshot partial-success fix; explicitly left recentBlocks Core fallback out of scope).
close_reason: Fixed per the bead's own candidate fix. getTip() (chain/index.ts) now falls back to Core getblockcount()+getblockhash() when electrum.headersSubscribe() fails and Core RPC is configured -- propagates the Electrum error unchanged when no Core RPC is configured (no fallback available). getRecentBlocks() now degrades EACH height's baseline (hash, time) independently via the existing neighborHeader() Electrum-preferred/Core-fallback helper (the same one tx-block-context/cairn-zc4x already uses and reorg-window-caches) instead of a single Promise.all of raw Electrum getBlockHeader calls that rejected the WHOLE batch on any one failure -- this was the actual root cause of the 'empty block list' the bead described, worse than a per-row degrade since the prior code had no per-height fallback at all. A height neither backend can answer is omitted from the list (never a fabricated row) rather than failing the whole page. Also fixed a pre-existing chain.test.ts getTxBlockContext test that assumed getTip() always throws when Electrum is down (now a legitimate Core-fallback success per this fix) by making its Core stub's getBlockCount self-consistent with the test's crafted tip. Added 4 new tests (getTip: Core fallback success + no-fallback passthrough; getRecentBlocks: Core fallback per-height + omit-not-fabricate when neither backend answers one height). Full chain.test.ts 90/90 pass; explorer block/tx/mempool/nodeTrust/ownership/electrum/pool/chainHealth/umbrelProbe suites 219/219 pass together. Updated MANUAL.md (ChainService method docs + QA appendix companion note to the existing cairn-kcxy entry).

title: Filename-sanitizing slug() duplicated 5x across export routes with zero test coverage
description: The Content-Disposition filename sanitizer (lowercase, replace [^a-z0-9]+ with '-', trim dashes, slice(0,48), || 'wallet' fallback) is copy-pasted verbatim into at least 5 separate route files (src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/config/+server.ts, .../[id]/descriptor/+server.ts, and the three multisig export routes) instead of living once in src/lib/server/walletExport.ts (the module that was specifically introduced in 588aa0a to hold shared wallet-export logic). It is not currently a live vulnerability — all copies correctly neutralize quotes/CRLF/path separators/unicode for header-injection purposes and currently agree with each other — but there is zero test coverage of the sanitizer anywhere in the repo (walletExport.test.ts only covers the descriptor-building functions), so a future edit to loosen the regex or drop the empty-name fallback in just one copy could silently diverge or reintroduce a Content-Disposition header-injection path with nothing to catch it. Fix: move slug() into walletExport.ts as a single exported function, update all 5 call sites to import it, and add unit tests covering header-injection payloads (quotes, CRLF, path traversal, unicode, empty/whitespace-only names).
close_reason: Extracted the duplicated filename sanitizer into a single exported filenameSlug(name, fallback='wallet') in walletExport.ts. Updated all 6 call sites (single-sig config + descriptor, multisig caravan/coldcard/descriptor/backup-pdf) to import it; removed every local copy. Added header-injection unit tests in walletExport.test.ts (quotes, CRLF, path traversal, unicode, empty/whitespace names) — all 11 pass.

title: Explorer search bar rejects valid testnet/regtest addresses that the address page itself accepts
description: src/lib/server/search.ts:67 gates address recognition in the search bar through isValidAddress() (src/lib/server/bitcoin/xpub.ts:335), which is explicitly mainnet-only by design (see comment at xpub.ts:2). But the actual address page route (src/routes/(app)/explorer/address/[address]/) uses a separate, more permissive isExplorerAddress() (xpub.ts:264) that explicitly accepts bc/tb/bcrt HRPs and testnet/regtest base58 version bytes, specifically so testnet/regtest lookups work when Cairn is pointed at a non-mainnet backend. Net effect: on a testnet/regtest-configured instance, pasting a valid tb1.../bcrt1.../testnet-legacy address into the search bar falls through every branch and returns 'unknown' (no results), even though navigating directly to /explorer/address/<addr> works fine and shows the correct data. Fix: have classifySearch() in search.ts use isExplorerAddress() instead of (or in addition to) isValidAddress() for the address-recognition branch, matching what the address page itself accepts.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: classifySearch() now uses isExplorerAddress() (accepts bc/tb/bcrt + testnet base58) instead of the mainnet-only isValidAddress(), matching what the address page accepts (search.ts).

title: friendlyCoreRpcTestError leaks raw ABORT_ERR on node timeout
description:
Admin > Settings > Bitcoin Core RPC > Test connection against an unreachable/non-routable node. When the 8s AbortController fires, the failure badge shows the raw abort error text, which a user cannot interpret as a timeout.

EXACT COPY OBSERVED (input url='http://10.255.255.1:8332', user='u', pass='p' — non-routable IP, TCP SYN blackholed):
"Couldn't connect to Bitcoin Core: Core RPC getblockchaininfo request failed: This operation was aborted (20). Check the RPC URL, username/password, and that Core is running and reachable from this server."

'This operation was aborted (20)' is the Node ABORT_ERR (errno 20) — leaked verbatim. UI recovers correctly (no infinite spinner; wall-clock ~11.5s in dev incl. SvelteKit action/SSR overhead, underlying abort at the 8s budget), so behavior is fine — only the copy is wrong.

FIX: in friendlyCoreRpcTestError() (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts ~line 1540), detect abort/timeout ('aborted', AbortError, errno 20) and map to something like: 'No response from the node after 8 seconds — check the address and that the node is reachable.'

Repro: url=http://10.255.255.1:8332 (or any non-routable IP), any creds, Test connection; wait ~8-11s.

Found during Core-RPC/post-Esplora live verification (single-sig-full-wallet @ d632f64).
close_reason: friendlyCoreRpcTestError (chain/index.ts) maps abort/timeout ('aborted'/'timed out'/errno 20) to 'No response from the node after 8 seconds — check the address and that the node is reachable from this server.' Test: chain.test.ts testCoreRpc 'maps an abort/timeout' (1). Full suite green.

title: No security response headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options)
description: hooks.server.ts:143-181 — handle hook sets no Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, HSTS, or Referrer-Policy headers. A Cairn instance can be framed by a malicious site. Clickjacking a Sign/Send button could authorize a real Bitcoin transaction. Fix: add X-Frame-Options:DENY, X-Content-Type-Options:nosniff, Referrer-Policy:strict-origin-when-cross-origin, frame-ancestors 'none', and HSTS on HTTPS.
close_reason: Fixed via commit 25c9a8f (worktree agent-a7e8633eb8364b6f2). Added CSP (default-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'; ...), X-Frame-Options:DENY, X-Content-Type-Options:nosniff, Referrer-Policy:strict-origin-when-cross-origin to every response in hooks.server.ts; HSTS gated on cookieSecure() (HTTPS only, matches existing session-cookie HTTPS detection). Verified WebUSB/WebHID hw-signer flows unaffected (no fetch/WS usage in src/lib/hw). vitest 1880/1 skipped, svelte-check 0/0, build clean, manual curl smoke test confirmed headers present.

title: Literal template placeholder '%sveltekit.head%' leaks as visible text on every page
description:
Confirmed via raw HTML fetch + DOM inspection on branch design/evergreen-identity @ d0dce27.

src/app.html line 27 has the SvelteKit head-injection placeholder %sveltekit.head% inside <head>. The raw server response for /login and /signup (verified via fetch()) contains this string completely unsubstituted, still literally present right before </head> (exactly 1 occurrence each; the <title> tag elsewhere in head IS correctly populated, so substitution partially works but this trailing token is not consumed).

Per the HTML5 parsing algorithm, a stray non-whitespace text token encountered in "in head" insertion mode implicitly closes </head> and reprocesses the token in body — so this literal string becomes the FIRST visible text node in <body>, before all real page content. Confirmed via get_page_text on /login, /signup, and Home (authenticated): output begins with "%sveltekit.head%" followed by the real page text on every page checked.

This affects every page in the app (shared app.html), not just auth pages — possibly tied to the CSP mode:'auto' nonce-injection change described in vite.config.ts's csp comment block (nonce/hash injection into the per-response head may be leaving the marker token behind in dev SSR). Needs verification against a production build (npm run build && npm start) to confirm whether this is dev-server-only or ships to prod.

Filed during cairn-sdx5.8 QA sweep (non-pixel pass).
close_reason: Root cause confirmed: src/app.html's new inline theme-preload script (added in d0dce27) contains a code comment that spells out the literal placeholder text '%sveltekit.head%' as prose ('...before %sveltekit.head% inserts the stylesheet link.'), duplicating the real placeholder that follows later in <head>. SvelteKit's dev/build template compiler (node_modules/@sveltejs/kit/src/core/sync/write_server.js line 55) performs template.replace('%sveltekit.head%', head) — plain String.replace, which only replaces the FIRST match in the whole file. Since the comment occurs earlier in file order than the real placeholder, the actual <head> assets get substituted into the comment instead (breaking/truncating the script tag, since the injected head markup contains its own literal </script>), leaving the real placeholder as literal un-replaced text — which the browser's HTML5 parser, hitting non-whitespace text while still in 'in head' insertion mode, implicitly closes </head> and relocates into <body> as the first visible text node. This is the exact mechanism observed (stray text leading every page). Fix: src/app.html comment reworded to no longer contain a second literal copy of the token, restoring single, correct substitution. Verified live on http://localhost:5173/login and /: document.title correctly 'Sign in — Heartwood', document.body.innerText no longer contains '%sveltekit' anywhere, <head> closes cleanly before <body>. npm test 3668 passed (1 pre-existing unrelated flaky timing test, reproduced independently as passing in isolation), npm run check 0 errors/0 warnings. Duplicate of cairn-sdx5.10 (closed as dupe).

title: Hide CPFP speed-up controls when parent fee is unknown
description: On an unconfirmed tx's Speed up expander, when the parent transaction's fee can't be resolved (Core returns fee:null because a prevout couldn't be decorated), executeCpfpDraft's CpfpError('parent_fee_unknown') always fires on submit. Today the UI still shows the Speed up button + target-rate input + the resulting 'fee is unknown' error stacked together, i.e. a visibly broken control with an apology attached. Fix: detect this case ahead of time (detectUnconfirmedInflows already fetches the parent tx and can see fee===null) and hide the CPFP speed-up affordance entirely for that tx (no button, no input, no warning). RBF speed-up must remain available if independently eligible for the same tx. Surfaces: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte, src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte, src/lib/server/transactions.ts (detectUnconfirmedInflows/UnconfirmedInflow), src/routes/(app)/explorer/ownership.server.ts (Speed this up CTA).
close_reason: Fixed. Root cause: executeCpfpDraft (feeBump.ts) needs the stuck parent's own network fee to price the CPFP child; when Core can't resolve it (fee: null from toTxDetailFromCore), CpfpError 'parent_fee_unknown' fires deterministically on every submit -- but the wallet-detail Speed up button + target-rate input + form still rendered, so the UI offered a control guaranteed to fail, with its own apology stacked underneath. Fix: detectUnconfirmedInflows (src/lib/server/transactions.ts) now surfaces parentFeeUnknown on UnconfirmedInflow (computed from the SAME getTx() call it already made, no extra chain round-trip; a failed lookup stays false since that's transient, not permanent). New shared predicate canOfferSpeedUp (src/lib/shared/speedUp.ts, unit-tested in speedUp.test.ts) gates: the inline Speed up button/form on both src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte and .../multisig/[id]/+page.svelte (now render NOTHING for a blocked tx -- no button, no input, no warning), the openSpeedUp() deep-link handler on both pages (silent no-op instead of opening a form that fails immediately), and the explorer tx-detail 'Speed this up' CTA index (ownership.server.ts, both the single-sig and multisig loops) so it doesn't link to a wallet page with nothing there to find. RBF stays independently available (it never reads the parent fee) -- verified live: a same-page RBF-eligible sent tx kept its Speed up button throughout. Tests: src/lib/shared/speedUp.test.ts (3 cases), src/lib/server/cpfp.test.ts (+3 detectUnconfirmedInflows cases: fee null -> flagged, fee resolved -> not flagged, lookup failure -> not flagged/transient), src/routes/(app)/explorer/ownership.speedUp.test.ts (+1 case, CPFP-fee-unknown excluded from the CTA index). 36/36 targeted + 240/240 broader related suite green; svelte-check 0 errors. Live QA (worktree cairn-wd2, wd2.localhost:5977, synthetic wallet_snapshots seed with parentFeeUnknown:true on an unconfirmed received tx): DOM confirms zero Speed-up affordance on that row (no button between the txid link and the amount), while a sibling parentFeeUnknown:false CPFP row keeps its button and a sibling RBF-eligible sent row keeps its button independently.

title: Umbrel: CAIRN_ORIGIN pins passkey expectedOrigin to http origin — WebAuthn on the HTTPS :4488 listener cannot verify (origin mismatch)
description:
Found during the cairn-wrph/cairn-9njl session-cookie review (2026-07-13).

On the Umbrel package, packaging/umbrel/heartwood/docker-compose.yml:33-42 sets:
  CAIRN_ORIGIN: http://${DEVICE_DOMAIN_NAME}:3211

This is deliberate and correct for the plain-HTTP app_proxy port (3211) — it keeps
the session cookie non-Secure there (cookieSecure() in auth.ts) and drives WebAuthn's
expectedOrigin for that listener.

But the SAME compose file also publishes a second, TLS-terminated listener for
hardware-wallet signing: host 4488 -> container 3443 (docker-compose.yml:16-18,
server.mjs's self-signed HTTPS listener, cairn-wgr8). src/lib/server/webauthn.ts's
getRp() (lines 47-51) is:

  const origin = env.CAIRN_ORIGIN ?? event.url.origin;
  const rpID = env.CAIRN_RP_ID ?? event.url.hostname;

Because CAIRN_ORIGIN is set (unconditionally, to the http://...:3211 value),
getRp()'s `origin` is PINNED to that http origin for every request regardless of
which listener served it. verifyRegistration/verifyAuthentication (webauthn.ts) pass
that pinned origin as `expectedOrigin` to @simplewebauthn's verify*Response() calls.
A WebAuthn ceremony actually performed in the browser against the HTTPS :4488
listener sends an attestation/assertion whose real origin is
https://<device>:4488 (or whatever the browser resolved) — which will NEVER match
the pinned http://...:3211 expectedOrigin, so @simplewebauthn's origin check fails
and passkey registration/authentication cannot complete on the HTTPS listener.

This is the exact listener Umbrel needs a secure context on for WebHID/WebUSB
hardware-wallet signing — passkeys are a plausible thing a user would also want to
set up/use from that same secure-context tab.

rpID itself is less broken (event.url.hostname is the same device hostname on both
ports, and CAIRN_RP_ID is unset in this compose file so it falls through to
event.url.hostname) — this bug is specifically about `origin`, not `rpID`.

Pre-existing bug, not introduced by the wrph/9njl fix (that fix only touches
server.mjs's protocol-header handling, not CAIRN_ORIGIN or webauthn.ts). Filing as a
separate P2 rather than folding into wrph/9njl's scope.

Suggested fix direction (not yet designed/reviewed): getRp() needs to derive origin
per-listener the same way cookieSecure() now correctly derives protocol, rather than
letting a single CAIRN_ORIGIN pin it across both ports — e.g. only use CAIRN_ORIGIN
when it matches the request's own scheme, or introduce a listener-aware origin
allowlist. Needs its own design pass before implementation.

Files: src/lib/server/webauthn.ts:47-51 (getRp), src/lib/server/webauthn.ts:90,126
(verifyRegistration/verifyAuthentication use of expectedOrigin),
packaging/umbrel/heartwood/docker-compose.yml:33-42.
close_reason: Fixed in c0e41c4 (integrated): WebAuthn expectedOrigin is now an allowlist (passkeyOrigin.ts allowedPasskeyOrigins) covering CAIRN_ORIGIN + the https://<host>:CAIRN_HTTPS_EXTERNAL_PORT listener variant; rpID unchanged (already consistent). UI hints + MANUAL.md corrected. Full suite green.

title: Send amount entry: BTC/fiat toggle icon reads as refresh
description: Alex (phone testing): on the send screen, the button that swaps the amount display between BTC and fiat looks like a REFRESH/RELOAD button (circular arrow). Users read that icon as 'refresh', not 'switch currency'. Needs a clear swap affordance (horizontal two-arrow swap glyph, not circular) plus visible alternate-currency unit/value so the button is self-explanatory. Component: src/lib/components/send/AmountEntry.svelte, used by both single-sig (wallets/[id]/send) and multisig (wallets/multisig/[id]/send) flows.
close_reason: Implemented: Icon.svelte adds a non-circular 'swap-horizontal' glyph (two opposing horizontal arrows). AmountEntry.svelte replaces the refresh-icon toggle with a 3-way BTC/sats/USD cycle button (swap glyph + next-unit label, aria-label/title='Change amount unit'); canonical sats value never recomputed on cycle (render-only), so no drift. npm run check: 0 errors/0 warnings. Visual verification BLOCKED: spent well over the ~10min time-box across 4 running dev-server tabs (main 'cairn' server requires an invite code to register; documented QA creds for ports 5311/5322/55230 all returned bad_credentials, likely rotated since memory was written; port 55230 session also hit the known hard admin-recovery wall and dropped on hard-nav; port 5347 login succeeded but is stuck behind the setup-admin first-run wall, matching the documented 'hard permission wall, Alex must unblock' hazard; port 5366 not running). Code change verified by reading the rendered logic and diff only, not a live screenshot.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Live-browser cycle verification (BTC to sats to USD to BTC at 375x812, equivalents update, no drift) deferred to cairn-5fpb, which is blocked on cairn-wrph. Code shipped in commit db4fc29.

title: Multisig owner-only mutation gates never tested cross-actor
description: multisigTransactions.ts — broadcastMultisigTransaction, bumpMultisigTransaction, deleteMultisigTransaction are owner-only gates but every test runs as owner. Nothing asserts a cosigner role is refused. attachMultisigSignature roster gate (L355) also untested for off-roster denial. A privilege-escalation regression would be invisible. Fix: add cross-actor tests (cosigner denied broadcast/bump/delete, off-roster denied attach).
close_reason: Merged fc9453c: cross-actor tests confirm cosigner refused broadcast/bump/delete and off-roster signer refused attach. Gate logic was already correct — no bug found, test-gap only.

title: ntfy notification channel has ZERO SSRF protection — any authenticated user can probe internal network/cloud metadata
description: src/lib/server/channels/ntfy.ts: resolveConfig() (~57-79) applies no URL validation whatsoever to the user-supplied 'server' field, and publish() (~93-127) does fetch(normalizeServer(cfg.server), {...}) directly with no scheme check, no DNS resolution, no private/loopback/link-local/cloud-metadata IP blocklist — unlike the sibling webhook.ts channel shipped in the SAME commit (a0b8002), which does implement an SSRF guard (checkTargetUrl/isBlockedAddress). Any authenticated non-admin user can set their ntfy server to http://169.254.169.254 (cloud metadata), http://127.0.0.1:<port>, or an internal LAN address via PUT /api/notifications/channels/ntfy (requireUser only, no admin gate), then hit POST /api/notifications/channels/ntfy/test — publish()'s fetch runs server-side, and on any non-2xx response up to 500 chars of the response body is captured and returned verbatim as ChannelSendResult.error in the JSON response to the calling user. This is a full SSRF response-reflection oracle against the Cairn server's network position, reachable by any user account (registration can be 'open' mode per RegistrationMode in src/lib/types.ts). Fix: apply the same SSRF guard webhook.ts has (or share one implementation) to ntfy.ts's server URL before every publish()/test() call.
close_reason: Fixed in fc8da90

title: [wallet-pool] Literal 'Coinbase' badge reaches users on explorer tx detail
description: explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.svelte:535-542 renders a 'Coinbase' badge; doctrine (UX-REDESIGN-SPEC jargon rule) says the term never surfaces. Fix: label 'Mining reward' with a Term gloss carrying the real term.

title: Explorer search bar: no 'not found' state for valid-format nonexistent txid
description:
Regression-adjacent to cairn-37gfa (v0.2.38 search-by-txid fix). The core fix works: a real, existing txid typed/pasted into the Explorer global search bar (/explorer) correctly triggers /api/search?q=<txid>, gets {type:"tx",redirect:"/explorer/tx/<txid>"}, and clicking the resulting suggestion link navigates to /explorer/tx/<txid> with full details rendered. Confirmed on QA server C:/dev/cairn-qa2 port 5192, real regtest txid 9139ae8600ec1a91c16030973b0d54133312bea043c5333d2e3f16be742301e3.

Bug: for a syntactically valid but NONEXISTENT 64-hex-char txid (e.g. 64x 'f'), the backend correctly and honestly responds 200 {"type":"unknown","redirect":null,"query":"<the 64 f's>"} (no crash, no 500) -- verified via direct fetch() in-page. But the search UI never surfaces this as a 'not found' state. The autocomplete hint area keeps showing the same generic 'keep typing — height, hash, txid, or address' copy that's shown for genuinely-incomplete input (e.g. 4-20 char fragments), even though the user has typed a full 64-char value and the server has already told the frontend it found nothing. Pressing Enter with the full-length nonexistent value in the field does nothing -- no navigation, no error toast, no not-found page; the user is stuck with a hint that (falsely) implies they haven't finished typing.

Expected: once input reaches a plausible complete length (64 hex chars) and the /api/search lookup comes back type:unknown, the UI should show an honest 'not found' / 'no match for this txid' state distinct from the 'keep typing' (still-incomplete-input) hint, so users get a definitive answer rather than an apparent dead end.

Repro:
1. QA server port 5192, authenticated session, navigate to /explorer.
2. Focus the 'Search the blockchain' box (search[role] > input[type=search]).
3. Type/paste 64x 'f' (ffffff...ffff, 64 chars) as the query.
4. Observe: hint stays 'keep typing — height, hash, txid, or address' indefinitely; network tab shows GET /api/search?q=ffff...ff -> 200 {type:unknown,redirect:null}; pressing Enter does not navigate anywhere.
5. Contrast with garbage input ('zzzz') or a short hex fragment (16 chars) -- same 'keep typing' hint appears, which IS correct/expected there since those genuinely aren't complete 64-char candidates.

Files likely involved: the Explorer search bar component (src/routes/(app)/explorer/+page.svelte or a shared SearchBar/GlobalSearch component) and its /api/search response handling -- needs a branch for 'complete-length, server said unknown' distinct from 'still typing.'

Severity: P2 -- not a crash/blank/500, backend is honest, but the search entry point (the actual thing cairn-37gfa fixed) has a dead-end UX for the not-found case with no user-facing signal.
close_reason: Backend classifySearch() already returns an honest {type:'unknown',redirect:null} for a genuine not-found (search.ts:83-111); the bug was frontend-only: the live-suggestion dropdown in ExplorerSearch.svelte and the Explorer index's inline search form always showed the 'keep typing' hint for any unknown result, even a complete 64-hex txid / height the backend had definitively resolved as not found. Added src/lib/searchShape.ts (isCompleteSearchCandidate, sharing HEIGHT_RE/HEX64_RE with search.ts so routing and UI never disagree) and wired a distinct 'We couldn't find that...' state into both search surfaces, keeping the optimistic capability-error routing from cairn-37gfa intact. Verified: npm run check clean, npm run build clean, new src/lib/searchShape.test.ts (9 cases incl. the bead's 64x-f repro) + existing search.test.ts all pass.

title: Signature-progress status updates lack aria-live (wallets and vaults send flows)
description: Both send flows use role="status" divs for live signing feedback but never pair it with aria-live="polite": src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1167 and src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1056,1161,1189 (.sign-flash, .attach-status, .quorum-done). role="status" alone has an implicit aria-live="polite" per the ARIA spec in most browsers/AT combinations, but this is inconsistently honored -- explicit aria-live="polite" alongside role="status" is the robust, spec-recommended pairing. On the vault flow specifically this matters more than single-sig: a screen reader user collecting signatures from multiple cosigners over time ("2 of 3 signatures collected") needs to actually hear that update, not just see it. Pattern-wide fix, not vault-specific -- apply to both flows in one pass.
close_reason: Both halves committed: wallets flow (1c506ff) and multisig flow (this commit) — aria-live on attach status, quorum counts, signer confirmations, flashes.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: WALLETS half done: explicit aria-live=polite added alongside role=status on single-sig send flow status surfaces — .attach-status, .device-saved-note, .saved-note, mass-panel (aria-live matches its status/alert role), TrezorSigner/LedgerSigner .signed-ok, CoinControl .mass-loading. QrSigner already had the correct idiom. Vaults/multisig half untouched (other agent's scope).

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Wallets half committed (attach-status, signer confirmations, mass panel, coin-control loading all carry aria-live). Multisig half pending with the running stepper agent.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Multisig-flow half done: explicit aria-live=polite added alongside role=status on .sign-flash, .attach-status, .quorum-done (confirm step + new quorum-met notice on the sign step), the quorum-head count line (now a polite status region so 'N of M signatures collected' changes are announced), and the Trezor/Ledger signed-ok / save-warning confirmations. Wallets-flow half untouched (other agent's scope).

title: hooks.server.ts runs auth+flags queries before static asset skip check
description: hooks.server.ts:144,148,151 — getSessionUser() (1 SQLite query) and resolveAllFlags() (2 queries + 20+ key object) run unconditionally on every request including JS/CSS/fonts. isAsset(pathname) fast-path only fires at line 151, AFTER both queries. A SvelteKit page pulls dozens of sub-requests per navigation, each paying 3 wasted sync SQLite calls. Fix: move isAsset check to top of handle(), before touching locals.
close_reason: Landed in 3dd2b4a (with v84z). isAsset() check moved to the top of handle() so static-asset requests no longer pay getSessionUser()+resolveAllFlags() (3 SQLite reads each) before the fast-path. Independently reviewed clean. Full suite 1859 pass; svelte-check 0/0.

title: Default unstyled SvelteKit error page shown on 404 (no Cairn branding)
description:
Any unmatched route (e.g. /wallets right now, or any typo'd URL) renders SvelteKit's bare default error page: a plain "404" heading and "Not Found" paragraph, no styling, no Cairn logo/sidebar, no link back into the app.

Expected: a branded error page consistent with The Forge design system (warm charcoal background, copper accent, Cairn logo, a "Back to Dashboard" or "Back to sign in" link depending on auth state) — matching the quality bar set by other pages (e.g. the empty/closed-registration state on /signup which IS well designed).

Repro: navigate to any non-existent route, e.g. http://localhost:<port>/wallets or /explorer/foo/bar.
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: branded +error.svelte on The Forge background with logo, status hero, and links back to the dashboard/explorer. Verified live on an unmatched route

title: Balance-over-time chart empty for imported wallets — no backfill from historical transactions
description: Importing a wallet with existing on-chain history shows an empty 'Balance over time' chart: balance_snapshots only records from the moment Cairn starts sampling, never backfilling from the wallet's historical transactions. Fix: derive historical balance points from the wallet's transaction history (running sum of tx deltas over confirmation time) on import/scan, or at minimum change the empty-state copy from 'Not enough history yet' (implies waiting helps) to 'History starts from when this wallet was added to Cairn'.
close_reason: Fixed: portfolio.ts backfills balance_snapshots from confirmed tx history on a wallet's first scan (buildBackfillPoints: running deltas, retention-aligned resolution, 13-month horizon carry-in; refuses when history can't reconstruct the confirmed balance), getBalanceSeries rewritten as carry-forward sum handling unaligned per-wallet timestamps + excluding deleted wallets' orphans, and BalanceChart empty-state copy no longer implies waiting helps. 11 new tests in portfolio.test.ts.

title: Admin/platform gaps vs. Gitea/Nextcloud, 2026-07-06 comparison audit
description: Comparison audit against Gitea and Nextcloud admin/platform UX (docs/ADMIN-COMPARISON-2026-07-06.md). Parent epic for the 6 gaps identified as most likely to make an experienced self-hoster say 'this feels unfinished' next to a mature multi-user app. See the doc for what's already ahead (backup/restore, per-user SMTP, notification breadth) and what's already tracked elsewhere (forced first-login reset, plaintext secrets, activity scoping).
close_reason: All 6 units (cairn-ivae.1 through .6) built and merged: API tokens (Bearer auth), update-availability notice, scheduled instance backups, PWA manifest, unified toast/banner component, operator name in app chrome. See docs/ADMIN-COMPARISON-2026-07-06.md for the original audit.

title: API tokens for scripting against a user's own instance
description:
Cairn's only auth mechanism is the browser session cookie (src/lib/server/auth.ts) -- there is no API token/key of any kind. Gitea has scoped Personal Access Tokens (per-resource read/write, since v1.23) plus OAuth2; Nextcloud has app passwords (unscoped) plus OAuth2. A Cairn user currently has no way to script against their own instance (pull balances into a spreadsheet, trigger a backup from cron, build a companion CLI) without replaying their session cookie.

Fix: add a personal-access-token system -- token hash stored server-side (same pattern as sessions), issued/revoked from /settings, Bearer-auth accepted alongside the existing cookie in requireUser()/requireAdmin() (src/lib/server/api.ts). Start unscoped (matches Nextcloud) rather than building Gitea-level per-resource scoping up front; scoping can follow later once real usage patterns show what needs restricting.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can create/name/revoke a token from /settings; token value is shown once, never stored in retrievable form (hash only, like sessions).
- Bearer <token> on any authenticated API route resolves to the same user/session context as the cookie.
- Revoked/expired tokens are rejected immediately.
- Rate limiting applies per-token the same way it applies per-session.
close_reason: Personal access tokens built. src/lib/server/apiTokens.ts: api_tokens table (hash-only via auth.ts hashToken, shown once at creation), create/list/revoke/resolve + per-IP invalid-Bearer throttle mirroring rateLimit.ts (20 fails/15min -> 429). requireUser/requireAdmin in api.ts accept 'Authorization: Bearer cairn_<secret>' alongside the cookie, populate locals.user+flags identically so downstream guards and per-user rate limits apply the same. Routes: GET/POST /api/tokens, DELETE /api/tokens/[id]; UI at /settings/tokens (name + optional 30/90/365d expiry, one-time reveal, revoke). Expired rows lazily deleted like sessions; disabled accounts rejected. Evidence: 16 unit+integration tests green (apiTokens.test.ts) incl. requireUser Bearer resolution, revoked->401, spray->429; live smoke on dev server: cookie create -> Bearer GET /api/tokens 200 (no cookie) -> Bearer POST /api/admin/backup 200 -> revoke -> immediate 401.

title: In-app update-availability notice in admin dashboard
description:
Cairn has zero update-awareness: no version check anywhere, no 'a new release is available' notice in /admin. Gitea ships an in-app update-checker cron job that at least surfaces a notice (even though the actual update is still an external image pull); Nextcloud goes further with a full in-app updater for non-container installs. Cairn's deployment target (Umbrel/Start9, container-based) means the update itself will always be an external image pull either way -- but the one-line 'you're on v0.1.4, latest is v0.1.6' notice costs little and both reference apps have it.

Fix: a background check (daily cron, similar shape to existing backupHealth.ts pattern) that compares the running package.json version against the latest GitHub release tag for the repo, surfaces a dismissible admin-dashboard notice when behind, and does nothing else (no auto-update -- Umbrel/Start9 own that lifecycle).

Acceptance criteria:
- Admin dashboard shows a notice when a newer release exists, with a link to the release notes.
- No notice shown when already on latest, or when the check fails (fail silent, log only -- don't nag on a network blip).
close_reason: In-app update notice built. src/lib/server/updateCheck.ts: compares package.json version against GitHub latest-release tag for AlexM223/cairn (repo confirmed in docs/PUBLISH-PLAN.md). Cached daily, refresh runs in background off the request path (never awaited, 5s timeout, fail-silent log-only) so the admin dashboard can never be blocked or slowed by GitHub. Admin overview (/admin) shows a dismissible banner with the latest version + release-notes link; dismissal is per-version in localStorage so a NEWER release re-surfaces it. No notice when up to date, never checked, or check failed. No auto-update (Umbrel/Start9 own that). Evidence: 7 tests green (updateCheck.test.ts) covering semver compare (incl. 0.1.10>0.1.9), notice-when-behind, silence-when-current, silence-on-403/network-error, instant null before first check.

title: Scheduled/automatic instance backups
description:
Cairn's instance backup (src/lib/server/backup.ts, POST /api/admin/backup) is manual-trigger only -- an admin has to remember to click it. Neither Gitea nor Nextcloud has scheduled backup either (both are equally manual), so this isn't 'behind' the references, but it's the natural next step given Cairn already has the one-click encrypted backup/restore flow neither reference app has -- automating the trigger completes what's otherwise the strongest category in the comparison.

Fix: an opt-in scheduled backup setting (interval + destination), reusing the existing backup.ts encryption/export path, writing to a configured local path (not a new cloud-storage integration -- out of scope). Surface last-scheduled-run status next to the existing 'last_instance_backup_at' display on /admin/backup.

Acceptance criteria:
- Admin can enable a daily/weekly scheduled backup with a destination path.
- Scheduled runs update last_instance_backup_at the same as a manual backup (so the existing backup-staleness notification logic doesn't double-report).
- Failure to write a scheduled backup surfaces as an admin notification, not a silent skip.
close_reason: Scheduled instance backups built. backup.ts gained getScheduledBackupConfig/saveScheduledBackupConfig (settings keys scheduled_backup_*; passphrase encrypted at rest via setSecretSetting in instance_secrets, excluded from exports by construction), runScheduledBackupIfDue, and startScheduledBackupWatcher (15-min tick, unref'd, armed from the (app) layout load since hooks.server.ts was out of this task's file scope). Daily/weekly cadence to an admin-configured absolute local path (created+write-tested at save time); write-then-rename so a crash can't leave a truncated file; newest 30 scheduled files kept (strict name match, foreign files untouched). Success updates the SAME last_instance_backup_at key as a manual download so backupHealth staleness never double-reports; failure sets scheduled_backup_last_error and fires an admin_server_health admin notification throttled to 1/day while retrying each tick. /admin/backup gained the settings UI + last-run/last-error status next to the existing staleness badge. Evidence: 12 tests green (scheduledBackup.test.ts): round-trip config, validation, decryptable output, due/not-due daily+weekly, notify-on-failure + throttle, missing-passphrase failure, prune retention.

title: PWA manifest for home-screen install
description:
Cairn has responsive CSS and dark mode but no PWA manifest -- can't be 'installed' to a phone home screen with its own icon/splash/status-bar theming. Neither Gitea nor Nextcloud has this either (Nextcloud relies on first-party native apps instead), so this would be a genuine differentiator rather than just catching up -- and for a Bitcoin wallet specifically, 'check my balance from my phone without a browser chrome' matters more than it does for a git host.

Fix: add a web app manifest (manifest.json) with icons/theme-color/display:standalone, and the corresponding <link> tags in app.html. No service worker / offline support required for v1 -- just installability.

Acceptance criteria:
- Manifest passes Lighthouse's installability check.
- Installed PWA opens to the app with correct icon and theme color, no browser chrome.
close_reason: Added static/manifest.json (name/short_name/description, start_url+scope /, display standalone, theme+background #1A1614, 192+512 PNG icons in any+maskable purposes) and static/icons/icon-{192,512}.png rendered programmatically from the existing favicon.svg cairn mark on the app background (content inside the maskable safe zone). app.html gained <link rel=manifest> and <link rel=apple-touch-icon>. Verified serving 200 with correct content on a live dev instance; meets Chrome installability criteria (secure context + valid manifest + >=144px icon; no SW required since Chrome 108). No service worker by design (v1 = installability only).

title: Unified toast/error-feedback component
description:
Cairn logs structurally (pino) and returns structured JSON errors from the API, but there's no centralized toast/banner UI component -- forms use hand-rolled '<div class="form-error">' / '<div class="saved-note">' elements scattered per-page (confirmed via grep across src/routes and src/lib/components during the 2026-07-06 admin-comparison audit). Nextcloud pairs a toast API (OCP.Toast) with consistent styling everywhere; Gitea has flash messages. This is the gap most likely to read as 'unfinished' to an experienced self-hoster, since inconsistent error/success UI is visible on every page with a form.

Fix: build one shared Toast/Banner Svelte component (success/error/info variants) in src/lib/components, and migrate the existing ad hoc form-error/saved-note divs to use it, starting with the highest-traffic forms (settings, send flow, admin settings).

Acceptance criteria:
- One component covers success/error/info feedback with consistent placement/styling/timing across the app.
- At least the settings and send-flow forms are migrated to it as the reference implementation for future forms.
close_reason: Built the shared feedback system in src/lib/components: Banner.svelte (one visual component, success/error/info/warning variants, optional dismiss + actions snippet for recovery buttons), toast.svelte.ts (imperative store: toast.success/error/info/warning(message, {duration}), consistent timing — success 4s, info 5s, warning 7s, error 8s, 0=sticky), Toasts.svelte (fixed bottom-right overlay host rendering the same Banner). Migrated reference forms: settings page (profile/password/passkeys/delete-account feedback now toasts via enhance result callbacks) and both send flows (all hand-rolled form-error divs -> Banner incl. action buttons; device-saved, address-saved, and multisig signature-added flashes -> toasts). Verified live in Chrome: success toast fires and auto-dismisses, error toast renders bottom-right with icon+dismiss. Admin pages deliberately untouched (owned by another agent).

title: Surface operator/instance name in app chrome, not just legal terms
description:
The admin-settable operator name (settings.ts, edited via /admin/settings) currently only appears on the legal-terms/disclosures page (disclosures.ts) -- it's invisible everywhere else in the app (header, page title, emails). Nextcloud's Theming app surfaces the equivalent instance name/branding throughout the UI. Cairn doesn't need Nextcloud-level full theming (logo/colors) since it already has its own fixed app identity (cairn-a77.6, cairn-it6, both closed) -- but the one field an operator does set should actually show up somewhere a user sees day-to-day.

Fix: surface the operator name in at least one visible day-to-day spot -- e.g. the document <title>, or a small 'operated by X' line in the settings/about area. Keep it minimal; this is not a request for a full branding/theming system.

Acceptance criteria:
- Setting the operator name in /admin/settings changes what's visible in the chosen surface (e.g. page title) without requiring a restart.
close_reason: Operator name surfaced in app chrome. (app)/+layout.server.ts threads operatorName (from getUserAgreement().operator, null while still DEFAULT_OPERATOR so the stock fallback never renders); (app)/+layout.svelte shows a muted 'operated by <name>' line in the sidebar footer above the Terms link, linked to /terms. Reads the settings row per request, so changing the name in /admin/settings is visible on the next navigation without a restart. instanceMode field left untouched. Evidence: npm run check 0 errors; full suite 1476 passed.

title: Ledger vault key import crashes with "Buffer is not defined"
description:
Reported by Alex testing with a real Ledger device during vault key import: connecting a Ledger throws "Buffer is not defined" and the flow fails outright. This blocks every Ledger user from adding a Ledger key to a vault at all -- confirmed P1.

Root cause confirmed in code: src/lib/hw/ledger.ts uses the Node.js global Buffer directly and unguarded -- `Buffer.from(sourceTx.toPSBT())` (line ~297), plus Buffer used as a type in several signatures (masterFp: Buffer, sigs: Map<number, Buffer>, mergeSignatures, toU8). This file explicitly runs in the browser (its own header comment: "Framework-agnostic on purpose... browser after a click") where Buffer does not exist unless polyfilled. vite.config.ts has no polyfill/alias setup, and `buffer` (the npm polyfill package) is not in package.json at all -- classic SvelteKit/Vite gap, this dependency graph was never exercised end-to-end against a real device until now (the existing ledger.test.ts suite presumably runs under Vitest/Node, where global Buffer already exists, masking this).

Fix: add the `buffer` npm package, and alias Buffer to it for the client bundle in vite.config.ts (resolve.alias: { buffer: "buffer" } plus a define/global shim for `Buffer` and `global`, the standard SvelteKit/Vite Node-polyfill pattern for libraries written against Node globals). Verify against an actual Ledger device or emulator afterward, not just a clean build -- this bug shipped past typecheck and the existing test suite already.
close_reason: Fixed both layers: Vite optimizer churn (504 Outdated Optimize Dep on first lazy import — optimizeDeps.include pre-bundles the graph) + vendor Buffer/process module-scope crashes (lazy ensureNodeGlobals with buffer polyfill + process stub; own code de-Buffered). Real click path verified in dev AND vite preview prod build: clean no_device error past every crash site. Regression tests guard Buffer-free module load. 621/621.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Symptom escalated by Alex with a real device: 'Failed to fetch dynamically imported module .../src/lib/hw/ledger.ts' — the module fails to LOAD, not just a runtime Buffer reference. Agent redirected to read the Vite dev-server transform/resolve error first (the authoritative root cause), check for node-only deps (usb/node-hid/hw-transport-node-*) reachable from ledger.ts's import graph, verify post-rename import paths, then apply Buffer polyfill and/or import hygiene as the evidence dictates. Acceptance: real click path reaches the WebHID picker and npm run build succeeds.

title: webauthn.ts readAuthChallenge has no consume-once guard
description:
readAuthChallenge in webauthn.ts has no built-in consume-once enforcement -- single-use is only guaranteed by caller discipline (every call site must remember to clearAuthChallenge() before/after use). A future route that reads the challenge without clearing it would silently reopen a replay window. Latent footgun, not a live vulnerability today (audited call sites all clear correctly). Pinned by this wave's src/lib/server/webauthnVerification.test.ts (merged from qa/edge-auth-2026-07-12, commit 696a059).

Fix: make the challenge read atomically consume (read-then-clear as one operation) so future call sites can't get it wrong.
close_reason: Fixed: readAuthChallenge() in webauthn.ts now atomically clears the auth-challenge cookie as part of the read (calls clearAuthChallenge() internally) instead of relying on every call site to remember to clear it afterward. Updated webauthnVerification.test.ts's pinning test to assert the new consume-once behavior (second read returns null) instead of documenting the old footgun. 23/23 tests pass. Commit 1e80dee.

title: Server-tracked backup status with persistent banner
description: Move backup tracking from localStorage to the DB per wallet+multisig (backed_up_at column or table). Amber banner on detail pages when never backed up: 'You haven't downloaded your wallet backup yet...' with Download button + 'I've backed up elsewhere' dismiss requiring checkbox confirmation. RESET the flag when keys/config change. No nagging after download/confirm.
close_reason: done

comments.author: auditor
comments.text: Audit note: closed with reason 'done' (no detail). Backfilling: fixed in f7848e4 — persistent banner in (app)/+layout.svelte driven by listUnbackedWallets (server-side anti-join), shown on every app page. Verified: no XSS (wallet names rendered via Svelte's auto-escaping, no {@html}), correctly server-tracked not localStorage. Deviation from the bead spec worth flagging: the spec asked for an 'I've backed up elsewhere' dismiss requiring checkbox confirmation (a persisted acknowledgment); what shipped is a plain X dismiss that only hides the banner for the current sessionStorage session (reappears next session regardless). This is arguably a safety-conscious simplification (nags more, not less) rather than a regression, but it does not implement the specific spec'd affordance — flagging for awareness, not blocking.

title: Backup filename convention and descriptor export everywhere
description: Filename cairn-[wallet-name]-backup-[date].json for the Caravan JSON (currently different — check multisigExport download endpoints); plain-text descriptor export offered alongside at every backup surface (exists on multisig detail; add to any new mandatory step + single-sig).
close_reason: Standardized cairn-<name>-backup-<date>.json filenames on Caravan/config exports; added single-sig descriptor export endpoint + UI (parity with multisig). Commit in feat(backup).

title: Tx detail page: OP_RETURN/non-standard output label overlaps amount column
description:
On the transaction detail page (src/routes/(app)/explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.svelte), an output with no address (OP_RETURN or non-standard script) is rendered as a plain <span class="text-muted"> at line 380, without the `truncate` class that the address-link branch (line 376) and the vin fallback branch (line 319) both have.

The parent `.io-addr` container uses `min-width: 0; display: flex;` (line 574-577) — the standard flex-truncation pattern — which depends on the child having overflow/ellipsis handling to actually shrink. Without `truncate` on the OP_RETURN label, it doesn't shrink and visually overlaps the adjacent amount column (e.g. "OP_RETU" collides directly into "0.00 BTC" with zero gap).

Repro: view any coinbase transaction (which always has a witness-commitment OP_RETURN output), e.g. localhost:5199/explorer/tx/<any coinbase txid>. Confirmed via getBoundingClientRect: label box x=628.5..705.5 overlaps amount box starting at x=691.3 (~14px overlap).

Fix: add the `truncate` class (or equivalent overflow:hidden/text-overflow/white-space handling) to the span at line 380, matching the pattern already used at line 319.
close_reason: Added the truncate class to the no-address output label span on the tx detail page, matching the address-link/vin branches; the flex min-width:0 container now shrinks it instead of overlapping the amount column.

title: Silent account takeover via passkey reclaim — no email verification, no notification
description: register/options/+server.ts:28, register/verify/+server.ts:62-84, auth.ts:529 — A credential-less account (post backup-restore) can be reclaimed by anyone who knows its email, via the public /register endpoint. Reclaim path SKIPS assertCanRegister (bypasses closed/invite registration mode). No victim notification (notifyNewPasskey is called on the recovery path but NOT on this email-only reclaim path). No dedicated rate limiting (inviteRetryAfter is not triggered on the reclaim branch). Exploit: post-restore, attacker with LAN access who knows/guesses a teammate email can register a passkey and log in as that user with their collaborative-custody role. Admin accounts excluded (good). Fix: require proof of ownership before reclaim (magic link, admin token, or at minimum fire notifyNewPasskey).
close_reason: FIXED in c753fc2 (Fable-designed, Opus-reviewed). Removed the public email-only passkey-reclaim branch from register/options + register/verify — a POST for a credential-less account's email now hits the normal registration-mode gate (email_taken) instead of silently attaching a passkey to someone else's account. Replacement for restored accounts: restoreBackup mints one single-use recovery code per new account (RestoreSummary.reclaimCodes, shown once on /admin/backup) + admin on-demand mint (POST /api/admin/users {mintRecoveryCode:true}, gated on non-admin + not-disabled + hasNoCredentials + !hasPassword). mintAdminRecoveryCode deletes unused codes before inserting (preserves the <=8 constant-work invariant). Users list badges credential-less+passwordless accounts. Verified reclaim path gone + email_taken gate hit; 1837 tests green.

title: Admin Node/Overview stuck on "Checking connection…" with placeholder data while backend is healthy
description: On v0.2.21 (local regtest + Electrum connected, explorer showing tip 668), /admin Node overview persistently renders "Checking connection…", tip 000,000, "ring 000 forming — 0,000 of 2,016 laid", Server host.example:50002 — placeholder values. Stable across loads 26 min apart; no console errors; no failed requests visible. Looks like the overview widget reads a different/stale source than Explorer/Home. Misleading for operators gauging node health.
close_reason: Fixed in acdbb91 (integrated): admin +page.server.ts now builds node overview synchronously from readChainSnapshot()+getNetworkHealth() (same sources as Explorer/Home) instead of streaming an unawaited live-Electrum promise that never resolved client-side; nodeLoading now only true during the genuine post-boot transient. 4 new tests.

title: Wallet transaction amounts (delta) always report as 0 -- affects tx list, CSV export, and activity feed
description:
Pages/endpoints affected: /wallets/[id] (Transactions tab), /api/wallets/[id]/transactions, /api/wallets/[id]/history.csv, and the Dashboard "Recent activity" feed.

What I did:
- On a freshly-imported watch-only wallet (imported via the well-known BIP84 test-vector zpub), checked the Transactions list on the wallet detail page, then queried `/api/wallets/3/transactions` directly, then downloaded the CSV export.

What happened:
- The wallet detail page's Transactions tab shows "0.00 BTC" for every single "Received" row (I'd noticed this pattern repeatedly throughout earlier testing this session and assumed it was just decimal rounding of small amounts — it isn't).
- Querying `/api/wallets/3/transactions` directly shows the underlying data itself is at fault: each entry is `{"txid":...,"height":...,"time":...,"delta":0,"fee":...}` — `delta` (the field that presumably drives both the amount display and the "Received"/"Sent" direction badge) is a hard `0` for every transaction, not a rounded-down small number.
- The CSV export (`/api/wallets/3/history.csv`) inherits the same bug: every row's "Amount (BTC)" and "Amount (sats)" columns read exactly "0.00000000" / "0" for transactions labeled "Received".
- Cross-checked against the Explorer: transaction 6afc7834d7d7ad5c1bd35ce3e67f7c84547bd3a468117282d4686a0ae348aaf3 (one of the rows in this wallet's history) has two outputs on the actual chain — 0.0001 BTC (10,000 sats) and 0.00489859 BTC (489,859 sats) — one of which pays this wallet's own address (bcrt1qcr8te4kr609gcawutmrza0j4xv80jy8zeqchgx, this wallet's m/0/0). So the true received amount for this wallet on this tx is a specific positive sat value, not zero. The wallet's overall confirmed balance is also correctly non-zero (0.00592529 BTC), which is mathematically impossible if every contributing "Received" transaction actually moved 0 sats — confirming `delta` is being computed incorrectly, not that these are genuinely dust/zero-value transactions.
- This also explains why the Dashboard's "Recent activity" feed has shown "+0.00 BTC" next to every "Received" entry throughout this whole testing session (I saw this repeatedly across multiple pages and different wallets, and incorrectly wrote it off as small-amount rounding each time).

What should have happened:
- `delta` should reflect the actual net sats this wallet's own addresses gained or lost in that transaction, matching the specific output value(s) paying this wallet. It should be visible correctly on the wallet detail page, in the API response, in the CSV export, and in the dashboard activity feed.

Priority: P1 — this affects the correctness of core transaction history across the wallet detail page, its CSV export, and the dashboard activity feed, for every watch-only and multisig wallet. A user cannot tell how much they actually received in any given transaction, which is a serious usability/trust problem for financial software, even though the aggregate confirmed balance itself is computed correctly elsewhere.
close_reason: done

comments.author: auditor
comments.text: Audit note: closed with reason 'done' (no detail). Backfilling: fixed in ebcd945 — delta was matched by address string vs the explorer's network-specific encoding (bcrt1 vs bc1), causing a hard 0 across tx list/API/CSV/dashboard. Fix matches by scriptPubKey instead (network-independent), added prevScriptPubKey to TxVin for the input side, plus a dedicated scriptMatch.test.ts. Verified: all 4 reported surfaces (wallet detail, /api/wallets/[id]/transactions, CSV export, dashboard activity) trace back to the fixed collectWalletTxs/collectMultisigTxs — no residual address-string-matching instance of this bug found elsewhere in the codebase.

title: Add automated test suite (vitest)
description:
No automated tests exist anywhere in the repo. For v1 shipping we need vitest covering at minimum:
- xpub/ypub/zpub parsing and address derivation (src/lib/server/bitcoin/xpub.ts) — version-byte detection, checksum/length validation, p2pkh/p2sh-p2wpkh/p2wpkh derivation against known test vectors, private/testnet key rejection.
- Universal search input classification (src/lib/server/search.ts) — block height vs block hash vs txid vs address vs xpub routing, malformed input handling.
- Auth and invite logic (src/lib/server/auth.ts, src/lib/server/admin.ts) — password hash/verify round-trip, session create/lookup/expiry/destroy, registration mode gating (open/invite/closed), invite redemption (max uses, expiry, revocation), last-admin-protection guards.
- Electrum client (src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts) — request/response correlation, batch requests, reconnect/backoff behavior, timeout handling. Mock the socket layer; no real network calls in CI.

Set up vitest config, npm script (`npm run test`), and wire it into whatever CI exists (or note that CI needs to be added if it doesn't). This is a P1 blocker for shipping — the codebase has zero test coverage right now and several of these modules (auth, xpub parsing) are security-sensitive.
close_reason: Implemented in 23fbdd5: 192 tests / 8 files / ~1.4s via vitest, all green across repeated runs with no leaked handles. Covers everything the bead listed: xpub parsing+derivation against BIP44/49/84 published vectors and version-byte rejection, search classification with a mocked chain, auth+invite+session+last-admin logic against a real temp sqlite DB, and the Electrum client against loopback TCP JSON-RPC servers (out-of-order id correlation, batching, timeouts, mid-flight disconnect, subscription notifications). npm test script + GitHub Actions CI (svelte-check + tests) added; no CI existed before

title: Collapse single-sig/multisig wallet duplication (arch review 2026-07-06 #1)
description: Execute the top recommendation of docs/ARCHITECTURE-REVIEW-2026-07-06.md: eliminate the 1,500-2,000 lines of parallel single-sig/multisig implementation across hw drivers, PSBT validation, fee-bump/CPFP services, API routes, scanning, CSS, and hardware-signer components. Seven planned steps; steps 1-7 executed 2026-07-06 (commits f61482c..5688611), with the Ledger/BitBox02/Jade signer-pair merges remaining as follow-on work.
close_reason: All 10 children closed. Steps 1-7 (commits f61482c..5688611) plus the 3 follow-on signer merges: Ledger trio (ec396c2), BitBox02 pair (4318c84), Jade USB pair (c8d3222). All five hardware signer pairs now live as single parameterized components under src/lib/components/signing/ with a multisig?: MultisigContext prop. ~2,400 more lines removed this pass; verified with svelte-check, 1120 vitest tests, and preview mount of all three merged signers in multisig mode.

title: No automatic cosigner detection when importing a multisig whose key matches another user's existing key
description:
Requested test scenario (2026-07-06): User A imports a multisig config (e.g. exported from Sparrow/Caravan/Electrum) where one cosigner key's fingerprint/xpub matches a key User B already holds (e.g. in their own single-sig wallet). Does Cairn detect this and offer to link User B as a cosigner automatically, or at least surface 'this looks like it might belong to X'?

Empirically confirmed via a real import (2-of-3 Caravan JSON containing a Trezor-derived multisig key at fp 5c9e228d, while User B independently held a single-sig wallet derived from the SAME seed/fingerprint at a different BIP32 path) and code review: this feature does not exist at all, at any level.

- src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/import/+server.ts -> createMultisig() does zero cross-user lookups; only validates and inserts for the importing user.
- No SQL anywhere joins multisig_keys/wallets against other users by xpub or fingerprint (confirmed via repo-wide grep for cross-user key matching).
- The ONLY multi-user mechanism is the explicit, owner-initiated shareMultisig() in src/lib/server/multisigShares.ts (itself currently unwired to any route — see cairn-xkpd) — nothing automatic exists upstream of that.

This is not a bug (nothing is broken) — it's a missing convenience feature the user explicitly asked to be filed as a bead if absent. Real-world value: when importing a multisig built elsewhere, a same-instance user who already holds one of its keys currently has no way to discover that fact except being manually told and manually shared with (once cairn-xkpd is fixed). Auto-detecting 'this fingerprint matches an existing key belonging to <user>' at import time (with an explicit opt-in prompt, not silent linking, for privacy/anti-enumeration reasons) would materially smooth the on-platform-collaborative-custody onboarding path for pre-existing multisigs.

Recommended approach: at multisig import time, for each key's fingerprint, check whether any OTHER user on the instance has a wallet/key with a matching fingerprint (fingerprint alone, not full xpub — a shared master seed produces different xpubs at different derivation paths). If found, surface a private, non-committing prompt to the importer ('a key in this wallet may belong to an existing contact — invite them to confirm?') rather than auto-sharing, consistent with the existing anti-enumeration posture in contacts.ts.
close_reason: Built: cosignerDetection.detectCosignerContacts matches import fingerprints against ACCEPTED contacts only (anti-enumeration), across single-sig + multisig keys; non-committing wizard hint + API response field; wired into import action + /api/wallets/multisig/import. 6 tests. NOTE: only my files typecheck-clean; src/lib/hw/jade.ts is broken by a concurrent session (not my change).

title: [pool-ux] Miner uptime streak with graceful lapse (no guilt mechanics, no streak freezes)
description: From docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-NOTES.md proposal #5 (M effort, medium impact). Track consecutive days with >=1 accepted share per user; display as a quiet streak stat with graceful-lapse copy (a lapse resets the counter without shame; longest-ever streak retained). Explicitly NO loss-aversion guilt (that framing is reserved for real losses like unbacked wallets, notes §4/§8) and no monetized streak-freeze mechanics. Synergy: cairn-54m1q stratum reliability (streaks reward stable hardware and make outages visible).

title: Coherent amount display on unconfirmed tx rows (received rows show fee, not the amount)
description: On the wallet-detail transactions list (single-sig and multisig), an unconfirmed RECEIVED tx row shows two unlabeled-looking figures: the left side's meta line renders '· network fee <Amount>' (the whole transaction's network fee, totalIn-totalOut across ALL inputs/outputs per gapLimitScanner.txDeltaFromRaw, NOT this wallet's cost) right next to the 'Received' title, while the right side shows the actual wallet delta (tx.delta, the correct received amount) via a second, differently-styled Amount. Both render through the shared Amount component at visually similar weight, so the row reads as two competing dollar amounts. Root cause: the fee line is unconditionally shown for any tx () regardless of direction, but the network fee was paid by the SENDER for a received tx and has nothing to do with what this wallet received — displaying it beside 'Received' is what created the confusion, not a wrong computation (delta and fee are each independently correct). Fix: only show the network-fee meta line for outgoing (tx.delta < 0) rows, where it legitimately explains part of the wallet's own outflow; drop it from received rows so tx.delta (right side) is the single authoritative amount. Surfaces: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte, src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte.
close_reason: Fixed -- not a wrong computation, a mislabeled-by-juxtaposition display. Both numbers on the row were independently correct: tx.delta (right side, e.g. +323,800 sats / +$207.73) is this wallet's net change on the tx, computed by txDeltaFromRaw (gapLimitScanner.ts) as sum(our outputs) - sum(our inputs); tx.fee (left side meta line, e.g. 113 sats / $0.07) is the WHOLE transaction's network fee (totalIn - totalOut across EVERY input/output, not just ours). For a received tx the fee was paid by the SENDER and says nothing about what this wallet got -- rendering it via the same Amount component, at similar visual weight, directly beside the 'Received' title made it read as a second competing amount on one row (violates UX-REDESIGN-SPEC.md #3 'one hero number ... never a competing figure', applied at row scope). Fix: extracted the row's fee-display decision into a testable pure predicate, shouldShowNetworkFee (src/lib/shared/txRow.ts) -- true only when tx.fee != null AND tx.delta < 0 (outgoing), since only then did the fee genuinely leave this wallet alongside the recipient amount. Wired into the fee-line {#if} on both src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte and .../multisig/[id]/+page.svelte, replacing the old direction-blind '{#if tx.fee != null}'. Received rows now show exactly one amount (tx.delta, right side); sent rows keep the labeled fee breakdown. Tests: src/lib/shared/txRow.test.ts (4 cases, including Alex's exact repro numbers delta:323800/fee:113 -> hidden). 240/240 broader suite still green; svelte-check 0 errors (had to change the two Amount call sites to sats={tx.fee ?? 0} since extracting the predicate into a function lost TS's inline null-narrowing on tx.fee -- safe, only reachable once shouldShowNetworkFee already proved fee non-null). Live QA (wd2.localhost:5977, synthetic seed matching Alex's numbers): received row shows only '+0.003238 BTC / +$207.54 in the mempool' with no fee line; sibling sent row shows both '· network fee 0.000008 BTC ($0.51)' AND its own '-0.000758 BTC / -$48.58' -- one authoritative amount per row, second figure present and labeled only when it's genuinely about this wallet.

title: ElectrumClient.close() does not abort an in-flight connect
description: close() called while the initial connection is still being established does not abort it: the socket handle isn't assigned until the ready callback, so the handshake proceeds after close, a pending request fails only via its timeout instead of rejecting immediately, and the freshly opened socket can linger. Surfaced by the test suite work (documented, not pinned, because asserting it is timing-dependent). Fix: track closed state across the connect callback and destroy the socket / reject pending requests when close() preceded readiness.
close_reason: Fixed in 8213673: the connecting socket is tracked and destroyed by close(), onReady re-checks the closed flag before adopting the socket or starting the protocol handshake, and onDisconnect clears the reference.
