comments.author: auditor
comments.text: Audit note: closed with reason 'done' (no detail). Backfilling: fixed in a816047 — single-sig wizard's Key step gained a restore-from-backup file upload (prefills xpub/name, still runs full server validation, no bypass). Verified: client-side JSON.parse only reads known string fields (no prototype-pollution/XSS risk), multisig/Caravan files are correctly detected and redirected to the multisig importer. Minor gap found: the wallets empty-state 'Restore from a backup' link points to the exact same /wallets/new URL as 'Add your first wallet' — it does not actually jump to the restore affordance (which only appears after clicking through step 1 of the wizard), so the two buttons are currently functionally identical despite different labels. Filing a follow-up bead.

title: P2: audit for other PHANTOM (undeclared) runtime dependencies that adapter-node inlines and can break on subpath resolution
description: v0.2.36 (cairn-jj6q) fixed a prod-boot crash caused by bitcoinjs-lib being an UNDECLARED dependency (pulled transitively via @ledgerhq/hw-app-btc). Because it was undeclared, adapter-node's rollup inlined it into the mining chunk, and its CJS require('@noble/hashes/ripemd160') then resolved to the wrong hoisted version at runtime. This is a class of bug: any undeclared dep used at runtime can be inlined and break on an exports-map/subpath mismatch, invisible to dev+vitest. ACTION: audit src for imports of packages NOT listed in package.json dependencies (e.g. grep imports vs deps), and either declare them or confirm they are dev-only. Add a prod-boot smoke test (node server.mjs must reach 'app ready') to CI so this whole class is caught before shipping — the on-device Umbrel prod-boot test being owed is exactly why cairn-jj6q shipped.
close_reason: Phantom-dep audit complete: static-scanned all imports in src/** (2094 files), diffed vs package.json deps. One phantom found: @ledgerhq/psbtv2 (dynamic-import-only, client-side lazy load in src/lib/hw/ledger.ts, resolves transitively via @ledgerhq/hw-app-btc@11.2.1). Added @ledgerhq/psbtv2 ^0.5.0 to dependencies, ran npm install. svelte/@sveltejs/kit devDep-only imports are standard SvelteKit pattern (bundled by adapter-node), not phantom risk; vitest is test-only. Verified: npm run build OK, npm run check 0 errors, prod-boot smoke (node server.mjs) reached 'cairn: app ready', HTTP 302 on / and {status:ok} on /api/health, killed cleanly. CI smoke test NOT added (out of scope, owned by testing session per brief) — full findings in session report.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: QA half of this bead's ACTION item (the CI prod-boot smoke test, called out as NOT added in the close_reason) is now done: scripts/qa/prod-boot-smoke.mjs + npm run qa:prod-boot. Builds for production (npm run build), boots the real entrypoint (node server.mjs -- same as Dockerfile CMD) against a fresh temp SQLite DB + dead-end Electrum target (127.0.0.1:1) on a probed free port, waits for the 'cairn: app ready' line, holds it alive 5-25s, then asserts GET / is 2xx/3xx and GET /api/health is 200 {status:ok}, with no fatal markers in output. Exits 0/1 for CI. 3 full end-to-end runs on design/evergreen-identity (dfe170f) all PASS: build ~16-18s, boot ~0.5s to ready, GET / -> 302, GET /api/health -> {status:ok}, exit code 0. Verified the fail path too (invalid port -> exit 1). Tracked as its own bead: cairn-pz45k (closed, cross-refs this one).

title: notify_* channel rows not hidden/degraded in Settings > Notifications when flag is off
description:
QA pass for bead cairn-1ytc (feature-flag matrix, my batch: notify_email/notify_telegram/notify_ntfy/notify_nostr/notify_webhook).

BUG: turning off any notify_* flag in /admin/feature-flags does NOT hide or grey out that channel's row on /settings/notifications. Server-side enforcement is CORRECT (requireFeature blocks save/test with a 403 carrying the right userMessage) -- this is a UX/discoverability gap, not a security hole.

Root cause: src/routes/(app)/settings/notifications/+page.server.ts's load() builds `channels` by mapping the hardcoded EXTERNAL_CHANNELS = ['email','telegram','ntfy','nostr','webhook'] with NO flag filtering/annotation, and +page.svelte renders all 5 rows unconditionally (no reference to notify_* flags anywhere in the file). Contrast with src/lib/components/DevicePicker.svelte's hw_* handling, which does filter client-side -- the notify_* flags never got the equivalent treatment on this page.

Net effect: with notify_email OFF, the Email row still shows "not connected", is fully expandable, and its form (address, personal-SMTP sub-section, PGP) is fully interactive. The user can fill it in and click Save -- only then does it fail, with a correct 403 message ("Email notifications have been disabled by your administrator.") rendered via the existing saveError UI. No console errors; the failure is caught and displayed, just never anticipated.

REPRO (verified live in browser, admin session qa-matrix@test.local, server on data/qa-matrix-b.db):
1. /admin/feature-flags -> toggle "Email channel" (notify_email) to Disable (also did Telegram/ntfy/Nostr/Webhook together for the other 4).
2. /settings/notifications -> all 5 channel rows render identically to the enabled state ("not connected", clickable, full config UI).
3. Expanded Email, set address to a test value, clicked Save -> 403 surfaced correctly as a form-error: "Email notifications have been disabled by your administrator."
4. Toggled notify_email back on, repeated the same save -> succeeded, row flipped to "connected", no error.
5. Confirmed the missing-gate pattern is identical for Telegram/ntfy/Nostr/Webhook by code (same EXTERNAL_CHANNELS loop, same lack of flag filtering) and by live inspection while all 5 were off together.

FIX: have the +page.server.ts loader include each channel's resolved flag state (event.locals.flags?.[`notify_${channel}`]), and have +page.svelte grey out / show the flag's userMessage on a disabled channel's row the same way DevicePicker.svelte does for hw_*, rather than relying solely on the save-time 403.

Evidence: HEARTWOOD_DB=data/qa-matrix-b.db, seeded via scripts/qa/seed-flagmatrix.mjs, dev server on port 5315, browser session isolated via http://qamatrixb.localhost:5315 (distinct *.localhost subdomain) to avoid httpOnly cairn_session collisions with other concurrent QA sessions sharing the bare localhost cookie domain.
close_reason: Client-side hide wired: notify_* flag off now hides the channel row entirely (was server-403-only). See comment for file list + test evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Fixed: Settings > Notifications now hides (not just server-403s) a channel row whose notify_* flag is off for the signed-in user. Added src/routes/(app)/settings/notifications/notifyChannelVisibility.ts (pure isChannelVisible/visibleChannelIds helpers, mirrors DevicePicker.svelte's hw_* !== false hide convention) + notifyChannelVisibility.test.ts (11 unit tests, since this repo has no DOM-render harness). +page.svelte wraps each of the 5 channel rows (email/telegram/ntfy/nostr/webhook) in an isChannelVisible(id, page.data.flags) guard, filters the event-preference channel-toggle chips and the greyed-out hint to visibleExternalChannels, and shows an explanatory line when every channel is flagged off. Server enforcement (403 on save) untouched. npm run check: 0 errors. vitest: 11/11 new + 39/39 in settings+featureFlags dirs pass.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Live browser re-verification 2026-07-13 (adminqa instance, port 5401, data/qa-adminqa.db): confirmed the shipped fix end-to-end, not just by code/unit-test read.

As user id2 with notify_email toggled OFF globally: /settings/notifications rendered zero occurrences of the string 'Email' anywhere on the page (channel row AND event-preference chips both gone -- full-page text search came back empty), while Telegram/ntfy/Nostr/Webhook rows stayed visible. Toggled notify_email back ON -> Email row returned ('Email not connected') alongside the other 4.

Also toggled all 5 notify_* flags OFF together: /settings/notifications showed the expected explanatory line verbatim -- "Your administrator has disabled every external notification channel. In-app alerts are still available." -- with the Channels section otherwise empty. Restored all 5 to enabled=1 afterward; confirmed all 5 rows render again.

No console errors, no dead UI, matches the fix description exactly. Adding this as the independent live re-verification requested by cairn-1ytc (bead itself already CLOSED from the original fix).

title: removeContact deletes contact before revoking shares — retained access on failure
description: contacts.ts:186-208 — DELETE contact row THEN calls revokeAllSharesBetween. If revoke throws, contact row is already gone (looks removed in UI) but multisig share/key access is RETAINED. Retry is a no-op since removeContact returns false (row already gone). Fix: reorder to revoke shares before deleting contact, or wrap both in one transaction.
close_reason: FIXED in 2e759e2. contacts.ts removeContact wraps DELETE FROM contacts + revokeAllSharesBetween in one BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK (accountDeletion.ts idiom), so a revoke failure rolls back the delete — access and contact row are retained together, making retry meaningful instead of a no-op. Test added.

title: Merge Jade USB signer pair (JadeUsbSigner/MultisigJadeUsbSigner) into one component

JadeUsbSigner.svelte (361 lines) vs MultisigJadeUsbSigner.svelte (525) — the multisig variant adds the Jade multisig-registration flow (deterministic registration names, cairn-1qkk). Preserve all copy/flows/error handling exactly; svelte-check + vitest + preview load of both send pages; ideally hardware-test.

title: Research: WebUSB hardware signing fails over HTTP on Umbrel (Ledger/BitBox02)
description: WebUSB requires a secure context (HTTPS or localhost); Umbrel serves Cairn over plain HTTP on the LAN, so Ledger/BitBox02 browser signing fails. Related: cairn-4b2b (Umbrel HTTP blocks all HW browser signing, deferred to redesign). Research whether any approach makes WebUSB work on Umbrel (Umbrel HTTPS options, .local TLS, Tor/https gateway, app_proxy) or whether paste/QR/file-PSBT fallback is the right answer. Outcome: recommendation documented in bead comments.
close_reason: Research complete: no zero-config WebUSB path on Umbrel (no HTTPS, .onion not secure context, localhost-bridge blocked by Chrome 142 LNA); Ledger/BitBox02 cannot sign without USB, so file/paste fallback is correct; Tailscale serve + Chrome flag are the documentable escape hatches. Full findings + recommendation in bead comments.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text:
Research complete (web research 2026-07-07, sources in session transcript). Bottom line: there is NO zero-config path to WebUSB on stock Umbrel, and Ledger + BitBox02 physically cannot sign a PSBT without USB (Ledger: no camera/SD/NFC-PSBT; BitBox02: microSD is backups-only, vendor explicitly rejects airgap signing). So paste/file PSBT is the right universal fallback — but two documentable escape hatches exist.

Findings by avenue:
1. umbrelOS built-in HTTPS: none. Issues getumbrel/umbrel#546 and #1731 (secure-context APIs) open since 2020, not in umbrelOS 1.5. Umbrel's own Tailscale remote-access doc still points at http://umbrel — insecure origin.
2. Tor .onion: dead end. 'Potentially trustworthy .onion' is Firefox/Tor-Browser-only, and those browsers have no WebUSB at all; Chromium does not treat http .onion as secure.
3. Tailscale (BEST real path): install Umbrel Tailscale app, enable MagicDNS+HTTPS, run 'tailscale serve --bg <port>' in the app terminal -> real Let's Encrypt cert at https://<host>.<tailnet>.ts.net -> genuine secure context, WebUSB/WebHID work with zero warnings. Confirmed by Vaultwarden users on Umbrel (same secure-context need). Guided power-user setup, worth a help article deep-linked from an insecure-context explainer panel.
4. Chrome flag chrome://flags/#unsafely-treat-insecure-origin-as-secure with the exact origin: still works (verified current Chromium docs); fine to document as a quick power-user workaround with warnings, never the primary instruction.
5. Localhost bridge (BTCPay Vault / Specter HWIBridge pattern): dead-ended by Chrome 142's Local Network Access permission gating — the LNA permission can only be requested FROM a secure context, so a page on http://umbrel.local can't call a localhost agent anyway. Also a heavy native-app build. Skip.
6. Self-signed TLS on a second app port: technically yields isSecureContext=true after the interstitial, but scary cert-warning UX + second URL; strictly worse than Tailscale path. Skip.
7. QR: display works over HTTP (pure DOM), but scanning the signed PSBT back needs getUserMedia = secure context. So over plain HTTP the return leg is file/paste for every device class. Jade/ColdCard-Q/Keystone/SeedSigner/Passport can QR-sign; Ledger/BitBox02 cannot.

Recommended Cairn work (carry into UI-redesign wizard-flow work, see cairn-4b2b):
(a) gate device-connect buttons on window.isSecureContext and show a purpose-built plain-language panel instead of a broken flow;
(b) for Ledger/BitBox02 document the honest fallback: download PSBT -> sign in Sparrow/BitBoxApp on the same computer over USB -> upload signed file back;
(c) help article: Tailscale serve path (primary) + Chrome flag (advanced);
(d) long-term: watch getumbrel/umbrel#1731; Start9's LAN-HTTPS may solve this for free on that platform.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: CORRECTION (2026-07-07, cairn-n5ok): two conclusions in this research were wrong, caught by Alex's observation that Caravan connects HW fine on the same Umbrel. (1) 'Trezor popup needs secure context' was circular — that was Cairn's own isSecureContext gate reporting unavailable; @trezor/connect-web has no such gate and the popup holds the transport in its own secure context. (2) 'BitBoxBridge is dead-ended by origin whitelist + Chrome LNA' overstated — the bridge PROMPTS the user to approve unknown origins (AllowedHosts), and it demonstrably works from http://umbrel.local today (Caravan uses bitbox02ConnectAuto). Still true: Ledger has no non-USB path and WebUSB/WebHID stay unavailable on insecure origins, so Ledger remains file/paste on Umbrel; Tailscale/Chrome-flag remain the Ledger escape hatches.

title: Pending/mempool block renders at bottom of explorer block list instead of top
description: The explorer's tip-view block list rendered the {#if pending} dashed pending-block row AFTER the {#each blocks} loop, so the next-block-to-be-mined appeared below all confirmed blocks instead of above them. Reported by Alex from real-device testing.
close_reason: Fixed in src/routes/(app)/explorer/+page.svelte: moved the {#if pending}...{/if} dashed pending-block row to render BEFORE the {#each blocks} loop instead of after it, so the next-block-to-be-mined sorts above all confirmed blocks. Verified via DOM order inspection on /explorer (querySelectorAll('.ring-row')): with a seeded mempoolBlocks projection, element 0 is .pending-row (text 'pending / projected next...'), element 1 is block 672 -- confirmed at both desktop 1280x720 and mobile 375x812 viewports. Full suite 3676 tests green.

title: Signup form: submitting with all fields empty gives zero feedback
close_reason: Client validation proven working on trusted submit; silent-submit was a synthetic-event artifact. Extracted validateSignup() + unit tests as regression seam.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Repro: navigate to /signup (open registration, no invite needed). Leave Display name, Email, Password all blank. Click "Create account".

Evidence: form has novalidate="" attribute (confirmed via JS: form.noValidate === true), and there is no client-side JS validation wired up either -- checked via network tab: zero HTTP requests fire (no GET/POST to /signup or /api/auth/*) when submitting blank. No error text appears anywhere on the page (confirmed via get_page_text before/after -- identical). Native browser required-field tooltips are also suppressed by novalidate, so there is truly no signal to the user that anything happened.

Contrast: submitting a too-short password (6 chars, min is 8) DOES correctly reach the server and returns a clear inline message "Password must be at least 8 characters." -- so server-side validation and error rendering both work fine when the request actually fires. The bug is specifically that required-field checks never even trigger a request, so a non-technical user who tabs through and clicks Create account without filling anything in sees the button appear to do nothing.

Fix suggestion: either remove novalidate and let native browser validation show (simplest), or add equivalent client-side required-field checks with inline messages matching the password-length message style.

Severity: P2 -- not a security issue, but a real first-impression usability dead-end for a non-technical audience (explicit QA target per CLAUDE.md UX philosophy).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: ANALYSIS: 'silent all-empty submit' is an automation artifact. Verified LIVE (port 5344): form.requestSubmit() runs validate() and renders Banner 'Enter a display name.', while synthetic click AND synthetic Enter both fail to trigger the browser default form-submit, so onsubmit never fires (click landed dead-center on enabled BUTTON.btn-primary). Trusted human click/Enter submits fine. validate() wired since 2026-07-05. FIX: extracted validateSignup() pure helper + 9 Vitest cases as regression seam; hardened invite branch vs undefined inviteCode.

title: Wallet config backup as a first-class safety requirement
description: Per Alex: losing a multisig config without backup can mean permanently losing funds — treat config backup with seed-phrase gravity. Required education copy: tooltip 'Your wallet configuration file contains the public keys and settings needed to find your bitcoin on the blockchain. Without it, even having your hardware wallet isn't enough for multisig — you need ALL the public keys to reconstruct the wallet.' First-time panel: safe-combination analogy (your key opens your lock, this file is the combination that says which safe). Include a section on importing the file into Sparrow/Electrum as a safety net. PRIOR ART already in tree (verify, extend, don't duplicate): multisig wizard Done step has backup downloads + localStorage first-download flag; detail page has a Download backup header action with reminder dot; admin encrypted instance backup/restore shipped in 66829fa; import validates via parseCaravanImport; stateless page covers fresh-instance use partially.
close_reason: Epic complete: all children closed (dcp/iyj/lun6 earlier; iylz/2vha/2xhw now). Wallet-config backup is first-class across creation, banners, filename convention, admin prominence, and periodic reminders.

title: Desktop layout overhaul (mobile design stretched on desktop)
description: Heartwood's desktop viewport currently renders the mobile layout stretched into a narrow centered column (main.main max-width:940px + assorted per-page caps), with unused whitespace on both sides and mobile-only nav persisting past where a sidebar should take over. This epic tracks the fix: a real desktop shell (HWSidebar) plus two content measures (reading/data) replacing all width caps, rolled out in 3 waves. Canonical spec: docs/DESKTOP-LAYOUT-DESIGN.md — read before starting any child task.
close_reason: Fully shipped in v0.2.35 (168380b): children closed with landed commits 21f84a7, 14e28a4, 9f0e636; all 14 per-page specs in docs/DESKTOP-LAYOUT-DESIGN.md sec.4 delivered as lane-pattern pages; unconverted admin/settings/wizard pages intentionally out of scope per doc; no sec.6 skinny-strip regressions (640px caps are deliberate prose-width). Scope-scout audit 2026-07-19.

title: Layout Wave 1: shell — HWSidebar + lane tokens + remove 940 cap
description: Wave 1 of docs/DESKTOP-LAYOUT-DESIGN.md. Ship the shell alone: app.css tokens+utilities (--measure-reading, --measure-data, .lane-reading, .lane-data, .page-grid, .quiet-rail, .full-bleed, --sidebar-w, --sidebar-w-wide, --rail-w, --lane-gutter), remove the global 940px cap AND every per-page cap (760 admin/activity, 660 settings, 640 mining/wallet-inner, 680 send), route-to-lane mapping in (app)/+layout.svelte, new HWSidebar component (extends HWRail, compact rail CSS carries over verbatim) for >=1160px, collapse toggle persisted to localStorage('hw.sidebar.collapsed') with SSR-safe hydrated-gated read. QA gate: mobile <900px must be byte-identical to today — this is the acceptance criterion.
close_reason: done

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Wave 1 shell implemented on design/evergreen-identity.

IMPLEMENTATION SHAPE: single morphing component (HWSidebar.svelte), NOT two.
The compact icon rail (901-1159px) and the labeled sidebar (>=1160px) share
one DOM tree, morphed entirely by CSS. Rationale: a single tree keeps exactly
one NotificationPanel mounted (one SSE stream) — a two-component or show/hide
split would have doubled the /api/notifications/stream connection. HWRail's
compact CSS carries over verbatim as the "compact tier" (default) rules; the
labeled tier is layered as @media(min-width:1160px) .rail:not(.collapsed)
overrides, so a collapsed sidebar falls straight back to the compact tier with
zero override bleed. HWRail.svelte is now unused (only +layout imported it) —
left untouched; a cleanup bead can remove it.

FILES CHANGED:
- src/app.css — tokens (--sidebar-w 236, --sidebar-w-wide 248, --rail-w 280,
  --lane-gutter 56, --measure-reading 780, --measure-data 1180,
  --measure-data-wide 1320) + utilities (.lane-reading, .lane-data, .page-grid,
  .quiet-rail, .full-bleed). Min-width tiers 1160/1600; mobile authority
  (max-width:900) untouched.
- src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte — HWRail->HWSidebar; laneFor() route->lane
  map; removed .main max-width:940; <main class="main lane-{lane}">.
- src/lib/components/heartwood/HWSidebar.svelte — new.

VERIFICATION (JS-measured; screenshot pipeline was down environment-wide):
- Desktop 1440: aside 236px, wordmark shown, nav rows flex-row 40px, label 15px,
  2px accent-bright left marker (#96bbe0 dark), reading-lane main=780px, no h-scroll.
- Data lane: /wallets main max-width=1180px.
- Laptop 1100: aside 92px, wordmark hidden, nav rows flex-column 68px, label 10px,
  no collapse toggle — byte-identical to old HWRail. No h-scroll.
- Mobile 375: aside display:none, main padding 20/18/48 preserved, main width=375
  (lane inert), no h-scroll — mobile byte-identical (QA gate met).
- Collapse toggle: click -> collapsed (92px, wordmark none, localStorage
  hw.sidebar.collapsed="1"); click -> expanded ("0"). Survives reload (reads "1",
  restores 92px, hydrated-gated $effect does NOT clobber) — SSR-safe pattern holds.
- npm run check: 0 errors (1 pre-existing mining warning). Tests: 178 passed
  (layout.server + src/lib/components).
- Light-theme AA: active pill reuses the SAME --accent-bright/--accent-muted pair
  the shipped compact rail already uses. Confirmed :root under light resolves
  --accent-bright=#375f8a, --accent-muted=light; computed contrast ~5.0:1 (label
  vs pill) / ~5.7:1 (vs rail bg) — AA pass. (Browser showed stale sub-4.5 reads,
  isolated to a Chromium CDP-emulation var-substitution artifact on pre-existing
  elements — fresh elements resolved the light tokens correctly; the product sets
  theme pre-paint so it cannot occur there. Flagged for the visual-QA phase.)

DEVIATION FROM SPEC (needs orchestrator awareness): per-page width caps
(760/660/640/680 across admin/activity/settings/mining/wallet/send) were NOT
removed. DESKTOP-LAYOUT-DESIGN.md §5 lists them under Wave 1, but the wave brief
scoped the commit to app.css/+layout/HWSidebar only and said "do NOT restyle
individual pages (Waves 2-3)". Removing a cap without restyling the page just
re-creates the skinny-strip-in-a-wide-lane problem, so deferring each cap to its
page's wave (per §2's "checked when its wave lands") is the safer reading. The
global 940px cap IS removed.

Also: sidebar sync-status line renders a neutral "Chain sync" placeholder, not a
fabricated block height — the live readout awaits cairn-koy4.8 (same reason
HWRail kept the epoch dial static).

title: Layout Wave 2: tx detail, explorer, wallet detail, admin sub-nav, settings nav
description: Wave 2 of docs/DESKTOP-LAYOUT-DESIGN.md — Alex's highest-pain surfaces, depends on Wave 1's shell/tokens landing first. Covers: tx detail quiet rail (confirmations/block/size/fee rate/RBF-CPFP), explorer home/block/address per-page specs, wallet detail single-sig and multisig (rail leads with QuorumArc + cosigner roster on multisig, full-width pending-PSBT band above the split), admin page-local 200px sub-nav replacing the 12-toggle bar (must preserve every existing route) + full-width tables, settings page-local left section nav (Display/Security/Notifications/Health/Advanced).
close_reason: Wave 2 desktop layout implemented and verified (check 0 errors, 368 tests pass, static layout audit). Authed visual re-verify deferred to QA phase per time-box.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Wave 2 implemented on design/evergreen-identity. Per-page:
- Tx detail (explorer/tx/[txid]): >=1160 quiet metadata rail (confirmations, in-block link, fee+rate, size/vsize/WU, version/locktime, SegWit/RBF/CPFP). Inline .metrics hidden at >=1160 (moved to rail); status band + BlockContext + flow/io preserved. TxFlowDiagram 1fr auto 1fr + its own 900px stack point untouched; live confCount/tipHeight data flow untouched.
- Explorer home: recent-blocks list + quiet network-summary rail (mempool vMB/tx, next-ring fee, difficulty+retarget, node sync) in a 2/3+1/3 grid at >=1160 from existing snapshot data (no new live wiring). Rail hidden <=1159; mobile net-footer retained.
- Block detail: tx list (main) + metadata rail (hash, timestamp, miner, size/weight, total fees+median, difficulty, merkle, bits/version, prev). Inline Header-detail collapsible hidden at >=1160.
- Address: history (main) + rail (QR, tx count, total received/sent, pending, first/last seen). Inline QR/stat-line/meta-dates hidden at >=1160.
- Wallet single: reading hero + Send with quiet rail (type, signs-with+xpub, receive addr, backup status). Inline sign-note hidden at >=1160. Live logic untouched.
- Wallet multisig: same, rail leads with larger QuorumArc + cosigner roster. DEVIATION: no full-width pending-PSBT band — this detail page carries no pending-PSBT/signing-session data (drafts re-enter via send/roster flow), so the band is N/A here.
- Admin (+layout): 200px vertical sub-nav at >=1160 (accent pill + left-edge marker), horizontal toggles kept 901-1159 + mobile; all routes preserved; 760 cap removed -> data measure.
- Settings: page-local left section nav (Account/Display/Notifications/Security/Advanced/About via in-page anchors to real rows) at >=1160; 660 cap swapped to var(--measure-reading). No Health section exists here so none invented.
Pattern: every rail/nav is display:none by default, revealed only inside @media(min-width:1160px); mobile @media(max-width:900px) untouched (byte-identical) on every page. Exclusive breakpoints 1160/900.
Verification: npm run check = 0 errors (1 pre-existing mining warning). vitest explorer/wallets/admin/settings = 44 files/368 tests pass (markup+CSS only, no *.server.ts). Static audit: all rails+nav confirmed display:none outside 1160; admin 760 + settings 660 gone. Browser walkthrough at 1440/1100/375 BLOCKED: login POST does not submit through automation (documented synthetic-submit hazard) even after QA admin pw reset; authed visual re-verify deferred to QA phase.

title: Layout Wave 3: Home rail, send summary rail, mining, mempool, activity, wallet list
description: Wave 3 of docs/DESKTOP-LAYOUT-DESIGN.md — remainder, can proceed in parallel with Wave 2 once Wave 1 is stable (disjoint route trees). Covers: Home quiet rail (multi-horizon deltas, epoch/sync status, wallets-at-a-glance, backup nudge), Send persistent running-summary rail + 3-up fee options, mining hero/worker-list/rail (onboarding stays reading-measure), mempool full-width fee-band viz + projected blocks, activity data-lane columns + optional filter rail, wallet list hairline columns (name+type/balance/m-of-n/last-activity/sync-ring).
close_reason: Wave 3 desktop layouts landed: Home/send-ss/send-ms/mining/mempool/activity/wallet-list. check=0 errors, 403 route tests green, static audit clean, mobile untouched.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Implemented on design/evergreen-identity (markup+CSS only; live wiring in 89157a0/921794a preserved verbatim).

Per-page:
- Home ((app)/+page.svelte): reading-measure hero column + quiet rail at >=1160 (BalanceHorizons deltas, wallets-at-a-glance, chain-health sync line, backup nudge if unbacked). Inline horizons/wallet-list hidden at >=1160, mirrored in rail. main widened only for Home via scoped :global(main.lane-reading:has(.home)) = reading+gutter+rail. One hero preserved.
- Send single-sig ((app)/wallets/[id]/send): removed 680 cap; step column at reading measure + persistent read-only running-summary rail at >=1160 (amount, fiat secondary, recipient+count, fee, remaining) derived from existing wizard state (review/rows/confirmed/feeRate/isMax) - no new logic. Wizard step chain / signer components / sticky footers untouched (only .send-layout + .send-main + <aside> containers added).
- Send multisig ((app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/send): SAME full rail applied (structure mirrors single-sig; spendableSats as balance source) - not a reduced equivalent, structures matched.
- Mining ((app)/mining): removed 640 cap; calm reading default, dashboard widens to data lane via .is-dashboard; hero at reading measure; workers list in main col + connection/payout/earnings/odds panels in right rail via grid placement (DOM order kept => mobile stack byte-identical). Onboarding/disabled/error stay reading. Live mining nudge subscription preserved.
- Mempool ((app)/explorer/mempool): unwrapped the 2-col .columns so FeeWeather runs full data measure; tier list capped at reading measure; projected blocks already side-by-side. mempoolStats live overlay logic untouched.
- Activity ((app)/activity): removed 760 cap; fills data lane; quiet filter rail at >=1160 (duplicated existing All/Wallets/Node + Needs-a-look/Refresh, inline hidden at >=1160 so mobile/laptop byte-identical); trailing amount/time aligned into a column. Live wallet/notification debounced refresh preserved.
- Wallet list ((app)/wallets): no page-level cap existed; rows enriched into aligned columns at >=1160 (last-activity | balance) via CSS-only row-reverse, no tile grid. Live refresh untouched.

Deviations noted: (1) Mining rail holds full instrument-panel components, so it does NOT use the .quiet-rail font utility (would shrink panel type) - placed as a plain grid rail column. (2) Home/Send are reading-lane pages whose main is capped at --measure-reading by the layout; added scoped :has() overrides to widen main just enough for hero+rail (contained to those pages, no +layout.svelte edit). (3) No per-wallet sync-ring column on wallet list - per-wallet sync state isn't in load data (only an unreachable flag); did not invent it.

Verification: npm run check = 0 errors (1 pre-existing warning, mining line 77, untouched). vitest src/routes/(app) = 47 files / 403 tests passed. Static audit: all page caps (940/680/640/760/columns) removed; rails display:none by default, revealed only in @media(min-width:1160); mobile @media(max-width:900) blocks untouched (git diff shows no +/- there); no min-width:900 boundary drift added; zero unused-selector warnings (all new classes wired). Browser/auth path skipped per brief (known env-wide failure); markup integrity confirmed by svelte-check + passing route tests.

title: CSV history export vulnerable to formula injection
description: historyExport.ts:50-52 — csvField() quotes fields per RFC 4180 but does not prefix-escape fields starting with =,+,-,@,|,tab. The Label field is user-controlled. In multi-user mode, one users labels could inject formulas that execute when another user opens CSV in Excel/Sheets. Fix: prepend single-quote to any field value whose first character is in [=+-@|tab].
close_reason: Fixed at CSV-writing seam (neutralizeFormula in historyExport.ts) + tests; see comment for evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Duplicate cairn-b1rg (filed 2026-07-08T01:48:47Z, ~7 min after this one) closed as a dup of this issue per the 2026-07-12 beads audit. Same defect: historyExport.ts:50-52 CSV formula injection via user-controlled Label field.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: FIXED. Root cause: historyExport.ts csvField() did RFC-4180 quoting only, no formula-injection guard on the user-controlled Label field. Added neutralizeFormula() at the CSV-writing seam (inside csvField, so all columns/callers covered): a cell whose first char is = + - @ | tab CR or LF is prefixed with a single quote, EXCEPT when the whole cell parses as a finite number (negative Amount cells like -0.00030200 stay numeric, not corrupted). Tests: historyExport.test.ts +2 (6 payloads incl. +cmd|DDE all neutralized; negative numeric amounts preserved). 10/10 pass.

title: Core RPC test-connection leaks SvelteKit internal error for invalid/relative URL
description:
Admin > Settings > Bitcoin Core RPC > Test connection. If the RPC URL field contains a relative/invalid value (e.g. 'not-a-url'), the failure badge shows a developer-facing SvelteKit framework error instead of a user validation message.

EXACT COPY OBSERVED (input url='not-a-url', user='u', pass='p'):
"Couldn't connect to Bitcoin Core: Core RPC getblockchaininfo request failed: Cannot use relative URL (not-a-url) with global fetch — use `event.fetch` instead: https://svelte.dev/docs/kit/web-standards#fetch-apis. Check the RPC URL, username/password, and that Core is running and reachable from this server."

Root cause: testCoreRpc()/CoreRpcClient hands the raw string to global fetch() with no URL validation; a relative URL throws SvelteKit's 'use event.fetch' error, which fetchErrorDetail() faithfully surfaces to the admin. The ftp://x variant is less bad ('fetch failed: unknown scheme') but still unvalidated.

Neither case crashes the page (good), but 'not-a-url' exposes SvelteKit internals + a docs link that means nothing to an operator.

FIX: validate the RPC URL before testing (must be a valid absolute http(s):// URL) and, on failure, show a plain message like 'That doesn't look like a valid URL. Enter something like http://127.0.0.1:8332'. Repro: paste 'not-a-url' into RPC URL, click Test connection.

Found during Core-RPC/post-Esplora live verification (branch single-sig-full-wallet @ d632f64). READ-ONLY QA agent — not fixing, filing per house rule.
close_reason: coreRpcUrlError() (chain/index.ts) validates absolute http(s) URL before any fetch; testCoreRpc returns plain 'That doesn't look like a valid URL — enter something like http://192.168.1.10:8332' for not-a-url/ftp:// (chain/index.ts:testCoreRpc), never SvelteKit's event.fetch error. Same guard on admin save path (admin/settings/+page.server.ts:174). Tests: chain.test.ts testCoreRpc mf9i (2) + coreRpcUrlError (3). Full suite green.

title: [pool-ux] Odds panel wastes probPerDayPct and offers a single horizon - no daily lottery-ticket framing
description: Evidence: getUserMiningView computes odds.probPerDayPct (readModels.ts:211) but MiningOddsPanel.svelte only renders expectedYearsPerBlock in one sentence; the per-day probability is dead data. ckpool shows block odds at 1-day/1-week/1-month/1-year horizons and that multi-horizon lottery framing (a 1-in-N ticket every single day) is what miners quote in the lucky-block stories that drive solo mining. The honest-odds doctrine (docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md, MANUAL.md) is satisfied - this is about making honesty vivid, not hype. Proposed fix (plain language): extend the odds card with per-day/per-year chance lines derived from the numbers already computed (e.g. every day your miner runs is roughly a 1 in 2.4 million ticket), keeping the existing muted, no-hype styling.

title: No RBF fee-bump flow for multisig transactions (single-key wallets have it, multisig doesn't)
description: src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/bump/+server.ts + bumpTransaction() in transactions.ts:555 implement a careful BIP-125-compliant fee bump for single-key wallets (checks RBF signaling, confirmed-status, min relay fee, exact-input replacement). There is no equivalent route under src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/ and no bumpMultisigTransaction in multisigTransactions.ts. This is despite constructMultisigPsbt setting RBF_SEQUENCE on every multisig input (multisigPsbt.ts:396) -- so multisig transactions ARE signaled as replaceable, they just have no UI/API path to actually bump. A stuck-fee multisig transaction has no recourse in Cairn except CPFP or waiting it out. This is a meaningful gap for a wallet marketed on M-of-N custody, since 'my transaction is stuck' is one of the most common support scenarios, and multisig transactions (larger, costlier per-byte) are more fee-sensitive than single-sig ones.
close_reason: Added multisig RBF fee-bump to parity with single-sig. constructMultisigPsbt gained an exactInputs mode (spend the original's exact input set, fee increase out of change, dust-guarded). bumpMultisigTransaction recovers inputs from the stored PSBT, reproduces recipients/amounts at a higher rate over the same inputs, and saves a replaces_txid draft that re-enters the roster sign/broadcast flow; enforces BIP-125 (all inputs signal RBF, new rate>old, replacement pays >= origFee+vsize, one live replacement per original), owner-only, marks the original 'superseded' on broadcast. New guarded db migration adds multisig_transactions.replaces_txid + 'superseded' status. New POST /api/wallets/multisig/:id/transactions/:txId/bump gated by fee_bumping. Tests: 143 pass across the affected suites incl. exact-input reconstruction, fee-floor/change-absorption guards, not-broadcast/duplicate refusals, supersede-on-broadcast. Follow-on: a Bump button in the multisig tx UI (the API/service/builder capability lands here).

title: Multisig create wizard can 500 with 'Cannot read properties of null (reading id)' when locals.user is unexpectedly null
description: src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts:25 does 'return { hasMultisigs: listMultisigs(locals.user!.id).length > 0 }' trusting the non-null assertion on locals.user, on the assumption that the parent (app)/+layout.server.ts guard always redirects unauthenticated/unauthenticated requests before this load runs. Observed in practice (server log, errorId 3f88cb7b and d425b05a): GET /wallets/multisig/new threw 'TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading id)' at this line with status 500, rendering the generic error page, then a retry of the same URL succeeded with 200. This happened around the same time as a concurrent agreement-version bump forcing re-acceptance, suggesting a race where the child route's load ran with a stale/null locals.user before the parent layout's redirect took effect (possibly via SvelteKit's parent-load-reuse-on-client-navigation optimization). Low severity/hard to reproduce on demand, but worth hardening: the load function should check locals.user directly and redirect(302, '/login') rather than relying on the parent guard's assumed ordering, matching how other server routes in this codebase (e.g. multisig/new actions) handle it.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: the multisig-new load now redirects to /login when locals.user is null rather than dereferencing it, eliminating the race-induced 500 (wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts).

title: Stratum pool host in connection card is derived from request Host header, not admin-configurable
description:
Epic: cairn-vn43 (mining pool). Found during QA wave 3 visual pass.

src/lib/components/mining/MiningConnectionCard.svelte:30:
    const host = $derived(page.url.hostname);
line 56: <CopyText value={`stratum+tcp://${host}:${stratumPort}`} mono />

The pool address shown to users (and copy-pasted into miner configs) is
built from whatever hostname the browser used to load the page
(page.url.hostname, which mirrors the request's Host header) -- there is no
admin setting to override it. This works fine for the common case (single
hostname/IP, direct access) but breaks down for:
  - reverse-proxy / multi-hostname deployments where the externally reachable
    Stratum host differs from the app's HTTP hostname (e.g. app reachable at
    a friendly domain over HTTPS, but Stratum TCP only reachable at a raw LAN
    IP or a different port-forwarded hostname).
  - Tailscale/VPN setups where the admin wants to advertise a stable
    tailnet hostname regardless of which address a given browser session used
    to reach the web UI.

Suggested fix: add an optional admin-configurable "public Stratum host"
setting (src/lib/server/mining/settings.ts, alongside mining_bind /
mining_stratum_port) that overrides page.url.hostname when set, falling back
to the current request-Host behavior when unset.

Not a security issue -- purely a usability gap for non-trivial network
topologies.

title: addressWatcher TOCTOU: wallet deleted mid-event still writes notified_txids + fires 404 notification
description: Found by independent review of acc0b69 (cairn-uzgu/gakd Phase 1). handleScripthashChange checks walletStillExists() ONCE near the top, then proceeds through several await points (baselineScripthash, chain.electrum.getHistory, spvVerifyConfirmed [2 round-trips], chain.getTx) before recordTxid/notify. A synchronous deleteWallet/deleteMultisig — or refreshWatches' periodic prune — can land BETWEEN those awaits for a handler that already passed its top-of-function existence check; the in-flight handler holds its own w closure and still writes a notified_txids row for the now-deleted wallet and fires a notification whose link 404s. Same in the on-demand baseline path (inserts confirmed=1 rows for a since-deleted wallet, quieter). This is the exact original uzgu symptom in a much narrower window — NOT a double-notify (byScripthash+inFlight are cleared together so new events short-circuit at 'if (!w) return'), just one stale write/notify escaping the guard. Fix (per reviewer): re-check state.byScripthash.has(scripthash) (cheap, in-memory, catches ALL removal paths — unwatch, prune, notify-guard) immediately before recordTxid/notify in the per-txid loop, and guard the baseline write too; bail if the entry is gone. Add a test simulating delete-during-await.
close_reason: Landed in db5ec6c. Added an in-memory state.byScripthash.has(scripthash) recheck immediately before each write on both paths — handleScripthashChange's per-txid loop (after getTx, before recordTxid/notify) and baselineScripthash (after getHistory, before the confirmed=1 inserts) — with no await between the check and the write, so a synchronous delete/prune landing in an earlier await can no longer produce a stale write/notify. Every removal path clears byScripthash synchronously, so one check covers all. Verified load-bearing (neutralizing the rechecks fails both new tests); addressWatcher 18/18, full suite 1874 pass; svelte-check 0/0.

title: [pool-ux] Community solo-wins feed card on /mining (curated real ckpool/Bitaxe block finds, updated per release)
description: From docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-NOTES.md proposal #2 (S effort, high impact). A static, curated per-release list of real-world solo block finds (ckpool 256TH wins, Bitaxe finds) rendered as a small feed card on /mining. Supplies the small-frequent-reinforcement layer ('someone has to win' is TRUE and verifiable on-chain) without manufacturing anything; doubles as winner-story content channel for the press kit (cairn-7msai). No network calls — ships as data in the release, consistent with no-third-party-API doctrine.

title: POST /api/wallets/multisig accepts a fingerprint that doesn't match the xpub (wizard path derives/validates it, API path doesn't)
description:
Repro (EDGE2 QA wave, http://qa-edge2.localhost:5388, user msig-owner):

POST /api/wallets/multisig with keys=[
  {xpub: TV1_MASTER (BIP32 spec test vector, true master fingerprint 3442193e
   per src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisig.test.ts comment), fingerprint: "ffffffff", ...},
  {xpub: TV2_MASTER, fingerprint: "deadbeef", ...}
]
-> HTTP 201, wallet created (id=1, "EDGE2-conflicting-fp") with key 1's stored
fingerprint = "ffffffff", i.e. a value the caller made up that does not match
the xpub's real derivable master fingerprint.

Compare to the wizard's `key` form action
(src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts normalizeKey(),
~line 108-136): it parses the pasted key via parseDescriptor and only falls
back to the client-supplied fingerprint field when the *parsed* fingerprint
is the "unknown" placeholder '00000000':
  `if (fingerprint === '00000000' && fp) fingerprint = fp;`
So the wizard path prefers the cryptographically-derivable fingerprint over
whatever the client claims, when one is derivable. The direct JSON API
(src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/+server.ts POST) has no equivalent check --
it takes body.keys[].fingerprint completely at face value.

Impact: P3 -- not exploitable for fund loss (fingerprint is provenance/display
metadata used to label which signer a key belongs to and to match PSBT
BIP32 derivation entries during signing), but it is a data-integrity gap:
a scripted/API caller can create a multisig whose recorded fingerprint
contradicts its xpub, which could confuse signer identification later
(attachMultisigSignature / the signing stepper's "which key signed" logic)
in ways the wizard already guards against. Worth aligning the two paths --
either have the API route call the same descriptor-derived-fingerprint
preference as normalizeKey, or explicitly document that the API trusts the
caller for fingerprint and the UI doesn't.
close_reason: fixed in 169855b (msig/qa-fixes-2026-07-12), merged to single-sig-full-wallet; readJson non-object guard / atomic duplicate-name 409 / shared keyInput normalizer

title: Live updates via WebSocket/SSE for dashboard/explorer

Should reuse the existing Electrum headers-subscribe mechanism (src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts already supports blockchain.headers.subscribe and emits a 'header' event) as the source of truth rather than polling. v1.1 polish, not a v1 blocker.

title: PUT /api/admin/settings has no validation, unlike the equivalent admin settings form action - can silently misconfigure registration mode / node connection
description: src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts PUT handler loops over KEY_MAP and calls setSetting(dbKey, String(body[key])) for whatever JSON the caller sends, with zero validation. Compare the admin settings form action (src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts save), which whitelists registrationMode to open/invite/closed, connectionMode to public/custom, and validates electrumPort as an integer 1-65535. The JSON API has none of that: posting a typo'd or garbage registrationMode is accepted verbatim. Traced through src/routes/(auth)/signup/+page.server.ts: needsInvite = not firstUser and mode is invite, closed = not firstUser and mode is closed - a garbage registrationMode value matches neither check, so the instance silently falls through to fully OPEN registration with no invite requirement and no error surfaced anywhere. electrumPort similarly accepts non-numeric strings that become NaN via parseInt() in getInstanceSettings(), breaking the configured node connection. This route is live and admin-session-authenticated (requireAdmin) even though the current UI never calls it (grepped src/ - zero fetch('/api/admin/settings') call sites) - reachable today by anyone scripting against the API with an admin cookie. Fix: apply the same enum/range validation as the form action, ideally via one shared validation function.
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Polymorphic (wallet_kind,wallet_id) FK integrity — add wallet_entities mapping + user_version migrations + collision audit
description: Strategic follow-up to the balance_snapshots orphan bug. Six child tables reference wallets/multisigs via (wallet_kind,wallet_id) with no FK; wallets.id and multisigs.id are disjoint AUTOINCREMENT spaces BOTH starting at 1, so wallet_id=5 exists in both — cross-kind collision is LIVE not latent: any query filtering wallet_id without a wallet_kind companion silently returns cross-kind rows (user_id scoping doesn't save it — one user owns both kinds). Fable's recommendation (option b, decisively — rejects status-quo-patching which failed 4x, and rejects full parent-merge as highest-risk): create wallet_entities(id INTEGER PK, kind TEXT CHECK(kind IN('wallet','multisig')), ref_id INTEGER, UNIQUE(kind,ref_id)) maintained by AFTER INSERT/DELETE triggers on both parents; children then get a composite FK (wallet_kind,wallet_id) REFERENCES wallet_entities(kind,ref_id) ON DELETE CASCADE with NO new column and NO data backfill (works because the parent cols are UNIQUE). Each child needs a 12-step table rebuild (foreign_keys=OFF outside txn, rebuild, foreign_key_check, VACUUM INTO backup first). Also: adopt PRAGMA user_version + ordered migrations NOW (set atomically inside the DDL txn) — the current probe-PRAGMA-table_info-and-patch approach cannot order the rebuild this needs and is the mindset that produced the orphan bug. AUDIT TASK: grep every 'wallet_id = ?' predicate lacking a wallet_kind companion. wallet_entities is NOT a dead end — it is the shared header table a future parent-merge would grow into.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: SPLIT (Fable roadmap + red-team): the cheap/immediate half of this bead has moved to cairn-97ui as TRIGGERS + introspective test (ships v0.2.2 with the orphan fix). What REMAINS here is ONLY the structural end-state, deferred until after the test-coverage batch and bundled with the next schema-touching change: wallet_entities(id,kind,ref_id,UNIQUE) mapping table + composite FK (wallet_kind,wallet_id) REFERENCES wallet_entities(kind,ref_id) ON DELETE CASCADE from every child (no new column/backfill) + PRAGMA user_version ordered migrations + the 'wallet_id=? lacking wallet_kind' grep audit. The 97ui triggers are ~20 lines that die naturally inside this migration — cheap insurance, not wasted work. Do NOT fast-track this many-table migration on a live funds DB while security P0s (jnlx/fame) are open.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
AUDIT DONE (the bead's stated audit task), no live bug found. Grepped every wallet_id predicate across the codebase (addressLabels.ts, backups.ts, backupHealth.ts, portfolio.ts, addressWatcher.ts, walletSync.ts, dataRetention.ts) that touches any of the SIX (not five -- wallet_snapshots was added after the doc was last updated, MANUAL.md corrected) polymorphic (wallet_kind,wallet_id) tables: every call site already pairs wallet_kind with wallet_id. transactions/tx_labels looked similar but are NOT polymorphic (real wallet_id FK to wallets(id), single-sig-only; multisig has its own multisig_transactions.multisig_id) so their unpaired wallet_id is correct, not a gap.

Added src/lib/server/walletMultisigIdCollision.test.ts (commit 2e5dab5): seeds a wallet and a multisig at the SAME numeric id (collision forced, not incidental) and proves (a) service-layer reads stay kind-scoped (address labels/backup status/balance snapshots) and (b) a generic introspective sweep across every discovered (wallet_kind,wallet_id) table shows deleting one kind's parent never touches the other kind's row. Full vitest run src/lib/server: 2208 passed (6 pre-existing failures in an unrelated featureFlags/audit.test.ts owned by a concurrent session, untouched by this work).

NOT DONE / still open: the structural end-state itself (wallet_entities(kind,ref_id) mapping table + composite FK from all 6 children + PRAGMA user_version ordered migrations, replacing the trigger-sweep). Deliberately NOT attempted this pass -- this is a large multi-table SQLite rebuild (12 steps/table per the bead) on a live-funds DB, and the bead's own prior comment says bundle it with the next schema-touching change rather than fast-track it standalone; nothing in this session's scope constituted that trigger. Leaving OPEN for that structural work specifically.

title: Contacts anti-enumeration is incomplete: timing side-channel + no rate limiting on POST /api/contacts
description:
src/lib/server/contacts.ts's requestContact is explicitly documented (file header, JSDoc, db.ts schema comment) as anti-enumeration: an unknown-email request and a known-email request both return an identical 200 {ok:true}. However this only covers response SHAPE, not two other channels:
1. Timing: an unknown email hits 1 read-only query then returns immediately; a known email runs a reciprocal SELECT + either an UPDATE or a SELECT+INSERT — up to 4 sequential SQLite operations including a write. An attacker with any authenticated account (registration can be 'open' mode per RegistrationMode in src/lib/types.ts, so this doesn't require special privilege) can statistically distinguish account-exists vs not by averaging response latency over repeated requests against a wordlist.
2. No rate limiting: src/lib/server/rateLimit.ts already exists and is wired into login and invite-redemption specifically to stop enumeration ('Invalid invite codes per IP — stops invite-code enumeration'), but POST /api/contacts has no rate limit at all, making bulk wordlist-based enumeration practical regardless of the timing issue.
Fix: add a rateLimit.ts bucket for POST /api/contacts (per-user and/or per-IP), and consider normalizing the unknown-email path to perform an equivalent-cost dummy read/write (or add a constant artificial delay) so timing no longer correlates with account existence.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: POST /api/contacts now rate-limited per-user (20) and per-IP (60) per 15-min window counting every request (rateLimit.ts + route), and the unknown-email path does equivalent-cost dummy reads so latency no longer correlates with account existence (contacts.ts). Rate limiting is the primary enumeration defense.

title: Match Caravan's HW-connect posture: Trezor popup + BitBox02 bridge work over plain HTTP
description: Alex's finding: Caravan on the same Umbrel (plain HTTP, same store) connects hardware wallets fine. Investigated Caravan source (AlexM223/caravan#umbrel + upstream caravan-wallets): (1) Trezor — Caravan runs the TrezorConnect popup with NO secure-context gate; the popup at connect.trezor.io holds the transport (WebUSB/Bridge) in its own secure context, so the host page scheme doesn't matter. Cairn's isTrezorConnectAvailable() wrongly requires window.isSecureContext — the cairn-4b2b 'popup needs secure context' claim was circular (it was Cairn's own gate reporting unavailable). (2) BitBox02 — Caravan calls bitbox02ConnectAuto (WebHID, falling back to BitBoxBridge on 127.0.0.1:8178); the bridge prompts the user to approve unknown origins (AllowedHosts confirm dialog), so http://umbrel.local works after one native confirmation. Cairn deliberately restricted to bitbox02ConnectWebHID. (3) Ledger — Caravan uses plain WebUSB, equally unavailable over HTTP; nothing to copy, file/paste stays the Ledger fallback. Fix: drop the isSecureContext gate in trezor.ts, switch bitbox02.ts to bitbox02ConnectAuto with bridge-aware availability + error guidance, update wizard/signer copy.
close_reason: Implemented Caravan parity. trezor.ts: isTrezorConnectAvailable no longer requires window.isSecureContext (the connect.trezor.io popup is its own secure context and holds the transport — verified no gate exists in @trezor/connect-web itself); bitbox02.ts: connectAndPair now uses bitbox02ConnectAuto (WebHID → BitBoxBridge fallback), new isBitbox02Available() browser probe, bridge-aware error copy when a bridge-only browser can't connect; UI copy updated (TrezorSigner/BitboxSigner/signMethods/deviceRead x2). Ledger unchanged — Caravan uses plain WebUSB there and is equally unavailable over HTTP, so file/paste remains the Ledger fallback. All 1604 tests pass incl. new isBitbox02Available + bridge-error tests; svelte-check clean; wizard smoke-tested in preview. Needs a real-device retest on the Umbrel instance.

title: TTL-cache the remaining uncached Dashboard/Explorer chain-data calls (recent blocks, mempool summary, hashrate, difficulty, mempool blocks)
description:
## Reported symptom

"Pages reload on every visit — Dashboard, Explorer, and Wallets all reload from scratch every time. There's no client-side caching."

## What's actually true

SvelteKit re-runs `load()` on every navigation by design — there's no page-level "keep alive"/component-cache primitive in SvelteKit (unlike e.g. Vue's `<keep-alive>`), so "stop reloading on revisit" isn't achievable by keeping pages alive. The two available levers are (a) request-level result caching server-side, and (b) SvelteKit's built-in preload-on-hover, both of which are already partly in place:

- `data-sveltekit-preload-data="hover"` is set globally on `<body>` in `src/app.html`, so any nav link the user hovers before clicking has its data fetch already in flight/done by click time — this already hides most of the latency for mouse-driven nav (not touch/mobile, and not back/forward/direct navigation).
- The genuinely expensive paths are already TTL-cached: `getFeeEstimates` (30s) and `getTip` (10min + header-event invalidation) in `src/lib/server/chain/cache.ts`; wallet/multisig gap-limit scans via `ScanCache` (60s TTL, `gapLimitScanner.ts`). A repeat Wallets visit inside 60s, or repeat fee/tip reads, are already served warm.

## What's NOT cached (the actual gap)

These have zero request-level memoization and pay a fresh esplora HTTP round-trip on every single Dashboard/Explorer visit:
- `getRecentBlocks` (esplora HTTP loop)
- `getMempoolSummary`
- `getHashrate`
- `getDifficultyInfo` (2-4 esplora calls, explorer/difficulty page)
- `getMempoolBlocks`

None of these are per-user or fast-changing enough to need live-freshness on every navigation (blocks arrive every ~10min, mempool summary/hashrate/difficulty change slowly). All streamed already (not blocking first paint), so this isn't urgent, but adding the same TTL-cache pattern from `chain/cache.ts` to these would cut repeat-visit cost, especially for back-navigation, direct URL visits, and mobile (no hover-preload).

## Scope

Add TTL caching (a reasonable default: 30-60s, matching the existing fee-estimate cache) to the five calls above, following the pattern already established in `src/lib/server/chain/cache.ts`. Low priority — none of this blocks first paint today, it's a "make the warm path even warmer" cleanup, not a fix for a broken/blocking experience. The genuinely blocking-feeling issue (rapid nav stutter) is tracked separately at cairn-xlrm.
close_reason: Superseded by cairn-2zxt.8.2 — persisted chain_snapshot + refresh-on-invalidate replaces the proposed TTL-cache-the-remaining-calls approach with a strictly better cache-first pattern that also survives cold start.

title: [pool-ux] No public or shareable pool stats page - the pools proof-of-life cannot be shown to anyone outside the instance
description: Evidence: every mining surface is behind login (src/routes has only (app)/mining and (app)/admin/mining; public routes are auth/agreement/terms/disclosure only). There is no logged-out pool page showing this instance exists, its pool hashrate, blocks found, or the no-fee full-coinbase promise - so an operator recruiting friends/family/community hash has literally no URL to send. Competitor research: Ocean, public-pool and ckpool all offer no-login stats URLs (Ocean: ocean.xyz/stats/<address>; ckpool: stats.ckpool.org/users/<address>) and reviewers cite that open watchability as a trust feature; Heartwoods sovereignty story (your own node builds the block, you keep the whole reward) is stronger than public-pools but is currently invisible pre-login. Proposed fix (plain language): an operator-controlled (default off) public pool page: pool hashrate, miners online, blocks found ledger (anonymized), the honest solo-odds explainer, and how to join this pool copy - a recruitment landing page, one privacy review required. Related but distinct from the logged-in pool view bead (cairn-et38g).

comments.author: pool-ux-audit
comments.text: Overlap note: cairn-19o5e [hash-strategy] (public read-only mining stats page, opt-in, shareable) is the same feature scoped strategically - treat 19o5e as canonical and this bead as the UX evidence (what competitors expose publicly, privacy toggle requirement). Coordinate; do not build twice.

title: Units preference (Settings) offers BTC/sats only; send screen also cycles to USD
description:
Browser QA finding (UX wave 2026-07-12, batch 2, item 5b): the Settings
"Units" preference lets a user choose BTC or sats as their preferred
denomination, but src/lib/components/send/AmountEntry.svelte's unit-cycle
button (cycleUnit/nextUnit) cycles through BTC -> sats -> USD (fiat) whenever
a price is available -- a third denomination the Settings page doesn't know
about or let the user pin/exclude. Inconsistent unit model between the two
surfaces. Filed for triage, no fix applied (beads-only, no code changes).
close_reason: AmountEntry (src/lib/components/send/AmountEntry.svelte) now reads the shared hw.unit preference (new src/lib/units.ts unitPref/setUnitPref store) for its default BTC/sats entry unit, and writes back to it when the user cycles to BTC or sats, keeping it in sync with Settings' own Units toggle (settings/+page.svelte updated to read/write the same store instead of its own local hw.unit copy). Fiat now only enters the unit-cycle when fiatPrimaryPref (lib/price, Settings Display's fiat-primary toggle) is on -- extracted the cycle-order logic into a pure, unit-tested nextUnit() in amountInput.ts (4 new tests in amountInput.test.ts, all passing). Verified live in browser (devb.localhost:5194, real wallet, real send page) across all 4 combinations of hw.unit and fiatPrimary: sats plus fiat-off gives sats hero and BTC-only cycle; sats plus fiat-on gives sats hero (explicit sats choice preserved) with USD joining the cycle; default-btc plus fiat-on gives fiat-primary hero with BTC in cycle; default-btc plus fiat-off gives BTC hero and sats-only cycle -- confirmed again at 375x812 mobile viewport. npm run check: 0 errors/warnings. Full vitest suite: 4031 passed (1 pre-existing unrelated e2e failure, not caused by this change).

title: No address-level labels/tags (only per-transaction labels exist)
description:
Found during a privacy-tools parity audit against Bastion (2026-07-06). Bastion has BOTH address-level tags and transaction-level tags (src/server/routes/tags.ts, address_tags + tx_tags tables). Cairn only has transaction labels (src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/labels/+server.ts, tx_labels table per src/lib/server/db.ts) — there is no way to label an individual address (e.g. 'exchange deposit address', 'donation address') independent of any specific transaction.

Impact: moderate. Users doing coin-control or reviewing address reuse/history have no way to annotate WHY a given address exists, only individual transactions that touched it. Lower severity than the sharing/Tor gaps, but a real UX gap for anyone actively managing privacy/labeling hygiene across many addresses.

Recommended fix: add an address_labels table (wallet_id or multisig_id, address, label) mirroring the existing tx_labels pattern, and surface it on the address-detail view (src/routes/(app)/wallets/.../address-detail equivalents).
close_reason: Built: address_labels table + addressLabels.ts (get/set/delete, wallet_kind for single-sig+multisig); GET/PUT address-labels endpoints (ownership-gated); inline-editable Label column in both wallet + vault Addresses tabs; cleanup on delete. 6 tests, svelte-check 0 errors.

Cheap hedge: run an SRI translation proxy (SV2 downstream -> SV1 upstream) against Heartwood's stratum ports on regtest, fix anything that breaks, and document the supported topology in MANUAL. Native SV2 remains epic-sized and is NOT this bead.

title: Paginate address transaction history past 50 txs
description: src/routes/(app)/explorer/address/[address]/+page.svelte and the underlying chain.getAddressTxs (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts) already support paging via afterTxid, but the address detail page only fetches/renders the first page (up to 50 txs from esplora's /address/{addr}/txs) with no 'load more' control. Addresses with more than 50 transactions are truncated with no way to see older history. Wire up a 'load more' button using the existing afterTxid cursor. v1.1 polish, not a v1 blocker.

Live-verified end to end: 176-tx address walked in pages of 50/25/25/25/25/25/1 with zero overlap, firstSeen appeared at full history, counter tracked loads, and navigating to a 5,562-tx address reset to a fresh first page

title: Recovery code burned before passkey capability check on Umbrel
description: recover/+page.svelte:52-136 — verify step burns a single-use recovery code (recovery.ts:194 sets used_at immediately) before checking whether the browser can complete passkey registration. On non-secure context (Cairn's default plain-HTTP Umbrel deployment), the code is spent and the flow dead-ends at the passkey step. Repeated attempts can exhaust all 8 recovery codes without ever recovering the account. Fix: gate verify screen on real passkey capability up front, or defer code consumption until registration completes.
close_reason: FIXED in c753fc2 (Fable-designed, Opus-reviewed). Kept burn-at-verify (deferring consumption would break the atomic single-use grant); added POST /api/auth/recover/password as a second recovery-completion path that validates password length BEFORE consumeRecoveryGrant (weak pw 400s without burning the grant), then setUserPassword + real session + warn-level security_password_changed 'was this you?' alert (stolen-secret parity). recover/+page.svelte gates the passkey button on browserSupportsWebAuthn() && isSecureContext so plain-HTTP Umbrel always gets the password form and can't dead-end. Both paths share consumeRecoveryGrant's atomic single-use guard. Tests: validate-before-burn, race, capability gating.

title: Signup invite-code error hint text regression: dynamic guidance dropped from register route (found auditing cairn-keo)
description: cairn-keo (closed via commit 6373511) added dynamic guidance to invite_required/bad_invite signup errors: 'Invites come from whoever runs this Cairn instance — ask them for a code.' appended at error time. While auditing cairn-keo for missing-regression-test coverage on 2026-07-06, found this dynamic append is now GONE from current code: src/routes/api/auth/register/password/+server.ts and src/lib/server/auth.ts throw raw AuthError messages with no hint suffix. Only a static hint span in signup/+page.svelte (lines ~119-121, always visible regardless of error state) survives — it appears to have been dropped when the passkey refactor (commit 119d5e4) replaced the old form-action flow with a client-side fetch call. Restore the dynamic hint-on-error behavior (or confirm the static hint fully substitutes for it and close as intentional), and add a regression test this time so it can't silently disappear a third time.
close_reason: Dynamic ask-your-admin hint restored on invite_required/bad_invite in the register password route; regression test asserts hint presence and absence on non-invite errors. Commit d353f7a.

title: [wallet-pool] No mining-income accounting: no ledger kind, no CSV tag, no fiat-at-receipt, exports omit mining tables
description: Activity has no mining-income row kind (only the block-found event); historyExport.ts Type column is Received/Sent only (verified: 0 mining/coinbase matches); no price-at-time is stored anywhere so cost basis is impossible; account data export omits mining_blocks/mining_prefs. Fix: 'Mining reward' type in history CSV, record spot fiat on payout receipt, include mining tables in account export.

title: Hostile-input sweep: unicode/RTL/emoji, XSS payloads, SQLi strings across user-writable fields
description: No test feeds hostile strings into wallet names, labels, contacts, invite fields, announcement text, or export paths. Notes from code inspection: zero {@html} in src so Svelte auto-escaping likely holds (unproven), and node:sqlite prepared statements are presumed (unproven). One matrix test asserting round-trip integrity and no markup/SQL interpretation. Complements — does not duplicate — cairn-b1rg/cairn-mf68, which cover only CSV formula injection in historyExport.ts.
close_reason: Hostile-input sweep covered by hostileText.test.ts (85 tests, test/qa-wave-2026-07-12), merged in 7bda1d1.

comments.author: qa-fable
comments.text: QA hostile-input sweep 2026-07-12 (isolated copy: qa-sweeps.db, port 5308). PAYLOAD SET (combined into single fields): , , SQLi '; DROP TABLE announcements;-- , RTL override U+202E, emoji, CJK (你好), accented (café), and a javascript: URI in link fields. SURFACE 1 — Admin announcement (Title + Message; renders as an instance-wide banner to every user, plus the admin list). RESULT: CLEAN. - Rendering: payload shown as literal escaped text in the live preview, the persisted admin list, AND the real top-of-page banner across pages. No /"> + emoji. RESULT: CLEAN. /terms renders it as literal escaped text; no execution; exact round-trip in settings.user_agreement_operator. CONCLUSION: cairn-nohi's two open questions are answered for these surfaces — (1) Svelte auto-escaping DOES hold (confirmed live, consistent with "zero {@html} in src"), and (2) node:sqlite prepared statements ARE parameterized (DROP TABLE proven inert). Two distinct render paths tested (authenticated banner/list + unauthenticated public page). No XSS or SQLi found. COVERAGE NOTE: The QA account had 0 wallets, so wallet-name/tx-label/address-label/recipient fields and settings/contacts were not exercised this pass. They share the same Svelte-escaping + node:sqlite stack, but a regression test should still assert round-trip integrity on at least one wallet-scoped field and one contacts field to fully close this bead. Recommend keeping cairn-nohi open until those are covered. All test data cleaned up (announcement deleted, operator name restored).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Hostile-input sweep DONE: 85 tests in hostileText.test.ts (test/qa-wave-2026-07-12). SQLi inert (parameterized everywhere), XSS stored as literal text, unicode/RTL/zalgo round-trip clean. 4 new beads filed for the real defects found (NUL truncation, displayName unbounded, surrogate mangle, UTF-16 cap). Candidate for closure once branch merges.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Merge commit 7bda1d1 (test/qa-wave-2026-07-12 into single-sig-full-wallet) confirmed: hostileText.test.ts (85 tests, unicode/RTL/emoji/XSS/SQLi sweep) present and passing in the merged suite (verified via npm test: 2662/2670 tests pass). Closing per the merge-candidate note.

title: Wallet detail page: fiat missing on hero + tx rows (root cause inconclusive)
description:
Browser QA found /wallets/[id] (single-sig wallet detail) rendering BTC/sats-only
everywhere (hero balance + all tx rows via Amount size=hero/row/inline), while the
SAME shared Amount component + $lib/price $btcUsd store render fiat correctly on:
Home activity feed (RecentActivity.svelte), Wallets list (+page.svelte), and Home
hero (via its own separate opt-in price fetch, unrelated mechanism).

30-min investigation (time-boxed per brief), static code review only, no live
repro run:
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte: grepped every Amount call site
  (lines ~525,531,640,803,842,954,1066,1150,1156) and the full file for any
  "price" prop override -- NONE found. No call site passes price/null; all rely
  on the shared $btcUsd store exactly like the working call sites.
- src/lib/components/Amount.svelte: effectivePrice = price===undefined ? $btcUsd
  : price -- confirmed no route-specific branching, single shared implementation.
- src/lib/styles/wallet-detail.css and the page's own <style> block: grepped for
  hw-amount/.line/display:none/visibility -- no matching hide rules.
- src/hooks.server.ts CSP: connect-src 'self' is set site-wide, not scoped
  per-route -- rules out a CSP block on /api/price specific to this route.
- src/lib/liveBlocks.ts (SSE new-block subscription mounted alongside the price
  store on this page): reviewed for a synchronous throw that could abort
  hydration -- none found, all fetch/EventSource paths are wrapped in try/catch.

Leading (unconfirmed) hypothesis: this route's onMount fires BOTH the
$btcUsd store's first tick() (fetch /api/price) AND a refresh() POST to
/api/wallets/:id/refresh at the same time. Per the already-documented
cairn-xlrm finding (sync node:sqlite DatabaseSync blocks Node's single-threaded
event loop), if refresh() does heavy synchronous DB work, it could delay the
event loop long enough that /api/price's response processing lags a few
seconds behind lighter-weight pages (Home/list), which would read as "fiat
never appears" if QA's screenshot was taken shortly after navigation rather
than after the store's ~1s+ round trip. This is theory, not a confirmed repro
-- no live server was hit against a seeded wallet within the 30-min box.

Did NOT find any code path in this file, Amount.svelte, or price.ts that
deliberately suppresses fiat on this route. If real, this is either (a) the
same event-loop-blocking class of bug as cairn-xlrm, now hitting the price
fetch too, or (b) a QA automation timing artifact (screenshot taken before
the ~1s async round trip completes on a heavier page). Needs a live-server
repro (seeded wallet, real navigation, wait >2s, check Network tab for
/api/price timing) to distinguish. Leaving open per brief -- do not fix blind.
close_reason: Closed as non-reproducible/working-as-intended: live regtest repro with a genuinely funded wallet (0.35 BTC, 2 confirmed txs, live /api/price) shows wallet-detail hero AND tx rows render fiat correctly, matching Home (both via Amount.svelte's shared $btcUsd store; no route-specific gating exists). Historical reports = harness artifacts: (1) DOM read before async price fetch resolves, (2) host-scoped session cookie wiped by concurrent tab logout -> 401 on /api/price silently degrades to BTC-only. If wild reports recur, next lead = auth-hiccup handling around /api/price, not Amount wiring.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Live repro attempt 2026-07-13 post-consolidation on the qa-wave DB (snapshot mode, $0 balance): fiat headline RENDERS correctly on /wallets/1 ($0.00 above 0.00 BTC / 0 sats). The reported gap was not reproducible structurally at zero balance. Remaining untested path per the original brief's leading hypothesis: a NONZERO balance + live price fetch, timed against the event-loop-blocking refresh() call this bead's investigation flagged as the leading (unconfirmed) theory. Leaving open.

title: backup_nudges missing from wallet/multisig delete-cascade triggers — orphan rows on every wallet delete (shipped in v0.2.41)
description: backup_nudges (cairn-gt05.5, commit 3b41927, in v0.2.41) keys rows off (wallet_kind,wallet_id) but was never added to trg_wallets_delete_children / trg_multisigs_delete_children in db.ts, so deleting a wallet/multisig leaves orphaned nudge rows. Caught exactly as designed by deleteCascade.test.ts's introspective sweep (and walletMultisigIdCollision.test.ts) — 4 of the 9 red tests under cairn-df4e2. Fix: both trigger bodies now sweep backup_nudges (DROP+CREATE redeploys to existing DBs), plus a boot-time orphan purge placed AFTER the table's CREATE (the main purge loop runs before it on a fresh DB; trigger forward-reference is safe — SQLite resolves trigger bodies at fire time, verified).
close_reason: Fixed in db.ts: backup_nudges added to both trg_wallets_delete_children and trg_multisigs_delete_children (DROP+CREATE pattern redeploys to existing DBs) + dedicated boot orphan purge after the table's CREATE TABLE. deleteCascade.test.ts (3) + walletMultisigIdCollision.test.ts (1) green in isolation and in suite.

title: Access-control gaps in collaborative custody + admin visibility
description:
docs/DATA-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md sections 2/3/5/6/7: the multisig viewer role (meant to be
read-only, less-privileged than cosigner) can fetch full unbroadcast PSBTs via the transaction
detail/file/list routes because they gate on getViewableMultisig instead of a signable-tier
check; ledger_multisig_registrations is backwards (owner-only, blocking legitimate cosigners);
tx_labels/address_labels have no defense-in-depth ownership check; the multisig address-labels
route lets a viewer role write, not just read; /admin/activity's API returns every user's full
untruncated detail JSON by default; users.last_login exposes exact behavioral timestamps to
every admin; and raw email/IP are logged outside logger.ts's redact list at four call sites,
readable by any admin via /admin/logs. Children: C1-C7 below.
close_reason: All 7 children done: viewer PSBT gate, ledger-registration tier, label defense-in-depth, label write tier, admin activity detail opt-in, last_login bucketing, email/ip log redaction.

title: Multisig viewer role can fetch full unbroadcast PSBT (detail/file/list routes)
description:
src/lib/server/multisigTransactions.ts's getMultisigTransaction (lines 135-145) and the
transaction-list function (~line 150) both gate via the internal viewableMultisig wrapper
(lines 86-88, which calls getViewableMultisig, src/lib/server/wallets/multisig.ts:148 -- owner
OR any share role including 'viewer'), not the stricter getSignableMultisig (multisig.ts:168
-- owner OR role='cosigner'). This means a read-only 'viewer' share can fetch the full raw
PSBT (recipient addresses, amounts, BIP32 derivation) for in-flight, unbroadcast draft
transactions via:
- GET /api/wallets/multisig/[id]/transactions/[txId] (detail)
- GET /api/wallets/multisig/[id]/transactions/[txId]/file (raw .psbt download) --
  src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/transactions/[txId]/file/+server.ts
- GET /api/wallets/multisig/[id]/transactions (list) --
  src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/transactions/+server.ts

This is inconsistent with the app's own stated intent elsewhere -- key-path redaction and
descriptor withholding already applied to viewers via multisigShares.ts's
redactMultisigKeysForViewer -- which implies viewers should see LESS than cosigners, not the
same level of transaction detail. The wallet-overview page,
(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts:116-121, is unaffected: it already projects saved
transactions down to {id, txid, status, feeRate} correctly for viewers.

Fix: swap the gate on these three routes' underlying data functions from
viewableMultisig/getViewableMultisig to getSignableMultisig (owner-or-cosigner). If a
pure-viewer-can-see-drafts behavior turns out to be an intentional product decision, note that
as an open question in the PR rather than silently keeping the looser gate.

Acceptance criteria:
- A viewer-role share gets a 403/redacted response from the transaction detail, file-download,
  and list endpoints for draft/unbroadcast transactions.
- A cosigner-role share and the owner still get full PSBT data via all three routes.
- Add a test case to the existing 3-tier access-gate test suite (multisigAccess.test.ts or
  equivalent) asserting a viewer is denied on all three routes.
