close_reason: getMultisigTransaction/listMultisigTransactions now gate on getSignableMultisig; viewer overview list preserved via new PSBT-free listMultisigTransactionSummaries; detail route comment/gate aligned; viewer-denial test added to multisigAccess.test.ts. Commit 341fb5e.

title: Align ledger_multisig_registrations access tier with cosigner needs
description:
src/lib/server/multisigRegistrations.ts's ownsMultisig (lines 67-73) queries
`multisigs WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?` directly -- strictly owner-only, with no
multisig_shares check at all (unlike getViewableMultisig/getSignableMultisig). This is
backwards relative to the sibling PSBT-exposure finding (this epic's other bead): a cosigner
share genuinely NEEDS the Ledger policy-HMAC registration to co-sign on a Ledger device, but
currently cannot fetch it through this table's routes at all, while a viewer (who has no
business signing) currently CAN see full transaction PSBTs elsewhere in the app.

Fix: change ownsMultisig's gate (or add a sibling function) to allow owner OR cosigner-role
share, mirroring getSignableMultisig's logic in src/lib/server/wallets/multisig.ts:168. Do NOT
extend access to viewer-role shares -- registration data has no read-only use case.

Acceptance criteria:
- A cosigner-role share can fetch the Ledger registration for a shared multisig.
- A viewer-role share still cannot.
- Add a test case to multisigRegistrations' test file covering both roles.
close_reason: canSignMultisig (owner-or-cosigner) replaces ownsMultisig across get/list/save; viewer still uniform-404; cosigner+viewer roles test-pinned. Committed.

title: Add defense-in-depth ownership check to tx_labels/address_labels
description:
src/lib/server/db.ts's tx_labels (lines 92-98: wallet_id, txid, label, created_at) and
address_labels (lines 467-474: wallet_kind, wallet_id, address, label, created_at) have no
user_id column -- access is enforced entirely by callers checking wallet/multisig ownership
BEFORE calling into the labels functions, with no check inside the label functions
themselves. Not broken today (all current call sites gate correctly, confirmed by tracing
every read/write path), but a latent risk: any future route that forgets the ownership check
would leak labels wallet-wide with no second line of defense. Contrast with
src/lib/server/addressBook.ts's saved_addresses, which is already strictly scoped by
user_id at the query level.

Fix: inside the label read/write functions (addressLabels.ts / wherever tx_labels functions
live), add an assertion that re-verifies the caller-supplied wallet_kind/wallet_id belongs to
the requesting user (via the same ownership/share-role check the callers already perform),
even though it's currently redundant -- cheap insurance against a future regression.

Acceptance criteria:
- A unit test calls the label function directly with a wallet_id belonging to a different
  user and confirms it throws/denies, independent of any route-level gating.
close_reason: getAddressLabels/setAddressLabel now take userId and re-check access internally (LabelAccessError); cross-user + role-tier denial test-pinned; tx_labels already had the internal getWallet gate. Committed.

title: Restrict multisig address-labels write access to cosigner/owner
description:
The multisig address-labels route currently lets a 'viewer' share PUT (write) labels, not
just GET (read) them -- broader access than a role named "viewer" implies. Reading shared
annotations is explicitly intentional for viewers (see (app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/
+page.server.ts:78's "shared annotations for this vault" comment) -- this bead is about
WRITE access specifically, which was never explicitly designed, just inherited from using the
same gate for both GET and PUT.

This is a policy decision, not purely a bug -- confirm the intended behavior with product/Alex
before shipping (default assumption below is the more conservative reading: viewers read,
cosigner/owner write).

Fix: gate the PUT handler in the multisig address-labels route on getSignableMultisig (or an
explicit owner-or-cosigner check) instead of getViewableMultisig, while leaving GET on the
current viewer-inclusive gate.

Acceptance criteria:
- A viewer-role share gets 403 on PUT to the address-labels route but still 200 on GET.
- Cosigner-role share and owner are unaffected (PUT still works for them).
- Add a test case covering all three roles x both HTTP methods.
close_reason: PUT gated on getSignableMultisig (403 for viewer, 404 for outsider), GET unchanged; 3-role x 2-method route test added. Shipped the bead's conservative default (viewers read, cosigner/owner write). Committed.

title: Scope /admin/activity's unfiltered cross-user detail exposure
description:
src/lib/server/activity.ts's listAllActivity (lines 276-329) returns every user's event
stream -- including the full, untruncated `detail` JSON column (e.g. full txid/multisig id
for a broadcast) -- with no user_id filter applied unless the caller explicitly passes
filters.userId (lines 290-295). The rendered /admin/activity page happens to truncate the
displayed message, but GET /api/admin/activity hit directly returns the full unfiltered
payload to any admin -- more than the UI implies is available. On a multi-admin instance,
every admin (not just the operator) can pull every other user's detail-level activity via the
API even if they'd never click through the truncated UI.

Fix: truncate/redact the `detail` field server-side in listAllActivity by default, to match
what the admin UI actually displays, and require an explicit opt-in query param (e.g.
?includeDetail=true) for the full payload if there's a genuine support/debugging need for it.

Acceptance criteria:
- Default GET /api/admin/activity response matches what the admin UI displays (truncated
  message, no raw detail JSON) unless the new explicit param is passed.
- Add a test asserting the default response shape excludes/truncates detail, and that the
  opt-in param restores it.
close_reason: detail nulled by default in listAllActivity; ?includeDetail=true opt-in on the API; default-vs-opt-in shape test-pinned. Committed.

title: Reduce users.last_login granularity in admin user list
description:
src/lib/server/admin.ts's listUsers() (~lines 7-36) returns exact last_login timestamps to
every admin via /admin/users -- more granular behavioral tracking than a "who has an account"
admin page needs, especially since on a multi-admin instance every admin sees it, not just
the operator.

Fix: replace the exact timestamp in the admin users list response with a coarser bucket (e.g.
"active in last 30 days" / "inactive 30+ days" / "never logged in"), or gate the exact
timestamp behind a per-row expand action instead of showing it in the default table.

Acceptance criteria:
- /admin/users default view shows a coarse activity signal, not an exact timestamp.
- Update admin.test.ts to assert the coarse-bucket behavior.
close_reason: lastActivity bucket (recent/inactive/never, 30-day window) replaces exact lastLogin in AdminUserInfo + /admin/users UI; bucketing + no-raw-timestamp test-pinned in admin.test.ts. Committed.

title: Redact email/IP from auth, recovery, and rate-limit logs
description:
Four call sites log raw PII outside src/lib/server/logger.ts's REDACT_KEYS list (lines
180-205, which already covers password/token/secret/xprv/mnemonic/etc. but not email or ip):
- src/lib/server/auth.ts:191 -- log.warn({ event: 'password_login_failed', email:
  normalizedEmail }, ...) on every failed login attempt.
- src/lib/server/auth.ts:194-199 -- log.warn({ event: 'password_login_denied', email:
  normalizedEmail, userId: row.id }, ...) on disabled-account login.
- src/lib/server/recovery.ts:387 -- log.warn({ userId, email }, 'Admin recovery login via
  environment variable') inside recordBreakGlassLogin.
- src/lib/server/rateLimit.ts:219 -- log.warn({ ..., ip }, 'invalid invite code submitted')
  inside noteInviteFailure (line 217).

These are all readable by any admin via /admin/logs (admin-gated per requireAdmin, but
visible to every admin on a multi-admin instance -- not just the operator) just from normal
login/recovery/invite traffic.

Fix: add `email` and `ip` to logger.ts's REDACT_KEYS array (lines 180-205) so pino's redact
mechanism strips them at every call site automatically (simpler and more future-proof than
hand-editing each of the four sites, and protects against new call sites making the same
mistake). Confirm all four sites still log userId where available (already true at 3 of 4)
so events remain traceable to an account without the raw PII.

Acceptance criteria:
- A fresh log run through all four code paths shows "[redacted]" in place of email/ip values.
- Add/extend a logger test asserting REDACT_KEYS includes 'email' and 'ip'.
close_reason: email+ip added to REDACT_KEYS (pino redaction covers all four sites + future ones); logger.test.ts pins all four call-site shapes with userId preserved. Committed.

title: RBF signaling boundary 0xfffffffe not pinned in tests
description: feeBump.ts:58/210 — tests cover 0xfffffffd (accept) and 0xffffffff (reject) but never the exact BIP-125 threshold 0xfffffffe. If >= were refactored to >, both tests pass but a 0xfffffffe tx is wrongly treated as replaceable. Cairn builds a replacement the network silently ignores — user thinks stuck tx was bumped. Fix: add three-fixture boundary test (0xfffffffd accept, 0xfffffffe reject not_rbf, 0xffffffff reject).
close_reason: DONE (test coverage added; NO code bug — false alarm corrected). Added src/lib/server/feeBump.rbfBoundary.test.ts (10 tests) pinning the BIP-125 boundary. IMPORTANT: a Sonnet subagent initially misread BIP-125 and flagged an off-by-one in feeBump.ts:210 (>= vs >). VERIFIED AGAINST Bitcoin Core policy/rbf.cpp SignalsOptInRBF (MAX_BIP125_RBF_SEQUENCE=0xfffffffd) and Cairn's own consistent convention (psbt.ts RBF_SEQUENCE=0xfffffffd, chain/index.ts rbf = seq<0xfffffffe, types.ts): a tx signals RBF iff nSequence<=0xfffffffd (<0xfffffffe). feeBump.ts (reject when seq>=0xfffffffe) is CORRECT. 0xfffffffe does NOT signal RBF. Changing >= to > would have been a real money-path bug. Corrected the TEST to pin 0xfffffffe on the non-signaling side; feeBump.ts unchanged. DO NOT 'fix' the >= — it is spec-correct. All 10 tests pass.

title: Nostr instance private key (nostr_sender_privkey) stored in plaintext with no encryption at rest or rotation
description: src/lib/server/channels/nostr.ts's getOrCreateSenderSecretKey() persists a freshly generated secp256k1 secret key via setSetting('nostr_sender_privkey', bytesToHex(sk)) into the flat, unencrypted settings table (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL — same pattern as core_rpc_pass). Unlike a rotatable relay password, this is a permanent signing/identity key: publishEncryptedDM() feeds it directly into nip44 conversation-key derivation and event signing. Any read access to the SQLite file (backup copy, disk image, misconfigured file permissions, path traversal, or another compromised in-process component) yields the raw key, letting an attacker impersonate the instance's Nostr identity indefinitely (send DMs as 'Cairn' to any recipient) with no rotation mechanism (only regenerated if absent/corrupt). This mirrors an existing project convention (core_rpc_pass is stored the same way) so it's not a new class of bug, but it's newly applied to a signing/identity key rather than a rotatable credential, which is a materially higher-value target if the DB ever leaks. Consider at minimum a rotation admin action, and evaluate whether instance secrets broadly warrant at-rest encryption given this precedent is now used for an identity key, not just relay passwords.
close_reason: nostr_sender_privkey is now stored via encryptSecret (AES-256-GCM at-rest envelope, same scheme as per-user SMTP passwords) instead of plaintext 64-hex, so a leaked settings DB is inert without the instance key file. Backward-compatible: a legacy plaintext key is adopted once and re-persisted encrypted (identity preserved, silent upgrade); an undecryptable stored key fails CLOSED (null) rather than silently minting a new identity. Added rotateSenderSecretKey() + admin-only POST /api/admin/nostr-identity rotation action for suspected-leak response. Tests cover encryption-at-rest, migration, fail-closed, and rotation (19 pass).

title: Caravan round-trip loses unknown-origin key paths
description: multisigExport.ts — export masks unknown-origin keys to fabricated m/0/0/0/0 for Caravan compatibility. Import reads literally with no recognition of the masking. Round-tripping Cairn's own export of an unknown-origin key silently turns it into a concrete (wrong) path. No test exercises the full round-trip. Fix: add sentinel or metadata marking masked paths, or normalize m/0/0/0/0 back to unknown on import.
close_reason: Normalize Caravan masked all-zero paths back to unknown 'm' on import (multisigExport.ts) + lossless round-trip test; see comment.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: FIXED. Root cause: caravanExport() masks an unknown-origin key (path '' or 'm') to a depth-preserving all-zero path via maskedPath() (e.g. depth-2 xpub -> 'm/0/0'), but parseCaravanImport() read bip32Path literally, so a Cairn export->import round-trip of an unknown-origin key silently hardened into a concrete (wrong) path. Fix (normalize-on-import, keeps Caravan interchange lossless): in parseCaravanImport, normalize any path matching /^m(\/0)+$/ (all-zero, all-unhardened = the mask, which a real hardened origin like m/48'/0'/0'/2' never matches) back to unknown 'm'. Chose normalization over a non-standard sentinel because Caravan uses the SAME masking convention, so 'm/0/0/0' semantically IS unknown-origin in a real Caravan file too. Tests: multisigExport.test.ts new lossless round-trip test with a depth-2 xpub (export masks to 'm/0/0', import restores 'm', deep-equal on name/xpub/fingerprint/path); updated the prior pinned test to expect 'm/0/0/0/0'->'m'. Full suite 40/40 pass.

title: Wallet-to-explorer links honor the explorer feature flag
description: With the 'explorer' flag disabled, tx/address links on wallet detail, send-sent, and activity surfaces still point into the gated route — closed cairn-8dup covered hiding UI elements on flagged pages, not inbound links from other surfaces. Either hide the links or swap to a non-explorer detail fallback, with cairn-8dup-style 'disabled by administrator' messaging on direct navigation.
close_reason: Fixed in commit (pending): guarded the 5 remaining unguarded explorer links (activity/+page.svelte:347,352 tx/block links; wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:820,1121,1141 tx/address/saved-tx links) using the svelte:element degrade-to-span pattern with a derived explorerEnabled = data.flags?.explorer !== false, matching RecentActivity.svelte's approach. npm run check: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Wallet-side explorer links flag-gated in 6 files (ed873e6, merged). Deferred: wallets/[id]/+page.svelte L797/L1099/L1120 and activity/+page.svelte L340/L342 — files temporarily owned by double-spend track. Post-merge visual QA pending.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Follow-up code survey 2026-07-12 (infra session, single-sig-full-wallet branch): remaining unguarded links re-located at CURRENT line numbers (previous comment's L797/L1099/L1120/L340/L342 have since shifted): - src/routes/(app)/activity/+page.svelte:347 (tx link) and :352 (block link) — no flags.explorer guard. - src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:820 (tx link), :1121 (recipient address link), :1141 (saved-tx link) — none guarded. Reference implementation already in the codebase - wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte:771-772 gates its equivalent tx link: {#if data.flags?.explorer !== false} ... {:else} ... {/if} Preferred pattern (degrade, not hide) - RecentActivity.svelte:21,31-33 uses a derived flag and to swap for
when the flag is off: const explorerEnabled = $derived(page.data.flags?.explorer !== false); ... This is preferred over hiding the link outright since the layout guard already 403s direct navigation to /explorer/** when the flag is off - degrading to a non-interactive element (rather than omitting the row) keeps the surrounding info intact. Re-verified the links already marked as gated in prior work are still intact (no regression): wallets/multisig/stateless/+page.svelte:880, wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1540, and wallets/multisig/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1534 all still carry the flags?.explorer !== false guard. Handoff note: this infra session deliberately did NOT edit activity/+page.svelte or wallets/[id]/+page.svelte - the concurrent UX orchestrator session has uncommitted changes in both files. Wrote docs/HANDOFF-o90e-wallet-explorer-links.md (untracked, not committed) with this same detail for that session to fold into its in-flight edits and close this bead with evidence once done.

title: Coinbase (mining-reward) maturity handling — audit follow-ups & hardening
description:
Audit 2026-07-14 of coinbase-output maturity (Ocean-pool / direct-coinbase miner use case; foundational for solo-mining epic cairn-vn43, esp. vn43.6 payout wiring + vn43.9 MiningRewards address wiring — a solo win pays the FULL coinbase into the user wallet and MUST be tracked immature for 100 blocks).

VERDICT: the consensus-critical path is ALREADY BUILT and sound. Evidence:
- Detection: src/lib/server/bitcoin/coinbaseScan.ts — coinbase-ness from RAW funding tx (getChain().getTxHex), synthetic prevout check (32 zero-bytes txid + index 0xffffffff), immutable process cache, fail-closed to 'unknown'. Backend-agnostic: getTxHex is Electrum-backed (chain/index.ts:1336) and Electrum is always present for wallet ops.
- Maturity math: src/lib/shared/coinbase.ts — COINBASE_MATURITY=100, coinbaseMaturity(height,tip): confirmations=tip-height+1, mature at confirmations>=100 (consensus-correct: coin at height H spendable in block >= H+100).
- Coin selection: src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts:358-432 selectSpendCandidates drops immature from auto-selection, rejects explicit coin-control pick of immature/unverifiable. Wired single-sig transactions.ts:422-477 + multisig multisigTransactions.ts:280-317 (both fetch tip, both annotateCoinbase).
- PSBT prev-tx: psbt.ts:527-537 coinbase inputs require+attach full nonWitnessUtxo (hash-verified L504-509).
- UI: MiningRewards.svelte + CoinControl.svelte on both wallet-detail pages.
Prior P0 (all Electrum deposits mislabeled immature) fixed in 55e3e20 by deriving from raw tx (getTx threw in Electrum-only mode).

Remaining work = children: one consensus-safety hole (tip-fetch fails OPEN), an end-to-end regtest proof, and UX honesty/polish.
close_reason: All children (oae1.1-.5) closed. Consensus-critical coinbase-maturity path verified: fail-closed guard on tip-fetch (oae1.1), regtest mine->mature->spend e2e proof (oae1.2), balance display excludes immature coinbase (oae1.3), maturing countdown UX (oae1.4), CPFP defense-in-depth guard (oae1.5). Ship gate 2026-07-14: npm run check 0 errors, npx vitest run 3433 passed/1 skipped (known vault E2E, needs live regtest), commit range b498c6f..cbe3682 pushed to origin/single-sig-full-wallet.

title: Coinbase maturity guard fails OPEN when getTip() is unavailable
description:
CONSENSUS-SAFETY. When a wallet holds coinbase coins but the chain-tip fetch fails, tipHeight is left undefined and the ENTIRE maturity guard is skipped (psbt.ts:393 gates on 'params.tipHeight != null'), so an immature coinbase can be auto-selected into a normal send and reach broadcast (network rejects the tx).

Evidence:
- src/lib/server/transactions.ts:422-429 — 'catch { tipHeight = undefined; // tip unavailable — skip the guard, don't block }'.
- src/lib/server/multisigTransactions.ts:280-285 — identical pattern (duality: fix BOTH).
This fail-OPEN contradicts the rest of the design, which is fail-CLOSED (coinbase-ness 'unknown' is treated as immature inside the maturity window, coinbaseScan.ts + psbt.ts:401). Tip 'unknown' should be equally conservative.

Failure mode: miner wallet + immature coinbase present + transient getTip() failure at draft time -> invalid/rejected broadcast (not fund loss, but a broken send for exactly the miner use case).

FIX: when tipHeight cannot be resolved AND any coinbase coin is present, do NOT skip — conservatively EXCLUDE all coinbase coins from automatic selection (keeps ordinary sends unblocked, keeps miner sends safe); for explicit coin-control selection of a coinbase coin with tip unknown, reject with a clear 'can't verify mining-reward maturity right now — try again shortly' message. Apply in selectSpendCandidates (or its callers) for BOTH single-sig and multisig.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Unit: with a coinbase UTXO present and tipHeight undefined, auto-selection excludes it; coin-control pick of it throws a clear PsbtError.
- Regtest: with an immature coinbase in the wallet, simulate getTip() failure (or point at a dead tip source) and confirm a send neither includes the coinbase nor silently builds an invalid tx.
close_reason: Fixed: selectSpendCandidates (src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts:393-437) now fails CLOSED when tipHeight is undefined. A coinbase-flagged coin (coinbase===true or 'unknown') is treated as unverifiable maturity when the tip can't be resolved: excluded from auto-selection, explicit coin-control pick rejected with "Can't verify this mining reward is ready to spend right now — try again in a moment." Ordinary non-coinbase sends unaffected. Shared by single-sig (transactions.ts) and multisig (multisigTransactions.ts) via the same selectSpendCandidates call. Unit tests added in psbt.test.ts describe('tip unavailable (cairn-oae1.1 fail-closed)'): excludes-from-auto-selection, rejects-coin-control-pick (with message match), treats-unknown-coinbase-ness-same-way, does-not-block-ordinary-send. Existing coinbaseScan.test.ts message-match updated for new wording. Full suite green: 3372 passed / 1 skipped. svelte-check 0 errors. Commit b498c6f.

title: End-to-end regtest verification of the coinbase mine->mature->spend lifecycle
description:
VERIFICATION. Unit tests cover selection logic (coinbaseScan.test.ts, psbt selection tests) but there is no documented end-to-end regtest proof of the full lifecycle Alex specified. Turn 'correct by reading' into 'verified on regtest' for both single-sig and multisig.

STEPS / ACCEPTANCE (run on the regtest stack — Electrum + Core; mine directly to a Heartwood-derived wallet address):
1. IDENTIFY: mine 1 block to a wallet receive address; verify the resulting UTXO is detected as coinbase (u.coinbase===true) and appears in the MiningRewards card, NOT as a broken/frozen coin.
2. COUNTDOWN: verify the maturing UI shows 'X of 100 confirmations' with a sane blocks-remaining + hours ETA, and that it advances as blocks are mined.
3. IMMATURE CANNOT SPEND: at reward_height + 98 (=99 confs) confirm a send (a) auto-selection excludes the coin and (b) an explicit coin-control pick is rejected with the immature-coinbase message — server-side (bypass the client), for single-sig AND multisig.
4. SPENDABLE AT EXACTLY 100 CONFS: at tip = reward_height + 99 (=100 confs) confirm the coin becomes selectable/spendable (matches consensus: coin at height H is spendable in a block >= H+100).
5. BROADCAST A REAL SPEND: build+sign+broadcast a spend of the now-mature coinbase; confirm the node accepts it and it confirms. Verify the PSBT carried the full nonWitnessUtxo for the coinbase input (psbt.ts:527-537) and that prev-tx fetch of a coinbase tx round-trips (getTxHex -> Transaction.fromRaw -> txid match, psbt.ts:504-509).
6. Repeat 3-5 for a multisig wallet (known single-sig/multisig duality-forking hazard).

Note regtest recipe caveats from memory: bc1->bcrt1 HRP, team-mode gate + contacts prerequisite for multisig. Record txids/heights as evidence in a bead comment on close.
close_reason: All 6 acceptance criteria verified E2E on regtest for single-sig and personal multisig (2-of-3 p2wsh); no bugs found. See bead comments for txids/heights/API evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: --text-omit

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
E2E VERIFIED on regtest (vault-e2e stack: vault-e2e-bitcoind RPC 127.0.0.1:18543 vaulte2e:vaulte2e, vault-e2e-electrs 127.0.0.1:60401). Dev servers: CAIRN_DB=data/vault-e2e-wave2b.db (solo, single-sig) on :5900, CAIRN_DB=data/vault-e2e-wave2a.db (team, multisig) on :5901. Funded wallets via the documented scriptPubKey bcrt1-HRP-swap bridge (MANUAL.md 16.5); verified swap correctness against `bitcoin-cli validateaddress` scriptPubKey byte-match before funding.

SINGLE-SIG (wallet id=1, "QA Coinbase Wallet", zpub 5159c173/84'/0'/0'):
1. IDENTIFY - mined 1 block (generatetoaddress) to bcrt1q0aw...dzrrz0 (= bc1q0aw...9dpaw4). Wallet snapshot showed coinbaseUtxos=[{txid:1ca41c11...,vout:0,value:625000385,height:467}], maturingTotal=625000385. Wallet-detail UI (read_page): "maturing - mining rewards not yet spendable" + MiningRewards card "Block 467 ... 1 of 100 confirmations - spendable in ~99 blocks (~16.5 hours)". PASS.
2. COUNTDOWN - format matches spec exactly ("N of 100 confirmations - spendable in ~M blocks (~H hours)"); advanced correctly as blocks were mined (verified at 1, 99, 100 confs). PASS.
3. IMMATURE CANNOT SPEND - (a) auto-select (no onlyUtxos): POST /api/wallets/1/psbt -> 400 {"error":"This wallet has no mature coins to spend right now.","code":"no_utxos"}. (b) explicit coin-control, bypassing the client (which disables the checkbox client-side - verified via JS: checked:false,disabled:true): POST with onlyUtxos=[{coinbase txid,vout:0}] -> 400 {"error":"A selected coin is an immature mining reward - it needs 99 more confirmations (~17h) before it can be spent.","code":"immature_coinbase"}. Send-page UI also showed "Plus $400,081.50 (6.25000385 BTC) from a mining reward still maturing - not spendable yet." PASS.
4. BOUNDARY - at tip=565 (99 confs, reward height 467): explicit coin-control still 400 immature_coinbase, message "needs 1 more confirmation (~1h)". At tip=566 (exactly 100 confs): snapshot maturingTotal flipped to 0, confirmed balance = 625000385 (mature). PASS both sides of the boundary.
5. REAL SPEND - built PSBT (POST /api/wallets/1/psbt, feeRate=2, amount=max) selecting the now-mature coinbase input (value 625000385). Signed externally (scripts/qa/sign-singlesig-psbt.mjs, seed byte 42, path m/84'/0'/0'/0/0 via the PSBT's own bip32Derivation). Broadcast via POST /api/wallets/1/transactions/1/broadcast -> txid dec8142203bc0d380ade742947f551f6944a244a0eb6f8db6ed3c3ebe0b586fd. `bitcoin-cli getmempoolentry` confirmed acceptance (fee 0.00000220 BTC = 220 sats = 110vsize*2sat/vB, matches requested feeRate exactly). Later confirmed on-chain (gettxout confirmations>0). PASS.

MULTISIG (personal 2-of-3 p2wsh, multisig id=2, "QA Personal Multisig", BIP-48 m/48'/0'/0'/2', collaborative:false so no team-mode/contacts flow needed - created directly via POST /api/wallets/multisig):
3. IMMATURE CANNOT SPEND - same both cases: auto-select -> 400 {"error":"This multisig has no mature coins to spend right now.","code":"no_utxos"}; explicit onlyUtxos on the immature coinbase -> 400 {"error":"A selected coin is an immature mining reward - it needs 99 more confirmations (~17h) before it can be spent.","code":"immature_coinbase"}. PASS.
5. REAL SPEND - mined coinbase at height 567, matured at tip 666 (100 confs, maturingTotal->0). Built multisig PSBT (POST /api/wallets/multisig/2/psbt), 2-of-3 signed externally (scripts/qa/sign-multisig-psbt.mjs, seed bytes 1 and 3, deriving from each PSBT input's own bip32Derivation full path). Broadcast via POST /api/wallets/multisig/2/transactions/3/broadcast -> txid b3d5f84a22c66883c30381fa78d13705a6d289f4dbe4aa97de6b08dd474457b4. getmempoolentry confirmed acceptance (fee 294 sats = 147vsize*2sat/vB). Confirmed on-chain. PASS.

6. AVAILABLE VS MATURING SPLIT - verified live in the single-sig wallet-detail UI at 1 conf: "$0.00 / 0.00 BTC" available header, separate "$400,081.50 (6.25000385 BTC) maturing - mining rewards not yet spendable" line, and send page "0.00 available" + "Plus ... from a mining reward still maturing". PASS.

Note: both dev-server instances hit the documented ~60s scanWallet/multisig cache (gapLimitScanner.ts SCAN_CACHE_TTL_MS) if you query addresses/receive right before mining - the first refresh after mining can serve a stale pre-fund snapshot; this is expected caching behavior per wallets.ts/walletScan.ts docs, not a bug (a second refresh past the TTL always showed correct data).

No bugs found - all 6 acceptance criteria pass for both single-sig and multisig. Closing.

title: Wallet balance display overstates spendable — includes immature coinbase
description:
UX-CORRECTNESS. The headline 'confirmed'/'available' balance includes immature coinbase value, but the send engine correctly refuses to spend it — so a wallet holding immature coinbase shows a positive 'available' balance yet send-max fails with 'no mature coins'. Misleading.

Evidence:
- Balance source: src/lib/server/bitcoin/gapLimitScanner.ts:76-99 — confirmed = Electrum blockchain.scripthash.get_balance.confirmed, which counts immature coinbase as confirmed (Electrum does NOT subtract maturity).
- Passed straight through: src/lib/server/walletSync.ts:465-466.
- Send eyebrow: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:615 'Send · {formatBtc(confirmed)} available'.
- Wallet-detail headline uses the same confirmed figure.
Contrast: the send ENGINE excludes immature (psbt.ts:358-432), so display and behavior disagree.

FIX: compute a spendable balance = confirmed − Σ(immature-coinbase value). All data already exists in the snapshot (coinbaseUtxos with heights + tipHeight — walletSync.ts:442-444/521-523). Surface an honest split, e.g. headline 'available' excludes immature and a secondary 'N maturing' line links to the Mining rewards card. Apply on wallet detail + multisig detail + the send eyebrow (single-sig AND multisig). Keep total = available + maturing + unconfirmed consistent.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Regtest: mine 1 block to the wallet (immature). Headline shows 0 available (or the non-coinbase remainder) and a 'maturing' figure equal to the reward; send eyebrow does not advertise the immature amount as available.
- After the coin matures (100 confs), 'available' includes it and 'maturing' drops to 0.
- No double counting; unconfirmed still handled separately.
close_reason: FIXED. walletSync.ts: added maturingTotal (sum of immature-coinbase value via new sumImmatureCoinbase()/coinbaseMaturity()) to WalletSnapshot + MultisigSnapshot, computed in doWalletScan/doMultisigScan from already-fetched coinbaseUtxos+tipHeight. confirmed itself UNCHANGED (still full net-worth total for portfolio/list-summary consumers). Wallet detail +page.svelte (single-sig) + multisig/[id]/+page.svelte: hero renders confirmed-maturingTotal as available, plus a '· N maturing - mining rewards not yet spendable' line to #mining-rewards (only when maturingTotal>0). Send +page.server.ts: loadSendLiveData folds maturingTotal out of live.confirmed too (from the utxos+tipHeight it already fetches) so eyebrow + max-amount validation agree with the build engine; +page.svelte shows a 'Plus N from a mining reward still maturing' hint. Tests: walletSync.test.ts +4 cases (28/28 pass), send/page.server.test.ts +3 cases (6/6 pass). svelte-check: 0 errors/0 warnings (1962 files). Visual: QA :5344 own tab - wallet/1, multisig/1, send/1 all render cleanly with zero coinbase (non-regression; with-coinbase visual belongs to oae1.2's regtest e2e). MANUAL.md route-table updated.

title: MiningRewards maturity label: add 'spendable in ~N blocks' per UX spec
description:
UX-POLISH. The immature label shows confirmations and an hours ETA but omits the blocks-remaining phrasing Alex specified ('42 of 100 confirmations — spendable in ~58 blocks (~9.7 hours)').

Evidence: src/lib/components/MiningRewards.svelte:56-61 renders 'Immature — {confirmations}/{required} confirmations' + '~{etaHours}h until spendable'. coinbaseMaturity() already returns blocksRemaining (src/lib/shared/coinbase.ts:31) — just not surfaced.

FIX: include blocksRemaining in the label to match the spec, e.g. '{confirmations} of {required} confirmations — spendable in ~{blocksRemaining} blocks (~{etaHours}h)'. Keep it plain-language per the app UX philosophy; keep the progress bar. Small, self-contained, no server change.

ACCEPTANCE: the immature row reads like Alex's example; mature rows still read 'Mature — spendable'; verify at a couple of confirmation counts on regtest that the block count and hours track correctly.
close_reason: FIXED. MiningRewards.svelte immature-row label now reads '{confirmations} of {required} confirmations - spendable in ~{blocksRemaining} blocks (~{eta})', e.g. '42 of 100 confirmations - spendable in ~58 blocks (~9.7 hours)' per Alex's spec. ETA formatting extracted to a new pure/exported formatMaturityEta() in shared/coinbase.ts (1-decimal hours once the wait crosses an hour, whole minutes below that, ~10min/block) rather than reusing coinbaseMaturity()'s integer-rounded-up etaHours field (left untouched - still used by CoinControl.svelte). Added id=mining-rewards anchor to the card (used by the oae1.3 wallet-detail maturing link). Tests: coinbase.test.ts +5 cases for formatMaturityEta (58 blocks->~9.7 hours, 100 blocks->~16.7 hours, 1 block->~10 minutes, 0 blocks->~0 minutes, 5 blocks->~50 minutes) - 12/12 pass. svelte-check clean. Mature rows unchanged ('Mature - spendable').

title: CPFP path lacks a defensive coinbase-maturity guard (currently safe by invariant)
description:
DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH. The CPFP builder forces coins via coin-control WITHOUT passing tipHeight, so selectSpendCandidates' maturity guard is skipped for that path.

Evidence:
- src/lib/server/transactions.ts:1039-1051 buildCpfpDraft -> constructPsbt with onlyUtxos but no tipHeight.
- Multisig CPFP path mirrors this (multisigTransactions.ts around L784/885).
SAFE TODAY only because CPFP exclusively targets the wallet's own UNCONFIRMED outputs (height<=0), and a coinbase output is always confirmed (in a block), so a coinbase can never be a CPFP candidate. This is an undocumented implicit invariant with no guard.

FIX (low urgency): either (a) assert/document that CPFP candidates are unconfirmed-only so coinbase is structurally impossible, or (b) pass tipHeight defensively so any future change can't regress into CPFP-ing an immature coinbase. Prefer (b) for symmetry with the normal send path. RBF (bumpTransaction, transactions.ts:1188) uses exactInputs and is intentionally exempt — leave as-is.

ACCEPTANCE: a unit test asserting CPFP never selects a coinbase coin (or that passing a coinbase into the CPFP candidate set is rejected/ignored); no behavior change for the normal unconfirmed-CPFP case.
close_reason: Added explicit defense-in-depth assertion in executeCpfpDraft (src/lib/server/feeBump.ts, shared by transactions.ts buildCpfpDraft and multisigTransactions.ts buildMultisigCpfpDraft): after filtering qualifying coins to height<=0 (unconfirmed-only), asserts none are coinbase-flagged (true or 'unknown') and throws an internal-invariant Error if so — documents and enforces the previously-implicit invariant that a coinbase output (always confirmed) can never qualify for CPFP. RBF (exactInputs path) intentionally left as-is per bead. Unit tests: cpfp.test.ts 'never lets a coinbase-flagged coin qualify as a CPFP input, even if reported unconfirmed' (forces a coinbase-shaped parent tx reported unconfirmed via mocked scan, confirms the guard throws) and the mirrored multisigTransactions.test.ts test. No behavior change for the normal case — full suite green 3372/1 skipped. Commit b4ecdaf.

title: No server-side logging anywhere -- errors vanish once caught
description:
Checked directly: Cairn has zero server-side logging infrastructure.

- No logging library in package.json (no pino/winston/anything).
- Zero console.* calls anywhere in src/ -- confirmed by grep across the whole tree.
- src/hooks.server.ts is 7 lines (session lookup + resolve) -- no request logging (method/path/status/latency), nothing.
- No handleError hook defined anywhere -- the dedicated SvelteKit mechanism for catching otherwise-unhandled request exceptions does not exist in this codebase. The only safety net is SvelteKit adapter-nodes own unconfigurable default behavior.
- The real problem is upstream of that default, though: 24 separate API-route catch-alls (grep for "status: 502" / "status: 500" under src/routes/api) catch an unexpected error, extract e.message for the client response, and let the actual error object -- and its stack trace -- fall out of scope. These never reach even the (nonexistent) handleError hook or SvelteKits default logging, because application code already fully handled the exception before that could happen.

Net effect: when something genuinely breaks -- a bug, a DB write failure, an Electrum client fault, a broadcast that fails for a reason none of the typed error classes anticipated -- there is currently NO server-side record of it. The self-hosting admin (who has no support line but themselves) finds out only if a user happens to report it, with nothing to debug from afterward.

What Cairn does well and should keep: typed domain errors (PsbtError, BroadcastError, AuthError, LedgerError/TrezorError) with good user-facing messages -- this is a UX strength, not the problem. The gap is purely on the "what does the operator see when something goes wrong" side.

Scope the fix for a self-hosted, privacy-first app -- NOT third-party error-reporting SaaS (Sentry etc. would contradict the self-sovereignty positioning; do not add telemetry that phones home by default). Instead:
1. Add a lightweight structured logger (pino is the standard, low-overhead choice) writing to stdout in a format self-hosters can pipe into whatever they already use (journald, Docker logs, a file, their own log aggregator if they want one).
2. Add a handleError hook in hooks.server.ts that logs any exception that reaches it with enough context (route, method, a request id) before SvelteKit returns its generic error response.
3. Audit the 24 API-route catch-alls: at minimum, log the underlying error server-side before converting it to a client-facing message, rather than only ever surfacing .message and discarding the rest.
4. Consider basic structured request logging (method, path, status, duration) in hooks.server.ts -- valuable for a self-hoster wanting to know whats hitting their instance, independent of whatever a reverse proxy in front of it might separately log.
close_reason:
3 of 4 fix items landed solidly, verified directly: (1) pino structured logger (src/lib/server/logger.ts) with dev-pretty/prod-JSON output, size-rotated local file (10MB x5, bounded), explicit no-secrets-logged discipline and comment. (2) handleError hook in hooks.server.ts logs 5xx exceptions with a short error ID, full context (route/method/userId), and path redaction (txids/addresses truncated) before returning a generic message + error ID to the client. (3) request logging is real: every non-asset request gets method/path/status/duration/userId logged at a severity matching its outcome. Admin log viewer (src/routes/(app)/admin/logs) is properly admin-gated and reads the same rotated file with a bounded tail (2MB) -- no unbounded growth risk. This is a strong, well-designed implementation, not a token fix.

Item 4 (audit the 24 API-route catch-alls) is partially done -- ~30 routes now call log.error() explicitly (multisig broadcast, stateless ops, auth, etc.) but a handful of single-sig routes still have bare catch-and-respond blocks with no log.error() call, most importantly src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/broadcast/+server.ts. Note for the record: these are NOT fully silent anymore -- hooks.server.ts's request-level logging still records a '502 in Nms' line for every request regardless, since that logging happens unconditionally after resolve() -- but the underlying stack trace/exact error is lost for these specific routes since the exception is fully handled locally and never reaches handleError. Filing a precisely-scoped follow-up bead for the remaining routes rather than leaving this one open unchanged, since the overwhelming majority of the original ask is genuinely done.

title: Explorer no-chain state is a dead end: raw '000,000 blocks' placeholder + 'height range' jargon
description:
Evidence: dev instance 2026-07-12, with no chain backend configured, the Explorer page shows a raw unformatted '000,000 blocks' placeholder and jargon like 'height range' instead of a friendly disconnected/setup state. This is a dead end for a brand-new user -- no explanation of what's missing or what to do next. Should follow the same calm 'not connected yet' pattern proposed for Home (see companion bead cairn-7zjo, item 2 of this QA pass) rather than leaking raw placeholder values and internal terminology.

Searched for overlap first: no existing bead matches this specific placeholder/jargon dead-end (searched 'explorer', '000,000 blocks', 'height range' -- only tangential hits like cairn-p2ol (chart axes/labels) and cairn-oqg (P1, explorer jargon tooltips, block/tx/address terms) which covers detail-page jargon, not this empty/disconnected-state placeholder). Filing new; recommend implementing alongside cairn-7zjo for a consistent disconnected-state component reused across Home and Explorer.
close_reason: Admin CTA added to the explorer no-connection banner (Check connection settings -> /admin/settings), live-verified admin vs non-admin rendering desktop+mobile. See comments for detail.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Fixed 0ff5d53 (merged): 3 sub-bugs -- eternal 000,000 skeleton on failed load, wrong 'Nothing found at this height range' message when never-synced, dead Retry on paged views (now invalidate('cairn:chain')); calm plain-language banner, no raw error strings. Never-synced branch verified by code (sandbox had live Esplora).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Follow-up fix (this pass): added an admin-only secondary action on the explorer index no-connection banner. src/routes/(app)/explorer/+page.server.ts now returns isAdmin (locals.user?.isAdmin ?? false, same pattern as tx/block/mempool routes). src/routes/(app)/explorer/+page.svelte:398-425 wraps Retry + a new 'Check connection settings' link (href=/admin/settings) in .chain-error-actions, shown only when data.isAdmin. Non-admins see Retry only, unchanged.

QA: live-verified on the shared dev instance (localhost:5173), which was genuinely Electrum-disconnected during this pass (real ECONNREFUSED, not simulated) -- seeded two throwaway DB users (qa-obg6-admin, qa-obg6-user; scrypt-hashed passwords, recovery/agreement rows inserted directly to clear onboarding gates) to log in as each role, confirmed via read_page + get_page_text at both 1280x800 and 375x812:
- admin: banner shows both 'Retry' and 'Check connection settings' -> /admin/settings
- non-admin: banner shows 'Retry' only, no settings link
Screenshot tool was unavailable this session (known hazard); used the read_page/get_page_text fallback per prior QA notes. Both throwaway users + their sessions/recovery/agreement rows deleted after QA -- confirmed zero rows remaining.

title: sendBoundaryDraft multisig boundary test flakes on 5s timeout under full-suite load
description: src/lib/server/sendBoundaryDraft.test.ts:299 ('multisig: one sat short of amount+fee rejects; the exact amount succeeds changeless') times out at the default 5000ms during a full npm test run, but the whole file passes in isolation in ~5.1s (12/12). Root cause is CPU contention under the parallel full suite pushing this multisig seed+buildDraft test past the per-test ceiling, not a logic bug. Fix: bump testTimeout for this file/case (e.g. 15000ms) or lighten the multisig seeding. Found during cairn-6efi.1 Wave 1 work; unrelated to explorer changes.

title: SOCKS5+TLS: no timeout on the TLS handshake over the proxy tunnel
description: Follow-up to cairn-vn48 (landed ed95397). The connect-timer added there covers only the two DIRECT dial branches. On the SOCKS5+TLS branch (socks5Host set AND useTls), SocksClient's timeout option bounds the SOCKS CONNECT, but the subsequent TLS handshake over the established tunnel is unbounded — a backend that completes the tunnel then stalls the TLS handshake could still hang ensureConnected() forever (proxy/Tor users only). Fix: extend the connect-timer to also cover the TLS-over-tunnel handshake (arm around the wrapTls(tunnel) step in the SOCKS+TLS path), or add a dedicated handshake deadline there.
close_reason: Fixed in 275f751 (worktree branch worktree-agent-a76bdb15881777d63). armConnectTimeout()/disarmConnectTimeout() now wrap the wrapTls(tunnel) TLS-over-SOCKS5-tunnel step at src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts:313-315 (previously only the two direct dial branches at ~324 and ~328 were armed). Reuses the existing onReady/fail machinery, no double-fire/leak risk (full connect path read and verified). Confirmed the plain-TCP-over-SOCKS5 sub-path (tunnel already connected, setImmediate(onReady)) cannot hang -- unchanged, request-level timeout already bounds the handshake there. New test: src/lib/server/electrum/client.test.ts 'rejects instead of hanging forever when the TLS handshake over an established SOCKS5 tunnel never completes (cairn-ocs9)', using a real SOCKS5 proxy fixture + mocked tls.connect. Full suite: 2032 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed (npx vitest run). svelte-check: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Fix landed on single-sig-full-wallet as 3fae3de (cherry-picked from worktree commit 275f751 referenced in the close comment above).

title: TODO comment references an internal, non-public issue-tracker ID
description: src/lib/server/bitcoin/signingMass.ts line ~160: "TODO(cairn-194): calibrate against real hardware..." references a local beads-tracker issue ID. .beads/ is explicitly gitignored and not pushed to the public repo (see the earlier repo-hygiene decision to untrack it) -- so this ID is meaningless and unresolvable to any external contributor reading the code, a dangling reference to a system that doesn't exist from their side. Either drop the specific ID and just describe the follow-up work in plain prose, or (once the project has public GitHub issues) reference a real public issue number the way OSS projects conventionally do (TODO(#123): ...).
close_reason: Fixed: tracker reference removed, substance kept as prose, stale harness path corrected.

title: Multisig-creation wizard collapses 3 distinct operations into one generic error message with no errorId
description: src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte:294-298's callAction() helper backs three distinct operations (adding a cosigner key, importing a Caravan config, previewing an address) with one shared "Something went wrong -- try again." fallback and no errorId for support correlation -- this is exactly the setup flow a self-hosting non-expert is most likely to get stuck in on their first wallet, with no path forward if retrying doesn't help. Fix: give each operation its own fallback message, and/or add an errorId matching the pattern used by hooks.server.ts's handleError. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: callAction() now takes a per-operation fallback message; add-key/import/preview/create each pass their own, replacing the single generic 'Something went wrong' (wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte).

title: Suppress (app) chain/sync banners on onboarding flow pages (recovery-setup, disclosure)
description:
recovery-setup inherits the (app) layout, so ChainHealthBanner/SyncBanner render above what should be a pure onboarding wizard -- these are chain-connectivity banners with no relevance during recovery setup, and they can visually stack with the page's own form-error surfaced by the auto generatePhrase $effect. This is a DIFFERENT root cause than cairn-7zjo (which deduped two banners firing for the same underlying cause); here the banners shouldn't be rendering on this route at all.

Suggested fix: add a hideChainBanners flag on flow/onboarding routes, or a !isFlowPage guard at (app)/+layout.svelte:119-126, so recovery-setup, disclosure, and similar onboarding-wizard pages never render the chain/sync banners.

Found-by: cairn-7zjo design review (deep-reasoner), 2026-07-12.

title: Admin miners/workers list is live-memory only; vanishes on restart until fresh share
description:
Epic: cairn-vn43 (mining pool). Found during QA wave 3 admin/isolation pass.

getAdminMiningView / getUserMiningView (src/lib/server/mining/readModels.ts)
source the "Miners"/"workers" lists entirely from the in-memory
MiningAggregates singleton (agg.liveAllMiners() / agg.liveWorkers(userId)),
never from the mining_workers DB table. The only DB read against
mining_workers anywhere in readModels.ts is:

    src/lib/server/mining/readModels.ts:95
    .prepare('SELECT MAX(best_share_diff) AS best FROM mining_workers WHERE user_id = ?')

i.e. purely for seeding the all-time best-share baseline (allTimeBest in
src/lib/server/mining/index.ts:298-311) -- the row-level worker list itself
(name, last-seen, shares, online/offline) is never hydrated from the DB
mirror on boot.

Effect: after a server restart, every previously-known worker disappears from
both /mining (user) and /admin/mining ("Miners" panel, "By user" breakdown)
until it submits a fresh share and repopulates the in-memory aggregates. The
DB-backed mining_workers table still holds the historical row (upserted by
the Stratum share handler elsewhere), so the data isn't lost -- it's just not
surfaced to the UI post-restart. An operator restarting the app (routine
update, crash recovery) sees an empty "No miners have connected yet." panel
even though the pool has an established user base.

Suggested fix: on boot (or on first read after a restart), hydrate a "last
known workers (stale)" view from mining_workers for display purposes --
clearly marked as stale/last-seen data -- until the in-memory aggregates
regain live shares for that worker.

Not a security issue (no cross-user leak; every read stays scoped to the
querying user or admin). Pure UX/observability gap.

title: notification_channel_config has no getPublicChannelConfig() redaction guard yet — forward risk for Units 9/10
description: commit c9483a6 (Notify Unit 1) shipped the notification_channel_config schema (src/lib/server/db.ts:553) with a comment promising 'Never returned to the client verbatim (see getPublicChannelConfig)', but no such function exists anywhere in the repo (confirmed by grep — only the comment and docs/NOTIFICATION-PLAN.md's prose reference it). No route reads the table yet, so there is no live leak today. But there is nothing structurally stopping a future Unit 9/10 author from doing SELECT config FROM notification_channel_config and returning it raw to a client-facing load function or API route, which would leak SMTP passwords/webhook HMAC secrets/ntfy tokens verbatim to the browser — exactly the class of bug this codebase already shipped once for coreRpcPass before getPublicInstanceSettings() (src/lib/server/settings.ts:65-70) fixed it with a hasCoreRpcPass boolean-flag pattern. Fix: add a getPublicChannelConfig() now (even as groundwork ahead of Units 9/10) that mirrors getPublicInstanceSettings()'s redaction pattern, so the secret-handling contract is enforced in code rather than just a comment.
close_reason: Added src/lib/server/notifyConfig.ts with redactChannelConfig() and getPublicInstanceNotificationSettings(), the getPublicChannelConfig()-style single source of truth. Wired all 4 former hand-rolled redactors (settings/notifications loader, channel API route, admin API route, admin page loader) to it.

comments.author: auditor
comments.text: Update: commit 6c3d1ec (notification settings + admin UI, Units 9&10) shipped and its message claims 'secrets redacted like coreRpcPass' — but getPublicChannelConfig() still does not exist anywhere. Instead there are now 4 independent hand-rolled redaction implementations (redact() in settings/notifications/+page.server.ts, redactForClient() in api/notifications/channels/[channel]/+server.ts, publicNotificationSettings() in api/admin/notifications/+server.ts, and a 4th copy duplicated inline in admin/notifications/+page.server.ts). Traced every current secret field (ntfy accessToken, webhook secret, smtp_pass, telegram_bot_token) through all 4 paths and confirmed each is currently redacted correctly — not an active leak today. But the fragmentation is worse than originally flagged: 4 places to keep in sync instead of 1, with nothing in the type system forcing a future secret field to be added to all of them. Recommend consolidating into one shared getPublicChannelConfig()-style helper now, mirroring settings.ts's getPublicInstanceSettings()/hasCoreRpcPass, before a 5th call site or a new channel's secret field gets added.

title: Missing/inadequate regression test: admin overview wallet count omits multisig wallets (cairn-xqfb)
description: cairn-xqfb (P2, closed in c110211) fixed the admin overview 'Wallets' count only summing the single-sig wallets table, omitting multisig wallets. A test exists in admin.test.ts (describe('instanceStats', ...) around line 172-179) but its fixture has zero rows in both the wallets and multisigs tables, so it cannot actually distinguish 'sums both tables' from 'counts only one' — it would pass against the pre-fix buggy code too. Strengthen the existing test: insert one row into wallets and one into multisigs for different users, then assert instanceStats().wallets === 2, so the test would genuinely fail against a regression back to counting only one table.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 834945e (admin.test.ts, notifiedTxidsCleanup.test.ts, settings.test.ts, api/admin/{settings,users,notifications}/server.test.ts). All green in full suite.

title: Admin settings dual write surface: form action ?/save and PUT /api/admin/settings hand-mirror the same validation
description: src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts (?/save action) and src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts (PUT handler, comments say it 'mirrors the validation') both write the same setting keys and both call reconfigureChain(). The duplicated validation logic must be kept in sync by hand across the two files, which is a drift risk (e.g. a new setting or validation rule added to one surface but not the other). Extract a shared validate+apply module (suggest src/lib/server/settingsWrite.ts) that both the form action and the REST endpoint call, so the two surfaces can't diverge. NOTE: this is not dead code -- do NOT propose removing the REST endpoint, it serves API-token automation.

title: Hardware wallet signing
description:
Device-specific signing integrations, each plugging into the guided signing stepper framework (cairn-yph.6) and the PSBT infrastructure epic (cairn-yph). Per Alex: bring over all the hardware wallets from Bastion (C:\dev\bastion). Important scope correction from the Bastion survey: Bastion actually has Ledger, Trezor, and Jade signing implemented, plus BitBox02 (not requested here, FYI only) -- but ColdCard signing is NOT implemented in Bastion, only a descriptor-registration file export. ColdCard's real SD-card sign workflow needs to be built from scratch for Cairn, using the air-gapped/file-based patterns as a starting point rather than a direct port.

Suggested build order within P2 (all same priority per Alex's instruction, but this is the natural dependency-free-to-hardest ordering): generic file-based signing first (no vendor SDK, exercises the whole stepper+PSBT round-trip end to end and immediately usable with Sparrow/Electrum/any PSBT wallet) -> Trezor Connect (web SDK, no native transport complexity) -> Ledger (WebHID) -> air-gapped QR (SeedSigner/Passport/Jade) -> ColdCard (needs the most from-scratch design work).

Security pattern to carry over into every device integration below, from Bastion's psbt.ts applyPartialSignatures(): never blindly trust a signature a device returns. Validate the returned signature's pubkey against the PSBT's own bip32Derivation fields before accepting it as valid for a given input. This is the difference between a hardware wallet integration and a hardware wallet integration with a spoofing hole in it.
close_reason: Epic complete: all five signing methods live in the Sign step — generic file, Trezor Connect (5f2d59a), Ledger WebHID (87c6ba6), air-gapped QR/BBQr (677c767), ColdCard SD card (e1d3d52), wired in 0f59954+a092c0f. Every device routes through the server-side substitution guard; every driver validates returned signatures against the PSBT's own bip32Derivation pubkeys per the epic's security note. npm run check 0 errors, 275 tests passing.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Adversarial browser verification (for S5's audit ask re: keys never touching Cairn / signatures validated against PSBT derivation / broadcast gate actually blocking):

1. No private key/seed ever touches Cairn: confirmed across all 5 signer components (generic file, Trezor Connect popup, Ledger WebHID, ColdCard SD file, QR/BBQr camera+paste) — every one of them deals exclusively in PSBTs. None has any input field, prompt, or code path that accepts a seed phrase or raw private key. Key custody stays entirely on the external device/Connect popup in every flow.

2. Signature validation, not blind trust: live-tested by uploading a PSBT with a tampered output amount through both the ColdCard file-upload path and the QR manual-paste path (real oncall wallet, real /file endpoint bytes, modified via @scure/btc-signer). Both were rejected server-side: "The signed transaction pays different outputs than the one you reviewed. It was not accepted." Also tested uploading a still-unsigned PSBT — rejected with "This PSBT was attached but still is not fully signed," confirming the client only advances to Confirm after the server (assertSameTransaction + summary.complete) actually validates it. This matches the epic's stated security pattern.

3. Confirmation gate before broadcast: PATCH-attach and broadcast are two separate server calls (transactions.ts), each independently re-running assertSameTransaction + finalizePsbt — so even a client that skipped the UI gate couldn't get an insufficiently-validated PSBT broadcast. Didn't attempt a real mainnet broadcast (no funded test wallet available + out of scope for browser testing without explicit sign-off), but the guard logic itself was exercised directly against real code in a prior session's oracle proof (constructPsbt -> sign -> assertSameTransaction -> finalizePsbt, full pass).

Also found and filed cairn-sta (P2, non-security): ColdCard/QR signer cards don't proactively warn when a wallet lacks stored key-origin data, unlike Ledger/Trezor which do — walks the user through physical device steps for a predictably-doomed signing attempt. Everything else checks out clean.

title: Generic file-based signing (universal fallback)
description: Download the constructed PSBT as a file, let the user sign it in any external PSBT-compatible tool (Sparrow, Electrum, a hardware wallet's own desktop companion app), then upload the signed result back into Cairn to finalize and broadcast. This is the universal path that works for literally any signing setup, needs no vendor SDK integration, and should be built first since it exercises the full construction -> stepper -> signed-upload -> broadcast round trip end to end without any device-specific complexity. This is effectively the same mechanism as PSBT download/upload (cairn-yph.2) with signing-flow framing/copy layered on top -- do not build a second implementation of the download/upload mechanics.
close_reason: Satisfied by the guided stepper's Sign step (commit 40cf0d8) — the 'Generic wallet / file' method fully implements download-unsigned / sign-externally (Sparrow, Electrum, ColdCard, any HW companion) / upload-or-paste-signed. Per the bead, this is the same mechanism as yph.2 with signing-flow framing, not a separate build. Verified live end to end: .psbt download (200 octet-stream), signed upload normalized from both binary-base64 and hex, mismatch guard, unsigned->incomplete. This is the universal fallback every other device path falls back to

title: Trezor Connect integration
description: Web-based signing via Trezor Connect -- user approves the transaction on-device over USB, no file export/import needed. Bastion reference: C:\dev\bastion\public\js\trezor.js (452 lines), using @trezor/connect-web (remote-loaded JS, opt-in). Bastion has a server-side /trezor/prepare -> /trezor/apply seam -- understand why before copying it verbatim; may or may not be needed depending on how Cairn structures its PSBT signing request/response. Must include on-device address/amount verification in the guided flow (the stepper's Confirm step should tell the user to check the destination and amount on the Trezor screen itself, not just trust the browser UI).
close_reason: Trezor Connect signing shipped: driver src/lib/hw/trezor.ts (5f2d59a) with client-side PSBT->Connect translation (no server prepare/apply seam needed — Cairn parses PSBTs client-side), device-account-vs-bip32Derivation pubkey verification before attributing positional signatures, DER+sighash merge-back; TrezorSigner.svelte with on-device verify instructions; wired into Sign step (a092c0f). 27 unit tests; check+tests green.

title: Ledger integration (WebHID)
description: Web-based signing via Ledger's Bitcoin app over WebHID. Bastion reference: C:\dev\bastion\public\js\ledger.js (334 lines), using @ledgerhq/hw-transport-webhid + ledger-bitcoin. Note Bastion persists a per-wallet, per-device 'policy HMAC' (schema.ts wallet.ledger_registrations table) for multisig registration -- not needed for Cairn's initial single-sig-only scope, skip that complexity for v2 and revisit if/when Cairn ever supports multisig wallets. Must include on-device verification step in the guided flow, same as Trezor.
close_reason: Ledger WebHID signing shipped: src/lib/hw/ledger.ts driver + LedgerSigner.svelte (87c6ba6), wired into Sign step gated on isWebHidAvailable with disabled-reason fallback (0f59954). Policy-HMAC multisig registration skipped per bead scope (single-sig only). 25 unit tests; browser-verified by tester.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Ledger WebHID lib + LedgerSigner.svelte committed (87c6ba6); Sign-step integration pending

title: ColdCard SD card workflow
description: Export unsigned PSBT to a file the user copies to an SD card (or via the newer virtual-drive-over-USB firmware, out of scope for v1 of this feature -- SD card only), signs on the ColdCard itself, then copies the signed PSBT file back and uploads it into Cairn. IMPORTANT: unlike the other four device beads in this epic, Bastion does NOT have this built -- it only exports a descriptor-registration file (coldcardMultisigFile() in wallet-manager.ts), no actual signing round-trip. This needs to be designed from scratch, not ported. It's mechanically closest to the generic file-based signing bead (cairn-ogw.1) -- same download/upload primitive -- the ColdCard-specific work is entirely in the guided-flow instruction copy (SD card insert/remove steps, PSBT filename conventions ColdCard expects) rather than new signing logic.
close_reason: ColdCard SD-card workflow shipped: ColdCardSigner.svelte file round-trip with guided SD insert/sign/re-import instructions (e1d3d52), wired into Sign step (0f59954). Designed from scratch as bead notes required (no Bastion port); reuses the download/upload primitive + substitution guard.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: ColdCardSigner.svelte SD-card flow committed (e1d3d52); Sign-step integration pending

title: Air-gapped QR signing (SeedSigner, Passport, Jade)
description:
Show the unsigned PSBT as an animated/paginated QR code (device camera scans it, fully air-gapped, no USB/file transfer), then scan the signed-PSBT QR the device displays back using the browser's camera (BarcodeDetector API or a JS QR-reading fallback for browsers without it). Bastion reference for the QR encode/scan mechanics: C:\dev\bastion\public\js\hw-signer.js (BBQr encoding via bbqrEncode + BBQrReassembler for the scan-back side) -- SeedSigner and Passport both use this same QR path in Bastion.

IMPORTANT scope note on Jade specifically: Bastion's actual Jade integration (C:\dev\bastion\public\js\jade.js, 534 lines) uses Web Serial (wired USB-serial transport, jadets library), NOT QR -- even though Jade the device supports true air-gapped QR signing as a hardware feature. This is a real decision point, not an oversight to just copy: Web Serial is proven/working in Bastion and is a faster port, but Web Serial has narrower browser support (no Safari, no mobile) than camera-based QR scanning, and true air-gapped QR is arguably the more fitting choice alongside SeedSigner/Passport in this bead's own grouping. Decide and note which transport Jade gets in Cairn before starting -- don't silently default to whichever is easier without that being a deliberate call.
close_reason: Air-gapped QR signing shipped: BBQr encode/scan lib + QrSigner.svelte animated multi-frame display, BarcodeDetector camera scan-back with per-frame progress and paste fallback (677c767), state_unsafe_mutation fix (d9d75c9), wired into Sign step (0f59954). Jade decision: camera QR (BBQr) not Web Serial — broader browser support, matches SeedSigner/Passport grouping. Tester verified full encode->sign->scan-back round trip with real BIP84 test vector.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: BBQr encode/scan lib + QrSigner.svelte committed (677c767); Sign-step integration pending. Jade transport decision: camera QR (BBQr), not Web Serial — matches SeedSigner/Passport grouping and broader browser support

title: No SOCKS5/Tor proxy support for the Electrum/Esplora chain backend
description:
Found during a privacy-tools parity audit against Bastion (2026-07-06) — neither codebase has this, but it's a real, material gap for Cairn specifically given its stated privacy-conscious design philosophy (plain language, no exposed internals, user-controlled backups).

src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts connects to the configured Electrum server via raw tls.TLSSocket/net.Socket (no proxy support at all). src/lib/server/settings.ts's electrum config (electrumHost/electrumPort/electrumTls/electrumTlsInsecure) has no proxy fields. Same story for the Esplora HTTP path.

Impact: every address/balance/UTXO query Cairn makes leaks the operator's real IP address to whichever Electrum/Esplora server is configured — including third-party public servers if the operator points at one instead of running their own node. A self-hosted Bitcoin wallet with no Tor option is a meaningful privacy gap; most privacy-oriented wallets (Sparrow, Electrum itself, Wasabi) offer at least a SOCKS5 proxy setting for exactly this reason.

Recommended fix: add a socks5Host/socks5Port (or torProxy) field to instance settings, and wrap the Electrum TCP connection (and Esplora HTTP client) in a SOCKS5 CONNECT handshake when set — a well-trodden pattern (e.g. the 'socks' npm package, or a raw SOCKS5 client, ~100 lines) that doesn't require bundling Tor itself, just the ability to point at a local tor SOCKS proxy (default 127.0.0.1:9050) or any other SOCKS5 proxy.
close_reason: Added SOCKS5/Tor proxy support for the chain backend end-to-end. Settings: new socks5Host/socks5Port on InstanceSettings + settings.ts (socks5_host/socks5_port), surfaced in getChainConfig() in BOTH public and custom modes (proxy is independent of connection mode). Electrum: ElectrumClient dials via socks.SocksClient CONNECT when a proxy is set, with TLS negotiated end-to-end over the tunnel (proxy sees only ciphertext); hostname sent to proxy for remote DNS (no leak). Esplora: EsploraApi routes fetch through socks-proxy-agent (socks5h://, remote DNS) via a node http/https path since global fetch/undici can't take a SOCKS dispatcher. Admin UI: new 'Privacy: SOCKS5/Tor proxy' field group on /admin/settings (always visible), with validation in both the form action and the JSON API; Test buttons exercise the proxy too. Declared socks + socks-proxy-agent as explicit deps. Tests: 2 new Electrum E2E tests (dials through an in-test SOCKS5 proxy; errors when proxy unreachable). Full suite 1059 pass, typecheck clean. Note: in-browser admin-page verification not driven (admin-gated); relied on svelte-check + unit tests per this branch's build constraints.

title: Settings desktop breadcrumb back-button loop — pushes history instead of replacing
description: Desktop breadcrumb links (viewport >=900px) at src/routes/(app)/settings/tokens/+page.svelte:41, settings/devices/+page.svelte:36, settings/contacts/+page.svelte:121, settings/notifications/+page.svelte:518 render a plain `<a class="crumb-link" href="/settings">`. This pushes a new history entry, so pressing Back from the sub-page returns to the sub-page itself (alternating-back loop) - same bug class fixed for mobile in cairn-y7ac via BackCircle.svelte using goto(href, {replaceState:true}). Fix: apply the same replaceState navigation pattern to the desktop breadcrumb links (or extract a shared BreadcrumbLink component).
close_reason: Fixed via commit 346827c (worktree agent-a17e2b1327fd84733). All 4 desktop settings breadcrumb links (tokens/devices/contacts/notifications) now use goto(href, {replaceState:true}) on click, matching BackCircle.svelte's mobile fix pattern exactly. vitest 1880/1 skipped, svelte-check 0/0.

title: Address page tx-history breaks on Electrum-only instances — capability errors swallowed (follow-up to cairn-37gfa)
description:
Follow-up to cairn-37gfa (closed, commit dfe170f). That bead fixed src/lib/server/search.ts's classifySearch() to distinguish genuine not-found errors from capability/transport errors via isNotFoundError() (search.ts:14), routing the latter optimistically instead of misreporting. The bead's own evidence also reproduced a second, distinct instance of the same error-swallow class on the address detail page's transaction-history section (tested against bc1q8mnhpqwuvymt9q856n5r5dnnvvajwf7k4dv5uk, a real address with 0.68 BTC — balance loads fine, only the tx-list breaks) and explicitly recommended filing a separate follow-up. That fix did NOT touch this site.

CONFIRMED FILE:LINE: src/lib/server/chain/index.ts:1519, inside getAddressTxs() (starts line 1492). getVerbose() (line 1516-1523) calls `this.electrum.getTransaction(txid, true)` directly — the raw Electrum verbose call, with none of the isNotFoundError-style branching that getTxViaElectrum() (chain/index.ts:1019-1026) already has for the single-tx lookup path. All page items are then verbose-fetched concurrently via Promise.all (line 1525), which fails fast: the first rejected getVerbose() call (e.g. Blockstream's public Electrum server replying `-32603 verbose transactions are currently unsupported` to ANY verbose call) rejects the whole Promise.all, so getAddressTxs() throws for the entire page rather than degrading only the affected entry.

FAILURE MODE: the rejection propagates up through src/routes/(app)/explorer/address/[address]/+page.server.ts's loadAddressTxs() (lines 43-52), which does catch it and does NOT literally swallow to a silent empty state — it surfaces sanitizeChainError(e, ...) as a raw string, rendered by +page.svelte:408 as `Banner variant="error"` "Couldn't load transactions — Electrum error: verbose transactions are currently unsupported". So the user sees an opaque backend-error banner instead of either (a) the tx list rendering normally where possible, or (b) the same honest "needs a Bitcoin Core node" degrade notice the tx/[txid] page already renders for this exact backend state (see search.ts's classifySearch fix comment, cairn-37gfa). Net effect: on the documented recommended out-of-box config (public Electrum server, no Core RPC), the ENTIRE address tx-history section is unusable, even though balance (non-verbose electrum calls) loads fine.

FIX DIRECTION: mirror the cairn-37gfa pattern. In getAddressTxs()/getVerbose(), catch each per-tx getTransaction() rejection and branch on isNotFoundError(e) (already exported from search.ts) — or better, hoist that helper to a shared chain-errors module both files import, since it's now needed in two places. A genuine not-found for a specific txid can be excluded from the page (defensive; only expected if history/tx state raced). A capability/transport error (verbose-unsupported, unreachable, etc.) should NOT fail the whole page silently as a raw backend string — either degrade the affected row gracefully or surface the same explicit "needs a Bitcoin Core node" CoreRpcRequiredNotice-style component already used elsewhere (see cairn-zoz8.9 / CoreRpcRequiredNotice.svelte) instead of Banner's raw sanitizeChainError() text, so self-hosters on the recommended public-Electrum default get an honest, actionable message instead of an opaque error dump.

v0.2.39 candidate.
close_reason: Fixed src/lib/server/chain/index.ts getAddressTxs() (~1524-1595): per-item try/catch around getVerbose(item.tx_hash) — a definitive not-found (matches getTxViaElectrum's own /no such|not found/i check) drops the row, any other error (capability/transport, e.g. public Electrum server rejecting verbose calls) degrades the row to index-only data (txid/height/fee from the history item, time+delta null) instead of rejecting the whole Promise.all. Prevout lookups inside the fee/delta calc now .catch(() => null) per-input so one bad prevout fetch degrades that tx's fee/delta precision instead of rejecting the row. Result list filters out only the genuine not-found nulls. Address page's existing rendering (+page.svelte) already treats delta===null/fee===null/time===null as '—' (Amount/formatSats/timeAgo/formatDateTime are all null-safe) so no template changes were needed — a degraded row renders as a plain dash, not an error banner. Added 2 unit tests to chain.test.ts (capability-error degrade, genuine-not-found drop) alongside the 3 existing getAddressTxs tests, all 5 pass. npx tsc --noEmit and npm run check both 0 errors/0 warnings. Commit 877bec4.

title: Undefined danger/danger-muted design tokens with wrong-semantic fallbacks
description:
Confirmed by grep: var(--danger, ...) and var(--danger-muted, ...) are used in 4 places across 3 files, but --danger/--danger-muted are never defined in src/app.css. CSS custom-property fallback silently kicks in, with inconsistent results per call site:
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1454 and src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1423 -- color: var(--danger, var(--text)) -- a "danger" indicator silently renders as plain neutral text, no red/alarm color at all.
- src/routes/(app)/vaults/stateless/+page.svelte:1486,1491 -- var(--danger-muted, var(--warning-muted)) and var(--danger, var(--warning)) -- renders as yellow warning instead of red danger, semantically wrong severity.

This is not new to vaults -- the wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte usage predates the vault feature, it was just never caught until this audit went looking for it specifically. Fix: add real --danger / --danger-muted tokens to app.css (most likely just alias them to the existing --error / --error-muted tokens, since "danger" and "error" are the same semantic red status here -- decide whether to standardize naming on --error everywhere and drop the --danger references, or keep both names as aliases for readability at call sites).
close_reason: Fixed in d5f9c73: added --danger and --danger-muted to src/app.css as aliases of --error/--error-muted, so the var(--danger)/var(--danger-muted) call sites resolve to the correct red instead of falling back to neutral text or yellow warning.

title: Descriptor import hardcodes scriptType p2wsh — sh(wsh()) and sh() descriptors create a wallet with the wrong script type
description: src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/import/+server.ts parseSource() and src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts import action both hardcode scriptType: 'p2wsh' for the descriptor branch, with a comment claiming 'parseDescriptor only accepts wsh(sortedmulti(...))'. That comment is stale: parseDescriptor (src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisig.ts) accepts sh(wsh(sortedmulti())) -> p2sh-p2wsh and sh(sortedmulti()) -> p2sh, and returns the recognized scriptType on the config. Importing a p2sh or p2sh-p2wsh descriptor therefore creates a multisig stored as p2wsh — every derived address is wrong, funds appear missing. Fix: use config.scriptType from parseDescriptor. Found while implementing cairn-1kc3.1 (the new 48' suffix-vs-scriptType check would falsely reject correct sh(wsh()) descriptors because of this).
close_reason: Wizard half fixed (API half was already fixed earlier on this branch): src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts import action now uses config.scriptType ?? 'p2wsh' from parseDescriptor instead of the hardcoded 'p2wsh', stale comment removed, and validateMultisigKeyPaths(config) added at the boundary to match the API route. sh(wsh(sortedmulti())) and sh(sortedmulti()) descriptors now prefill the wizard with the real script type (p2sh-p2wsh / p2sh) instead of a confusing suffix-mismatch rejection at create. check + full suite green.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: API half FIXED in this change: src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/import/+server.ts parseSource now uses config.scriptType from parseDescriptor (and runs validateMultisigKeyPaths on the parsed config). REMAINING (why this stays open): src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts 'import' action (~line 225) has the identical hardcoded 'p2wsh' + stale comment — untouched here because the wizard's server action is shared surface with cairn-fdlf (Wave-A scope boundary). Note the blast radius is now smaller: even via the wizard prefill, createMultisig's new suffix-vs-scriptType check (cairn-1kc3.1) rejects the mismatched creation instead of silently storing a wrong-script-type wallet — the remaining bug is a confusing rejection, no longer silent corruption.

title: No educational tooltips anywhere in the explorer (block/tx/address jargon unexplained)
description:
Cairn is framed as an educational Bitcoin explorer, but block/tx/address detail pages surface raw protocol jargon with zero explanation: DIFFICULTY, NONCE, BITS, VERSION, WEIGHT, MERKLE ROOT on block pages; LOCKTIME on tx pages; address type codes like "P2WSH"/"P2WPKH" on address pages. None of these have hover tooltips, info icons, or inline help text.

Verified via code search: `grep -ri "tooltip|educational|help-text|info-icon"` across src/ returns zero matches — there is no tooltip infrastructure in the codebase at all.

Repro: visit /explorer/block/<any height> and hover over "DIFFICULTY", "NONCE", "BITS" labels — nothing happens. Same for address type badge on /explorer/address/<addr>.

Impact: for an app whose differentiator is explaining Bitcoin to newcomers, this is a significant gap versus the stated goal, not just a nice-to-have.
close_reason: Fixed in 0046d11: Term tooltip component with dotted-underline affordance now explains nonce, difficulty, bits, merkle root, weight, fees, vsize, locktime, RBF, SegWit, address types, and more across block/tx/address pages, plus collapsible How-does-this-work panels per page

title: Add-wallet wizard: hardware wallet option buttons (Trezor/Ledger/ColdCard/etc.) have no accessible name
