title: Balance-history chart shows stale snapshot after switching node connection, contradicting live 0 balance
description:
Observed live on the v0.1.3 Umbrel instance after switching Admin > Settings > Node connection from the default public servers to a custom regtest Electrum server: the Dashboard's "Portfolio" confirmed balance correctly showed 0.00 BTC / 0 sats (live, correctly re-scanned against the new regtest chain), but the "Balance over time" chart directly below it kept showing a flat line labeled 0.0000186 BTC on both the start and end of the visible range, contradicting the live number.

Root cause: balance history is stored in an append-only balance_snapshots table (src/lib/server/portfolio.ts), written by recordSnapshot() (throttled to ~hourly) and backfilled on wallet import by buildBackfillPoints() (added in commit 64c47af, cairn-ittq). Nothing invalidates or re-derives existing snapshot rows when the node/chain connection changes (or generally whenever a wallet's live balance changes to contradict its last snapshot) -- src/lib/server/dataRetention.ts only downsamples/prunes by age, never by correctness. getBalanceSeries() simply carries forward whatever was last snapshotted.

Impact: low severity, self-corrects within about an hour once the next snapshot is recorded, and this specific trigger (switching Electrum backend to a different chain) is a power-user action rather than routine flow -- but it does mean the chart can show a materially wrong number that visibly contradicts the live balance card immediately above it for up to an hour, which reads as a real correctness bug to anyone who notices, especially if they'd just switched to their own node for the first time (exactly the "point it at your own node" flow the settings page invites new users to try).

Fix shape: either (a) invalidate/re-derive balance_snapshots when the node connection settings change, or (b) have the chart refuse to show a snapshot whose value has drifted from a fresher live-scanned confirmed balance, falling back to its empty state instead of a stale number.
close_reason: Chose a variant of option (b), implemented entirely in src/lib/server/portfolio.ts (the node-connection settings path was out of this task's file scope): recordSnapshot() now bypasses the hourly throttle whenever any wallet's live, fully-scanned balance contradicts that wallet's latest snapshot (new contradictsLatestSnapshot() helper). Since getPortfolioDetail records the snapshot BEFORE building the series, the chart's newest point agrees with the live balance card on the very next portfolio fetch instead of up to an hour later — covering the chain-switch case observed on Umbrel and ordinary balance changes alike. Only complete scans reach recordSnapshot (existing guard), so a partial Electrum outage can never fake a contradiction. Growth stays bounded: extra rows only on real balance changes, and dataRetention's existing downsampling applies. portfolio/dataRetention tests pass.

title: First-boot HTTPS window: 3212 accepts-and-drops while the TLS key generates
description: Field report v0.1.6 (Alex, 2026-07-07): https://umbrel.local:3212 gave ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE immediately after updating. Root cause verified live against the device the same night: NOT an Umbrel networking problem — raw ports: publishes are the sanctioned mechanism (umbrel-package-app skill: 'integrations that must connect without app_proxy'; bitcoin/electrs/adventurelog all do it) and by probe time the port served TLS1.3 + full pages to curl, openssl, and real Chrome (cert interstitial reached via both umbrel.local and LAN IP; device cert had correct SANs, generated 04:12:12 UTC). The failure window: server.mjs starts the HTTP listener first, then awaits ensureCert; node-forge 2048-bit RSA keygen is pure JS and can take tens of seconds on ARM, and during image pull + boot + keygen the published port 3212 has docker-proxy accepting connections with no backend -> accept-then-close = ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE. First boot after update only (cert persists in /data/tls). Fix ideas: (1) generate the key with node:crypto generateKeyPairSync (native, <1s even on ARM) and keep node-forge only for cert assembly, or switch cert generation off selfsigned entirely; (2) SecureContextHelp copy: 'just updated? give it a minute' note. No store/compose change needed or wanted.
close_reason: server.mjs binds listeners before app import (503 placeholder), ensureCert degrades to in-memory cert; boot-hammer verified zero empty replies; ships in 0.1.7

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Root-caused and fixed. The dead-port window was NOT keygen (selfsigned v5 uses native crypto.subtle — full local suite generates in <1s; the node-forge assumption was stale): it was server.mjs importing ./build/handler.js (DB open, migrations, Electrum pool) BEFORE binding any listener, while Docker forwards the published port from container start. Fix: server.mjs now binds HTTP+HTTPS immediately with a self-refreshing 503 'Cairn is starting' placeholder and hot-swaps in the SvelteKit handler when the import completes; ensureCert() additionally degrades to an in-memory cert if /data/tls is unwritable (previously: swallowed error -> listener disabled -> permanently dead port, the exact ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE signature). Verified: boot-hammer against the built image on the 0.1.6 volume — first HTTPS 200 at 379ms from docker run, zero empty replies (was: empty replies until full app init). Also verified live against the device pre-fix: curl/openssl/Chrome all reached Cairn on https://umbrel.local:3212 once booted — compose raw-port publish is architecturally correct, NO store/compose change needed.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: SHIPPED in v0.1.7: public c0b73af, tag v0.1.7, image ghcr.io/alexm223/cairn:0.1.7@sha256:bfb32b52632998129aca1448ace1cefa4736e15a12ce6f048ea74d014dc23723 (amd64+arm64 verified via ghcr manifest API), store commit a0d40c8, release https://github.com/AlexM223/cairn/releases/tag/v0.1.7. CI caveat: first tag build failed on svelte-check (err unknown in checked .mjs + selfsigned types have notAfterDate not days) — fixed in private 0bc8e87, public release commit amended + tag moved. Published-digest update-path re-test was NOT possible this session (host DNS outage, see below) — the locally built image from the identical source commit passed the full matrix instead. NOTE for the session log: the entire GitHub leg (push/API/release) was done through docker run --dns 1.1.1.1 because Tailscale MagicDNS points at a dead tailnet resolver (100.109.113.98) and ALL host DNS is down — Alex needs to fix the tailnet nameserver.

title: Sync-SQLite concurrency collapse under multi-user load
description:
Evidence from this wave's parallel QA tracks:
(i) stress track: p50 1.5-2.0s at 200 concurrent requests vs 1-4ms sequential on the same warm server -- node:sqlite DatabaseSync serializes on the event loop under concurrency (same mechanism as cairn-xlrm, now quantified at scale).
(ii) load harness: mixed-40-20-40 scenario at tier-200 hits 88.4% errors (baseline 0.3%) -- the 20% unbounded POST /api/wallets writes mixed into 80% reads collapses bimodally (instant failures + 10s timeouts), consistent with cairn-y802's narrower single-scenario finding but far worse at this tier.
(iii) event-loop lag tracks p99 latency closely even in read-only scenarios, confirming the sync-SQLite serialization point as the shared root cause across read and write paths.

Refs: docs/TEST-REPORT-2026-07-12-loadtest.md, scratchpad track-d notes (this wave).

This is the multi-user-load-scale confirmation of cairn-xlrm's root cause and extends cairn-y802's single-scenario write finding to a full concurrency sweep.
close_reason: Fixed in 4095ee9 (integrated): per-user single-flight of the whole refreshPortfolio pass (walletSync.ts) — concurrent triggers share one pass + one synchronous buildPortfolioAggregate rebuild. Prior waves had already banked WAL/busy_timeout, SWR snapshot caches, derivation memoization; remaining headline lever is worker-thread scan driver = cairn-1q4b. Full suite green.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Note from cairn-wcxw (sync engine Phase 1 dirty-tracking, shipped @ 960ed39): Phase 1 does NOT touch DB access / the sync-SQLite serialization that IS the qyvl cliff -- that stays out of scope (Phase 2, cairn-1q4b worker threads / async driver). But it does REDUCE the background scan pressure that competes on the single event-loop thread: clean idle wallets now skip the full gap-limit scan (Electrum round-trips + raw-tx CPU + JSON.stringify + the synchronous writeSnapshot upsert) for up to 30 min instead of re-scanning every 20s. The biggest relief is warmAllSnapshots (every user's wallets at boot) and the periodic portfolio pass -- both previously re-scanned every wallet unconditionally and now skip clean ones (runPortfolioRefreshPass is dirty-aware). Also, refreshPortfolio's per-item meta read was switched from readWalletSnapshot/readMultisigSnapshot (which JSON.parse the whole address+tx blob) to a new readSyncMeta() that reads just (last_synced_at, dirty_since) -- one fewer full-blob parse per item per pass. Net: fewer + lighter SQLite writes/reads under the background lane, so the qyvl mixed-load cliff should improve at the margins, but the write-heavy POST /api/wallets serialization (cairn-y802) is UNCHANGED and remains the headline. Measure before/after with the load harness when qyvl is picked up.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Investigated with the load harness (scripts/load-test) 2026-07-14, single-sig-full-wallet. REFRAME: the mixed-load collapse is NOT sync-SQLite serialization. Isolated by scenario: (d) write-pressure (pure DB writes) tier200 396-462 rps 0% err — DB write path scales; (a) steady browsing SSR+portfolio tier200 301 rps 0% err — read path scales; (c) the cliff tier200 47-61 rps p50 ~10s err 54.7% — the ONLY difference is c's 40% GET /wallets/:id/send. Per-request logs: POST /api/wallets 2ms, /api/portfolio 0ms, /wallets/:id/send 60-165ms. Root cause = send-page Svelte SSR render (~100ms/req), filed as cairn-97gt. IN-LANE improvement shipped: PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL (commit d0fb319) cuts the write fsync tail (+16% rps, p99 1144->812ms) but does NOT move scenario c (still 58% err), confirming the cliff is SSR-bound. Leaving OPEN; actionable fix is cairn-97gt (UX/perf).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: UPDATE 2026-07-14 (single-sig-full-wallet): the send-page RENDER angle (cairn-97gt) has been fixed + verified (commit b178e8f: CSR-gate the wizard, tier200 scenario c 46->70 rps, p50 10s->5.4s) — but this did NOT clear the mixed-load cliff. err at c200 is still ~63pct. Root cause of the RESIDUAL cliff is now precisely isolated: the send page streams loadSendLiveData (a DELIBERATE live wallet re-scan on every send GET — getWalletUtxos->scanWallet, per walletScan.ts, bypassing the cached snapshot), and the harness driver waits for it (full-body drain). At tier1 the send handler is ~105ms EVEN with the render fully gated out of SSR, proving the residual is the live scan, not the render. Filed as cairn-g1u2. This is the data/sync lane you flagged (sync-SQLite is still a separate contributor, but the send-page cliff specifically is the live re-scan CPU serializing on the event loop). Keep OPEN; actionable residual is cairn-g1u2 (relates cairn-1q4b worker-thread / async-driver).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: cairn-g1u2 (send-page clean-wallet snapshot fast path) shipped — send GET for a clean wallet is now 2-3ms (snapshot-served) vs 365ms live, proven on real electrs. This does NOT clear scenario c in the dead-Electrum load harness: that harness's send scan fails fast on the closed port, so the residual tier-200 collapse there (~83-88% err) is THIS bead's sync-SQLite / event-loop saturation, not the send scan. Staying OPEN — the actionable lever remains cairn-1q4b (worker-thread / async DB driver).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
cairn-8ubd derivation memoization shipped @ 0ad9261 (this lane). CPU profile of scenario c (dead Electrum, tiers 50/200) confirmed the tier-200 event-loop saturation was ~70% secp256k1 ADDRESS DERIVATION, not sync-SQLite (portfolio/label writes stayed 0-1ms throughout). Memoizing it (parseXpub LRU + change-node WeakMap + address LRU + multisig deriver factory) moved tier-200: 53->113 rps (+113%), p50 4697->1544ms (-67%), warm send GET 60-200ms -> <10ms, zero non-2xx.

NOT closing: err at tier-200 is still ~100% on this run, but that is the client's 10s abort while a saturated event loop queues sends up to ~30s server-side (max logged send 29788ms) on a heavily-contended shared box (concurrent agent builds) with the dead port defeating the 60s ScanCache — NOT a clean "comes clean" measurement. Residual is cold first-derivation + box contention + harness artifact; the clean production lever is cairn-1q4b Stage 2 (worker/async scan). Re-measure on a quiet box + live Electrum before closing.

title: Wallet detail pages: collapse technical/export block behind disclosure
description:
Progressive disclosure for the single-sig (src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte)
and multisig (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte) wallet detail pages.
Both pages exposed always-visible technical depth: the receive panel's raw
derivation path, and a long "Export config" explanatory paragraph + button row +
format-notes caption.

Fix: reused the multisig detail page's existing .disclosure/.disclosure-toggle/
.chev/.disclosure-body convention (already used there for the receive panel's
derivation path, itself noted as matching "the multisig-new wizard's convention").

- Single-sig receive panel: moved the always-visible derivation path span into a
  new collapsed "Advanced" disclosure (showReceiveAdvanced), bringing it to parity
  with the multisig page which already collapsed this.
- Export config section (both pages): section header (title + downloaded/not-
  downloaded badge) stays always visible; the explanatory paragraph, download
  button row, and format-notes caption now collapse behind a
  "Show/Hide export options" disclosure toggle (showExportDetails), default
  collapsed.
- Added matching .disclosure CSS to the single-sig page's <style> block (copied
  from the multisig page, which already had it).
- Left the header sign-note's truncated xpub and the breadcrumb's SCRIPT_TYPE_LABELS
  friendly label as-is -- both are compact single-line identifiers already using
  plain-language labels, not the "long explanatory block" this item targets, and
  touching them risked the "keep Send/Receive/receive QR/address exactly as-is"
  constraint. Flagging here in case a future pass wants to fold those in too.

No data removed -- everything still reachable, just collapsed by default.
Verified: npx svelte-check reports 0 errors/warnings across both files.
close_reason: Shipped: both wallet detail pages now collapse the derivation-path + export-config block behind a disclosure toggle, matching the pre-existing multisig receive-panel convention. svelte-check clean (0 errors).

title: Settings section nav omits spec'd Health section
description:
Desktop-layout QA (branch design/evergreen-identity, commits 489e23f..9f0e636).

docs/DESKTOP-LAYOUT-DESIGN.md sec.4 Settings specifies the page-local section nav as: Display, Security, Notifications, Health, Advanced -- and sec.4 further states 'Health: its duty breakdown renders as an instrument panel, consistent with sec.3.2 density-cluster treatment.'

Shipped settings section nav (verified at /settings, 1440x900) is: Account, Display, Notifications, Security, Advanced, About. There is NO Health section anywhere: body text contains 0 'health' matches, and /settings/health returns 404. The section nav uses in-page anchors (#set-account etc.).

Impact: low. Not a layout break -- settings otherwise renders correctly at reading measure (780px cap; nav 150px + content 478px inside the lane; no overflow at any tier; section nav hidden and content stacked on mobile 375). This is a spec-completeness gap: the Health duty-breakdown instrument panel called for by the spec is absent from the settings surface.

Context: memory indicates Settings/Health was an open item from a prior epic (gt05.2-4) and may have been deferred rather than dropped by this overhaul. Filing P3 so it is tracked against the canonical desktop-layout spec. Verify against product intent -- if Health-in-settings is intentionally deferred, close with that note.

title: [wallet-pool] Explorer pool-blind: instance-found blocks never recognized — no 'found by this pool/you' on block detail
description: mining_blocks (block_hash, coinbase_txid, user_id, wallet_id, coinbase_value_sats) is read ONLY inside src/lib/server/mining/; explorer/block/[id]/+page.server.ts queries no mining data; identifyPool() (chain/pools.ts) matches public pools only (Foundry/AntPool...). The product's emotional payoff — 'our pool found this block' — is unbuilt; a finder gets at most a coincidental wallet-ownership 'Yours' badge. Fix: add findByBlockHash/Height accessor in mining/readModels.ts, pass poolFound {finderName,isYou,rewardSats,walletHref} to block detail, render a growth-green 'You found this block — [wallet] earned N sats' banner per DESIGN-MANIFESTO (green=growth).

title: Fiat Hidden setting leaks on wallet-detail pages (tx rows, addresses, fee lines)
description:
Alex device testing (2026-07-16, design/evergreen-identity @ 8dd0ae6): with Settings -> Display -> Fiat display set to Hidden, wallet detail pages (single-sig wallets/[id] and multisig wallets/multisig/[id]) still show dollar amounts ($0.07, $207.73, $2,575.91, $1,455.12, $0.86, $1.31) on tx rows, fee lines, address rows, and the maturing-balance line.

Root cause: Amount.svelte's fiat visibility was never centrally gated by the Settings -> Display 'Fiat display: Hidden/USD shown' toggle (localStorage cairn.fiat). Only the 3 page heroes (Home, wallets/[id], wallets/multisig/[id]) explicitly compute a gated price via gatedFiatPrice() and pass it as an explicit price prop (R6 lazy-fiat change, cairn-d326, v0.2.27). Every OTHER Amount call site on both wallet-detail pages (tx-row amounts, network-fee inline amounts, address-row balances, unconfirmed/maturing inline amounts) passes no price prop at all, so Amount.svelte's default `effectivePrice = price === undefined ? $btcUsd : price` falls through to the raw live-ticking $btcUsd store, which is completely unaware of the Hidden setting. R6 only ever fixed the hero's live-repaint problem, not visibility.

Fix: centralize enforcement in Amount.svelte itself so no call site (present or future) can leak fiat regardless of whether it remembers to pass a gated price prop.
close_reason:
Fixed and verified. Root cause: Amount.svelte's fiat price resolution had no awareness of the Settings -> Display 'Fiat display: Hidden' toggle -- only the 3 page heroes (Home, wallets/[id], wallets/multisig/[id]) computed their own gated price via gatedFiatPrice() and passed it explicitly; every other Amount call site (tx-row amounts, network-fee inline amounts, address-row balances, maturing/unconfirmed inline amounts) passed no price prop and fell through to the raw live-ticking $btcUsd store, leaking fiat on both wallet-detail pages regardless of the setting.

FIX (central enforcement, not per-call-site patches):
- src/lib/price.ts: new fiatVisible writable store + setFiatVisible(), backed by the same cairn.fiat localStorage key.
- src/lib/format.ts: new resolveAmountPrice(fiatVisible, explicitPrice, liveStorePrice) -- Hidden always wins, even overriding an explicit non-null price prop, per Alex's 'regardless of price prop' requirement.
- src/lib/components/Amount.svelte: effectivePrice now resolves through resolveAmountPrice($fiatVisible, price, $btcUsd) -- the single, central gate every Amount instance app-wide goes through.
- src/routes/(app)/settings/+page.svelte: fiat toggle now writes through the shared fiatVisible store (setFiatVisible) instead of a page-local copy, so the setting propagates live across SPA navigation, not just on hard reload.
- src/lib/components/send/SendReviewCard.svelte: the two bare formatFiat call sites (amountText summary sentence, fiatSuffix Details-expander helper) now also gate on $fiatVisible -- same leak class, different component, same fix.

Tests: 7 new resolveAmountPrice unit tests in format.test.ts (Hidden wins over live store, Hidden wins over explicit price, Hidden wins over explicit null, Visible falls back to live store, Visible honors explicit price/null, both-null stays null). Full suite 3860 passed. npm run check (svelte-check+tsc): 0 errors.

Visual QA (fresh DB, fiatfix.localhost:5470, session-seed pattern, scripts/qa/seed-fiatfix.mjs):
- Hidden (default): zero '$' anywhere on wallets/[id] (hero, unconfirmed inline, maturing inline, tx-row amounts, fee inline, Sending draft amount) across Transactions AND Addresses tabs, and identically on wallets/multisig/[id] (hero, maturing inline, tx-row amounts, fee inline) across Transactions AND Addresses tabs -- verified via get_page_text plus a body.innerText $-regex scan returning null.
- Visible: toggled via the real Settings UI control, then SPA-navigated (client-side, no hard reload) to both wallet-detail pages -- fiat correctly reappeared on every previously-leaking surface (hero $525.59, maturing ($134.60), tx rows +$1,922.88/-$1,262.69, fee ($1.24) on multisig; equivalent on single-sig).
- Live-toggle-without-reload: confirmed by the SPA-nav propagation above; a raw localStorage write (bypassing setFiatVisible) correctly did NOT update the live store, confirming the store, not a localStorage poll, is the source of truth.

Commit: (pending).

title: docs(manual): sync MANUAL.md for v0.2.26-v0.2.29 shipped behavior
description: Repo-root MANUAL.md was stale against five same-day releases (v0.2.26-v0.2.29). Updated sections: explorer route-map row + heartwood component list (NodeTrustChip popover push-down not overlap, cairn-klxj; pending block sorts to top of tip-view list, cairn-lynf); Amount/portfolio component docs (fetch-once-per-navigation fiat snapshot on Home+wallet-detail heroes vs live-ticking default, cairn-d326; new BalanceHorizons multi-horizon growth widget + v0.2.29 changeWithTxFallback retention-purge hardening, cairn-ht11); Send-flow wizard docs (R1 amountInput.ts live dual-unit conversion + isHighSpend >50% amber note, cairn-9nvo; R2 recipientVerify.ts stake-triggered verify-last-4 micro-step, cairn-l7sv; R3 broadcastGrace.ts 5s grace window replacing direct-broadcast Confirm button, cairn-avzs); recovery-setup account-recovery wizard QA procedure (R4 explain-before-reveal + recognition verify replacing old write-it-down-then-recall flow, cairn-ux-r4-7fzr). Added QA-runbook items: 20.17 (horizon accuracy across a data-retention purge), 21.5 grace-window + verify-micro-step checklist bullets, 22.4 item 2 rewrite, 22.5 items 5-6 (trust popover layout, pending block position).

All claims verified against source before writing (amountInput.ts, recipientVerify.ts, broadcastGrace.ts, NodeTrustChip.svelte, portfolio.ts, BalanceHorizons.svelte, recovery-setup/+page.svelte, recognitionVerify.ts).

title: Home activity feed leaks fiat past the privacy-gated hero toggle
description:
Browser QA finding (UX wave 2026-07-12, batch 2, item 5a): the Home hero's
fiat estimate is privacy-gated -- off by default, no /api/price call until
the user clicks "show fiat" (src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte, showFiat/
fetchPrice, deliberately separate from the shared $lib/price store per the
comment at lines 134-139: "no price call until opt-in"). But the Home
activity feed (src/lib/components/portfolio/RecentActivity.svelte) renders
Amount without a price prop, which means it always subscribes to the shared
$btcUsd store and shows fiat regardless of the hero's privacy toggle state --
the privacy gate the hero establishes doesn't extend to the feed directly
below it on the same page. A user who intentionally keeps fiat hidden still
sees dollar amounts in their recent activity.
Needs a product decision: either gate RecentActivity's Amount behind the
same showFiat flag (passing price={showFiat ? $btcUsd : null}), or treat the
hero toggle as hero-only by design and rename/reword it to stop implying
page-wide privacy. Filed for triage, no fix applied (item 5 of the batch --
beads-only, no code changes).
close_reason: Fixed in e5acce1 (integrated): activity feed Amount rows now receive the hero's gated price via gatedFiatPrice(showFiat, usdPrice) in format.ts instead of subscribing to the shared price store; Home-scoped per bead report. 3 new tests.

title: Wallets empty-state 'Restore from a backup' link is identical to 'Add your first wallet'
description: In src/routes/(app)/wallets/+page.svelte (added in a816047, cairn-lun6), the empty-state now shows two buttons: 'Add your first wallet' and 'Restore from a backup', both linking to the same href="/wallets/new". Both land on step 0 (wallet-type picker) — clicking 'Restore from a backup' does not skip ahead to the restore-file-upload affordance (which only appears on step 1, the Key step, for single-sig). The two buttons are currently functionally indistinguishable despite different labels, which is confusing and doesn't actually shortcut the restore journey as the cairn-lun6 bead intended ('make the entry point discoverable from login/onboarding'). Fix: either deep-link to step 1 with the restore box pre-focused (e.g. /wallets/new?restore=1 read on mount), or open the file picker directly from the empty-state link before navigating.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: the wallets empty-state 'Restore from a backup' link now targets /wallets/new?restore=1, which lands directly on the Key step where the restore box is the first affordance (wallets/+page.svelte, wallets/new/+page.svelte).

title: Unify buildDraft/broadcast behind the feeBump spec engine before CPFP forks it a 3rd time
description: transactions.ts (single-sig) and multisigTransactions.ts (multisig) carry near-verbatim buildDraft vs buildMultisigDraft and broadcastTransaction vs broadcastMultisigTransaction. The atomic broadcast-claim UPDATE (transactions.ts:618-627 vs multisigTransactions.ts:473-482) is copy-pasted, differing only by table name + owner column — the multisig file's own comment says 'identical idiom to transactions.ts'. Fable (arch advisor, verified feeBump.ts:91 in-repo): this is the codebase's OWN proven pattern left half-applied — feeBump.ts already genericized RBF-bump + CPFP across both paths via executeRbfBump<TDraft,TDetails>/executeCpfpDraft parameterized by spec:{table,ownerColumn} + a buildReplacement callback, with ZERO lifecycle divergence lost. The copy-pasted broadcast-claim UPDATE is the double-broadcast guard — 'the most dangerous line in the codebase to have two of'; a timeout/predicate fix applied to one table and not the other is a money-losing bug no test on the edited file catches. Rule-of-three trips now: cairn-u9ob CPFP/package-relay would fork it a third time (tryPackageRescue at transactions.ts:509 is already single-sig-only). ABSTRACTION: do NOT build around 'single-sig = M=1 multisig' (leaky — script type, PSBT finalization, signer coordination genuinely differ, breeds if(n===1) branches). Build around 'a spend record has one lifecycle' (draft->claimed->broadcast->confirmed) parameterized by storage location, construction/signing injected as callbacks — exactly what feeBump embodies. SCHEMA stays split (deliberate). SEQUENCE: (1) extract broadcast-claim UPDATE + broadcastTransaction into the spec engine, (2) unify buildDraft persistence, (3) THEN build CPFP against the engine once, (4) do NOT start CPFP on the duplicated code.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: CORRECTION 2026-07-07: cairn-u9ob (CPFP/package-relay) is ALREADY CLOSED (built+shipped 2026-07-06), and it was built ON the generic feeBump engine (executeCpfpDraft/executeRbfBump), so CPFP did NOT fork buildDraft/broadcast a third time. The 'unify before CPFP forks it again' timing driver is therefore MOOT. This bead remains VALID as latent-safety tech-debt — the copy-pasted broadcast-claim UPDATE (the double-broadcast guard) still exists in two places and a fix to one and not the other is a money-losing bug — but it is NO LONGER time-gated by an incoming feature. Reclassify: background / when-touching-the-broadcast-path, and pair it with the next change that lands on transactions.ts or multisigTransactions.ts. Priority stays P2 on latent-risk grounds, but it does NOT jump the security/robustness backlog.

title: resetInstance() factory reset leaves instance-wide events (user_id IS NULL) behind, contradicting the 'nothing else survives' claim in the Settings UI
description: src/lib/server/admin.ts resetInstance() runs DELETE FROM wallets/invites/sessions/users/settings in a transaction and relies on ON DELETE CASCADE (users(id)) to clean up everything else. But src/lib/server/db.ts L424 defines events.user_id as nullable with a comment 'NULL = instance-wide' — rows with user_id IS NULL have no FK target to cascade from, and resetInstance() never issues a DELETE FROM events. Confirmed by reading src/lib/server/activity.ts: recordActivity() writes instance-wide rows (userId omitted/null) for things like chain/electrum switches, backups, restores. src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte's danger-zone copy tells the admin a reset 'deletes all users, sessions, wallets, and invites... nothing else survives' — that's false for instance-wide activity history, which a NEW operator (or the same operator post-reset) will see in /admin/activity, including entries like 'instance restore applied' from the prior operator/session. Fix: add DELETE FROM events; to resetInstance()'s transaction (or DELETE FROM events WHERE user_id IS NULL if any events should be considered structural rather than history).
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Factory reset leaves instance_secrets + global feature flags intact
description:
resetInstance (src/lib/server/admin.ts:115-148) never deletes the instance_secrets table (which holds smtp_pass, core_rpc_pass, telegram_bot_token, nostr_sender_privkey; see db.ts:1160). feature_flags rows also survive the reset, just with updated_by nulled out. UI copy and docs/RECOVERY.md:49 both claim a factory reset wipes everything.

This is a confidentiality leak on operator handover / device resale scenarios: SMTP credentials, Core RPC password, bot tokens, and Nostr private keys persist on a 'reset' instance that the next operator believes is clean.

Fix: resetInstance must also clear instance_secrets and feature_flags (or explicitly document what factory reset does and does not wipe, and fix the UI/RECOVERY.md claims to match).
close_reason: Fixed: resetInstance() now deletes instance_secrets and feature_flags outright. Commits 6be9373 (fix + tests), 41a9f39 (MANUAL.md). 26/26 targeted vitest passing. See evidence comment for full root cause + test detail.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: RUNTIME CONFIRMED (2026-07-12, cairn-preview-fresh port 4199, dev-fresh.db, preview build), twice: instance_secrets.smtp_pass survived two consecutive factory resets while all smtp_* settings rows were wiped. Worse: the fresh admin's SMTP settings UI shows "A password is stored. Leave blank to keep it, or clear it." -- actively inviting the new operator to reuse the prior operator's orphaned secret (silently reused if a host is saved with password blank). feature_flags row (explorer, enabled=0) also survived with original seed timestamp.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Priority raised P2 -> P1: the SMTP settings UI copy ("A password is stored. Leave blank to keep it, or clear it.") actively invites the new operator to silently reuse the prior operator's orphaned secret post-reset, turning a confidentiality leak into an active credential-reuse trap on operator handover / device resale.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Pinned as KNOWN GAP in destructiveOps.test.ts: readSecretSetting('smtp_pass') survives resetInstance; feature_flags rows survive. Test flips when fixed.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
FIXED (commits 6be9373 admin.ts/destructiveOps.test.ts, 41a9f39 MANUAL.md, branch single-sig-full-wallet).

Root cause: resetInstance() (src/lib/server/admin.ts) deleted users/sessions/wallets/invites/settings/events/notified_txids/announcements/multisig_service_referrals but never touched instance_secrets (no user_id to cascade from) and only nulled feature_flags.updated_by instead of deleting the rows.

Fix: resetInstance() now runs 'DELETE FROM feature_flags' (before DELETE FROM users, to satisfy the NO-ACTION updated_by FK -- doing it after throws FOREIGN KEY constraint failed, caught during implementation) and 'DELETE FROM instance_secrets' inside the same transaction. This wipes smtp_pass, core_rpc_pass, telegram_bot_token, nostr_sender_privkey, and scheduled_backup_pass (backup.ts) in one shot since they all live in the same table. Per-user secrets (personal SMTP password embedded in notification_channel_config.config, secretKey.ts envelope) were already correctly cascaded via notification_channel_config.user_id ON DELETE CASCADE -- confirmed via new test, not a missed store.

Tests: destructiveOps.test.ts -- flipped the pinned KNOWN GAP test (now asserts instance_secrets/feature_flags are 0 rows post-reset) and added a new table-driven test enumerating all 5 instance_secrets keys plus the per-user encrypted-SMTP case, run via targeted 'npx vitest run src/lib/server/destructiveOps.test.ts src/lib/server/admin.test.ts': 26/26 passed.

Docs: MANUAL.md gets a new Factory reset (resetInstance(), admin.ts) section under 6. Server: Database Layer, documenting the fixed wipe behavior and the delete-order constraint.

Not closing per orchestration instructions -- leaving open for integration review.

title: Good-Umbrel-citizen UX conformance: unified auth + backup coherence + node auto-detect
description: From docs/COMPETITOR-ANALYSIS-R2-2026-07-19.md sec5 (Alby Hub / umbrelOS) + sec7 rec #5. Make Cairn feel native to Umbrel, not merely running on it, as an explicit checklist: (1) honor umbrelOS unified auth/session so an Umbrel user is not double-gated by a second Cairn login wall (respects standing cairn-auth-model rule: keep password mode, do NOT remove password auth); (2) make Cairn's wallet-config backup coherent with and legible against umbrelOS's hourly encrypted backups rather than a parallel/competing scheme - Health copy should acknowledge the platform layer where true ('your wallet setup is included in your Umbrel backup') instead of implying Cairn is the only safety net; (3) keep Core-RPC/node auto-detection the default (Alby Hub auto-wires to the native node). VERIFY before any Health copy claims it: whether Cairn app data is automatically included in a umbrelOS backup vs requiring app-level opt-in. Touches Health surface (spec sec2.6a), auth/session layer, update path.

title: Per-user SMTP host has no internal-range validation — blind LAN port scanner
description: channels/email.ts:237-247 — per-user SMTP host validated only for port range/TLS mode. SMTP isn't HTTP so ssrf.ts doesn't apply. No range check before nodemailer connects. Any user can set host=10.x.x.x, hit test-smtp (unthrottled, doesn't require saving) repeatedly — timing gives blind LAN port scanner. Not an open relay (recipient pinned to user's own address). Fix: validate smtp.host through same range policy as ssrf.ts, set connectionTimeout, rate-limit test-smtp.
close_reason: Merged 4418db9: per-user SMTP host validated via checkTargetHost before test-connect and save, closing the blind-LAN-port-scanner oracle.

title: Docs: reverse-proxy/TLS-termination section — X-Forwarded-Proto requirement + ORIGIN/CAIRN_ORIGIN escape hatches
description:
Follow-up from cairn-wrph/cairn-9njl (session cookie stamped Secure over plain HTTP
on unconfigured deployments, fixed 2026-07-13).

The fix makes bare/unproxied server.mjs deployments correct by default (defaults
PROTOCOL_HEADER=x-forwarded-proto per listener, fill-when-absent via
scripts/serverProto.mjs), but a deployment that puts its OWN TLS-terminating reverse
proxy in front of Cairn's plain HTTP port now has a hard requirement: that proxy MUST
set X-Forwarded-Proto itself, or the operator must set ORIGIN (adapter-node) /
CAIRN_ORIGIN (Cairn's own app-level override, src/lib/server/auth.ts cookieSecure())
to their externally-visible https URL. Getting this wrong now fails LOUD (missing
Secure attribute) rather than the old silent failure mode, which is the right
trade-off but needs to be documented somewhere a self-hosting operator will actually
find it before they hit it.

Currently this is only documented in code comments (server.mjs header comment,
Dockerfile note near the ADDRESS_HEADER note, scripts/serverProto.mjs header) and in
the cairn-wrph/cairn-9njl bead history — nothing in user-facing docs.

Scope: add a "Reverse proxy / TLS termination" section to the operator-facing docs
(check docs/ and MANUAL.md — see also memory note "Cairn MANUAL.md" for where the
docs live and the rule that manual/behavior disagreement is itself a bug) covering:
  - The X-Forwarded-Proto requirement for any TLS-terminating proxy in front of the
    plain HTTP port.
  - The ORIGIN (adapter-node) and CAIRN_ORIGIN (Cairn app-level) escape hatches, and
    when to use each.
  - ADDRESS_HEADER / HOST_HEADER's existing analogous requirement (already
    documented in the Dockerfile — cross-reference or fold in for one coherent
    "behind a proxy" section rather than three scattered notes).
  - Umbrel's own docker-compose.yml as the worked example (it already sets
    PROTOCOL_HEADER=x-forwarded-proto, ADDRESS_HEADER=x-forwarded-for,
    HOST_HEADER=x-forwarded-host, and CAIRN_ORIGIN — a concrete reference
    config for anyone building their own reverse-proxy setup).

No code changes — docs only.

title: Extract shared gap-limit scan engine (gapLimitScanner.ts)
description: src/lib/server/bitcoin/gapLimitScanner.ts: runGapScan() with pluggable address derivation + ScanCache<T> replace the duplicated discovery loops, scripthash batching, tx attribution, 60s caches, and six-constant tuning blocks in walletScan.ts/multisigScan.ts. Public scan APIs unchanged.

title: All commits carry an AI co-author trailer — confirm this is intentional for the public repo
description: Every commit so far (14/14) ends with 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>'. This is a defensible, transparent choice (comparable to how Claude Code itself attributes commits) and I'm not flagging it as a defect — but since this pass was asked to specifically check for 'no AI mentions,' I want it surfaced as a decision point rather than silently left or silently scrubbed. Rewriting it now would mean rewriting all existing commit messages (destructive, history-altering), so I'm not doing that unilaterally. If the intent is to keep attribution, no action needed. If the intent is to drop it going forward, that's a one-line change to how commits are authored from here on (existing history would need an explicit rebase decision, separately).
close_reason: Confirmed intentional: Co-Authored-By trailers are deliberate, factual attribution on AI-assisted commits and are required by the contributing tooling's policy; they are commit metadata, not repo artifacts. Whether to strip them via history rewrite before first publication is the owner's call — flagged in the session summary

title: validateCosignerKeyPath does not verify hardened bit on BIP-48 levels
description: multisig.ts:307-330 — checks BIP-48 coin/account/script-type levels by value only via unhardened(), never confirming they are actually hardened. m/48'/0/0'/2' (unhardened coin) and m/48'/0'/0'/2 (unhardened script-type) both pass. Every acceptance test uses fully-hardened paths so the hole is invisible. Impact: recovery/interop confusion with strict external tools. Fix: add hardened-bit check on levels 1-3, add test with unhardened variants.
close_reason: Hardened-bit check added on BIP-48 levels 1-3; half-hardened paths now rejected; tests green.

comments.author: deep-reasoner
comments.text: FIXED. Root cause: validateCosignerKeyPath (bitcoin/multisig.ts) checked BIP-48 coin/account/script-type by numeric value via unhardened(), never the hardened bit — m/48'/0/0'/2' and m/48'/0'/0'/2 both passed. Fix: after length>=4, reject any of levels 1-3 that are < HARDENED with a level-named MultisigError. All-zeros mask early-return still short-circuits; canonical fully-hardened paths still pass. Tests (wallets-multisig.test.ts > 'validateCosignerKeyPath rejects half-hardened BIP-48 paths (cairn-ryjc)'): unhardened coin/account/script-type/fully-unhardened body all throw /unhardened/; canonical passes; half-hardened rejected through createMultisig too.

title: Wallet-detail scanner-error state shows the same message twice back to back
description:
On a wallet detail page when the wallet scanner/Electrum is unreachable, the error panel shows two near-identical lines in a row:
  "Can't reach the wallet scanner"       <- hardcoded heading
  "Could not reach the wallet scanner"   <- refreshError body text
Reads like a copy-paste leftover to a non-technical user (duplicate content, no added information in the second line). Repro: navigate to any wallet detail page (/wallets/[id]) while the Electrum/chain backend is unreachable -- confirmed live at http://qa-a2.localhost:5344/wallets/2 during QA session qa-2026-07-12b.

Source: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:70,76 (refreshError = 'Could not reach the wallet scanner') and :614 (hardcoded heading "Can't reach the wallet scanner"). Same duplicate pattern in src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte:66,72,531.

Suggested fix: either drop the hardcoded heading and let refreshError be the single message, or differentiate the two lines (e.g. heading + a more specific/actionable body like a Retry hint or timestamp of last successful sync).

Severity: P3 -- cosmetic/copy polish, not blocking. The surrounding error state is otherwise good (clear top-level banner "Heartwood isn't connected to the Bitcoin network yet... Ask your instance operator", a working Retry button).

title: notify() can silently drop external-channel delivery after in-app record succeeds
description: src/lib/server/notifications.ts, notify() (around lines 172-206): the outer try/catch wraps both the in-app activity write and the entire external-channel enqueue loop. If the DB insert throws partway through enqueueing, the in-app record already succeeded but no external channel (email/telegram/ntfy/nostr/webhook) ever receives the alert -- visible only as one generic log.error line, and notify() returns void so callers have no way to detect partial failure. Affects tx_large, security_failed_login, admin_restore, backup_missing, key_health_due. rateLimit.ts's noteLoginFailure() calls notify() with no local guard, compounding the risk. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

The centerpiece of v1: every piece of data should be transparent and educational, so that someone new to Bitcoin understands what they are looking at after a few minutes of browsing.

Includes an introductory 'what is a block' panel, miner/pool identification from the coinbase, fee statistics (total, average rate, range) with an explanation of why fees exist, a highlighted coinbase transaction with subsidy + fees = reward breakdown, and a confirmations count explaining why more confirmations mean more settlement assurance. Explains change outputs by analogy, shows fee as the input/output difference with sat/vB rate, indicates SegWit usage and the size vs virtual-size distinction, flags RBF (sequence-based) replaceability, and renders confirmation status as a 0-6 progress meter. Unconfirmed transactions get an ETA based on current fee rates.

title: Esplora fetch errors swallow the real cause (DNS/TLS/refused) behind generic 'fetch failed'
description:
src/lib/server/chain/esplora.ts's fetchPath() caught fetch() failures and used only e.message, which for Node's global fetch (undici) is always the generic 'TypeError: fetch failed' -- the actual underlying reason (DNS ENOTFOUND, ECONNREFUSED, TLS cert error, timeout) lives in e.cause and was being discarded. This made 'Can't reach chain data sources' on the Explorer/Home/mempool/difficulty pages, and the Admin Settings 'Test connection' badge for the Esplora backend, diagnostically useless -- impossible to tell DNS failure from blocked egress from a bad URL.

Found while investigating a real user report of the Explorer page failing to reach chain data on their Umbrel install even with electrs already connected. Electrum and Esplora are separate dependencies -- electrs alone does not satisfy the Esplora HTTP dependency the Explorer/Home/mempool/difficulty pages need (see src/lib/server/chain/index.ts header comment). Default esplora_url is the public https://mempool.space/api; default electrum host is electrum.blockstream.info:50002 -- two separate endpoints, and the user reported both public servers unreachable.

Fix: added a fetchErrorDetail() helper in esplora.ts that walks the .cause chain (up to 3 levels) and joins each level's message, so errors like 'Esplora GET /blocks/tip/height failed: fetch failed: getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND mempool.space' now surface the real cause instead of bare 'fetch failed'.
close_reason: Fixed in src/lib/server/chain/esplora.ts (uncommitted working-tree change): added fetchErrorDetail() helper that walks e.cause up to 3 levels and joins each level's message, replacing the old 'e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)' extraction in fetchPath()'s catch block. Verified live: started cairn-fresh dev server, logged in, set Admin Settings esplora_url to a nonexistent hostname, clicked Test connection -- badge changed from what would have been bare 'fetch failed' to 'Esplora GET /blocks/tip/height failed: fetch failed: getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND this-host-does-not-exist-cairn-perf-test.invalid'. Ran full chain.test.ts suite (18/18 pass) and tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json (no new errors, only a pre-existing unrelated chainEpochs.ts svelte-module-typing error). NOTE: this fix makes the error message diagnosable going forward -- it does not by itself fix the user's actual Umbrel connectivity problem (both electrum.blockstream.info and mempool.space reported unreachable from their container), which still needs the real error text from their box (now obtainable via Admin Settings > Test connection or the explorer page's error line) to diagnose as DNS/egress/proxy.

title: Missing regression test: Trezor driver silently swallows multisig-change/cosigner-xpub parse failures (cairn-yaw1)
description: cairn-yaw1 (P2, closed in 7ebd68c) added console.warn logging (confirmed present at src/lib/hw/trezor.ts:1081,1202,1211) when multisig-change verification or cosigner-xpub parsing fails, instead of silently discarding the exception. No test asserts these warnings fire — trezor.test.ts only checks the resulting wrong_device error path. Add a test: spy on console.warn (or the logger, if migrated), feed a malformed cosigner xpub into selectMultisigKeyForDevice and a bad multisig-change scenario, and assert a warning is logged in each case.
close_reason: Committed in 81181db: Trezor warn-not-swallow tests, signPsbtWithTrezor + signPsbtWithJade (scripted device + PIN-relay fetch) coverage, sign-method list extracted to pure signMethods.ts with unit tests (capability-false renders disabled-with-reason, never vanishes).

title: Legacy umbrel-community-app-store is stale at v0.2.9 while heartwood-app-store shipped v0.2.15
description: 0.2.15 smoke test (cairn-4pio follow-up) found two competing Umbrel community-store repos both distributing Cairn/Heartwood: (1) C:/dev/umbrel-community-app-store (caravan-store-cairn / caravan-store-heartwood app ids, port 3211) — last touched 2026-07-11, manifest still pinned at v0.2.9, README still tells users to install 'Cairn (caravan-store-cairn)'; (2) github.com/AlexM223/heartwood-app-store (heartwood-bitcoin app id, port 3217) — the actual current store, already at v0.2.15 with clean linear history 0.2.9->0.2.15. There is no cross-link, deprecation notice, or migration note in the old repo pointing users at the new one. Anyone who added the old community-store URL to their Umbrel (documented in older release notes / this repo's own README) is stuck on 0.2.9 forever with a stale app id/port, unaware a maintained v0.2.15 store now exists elsewhere. Recommend: either archive/redirect the old umbrel-community-app-store repo's Cairn/Heartwood entry with a README note pointing at heartwood-app-store, or restore it to a synced/tracking state. Confirm with Alex which repo is meant to be canonical going forward.

title: Instance backup omits multisig_shares -- restore loses all collaborative-custody sharing
description:
buildBackup (src/lib/server/backup.ts:64-83) captures 8 tables but not multisig_shares (db.ts:956), the table that records which cosigners a multisig wallet is shared with. restoreBackup never recreates rows in this table, and the omission is silent -- it is not surfaced in RestoreSummary.

Failure scenario: an owner takes an instance backup, later restores it (new instance, disaster recovery, or migration), and every multisig wallet that was shared with cosigners comes back as unshared -- collaborative custody relationships are silently severed with no warning during or after restore.

Fix: add multisig_shares to buildBackup's captured tables and to restoreBackup's recreation logic, and surface it in RestoreSummary. Cross-reference cairn-a857 (mid-operation disruption tests) and cairn-684u (cascade test-gap) as related but distinct.
close_reason: buildBackup+restoreBackup now capture/recreate multisig_shares and multisig_keys.assigned_user_id, surfaced on RestoreSummary.shares; tests+MANUAL updated. See comment.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: RUNTIME CONFIRMED (2026-07-12 battery, cairn-preview-fresh port 4199, dev-fresh.db, preview build): backup captures exactly 8 tables (users, wallets, multisigs, multisig_keys, ledger_multisig_registrations, saved_addresses, tx_labels, settings). multisig_shares confirmed excluded and not in RestoreSummary. NOTE: multisigs + multisig_keys DO round-trip -- the wallet config itself is preserved; only shares are lost.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Pinned in backupRoundTrip.test.ts: buildBackup captures no multisig_shares; restore yields 0 shares; also pinned absent notification prefs/config, contacts, address_labels, device_keys, user flag overrides (cairn-ldhm overlap).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: FIXED. Root cause: buildBackup captured 8 tables but not multisig_shares; restoreBackup never recreated it and RestoreSummary had no field for it, so a restore silently severed every collaborative-custody share. Also found the sibling gap: multisig_keys.assigned_user_id (the per-key collaborator assignment) was captured via SELECT * but never re-inserted on restore. Fix (all in backup.ts): (1) BackupData gains multisig_shares; buildBackup selects it. (2) restoreBackup recreates each share after BOTH the users and multisigs loops, remapping all three refs (multisig_id, owner_id, shared_with_id); a share whose multisig or either account was not itself restored is skipped (no valid endpoint), matching how wallets/multisigs for skipped users are dropped. (3) insertKey now carries assigned_user_id, remapped via userIdMap (NULL if that account was not restored). (4) RestoreSummary.shares added, surfaced in api/admin/restore (detail.sharesRestored) and the admin/backup summary panel. Tests: backupRoundTrip.test.ts flipped from KNOWN-GAP pins to positive assertions (9-table round-trip; restore recreates the share with remapped ids + role 'cosigner' + assigned key -> restored admin); backup.test.ts wipe clears multisig_shares. MANUAL.md Backup&restore section documents the fix. Ran backup+adjacent suites: 116 tests pass.

title: Explorer breadcrumb says 'The timechain' — jargon with no gloss slot (follow-up to cairn-vxbk)
description: cairn-vxbk's jargon-gloss pass (shipped v0.2.38) could not fix the explorer breadcrumb because the EyebrowBreadcrumb component API has no Term-gloss-capable slot; fix = plain-language breadcrumb copy plus a Term-capable slot in EyebrowBreadcrumb; glosses should come from src/lib/termGlosses.ts (single source, added in v0.2.38). Note: an earlier carve-out mistakenly cited cairn-oqg, which is closed with different scope.
close_reason: Added TIMECHAIN_TIP to src/lib/termGlosses.ts; added optional tip prop to EyebrowBreadcrumb.svelte (wraps last path segment in <Term> when provided); wired on explorer/+page.svelte:440. npm run check clean.

title: Add-wallet method list: surface 'Paste public key' above the six hardware options on mobile
description: Visual audit (2026-07-12): on mobile, the Add-wallet wizard Key step (src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte, METHOD_CARDS) stacks: restore box -> multisig hand-off card -> Trezor -> Ledger -> ColdCard -> BitBox02 -> Jade -> Air-gapped QR -> 'Paste public key' (last). A keyless beginner -- the person most likely to paste an xpub from another app -- must scroll past six hardware-brand options first. Fix: on mobile, surface 'Paste public key' and 'Connect a device' as the top two choices, or group the six device brands behind a single 'Connect a hardware wallet' expander. Searched for overlap first: br search for 'paste public key'/'METHOD_CARDS'/'hardware wallet options' returned no matches -- filing new.
close_reason:
Implemented: added data-method attribute to each .method-cell in src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte (line ~798) and a CSS rule '@media (max-width: 900px) { .method-cell[data-method=paste] { order: -1; } }' (line ~1855, the app-wide mobile breakpoint convention used across 34+ other files). This is a grid-item order override: CSS Grid auto-placement sorts items by order value before document position, so on any viewport <=900px wide (confirmed 375x812 target) the Paste public key card is placed first, ahead of the six hardware-brand cards (Trezor/Ledger/ColdCard/BitBox02/Jade/Air-gapped QR), matching the bead's ask that the most keyless-friendly option leads on mobile. Above 900px (confirmed 1280x800 desktop target) the media query does not match, all cells keep the default order:0, and the original DOM order (device brands first, paste last) renders unchanged -- desktop is untouched. METHOD_CARDS is a fixed, unfiltered constant array (verified via grep -- no conditional add/remove of entries before the #each), so paste is deterministically the last DOM child and this rule's target is stable.

Verification: npm run check = 0 errors/0 warnings (1876 files). Live browser visual verification was attempted (registered a throwaway user via POST /api/auth/register/password with existing dev invite code CLAUDE-TEST-2026 against the running 'cairn' preview server on :55467, confirmed /api/auth/me 200) but blocked by the same session-persistence bug tracked in cairn-wrph (fetch()-based login/agreement-accept returns success but the session cookie does not survive -- /api/auth/me flips back to 401, and the agreement accept action redirects to /login instead of persisting, confirmed empty via direct sqlite read of user_agreement_acceptances for the throwaway user). This is the identical blocker already tracked against cairn-5fpb (QA visual re-verification blocked on cairn-wrph). Did not spend further time routing around it; falling back to deterministic code verification (CSS Grid order semantics + confirmed-static DOM order) per orchestrator instruction. Recommend a real screenshot pass of this change ride along whenever cairn-5fpb/cairn-wrph unblocks QA auth.

title: notifyConfig credential stripping only proven for ntfy channel
description: notifyConfig.ts — redactChannelConfig feeds 4 call sites (settings loader, channel API, admin API, account export). Only the ntfy branch is proven by accountExport.test.ts. Webhook branch (secret/secretEnc -> hasSecret) and email branch (smtp.passEnc -> smtp.hasPass) untested. Legacy plaintext fields also untested. getPublicInstanceNotificationSettings (smtp_pass, telegram_bot_token) entirely untested. A regression leaks webhook HMAC secret, personal-SMTP password, or Telegram bot token to browser. Fix: add notifyConfig.test.ts.
close_reason: Added notifyConfig.test.ts covering redactChannelConfig for every ConfigurableChannel: ntfy (encrypted + legacy-plaintext accessToken), webhook (encrypted + legacy-plaintext secret), email/personal-SMTP (passEnc -> smtp.hasPass, no-auth relay, no-smtp-block passthrough), and telegram/nostr (verified their per-user config genuinely carries no secrets, so default passthrough is correct, not a gap). Also covers getPublicInstanceNotificationSettings for smtp_pass and telegram_bot_token, asserting the raw secret never appears in the serialized output.

title: Mobile: app freezes after backgrounding/foregrounding — SSE stream has no visibilitychange handling
description:
## Repro (reported by Alex)

On mobile, switch away from the browser to another app, then switch back to Heartwood. The app freezes / shows an error page — nothing is responsive.

## Root cause

App is SSE-only (no WebSocket usage anywhere — grepped, confirmed). Two SSE endpoints:
- `src/routes/api/events/+server.ts` — new-block stream, 25s server heartbeat.
- `src/routes/api/notifications/stream/+server.ts` — notification badge stream.

Client-side EventSource setup:
- `src/lib/liveBlocks.ts:18` — `const source = new EventSource('/api/events');` inside `onNewBlock()`. **No `source.onerror` handler at all.** Only a `block` event listener (L31) and `.close()` on unsubscribe (L66). A code comment on L10 claims "Native EventSource handles reconnects" — true for ordinary network drops, but the browser's own retry timer is itself throttled/frozen while the tab is backgrounded on mobile, so it doesn't reliably fire on its own.
- `src/lib/components/NotificationPanel.svelte:176` — `source = new EventSource('/api/notifications/stream')`. Same pattern: no `onerror`, no reconnect/backoff logic.
- Consumers wiring `onNewBlock` in `onMount` with no visibility-aware re-subscribe: `src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte:53-54`, `explorer/+page.svelte:70`, `explorer/mempool/+page.svelte:43`, `wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:64`, `wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:787`, `wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte:67`, `wallets/multisig/[id]/send/+page.svelte:78`.

**`document.visibilitychange` / `pagehide` / `pageshow` / `online`/`offline`: not used anywhere for SSE lifecycle** (grepped the whole client codebase). The only `document.hidden` reference at all is `explorer/mempool/blocks/+page.svelte:53`, which just skips a `setInterval` poll while hidden — unrelated to EventSource, and still has no visibilitychange listener (checks `document.hidden` inline inside an existing timer tick).

No global error/crash boundary either — no `window.addEventListener('error'|'unhandledrejection')` anywhere in `src`; only SvelteKit's routing-level `+error.svelte`, which doesn't fire for a stuck runtime connection.

## Theory

Mobile Safari/Chrome suspend background tabs and tear down the SSE stream's underlying connection. `EventSource`'s native auto-reconnect is timer-driven and gets throttled along with the rest of the backgrounded tab's JS — it doesn't reliably reconnect until the tab regains a full scheduling slice, which may never happen without some other trigger. On foreground, since nothing listens for `visibilitychange`/`pageshow`, nothing proactively checks `source.readyState` or forces a fresh subscription/data refetch. State only updates via `invalidate('cairn:chain')` triggered from the `block` SSE event, so once the passive channel goes silent the UI simply stops receiving updates and appears frozen — no fallback poll, no manual reconciliation, no forced reconnect on resume.

## Fix

Add a `document.visibilitychange` (or `pageshow`) listener wherever EventSource connections are created (`liveBlocks.ts`, `NotificationPanel.svelte`) that, on becoming visible again: closes any existing EventSource, opens a fresh one, and triggers an immediate data refresh/invalidate so state catches up even if some `block` events were missed while backgrounded. Also add `source.onerror` handlers so a dead connection is detected and can be proactively replaced rather than silently relying on the browser's own (throttled) retry.
close_reason: liveBlocks.ts: visibilitychange reconnect + 90s staleness timer + reconnect() helper. Preserves throttle logic across reconnects. Cleanup tears down all listeners/timers.

title: Code-organization consistency: partial subdomain folders + confusable backup.ts/backups.ts naming
description: Low-priority cleanup found during the arch audit. (1) src/lib/server/ has SIX subdomain folders (bitcoin/, chain/, electrum/, channels/, featureFlags/, wallets/) but wallets/ holds ONLY multisig.ts while its single-sig sibling wallets.ts sits flat one level up, and 8 other multisig-domain files (multisigTransactions/Export/Scan/Shares/Roster/Registrations/BackupPdf) + 6 notify-domain files stay flat — a newcomer sees folders, assumes full grouping, then hits 100+ flat files. Either commit to folder-per-domain (multisig/*, notify/*, wallets/singlesig.ts) or drop the partial folders; the half-measure is worst. (2) backup.ts (instance backup/export/restore + scheduled-backup watcher) vs backups.ts (per-wallet 'has this been backed up?' reminder tracking) — singular/plural is the ONLY distinguishing signal for two unrelated responsibilities; rename backups.ts -> backupReminders.ts. (3) wallets-multisig.test.ts breaks the camelCase test-file convention (walletsMultisig.test.ts). CORRECTION for memory: the prior review's 'Ledger/BitBox/Jade signer pairs still unmerged' is STALE — they ARE merged (one component per device, shared via optional multisig prop); gap-limit scanning is also already deduped via bitcoin/gapLimitScanner.ts runGapScan/scanChainAddresses<T>.

title: Coin control: manual UTXO selection
description: Coin selection in constructPsbt (src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts, selectUTXO call) is always automatic/greedy -- there is no way for a user to pick specific UTXOs to spend from. Useful for privacy (avoiding linking unrelated deposits) and deliberate dust consolidation. Small-medium effort: ConstructParams would need an optional UTXO allowlist, and the Create step needs a UTXO picker UI (checkbox list, probably behind a disclosure toggle so it doesn't clutter the default flow).
close_reason: Shipped: onlyUtxos allowlist in constructPsbt (restriction of candidate set, not exactInputs; distinct no_utxos/insufficient_funds errors; send-max over subset tested), CoinControl.svelte disclosure with per-coin checkboxes and selected totals, UTXO list loaded from the cached scan. Live-preview verified: only the allowlisted coin was spent.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified with a real emulator round trip (commit 38679b2), per standing instruction to run PSBT-construction-touching changes through the regtest+emulator infra.

Funded wallet 4 with 3 distinct regtest UTXOs (30,000 / 68,530 / 20,000 sats). Two checks:

1. Restriction is real, not a preference: built a draft allowlisting only the 30,000 and 20,000-sat UTXOs. Selection correctly picked just the 30,000-sat one (sufficient alone) rather than needing both -- normal minimal-selection semantics preserved within the allowed subset, as designed.
2. Proved the allowlist is a hard restriction: built a draft allowlisting ONLY the 20,000-sat UTXO and requested 25,000 sats -- correctly rejected ("The selected coins don't cover that amount plus the network fee"), even though the untouched 68,530-sat UTXO sitting in the same wallet could easily have covered it. Coin control never silently reaches outside the allowed set.
3. Signed the resulting coin-controlled draft with the real Trezor emulator (genuine device round trip, same as the RBF verification) and broadcast for real -- confirmed via the real regtest chain.

No issues found. Full suite: 366/366 passing.

title: disabled sole admin can self-delete leaving zero admin rows
description: Last-admin guard (accountDeletion.ts:38 + admin.ts adminCount()) filters is_admin=1 AND disabled=0, so a DISABLED sole admin is unguarded — deleteOwnAccount succeeds leaving no is_admin=1 rows at all; recovery only via direct DB intervention. Pinned as KNOWN GAP in destructiveOps.test.ts Group 2. Evidence on branch test/qa-wave-2026-07-12, worktree C:\dev\cairn-testqa.
close_reason: Fixed in 6aff163: server-side user-deletion path hardened (FK-clean purge, robust last-admin guard incl. disabled sole admin, shared-multisig owner-deletion guard + cosigner notification). 46 targeted tests green.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: FIXED in 6aff163 (branch single-sig-full-wallet). Root cause: the last-admin guard in accountDeletion.ts and admin.ts adminCount() counted only is_admin=1 AND disabled=0, so a DISABLED sole admin was unguarded and deleteOwnAccount/deleteUser left zero admin rows. Fix: new deletionOrphansAdmins() in userDeletion.ts refuses to delete an admin when (a) no OTHER admin ROW of any kind would remain (covers the disabled sole admin), OR (b) the target is itself a usable admin and no OTHER usable (enabled) admin remains (preserves the pre-existing 'can't delete the last active admin' behavior even when a disabled admin row also exists). Both entry points now use it. Evidence: destructiveOps.test.ts Group 2 KNOWN GAP flipped to green ('a DISABLED sole admin can NOT self-delete or be deleted into a zero-admin instance'), plus a predicate matrix test in userDeletion.test.ts. Targeted vitest: 46 passed.

title: backup.ts restoreBackup() silently skips malformed/duplicate rows, zero logging
description: src/lib/server/backup.ts lines 241, 284, 305, 325 (per-row insert loops for wallets/multisig keys/Ledger registrations/addresses in restoreBackup) all catch and skip a malformed/duplicate row with only a code comment, no log call. An admin performing a break-glass restore has zero visibility into which rows were silently dropped or why -- the RestoreSummary just shows lower counts than expected. Fix: log.warn each skipped row with enough context (table, reason) to diagnose after the fact. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).

title: Literal %sveltekit.head% text renders on every page

[P0] cairn-twc3 — .btn-primary (button AND anchor variants) renders as unstyled browser-default control on login (bg 240,240,240/black/black border) and Home's Send CTA (transparent bg, default link-blue text rgb(0,0,238)), in BOTH themes. --accent/--on-accent vars resolve fine; the CSS rule exists verbatim; something still overrides it. .btn-secondary is unaffected. One-line token fix.

5. [OK] Login light: bg rgb(243,239,231) = #f3efe7 exact match; body-text contrast 13.47:1 (AA/AAA pass). Button contrast not meaningful given finding #1 (unstyled, not themed).
6. [OK] Home (authenticated, qa-home-solo user): sats-first confirmed — "0 sats" primary slot, "0.00 BTC" secondary slot. Zero console errors. Zero old-palette color hits (rgb(232,147,90)/rgb(16,13,11)) walked across visible elements.

8. [Inconclusive] tabular-nums on hero amount: one sampled element showed fontVariantNumeric: normal, not tabular-nums — low-confidence single sample, needs a targeted pixel-QA follow-up rather than a filed bug.
9. [OK] Explorer dark: confirmed real theme bg rgb(14,19,18) applied at <body> (ancestor-chain walk from a live block-list element). 34 SVG-based visualization components render, zero console errors. Explorer light: theme-toggle mechanism verified working (see #10) but I did not re-measure Explorer's own computed colors under light before moving on — not independently re-certified for light.

title: Missing regression test: multisig PSBT-substitution guard untested on the multisig path (cairn-973)
description: cairn-973 (P1, closed in 40cf0d8) added assertSameTransaction() to guard against a signed PSBT with different outputs than the reviewed draft being accepted/broadcast — wired into both single-sig and multisig upload/broadcast paths. src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.test.ts covers the single-sig path (recipient-swap, amount-change, signing-only-doesn't-trip-guard), but src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisigPsbt.ts's identical call site (around combineMultisigPsbts, confirmed still wired post the 881ee90 shared-handler-plumbing refactor) has NO dedicated substitution test — multisigPsbt.test.ts only covers an unrelated script-type-mismatch guard. Add a test: construct two multisig PSBTs from the same UTXO set with different recipients/amounts, and assert combining/finalizing draft A with a co-signer's PSBT for draft B throws the mismatch error. This is a fund-safety-critical gap on the higher-complexity (M-of-N) code path.

title: [UX-QA f0d423f] Raw ECONNREFUSED/scan error string leaks to UI via scanError (server sanitization)
description:
Follow-up to the client-side patch on ux/receive-fix. The raw chain error string (e.g. 'connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:60401') ORIGINATES server-side and is surfaced verbatim to multiple UI surfaces via the snapshot's scanError field. Per cairn-s17j, raw socket/DNS/refused detail must never reach the user.

RAW-STRING ORIGIN (server, off-limits to the ux session):
- src/lib/server/wallets.ts:439  getWalletDetail catches the scan failure and re-throws:  new Error(e instanceof Error ? e.message : 'Wallet scan failed', { cause: 'unreachable' })  -- e.message is the raw Electrum/socket message, propagated verbatim.
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.server.ts:90-91  catches that and does scanError = e.message  -> raw string into the streamed live snapshot.

CLIENT LEAK (already patched on ux/receive-fix): src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:851-855 interpolated the raw {scanError} after a plain-language lead. Patched to drop the raw suffix and show plain language only. BUT scanError is consumed by other surfaces too (the send banner was one; audit the wallet detail page and any portfolio/explorer error strips that read snapshot.scanError), so the durable fix belongs at the server boundary.

PROPOSED FIX: sanitize at the throw site. In src/lib/server/wallets.ts:439 (and/or a shared helper), map raw connectivity errors (ECONNREFUSED / ETIMEDOUT / ENOTFOUND / socket / TLS) to a stable plain-language message BEFORE they become scanError, so every consumer inherits clean copy. This mirrors the 7fcb734 error-copy Wave B standard which covered broadcast/build paths but not the scan/scanError path. Consider reusing the same error-copy helper introduced in 7fcb734.

NOTE: files are .server.ts / $lib/server -- off-limits to the ux/receive-fix session, hence this bead rather than an inline fix.
close_reason: Fixed in 492845e (integrated): new src/lib/server/chainErrors.ts (classifyChainError/sanitizeChainError, raw error logged server-side) applied at wallets.ts, multisigScan.ts, walletSync.ts refresh, and both receive form actions; downstream API routes inherit. 16 new unit tests, full suite green. Remaining surfaces -> follow-up bead.

title: Invite creation silently clamps out-of-range count/maxUses/expiresDays with no feedback to the admin

The form at src/routes/(app)/admin/invites/+page.svelte has number inputs with min/max attributes but no enforcement of them (a user can still type an out-of-range value and submit). If an admin requests count=200 expecting 200 invite codes, they silently get 50 with no indication anything was capped - the returned form.created array (length 50) doesn't explain why it's short of the requested count. Fix: either reject out-of-range values with a form error (consistent with how registrationMode/connectionMode/electrumPort are validated elsewhere in this same admin area) or return a note in the action result explaining the count was clamped.

title: Electrum reconnect backoff bypassed by ambient requests
description: client.ts:144-148 vs L311-327 — ensureConnected() never checks reconnectTimer. Any request() call triggers a fresh connect attempt, ignoring the scheduled backoff delay. During outage, every user request hammers the dead server. Fix: ensureConnected() should respect the backoff timer and fail fast if a reconnect is already scheduled.
close_reason: Fixed with tests + MANUAL.md updates; see per-bead comments for root cause + evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Fixed in client.ts ensureConnected(): if a reconnect is already scheduled (this.reconnectTimer set) it now fails fast ('...is backing off; try again shortly') instead of dialing a fresh connection on every ambient request. Also reordered onDisconnect() to ARM the backoff timer BEFORE emit('disconnect'), closing the window where a request fired synchronously off the disconnect event still bypassed backoff. Only bites when a subscription-driven reconnect loop is active; a purely lazy client schedules no timer and still reconnects on demand. Test: 'respects the reconnect backoff timer -- an ambient request fails fast instead of redialing (cairn-sp74)' asserts reject /backing off/ and no new handshake.

title: Multisig PSBT combine never enforces SIGHASH_ALL on incoming co-signer signatures
description:
Security audit 2026-07-05 (docs/SECURITY-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md), finding F1. Verified CONFIRMED by 3/3 independent adversarial reviewers.

src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisigPsbt.ts combineMultisigPsbts() (~lines 603-662) validates incoming partialSig entries only for (a) same-transaction commitment via assertSameTransaction (psbt.ts ~lines 682-733, which compares serialized input/output lists and never reads signature bytes) and (b) that the signing pubkey belongs to the multisig's own keys. The signature bytes themselves -- including the trailing sighash-type byte -- are copied through unexamined. multisigPsbtProgress (quorum counting) and finalizeMultisigPsbt/the underlying @scure/btc-signer finalize path likewise never inspect the sighash byte before assembling and broadcasting the final witness. No test in multisigPsbt.test.ts exercises non-SIGHASH_ALL signatures (its signWith test helper always signs with the library default, SIGHASH_ALL).

Impact: a co-signer (or a buggy/malicious hardware wallet) could supply a signature using a different sighash flag (SIGHASH_SINGLE, SIGHASH_NONE, or an ANYONECANPAY variant) and Cairn would accept, count toward quorum, finalize, and broadcast it without complaint. This does not enable direct fund theft by an outside attacker (assertSameTransaction still pins down the reviewed inputs/outputs before combine, and pubkey membership is checked), but it is a real, unmitigated malleability/scope-creep gap: a SIGHASH_SINGLE|ANYONECANPAY-class signature commits to less than the full transaction and could legally be replayed onto a different transaction spending the same input with different outputs, defeating the very guarantee assertSameTransaction is meant to provide for that input. Verifier consensus rates this medium-to-medium-high severity -- real and worth fixing, but requires a cooperating malicious or buggy signing device to trigger, not remotely exploitable by a pure network attacker.

Fix: in combineMultisigPsbts (and ideally also finalizeMultisigPsbt as defense in depth), reject any partialSig whose trailing sighash byte is not 0x01 (SIGHASH_ALL), with a clear error message mirroring the existing foreign-signature guard style. Add regression test coverage for non-ALL sighash rejection.
close_reason: Fixed in security-audit remediation pass; see commit. All 897 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: ColdCard/QR signer flows don't warn when wallet lacks key-origin data
description:
Page: /wallets/[id]/send (Sign step) — found while live-testing the newly-wired
ColdCardSigner/LedgerSigner/QrSigner components (verifying cairn-5i3 once
patched in locally).

What I did: on wallet 2 ("Legacy wallet", imported via bare xpub with no
master_fingerprint recorded), walked through Create -> Review -> Sign and
tried each hardware-signer method.

What happened:
- Ledger: clicking "Connect Ledger & sign" immediately shows a clear error
  before ever touching WebHID: "This transaction is missing the key-origin
  information the Ledger needs to sign. Re-create it from a wallet set up
  with its master fingerprint." (ledger.ts's accountOriginFromPsbt correctly
  detects the PSBT has no bip32Derivation, since constructPsbt only attaches
  it when wallet.master_fingerprint is known — see src/lib/server/transactions.ts
  ~line 107 and src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts ~line 178.)
- ColdCard: walks the user through all three steps (download unsigned PSBT,
  "sign on your ColdCard", upload signed file) with zero indication that a
  real ColdCard has no bip32Derivation to match against its own keys and
  will very likely be unable to identify/sign the inputs. The user only
  finds out after physically moving an SD card back and forth.
- QR/Animated: same gap — displays the QR immediately and walks through
  "approve on the device" with no upfront warning, even though a real
  SeedSigner/Passport/Jade would hit the identical missing-derivation
  problem.

What should happen: ColdCardSigner.svelte and QrSigner.svelte should perform
the same upfront check LedgerSigner already benefits from (via
accountOriginFromPsbt or an equivalent "does this PSBT have bip32Derivation"
check) and show the same clear message — something like "This wallet doesn't
have key-origin data recorded, so air-gapped/hardware signing isn't
available for it. Use the Generic wallet/file method instead." — before
walking the user through a physical device round-trip that's doomed to fail.

Priority: P2 — not a security issue (no wrong signature could be produced;
the guard downstream still requires valid signatures), but a real UX gap
that sends users through a multi-minute physical hardware dance for a
predictable, detectable failure. Affects any xpub-only imported wallet
without a stored master_fingerprint (at least "Legacy wallet" id 2 in this
instance).
close_reason: Fixed in 0d191b2: shared psbtHasKeyOrigin helper (src/lib/hw/keyOrigin.ts, checks bip32Derivation/tapBip32Derivation on every input, 5 unit tests); ColdCardSigner and QrSigner now show a clear pre-flight warning with a 'Choose another method' action instead of walking the user through a doomed physical round trip. Connected signers (Ledger/Trezor) already failed fast in their drivers.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified fixed by commit 0d191b2 "fix: warn upfront when a wallet lacks key-origin data". Added src/lib/hw/keyOrigin.ts + keyOrigin.test.ts, and both ColdCardSigner.svelte and QrSigner.svelte now check the PSBT's bip32Derivation before walking the user through the physical round trip.

Live-tested on wallet 2 ("Legacy wallet", no master_fingerprint recorded):
- ColdCard: selecting it now immediately shows "This wallet can't sign on a ColdCard directly. It was added without key-origin data (no master fingerprint recorded)... Use the Generic wallet / file method... or re-import the wallet with its master fingerprint." No more walking through download/sign/upload steps first.
- QR: same upfront message, correctly device-specific ("a SeedSigner, Passport, or Jade won't recognize these coins").

Full suite: 280/280 tests passing. Closing.
