title: Admin Settings: 'Test connection' buttons hang forever, never submit
description:
Page: /admin/settings (Node connection > Custom)

What I did: switched Node connection to "Custom", entered a real, working
Electrum server (127.0.0.1:60401, TLS off) and Esplora URL
(http://127.0.0.1:3002) -- both independently verified reachable and correct
(raw electrum JSON-RPC calls to server.version and blockchain.headers.subscribe
both respond in well under 100ms; the Esplora REST endpoint is also live).
Clicked "Test connection" under Electrum server.

What happened: the button shows a spinner and becomes disabled (client state
briefly correct -- `testing = 'electrum'` fires), but it never resolves.
Waited 40+ seconds on one attempt, reproduced again after a server restart
(13+ seconds, well past testElectrum()'s own 8-second internal timeout in
src/lib/server/chain/index.ts). Checked the browser's network log across both
attempts: zero POST requests to `?/testElectrum` ever left the browser. The
dev server's own stdout shows no incoming request either.

Confirmed the button itself is correctly wired at the HTML level: it's a real
`type="submit"` button, `formaction="?/testElectrum"`, genuinely inside the
settings `<form>` (whose own action is `?/save`). Since it's a proper submit
button with a valid formaction and no request ever fires, the most likely
cause is the form's `use:enhance` (or equivalent submission-interception
logic) swallowing/failing the submission before it reaches fetch() -- possibly
throwing before calling `preventDefault`'s paired submit, or awaiting a
promise that never resolves/rejects. The exact same symptom applies to the
mirror "Test connection" button under Esplora (same code shape, not
separately reproduced but very likely to share the bug).

What should happen: clicking "Test connection" should hit the server (the
network tab should show a POST to `?/testElectrum`), and within ~8 seconds
either show "Connected — tip N" or a red failure badge with the error message
-- never hang indefinitely with no visible way to cancel or retry (the button
stays disabled forever, `disabled={testing !== null}` never gets reset since
`testing` state has no path back to null).

Impact: an admin configuring their own node has no way to verify their
Electrum/Esplora settings actually work before saving -- the one feature
built specifically for that (pre-save validation) is completely non-
functional. I worked around it entirely by writing directly to the settings
table via node:sqlite, which is not something a real admin could do.

Priority: P2 -- doesn't block saving/using custom settings (I verified those
still take effect once actually saved, requiring a server restart to
pick up in this dev environment specifically -- reconfigureChain is
presumably meant to hot-apply on Save, separate from this bug), but the
verification UX is completely broken, and a stuck-forever spinner with no
error and no way to retry is a real dead end for the person using it.
close_reason: Fixed: root cause was self-disabling submit buttons cancelling their own form submission. Pending state moved into use:enhance, 20s watchdog, mutual disable. Verified via direct action POST (6ms response) + SSR 200.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Verified fixed: clicking 'Test connection' on Admin > Settings now correctly shows 'Connected -- tip 127' immediately. Retested during the final polish QA pass.

title: Mid-broadcast delete race erases record of real broadcast
description: transactions.ts:912-920 and multisigTransactions.ts:389-399 — delete gates on status but not broadcast_started_at. Between atomic claim and trailing status update, concurrent delete succeeds. Funds moved on network but DB has zero record. Also: deleteMultisigTransaction doesn't exclude superseded status (unlike deleteTransaction). Fix: add broadcast_started_at IS NULL check to delete guard, add superseded exclusion to multisig delete.
close_reason: Landed in 94ddd83. Both deleteTransaction and deleteMultisigTransaction replaced check-then-delete with a single atomic conditional DELETE: WHERE id=? AND wallet_id/multisig_id=? AND status NOT IN ('completed','superseded') AND broadcast_started_at IS NULL, returning changes>0 — closes the TOCTOU against broadcastTransaction's atomic broadcast_started_at claim. Added the missing 'superseded' exclusion to the multisig delete. Verified: broadcast-claim mechanism is broadcast_started_at TEXT on both tables (db.ts:132-137, 346), claimed by broadcast*/released on failure. Tests: transactions+multisigTransactions 55 pass (new: in-flight-claim refusal single+multisig, superseded multisig refusal); svelte-check 0/0.

title: R4: explain-before-reveal backup flow + recognition verify
description:
Implement R4 from docs/UX-PSYCHOLOGY-RESEARCH-2026-07-15.md (finding F7, CHI 2021 Voskobojnikov et al. -- self-custody recovery-phrase anxiety is responsibility-without-confidence).

Target flow: src/routes/(app)/recovery-setup/+page.svelte (the only 12-word phrase reveal in Cairn's watch-only architecture -- bitcoin keys never touch the app, so this account-recovery phrase is the analogous 'seed moment').

Scope:
1. Plain-language stakes screen BEFORE words render: what the words are, what someone with them can do, what losing them means, one storage suggestion. Calm register, never WARNING/skull framing.
2. Recognition-based verify after reveal (pick correct word among distractors for 2-3 random positions) -- currently only a checkbox gate, no verify at all.
3. Keep deferral legitimate -- confirm existing Settings amber recovery-banner (.recovery-banner, --attention tokens) still reflects incomplete/deferred state after the flow changes; do not build a new notification system.
4. Reuse the wizardProgress.ts sessionStorage resume pattern (wallets/multisig/new/_components/wizardProgress.ts is the reference) so reload mid-flow doesn't lose step position -- gate persistence effects on a hydrated flag. Never persist the phrase words, only step position.
close_reason:
Implemented and QA-verified on branch improve/ux-backup-reveal (worktree cairn-ux-r4), full test suite green (3745 passed/1 skipped, unrelated e2e), svelte-check 0 errors.

Scope delivered in src/routes/(app)/recovery-setup/+page.svelte + new _components/recognitionVerify.ts + wizardProgress.ts:
1. Stakes screen (phraseStage='stakes') renders plain-language stakes BEFORE any word is generated/rendered -- phrase fetch only fires on "Show my recovery phrase" click, not on mount. Verified in DOM (desktop + mobile 375x812): no seed words present at this stage.
2. Recognition-based verify (phraseStage='verify'): 2 random positions, 4 options each (1 correct + 3 BIP39-style decoys), Continue gated on all-correct. Verified both positions render exactly one correct option and Continue unlocks only once both are answered correctly (desktop + mobile).
3. Deferral: "Skip for now" leaves recovery incomplete; Settings page still shows the existing amber .recovery-banner ("Finish setting up account recovery" / "Recovery: not set up") -- confirmed at desktop and mobile, no new notification system built.
4. Reload mid-wizard resumes step position via sessionStorage key cairn.recovery-setup-wizard.v1, storing ONLY {step, savedAt} -- confirmed the phrase words and verify answers are NEVER written to sessionStorage or localStorage at any sub-stage (stakes/reveal/verify), on both desktop and mobile. A resume into the phrase step always lands back on the stakes explainer (phraseStage defaults to 'stakes'), matching design intent since the actual secret can't survive a reload.

Security check: grepped the diff for storage writes -- exactly one sessionStorage.setItem call, writing {step, savedAt} only. Zero localStorage writes.

title: Deleted wallets keep firing notifications via address watcher
description: addressWatcher.ts:645-700 — refreshWatches only adds new subscriptions, never removes them. deleteWallet/deleteMultisig dont call back into watcher. Deleted wallet's ~60 scripthash subscriptions live forever until restart, manufacturing new orphaned notified_txids rows and sending notifications linking to 404 pages. Fix: add unwatchWallet/unwatchMultisig methods called from delete functions, or check wallet existence in handleScripthashChange before notifying.
close_reason: Landed in acc0b69. Deleted wallets/multisigs no longer fire notifications: unwatchWallet/unwatchMultisig strip scripthashes from watcher state on delete, refreshWatches now prunes stale entries (covers accountDeletion's FK-cascade path too), and handleScripthashChange guards on walletStillExists() (fail-closed) before notifying + self-prunes. Tests: addressWatcher/wallets 85 pass incl. removal-on-delete (single+multisig) and notify-guard-blocks-and-self-prunes; full suite 1843 pass in-worktree; svelte-check 0/0.

title: addressWatcher.ts reintroduces the exact Tier-0 cairn-j6fv bug in fresh code: tx amounts computed by address-string match, not scriptPubKey
description: src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts (~line 317, handleScripthashChange): 'if (out.address && walletAddrs.has(out.address)) receivedSats += out.value' — walletAddrs is built from Cairn's own MAINNET-ONLY derived addresses (deriveAddress()/deriveMultisigAddress()), while out.address is whatever encoding the configured chain backend reports (bcrt1.../tb1... on regtest/testnet). This is the IDENTICAL bug already diagnosed and fixed today in commit ebcd945 ('fix(cairn-j6fv): compute tx delta by scriptPubKey, not address string') for walletScan.ts/multisigScan.ts — but addressWatcher.ts is NEW code (commit 0a42065, shipped in the same session AFTER the fix) that does not reuse scriptPubKeyHex()/the scriptPubKey-matching pattern at all, reintroducing the exact defect from scratch despite TxVout already exposing a network-independent scriptPubKey field (chain/index.ts) that could have been used directly. Effect: on any non-mainnet backend (the same regtest setup that originally surfaced cairn-j6fv), receivedSats stays 0 for every genuine deposit, tx_large never fires (requires receivedSats>0), and every real payment silently downgrades to a generic 'New wallet activity' notification with no amount — the notification-plan equivalent of the original bug, in new code that shipped without applying the lesson from the fix made hours earlier in the same session. Fix: change addressWatcher.ts to match tx.vout[].scriptPubKey against a Set of scriptPubKeyHex(address) for each watched address, exactly as walletScan.ts/multisigScan.ts now do, including matching prevScriptPubKey for spent-input detection if relevant here.
close_reason: Fixed in fa4baec

title: wallets/multisig/[id] receive/delete actions missing requireUser (cairn-wqkk follow-up)
description: Follow-up from cairn-wqkk's non-admin (app) form-action sweep: src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts's 'receive' and 'delete' actions still use locals.user!.id with no requireUser(event) call (async ({ params, locals, request }) => ... at lines ~133 and ~158), same masked-500-on-anon pattern the rest of the sweep fixed. Not touched in the wqkk sweep because this file is in active concurrent use by another session's multisig stateless-import/FK-integrity work on the same branch — deferred to avoid a collision. Fix: add requireUser(event) as the first line of both actions, same pattern as wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts's receive/delete (fixed in cairn-wqkk).
close_reason: Fixed: added requireUser(event) as first line of receive and delete actions in src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts, matching ce8b03d's pattern (converts masked 500-on-anon into clean 401). Added regression test src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/page.server.test.ts mirroring wallets/new/page.server.test.ts. Targeted vitest run: 3 files / 7 tests passed.

title: Send summary sidebar hardcodes BTC, ignores sats unit preference
description: Follow-up gap in cairn-nb8e (v0.2.38 'unit-respecting amounts' fix). The fix correctly wired AmountEntry.svelte's hero input to respect the hw.unit (sats/BTC) preference, but the adjacent Send-page summary sidebar was missed. Repro (QA server C:/dev/cairn-qa2, port 5192): 1. Settings -> Display -> Units = sats (confirmed active). 2. Go to /wallets//send (single-sig wallet). Amount field correctly switches to sats mode (placeholder '0', unit label 'sats', cycle button offers 'BTC' next) -- this part respects the preference correctly. 3. Type an amount, e.g. 50000 (sats). Hero field and its secondary line correctly show sats-denominated text. 4. The right-rail 'Send summary' aside, however, always renders '{formatBtc(summaryAmountSats)} BTC' for the Amount line (e.g. '0.0005 BTC') and '... BTC' for Remaining, regardless of the sats preference -- literal ' BTC' suffix is hardcoded in the template, not unit-aware. Source: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte line ~1687: {formatBtc(summaryAmountSats)} BTC and the 'Remaining' block further down uses the same pattern. Neither reads $unitPref (src/lib/units.ts) the way AmountEntry.svelte now does. Note: the fiat sub-line in this same summary block (line ~1688) DOES correctly gate on $fiatVisible -- only the BTC/sats unit-respecting part is missing, not the fiat-gating part. See also cairn-8pl9w (fiat-gating gap in AmountEntry itself) -- separate root cause, same QA pass. Expected: Send summary's Amount/Remaining lines should render in sats when Units=sats, matching the hero field above them.
close_reason: Fixed: send/+page.svelte imports unitPref from $lib/units and adds summaryUnitAmount() helper; the summary rail's Amount and Remaining lines now render sats when Units=sats instead of hardcoding ' BTC', matching AmountEntry's hero field. Fiat sub-line untouched (already correct). npm run check clean (0 errors); amountInput.test.ts + format.test.ts (126 tests) pass.

title: Layout runs 13-15 sequential SQLite queries per navigation, none cached
description: +layout.server.ts — every nav fires: mustResetPassword (1q) → disclosure/agreement gate (getUserAgreement 3q + acceptedVersion 1q) → recovery gate (2q) → getInstanceSettings (full table scan + decrypts RPC password) → getUserAgreement AGAIN (3q duplicate) → listUnbackedWallets → shouldShowBackupReminder (2q) → listActiveAnnouncementsFor. None cached, unlike chain/cache.ts TTL pattern used elsewhere. getUserAgreement runs twice. Most likely single largest contributor to nav stutter. Fix: short-TTL cache for instance settings and user agreement, dedupe getUserAgreement, skip RPC password decrypt when only instanceMode needed.
close_reason: Landed in 3dd2b4a; independently reviewed (no blockers/majors). Root cause was the (app) layout load reading url.pathname, forcing a full server re-run of ~13-15 sequential SQLite queries on every client nav. Fix: moved all four gates into a pure appGateRedirect() in hooks.server.ts (scoped by event.route.id startsWith '/(app)'); layout load is now ({ locals }) with zero tracked deps so SvelteKit caches it across navs. Reviewer verified gate order/semantics are a byte-for-byte match, the hooks gate is a strict superset of the old protection (form actions now gated too), no redirect loops, no reachability gap, and caching is UI-data-only (not a security boundary). Full suite 1859 pass; svelte-check 0/0.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: ARCH-AUDIT 2026-07-07 (Opus orchestration + Fable advisor): Found the STRUCTURAL root cause behind the 13-15 queries, and it's a near-free fix superior to per-query caching. The layout load re-runs on EVERY navigation ONLY because it reads url.pathname (line 19, for the /login?next= redirect target). A SvelteKit 2 server layout load that reads a url/params property is re-invoked (server round-trip included) whenever that property changes = essentially every nav; a load that reads only untracked locals is cached and does NOT re-run. Fable's recommended fix: (1) wrap the read as untrack(() => url.pathname) OR drop url entirely and move the auth/disclosure/recovery GATING into hooks.server.ts (which already runs per-request and already computes locals.user) — then flags/instanceMode/operatorName/announcements load ONCE per session and only refresh on an explicit invalidate('cairn:settings') after a mutation. (2) For the reads that must stay, stream the non-critical ones (unbackedWallets, showBackupReminder, announcements) as un-awaited top-level promises after an await setImmediate() so the shell flushes first; keep the redirect gates awaited (they're control flow). Caveat: a non-rerunning layout redirect is UX, not security — keep real authz in hooks + per-endpoint (already the case). This makes cairn-v84z's caching approach a fallback, not the primary fix.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: RECOMMENDED FIX DESIGN (Fable, verified vs hooks.server.ts + layout.server.ts). Move ALL FOUR gates (auth+?next=, mustResetPassword, disclosure/agreement, recovery) out of the layout load and into hooks.server.ts. Then the layout load signature becomes ({ locals }) only — no url/params/depends access — so it has NO tracked dependencies and SvelteKit genuinely CACHES it across client-side navs (the layout node returns type:'skip', body never executes; re-runs only on invalidateAll/matching invalidate/full document load). SHAPE: new pure fn $lib/server/appGate.ts returning a redirect target or null; call it in handle AFTER the isAsset early-return (synergy with cairn-isda) and scope it via event.route.id?.startsWith('/(app)') — NOT pathname string-matching — which auto-excludes /login,/disclosure,/agreement,/setup-admin,/api/*,SSE,assets. TRAP + BONUS: a thrown redirect() in handle becomes a raw 302 that use:enhance's applyAction chokes on for POST form actions, so branch: for non-GET/HEAD return error(401) (same pattern as the existing isAdminMutationRequest backstop). The BONUS is structural: gates now cover FORM ACTIONS, which the layout load NEVER did (actions don't run parent loads — the exact hole documented in hooks.server.ts's isAdminMutationRequest comment and the root cause behind the admin-action P0s cairn-jnlx/cairn-fame). DON'T split the layout data — use:enhance's default invalidateAll() already self-heals the volatile slices (unbacked/announcements/reminder); TTL/streaming is over-engineering once URL-tracking is gone. So the caching approach originally noted on this bead is the FALLBACK; the primary fix is stop-being-URL-tracked via the hook move.

title: Send flow a11y/mobile polish sweep
description:
Batch of smaller independent fixes found auditing the new send/sign flow -- fine to do together:
1. Fee-tier buttons and BTC/Max mode toggle (send/+page.svelte .seg-btn, .fee-card) have short vertical padding (~24-32px effective height) -- under the ~44px WCAG 2.5.5 touch-target guidance, worth padding out on mobile specifically.
2. DeviceCard.svelte disabled state uses opacity:0.6 on the whole card rather than a proper muted color token -- text contrast on the disabled state likely dips below AA; use --text-muted-style tokens instead of blanket opacity.
3. The 'coins being spent' disclosure toggle (send/+page.svelte .utxo-toggle) has no aria-expanded/aria-controls -- screen reader users can't tell it's a toggle or what it controls.
4. QrSigner's animated BBQr frame display has no aria-live status announcing frame progress (e.g. 'frame 2 of 5') for screen reader users, and no text-based alternative for users with prefers-reduced-motion who may have animation-dependent cues suppressed.
close_reason: All four items done: 44px touch targets on .seg-btn/.fee-card via max-width/pointer:coarse media rule and aria-expanded/aria-controls on the utxo disclosure (7c944da); DeviceCard AA-contrast disabled state (40c7417); QR scan aria-live progress + prefers-reduced-motion manual frame stepping (bb33a3c).

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Items 2 and 4 done (bb33a3c, 40c7417): DeviceCard AA contrast, QR scan aria-live + reduced-motion manual stepping. Items 1 and 3 (touch targets, utxo-toggle aria) in flight with the send-page agent.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified items 2 and 4 (commits bb33a3c, 40c7417):

Item 2 (DeviceCard AA contrast) — live-tested: forced a disabled state by patching navigator.hid to undefined so Ledger renders "Unavailable" with its reason text, then measured actual contrast against the card's real background (rgb(26,22,20)): hint text rgb(146,133,122) = 5.0:1, title text rgb(168,158,148) = 6.8:1. Both clear WCAG AA passes (>=4.5:1 for normal text). opacity is 1 on the card now, confirming the blanket-opacity approach is gone.

Item 4 (QR aria-live + reduced motion + paste discoverability) — verified by reading the current QrSigner.svelte source (couldn't drive it live end-to-end, see caveat below):
- "Paste the signed transaction instead" now sits beside "Start camera" up front (scan-actions div), not just after a camera failure.
- aria-live="polite" status region is always mounted in the scan phase, announcing frame progress.
- prefers-reduced-motion is read via Svelte's MediaQuery reactive class; when set, the auto-cycling frame timer is skipped (checked in the same effect that starts the interval) and manual Previous/Next frame stepping buttons appear instead.
This all matches the commit message description and looks correctly wired.

Caveat: I could not drive the actual QR scan/paste UI live end-to-end in the browser this round. psbtHasKeyOrigin() now gates the entire QrSigner render (cairn-sta's fix), and the only wallet with a real spendable balance in this dev environment (wallet 2, "Legacy wallet") has no master_fingerprint, so it always hits the key-origin warning before reaching the scan phase. The only wallet with key-origin data (wallet 4, test vector) has zero balance and can't build a draft. Same test-data gap I've flagged before for the hardware-signer happy path — not a new issue, just noting why this is a code-review pass rather than a click-through for item 4.

Items 1 (touch targets) and 3 (utxo-toggle aria) not yet landed — will test when they land.

title: Accessibility & mobile polish sweep
description:
Batch of smaller, independent a11y/mobile fixes found during audit — fine to do together in one pass:
1. --text-faint (#4a4038 on #1a1614 bg, src/app.css) is ~3.6:1 contrast, fails WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text. Used for secondary labels in wallets, admin/invites, dashboard, explorer pages. Either lighten the token or restrict its use to large/decorative text only.
2. Wallet detail page's banner-dismiss button (src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte, .banner-dismiss) relies on opacity shift for focus with no :focus-visible ring — inconsistent with the app's global :focus-visible rule in app.css.
3. Admin tables (admin/users, admin/invites) have overflow-x:auto wrappers but no visual affordance that they're scrollable on mobile (no breakpoint-specific hint/shadow).
4. Icon-only buttons missing aria-label in several places (e.g. the sidebar logout button in src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte only has a title attribute, which isn't reliably announced by screen readers).
5. Admin invite-creation form (admin/invites/+page.svelte .create-form) has no mobile breakpoint — fields compress awkwardly on narrow viewports instead of stacking.
6. Mempool visualizer tooltip (explorer/mempool/blocks/+page.svelte) uses fixed positioning that can still overflow on very narrow (<256px) viewports.
close_reason: Fixed in 95a585c, all six items plus one the audit underestimated: measured contrast in-browser showed --text-faint at 1.78:1 AND --text-muted at 2.9:1 (worse than the filed 3.6 estimate), so muted was lightened to a verified 4.56:1 on the darkest surface and faint became decorative-only with its four informative call sites promoted; focus-visible ring on the wallet banner dismiss; scroll shadows on all overflowing tables (local-attachment cover technique, appear only when scrollable); aria-label added to the clear-recent control (the flagged logout button already had one); invite form stacks below 560px (verified at 375px); visualizer tooltip clamps to >=8px left and viewport-capped width. svelte-check 0/0, 195 tests green, mobile verified live

title: Admin notifications settings: saving crashes with '.trim is not a function' if SMTP Port field is touched
description: src/routes/(app)/admin/notifications/+page.svelte: smtpPort is declared '$state(init.smtpPort)' (a string from the server load) and bound via 'bind:value={smtpPort}' to '<input id="smtpPort" type="number" ...>' (line ~180). Svelte's bind:value on a <input type=number> always writes back a JS number, not a string, once the user edits the field — but currentBody() (line ~60) unconditionally calls 'smtpPort.trim()' when building the PUT payload for /api/admin/notifications. The moment an admin changes the port (e.g. typing a custom SMTP port), smtpPort becomes a number, and clicking 'Save settings' or 'Save & send test email' throws 'smtpPort.trim is not a function' (surfaces client-side as an uncaught-error banner reading roughly $.get(...).trim is not a function under Svelte 5's compiled runes). Because the exception is thrown synchronously inside currentBody(), which save() calls before the fetch(), the fetch to /api/admin/notifications never happens — so the SMTP settings are silently NOT persisted, and 'Save & send test email' then correctly reports back SMTP is not configured on this instance (a confusing secondary symptom of the same root cause). Reproduced live: started a fresh instance (npm run dev with a clean CAIRN_DB), navigated to /admin/notifications, filled Host/From, changed Port from 587 to 2599 via the Port input, clicked 'Save & send test email' -> got the client-side crash banner and the settings were not saved. Fix: coerce smtpPort to a string in currentBody() (e.g. 'String(smtpPort).trim()'), or declare smtpPort with an explicit string type guard / parse it as a number consistently end-to-end instead of mixing string state with a number-typed input.
close_reason: Fixed: currentBody() in admin/notifications/+page.svelte now uses String(smtpPort).trim() instead of smtpPort.trim(), so editing the Port field (which makes Svelte bind:value write back a number) no longer throws 'smtpPort.trim is not a function' and silently skips the save.

title: Possible race condition: rapid 'Test connection' clicks in admin node settings can silently drop results / reset form
description:
While testing Admin > Settings > Node connection (Custom mode), I observed the following once and could not reliably reproduce it on retry, so filing at low confidence for someone with more time to investigate:

Sequence: switch connection mode to "Custom" (client-side only, not yet saved) -> click "Test connection" next to Electrum server (got "Connected — tip N") -> immediately click "Test connection" next to the Esplora base URL field -> the whole Node connection section reverted to "Public servers" and the Custom-mode fields (Electrum server, Esplora URL, Bitcoin Core RPC) disappeared entirely, discarding the unsaved mode selection.

On a clean retry of the same two-rapid-clicks sequence, the mode did NOT fully reset, but the second ("Esplora") test-connection's result text was silently dropped (button reverted to plain "Test connection" instead of showing "OK — tip N") — suggesting a race between the two forms' `use:enhance` submissions where a slower response overwrites a faster one, or an `invalidateAll()`/reload triggered by one form's success handler stomps the other's in-flight local state.

Suggested investigation: check whether the "Test connection" buttons' form actions call `invalidateAll()` or otherwise trigger the parent `+page.server.ts` load to re-run and overwrite local $state (connectionMode, per-field test results) with stale server data, especially when two test-connection requests are in flight concurrently.
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: per-backend test results are copied into local state as they arrive, so the shared ActionData slot can no longer drop or overwrite the other test's badge; buttons stay disabled while a test is in flight. The one-off full mode reset was not reproducible; local-state preservation removes the suspected stomp path

title: multisig name length cap counts UTF-16 code units not glyphs
description: createMultisig name.length > 60 rejects emoji-heavy names that look ~16 chars. UX nit. Pinned in hostileText.test.ts on branch test/qa-wave-2026-07-12, worktree C:\dev\cairn-testqa.
close_reason: Fixed: createMultisig's name cap in wallets/multisig.ts replaced raw 'name.length > 60' (UTF-16 code-unit count) with graphemeLength() (new shared helper in textGuard.ts, Intl.Segmenter-based grapheme-cluster count) -- a 60-'character' cap now tracks what a user perceives as 60 characters regardless of script or how many UTF-16 units/codepoints a glyph costs. Updated hostileText.test.ts's pinning test (was documenting the bug, now documents the fix) plus added a genuinely-over-60-glyph rejection case. hostileText.test.ts + wallets-multisig.test.ts: 142/142 pass. Commit 7461058.

title: Notifications bell/panel not reachable on mobile nav (375px) — only via full Activity page
description:
Wave-2S mobile QA sweep (qa2 instance, port 5192, http://qa2s2.localhost:5192, 375x812 viewport).

The header "Notifications" bell button (aria-label "Notifications (1 unread)") exists in the DOM but lives inside the desktop-only <aside> sidebar, which is display:none below the mobile breakpoint. Confirmed via DOM query: the button is present but getBoundingClientRect().width === 0 (not rendered) at 375px. The mobile top/bottom nav only exposes Home / Explorer / Wallets / Activity -- no notifications entry point of any kind (no bell icon, no badge on another nav item).

Impact: mobile users have no quick-access notification panel/dropdown -- the only way to see notifications is navigating to the full /activity page, which does show the same underlying events (e.g. "1 event today Â· all quiet" / "Needs a look" badge observed in this session) but loses the lightweight bell/badge affordance desktop users get. Low severity since the information isn't lost, just less discoverable/one extra navigation step on mobile.

Filed by Wave-2S mobile QA sweep, 2026-07-19.
close_reason: Fixed: bell was only mounted inside HWSidebar's <aside class="rail">, which is display:none below 900px, so mobile had zero notification entry points. Mounted a second NotificationPanel instance in MobileTopBar.svelte (src/lib/components/heartwood/MobileTopBar.svelte:119, right cluster before the avatar menu) -- safe to duplicate since liveClient.ts's EventSource is a refcounted singleton and the two mounts are CSS-complementary (never both visible). Also fixed NotificationPanel.svelte's dropdown to open downward (not upward off-screen) when the bell is top-anchored (<=900px), and widened its tap target to ~44px via ::after, matching MobileTopBar's existing avatar/search-icon pattern. Verified in browser at 375x812 (dev server port 5194, seeded QA session): getBoundingClientRect() on the live DOM showed the desktop-rail bell at 0x0 (correctly hidden) and the new MobileTopBar bell at 30x30 width/height, positioned top:16 left:287, with aria-label 'Notifications (1 unread)' correctly reflecting a seeded event -- i.e. the mobile entry point now renders and is tappable. Click-to-open panel interaction was inconclusive in this session (shared/flaky browser pane, verification deferred to QA) but the rendering/reachability fix -- the actual bug -- is confirmed. npm run check: 0 errors 0 warnings.

title: Perf: stream blocking wallet/send loads + chain-data TTL cache (Umbrel lag fix)
description: Architectural fix for persistent Umbrel/ARM navigation lag. Root cause: 4 load() functions still block full page render on synchronous Electrum/esplora round-trips instead of streaming (established pattern already used elsewhere in the app). Full scope, design, and per-track complexity in docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md. Sub-beads are file-disjoint and safe to build in parallel.
close_reason: All 6 children (vknb.1-.6) closed; QA-verified on live Umbrel/ARM hardware 2026-07-08 (vknb.6). br epic status --eligible-only confirms eligibility. Closed per 2026-07-12 beads audit recommendation #3.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: ARCH-AUDIT 2026-07-07: Two additional levers beyond the streamed loads. (1) Dashboard portfolio bypasses SvelteKit's load cache entirely — (app)/+page.svelte:78-86 fetches /api/portfolio in onMount, so every return to Home re-runs the full portfolio recompute (~6 sync SQLite queries + all-wallet scan), no ETag/staleness reuse. Consider moving it to a streamed depends('cairn:portfolio') promise in load (keeps it OUT of the cairn:chain block-invalidation path while gaining SSR-stream + client cache). (2) Fable flags that the biggest sustained event-loop blocker on ARM is likely NOT request-path reads but the BACKGROUND WORKERS (addressWatcher, notificationQueue) doing synchronous DB work in the web process under concurrent load — batching/yielding their DB access or moving them to a worker_thread is worth more than any request-path DB worker. Also: page loads chaining await parent() create a serial layout->page waterfall; once gating moves to hooks (see cairn-v84z), drop await parent() where it was only for auth ordering.

title: Stream wallets/[id] detail load (single-sig wallet page)
description: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts currently awaits getWalletDetail (full gap-limit scan) -> peekReceiveAddress -> Promise.all([getWalletUtxos, getChain().getTip()]) -> detectWalletUnconfirmedInflows before the page can render at all. Apply the same deferred-promise streaming pattern already used in (app)/+page.server.ts (loadChainSnapshot) and wallets/+page.server.ts (scans): return the Electrum-dependent fields as an unawaited async function, add depends('cairn:wallet:{id}'), keep the existing try/catch degrade-gracefully behavior. Mirror (app)/+page.svelte's $state + $effect + onNewBlock -> invalidate() pattern on the client for a skeleton + live refresh on new blocks. See docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md Track 1 for full detail. Complexity M. File-disjoint from the other three page beads -- safe to build in parallel.
close_reason: Merged commit 52a6d1a (worktree agent) + fb8876f (test-mock fix). Streamed Electrum-dependent chainData; svelte-check clean; full suite green (1684 passed).

Apply the same deferred-promise streaming pattern as the dashboard/wallet-list (see docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md Track 1): stream the Electrum-dependent fields, add depends('cairn:multisig:{id}'), keep existing degrade-gracefully catch blocks (base fields like role/collaborators/descriptor already computed synchronously and must stay that way). title: Add tip + fee-estimate TTL cache to ChainService
description: ChainService.getTip() and getFeeEstimates() (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts) currently have no cache -- every call is a fresh esplora HTTP round-trip. Once the four wallet/send pages stream (other sibling beads), concurrent tabs/pages still redundantly re-fetch the same tip/fees. Add a tipCache (10min TTL ceiling, actively invalidated the instant a new block arrives via a hook into wireChainEvents' electrum.on('header', ...) handler in chainEvents.ts) and a feeCache (flat 30s TTL, no event invalidation needed). Reset both in reconfigureChain() alongside the existing resetChainHealth()/resetPackageRelaySupport() calls. Wrap the two ChainService methods in place so no call site changes. See docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md Track 2. Complexity S. Touches chain/index.ts + chainEvents.ts only -- file-disjoint from the four page beads, safe to build in parallel with them.
close_reason: Merged commit aa2f695. New src/lib/server/chain/cache.ts: tipCache (10min ceiling, invalidated on header event), feeCache (30s flat TTL); both reset in reconfigureChain(). Drop-in wrap of getTip/getFeeEstimates, zero call-site changes. svelte-check clean; test suite green (2 new cache tests, 17 total in chain.test.ts).

title: Verify perf fix on Umbrel/ARM hardware
description: Manual QA pass once the streaming + cache beads land: confirm navigation to all four migrated pages (wallet detail, multisig detail, single-sig send, multisig send) feels instant on Umbrel/ARM hardware, no skeleton flash/jank on revisit, SSE-triggered background refresh works on new block, and the existing degrade-gracefully paths (Electrum killed mid-scan) still behave correctly under streaming. See docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md bead 6.
close_reason: done

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Partial verification done via local dev server (cairn-fresh launch config, scratch dev-fresh2.db, Electrum unreachable so degrade-gracefully paths were exercised, not the happy-path timing win):

- wallets/[id] detail (vknb.1): shell (name, xpub, nav) renders immediately; streamed scan resolves to "Can't reach the wallet scanner" banner + Retry, never a 500. GET /wallets/246 200 373ms (ECONNREFUSED fails fast so this isn't a real ARM-latency signal).
- wallets/multisig/[id] detail (vknb.2): same pattern -- shell (keys, collaborators, backup banner) renders immediately, scan degrades to the same graceful banner.
- wallets/[id]/send (vknb.3): shell renders with "Loading live fee estimates..." skeleton, then resolves to "Live fee estimates are unavailable -- set a custom sat/vB rate." + spendable-coins error alert. Never hangs, never 500s.
- wallets/multisig/[id]/send (vknb.4): same degrade pattern, plus correct 2-of-3 multisig fee note.
- No console errors on any of the four pages.

NOT yet verified: real Umbrel/ARM hardware timing (this bead's actual scope), SSE-triggered live refresh on a new block (no live Electrum/regtest stack was running), no-skeleton-flash-on-revisit. Full test suite (1684 tests) and svelte-check both pass on the merged code. Leaving this bead open pending an actual Umbrel/ARM pass.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Umbrel/ARM hardware pass complete (live instance, umbrel.local:3211, v0.2.1, real Electrum backend electrum.blockstream.info, NOT local dev with ECONNREFUSED).

CONFIRMED FAST (this bead's actual scope):
- Dashboard, Explorer, Activity, Settings, Admin/Node all navigate instantly with proper skeleton -> content transitions. No jank on revisit.
- Notification panel opens instantly with real data.
- Send flow for a healthy wallet (wallets/1/send): TTFB 47ms, full resolution <1s once scan cache warm (first cold hit ~11s, which is the gap-limit scan cost the streaming shell already hides behind a skeleton -- not a freeze).
- No console errors on any tested page.
- Charts render correctly (balance history, block/difficulty chart).

NEW P1 FOUND (filed separately, not blocking this verification): cairn-vn48. On real hardware there's a QA wallet ("QA Trezor (regtest)") whose Electrum backend is unreachable at the network level (packets silently dropped, NOT ECONNREFUSED). That distinction matters: Alex's local dev pass exercised the ECONNREFUSED degrade path (fails fast, shows "Can't reach the wallet scanner" banner correctly). But a true network black-hole never fails at all -- ensureConnected() in electrum/client.ts has no connect-level timeout for direct (non-proxy) TCP/TLS, so the connection attempt hangs forever, which hangs the wallet's streamed chainData indefinitely and, intermittently, the whole /wallets list page (Promise.all never settles). This is likely the root cause behind the "Electrum-freeze" symptom tracked under cairn-2zxt. Root-caused down to the exact missing timeout; see cairn-vn48 for details and fix approach.

Also filed cairn-hoee (P3): HTTPS port 3212 dashboard is no longer permanently empty (prior bug fixed) but pays a one-time 10-30s cold-cache cost on first load after idle.

NOT verified (no safe way to trigger on this instance without mining/instructing infra changes): SSE-triggered live refresh on a new block.

Closing this verification bead -- the streaming/TTL-cache perf fix itself is confirmed working on real Umbrel/ARM hardware for the happy path. The freeze Alex is hitting in practice is the unreachable-backend connect-timeout gap now tracked at cairn-vn48.

title: W2A- admin recovery-setup phrase step stuck on 'Generating your phrase...' despite 200 API response
description: Repro: seed a fresh admin user (password_hash NULL, session minted directly, no passkey), accept /disclosure, then GET /recovery-setup. The page fires POST /api/auth/recovery/phrase via its Svelte component; network tab shows 200 OK with a real phrase in the JSON body, and a duplicate POST also fires (200). But the UI never advances past 'Your phrase... Generating your phrase...' — read_page/get_page_text confirm the DOM never renders the 12 words or the Continue button. This is a HARD gate (appGate.ts: admin cannot reach ANY other route, including /admin/settings, until hasRecoverySetup() is true) with no visible escape hatch in the UI. Workaround used for QA: call POST /api/auth/recovery/phrase and POST /api/auth/recovery/codes directly via curl with the session cookie, which flips hasRecoverySetup() to true and unblocks navigation immediately. Expected: the phrase step renders the returned words and lets the admin continue. Actual: stuck forever on the loading state; admin is fully blocked from the instance without a curl workaround. Verified 2026-07-13 on branch single-sig-full-wallet, instance data/vault-e2e-wave2a.db port 5399.
close_reason: not-a-product-bug: root-caused to shared node_modules/.vite cache re-optimization stalling hydration across concurrent dev instances (cairn-sx6f hazard); endpoint and component correct; impossible in production builds

comments.author: deep-reasoner
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE (deep-reasoner, 2026-07-13): NOT a product bug — it is the shared-Vite-cache re-optimization stall (see MEMORY cairn-sx6f "Shared Vite cache hazard"). The recovery-setup component, the /api/auth/recovery/phrase endpoint, and appGate are all correct.

MECHANISM: src/routes/(app)/recovery-setup/+page.svelte kicks off the phrase fetch from a client $effect (lines 132-134) that only runs AFTER hydration. In the QA dev environment, multiple vite dev servers (ports 5388/5399/5400) run off ONE checkout and share node_modules/.vite. When any sibling triggers a dependency re-optimize (file writes across concurrent sessions do this), the optimizer holds/invalidates module requests on the other instances. Hydration is blocked -> the $effect never runs -> generatePhrase() (line 46) never fires -> the page sits on "Generating your phrase..." with NO POST ever leaving the browser. This looks "stuck forever" to a human who does not wait out the re-optimize.

LIVE EVIDENCE (w2a-admin.localhost:5399, force=1): First load reproduced the hang. performance.getEntriesByType('resource') showed the client-JS waterfall BLOCKED for ~25s: modules start ~1.8s, then a dead gap, then ~15 modules (+layout.svelte, recovery-setup/+page.svelte, Stepper, CopyText, secureRedirect...) ALL released simultaneously at startTime~25500ms and finished ~28155ms (waterfall span 26,370ms). The component's own POST fired only at startTime 28084ms (once hydration finally completed) and rendered fine. No console errors. A raw fetch() to the endpoint from the page returned 200 with a valid phrase in <200ms the entire time -> server never at fault. Reloading on the now-WARM cache: hydration + POST + render in ~1.9s (POST start=1856 dur=100ms), waterfall span 1,657ms — completely healthy.

WHY IT CANNOT HAPPEN IN PROD: production is a bundled build (adapter-node) with no per-module Vite waterfall and no dep optimizer. Hydration is a single bundle load. This stall is strictly a dev-server artifact.

BLAST RADIUS: the non-admin recovery flow (entered from /settings) and the post-first-passkey first-run flow all funnel through this same single component (the only "Generating your phrase" match in src is recovery-setup/+page.svelte) and the same hydration-gated $effect, so they are identically affected in dev and identically fine warm/in-prod. No admin-specific code path is involved in the trigger.

FIX: environmental, not code. Give each concurrent QA dev instance its own vite cache (cacheDir per instance) or run each in its own git worktree, per the cairn-sx6f memory. No change to product code is warranted. Optional product hardening (separate, low priority): because the admin recovery gate is mandatory, a hard hydration failure hard-locks the admin behind only a client-rendered spinner; a server-rendered fallback/timeout hint could be considered, but that is a general SPA concern, not specific to this page.

Recommendation: reclassify/close as environment (QA-infra), not a shippable code defect. Verified on branch single-sig-full-wallet.

title: Self-disable and self-demote are only blocked client-side; unlike self-delete, the server actions don't check id === admin.id
description: src/routes/(app)/admin/users/+page.svelte disables the Disable/Demote buttons via disabled={user.id === me?.id} — a client-side-only guard. The server side (src/routes/(app)/admin/users/+page.server.ts actions.disable/actions.demote, and src/routes/api/admin/users/+server.ts POST) call setUserDisabled/setUserAdmin directly with no id===admin.id check; src/lib/server/admin.ts only blocks disabling/demoting when adminCount()<=1 (the LAST admin), not self specifically. Compare the DELETE handler in src/routes/api/admin/users/+server.ts, which explicitly checks 'if (body.id === admin.id) return json({ error: \'You cannot delete your own account.\' }...)' — the codebase clearly considers self-action guards worth enforcing server-side for destructive-to-self operations (see also the resetInstance comment: 'the admin layout already gates this route, but a factory reset deserves a second, explicit check'), but disable/demote didn't get the same treatment. With 2+ admins, an admin can self-disable via a raw POST to /api/admin/users (devtools, curl, or a stale/tampered DOM) and setUserDisabled immediately calls destroyUserSessions(id), killing their own session instantly with no confirmation step, recoverable only by asking another admin to re-enable them. Fix: mirror the DELETE handler's self-id check in the disable/demote actions and API route (at minimum for disable, which is destructive to the caller's own session).
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Heartwood solo mining — Tessera-solo sidecar (MVP)
description: Epic for building first-party solo-mining support in Heartwood: a Mining tab where a home user points a Bitaxe/small ASIC at their own node and, on a win, the full coinbase pays directly into their Heartwood wallet. Architecture is a 'Tessera-solo sidecar': a solo-only engine extracted from C:\dev\raffle (Tessera), run as a separate process/container, with Heartwood providing config, payout addresses, stats UI, and notifications. Solo-only, non-custodial, Stratum V1; the raffle layer is NOT ported. Full scoping doc: docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
DOCTRINE PIVOT (2026-07-17): mining scope shifts from a single-user Tessera-solo sidecar (one instance = one operator = one payout address, engine as a separate container) to MULTI-USER SOLO. Any user on the instance can point a miner at the in-process Stratum engine using an opaque per-user mining ID (hw_xxxxxxxx.workername, backed by a new mining_prefs table); the engine builds each connection's job template with a coinbase paying THAT miner's own wallet address, keyed off the authenticated username at connect time. A block finder keeps the FULL reward -- no splitting, no pooled payout, no custody of anyone else's funds. Shares remain tracked for stats/leaderboard purposes only (mining_workers/mining_stats), never as a basis for distributing value.

Architecturally this drops the separate-sidecar-container framing for local dev/typical installs (engine runs in-process, see cairn-vn43.12); Umbrel packaging (cairn-vn43.11) may still want engine isolation for resource/crash containment -- to be resolved when that bead is worked.

cairn-vn43.14's hard legal gate STILL STANDS UNCHANGED: this design was deliberately reviewed against it and stays entirely inside it (no reward-splitting, no custody, no forwarding of one user's coins to another) -- it is explicitly NOT the raffle/PPS/PPLNS mode that gate blocks.

Affected children re-scoped (see their own updated descriptions): .1 (engine -> per-miner coinbase), .7 (dashboard -> per-user /mining view w/ multi-user isolation), .9 (per-user blocks-found + MiningRewards), .10 (admin view expands to a cross-user dashboard + settings + engine control).

New foundation work landed this session (cairn-vn43.5 scope): mining feature flag + soft-launch default-off migration (miningDefaultMigration.ts, mirrors explorerDefaultMigration.ts), nav entry (flame icon, avatar-menu on mobile), mining_prefs/mining_workers/mining_stats/mining_blocks schema in db.ts, and 3 new notification types (mining_block_found/mining_worker_offline/mining_best_share) in notifyTypes.ts + activity.ts's USER_FEED_TYPES. /mining route scaffold intentionally deferred to next wave (see cairn-vn43.5's status).

title: mining: extract Tessera-solo engine (stratum/wire/TipPoller port + solo job builder + coordinator)
description:
MVP unit 1 (L) of epic cairn-vn43. Port pool/src/stratum.ts, pool/src/wire.ts, TipPoller (pool/src/rpc.ts), and pool/src/types.ts from C:/dev/raffle nearly verbatim into a standalone solo-mining engine. Rewrite pool/src/job.ts as a solo coinbase builder (keep BIP34 height push, extranonce placement, coinb1/coinb2 split, witness-commitment output; single payout output, no RAFFLE/RAFFND OP_RETURNs). Rewrite pool/src/pool.ts as a thin solo coordinator (keep the serialized tip/solve promise queue; drop RaffleEngine/draw/finder/reorg-void plus flat-file persistence). Must be standalone runnable via a pool-runner entrypoint. See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Tessera reuse map section.

DOCTRINE PIVOT (2026-07-17, see epic comment): engine -> PER-MINER COINBASE. The job builder must key the coinbase payout address off the authenticated connection's own mining_prefs.payout_wallet_id, not a single instance-wide address -- every Stratum connection/username maps to its own miner (opaque hw_xxxxxxxx.workername id) and its own wallet. Runs in-process (see cairn-vn43.12), not necessarily a separate sidecar container. Shares still stats-only; no reward splitting (cairn-vn43.14 unchanged).
close_reason: Engine core complete. Commits: 2b34bd7 (types), 5d876f8 (wire/address/job), 9258fb6 (stratum), 48d37fb (tipPoller/miningPool/client wrappers), 38252ea (tests). 53 vitest tests green (wire 5, job 16, stratum 24, miningPool forced-solve 2 + shared). svelte-check 0 errors. Solo single-value-output invariant asserted (legal gate cairn-vn43.14). NOTE: bitcoinjs-lib used as transitive dep (matches scripts/mining precedent) — recommend promoting to direct dep.

title: mining: admin settings section (enable, bind, port, vardiff, engine health)
description:
MVP unit 10 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Add an admin settings section following the per-section save/test pattern in src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte: enable toggle, bind address (loopback default, LAN opt-in requiring an explicit consent step per open question 2), stratum port, target wallet, vardiff bounds, and engine health/restart controls. Plain kv for port/bind/vardiff/enabled; instance_secrets for the engine API auth token.

DOCTRINE PIVOT (2026-07-17, see epic comment): EXPANDS TO an admin DASHBOARD + settings + engine control, not just a settings form -- there is no single "target wallet" anymore (each user brings their own), so drop that field and add a cross-user aggregate view (all workers/hashrate/blocks-found across every user) for the admin's operational visibility, alongside the enable/bind/port/vardiff/engine-health controls, which stay instance-wide.
notes: Shipped: /admin/mining route (+page.server.ts, +page.svelte), 7 new Admin*.svelte components under src/lib/components/mining/, Mining tab added to admin nav (+layout.svelte) and admin overview link (+page.svelte). Actions: ?/save (validated settings persist + reconfigureMiningEngine), ?/startStop, ?/restart, all admin-gated. Security regression test page.server.test.ts (7 tests, mirrors admin/settings' 403-guard pattern) passes. svelte-check: 0 errors project-wide. Commits 5bd2042, b1e3276 on design/evergreen-identity.

title: mining: Umbrel packaging (second container, port 3333, bitcoin dependency)
description: MVP unit 11 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Package the engine as a second container from the same Heartwood image (pool-runner command as entrypoint), publish port 3333 directly in docker-compose (app_proxy is HTTP-only, same mechanism as the existing 4488:3443 HTTPS mapping), add the bitcoin app as a dependency in umbrel-app.yml (currently dependencies: []), and digest-pin the image. See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md architecture overview section.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Packaging done except the release-time image pin (leaving this IN_PROGRESS,
not closing -- see "what remains" below).

The multi-user-solo doctrine pivot (epic cairn-vn43's 2026-07-17 comment)
changed this bead's shape: the engine runs IN-PROCESS in the same Heartwood
web container (src/lib/server/mining/index.ts -- no child-process
supervisor, cairn-vn43.12 is obsolete), so "second container from the same
image (pool-runner command)" is no longer the right packaging shape. What
was actually needed instead:

- packaging/umbrel/heartwood/docker-compose.yml: added
  "3333:3333" to the existing web service's `ports:` list, right after the
  4488:3443 HTTPS mapping, with a comment explaining why (app_proxy is
  HTTP-only and can't proxy raw Stratum TCP, same reasoning as the existing
  4488 mapping) and noting it's a no-op unless the operator sets bind to
  lan/all in /admin/mining (default is loopback-only). No new env vars
  needed -- all mining_* config lives in the settings table (src/lib/server/
  mining/settings.ts), read fresh on every call, nothing baked at image-build
  or container-start time.
- packaging/umbrel/heartwood/umbrel-app.yml: added
    dependencies:
      - bitcoin
  ("bitcoin" is the app id already used elsewhere in this codebase's own
  Umbrel-detection code -- src/lib/server/umbrelCoreProbe.ts's comments and
  src/lib/server/chainEnvSeed.ts both refer to the "bitcoin"/"electrs" app
  dependencies by that id -- so this matches the existing convention rather
  than guessing). Also added a mining paragraph to `description` and a
  mining sentence to `releaseNotes`, per the file's existing per-release
  conventions.

What remains (release-time, per the umbrel-update-app skill checklist, NOT
done here per this bead's own instructions): bump `version:` in
umbrel-app.yml and the pinned `image:` tag+digest in docker-compose.yml
together, once an actual release build exists that includes this mining
work -- both currently still point at 0.2.17, which is already stale
against the repo's real HEAD (v0.2.33 per recent tags) independent of
mining, so this isn't a new gap introduced here. Also not done: an
umbrelOS test-install pass exercising the new port mapping and bitcoin
dependency end-to-end (umbrel-test-app skill) -- that needs the real
release image, not the working tree.

title: mining: dev-mode child-process supervisor for the engine
description: MVP unit 12 (S) of epic cairn-vn43. In local dev (no Docker), run the Tessera-solo engine as a supervised Node child process instead of a second container, using the server.mjs shutdown() seam and the hooks.server.ts init()/start pattern already used for other background services. Must be Windows-friendly (dev box is Windows 11).
close_reason: Obsolete: in-process engine (no child process to supervise). See comment.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: OBSOLETE. The engine ships IN-PROCESS (one MiningPool per instance inside the SvelteKit server bundle), started from hooks.server.ts init() alongside the other background watchers and stopped via a bundle-side process signal handler in src/lib/server/mining/index.ts (ensureShutdownFlush: synchronous final aggregates flush on SIGTERM/SIGINT). No child-process supervisor is needed — there is no second Node process to supervise, on Windows dev or in Docker. server.mjs's shutdown() seam can't cleanly reach the bundled singleton (separate module graph), so durability is handled bundle-side instead. Closing as obsolete.

title: mining: docs (MANUAL.md sections, help panel, release notes)
description: MVP unit 13 (S) of epic cairn-vn43. Write MANUAL.md sections covering setup, the honest-odds framing, and troubleshooting for the mining feature, add an in-app help panel, and draft release notes once the feature ships. Companion doc: docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md.
close_reason: Docs done: MANUAL.md + docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md updated, see comment for section-by-section evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Docs done (design/evergreen-identity branch, committed separately).

MANUAL.md additions (read the real code in src/lib/server/mining/*, the two
+page.server.ts files, registry.ts, notifyTypes.ts before writing):
- Section 6 "Main tables": new "Mining (multi-user solo pool, epic cairn-vn43)"
  table entry for mining_prefs/mining_workers/mining_stats/mining_blocks.
- Section 8 "Server: API Routes & Cross-Cutting Server Concerns": new
  "Mining engine (src/lib/server/mining/, epic cairn-vn43)" subsection --
  doctrine-pivot summary, in-process lifecycle from hooks.server.ts, the
  authTable.ts hot-path synchronous snapshot + refreshAuthTable() rebuild
  triggers, aggregates.ts's 15s-batched flush cadence and the cairn-xlrm
  sync-SQLite rationale for why it's never per-share, handleBlockAccepted's
  payout-cursor-advance + notify + activity wiring, a settings-keys table
  (mining_enabled/bind/stratum_port/share_difficulty/vardiff_enabled/
  vardiff_target_rate/pool_tag), the 3 notification types with their

  ignored identity contract incl. the regenerate caveat.
- Section 9 "Client": route-table rows for mining/+page.svelte and
  admin/mining/+page.svelte, a components-list addition, and a new "Mining
  dashboard client" subsection covering the 10s poll model (full-replace vs
  admin's settings-excluded merge), the exact empty-state precedence order
  (cairn-vn43.24), an admin runbook (enabling, bind/LAN exposure warning,
  reading each dashboard panel), and a user guide (Bitaxe/ASIC connection,
  worker-name format, honest measured-hashrate odds framing, payout wallet
  eligibility, 100-conf maturity).
- Section 13: corrected the existing "Mining forced-solve regtest harness"
  subsection, which was still written as "the (not-yet-built) solo-mining
  feature" / "cairn-vn43.1 hasn't landed yet" -- both now false since the
  real engine landed. Clarified the harness still targets its own
  standalone soloBlockBuilder.mjs reference implementation, not the real
  engine, pending the already-documented cairn-vn43.2 follow-up.
- Part II: new "20.18 Mining pool (multi-user solo) QA matrix" -- forced-
  solve-against-the-real-engine and load-test items described generically
  by planned script name/flag shape (a concurrent QA-wave session owns the
  actual scripts.qa/mining-* filenames), flag-gate on/off check, the
  empty-state matrix, a multi-user isolation check (per-user endpoint vs
  admin endpoint), and the regenerate-ID caveat (old id keeps resolving
  until the next authTable refresh, then UNAUTHORIZED, no grace period).

title: mining: HARD GATE - legal review required before any multi-user/shared-reward mode
description:
Hard gate from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Key decisions item 2. Heartwood mining scope is solo-only and non-custodial for the MVP (epic cairn-vn43); the raffle/multi-user layer from Tessera (C:/dev/raffle) is explicitly NOT ported because operator-run prize draws for third parties amount to gambling (consideration plus chance plus prize; licensing; some US states ban online raffles) and multi-user PPS/PPLNS creates reserve liability, custody of others funds, and money-transmission exposure. This bead exists to block any future multi-user or shared-reward mining mode from being coded until Alex has obtained legal review. Do not close by shipping code; close only when a review process is documented or the feature is formally deferred.

ANNOTATION (2026-07-17): the epic's "multi-user solo" pivot (see epic comment) was deliberately reviewed against this gate and stays entirely inside it -- multiple users each mining to their OWN wallet via their OWN per-connection coinbase is not the blocked mode. What remains blocked, unchanged: any reward SPLITTING (PPS/PPLNS/proportional), any POOLED payout, any CUSTODY of one user's mined coins pending distribution, or FORWARDING one user's coinbase to another. This gate still stands and is not satisfied by the multi-user pivot alone -- it stays open until Alex documents a review or formal deferral, per the original close condition.

title: mining scope Q: is the raffle layer confirmed dead for Heartwood?
description: Open question 1 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. Recommendation: yes, the raffle/prize-draw layer from Tessera (C:/dev/raffle) is dead for Heartwood, for legal reasons (see the HARD GATE bead) and because it is dead weight at home mining scale (the shared block essentially never comes). Tessera continues to exist and develop separately as its own product; this only concerns what gets ported into Heartwood. Needs Alex sign-off to close.
close_reason: Confirmed dead as-built -- no raffle code anywhere in src/lib/server/mining/, see comment.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Confirmed dead, as-built: no raffle/prize-draw code exists anywhere under src/lib/server/mining/ (miningPool.ts's own header comment: 'Dropped from Tessera entirely: the raffle engine/draw/finder, reorg-void reconciliation, shadow draws, and all flat-file persistence... Solo keeps only: build the job for the new tip, and on a solve submit the block and fire a callback.'). The shipped schema (mining_prefs/mining_workers/mining_stats/mining_blocks) has no draw/ticket/prize table of any kind, and the hard legal gate cairn-vn43.14 was reviewed against and holds for the multi-user-solo design that did ship. Closing per this recommendation.

title: mining scope Q: LAN bind default for the stratum port
description: Open question 2 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. Recommendation: loopback-only bind by default, with an explicit LAN opt-in consent step in admin settings (unit cairn-vn43.10) before the stratum port 3333 is exposed to the LAN where a Bitaxe or ASIC needs to reach it. Needs Alex sign-off to close.

title: mining scope Q: external payout addresses in MVP or phase 2?
description: Open question 3 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. Recommendation: phase 2. MVP payout wiring (unit cairn-vn43.6) pays only the user's own Heartwood wallet via peekReceiveAddress/nextReceiveAddress; supporting arbitrary external payout addresses would require validateAddressEncodable on untrusted input and is deferred. Needs Alex sign-off to close.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Confirmed as shipped: MVP payout wiring (authTable.ts/prefs.ts) only ever resolves a miner's OWN wallet via peekReceiveAddress/nextReceiveAddress -- setPayoutWallet (prefs.ts) rejects any walletId that isn't the caller's own or lacks an xpub. There is no code path anywhere in src/lib/server/mining/ that accepts or validates an arbitrary external address string as a payout target. This matches the original recommendation (phase 2) exactly. Leaving OPEN, not closing -- this bead's own text says 'Needs Alex sign-off to close,' and that sign-off hasn't happened; this comment is just confirming the as-built state matches the recommendation pending that sign-off.

title: mining scope Q: mainnet posture - ship mainnet-enabled day 1 or testnet4-first soak?
description: Open question 4 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. Choice is between shipping the mining feature mainnet-enabled from day 1 with honest-odds UI, versus requiring a testnet4-first soak period before mainnet is allowed (mirroring the 33-hour real-Bitaxe testnet4 soak Tessera already ran). No recommendation given in the scoping doc; needs an explicit decision from Alex. Related to the forced-solve CI gate, unit cairn-vn43.2.

title: mining scope Q: Tessera licensing - relicense/vendor decision needed
description: Open question 5 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. Verified facts (2026-07-12): C:/dev/raffle/package.json declares license GPL-3.0 (package name raffle-pool; there is no separate pool/package.json, the pool code lives under raffle/pool/ but is covered by the root manifest). Cairn/Heartwood's own C:/dev/cairn/LICENSE is MIT. Alex owns the Tessera code so relicensing or vendoring it is his call. Needed action: Alex must decide whether to relicense the extracted solo-engine code before vendoring it into the MIT-licensed Cairn/Heartwood repo (unit cairn-vn43.1), or keep it in a separate GPL-3.0 sidecar package/container so the two license terms are not mixed in one source tree/image.

title: mining: forced-solve regtest harness plus CI gate (P1 schedule long pole)
description: MVP unit 2 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Build a maintained CI harness that exercises getblocktemplate to job to share to submitblock on regtest and verifies the block is actually confirmed on-chain. Adapt Tessera existing regtest e2e (which already exercises accepted submitblocks). This is the one code path that must be flawless for the rare real win, and is a required gate before mainnet enablement of the mining feature. Schedule long pole for the whole epic, should start first. See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Key decisions item 4 and Risks item 1.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Built: forced-solve regtest harness + CI gate, self-contained.

- scripts/mining/soloBlockBuilder.mjs — pure solo coinbase/block builder
  (BIP34 height push, BIP141 witness commitment, PoW grind via
  bitcoinjs-lib's Block.calculateMerkleRoot/checkProofOfWork/checkTxRoots).
  Fresh implementation, NOT ported from Tessera (C:\dev\raffle pool/src/job.ts
  + wire.ts) since Tessera is GPL-3.0 and the vendoring/licensing call
  (scope doc Open questions #5) is still pending Alex; bitcoinjs-lib is MIT
  and already resolves via node_modules (transitive, via src/lib/hw/ledger.ts).
  19 unit tests in soloBlockBuilder.test.mjs (pure, no docker) - added
  `scripts/**/*.test.mjs` to vitest.config.ts's test.include so `npm test`
  picks them up.
- scripts/mining/regtestNode.mjs + docker-compose.yml — ephemeral regtest
  bitcoind lifecycle (own project `cairn-mining-forcedsolve`, RPC :18546,
  distinct from vault-e2e :18543 / qa-sub1 :18544) + minimal fetch-based
  JSON-RPC client. Always `down -v` before AND after every run.
- scripts/mining/forcedSolveHarness.mjs — boots node, mines 101 warm-up
  blocks, force-solves 3 blocks via real getblocktemplate -> our coinbase
  builder -> submitblock, matures 100 blocks, verifies each solved block
  on-chain (main-chain-not-orphaned, coinbase unspent w/ >=100 confs, exact
  sats conservation vs template.coinbasevalue, correct payout script).
  `npm run mining:forced-solve-harness`.
- CI: new independent `mining-forced-solve` job in .github/workflows/ci.yml
  (ubuntu-latest ships Docker preinstalled; checkout, Node 22, npm ci, run
  the harness).
- MANUAL.md section 13 new subsection documents all of the above.

Evidence (two consecutive fresh runs on this box, both clean teardown after):
  Run 1: 3/3 blocks forced-solved, PASS, elapsed 18.7s
  Run 2: 3/3 blocks forced-solved, PASS, elapsed 13.6s
  docker ps -a --filter name=cairn-mining-forcedsolve empty after each run
  (no orphaned containers/volumes).
  soloBlockBuilder.test.mjs: 19/19 passed.
  Full repo suite (247 files / 3504 tests incl. these new 19) ran clean
  before the two other concurrent agents' further edits landed.

Scope note / what's intentionally NOT done here (not blocked, just future
work tracked as a follow-up, not a gap in this bead): the harness exercises
the winning path directly against bitcoind (getblocktemplate/submitblock),
not through an actual Stratum TCP miner -> engine -> submitblock chain,
because the real engine (stratum.ts/pool.ts) doesn't exist in this repo yet
(cairn-vn43.1, not started). That's by design per the bead's own framing
("adapt Tessera's e2e" / "the code path that must be flawless" = the
job-building + submission correctness, not the wire protocol, which Tessera's
stratum.ts is separately ported ~verbatim for). When cairn-vn43.1 lands and
the Tessera-licensing question resolves, this harness should be repointed at
the real engine's job builder/submit path instead of soloBlockBuilder.mjs's
standalone reference implementation.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Forced-solve regtest e2e + load/edge/security harness DONE, committed 5412972 on design/evergreen-identity. Both harnesses drive the REAL engine (src/lib/server/mining/*) via a .ts resolve hook + node --experimental-transform-types; ZERO engine modifications / no reimplementation.

REPRO:
  node scripts/qa/mining-forced-solve.mjs        # real regtest bitcoind (local binary or docker)
  node scripts/qa/mining-load-test.mjs           # fake-rpc, no bitcoind; --seconds/--miners/--users
  npx vitest run src/lib/server/mining/forcedSolve.e2e.test.ts   # CI gate, skipIf(!bitcoind)

FORCED-SOLVE (real bitcoind Core v28.1, local): PASS 22/22 in 1.5s. Real engine solved+confirmed a block at height 102; coinbase pays derived bcrt1 address the FULL 5,000,000,000 sats (exactly one value output + zero-value witness commitment); engine coinbase txid == on-chain; mining_blocks row 'accepted'; wallet receive_cursor 0->1; fatalErrors empty; coinbase matured (101 confs >= 101); PAYOUT ISOLATION verified — a 2nd user replaying the winner share on its own connection is rejected, produces no block, and has no block row.

LOAD (60 miners / 20 users / 60s, fake-rpc, grinding on worker-thread pool so main-thread drift measures the ENGINE): PASS. 15,971 shares @ 266.2 shares/s; every client-confirmed share accounted (15971==15971); 0 rejects; drift p95 8ms / max 14ms (0/1147 >250ms); rss growth 115MB (<200MB); 0 fatalErrors.

EDGE (6/6): partial-JSON-then-destroy; disconnect-after-submit (share counted); zero-hashrate (no NaN); maxConnections exceeded (refused, stable); reconfigure mid-session (reconnect, no double-count); protocol-fuzz (destroyed, engine unaffected).

SECURITY (4/4): below-target rejected low_difficulty (no stat inflation); duplicate rejected; unknown mining_id rejected; authorize-time frozen payout — solve pays ONLY the authorizing miner's script.

Full mining vitest suite green: 10 files / 82 tests. No engine bugs found.

title: mining scope Q: engine fallback trigger - how long to timebox Tessera-solo extraction?

If the Tessera-solo engine extraction (unit cairn-vn43.1) stalls badly, the documented fallback is bundling Public Pool (GPL-3.0, NestJS, proven mainnet blocks including 920440) as an unmodified sidecar container, relying on mere aggregation for the GPL boundary. No specific timebox duration has been set; needs a concrete decision (e.g. N days/weeks of stalled extraction effort) from Alex or the orchestrating lead before the build wave starts.

Candidate names for the feature/nav tab: plain Mining, Solo Mining, or something with lottery-flavored framing (consistent with the honest lottery-ticket odds framing used elsewhere in the UI per Key decisions item 2). Affects nav copy (unit cairn-vn43.5) and the dashboard/onboarding copy (unit cairn-vn43.7). Needs a decision before UI copy is finalized.

The honest-odds/lottery-ticket FRAMING still lives inside the page copy (MiningOddsPanel's measured-hashrate odds calculator, the onboarding copy's 'at ~1 TH/s expect a block roughly every N years' language) per Key decision #2 in docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md -- it's just not in the nav label/branding itself.
