title: mining: identity contract (hw_ mining ID, mining_prefs helpers, worker-name parsing, AuthProvider snapshot)
description:
New unit from the 2026-07-17 multi-user-solo doctrine pivot (see epic cairn-vn43's pivot comment). Define and implement the mining identity contract that every other multi-user piece depends on:
- Opaque per-user mining ID format hw_xxxxxxxx (random, non-guessable, stable once minted) used as the Stratum username prefix; full worker identity on the wire is hw_xxxxxxxx.workername.
- mining_prefs table (already landed in db.ts this session: user_id, mining_id UNIQUE, enabled, payout_wallet_id, updated_at) — this bead wires the actual mint-on-first-enable flow, uniqueness/collision handling, and read/write helpers (mirror the shape of an existing per-user settings module, e.g. src/lib/server/notificationPrefs.ts if one exists, or settings.ts's per-user helpers).
- Worker-name parsing/validation contract: how the Stratum auth handler splits hw_xxxxxxxx.workername back into (user lookup via mining_id, worker_name), rejects malformed/unknown mining IDs, and what the engine snapshots at connect time (an AuthProvider-style read-through: user's enabled flag + payout_wallet_id + current receive address) so a later toggle-off or wallet change doesn't retroactively rewrite an already-issued job's payout.
This is foundational — the in-process engine (cairn-vn43.1), the stats bridge (cairn-vn43.4), and the /mining view (cairn-vn43.7) all depend on this identity contract being settled first.
close_reason: Implemented + tested on design/evergreen-identity (01e1acb impl, a7bbcba tests). See per-bead comments for file/evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Done in 01e1acb (+tests a7bbcba). Identity contract: prefs.ts ensureMiningPrefs generates hw_+8-lowercase-hex mining_id (unique-retry); setPayoutWallet (ownership-validated), setUserMiningEnabled, regenerateMiningId — all fire onPrefsChanged -> authTable refresh. authTable.ts = in-memory AuthProvider snapshot (Map<miningId,MinerAuth>), resolve() pure sync zero-I/O, rebuilt on start + 60s timer + prefs-change. Worker-name parsing (<miningId>.<workerName>) lives in the engine stratum layer; readModels surfaces workerFormat. prefs.test.ts + authTable.test.ts cover id format/uniqueness, ownership rejection, enabled+wallet->entry, per-user-failure skip.

title: mining: stats poll endpoints /api/mining/me + /api/admin/mining
description:
New unit from the 2026-07-17 multi-user-solo doctrine pivot (see epic cairn-vn43's pivot comment). Add the two Heartwood-side stats poll endpoints the pivot's per-user isolation requires:
- GET /api/mining/me — the current session's own mining_prefs (mining ID, enabled, payout wallet), own mining_workers rows, own recent mining_stats, own mining_blocks. Must scope strictly to req user_id (fail-closed, same posture as activity.ts's USER_FEED_TYPES split) — never leak another user's worker/hashrate/block data.
- GET /api/admin/mining — admin-only cross-user aggregate: all users' workers/hashrate/blocks-found, engine health, for the admin dashboard (cairn-vn43.10).
Both poll the Heartwood stats bridge/cache from cairn-vn43.4 (or the engine API from cairn-vn43.3 directly if the bridge isn't landed yet) rather than hitting the engine on every request. Depends on the identity contract bead (mining_prefs helpers) and informs both cairn-vn43.7 (user view) and cairn-vn43.10 (admin view).
close_reason: Implemented + tested on design/evergreen-identity (01e1acb impl, a7bbcba tests). See per-bead comments for file/evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Done in 01e1acb (+tests a7bbcba). GET /api/mining/me (requireFeature 'mining' -> getUserMiningView(user.id)) and GET /api/admin/mining (requireAdmin -> getAdminMiningView). Exact JSON shapes in readModels.ts (UserMiningView/AdminMiningView types). SECURITY tests: server.test.ts guards (non-admin=403, signed-out=401, flag-off=403); readModels.test.ts asserts user A view never contains user B workers/earnings.

title: mining: multi-user degraded/onboarding states (no-wallet, engine-stopped, no-workers card)
description:
New unit from the 2026-07-17 multi-user-solo doctrine pivot (see epic cairn-vn43's pivot comment). cairn-vn43.7's original scope already covers "empty states for flag-off, Core missing (CoreRpcRequiredNotice.svelte), and no workers" for a single-owner dashboard — this bead covers the ADDITIONAL degraded/onboarding states the multi-user pivot introduces, which are genuinely new (not duplicates of .7's list):
- no-wallet: a user who enables mining before creating any wallet has no payout_wallet_id to mint a mining_id against — needs a blocking "create a wallet first" onboarding card, not a silent/broken state.
- engine-stopped: the in-process engine (unlike the old separate-container model) can be stopped/crashed independent of the Heartwood server process itself (see admin engine-control in cairn-vn43.10) — the per-user /mining view needs an honest "mining is currently off at the instance level" state distinct from "you haven't set up a worker yet."
- no-workers onboarding card: the first-run experience for a user who has enabled mining and has a mining ID, but has never connected a miner — host/port/mining-ID copy-paste setup card, not just a bare empty list.
Coordinate with cairn-vn43.7 so these don't get built twice.
close_reason: Degraded/onboarding states built in MiningOnboarding.svelte, commit 6c478a4

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: All three degraded/onboarding states built in MiningOnboarding.svelte (commit 6c478a4, design/evergreen-identity), selected in src/routes/(app)/mining/+page.svelte with this precedence: loadError (getUserMiningView threw) -> engine core_missing (CoreRpcRequiredNotice, reused as-is) -> engine stopped ('Mining isn't running yet -- your operator hasn't started the pool yet') -> no eligible wallet (blocking 'You need a wallet first' + /wallets link, wallet selector suppressed) -> not-enabled (onboarding Enable card) -> full dashboard. Enabled-but-no-workers is handled in MiningConnectionCard (a 'waiting for your first share...' hint alongside the setup fields, per the brief -- the connection card IS the main state, not a separate empty list). Closing.

title: mining: engine stats/control HTTP API
description: MVP unit 3 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Add a minimal HTTP API to the Tessera-solo engine (replacing pool/src/api.ts, not reusing it as-is): status, workers, shares/best-share, blocks endpoints; config push for payout-address/port/vardiff; health check. Auth via bearer token stored through instance_secrets/setSecretSetting. This is what the Heartwood stats bridge (unit 4) polls. See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md architecture diagram and Integration points section.

title: mining: Heartwood stats bridge plus aggregate persistence
description: MVP unit 4 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Server-side client in Heartwood that polls the engine stats/control API (unit 3) with a light cache, and persists only low-rate aggregates (worker rollups, best_share, blocks_found, hashrate history) via batched writes to new tables (mining_workers, mining_stats rollups, mining_blocks) added CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS style in src/lib/server/db.ts. Per-share state must stay in the engine process (in-memory plus periodic flush); do not let per-share work leak into the wallet sync node:sqlite (cairn-xlrm hazard). See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Integration points section.
close_reason: Implemented + tested on design/evergreen-identity (01e1acb impl, a7bbcba tests). See per-bead comments for file/evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Done in 01e1acb (+tests a7bbcba). Bridge = src/lib/server/mining/{aggregates,index,settings}.ts. In-memory share accounting keyed userId:worker; ONE batched tx every 15s upserts mining_workers (delta increments, MAX best) + appends closed 1-min mining_stats buckets (per-worker + user_id NULL pool row); 7-day prune; bounded windows (24h + 5000 cap). Zero per-share DB writes (cairn-xlrm). readModels prefers live in-memory now-values over DB mirror.

Add a mining feature flag in src/lib/server/featureFlags/registry.ts (defaultEnabled true is compiler-enforced), enforce with requireFeature() in src/lib/server/api.ts, add a one-line nav tab in src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte around line 29 following the absent-when-flag-off convention, and scaffold src/routes/(app)/mining/ modeled on explorer/. title: mining: payout wiring (peek-hold plus advance-on-block)
description: MVP unit 6 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Wire a dedicated labeled mining address using peekReceiveAddress(walletId) (does not advance the cursor) held stable across template rebuilds in src/lib/server/wallets.ts, with nextReceiveAddress() advancing the cursor only when a block is actually found (withLock). Push the resulting address to the engine as job payout config. MVP pays own-Heartwood-wallet only; external addresses are phase 2 (see open question 3). See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Key decisions item 6.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Done in 01e1acb (+tests a7bbcba). authTable.ts builds MinerAuth via peekReceiveAddress (peek-hold, no advance) off the socket path; onBlockAccepted advances the finder cursor exactly once via nextReceiveAddress + records mining_blocks + notifies. engineBlocks.test.ts asserts advance-once + row + notifies.

title: mining: dashboard UI (setup card, worker status, hashrate chart, odds calculator)
description:
MVP unit 7 (L) of epic cairn-vn43. Build the /mining dashboard: connection setup card (host/port 3333/username), worker status list, hashrate chart via CairnChart.svelte, best-share high score, honest odds calculator (measured hashrate to expected time to block, framed like at roughly 1 TH/s expect a block roughly every 15000 to 35000 years), and empty states for flag-off, Core missing (CoreRpcRequiredNotice.svelte), and no workers. Follows the MVP user journey in docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md.

DOCTRINE PIVOT (2026-07-17, see epic comment): this becomes a PER-USER /mining VIEW WITH MULTI-USER ISOLATION -- each logged-in user sees only their OWN mining ID, workers, shares, and best-share (queried by mining_workers/mining_stats.user_id), never another user's. Connection setup card shows this user's own hw_xxxxxxxx.workername mining ID, not an instance-wide username. Odds calculator/copy risk and additional degraded states (no-wallet, engine-stopped) tracked separately, see new beads from this session.
close_reason: Dashboard UI shipped, commit 6c478a4

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Flagging separately per this session's bead-hygiene pass (task item v in the cairn-vn43.5 foundation brief): the honest-odds calculator copy in this bead's own description is a product-risk item — the framing needs care so it reads as honest expectation-setting (e.g. 'at ~1 TH/s expect a block roughly every 15000-35000 years') and never as gambling-adjacent hype, especially now that this is a per-user view every miner sees, not an admin-only panel. Not spinning out a separate bead since the odds calculator is already explicitly in scope here — just flagging so whoever picks this up treats the copy as a review item, not boilerplate.

comments.author: alexl

title: Electrum ensureConnected() has no connect-level timeout — unreachable backend hangs page forever
description:
## What's broken

Any wallet whose Electrum backend is unreachable (dead host, dropped packets, no RST) causes the wallet's chain-data fetch to hang **indefinitely** — not slow, never resolves. Confirmed via curl with a real authenticated session against the v0.2.1 Umbrel instance (umbrel.local:3211):

- GET /wallets/2 (wallet pointing to a dead "QA Trezor (regtest)" backend): hung past 20s, streamed shell painted but `chainData:__sveltekit_ywkcgt.defer(1)` never resolved.
- GET /wallets (the list page): intermittently hangs (15s+, incomplete response) whenever this wallet's 60s scan cache (`scanCache` in walletScan.ts) has expired and `listWallets()` re-scans it — `Promise.all([...])` in `(app)/wallets/+page.server.ts` never settles because one of the awaited scans never rejects, just hangs.
- In the browser this manifests as: clicking into Wallets/Explorer/any nav after visiting the bad wallet leaves the tab permanently stuck on the previous page (client-side nav awaits the same hung promise before swapping the view), and DevTools Runtime.evaluate itself times out — the tab looks fully frozen.

Root cause: `src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts` `ensureConnected()` has no timeout on the initial TCP/TLS connect for direct (non-proxy) connections:
- `net.connect({ host, port }, onReady)` (line ~259) — no connect timeout.
- `tls.connect({...}, onReady)` (lines ~201-209, used at line ~256) — no connect timeout.
- Only the SOCKS5 proxy path passes `timeout: this.timeoutMs` to `SocksClient.createConnection` (line ~238).
- `this.timeoutMs` (via `rawRequest`'s `setTimeout`, line ~407-408) only covers a request sent on an *already-established* socket — it never fires while still dialing.
- `s.setTimeout(0)` (line 214) explicitly disables the socket idle timeout too (intentional, for keepalive per cairn-u7bw), so nothing bounds a stalled connect.

So: dead host that silently drops SYN/never completes TCP handshake → `ensureConnected()`'s promise never settles → every caller up the chain (`scanWallet` → `getWalletDetail`/`listWallets` → the page's streamed `chainData`/`scans` promise) hangs forever, even though those callers have "never rejects" try/catch wrappers — those can't help because the underlying promise doesn't reject, it just never resolves.

## Why this matters now

This is NOT fixed by the cairn-vknb streaming perf work that just shipped in v0.2.1. Streaming makes the shell paint instantly, but once the deferred chainData promise hangs, the affected wallet's card/page/list never fills in and (via client-side nav blocking on the same await) can make the whole tab look frozen. This is very likely the same "Electrum-freeze" symptom tracked at a high level under cairn-2zxt (post-Heartwood-launch fixes).

## Fix

Add a connect-level timeout to `ensureConnected()` for the direct `net.connect`/`tls.connect` paths (e.g. wrap in `Promise.race` with a timer, or use `net.connect(...).setTimeout(timeoutMs, ...)` on the connecting socket before it's ready), matching the SOCKS5 path's behavior — reject with a clear "connect timed out" error so the existing try/catch degrade-to-empty-state logic in the load functions actually gets a chance to run.

## Repro

1. Have a wallet configured against an unreachable Electrum host (regtest node that's down, wrong IP, firewalled with silent drop).
2. Wait for its scanWallet 60s cache to be cold (or first load).
3. GET /wallets/<id> or /wallets (list) while authenticated — request hangs past any reasonable timeout, confirmed to 20s+ with curl, never completes.

Verified against live umbrel.local:3211 instance during v0.2.1 perf-fix QA pass (2026-07-07 / 2026-07-08 UTC).
close_reason: Landed in ed95397. Added a standalone connect-timer (this.timeoutMs) to ensureConnected(), armed on the two DIRECT branches (wrapTls + net.connect) only; on expiry it destroys the dialing socket and rejects via the existing fail() so scanWallet/getWalletDetail/listWallets/streamed loads stop hanging on a black-holed SYN. Disarmed in onReady (success) and fail() (error/close/timeout); settled flag prevents double-reject; timer unref'd. Idle timeout (s.setTimeout(0), cairn-u7bw) untouched. SOCKS5 path untouched (already bounded by SocksClient timeout). Verified: fail() rejects, attach() sets this.connectingSocket synchronously. Tests: electrum 26 pass (2 new, deterministic via mocked socket + fake timers, load-bearing); svelte-check 0/0.

title: combineMultisigPsbts adopts finalScriptWitness from untrusted PSBT
description: multisigPsbt.ts:652-661 — unlike partialSig path (key membership + SIGHASH_ALL enforced), finalization fields are copied verbatim once !curFinal. A cosigner can attach garbage finalScriptWitness with zero real signatures. assertSameTransaction passes (inputs/outputs unchanged). Draft is durably marked ready-to-broadcast but network rejects it. Honest signers can't recover — only owner deleting/rebuilding. Availability DoS on shared funds. Fix: validate finalization fields or re-derive from partialSig.
close_reason: Fixed on track/signing @ f6a6837, merged @ 5f5aa72, final SHA 66f0c36.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Reprioritized P2->P1 per 2026-07-12 beads audit (docs/BEADS-AUDIT.md sec 2/7): untrusted finalScriptWitness adopted in multisig combine, contradicts MANUAL.md sec 18.9's tampered-PSBT-rejected claim, underweighted at P2.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Fixed on track/signing @ f6a6837 (fix(bitcoin): validate pre-existing finalization at the multisig finalize entry, commit f6a6837 within that branch). combineMultisigPsbts and finalizeMultisigPsbt no longer adopt finalScriptWitness/finalScriptSig verbatim from an untrusted PSBT: both combine entry points and the finalizeMultisigPsbt pre-loop now validate structural binding (script + DER SIGHASH_ALL + quorum count) before treating an input as already-finalized. New tests in multisigPsbt.test.ts (+130 lines). Merged into single-sig-full-wallet at 5f5aa72. Full suite green post-merge (2411 passed / 1 skipped). Final pushed SHA: 66f0c36. Residual: validation is structural, not full cryptographic signature verification against descriptor pubkeys -- filed as a separate P3 follow-up task, tagged hardening-followup (same follow-up bead as cairn-u2r5's residual, covers both).

title: Wallet/account deletion cascades away broadcast records, bypassing the up0q delete guard
description: Found by independent review of 94ddd83 (cairn-up0q). cairn-up0q hardened per-transaction delete (deleteTransaction/deleteMultisigTransaction) against erasing a mid/post-broadcast record. But deleteWallet (wallets.ts:283-291) does an unconditional DELETE FROM wallets WHERE id=? AND user_id=?, and transactions.wallet_id has ON DELETE CASCADE (db.ts:83), so deleting a whole wallet removes every transaction row regardless of status — including rows with a live broadcast_started_at claim or a completed/superseded broadcast record. accountDeletion.ts has no guard either. Same likely applies to deleteMultisig + multisig_transactions cascade (unverified). NUANCE for triage: unlike deleting one pending tx, deleting a WHOLE wallet is a deliberate total-removal intent, so cascading its tx history is arguably correct — the residual concern is only the narrow race where a wallet delete lands concurrently with an in-flight broadcast, erasing a just-broadcast record. Decide whether to (a) block wallet/account deletion while any child tx has broadcast_started_at IS NOT NULL, or (b) accept it as intended destructive behavior. SECONDARY nit from the same review: db.ts:337 multisig_transactions.status column comment still reads 'draft | awaiting_signature | completed' and omits 'superseded'.
close_reason: Fixed at bbea667: deleteWallet/deleteMultisig now refuse (AuthError broadcast_in_progress) while any child transaction has a LIVE (<60s) broadcast_started_at claim, mirroring deleteTransaction's cairn-up0q guard and broadcastTransaction's own 60s staleness window. Completed/superseded transactions and stale claims don't block a deliberate whole-object delete (per the bead's own nuance). Also closed the accountDeletion.ts/admin.ts gap the bead flagged as unguarded (hasLiveBroadcastClaimForUser checked before purgeUserRow in both deleteOwnAccount and deleteUser) and fixed the stale multisig_transactions.status column comment (db.ts:348, missing 'superseded'). 12 new targeted tests across wallets.test.ts, wallets-multisig.test.ts, accountDeletion.test.ts, userDeletion.test.ts, all passing; full related suite (transactions/multisigTransactions/admin/deleteCascade/etc, 200+ tests) green; svelte-check 0 errors.

title: Test hygiene: electrum/client.test.ts uses a real-timer timeout assertion (CI-flaky)
description: src/lib/server/electrum/client.test.ts:151-162's timeout test uses real Date.now() and a real 200ms timer with a 150ms-1500ms assertion window -- a classic CI-flake shape where a slow runner can tip either bound. Related real-timer waits at lines 168, 239. Fix: use vi.useFakeTimers(). Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Replaced the CI-flaky wall-clock assertion window (elapsed >=150ms && <1500ms) in the request-timeout test with a deterministic fake-timer drive: warm the connection under real timers so handshake IO completes, then vi.useFakeTimers({toFake:['setTimeout','clearTimeout']}) and advanceTimersByTimeAsync(200) to fire the timeout exactly. Restricted faking to setTimeout only (leaving setImmediate/IO real) so the loopback socket keeps working and vitest's own tick loop isn't disturbed; attach the .rejects assertion before advancing so the timer-driven rejection is never momentarily unhandled. Added vi.useRealTimers() safety-net in afterEach. Lines 168/239 real-timer waits are event-sequencing delays (not tight assertion windows) and were left as-is — forcing fake timers onto their real-socket IO reintroduces tick collisions. 12 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: Desktop nav rail: add persistent text labels (icon-only rail illegible to first-timers)
description: Visual audit (2026-07-12) confirmed by screenshot + zoom: the desktop left rail in src/lib/components/heartwood/HWRail.svelte is a ~55px strip of stacked icons (logo-ring, grid, target, wallet, activity-squiggle, gear, a second ring-mark, a second squiggle, avatar) with no visible text labels. A non-technical first-timer cannot tell that the 'target' icon is Explorer or what the ring-marks do, and two pairs of icons look nearly identical at that size. Sharp inconsistency with the mobile shell, which has an excellent labeled tab row (Home / Explorer / Wallets / Activity). Best-in-class (Muun/Cash App/Strike) all use labeled tabs. Fix: add persistent text labels beside the rail icons (always-on, not expand-on-hover -- hover is insufficient for first-timers) on desktop >= the 940px content width, and differentiate the two look-alike icon pairs. Searched for overlap first: br search for 'rail'/'icon-only'/'HWRail'/'text label' returned no matches -- filing new.

VERIFICATION:

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Scout verdicts: (1) USB: jadets@1.1.20 exact-pin (de-facto standard, Caravan ships it; Blockstream has no official JS SDK). Vite: alias-stub tiny-secp256k1/bip32/crypto, never call getMasterFingerPrint (use scure xfpFromXpub client-side), Buffer global needed, optimizeDeps.include jadets+cbor2. Web Serial gate; pinserver blind-oracle relay for PIN-locked devices (jadepin.blockstream.com; already-unlocked = zero network — say so in copy). Registration: 16-slot device store, deterministic name = sanitized prefix + 6-hex sha256 of canonical descriptor (re-register same name silently REPLACES), firmware >=1.0.27 for getMultiSigName dedupe. All three script types supported. get_xpub is silent by protocol — no path-confirm exists. (2) QR MODE: Jade speaks BC-UR crypto-psbt ONLY, NOT BBQr — existing QrSigner does not cover it. Per Alex both modes are wanted: add urPsbt.ts with @ngraveio/bc-ur@1.1.13 (+@keystonehq/bc-ur-registry for crypto-psbt CBOR; avoid the 2.0 beta) and a QrSigner format toggle. (3) COPY BUG today: DevicePicker.svelte:26 + wallets send +page.svelte:630 list Jade under the BBQr QR blurb — move Jade to its own tiles, drop from that blurb. Full brief in scratchpad/bitbox-jade-brief.md.

title: Backup filename convention not applied to ColdCard/descriptor exports — inconsistent with the new dated cairn-<name>-backup-<date> pattern
description: commit 588aa0a introduced a standard cairn-<name>-backup-<date>.json filename for the Caravan export (src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/caravan/+server.ts) and the new single-sig config export (src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/config/+server.ts), but left src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/coldcard/+server.ts:37 and src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/descriptor/+server.ts:38 on the old cairn-multisig-<name>-coldcard.txt / -descriptor.txt pattern — no date segment, no '-backup-' token. A user backing up one multisig wallet via the wallet detail page's three adjacent download buttons (Wallet config JSON, ColdCard file, Descriptor) ends up with three inconsistently-shaped filenames, two of which carry no date and can't be freshness-compared after a key rotation or re-download — undermining the commit's stated goal of a standard, comparable naming convention. Fix: apply the same dated cairn-<name>-backup-<date>-<kind>.<ext> pattern to the coldcard and descriptor endpoints.
close_reason: ColdCard and multisig-descriptor exports now use the standard dated cairn-<name>-backup-<date>-<kind>.<ext> filename (coldcard.txt / descriptor.txt), matching the caravan/config/backup-pdf exports. Also aligned the single-sig descriptor export to -backup-<date>-descriptor.txt. All export buttons on a wallet now emit consistent, freshness-comparable filenames.

title: Missing regression test: broadcast routes swallow malformed JSON body instead of 400 (cairn-1yw7)
description: cairn-1yw7 (P2, closed in 46dd16f) fixed both wallet and multisig broadcast routes doing readJson(event).catch(() => ({psbt: undefined})), which silently discarded readJson's deliberate 'malformed JSON body' 400 error and proceeded as if no PSBT were attached — on the single most irreversible action in the app. Fix confirmed present, no test exists (grep for readJson/malformed/'Invalid JSON' across all *.test.ts returns zero hits for this scenario). Add a test in src/lib/server/api.test.ts (or a route-level test) asserting a malformed JSON body to either broadcast route returns 400 'Invalid JSON body', not a silent no-op.
close_reason: Committed in 8603ac6: multisigShares.test.ts (first coverage), contacts.test.ts share-revocation, notifications.test.ts stage isolation, broadcast route malformed-JSON 400 + log.error tests, walletsDraft.test.ts buildDraft/coin-control coverage.

title: UX fiat sweep: Amount.svelte everywhere balances/amounts render BTC-only
close_reason: SHIPPED: merged to single-sig-full-wallet 528d95b. Fiat via Amount.svelte on wallet heroes (single+multisig), tx rows/fees, address tabs, saved/pending, activity feed (+copyable txid), explorer address; raw scriptType fallback removed; dead CSS dropped. check/build/full-suite green. Post-merge visual pass on populated instance pending as follow-up evidence.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Post-merge visual pass on populated instance: fiat mode live via /api/price, all Amount surfaces fiat-primary, network-fee labels, explorer plain-language script type, danger-zone bullets, quorum panel GREEN/SALMON verified. 7/8 checks PASS; mobile viewport check blocked by env resize quirk (pre-merge mobile QA already green).

title: createMultisig is not transactional — partial failure leaves corrupt state
description: wallets/multisig.ts:328-366 — multisigs row INSERT and N separate multisig_keys INSERTs are independent auto-committed statements. No db.transaction() wrapper. A failure partway through key-insert loop leaves a multisigs row whose threshold/key count doesnt match actual multisig_keys rows. Every downstream consumer assumes this cant happen. Grep shows zero uses of transaction wrapper in entire src/lib/server. Fix: wrap multi-row writes in db.transaction().
close_reason: Transactional BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK wrapper added to createMultisig; atomicity tests green.

comments.author: deep-reasoner
comments.text: FIXED. Root cause: createMultisig (wallets/multisig.ts) wrote the multisigs row, the receive_cursor UPDATE, and the N multisig_keys rows as separate auto-committed statements with no transaction — a failure partway through the key-insert loop left a multisigs row whose stored threshold/key count disagreed with the multisig_keys present. Fix: wrapped the multi-row write in BEGIN/COMMIT with ROLLBACK-on-error (node:sqlite has no .transaction(); drove db.exec('BEGIN'/'COMMIT'/'ROLLBACK') like backup.ts). Tests (wallets-multisig.test.ts > 'createMultisig is transactional (cairn-vzvw)'): mock db.prepare to throw on the multisig_keys INSERT after the multisigs row inserts, assert error propagates AND both table counts are 0; plus happy-path all-N-rows-together.

title: Canvas heartwood components stay theme-unaware in light mode
description:
cairn-sdx5.7 (app-wide light mode rollout) promoted the Daylight-grove
light token set from Explorer-only page-root classes to a real
:root/data-theme mechanism (src/app.css), so CSS-var-driven components
now retint correctly in light mode. Two components draw with a raw
canvas 2D context instead of CSS, so they cannot read custom
properties at all and keep their Wave-1 DARK literal fills regardless
of theme:

- src/lib/components/heartwood/ChainStrip.svelte — fillStyle '#111716'
  base + strokeStyle '#d4e5f4'/'#6796c9' (dark bg-strip/accent hex).
  Used on /explorer and /explorer/block/[id], both light-mode-reachable
  today (Explorer already had a light theme pre-sdx5.7). Verified this
  renders as a dark rounded strip on the parchment page — but it is
  fully bounded (border-radius, canvas paints its own opaque
  background, no gaps) so it reads as an intentional bounded
  "instrument glass" panel per DESIGN-MANIFESTO.md §4's density-panel
  language, not as broken chrome. No containment change made; left as
  interim-acceptable.
- src/lib/components/heartwood/FirstSyncGrowth.svelte — radial gradient
  colorstops '#1c2523'/'#141b19'/'#090d0c' + fillStyle '#d4e5f4'. Used
  only on /sync inside a fixed 480x480px (mobile: min(78vw,320px))
  .wood container that the canvas fills edge-to-edge with its own
  paint, so it is also fully bounded/self-contained — same
  interim-acceptable verdict.

Lower-priority, same root cause, not fully audited:
- src/lib/components/heartwood/GroveField.svelte — the background-wash
  rings are literal `rgba(103, 150, 201, alpha)` (the DARK accent hex)
  baked into a CSS `background` gradient stack instead of `var(--accent)`,
  so the faint decorative rings don't retint in light mode either (low
  alpha, "felt not read" per its own doc comment, so likely low-impact).
- src/lib/components/heartwood/HeartwoodMark.svelte's 'ink' variant and
  the drop-shadow glow filters in RingStub.svelte/QuorumArc.svelte/
  EpochDial.svelte (`rgba(103, 150, 201, 0.5)`) also hardcode the dark
  accent hex rather than reading var(--accent) — minor glow-color drift
  in light mode, not a broken-background case.

None of the above are broadcast-blocking or unreadable; this bead is
to eventually wire these to the DESIGN-MANIFESTO.md §8 hand-sync list's
"shared JS token-mirror" recommendation (a getComputedStyle-based
constants module) so canvas/gradient-literal components repaint on
theme change instead of drifting further each time the palette moves.

title: Copy-to-clipboard buttons fail on HTTP deployments (Umbrel)
description: navigator.clipboard.writeText requires a secure context (HTTPS or localhost). Umbrel serves Cairn over plain HTTP at umbrel.local, so navigator.clipboard is undefined and every copy button silently fails. Affected call sites: src/lib/components/CopyText.svelte, src/routes/(app)/admin/invites/+page.svelte, src/routes/(app)/recovery-setup/+page.svelte, src/routes/(app)/explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.svelte. Fix: shared copyToClipboard() util with document.execCommand('copy') textarea fallback for insecure contexts; use it at all call sites.
close_reason: copyToClipboard() fallback shipped and verified in both secure and insecure contexts

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Verified end-to-end in Chrome against the dev server: (1) simulated Umbrel HTTP by defineProperty navigator.clipboard=undefined + isSecureContext=false, real trusted click on 'Copy all 12 words' in /recovery-setup -> execCommand('copy') returned true and pasting into a scratch textarea reproduced the exact 12-word phrase; (2) regression check without overrides (secure localhost) -> navigator.clipboard path still copies the exact phrase. Fix: new src/lib/clipboard.ts copyToClipboard() with textarea+execCommand fallback (preserves prior selection, restores it after); swapped in at all 4 call sites: CopyText.svelte, admin/invites, recovery-setup, explorer/tx. svelte-check: 0 errors.

title: Explorer/mempool API routes leak raw Electrum host:port in error responses
description: Live-verified on umbrel-s15 v0.2.22: GET /api/mempool/fees returns {"error":"Electrum connection closed (electrum.blockstream.info:50002)"} to any authenticated user. cairn-sgtr/cairn-cdbb covered wallet surfaces; the explorer/mempool routes (fees, blocks, projected, and siblings that call the chain service directly) were out of scope. Apply sanitizeChainError from chainErrors.ts.
close_reason: Fixed in 58f09db (integrated): sanitizeChainError applied to 8 explorer/mempool/chain API routes + 4 explorer page-server loads; leaky chainErrorMessage helper retired; live-verified leak on umbrel-s15 (/api/mempool/fees) is the pinned regression test.

title: auth.ts, webauthn.ts, and rateLimit.ts have zero logging -- security events invisible to admin logs
description: src/lib/server/auth.ts and src/lib/server/webauthn.ts have no logger import at all, so failed login attempts and WebAuthn ceremony failures never reach the structured log pipeline. src/lib/server/rateLimit.ts (brute-force throttling for login/recovery/invites) also has zero logging -- trips are tracked only in an in-memory map and surfaced solely via in-app notify() to the affected user, never to the operator-facing log. An operator watching /admin/logs during an active credential-stuffing or recovery-brute-force attempt would see nothing beyond generic 4xx request noise. Fix: add log.warn calls on failed auth attempts, WebAuthn ceremony errors, and rate-limit trips (with hashed/truncated key plus count). Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in d56e77f

title: Sync engine Phase 1: wire Electrum status-hash into dirty-tracking, skip clean rescans
description:
gapLimitScanner.ts always full-rescans up to 400 addrs/chain on every 20s-throttled refresh (doWalletScan/doMultisigScan in walletSync.ts) -- never checks whether anything changed. addressWatcher.ts:1021 onScripthash literally discards the Electrum status-hash argument it receives -- the subscription infra and the balance/data path are completely disjoint despite the wallet already holding live scripthash subscriptions for every watched address.

Bonus bug found during audit: addressWatcher.ts:45 WATCH_WINDOW=30 is a FIXED window from index 0, not gap-trimmed -- wallets with >30 used addresses on one chain have addresses beyond index 30 that are never subscribed at all. Fix this in the same bead (live notification-correctness bug, not just perf).

Fix (Phase 1, ~150-250 LOC -- rejected the full normalized wallet_addresses/wallet_txs/wallet_utxos rewrite as Phase 1; that's ~2000-2500 LOC with a hard tx-eviction/refcounting risk under reorg/RBF and a scan-storm-on-migration-backfill problem; gate that as Phase 2, its own bead, opened only if Phase 1 proves insufficient): persist last-seen status per scripthash, mark wallet dirty on real status change, skip scanWallet/getMultisigDetail entirely when clean, use subscribeScripthash's return value as a free reconciliation checkpoint on every (re)connect, not just a periodic sweep.

Acceptance criteria MUST include: reorg, RBF replacement, and reconnect-after-outage all correctly mark dirty (status-hash changes on confirmations too, not just new txs). Send/spend path (getWalletUtxos/getMultisigUtxos) stays on live Electrum unchanged -- already narrow, already guarded (walletSync.ts:24), do not touch.

Medium risk -- a false-clean bug silently shows stale balances. Do NOT run concurrently with the derivation-memoization bead (both touch addressWatcher.ts) -- sequence after it, single-session only (concurrent-session branch hazard is a repeat offender in this repo).
close_reason: Implemented + shipped @ 960ed39; suite 3455 green, svelte-check clean; acceptance criteria covered by tests (see comment).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Phase 1 design landed: docs/SYNC-ENGINE-PHASE1-DESIGN.md (Fable deep-reasoner, 2026-07-13).

Verified both audit findings against current code (bead line numbers had drifted as the file grew):
- Status-hash discarded: subscribeScripthash RETURNS status (electrum/client.ts:626-632) and the 'scripthash' event carries (sh,status) on both live change (client.ts:501) and reconnect replay (client.ts:439), forwarded verbatim by pool.ts:95. WatchState.onScripthash even DECLARES the second arg (addressWatcher.ts:87) but the installed closure ignores it -- state.onScripthash = (sh) => {...} is at :1283 (bead said :1021), and the subscribeScripthash return at refreshWatches :1184 is awaited-and-dropped. Finding holds.
- WATCH_WINDOW=30 fixed-from-0 window (addressWatcher.ts:45, walletAddresses :182) vs scanner gap-trim to lastUsed+GAP_LIMIT (gapLimitScanner.ts:107-109): a >30-used-address chain has live addresses that are never subscribed. This is a PREREQUISITE for dirty-tracking, not just a bonus -- the dirty signal is only sound if the watch set covers the scanned set.

Design decisions:
- Data model: persist scripthash_status (last-seen status baseline, survives restart -> cheap+correct reconnect reconciliation) + wallet_snapshots.dirty_since column (NULL=clean; ms-epoch=marked-dirty). Per-wallet dirtiness (a scan re-scans the whole wallet). Both additive, PRAGMA-guarded like the existing summary-column migration (db.ts:497-504).
- Invalidation: live status-change in the watcher handler; reconnect/client-swap reconciliation using subscribeScripthash's now-captured return; user actions (create=absent snapshot, send-broadcast marks dirty, receive-cursor advance); MAX_CLEAN_TTL fallback (~30min, tunable) as the self-healing net. Status hash covers new-tx/confirmation/reorg/RBF (hashes heights) -> meets the acceptance criteria.
- Refresh gate: singleFlightThrottled (walletSync.ts:313) skips the scan when clean AND within MAX_CLEAN_TTL; scans + clears dirty_since on SUCCESSFUL persist (preserves the failed-scan-never-clobbers contract, :21-24); runPortfolioRefreshPass (:682) skips clean items -- biggest lever for warmAllSnapshots + the periodic pass.
- Send path untouched (getWalletUtxos stays live, :24).

Honest scope: Phase 1 cuts scan FREQUENCY (Electrum round-trips + sync raw-tx CPU + snapshot write per skipped refresh), NOT the sync-SQLite serialization that IS the qyvl/y802 concurrency cliff -- that stays Phase 2 (cairn-1q4b worker threads / async driver swap; normalized tables rejected for Phase 1 per this bead). Rollout is 4 steps (window fix -> schema -> watcher observe-only -> flag-gated clean-skip) each shippable+testable on existing exported seams + the load harness for before/after. Open Qs for Alex in the doc section 5: MAX_CLEAN_TTL value, window-fix approach (snapshot-derived vs raised fixed cap), flag y/n, and confirm cairn-8ubd is parked first (both touch addressWatcher.ts, single-session only).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED + shipped on single-sig-full-wallet @ 960ed39 (feat(sync): electrum status-hash dirty-tracking).

As-built (design doc docs/SYNC-ENGINE-PHASE1-DESIGN.md now marked IMPLEMENTED):
- Schema (db.ts, PRAGMA-guarded/additive): scripthash_status(wallet_kind,wallet_id,scripthash -> status,updated_at) baseline + wallet_snapshots.dirty_since (NULL=clean). Both keyed (wallet_kind,wallet_id) so they match the polymorphic-child shape and are swept by both trg_*_delete_children triggers (added there + to the orphan-purge loop; deleteCascade.test.ts enforces coverage).
- Watcher (addressWatcher.ts): reconcileStatus() persists status + marks the owning wallet dirty on a REAL change or ABSENT baseline; NO-OP on unchanged status. Wired on (a) the live 'scripthash' event and (b) subscribeScripthash's now-captured return value -- so initial subscribe, client swap, AND the reconnect resubscribe replay all reconcile. Independent of the notification baseline gate; never notifies.
- WATCH_WINDOW blind-spot fix (prerequisite): watchDepthFor() sizes per-chain subscription depth = highest used index (from persisted snapshot) + GAP_LIMIT, floored at 30. A >30-used-address chain no longer has unsubscribed live addresses. Both single-sig and multisig snapshot shapes.
- Refresh gate (walletSync.ts): shouldSkipScan() -- force => scan; no snapshot => scan (never skip first scan); CLEAN => skip up to MAX_CLEAN_TTL_MS (30 min); DIRTY => only coalesce within THROTTLE_MS then rescan. Used by BOTH singleFlightThrottled and runPortfolioRefreshPass (warm + periodic pass). On successful persist, clearDirtyIfUnchanged() compare-and-swaps dirty_since->NULL only if unchanged from scan start (mid-scan status change => stays dirty => rescans). Failed scan never clears. Send/spend path untouched.
- Kill-switch: CAIRN_SYNC_DISABLE_DIRTY_SKIP / HEARTWOOD_SYNC_DISABLE_DIRTY_SKIP collapses the clean ceiling back to THROTTLE_MS. Chose an ENV var over a feature-flag registry entry (that registry is admin/user-facing with userMessage -- wrong instrument for an ops toggle).

Acceptance criteria covered by deterministic tests:
- status-unchanged => scan skipped: walletSync.test.ts "a CLEAN wallet past the throttle but within TTL is served from cache -- no scan" (scanWallet NOT called after mockClear) + shouldSkipScan clean-window units.
- status-changed => rescan: "a DIRTY wallet past the throttle rescans and the scan clears the flag"; reconcileStatus "re-marks dirty when the status actually changes".
- confirmation/reorg/RBF mark dirty: status hash covers heights => any changes the hash => reconcileStatus marks dirty (addressWatcherDirty.test.ts).
- reconnect-after-outage => conservative invalidation: reconcile fires on resubscribe replay + subscribe-return; ABSENT baseline => dirty test locks the conservative default.
- first-seen always scanned: shouldSkipScan "never skips the FIRST scan (no snapshot yet)".
- missed-notification safety net: MAX_CLEAN_TTL rescan (shouldSkipScan "older than the ceiling" + runPortfolioRefreshPass dirty-aware test).
- mid-scan race: clearDirtyIfUnchanged CAS units (unchanged->clear, value-moved->stays dirty).
- WATCH_WINDOW: watchDepthFor tests (extends to 55 for a used index-34 chain; multisig shape; unused ignored; floor fallback).

Evidence: full suite 3455 passed | 1 skipped (baseline 3433; +23 new tests). svelte-check: 0 errors / 0 warnings.

Measured skip lever: a CLEAN wallet re-refreshed at 25s (past THROTTLE_MS, within TTL) executes ZERO scanWallet calls vs TWO per refresh pre-change (doWalletScan's scanWallet + getWalletUtxos' scanWallet) -- the whole Electrum scan + raw-tx CPU + snapshot write is elided per skipped refresh. Multiplies across warmAllSnapshots (every user at boot) and the periodic portfolio pass, which previously re-scanned every wallet regardless.

title: Vault creation wizard a11y/mobile polish sweep
description:
Batch of independent a11y/mobile findings from auditing the vault creation wizard and send flow -- fine to fix together:
1. Cosigner slot-remove buttons (vaults/new/+page.svelte ~1887-1898, .slot-remove) are ~26x26px, under the ~44x44px touch-target guidance, with no mobile breakpoint bump.
2. Custom M-of-N threshold input fields (vaults/new/+page.svelte ~679-689, .custom-inputs) have no mobile breakpoint to stack vertically -- may overflow at 375px.
3. The signing-method card grid (Trezor/Ledger/ColdCard/QR/Paste, vaults/new/+page.svelte ~1923-1947) uses repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px, 1fr)) with no explicit mobile rule -- may produce cramped single-wide cards with hard-to-read labels at 375px.
4. QR scanning video element (vaults/new/+page.svelte ~1011) has a svelte-ignore a11y_media_has_caption pragma suppressing the linter without actually addressing it -- add a live-region announcement ("Camera scanning in progress...") so screen reader users know a camera feed is active and what to do.
5. Vault signer components' file upload/download controls (VaultFileSigner.svelte ~156-201) rely on surrounding paragraph text for context rather than descriptive button text or aria-describedby -- icon+short-label buttons like 'Download registration file' don't carry enough standalone context for screen reader users navigating by control.
6. Device-unavailable explanations (VaultTrezorSigner.svelte ~141-150) aren't semantically grouped with their disabled control (no aria-describedby linking the reason text to the button), so screen reader users navigating by control may not discover why a device option is greyed out.
close_reason: All six items: wizard touch targets/stacked inputs/single-column grids/QR live region (146ebfb), signer download/input accessible names + described-by fallbacks (this commit).

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Items 1-4 committed (touch targets, stacked custom inputs, single-column grids, QR live region — live-verified at 375px). Items 5-6 target the signer components under wallets/multisig/[id]/send/_components, owned by the running stepper agent — relayed there.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Items 5-6 done in the multisig send flow signer components: (5) MultisigFileSigner download/upload/paste controls got standalone accessible names (registration + .psbt download aria-labels with wallet/key context, stable aria-label on the file input, aria-label on the paste textarea); same treatment for the QR register-callout download on the send page. (6) Trezor/Ledger device-unavailable reasons now carry ids and the 'Use the file method' fallback buttons reference them via aria-describedby.

title: Vault quorum signing-time warning
description: Vault send flow: each of M signers processes the full mass — total time = M x per-device estimate. Casa-summary Impact line gains 'approximately 15-20 minutes across all N signing devices' when mass is high, per-key device-specific estimates on the stepper chips. Blocked on the Trezor/Ledger stepper wiring task finishing (owns the vault send page).
close_reason: Committed: Review/Sign ceremony warnings from signingMass.warnLevel, per-key device estimates on stepper chips, vault utxo-mass endpoint + live coin-list estimate line (client-side math after one lazy fetch). Vault coin control doesn't exist so the live line attached to the Review coins list — documented adaptation.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Scope extension from Alex: (1) vault SEND — Casa Impact line shows totalSeconds (per-signer x M) with amber (>10min) / red (>30min or timeout risk) tones from signingMass.warnLevel; red includes the split suggestion; per-key device-specific estimates on stepper chips. (2) vault COIN CONTROL — live estimate line updating as UTXOs are (de)selected: 'Selected 3 UTXOs - Estimated signing mass: HIGH - ~8 min per signer x 4 signers = ~32 min', using the pure estimateSigningSeconds export from signingMass.ts (client-callable math on utxo-mass data) so no server round trip per click; suggest removing high-mass UTXOs when extreme. Depends on cairn-194's v2 contract + the stepper wiring agent releasing the vault send page.

title: wallet_config_import flag OFF doesn't hide 'Import it' entry point in multisig wizard
description:
Found during cairn-1ytc flag-matrix QA pass (wallet-flags subset, port 5314, HEARTWOOD_DB=data/qa-matrix.db). Third instance of the same pattern as cairn-de7e (address_book) and cairn-puyb (wallet_config_export) -- API correctly gated server-side, client UI doesn't follow the flag.

REPRO: Admin -> Feature flags -> toggle "Import wallet config" OFF (wallet_config_import flag). As a plain user, visit /wallets/multisig/new. The "Already have this wallet in another app? Import it" button is still present and clickable (confirmed live via DOM query right after toggling the flag off -- button text/existence unchanged). Server-side, requireFeature(event, 'wallet_config_import') IS correctly wired at src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts:297 and src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/import/+server.ts:75, so an actual import submission should fail -- but the entry point isn't hidden or disabled first, so a user can walk through the whole paste/scan-config flow before hitting a late failure.

ROOT CAUSE (code-read confirmed): src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte has no `data.flags?.wallet_config_import` (or similar) check anywhere -- the only flags reference in the file is `cameraDisabled={page.data.flags?.qr_scan === false}` at line 1999 for the camera-scan sub-feature. The "Import it" toggle buttons (lines 1216 and 1526) and the "Have this wallet in Sparrow, Caravan, or another app? Import its wallet config" panel (line 1294) are unconditionally rendered.

IMPACT: minor/P3 -- late failure instead of a hidden entry point (not a data leak), but a confusing dead-end UX and the same regression class the parent bead cairn-1ytc calls out.

FIX (not done here -- flagged only, owned by another session per current branch ownership): gate the "Import it" buttons/panel in src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte behind `data.flags?.wallet_config_import !== false`, mirroring the qr_scan camera gate already in the same file.

Filed by: OPS QA session (cairn-1ytc wallet-flags subset), 2026-07-12.

title: Umbrel secure-context fix: serve self-signed HTTPS so WebHID/WebUSB/camera work
description: umbrel.local over plain HTTP is not a secure context, so WebHID (Ledger/BitBox02), Web Serial (Jade USB), WebUSB, camera QR-scan-back, and WebAuthn are all unavailable — blocking hardware signing on the primary deployment target. Bridge-based workarounds are fragile (Caravan/BitBoxBridge 'worked once then stopped' — session-scoped origin approval + Chrome LNA gating). Chosen fix: Cairn terminates TLS itself on a second port with a self-signed cert generated at first boot and persisted in the data dir; Umbrel compose maps the extra port; the UI detects !isSecureContext and offers the https:// address with plain-language one-time-warning guidance. Evaluated and rejected: WebHID (same secure-context rule as WebUSB), app_proxy TLS (unsupported), Chrome flag (docs-only), custom bridge daemon (fragile, LNA-gated). Pending verification: WebHID/WebUSB must actually function after a bypassed cert interstitial.
close_reason: Built and verified. Cairn serves a self-signed HTTPS listener (server.mjs + scripts/tls-cert.mjs, CAIRN_HTTPS_PORT default 3443 in the image, cert persisted 10y in <data>/tls) and the UI offers the secure address from all USB-signing surfaces via SecureContextHelp. Live-browser verification confirms WebHID/WebUSB/Web Serial + clipboard + camera all function after the one-time (weekly) cert bypass; WebAuthn/service workers do not — copy directs users to password login there. Committed cd887bb + copy follow-up; Umbrel compose (3212→3443) staged in caravan-store-cairn for the 0.1.6 release. Remaining: real-device pass on the Umbrel box after 0.1.6 ships.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Implemented. (1) scripts/tls-cert.mjs — standalone self-signed cert module (selfsigned pkg, 10y validity, SANs umbrel.local/localhost/*.local/127.0.0.1, persisted key.pem+cert.pem, regenerates when <30d left or unparsable; unit-tested in src/tests/tlsCert.test.ts). (2) server.mjs — new prod entry: adapter-node handler on HTTP (PORT) + HTTPS listener when CAIRN_HTTPS_PORT is set; cert dir defaults to <CAIRN_DB dir>/tls; a cert failure logs and degrades to HTTP-only, never takes the app down. adapter-node's get_origin falls back to 'https' when x-forwarded-proto is absent, so cookies/CSRF are correct on the direct TLS port. (3) Dockerfile: CMD node server.mjs, CAIRN_HTTPS_PORT=3443 default, EXPOSE 3443. (4) (app) layout exposes httpsPort (CAIRN_HTTPS_EXTERNAL_PORT wins) to the client. (5) New SecureContextHelp.svelte — renders ONLY on insecure pages when the listener is advertised; links to https://<hostname>:<port><same path> with plain-language one-time-cert-warning guidance; wired into LedgerSigner/JadeUsbSigner/BitboxSigner, the send-page method grid, and both wizards' device panes (Ledger/Jade/BitBox02 connect boxes + camera-unavailable QR states; Trezor excluded — its popup works over HTTP). Umbrel compose in caravan-store-cairn updated (host port 3212 → container 3443 + CAIRN_HTTPS_EXTERNAL_PORT), takes effect with the 0.1.6 image. Verified: prod build boots both listeners, TLS handshake serves CN=umbrel.local, /login + /api/health 200 over https; 1610 tests pass; svelte-check clean. Pending: background research on whether Chrome fully enables WebHID after a bypassed cert interstitial (Home Assistant ecosystem precedent says yes); real-device test on the Umbrel box.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Verification verdict (live experiment: Chrome 149/Win11, real self-signed cert on a LAN-IP origin, interstitial bypassed via certificateErrorPageController.proceed(), APIs probed under trusted user activation; 2 clean runs + Chromium source evidence): (1) isSecureContext=true after bypass — scheme-based per W3C spec. (2) WebHID/WebUSB/Web Serial WORK — choosers open normally, and Chromium source has NO cert gate in content/browser/{usb,hid,serial} (unlike WebAuthn and Service Workers, which were simultaneously blocked on the same test page — proving the state was genuinely cert-error). (3) navigator.clipboard works; (4) getUserMedia (camera QR) works — passed the security layer. (5) Bypass persists 7 days per host+cert-fingerprint+error (kCertErrorBypassExpirationInSeconds=604800), survives browser restart, resets if the cert changes — Cairn's persisted 10y cert is exactly right; never regenerate per boot. (6) WebAuthn BLOCKED (AuthenticatorStatus::CERTIFICATE_ERROR, IsSecurityLevelAcceptableForWebAuthn) — passkeys can never work on the self-signed port; password login is the path (SecureContextHelp copy updated to say so, and to set the ~weekly re-warning expectation). (7) Service workers blocked on that port (no PWA install there). (8) Brave caveat: Web Serial off by default + shields interfere with WebUSB identity; Chrome/Edge behave as tested. Full sources + raw results in the session transcript.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: SHIPPED in v0.1.6 (2026-07-07): public 273725d, tag v0.1.6, image ghcr.io/alexm223/cairn:0.1.6@sha256:616ea083129a57261911d30550db34516163d1342416231dbdbaf188c46aeedb (amd64+arm64 verified), store commit 48be233 (compose 3212->3443 + CAIRN_HTTPS_EXTERNAL_PORT, manifest 0.1.6). Update-path 0.1.5->0.1.6 smoke-tested locally on a named Docker volume: pre-update admin login persists, server.mjs entrypoint boots, HTTPS listener 200s with CN=umbrel.local 10y cert persisted in /data/tls. Still pending: real-device pass on the Umbrel box.

title: Real-time updates everywhere (SSE live stream)
description: mempool.space-feel live UX per docs/LIVE-UPDATES-DESIGN.md; no manual refresh anywhere. docs/LIVE-UPDATES-DESIGN.md is the canonical design doc for this epic — defer to it for architecture, event taxonomy, wiring, and wave scope.

title: Wave 1: liveHub + /api/live + liveClient + confirmationsFor
description: Build liveHub (server) + /api/live endpoint (block+notification folded in first) + liveClient singleton + tipHeight rune store + src/lib/confirmations.ts. Point liveBlocks.ts onNewBlock at the new hub. Wire all existing confirmation displays through confirmationsFor. Old /api/events and /api/notifications/stream become thin shims forwarding from liveHub. Deliverable: live confirmations everywhere. See docs/LIVE-UPDATES-DESIGN.md section 7.
close_reason: Wave 1 shipped: liveHub + /api/live multiplexed SSE + liveClient + tipHeight rune + confirmationsFor; block+notification folded in; chainEvents single process-level block fan-out; liveBlocks delegates to liveClient (API preserved); explorer tx + wallet detail confirmations live via confirmationsFor. 24 new tests, api suite 179 green, full suite 4010 passed/1 skipped, npm run check 0 errors. Old endpoints left as shims (retire Wave 3).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Wave 1 implemented (branch design/evergreen-identity).

NEW FILES:
- src/lib/confirmations.ts — pure confirmationsFor(txBlockHeight, tip): null/undefined/<=0 height -> 0; tip<=0 -> 0; else max(0, tip-height+1) (reorg-clamped).
- src/lib/server/liveHub.ts — Set<LiveConnection>{userId,isAdmin,wantsMempool,send}; publish(topic,scope,data) with scope {broadcast}|{userId}|{admin}; payload serialized once; NEVER reads SQLite. notifyBus bridge subscribes once at module load, computes unreadUserFeedCount ONCE per event (one DB read notify-side, not per client) and publishes a user-scoped notification frame; null-userId events ignored (matches retired stream).
- src/routes/api/live/+server.ts — requireUser; ?topics= suppression hint (only mempool matters now); primes block{height} (via headersSubscribe, which also arms the process-level header pushes) + notification{unread} on connect; ReadableStream + 25s heartbeat + idempotent cleanup; heartbeat detects reconfigureChain() client swap and ends the stream (browser reconnects) — same strategy as /api/events. Does NOT attach its own header listener.
- src/lib/live/liveClient.ts — singleton over ONE EventSource to /api/live; subscribe(topic,handler) refcounted; reconnect/visibility/stale hardening mirrors sseReconnect.ts; SSR-safe.
- src/lib/live/tipHeight.svelte.ts — Svelte 5 rune store seeded 0, fed by block frames, lazily started on first read; monotonic.

EDITED:
- src/lib/server/chainEvents.ts — the existing single process-level electrum.on('header') listener now also calls liveHub.publish('block',{broadcast},{height}) (section 3.3 single fan-out).
- src/lib/liveBlocks.ts — onNewBlock now delegates to liveClient.subscribe('block'); public API + 3s throttle/dedupe preserved; reconnect logic removed (owned by liveClient). All 8 consumer pages unchanged.
- src/routes/(app)/explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.svelte — confCount now prefers confirmationsFor(blockCtx.height, tipHeight.height) via max() once streamed context has a known height, so it climbs live without reload.
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte — confirmationsOf() routed through confirmationsFor with max(snapshotTip, liveTip); tx rows expose per-tx block heights so this is in scope for Wave 1.

SHIMS: /api/events and /api/notifications/stream left as-is (retire in Wave 3) per spec 'simplest safe choice'.

TESTS: confirmations.test.ts (8-case matrix incl. reorg clamp + tip 0), liveHub.test.ts (scope isolation user/broadcast/admin, mempool suppression, cleanup/idempotent, no-conn no-op, throwing-conn resilience, notifyBus bridge one-frame-per-event + null ignored), api/live/server.test.ts (primes block+notification, no self header listener, heartbeat ping, swapped-client end, abort cleanup idempotent). New: 24 tests. src/routes/api: 179 passed. npm run check: 0 errors (pre-existing mining warning only). Full suite: 4010 passed | 1 skipped, 0 failures.

DEFERRED to Wave 2/3 per spec: wallet frames (addressWatcher), mempool/health/mining topics + liveTickers, poll removals, shim retirement, per-connection bounded-queue hardening (P2 follow-up).

title: Wave 2: wallet frames + live balances/tx lists + activity live
description: addressWatcher.ts emits wallet frames (user-scoped) next to existing notify() call sites. Home, wallet detail (single-sig + multisig), and activity feed consume via invalidate-driven strategy. Activity feed's 10s poll removed. Deliverable: balances + tx lists live. See docs/LIVE-UPDATES-DESIGN.md section 7.
close_reason: Wave 2 shipped: wallet frames + live balances/tx lists + live activity feed. All tests green (4016/1 skipped), 0 check errors.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Wave 2 implemented.

SERVER — src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts: user-scoped livePublish('wallet', {userId}, {walletKind, walletId, txid?, event, amountSats?}) added next to each notify() site:
- tx_received (valued): event 'received', amountSats=receivedSats
- tx_large: event 'large', amountSats=receivedSats
- tx_received (unvalued/generic-activity branch): event 'received', amountSats omitted (no new query, per section 3.4)
- tx_confirmed (handleNewBlock): event 'confirmed', amountSats=row.amount_sats ?? undefined
- tx_replaced (reconcileDisappeared): event 'replaced', amountSats=amount, no txid (tx gone on-chain)
All data already in hand at each site — zero new SQLite reads on the publish path (section 3.1 invariant honored).

CLIENT — new src/lib/live/walletEvents.ts: onWalletEvent(cb) wraps liveClient.subscribe('wallet'); debounced(fn, 800) shared trailing-edge coalescer (WALLET_DEBOUNCE_MS=800) with cancel().
- Home ((app)/+page.svelte): wallet frame -> debounced refreshPortfolio(). Home uses client-fetch SWR (GET /api/portfolio), not a depends()/invalidate tag, so the invalidate-driven strategy is a debounced aggregate re-sync rather than invalidate() — noted as a deviation.
- Wallet detail single ((app)/wallets/[id]): frame matching {kind:'wallet', id} -> debounced refresh() (POST /refresh + invalidate('cairn:wallet:<id>')). Used refresh() not bare invalidate because the addressWatcher receive path does not update the persisted snapshot, so bare invalidate would re-read stale data.
- Multisig ((app)/wallets/multisig/[id]): frame matching {kind:'multisig', id} -> debounced refresh() (invalidate('cairn:multisig:<id>')). ALSO rerouted confirmationsOf() through confirmationsFor(height, max(scan.tipHeight, liveTip.height)) — per-tx heights are exposed (tx.height/detail.history[].height), so confirmations now climb live off the shared tip rune like single-sig (section 4.3), instead of its own tipHeight-only math.
- Activity ((app)/activity): removed the 10s setInterval poll AND its now-obsolete 'Auto-refresh' toggle (+ dead .auto CSS); subscribes to 'wallet' AND 'notification' frames -> debounced refresh() of /api/activity; added a visibilitychange return-to-tab refresh as a self-healing net (no replay buffer, section 1.2).

Did NOT touch app.css / (app)/+layout.svelte / HWRail/HWSidebar / mining / mempool / health / shims (Wave 3 + layout agent).

TESTS:
- src/lib/live/walletEvents.test.ts (4): fake-timer debounce — N calls in-window -> 1 invocation; second burst -> 2nd invocation; cancel() drops pending; default 800ms boundary.
- src/lib/server/addressWatcherLive.test.ts (2): spy on liveHub.publish — received frame carries {userId} scope + {walletKind,walletId,txid,event:'received',amountSats:123456}; confirmed frame on next block carries event:'confirmed' with correct userId.

VERIFY: targeted 25 passed. npm run check = 0 errors (1 pre-existing warning in mining/+page.svelte, out of scope). Full npx vitest run = 4016 passed / 1 skipped (baseline 4010/1; +6 new).

Old /api/events and /api/notifications/stream shim endpoints retired. Deliverable: explorer/mining/health live. See docs/LIVE-UPDATES-DESIGN.md section 7.

MEMPOOL TICKER: new src/lib/server/liveTickers.ts — single shared 5s unref'd timer, started from hooks.server.ts init after startMiningEngine. Dormant while liveHub.mempoolSubscriberCount()===0 (no chain read, no frame). Reads the 30s-TTL-CACHED getMempoolSummary/getFeeHistogram (one read per tick per process, no forced refresh), projects mempoolBlocks from the held histogram, diffs vs last emitted, publishes broadcast `mempool` {count,vsizeVb,feeHistogram,mempoolBlocks,updatedAt} on change only. Both mining pages dropped the 10s poll → subscribe(mining|mining:pool) + debounced refetch; visibilitychange kept.

HEALTH: chainEvents.ts publishes broadcast `health` {electrum,tipHeight,tipAgeMs} on connect/disconnect transitions (no new query). Client store src/lib/live/chainHealth.svelte.ts seeds via one /api/chain-health fetch, treats each frame as a nudge to re-read the authoritative union verdict. ChainHealthBanner + Home dropped their 15s polls.

NOTIFICATION TRANSPORT SWAP: NotificationPanel now uses liveClient.subscribe('notification') instead of its own EventSource (behavior identical).

RETIRED SHIMS: deleted src/routes/api/events/+server.ts (+test), src/routes/api/notifications/stream/+server.ts, src/lib/sseReconnect.ts (now unused). Grep confirms no remaining client references; stale comments updated.

TESTS: new liveTickers.test.ts (dormant/change/no-change/single-backend), aggregates flush-nudge tests (user + admin:pool scopes), chainEvents health-frame tests. npm run check = 0 errors (1 pre-existing state_referenced_locally warning, byte-identical to HEAD). Full suite: 4025 passed | 1 skipped (was 4016/1; +9 net after removing the deleted-endpoint test).

Remaining polls in the app: admin logs 5s log-tail poll only (out of scope by design). Follow-ups filed: cairn-102c (P2, explorer landing live mempool), cairn-mzk5 (P3, remove dead HWRail.svelte).

title: liveHub hardening: per-connection bounded queue with drop-and-collapse
description: Add a per-connection bounded outbound queue to liveHub with drop-and-collapse semantics for coalescable topics (e.g. mempool) so a slow client falls behind gracefully instead of buffering unboundedly or blocking the publisher. Explicitly deferred out of Wave 1-3; see docs/LIVE-UPDATES-DESIGN.md section 3.5.

title: Harden non-admin (app) form actions with explicit requireUser — the systemic root cause
description: Root cause (Fable, review-2026-07-07): SvelteKit form actions never run a parent layout load(), so the (app)/+layout.server.ts LOGIN redirect also never gates actions — only the locals.user!.id deref (masked 500) saves the non-admin (app)/* actions today. This is defense-by-accident. Do ONE mechanical sweep adding requireUser(event) as the first line of every (app)/* form action (wallets/*, settings/*, etc.), converting masked-500 -> clean 401 and hardening against a future refactor flipping a ! to ?.. Two anon-reachable actions confirmed to use optional chaining (harmless today, no mutation): wallets/multisig/new key/preview and wallets/new preview. Keep separate from the P0 auth fix so it can't destabilize money-path files. Optionally also evaluate a hooks.server.ts authenticated-non-GET guard for all protected routes (needs a public-POST allowlist: login/signup/logout/agreement/disclosure/setup-admin) — larger change, real false-positive risk, scope carefully.
close_reason: Mechanical sweep done for the non-admin (app)/* surfaces named in the bead. Added requireUser(event) as the first line of every action in: settings/+page.server.ts (profile/password/deleteAccount), settings/devices/+page.server.ts (revokeSession/forgetDevice), settings/tokens/+page.server.ts (create/revoke), wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts (receive/delete), wallets/new/+page.server.ts (preview/create/rememberSharedKey — preview was the bead's named anon-reachable gap), wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts (key/preview only — the bead's two named anon-reachable gaps; create/import already gate via requireFeature which calls requireUser internally, knownKeys already had an explicit locals.user check). Admin (app)/admin/* actions were confirmed OUT of scope — the bead's title and root cause are specifically about NON-admin actions; admin actions already call requireAdmin (which calls requireUser) or an equivalent explicit isAdmin check. Time-boxed per orchestrator direction: did NOT touch wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts's receive/delete actions (same gap, confirmed real) because that file is in active concurrent use by a sibling session's multisig stateless-import/FK-integrity work — filed as follow-up cairn-v27o instead of risking a collision. Regression tests: src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/page.server.test.ts (3 tests) and src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/page.server.test.ts (2 tests), both green; svelte-check clean (0 errors) across the whole sweep. Did not evaluate the optional hooks.server.ts authenticated-non-GET guard mentioned in the bead as a stretch goal — out of time-box.

title: [wallet-pool] Explorer flag gate inconsistent: tx detail renders while /explorer and block pages 403 — wallet rows deep-link into dead block links
description: Live repro: with feature_flags.explorer=0, GET /explorer and /explorer/block/[id] return 403 but /explorer/tx/[txid] renders fully (wallet tx rows deep-link there). From the tx page, the BlockContext block links (e.g. 'in block 111') then 403. Either gate the tx page identically and give wallet rows a non-explorer tx view, or exempt block detail the same way the tx page is exempted. Also relevant: notif-deeplink QA gate relies on the /explorer/tx fallback — a flag-off instance breaks notification deep links the same way.

title: Auth: fetch()-based login returns 200 but session cookie not persisted (/api/auth/me 401 immediately after)
description: Reproduced 2026-07-12 during browser QA on dev instances (:5311 and default dev server). Raw fetch POST to /api/auth/login/password returns 200 + user object, but a follow-up GET /api/auth/me returns 401 immediately after. Suspect Set-Cookie attributes (Secure/SameSite) vs plain-http localhost/preview proxy. UI form-based login also silently no-ops pre-d1cb050. Server/auth domain — NOT for the dev/UX session to fix.
close_reason: Fixed: server.mjs defaults PROTOCOL_HEADER=x-forwarded-proto for unconfigured deployments + scripts/serverProto.mjs fill-when-absent helper on both listeners. Curl-verified against the real built server: plain-HTTP login no longer sets Secure (session now persists); inbound x-forwarded-proto:https still honored (Secure preserved for real reverse-proxy topologies); CSRF-origin check also confirmed correct in both directions. See bead comment for full root cause, curl matrix, and sec sign-off. Tests: src/tests/serverProto.test.ts (5/5), auth.test.ts cookieSecure block unchanged/green, npm run check 0 errors.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE (confirmed 2026-07-13, server-side): adapter-node's `get_origin()` treats
every request as https whenever NEITHER `ORIGIN` NOR `PROTOCOL_HEADER` is configured
(build/handler.js, the compiled adapter-node runtime — see the SvelteKit adapter-node
source around the same logic, `~208-211` in the built handler for this version). Cairn's
server.mjs (bare-node entry point, no reverse proxy) never set either env var, so on an
unconfigured/direct deployment EVERY request — including ones that hit the plain HTTP
listener — "looked" https to adapter-node. src/lib/server/auth.ts:130-140 `cookieSecure()`
follows that (derived) request protocol unless CAIRN_ORIGIN explicitly declares plain
http, so the session cookie got `Secure` stamped on a plain-HTTP response. Browsers
silently drop a `Secure` cookie set over plain HTTP — login returns 200 with a user
object, but the cookie never sticks, so the next request looks unauthenticated. This is
exactly the fetch-200-but-/api/auth/me-401 symptom in the bead title.

EMPIRICAL CURL MATRIX (built prod bundle, `node server.mjs` on a spare port + scratch
DB, no ORIGIN/PROTOCOL_HEADER set — reproducing a bare unproxied deployment):
  - BEFORE the fix: POST /api/auth/login/password over plain http, no
    x-forwarded-proto header -> Set-Cookie carried `Secure` (bug reproduced).
  - AFTER the fix, same request -> `set-cookie: cairn_session=...; Path=/; ...;
    HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax` — NO Secure attribute.
  - AFTER the fix, same request but with an injected `x-forwarded-proto: https`
    header -> `set-cookie: ...; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax` (fill-when-absent
    correctly honors an inbound reverse-proxy header instead of clobbering it).
  - Bonus verification (CSRF-origin, same server): POST to a real form action
    (/setup-admin?/complete) with a matching `Origin: http://127.0.0.1:<port>` ->
    200/redirect (succeeds); same POST with a cross-scheme `Origin: https://...` ->
    403 "Cross-site POST form submissions are forbidden". Confirms SvelteKit's
    built-in CSRF-origin check now derives the correct http origin for the plain
    listener too (it was ALSO broken by the same https-default before this fix,
    though that direction fails closed rather than silently).

FIX (fill-when-absent, not an unconditional overwrite):
  1. server.mjs now defaults `PROTOCOL_HEADER=x-forwarded-proto` when the operator
     has set neither `ORIGIN` nor `PROTOCOL_HEADER` (respects an explicit operator
     choice either way).
  2. New pure helper `scripts/serverProto.mjs` `fillForwardedProto(headers, proto)` —
     sets `x-forwarded-proto` to the listener's own protocol ONLY when the header is
     absent; never overwrites an inbound value. Wired into both server.mjs listener
     wrappers (http fills 'http', https fills 'https').
  3. Dockerfile updated: runtime image now also copies scripts/serverProto.mjs
     (server.mjs's new dependency), plus a doc note next to the existing
     ADDRESS_HEADER note explaining the new default and that a TLS-terminating
     reverse proxy in front of the HTTP port MUST set its own X-Forwarded-Proto
     (or set ORIGIN/CAIRN_ORIGIN) since the fill never overwrites.
  4. src/lib/server/auth.ts cookieSecure(), webauthn.ts, and
     packaging/umbrel/heartwood/docker-compose.yml deliberately left untouched —
     Umbrel already sets its own PROTOCOL_HEADER=x-forwarded-proto via app_proxy,
     so this fix targets exactly the bare/unproxied deployment gap cookieSecure()
     couldn't see.

TEST EVIDENCE:
  - New unit tests, src/tests/serverProto.test.ts (5 tests): fills 'http' when
    absent, PRESERVES an inbound x-forwarded-proto: https (regression guard for
    reverse-proxy topologies), fills 'https' on the https listener, never mutates
    other headers, returns the same object.
  - src/lib/server/auth.test.ts `cookieSecure` describe block: unchanged, still
    green (6 tests) — this fix does not touch cookieSecure() itself, only what
    protocol the request URL carries by the time cookieSecure() sees it.
  - `npm run check`: 0 errors, 0 warnings (1883 files).
  - Runtime curl matrix above, against the actual built `build/handler.js` via
    `node server.mjs`.

SEC SIGN-OFF (explicit, per review request):
  1. Fill-when-absent spoofing residual is self-inflicted-only: on the HTTP
     listener, a client that sends its OWN `x-forwarded-proto: https` gets a
     `Secure` cookie stamped on a plain-HTTP response — but that cookie is then
     silently dropped by THAT SAME client's own browser (Secure cookies are never
     set over a non-TLS connection per browser cookie rules). There is no cross-user
     or cross-origin impact; the only party who can trigger it is also the only
     party who "loses" (their own login stops sticking). Accepted.
  2. Accepted loud regression for TLS-terminating proxies that omit
     X-Forwarded-Proto in front of the plain HTTP port: such a deployment now gets
     an INCORRECTLY-NON-Secure cookie over what the proxy presents to the browser
     as https (rather than the previous default of incorrectly-Secure-over-http).
     This is a "loud" failure (cookie still transmitted, just missing a hardening
     attribute) rather than the previous "silent total auth breakage" failure, and
     is documented as a hard requirement in the Dockerfile and this bead. No known
     Cairn deployment topology (Umbrel's app_proxy, the primary target) hits this,
     since it already sets its own X-Forwarded-Proto.
  3. Trust model: the HTTP listener now honors an inbound `x-forwarded-proto`
     header from ANY client, not just a configured trusted proxy (there is no
     source-IP or shared-secret gate on it — matching adapter-node's own
     PROTOCOL_HEADER trust model, which has no such gate either). This is the same
     trust boundary Cairn already accepts for ADDRESS_HEADER (x-forwarded-for,
     Dockerfile comment) and ADDRESS_HEADER/HOST_HEADER in Umbrel's compose file.
     Given finding (1) above, honoring it on the unauthenticated pre-session path
     has no exploitable effect beyond the requesting client's own cookie.

Files: server.mjs, scripts/serverProto.mjs (new), src/tests/serverProto.test.ts (new),
Dockerfile.

title: QA admin credential qa-wave-admin@test.local rejects documented password (401)
close_reason: Added scripts/qa/reset-qa-admin-password.mjs reproducing auth.ts scrypt format; ran it against data/qa-wave-2026-07-12.db for qa-wave-admin@test.local=QaWave2026!Admin. Verified POST /api/auth/login/password -> HTTP 200. Script committed, .db not.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Repro: navigated to http://qa-admin.localhost:5344/login, drove the form via native-setter input+change events (screenshot/computer-click pipeline unreliable on this box per known hazard) with email=qa-wave-admin@test.local password=QaWave2026!Admin (creds as given in QA brief), then form.requestSubmit(). Network tab shows POST /api/auth/login/password -> 401 Unauthorized (confirmed via read_network_requests, not a UI-dispatch artifact -- also independently corroborated by the app's own activity log recording "Repeated failed sign-in attempts -- 5 failed sign-in attempts on your account in the last 15 minutes" for the Admin user immediately after).

Direct DB check (data/qa-wave-2026-07-12.db, read-only node:sqlite query) shows users.id=1 email=qa-wave-admin@test.local is_admin=1 disabled=0 password_hash='scrypt:16384:8:1:...' (populated, NOT NULL) -- so the documented password simply does not match the stored hash; it is not a NULL-hash seed artifact.

Workaround used to continue the QA pass (per project's own documented QA session-seeding pattern, scripts/qa/seed-flagmatrix.mjs / user memory "Cairn QA session-seeding pattern"): inserted a single sessions row for the EXISTING user id=1 (token_hash=sha256(random token), 7-day expiry) without touching the users table, then set document.cookie=cairn_session=<token>. This restored admin access and let the rest of the admin-track QA checklist proceed.

Impact: the documented admin credential (qa-wave-admin@test.local / QaWave2026!Admin) in QA briefs is stale/wrong for this DB -- any QA worker following the brief literally will burn time on a real 401 before finding the session-seed workaround. Recommend either fixing the seed data to set this exact password, or updating QA brief docs to point at the session-seed pattern directly instead of a password that doesn't work.
