Unit B3 (UI): +page.svelte renders a plain-language "Bitcoin Core detected on your Umbrel" card (address/username prefilled from hardcoded constants, never from the probe response) with Connect / Dismiss actions, plus provenance cards for umbrel-env/umbrel-detect once actually connected. Manual Core RPC fields also now render mode-independently (cairn-3p9z item 1) for non-assisted hand-entry.

Test coverage: src/lib/server/umbrelCoreProbe.test.ts (25 tests: all gating branches, all detection fingerprints incl. false-positive rejection on generic 503/404, transport-failure silence incl. fake-timer abort/timeout, garbage-response resilience, idempotence) + admin/settings page.server.test.ts and page.server.load.test.ts (persistence + secret-non-leak proof). This session's own evidence run:
- `npx vitest run "src/lib/server/umbrelCoreProbe.test.ts" "src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/page.server.test.ts" "src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/page.server.load.test.ts"` -> 3 files, 51 tests passed.
- `npx vitest run` (full suite) -> 244 files / 3455 tests passed, 1 skipped (unrelated live-regtest test), 0 failed.
- `npx svelte-check --output human` -> 0 errors, 0 warnings.
- `npx tsc --noEmit -p .` -> no errors touching umbrelCoreProbe.ts or admin/settings files.

Non-Umbrel safety confirmed by test: "never probes when CAIRN_PLATFORM is unset" / "...set to something other than umbrel" — zero fetch calls, fully inert off-Umbrel.

No code changes were needed this session (nothing left in scope); this close is a verification-only pass. Design doc: docs/UMBREL-AUTOCONNECT-WAVE-B-DESIGN.md (tracked). Handoff doc: docs/HANDOFF-wave-b-unit-b2-settings-form.md (tracked, historical — cairn-6uok resolved it).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
DESIGN COMPLETE -> docs/UMBREL-AUTOCONNECT-WAVE-B-DESIGN.md (single-sig-full-wallet, uncommitted).

KEY DECISION: silent full auto-connect (the Wave A model) is IMPOSSIBLE for Core RPC. Evidence (getumbrel/umbrel-apps bitcoin app + getumbrel/umbrel-bitcoin, verified 2026-07-12): bitcoind sits at 10.21.21.8:8332 on umbrel_main_network, rpcallowip=<subnet>/16 so any bridge app can CONNECT, but auth is a hard wall -- the RPC password is cryptographically generated per-install and lives ONLY in the bitcoin app's private .env (injected solely into apps declaring a hard dependencies:[bitcoin] manifest entry, i.e. Wave A2/chainEnvSeed). No cookie file is exported to non-dependent apps, and bitcoind runs WITHOUT -rest, so there is no unauthenticated endpoint. A network probe fundamentally cannot obtain creds without the manifest dependency we deliberately do not declare (cairn-2ldr "no node required").

CHOSEN DESIGN: detect-and-surface, not silent-connect.
- New src/lib/server/umbrelCoreProbe.ts: credential-free JSON-RPC POST to 10.21.21.8:8332; a 401 (or 503/-28 warmup) uniquely fingerprints bitcoind. Seeds ONE advisory marker core_rpc_detected=umbrel; writes NO core_rpc_* connection settings and leaves getChainConfig() byte-identical (so Core stays cleanly "not configured" -- no silent 401 storm, never worse than today's CoreRpcRequiredNotice empty-state).
- Assisted-connect UI (extends cairn-mz9p card, NOT a new card): pre-fills url=http://10.21.21.8:8332 + user=umbrel from HARDCODED constants (never from the responder, so a rogue bridge container cannot inject an endpoint), leaving the admin one field -- paste the RPC password (copyable from Umbrel's Bitcoin app Connect screen). Submit runs existing testCoreRpc() before going live.

GATING (differs from Wave A): Core RPC is independent of connection_mode -- getChainConfig returns coreRpc* in BOTH public and custom modes. So gate on CAIRN_PLATFORM===umbrel + coreRpcConfigured()===false + core_rpc_detected unset. Do NOT gate on or touch connection_mode.

PROVENANCE: new core_rpc_provisioned_by (null | umbrel-env | umbrel-detect), kept SEPARATE from Wave A chain_provisioned_by (Electrum-scoped). Plus pre-connect marker core_rpc_detected.

SECURITY: no credential adoption off the wire; blast radius of even a rogue Core is read-only (Cairn only does getblock/getrawtransaction/gettxout/estimatesmartfee -- never sends PSBTs/keys/xpubs). Human password paste is the trust gate.

PAYOFF is complete: Umbrel enables txindex=1 by default (umbrel PR #305) and is unpruned by default, so a connected default Umbrel Core fully serves every Core-RPC-gated explorer feature.

SAVE-PATH BUG TO FIX (Unit B2): admin settings action writes core_rpc_* only inside if(connectionMode===custom) (+page.server.ts ~L101/L123-130), but Core is mode-independent; the assisted connect must persist core_rpc_* regardless of mode and must not flip connection_mode. The JSON endpoint api/admin/settings already writes them unconditionally -- align the form action.

PLAN: B1 detection probe+plumbing (inert, marker-only, independently shippable) / B2 assisted-connect save path / B3 provenance+assisted card (folds into cairn-mz9p) / B4 live umbrel-s15 verification gate (ties to cairn-x26e next package cut). Failure modes (IBD/pruned/auth-fail/uninstall) + full unit+live test plan in the doc. Non-goal: declaring the hard manifest dependency (cairn-2ldr).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Unit B1 SHIPPED (commit 85a24da, single-sig-full-wallet): src/lib/server/umbrelCoreProbe.ts (new) - probeAndDetectUmbrelCore(), UMBREL_CORE_RPC_URL='http://10.21.21.8:8332'/UMBREL_CORE_RPC_USER='umbrel' constants exported for B2/B3 reuse, credential-free JSON-RPC POST with 2s timeout, 401/403/warmup-503(-28) fingerprint => detected, seeds ONLY core_rpc_detected='umbrel' via seed-once, writes NO core_rpc_url/user/pass/connection_mode/chain_provisioned_by, never throws. Wired into src/hooks.server.ts right after the Wave A Electrum probe (own try/catch, folded into seededThisBoot). src/lib/server/settings.ts + src/lib/types.ts: getInstanceSettings()/getPublicInstanceSettings() now expose coreRpcDetected + coreRpcProvisionedBy (both display-only, never consulted by getChainConfig()/coreRpcConfigured()). Gate order verified: CAIRN_PLATFORM!=='umbrel' short-circuits before any fetch (inert on non-Umbrel); coreRpcConfigured() and core_rpc_detected-unset gates checked next, matching design SS4. `npx tsc --noEmit` clean on all touched/new files (one pre-existing unrelated error in chainEpochs.ts, not ours).

Unit B2 BLOCKED-ON-HANDOFF: filed cairn-6uok (P2) -- admin settings form action (src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts ~L101/L123-130) writes core_rpc_* only inside connectionMode==='custom', contradicting Core's mode-independence (getChainConfig returns coreRpc* in both modes; the JSON PUT endpoint already writes unconditionally). That file is outside this session's edit-ownership boundary, so B2 itself is NOT implemented here. Handoff doc written at docs/HANDOFF-wave-b-unit-b2-settings-form.md (untracked, not committed -- for whichever session/orchestrator picks up cairn-6uok).

Unit B3 (provenance/assisted-connect card, folds into cairn-mz9p) is next once B2 lands -- settings.ts/types.ts plumbing for coreRpcDetected/coreRpcProvisionedBy is already in place for it to consume.

Unit B4 (live umbrel-s15 verification) deferred to the next heartwood-app-store package cut per the design doc, ties to cairn-x26e.

TEST NEEDS for the testing orchestrator (no test files written by this session per its constraints) -- new file src/lib/server/umbrelCoreProbe.test.ts, mirroring umbrelProbe.test.ts/chainEnvSeed.test.ts:
1. Gated out (returns [], no fetch, nothing written) when CAIRN_PLATFORM !== 'umbrel'.
2. Gated out when core_rpc_url already set (coreRpcConfigured()===true), or when core_rpc_detected already present.
3. Probe HTTP 401 -> seeds core_rpc_detected='umbrel', returns ['core_rpc_detected'], writes no core_rpc_url/user/pass/connection_mode/chain_provisioned_by.
4. HTTP 403 -> same as 401.
5. HTTP 503 with a warmup/-28 body -> detected; plain 503 with unrelated body -> NOT detected.
6. HTTP 200 -> detected (marker only, still no connection settings written).
7. Connection-refused / timeout / DNS error -> returns [], nothing written, no throw.
8. getChainConfig() output identical before vs after a successful detection.
9. Seed-once idempotence: two consecutive calls seed once, second call returns [].
Full plan (incl. Unit B2 settings/UI tests and the Unit B4 live-Umbrel checklist) is in docs/UMBREL-AUTOCONNECT-WAVE-B-DESIGN.md SS10.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Unit B3 SHIPPED (single-sig-full-wallet, src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte -- no .server.ts change needed, since the load already spreads getPublicInstanceSettings() -- which B1 extended with coreRpcDetected/coreRpcProvisionedBy/chainProvisionedBy/coreRpcUrl/coreRpcUser -- straight into data.settings). Shipped: (1) Electrum provenance card (folds cairn-mz9p, closed) when chainProvisionedBy is umbrel-env/umbrel-probe. (2) Core-detected assisted-connect card when coreRpcDetected==='umbrel' && !coreRpcUrl (mirrors coreRpcConfigured()'s own url-only check): 'Bitcoin Core detected on your Umbrel' with a Connect it / Re-fill detected values button that pre-fills the EXISTING coreRpcUrl/coreRpcUser inputs (converted to bind:value local ) with the hardcoded UMBREL_CORE_RPC_URL/USER constants duplicated client-side (design doc SS9 -- umbrelCoreProbe.ts is /server-only, can't import into the isomorphic component), flips connectionMode to custom so the mode-gated Core RPC fields become visible, and focuses the password field via tick(). Never auto-submits -- admin pastes the password and uses the existing Test connection / Save settings buttons. (3) Forward-compat Core 'Connected' provenance states (umbrel-env/umbrel-detect) for once B2 ships. KNOWN LIMITATION documented as a code comment on useDetectedCoreNode() in +page.svelte (not user-facing): saving core_rpc_* in public connection_mode is still broken until cairn-6uok/B2 lands (+page.server.ts's save action only persists core_rpc_* inside the connectionMode==='custom' block) -- the card works around it by flipping the radio to custom client-side, so it works on the common Umbrel path where Wave A already flipped it, or now via this card's own flip. Dismiss action (design SS9's core_rpc_detected='dismissed') NOT implemented -- it needs a new +page.server.ts form action, outside this session's edit boundary; left for B2/B4. Verified: npx svelte-check clean (0 errors/warnings). QA evidence in the cairn-mz9p comment above (same card component). B4 (live umbrel-s15 verification) still pending, ties to cairn-x26e.

title: Core RPC test HTTP 403 shows dangling 'HTTP 403: .' and no rpcallowip hint
description:
Admin > Settings > Bitcoin Core RPC > Test connection against a reachable bitcoind that rejects this host via rpcallowip (returns HTTP 403 with an empty body).

EXACT COPY OBSERVED (input url='http://192.168.50.146:8332' [Alex's node, rejects this box], user='someuser', pass='somepass'):
"Couldn't connect to Bitcoin Core: Core RPC getblockchaininfo failed: HTTP 403: . Check the RPC URL, username/password, and that Core is running and reachable from this server."

Two nits:
1. Empty response body renders as 'HTTP 403: .' — dangling colon+space+period.
2. HTTP 403 from bitcoind almost always means the caller's IP is not in the node's rpcallowip allowlist, NOT a credential problem. The generic 'check username/password' guidance misdirects; 'reachable from this server' only partly covers it.

FIX: in friendlyCoreRpcTestError() / call() HTTP-status branch, special-case 403 -> e.g. 'The node refused this connection (HTTP 403). Your server's IP is probably not in the node's rpcallowip allowlist.' and trim empty bodies so there's no dangling punctuation.

Repro: point RPC URL at a bitcoind whose rpcallowip excludes this host, Test connection. Low severity (polish).

Found during Core-RPC/post-Esplora live verification (single-sig-full-wallet @ d632f64).
close_reason: friendlyCoreRpcTestError special-cases HTTP 403 -> rpcallowip hint (chain/index.ts); CoreRpcClient omits empty body so no dangling 'HTTP 403: .' (bitcoinCore/client.ts:call). Trailing punctuation trimmed generally. Tests: chain.test.ts testCoreRpc ymcg (2). Full suite green.

title: Electrum client: add small connection pool (2-4 conns) instead of single shared socket
description:
Load test (docs/LOAD-TEST-RESULTS-2026-07-05.md, Headline + Recommendation 2) identified that ALL Electrum traffic for the entire app funnels through one shared, pipelined TCP connection, causing address/balance lookups to queue behind each other rather than parallelize (directly responsible for the ~17.5ms/wallet portfolio-scan cost compounding linearly with wallet count - see companion bead cairn-fd56).

Confirmed by code read:

Core implementation: src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts (428 lines) - hand-rolled Electrum JSON-RPC 2.0 client over raw TCP/TLS (node:net/node:tls), class ElectrumClient (line 74), one net.Socket/tls.TLSSocket per instance (line 81). No electrum-client npm package used; this is the only wire-protocol implementation. Public API: request(), batchRequest(), getBalance(), getHistory(), listUnspent(), broadcast(), getTransaction(), getMerkleProof(), getBlockHeader(), estimateFee(), headersSubscribe(), subscribeScripthash(), banner(), serverFeatures(), ping(), close().

Singleton wiring: src/lib/server/chain/index.ts - ChainService constructor (196-207) builds one ElectrumClient. Module-level singleton at line 601 (`let instance: ChainService | null = null`), lazy accessor getChain() (604-607, builds on first call, not at boot). reconfigureChain() (613-632) is the only place that replaces the singleton - nulls instance, closes old client; next getChain() rebuilds from fresh admin settings. Called from src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts:66 and src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts:80.

Call sites (~40 files via getChain(), mostly through the ChainService facade - transparent to a pool). Direct `.electrum` access that bypasses the facade (these need explicit attention):
- src/lib/server/bitcoin/walletScan.ts:49,52 - chain.electrum.batchRequest(...)
- src/lib/server/multisigScan.ts:165,168 (batchRequest), :333 (listUnspent)
- src/lib/server/transactions.ts:79 (listUnspent), :418 (broadcast)
- src/lib/server/multisigTransactions.ts:402 (broadcast)
- src/lib/server/stateless.ts:292 (broadcast)
- src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts:315-316,360,607 (getMerkleProof/getBlockHeader/getHistory), :640-641 (.off('scripthash'/'header', ...) event listeners directly on the client instance)
- src/routes/api/events/+server.ts:20,90 - PINS getChain().electrum for the lifetime of an SSE stream and compares by reference (`getChain().electrum !== electrum`) to detect a reconfigureChain() swap and end the stream. Most identity-sensitive call site - a pool must preserve some notion of 'the client' for this comparison, or the check needs redesigning (e.g. a generation counter instead of object identity).

Config - already admin-configurable: src/lib/server/settings.ts DEFAULTS (4-15) has electrumHost/electrumPort/electrumTls/electrumTlsInsecure; getInstanceSettings() (38) overlays DB-stored settings table rows; getChainConfig() (78) returns effective values. Stored in SQLite via getSetting/setSetting (25-36). Admin UI: src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte + +page.server.ts, API src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts (PUT validates + calls setSetting + reconfigureChain()). No env var path - 100% DB-backed. A pool-size setting (2-4 connections) belongs in this same settings table/UI.

Existing reconnect/retry logic (per-client, in client.ts): lazy connect-on-demand via ensureConnected() (113, dedupes concurrent connects via this.connecting promise); auto-reconnect with exponential backoff ONLY when there are active subscriptions (onDisconnect(), 197-223, backoff RECONNECT_MIN_MS=1000 to RECONNECT_MAX_MS=30000, doubling); resubscribe() (233) replays subscriptions after reconnect; per-request timeout DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS=15_000 (line 10) with no retry-on-timeout. No jitter, no circuit breaker, no max-retry cap.

Existing concurrency control: NONE. batchRequest() (322-325) does plain Promise.all with no cap - fully pipelined on the single socket. multisigScan.ts chunks lookups via BATCH_SIZE=20 (line 31) before calling batchRequest, but that's an app-level chunk size, not a concurrency limiter - still one Promise.all per chunk against the one TCP connection. No p-limit/semaphore/mutex/queue package anywhere in src/lib/server/electrum or callers (confirmed via grep).

Fix: replace the single `this.electrum = new ElectrumClient(...)` in ChainService's constructor (chain/index.ts:198) with a small pool (2-4 ElectrumClient instances, configurable via the existing settings table/admin UI) exposing the same method surface, dispatching via round-robin or least-busy (track in-flight request count per connection). Since ~35/40 call sites go through the ChainService facade, the pool can be transparent there. The ~5 direct-.electrum call sites need either (a) the pool class mimicking ElectrumClient's method signatures so `.batchRequest`/`.broadcast`/`.listUnspent`/etc. 'just work' picking a connection per call, or (b) updating those call sites to explicitly acquire a connection.

Complexity: medium-large. Core pool class is a moderate, well-scoped addition, but wiring the ~5 direct-access call sites and the events/+server.ts identity check correctly is where the real risk/effort is.

Risks/gotchas:
- Subscriptions (headersSubscribe, subscribeScripthash) are inherently stateful per-socket - decide whether subscriptions live on one designated 'primary' pooled connection or are replicated/deduped across the pool. addressWatcher.ts attaches header/scripthash listeners directly to the client's EventEmitter (lines 640-641), so this needs explicit design, not an incidental side effect of pooling.
- events/+server.ts's reference-equality check against getChain().electrum will break silently if a pool object doesn't preserve identity semantics across reconfigureChain() - needs a deliberate fix (e.g. generation counter), not just swapping in a pool and hoping the check still works.
- reconfigureChain() must tear down and rebuild the WHOLE pool (close every pooled connection), not just one.
- No existing concurrency limiter to reconcile with - a pool is close to pure upside here since currently all concurrent load funnels through one socket, but adding a pool without also adding admin-configurable pool size would just move the hardcoded-constant problem rather than solve it - the admin-configurability requirement (per this ticket's ask) is part of the actual fix, not a nice-to-have.
- Test coverage: check whether existing Electrum client tests mock a single instance in a way that assumes singleton behavior - pool changes could break test setup if so.
close_reason: Replaced the single shared Electrum socket with a small connection pool. New src/lib/server/electrum/pool.ts: ElectrumPool wraps N ElectrumClient instances behind an ElectrumClient-shaped, EventEmitter facade and is a drop-in for ChainService.electrum — so all ~40 call sites (facade + the ~5 direct .electrum sites: walletScan/multisigScan batchRequest, listUnspent, broadcast, getMerkleProof/getBlockHeader/getHistory, and the SSE endpoint's identity check + on/off) work transparently. Stateless requests (request/batchRequest/getBalance/getHistory/listUnspent/broadcast/getTx/getMerkleProof/getBlockHeader/estimateFee/serverFeatures/ping) round-robin across connections so concurrent lookups run on parallel sockets; ALL subscriptions (headersSubscribe/subscribeScripthash) + banner stay on a designated PRIMARY connection, whose connect/disconnect/header/scripthash events are re-emitted as the pool's own so wireChainEvents/addressWatcher/SSE listen in one place. Identity semantics preserved: ChainService.electrum is the stable pool object (changes only on reconfigureChain, which now closes the whole pool). Admin-configurable pool size (1–4, default 2): electrumPoolSize on InstanceSettings + settings.ts (electrum_pool_size), surfaced in getChainConfig (both modes), with a 'Connection performance' field on /admin/settings + validation in the form action and JSON API. Updated ElectrumClient type refs in chainEvents.ts/addressWatcher.ts to ElectrumPool. Tests: 5 new pool tests (round-robin distribution, size-1 = single socket, subscription-on-primary + event forwarding, reject-after-close, clamp-to-4). Existing tx/multisig/stateless tests mock ./chain wholesale so are unaffected. Full suite 1067 pass, typecheck clean.

title: PSBT infrastructure & transaction lifecycle
description: Foundational transaction-construction layer for Cairn's v2 signing feature. This is the first real departure from Cairn's pure watch-only positioning (README currently promises 'private keys never touch the server') -- that promise must stay true: this epic builds and moves PSBTs, never keys. Everything under HARDWARE WALLET SIGNING (separate epic) depends on this existing first, since there's nothing to sign without it. Order within this epic: construction before download/upload before broadcast; saved transactions and labels can proceed in parallel once construction exists.
close_reason: PSBT infrastructure & transaction lifecycle epic complete: construction (yph.1), download/upload (yph.2), broadcast (yph.3), saved transactions (yph.4), labels (yph.5), signing stepper (yph.6), plus the critical signer-substitution guard (cairn-973). Watch-only promise preserved — Cairn builds and moves PSBTs, never keys

title: PSBT construction (unsigned tx builder)
description:
Build an unsigned PSBT from a wallet: recipient address, amount, fee rate (reuse existing fee estimate plumbing from src/lib/server/chain/index.ts), coin selection over the wallet UTXOs (from the existing gap-limit wallet scan in src/lib/server/bitcoin/walletScan.ts), and change output derivation using the wallet own change chain (src/lib/server/db.ts already tracks receive_cursor per wallet; change derivation should follow the same xpub/scriptType pattern as src/lib/server/bitcoin/xpub.ts).

ARCHITECTURE CAVEAT -- read before copying Bastion patterns: Bastion (C:\dev\bastion\src\server\psbt.ts) builds PSBTs via Bitcoin Core RPC walletcreatefundedpsbt, which assumes a Core wallet backing every watch-only wallet. Cairn explicit pitch (README) is "works without your own node" -- Electrum + public Esplora by default, Core RPC optional and currently unused. PSBT construction here MUST work from Electrum UTXO data (blockchain.scripthash.listunspent) and manual coin selection/fee math, falling back to Core walletcreatefundedpsbt only as an optional accelerated path when a user has configured Core RPC (src/lib/server/settings.ts coreRpcUrl). Do not make Core RPC a hard requirement -- that would break the no-own-node promise that is Cairn biggest differentiator from Bastion and from Sparrow.

PSBT library: Bastion uses bitcoinjs-lib. Cairn already depends on the @scure ecosystem (@scure/bip32, @scure/base) for xpub parsing -- @scure/btc-signer is the natural fit to avoid adding a second, larger dependency family, but this is Fable call; note it as a deliberate decision either way rather than a default.

Security-critical: only ever handles public data (UTXOs, addresses, amounts) -- no private key material touches this code path at any point. Must avoid dust outputs, handle insufficient-funds and fee-too-high-for-value edge cases explicitly, and must not reuse an already-issued receive address as change.
close_reason: Implemented in 81b4127 (+ schema in dacbbf0): Electrum-native construction via @scure/btc-signer — no Core dependency, per the bead's architecture caveat. Coin selection with own-change-chain outputs, send-max, confirmed-only inputs, nonWitnessUtxo for legacy, bip32Derivation only when the key origin is genuinely known. Proven three ways: 10 unit tests incl. signing the PSBT with the published BIP84 vector key via its embedded derivation paths and finalizing/extracting; a live mainnet draft on the legacy test wallet (223-sat fee at a requested 1 sat/vB, 1,037 sats change conserved); and a regression test for a discovered fund-burning trap in the selector's dust option that swallowed sub-18k-sat change as fee

title: PSBT download/upload for external signing
description: Download an unsigned PSBT built by the construction step as a .psbt file (standard binary or base64, whichever Sparrow/Electrum/other PSBT-compatible wallets expect by default -- check what's most broadly compatible), and accept upload of a signed PSBT for the broadcast step. This is also the foundation the 'generic file-based signing' hardware-wallet path (separate epic) reuses directly -- build it generically enough that the fallback device flow is just this same download/upload with different framing copy, not a separate implementation.
close_reason: Implemented in 7504822: binary .psbt download (GET /api/wallets/[id]/tx/[txId]/file, Content-Disposition attachment — the format Sparrow/Electrum/ColdCard expect) and signed-PSBT upload (PATCH .../[txId] validates parseability, moves the row to awaiting_signature). Built generically so the file-based device fallback is the same endpoints with different framing. Verified live: 762-byte .psbt served, summary parses

title: PSBT broadcast
description: Accept a fully-signed PSBT (from upload, or directly from an in-browser hardware wallet signing flow once that exists), finalize it, and broadcast via the existing Electrum client (src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts already has the connection -- broadcast is blockchain.transaction.broadcast) or Bitcoin Core RPC if configured (src/lib/server/settings.ts already has coreRpcUrl/coreRpcUser/coreRpcPass stored but unused -- 'stored for upcoming features' per its own comment; this is that feature). Validate the PSBT is actually fully signed and finalizable before attempting broadcast, surface Electrum/Core rejection reasons in plain language, and guard against double-broadcast (e.g. disable the broadcast action once a txid comes back, and record it against the saved-transaction record).
close_reason: Implemented in 7504822: POST .../[txId]/broadcast finalizes via btc-signer, refuses not-fully-signed PSBTs (verified live: unsigned draft returns 400 incomplete 'Not enough partial sign'), guards double-broadcast by rejecting a row that already has a txid (409 already_sent), surfaces Electrum rejection reasons in plain language, and records the txid + completed status on success. Uses the existing Electrum blockchain.transaction.broadcast

title: Saved transactions (drafts, pending-signature, completed)
description: Persist transaction lifecycle state per wallet: draft (constructed, not yet exported for signing), awaiting-signature (exported, waiting on a signed PSBT to come back), and completed (broadcast, with the resulting txid bookmarked). Needs a new DB table alongside the existing wallets/invites/settings schema in src/lib/server/db.ts. This is what makes the multi-step signing flow resumable -- a user should be able to start a transaction, export it to sign on a ColdCard, close the browser, and come back later to upload the signed result against the same draft rather than starting over.
close_reason: Implemented in 40cf0d8: saved-transactions management tab on the wallet page (status badges, recipient/amount/fee, resume + download + two-click delete for drafts, txid link for completed) plus GET .../transactions/saved. Draft/awaiting/completed lifecycle persisted; completed rows kept. Live-verified

title: Transaction labels
description: Let users attach a free-text label to a transaction (sent or received) for their own bookkeeping -- 'rent', 'invoice #4021', etc. Store per-wallet, keyed by txid. Independent of the rest of the PSBT epic -- can be built and shipped without waiting on construction/broadcast, and is also useful immediately on existing read-only wallet transaction history (src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte) even before any signing capability ships.
close_reason: Implemented in 60394b9: tx_labels table (already in dacbbf0), ownership-scoped getLabels/setLabel, GET/PUT /api/wallets/[id]/labels, inline click-to-edit UI with optimistic updates on the wallet history tab, 8 new DB-backed tests (203 total green). Live-verified end to end incl. trim, clear, caps, 404/400/401 paths

title: Guided signing stepper framework (Create -> Review -> Sign -> Confirm -> Sent)
description:
Shared UI shell every signing flow (every hardware wallet device, and the generic file fallback) plugs into -- build this once, not per-device. Requirements from the guided-flow work:
- 5-step top-level stepper: Create -> Review -> Sign -> Confirm -> Sent
- The Sign step is itself a sub-stepper, one screen per required signature ("Key 1 of 2") for future multisig support even though Cairn v2 signing starts single-sig
- Per-device guided instructions live inside the Sign step and swap based on which signing method the user picked (copy examples: Trezor "Connect via USB, approve on screen." / Ledger "Open the Bitcoin app, connect via USB." / ColdCard "Insert the SD card, sign on device." / QR "Scan this QR with your signing device.")
- Plain language throughout -- "Review and sign", never "Sign PSBT"; technical details (raw PSBT, derivation paths, xpubs) live behind a disclosure toggle, not inline
- A confirmation gate before broadcast that cannot be skipped

Bastion has this already built and is the direct reference to port the pattern (not necessarily the code, since Bastion is vanilla JS and Cairn is Svelte 5, but the structure and copy patterns translate directly):
- Shell components: C:\dev\bastion\public\js\components\guided-flow.js -- flowTracker (progress persistence), ExplainPanel (education disclosure, auto-collapses after repeat use), ConfirmGate (survives "experienced mode"), Wizard (step-by-step or collapsed)
- Send flow implementation: C:\dev\bastion\public\js\views\wallet-detail.js (~2900 lines, steps at line 1055 flowStepper call) -- flowStepper({steps: ["Create","Review","Sign","Confirm","Sent"]}), signerSubStepper, signatureProgress (per-input per-fingerprint attribution UI), techDetails (the disclosure toggle)
- Per-vendor instruction copy: C:\dev\bastion\public\js\hw-signer.js signingGuideHtml()

Build the Svelte version of this shell as its own reusable component set before any individual device integration starts -- every device bead in the Hardware Wallet Signing epic depends on this existing first.
close_reason: Implemented in 40cf0d8: five-step Create->Review->Sign->Confirm->Sent stepper at /wallets/[id]/send, resumable via ?tx=N from saved status, Sign sub-stepper (Key 1 of 1) with file-based signing fully working + disabled Trezor/Ledger/ColdCard/QR device seams for the hardware epic, gated transitions (Sign->Confirm only when summary.complete). Generic Stepper component. Live-verified end to end

title: Extract client-safe hw/common.ts (HW-driver error classes + SLIP-132 xpub normalization)
description: src/lib/hw/common.ts shares HwError base class, normalizeXpub/xpubWithVersion, parseKeyPath/formatKeyPath, account-path builders, and MultisigScriptType/MultisigSignKey across the Trezor/Ledger/BitBox02/Jade drivers; drivers re-export so public APIs are unchanged. Depends only on scure/noble + $lib/types (never server code).
close_reason: Committed in f61482c; svelte-check clean, all 219 hw tests + full suite pass

title: SendReviewCard Details rows show raw sats with no fiat equivalent
description:
src/lib/components/send/SendReviewCard.svelte Details expander rows (change, total input, individual UTXOs) print formatSats(...) sats only, with no fiat equivalent -- inconsistent with the rest of the card (summary sentence, money-hero, fee/total core rows) which all show fiat via Amount.svelte / formatFiat+btcToFiat when a price is available. Alex priority: fiat everywhere money appears.

Fix: add fiat as a muted suffix next to the sats value on money rows only (change, total input, per-UTXO value) -- degrade gracefully to sats-only when no price is available (consistent with Amount.svelte's degrade behavior). Do NOT add fiat to vsize/feerate rows (not money). Keep Details layout tidy at 375px width using existing text-muted suffix pattern (matches the existing fee-pct '· X% of amount' muted suffix already in the fee-rate row).
close_reason: Fixed: SendReviewCard.svelte Details rows for change/total-input/per-UTXO now show a muted fiat suffix (e.g. '12,345 sats · $5.67') via a new fiatSuffix(sats) helper composing the existing btcToFiat+formatFiat from format.ts, matching the existing '· X% of amount' muted-suffix pattern already used on the fee-rate row. Degrades to sats-only (no fiat span rendered) when $btcUsd is null, same rule Amount.svelte applies elsewhere. vsize/feerate/signatures-required rows intentionally left alone (not money). No .svelte component-test infra exists in this repo (no *.svelte.test.ts anywhere, no testing-library dep) so per instructions I did not invent one; instead extended the existing src/lib/format.test.ts (established vitest pattern) with direct coverage of btcToFiat and formatFiat, the two functions fiatSuffix composes -- all 74 tests in that file pass. npm run check: 0 errors. Checked MANUAL.md for SendReviewCard/Details-expander/review-card references -- none exist, so no manual update needed for this fix.

title: Admin announcement/banner system
description: Instance-wide banners admins create/manage to message all users (or eventually specific users) — maintenance notices, outage notices, promotions (ties into referral system), educational callouts, warnings, custom text. Types: info/warning/urgent/promotion. Optional CTA link, optional dismissible (per-user persisted), optional expiry. Renders below nav / above content on every authenticated page. Distinct from the existing per-user events/notifications system. Gated by new announcement_banners feature flag. Plain text body in v1 (no markdown/HTML renderer exists in the codebase today; CTA link field covers the 'link' use case without introducing an HTML/XSS surface). See scope doc discussed 2026-07-06.
close_reason: All 5 children built + closed (024542f, b674fbd).

title: delete guard blocks on stale (>60s) broadcast_started_at claim after a crashed broadcast
description: Follow-up to cairn-up0q (landed 94ddd83). deleteTransaction/deleteMultisigTransaction now refuse delete whenever broadcast_started_at IS NOT NULL. That intentionally includes a STALE claim left by a broadcast that crashed mid-flight: broadcastTransaction/broadcastMultisigTransaction treat a claim older than 60s as reclaimable (they let a retry overwrite it), but the delete guard does not replicate that staleness window — so after a crashed broadcast the row can't be deleted until the claim is explicitly released or the row transitions to completed. Low priority (errs toward safety, never loses a broadcast record). Options if we want it: mirror the 'broadcast_started_at < now()-60s' predicate in the delete guard, or release stale claims on a sweep.
close_reason: Fixed at 260b707: deleteTransaction/deleteMultisigTransaction now mirror broadcastTransaction's/broadcastMultisigTransaction's own 60s staleness window instead of blocking on ANY non-null broadcast_started_at. A live claim (<60s) still refuses delete; a stale claim (crashed mid-broadcast) now allows it. 2 new tests (transactions.test.ts, multisigTransactions.test.ts) confirm both the live-block and stale-allow paths; full suite green (71 tests).

title: Self-signed cert is SHA-1-signed (selfsigned default) — browsers distrust it
description: generateCert() in scripts/tls-cert.mjs never passes an algorithm to selfsigned.generate(), whose default is sha1 (verified in node_modules/selfsigned/index.js: getAlgorithmName(options.algorithm || 'sha1')). Live device cert confirmed via openssl s_client: sigalg sha1WithRSAEncryption. Consequences: users who try to permanently trust the cert in an OS store hit hard failures (Windows/macOS/iOS reject SHA-1 chains), and browsers may treat it as weak-signature. Also the cert carries ONLY a subjectAltName extension (our extensions option REPLACES selfsigned's defaults) — no keyUsage/extKeyUsage/basicConstraints, which trust-import paths on macOS/iOS require. Fix: algorithm sha256, full extension set, and a migration in ensureCert() that regenerates already-persisted SHA-1 certs (certUsable() alone would keep them for 10 years). Related: cairn-qv6h (dead-port window behind the same field report).
close_reason: sha256 signature + full leaf extensions + 825d validity + SHA-1 migration in certUsable; verified in Docker incl. 0.1.6-volume migration; ships in 0.1.7

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Fixed in scripts/tls-cert.mjs: algorithm sha256 passed to selfsigned.generate (was defaulting to SHA-1 — confirmed live on the device: openssl s_client showed sigalg sha1WithRSAEncryption); full leaf extension set (basicConstraints CA:false critical, keyUsage digitalSignature+keyEncipherment critical, extKeyUsage serverAuth, SANs unchanged); validity dropped 3650->825 days to stay under Apple's trust-import ceiling (auto-rotation at <30d remaining already existed, so cost is one fresh warning every ~2.2y); certUsable() now rejects SHA-1/MD5-signed certs via raw-DER OID scan (node X509Certificate exposes no sigalg), which migrates every cert persisted by <=0.1.6 on first 0.1.7 boot. Verified in Docker on a real 0.1.6 volume: sha1WithRSAEncryption before update, sha256WithRSAEncryption + CA:FALSE + TLS Web Server Authentication + 825d after, replacement persisted (restart reuses same fingerprint), pre-update admin session still valid. 3 new vitest cases; suite 1615 green.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: SHIPPED in v0.1.7 (same artifacts as cairn-qv6h comment). Device migration expectation: first boot after update logs a regenerated cert; https://umbrel.local:3212 then serves sha256WithRSAEncryption with CA:FALSE + serverAuth + 825d — verify with: openssl s_client -connect umbrel.local:3212 | openssl x509 -noout -text

title: Caravan config round-trip compatibility verification
description: Prove import/export interop: Cairn imports configs Caravan exports (field/casing/notation quirks per the research report), Cairn exports configs Caravan accepts, round-trip export->import->identical vault test, and a real-config fixture test using an actual Caravan-generated JSON. Depends on eo3 (import/export UI) + the research report's contract.
close_reason: Committed: real caravan-bitcoin golden fixtures (mainnet P2WSH imported verbatim — no transformation needed; testnet kept for the rejection test) in src/lib/server/__fixtures__, 5 interop tests incl. semantic round-trip and the export-contract assertions from the parity study. 27/27 vaultExport tests.

title: PUT /api/admin/settings has no validation, unlike the equivalent admin settings form action — can silently misconfigure registration mode / node connection
description: src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts PUT handler loops over KEY_MAP and calls setSetting(dbKey, String(body[key])) for whatever JSON the caller sends, with zero validation. Compare src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts's 'save' form action, which explicitly whitelists registrationMode to ['open','invite','closed'], connectionMode to ['public','custom'], and validates electrumPort as an integer 1-65535 before accepting it. The JSON API has none of that: POSTing {registrationMode:'oepn'} (typo) or any non-enum string is accepted verbatim. Consequence, traced through src/routes/(auth)/signup/+page.server.ts: needsInvite = !firstUser && mode==='invite' and closed = !firstUser && mode==='closed' — a typo'd/garbage registrationMode value matches neither check, so the instance silently falls through to fully OPEN registration with no invite requirement, with no error surfaced anywhere. Similarly electrumPort accepts non-numeric strings that become NaN via parseInt() in getInstanceSettings(), breaking the configured node connection. This route is live and admin-session-authenticated (requireAdmin) even though the current SvelteKit UI doesn't call it (only the form action does) — grepped src/ and found zero fetch('/api/admin/settings') call sites, so it's reachable today by anyone scripting against the API with an admin cookie. Fix: apply the same enum/range validation as the form action inside setSetting's caller (or share one validation function between the two entry points).
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Destructive db.ts vault->multisig migration has zero test coverage
description: db.ts:178-270 — destructive one-shot unattended migration renames vaults->multisigs tables, renames vault_id->multisig_id columns, and has an empty-shell recovery branch (L212-235) that executes DROP TABLE on multisig_keys/multisig_transactions/etc. These tables hold multisig wallet definitions and xpubs. For source=created multisigs, this config exists NOWHERE else — losing multisig_keys = permanent fund loss. Tests never reach this code because setup.ts creates fresh DBs with new schema. The harness pattern exists in envAlias.test.ts. Fix: add db.test.ts using vi.resetModules + vi.stubEnv with old-schema fixture, assert rows preserved, columns renamed, old tables gone.
close_reason: DONE. Added src/lib/server/db.vaultMigration.test.ts — 5 tests: golden-path data preservation (vaults/vault_keys/vault_transactions/ledger regs -> multisig* with FK column renamed), idempotency, empty-shell recovery positive, and the CRITICAL empty-shell abort case (non-empty multisig_keys shell must block the DROP and leave vault* intact), fresh-install no-op. All pass — migration incl. its empty-shell DROP guard behaves correctly, NO data loss. Testability finding: migration is top-level import side-effect in db.ts (not an isolatable fn); tested via the repo's vi.resetModules + CAIRN_DB temp-file pattern. Follow-up (out of scope): extract migrations into named callable fns for cleaner coverage.

title: Agreement page (/agreement) crashed with 500 for a real signup: reserved action name 'default'
description: Found in data/logs/cairn.log for a real user (id 5) going through signup: POST /agreement returned 500 with 'Cannot use reserved action name "default"' (a SvelteKit form-action bug -- an action named 'default' can't coexist with other named actions, or vice versa, in the same +page.server.ts). This is the terms-acceptance step every new non-admin user must pass through immediately after signup. It eventually succeeded on a later retry, but a beginner hitting a raw 500 on their very first screen after creating an account would likely think the product is broken and abandon it. High severity because it blocks the mandatory onboarding gate for some fraction of new signups.
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: GET /logout crashes with 500 (route only supports POST)
description: Navigating directly to /logout (e.g. a bookmark, browser back button landing on it, or manual URL entry) returns a 500 Internal Server Error: 'Missing +page.svelte component for route /logout'. The route only implements a POST form action (used by the sidebar Sign-out button) with no GET page. Confirmed in data/logs/cairn.log: GET /logout 500 4ms, errorId 6bc4d3b5. Should redirect to / or /login instead of crashing.
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: No guard/notice when a cosigner with pending multisig signature slots self-deletes
description:
When a cosigner deletes their own account, multisig_transaction_signers cascades away (db.ts:980) and multisig_keys.assigned_user_id is SET NULL (db.ts:998). This leaves the wallet owner's cosigner roster frozen in an inconsistent state -- a signature slot that was assigned to a now-deleted user -- with no notification sent to the owner or remaining cosigners.

Mitigating factor: PSBT-embedded signatures already collected survive (they live in the PSBT itself, not just the DB row), so the underlying transaction is still recoverable in principle. But the UI/roster state is orphaned and nothing tells the owner a cosigner departed.

Fix: notify the multisig owner (and ideally remaining cosigners) when a cosigner with pending/assigned signature slots deletes their account, and/or reflect the vacancy clearly in the roster UI rather than leaving assigned_user_id silently nulled.
close_reason: Fixed at 1f6e28c: added pendingCosignerAssignments() (userDeletion.ts) which enumerates, before the cascade, every multisig this self-deleting user co-signs (not owns) with a still-awaiting_signature transaction they hadn't signed yet; notifyCosignerDeparture() fires a new in-app cosigner_left notification (registered in notifyTypes.ts, notifications.ts DEFAULT_PREFERENCES, activity.ts USER_FEED_TYPES) to each affected owner after deleteOwnAccount's purgeUserRow() commits. destructiveOps.test.ts Group 6b's pinned KNOWN GAP assertion (previously expect(count(events...)).toBe(0)) now asserts the notice IS sent and passes. Data-cleanliness (multisig/keys/tx survive, roster vacates via SET NULL) was already correct per the 2026-07-12 runtime finding; this closes the remaining 'no notice to the owner' half. svelte-check 0 errors; destructiveOps/accountDeletion/userDeletion/notifications/activity suites all green (28+15 tests).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: RUNTIME RESULT BETTER THAN AUDIT PREDICTED (2026-07-12 battery, cairn-fresh/dev-fresh2.db, v0.2.16): Bob (assigned cosigner) self-deleted during pending 2-of-3; tx stayed awaiting_signature, PSBT intact, signer row + share cascaded cleanly, 3 keys + threshold intact, quorum reachable, owner can finalize, no orphaned roster rows. Remaining gap is only the missing notice to the owner that a cosigner left.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Pinned in destructiveOps.test.ts Group 6b: data-clean on cosigner self-delete (multisig/keys/tx survive, SET NULL vacancy) but zero 'cosigner left' notice to owner — that half pinned as KNOWN GAP.

title: notified_txids rows orphaned forever: no FK to users/wallets/multisigs, never cleaned on delete or factory reset
description: src/lib/server/db.ts ~L606-616: CREATE TABLE notified_txids has user_id INTEGER NOT NULL and wallet_id INTEGER NOT NULL but neither column has a REFERENCES/FK clause (every other user_id column in db.ts uses REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE). addressWatcher.ts is the only writer, and there is no DELETE FROM notified_txids anywhere in the codebase - not in deleteUser(), not in wallet/multisig deletion paths, not in admin.ts resetInstance(). Every row this table has ever written for a deleted user, wallet, or multisig becomes a permanent orphan. resetInstance() documents itself as a full factory reset and the Settings UI tells the admin nothing else survives, but notified_txids rows survive indefinitely with no cleanup path, even outside of factory reset. Fix: explicit DELETE FROM notified_txids WHERE user_id = ? alongside deleteUser()/wallet deletion/resetInstance().
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: explorer: tx block-context dead-ends to 'none' when Electrum down but Core up
description: getTxBlockContext() decodes the tx via this.electrum.getTransaction() and fetches neighbour headers via this.electrum.getBlockHeader(). In a Core-up / Electrum-down deployment (Core-only, or misconfigured/unreachable Electrum host) the decode step throws and the whole section returns richness:'none' -> UI shows 'Couldn't reach your node just now to place this transaction in its block', directly contradicting the page's own 'Verified by your Bitcoin Core node' provenance. Fix: degrade per-source. Fall back to Core getrawtransaction verbose for the decode, Core getblockhash+getblockheader for neighbours; omit merkle position (Electrum-only) in the Core path. Neither backend -> 'none'. Add Core-only regression test expecting a usable tier (full), keep Electrum-only + both-up green. Found during v0.2.20 explorer visual-QA wave on single-sig-full-wallet HEAD 8c62969.
close_reason: Fixed in getTxBlockContext + neighborHeader (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts): decode now falls back to Core getrawtransaction verbose when Electrum is down; tip recovered from the tx's own block height + confirmations (or getblockcount for mempool); neighbours resolved via Core getblockhash+getblockheader; merkle position honestly omitted in the Core path (electrumOk gate); neither backend -> 'none'. Added 2 regression tests (Core-only full-tier recovery incl. position omission; Core-cannot-decode -> none) in chain.test.ts; CoreStub gained getBlockHeader/getBlockCount. npm run check clean; full suite 3291 pass / 1 skip. Verified live in browser on Core-up/Electrum-down QA env (port 5478, HEAD 8c62969+fix): /explorer/tx/822ddd… now renders green '6+ confirmations' badge, 3-block rail 459/460/461 with dates, honest summary, no position marker, no 'Couldn't reach your node' error; neighbour links go to /explorer/block/[h]; mobile 375x812 rail scrolls in its own overflow-x:auto container with no page-level horizontal overflow.

title: No coin-control UI for multisig send flow (single-key wallets have it, multisig doesn't)
description: src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisigPsbt.ts (constructMultisigPsbt) and src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/psbt/+server.ts fully support manual UTXO selection via an 'onlyUtxos' parameter -- the backend capability exists. But src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/send/+page.svelte (2246 lines) never imports or renders CoinControl.svelte and has zero references to onlyUtxos/coin selection anywhere in the UI. Compare to the single-key flow at src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte + _components/CoinControl.svelte, which has full coin control. Net effect: multisig wallet users get automatic largest-first UTXO selection only (multisigPsbt.ts:477) with no way to manually pick which UTXOs to spend -- a real parity gap since Sparrow supports coin control uniformly across single-sig and multisig, and multisig users (the power-user segment) are exactly the ones most likely to want manual control for privacy/consolidation reasons.
close_reason: Wired the existing CoinControl.svelte into the multisig Create step (parity with single-sig). The multisig send loader now supplies the confirmed spendable coin set (largest-first, from getMultisigUtxos — the same set /psbt selects from) plus the block tip; the Create step renders CoinControl honouring the admin coin_control flag (FeatureDisabled note when off) and threads the selection into the build as onlyUtxos (backend already supported + enforced it). CoinControl/fetchUtxoMass gained an optional endpoint override so the signing-mass chips hit the multisig utxo-mass endpoint; single-sig unchanged. Typecheck clean.

title: Double UTXO fetch on wallet detail pages
description: wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts:84,99 — getWalletUtxos(xpub) at line 84, then detectWalletUnconfirmedInflows at line 99 calls getWalletUtxos(wallet.xpub) again internally (transactions.ts:231). No cache on getWalletUtxos, so this doubles real Electrum round-trips (1 listUnspent per used address). Same pattern in wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts:79,90 for multisig. Fix: fetch UTXOs once, pass array into detectUnconfirmedInflows directly.
close_reason: done

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
FIXED. The load path moved to a cache-first snapshot model since this was filed (wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts:36 now reads a persisted snapshot synchronously, no Electrum), but the double UTXO fetch simply RELOCATED into the background refresh (walletSync.ts doWalletScan / doMultisigScan) rather than being fixed by the refactor.

Root cause (confirmed still live, single-sig): doWalletScan fetched getWalletUtxos(row.xpub) for the coinbase bucket, THEN called detectWalletUnconfirmedInflows(userId, row.id) for the speed-up (RBF/CPFP) verdicts, which re-fetched the UTXO set internally (transactions.ts:257). Each getWalletUtxos is a full batched listunspent-per-used-address round-trip, so one refresh hit the UTXO set twice. Worse, the second call defaulted to the INTERACTIVE lane, leaking background work onto the reserved interactive socket.

Multisig (same pattern, also confirmed live): doMultisigScan already had detail.utxos from getMultisigDetail, but still called detectMultisigUnconfirmedInflows -> getMultisigUtxos, a second listunspent pass. Only scanMultisig is cached (TTL); getMultisigUtxos' listunspent is NOT — so the walletSync comment claiming 'getMultisigUtxos hits the cache' was only half-true.

Fix (walletSync.ts): fetch the UTXO set once per refresh and pass the array straight into detectUnconfirmedInflows(utxos, ownBroadcastTxids/ownMultisigTxids), replacing the re-fetching detectWalletUnconfirmedInflows/detectMultisigUnconfirmedInflows wrappers in this path. Single-sig now fetches on the background lane once; multisig reuses detail.utxos. Rendered data is unchanged (same coinbase bucket, same speed-up list). The detectWallet*/detectMultisig* wrappers remain exported for their other callers.

Test (walletSync.test.ts, all 27 green): 'fetches the wallet UTXO set only ONCE per refresh (cairn-zdgt)' asserts listUnspentMock is called exactly once for a single-used-address wallet through the REAL doWalletScan (via refreshWalletSnapshot) — it was 2 before the fix.

title: Missing regression test: signup invalid invite code fails silently (cairn-gy79)
description: cairn-gy79 (P1, closed in 46dd16f) fixed the signup form silently failing (no visible error) when an invalid invite code was submitted, most of the time. Fix confirmed present. Add a test in src/lib/server/auth.test.ts: call registerUser (or the register route handler) with an invite code that doesn't match any row in the invites table while needsInvite is true, and assert it throws/returns a typed AuthError with an 'invalid invite' code/message rather than silently succeeding or failing without signal.
close_reason: server.test.ts for the register route: invalid invite returns typed 400 bad_invite with visible message, no silent account creation. Commit d353f7a.

title: Receive panel: derive next address offline at wallet.receive_cursor
description: cairn-zke0 shipped the minimum fix (friendly waiting state when chain data unavailable). The full fix is deriving the receive address locally at wallet.receive_cursor without the Electrum gap-scan (findNextUnusedIndex), so users can receive while the node is unreachable. See commit 73a4a1b and cairn-zke0 close notes.

title: Electrum broadcast success is never verified against locally-computed txid
description:
Security audit 2026-07-05 (docs/SECURITY-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md), finding F4. Verified CONFIRMED by 3/3 independent adversarial reviewers.

src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts finalizePsbt() (~lines 739-743) computes a real, locally-derived txid (tx.id -- the actual double-SHA256 of the finalized transaction bytes) alongside rawHex. src/lib/server/transactions.ts broadcastTransaction() (~lines 382-431) captures this result but only ever reads its rawHex field when calling getChain().electrum.broadcast(...); the locally-computed txid field is computed and then discarded. The value actually stored/returned as the transaction txid is whatever string the Electrum server's blockchain.transaction.broadcast RPC response contains (src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts broadcast(), ~lines 324-326), with zero validation against the real txid. The identical unguarded pattern exists in the multisig broadcast path (multisigTransactions.ts, ~lines 342/370-385).

Impact: a malicious, compromised, or simply buggy Electrum server can return an arbitrary fabricated txid for a broadcast it silently never performs (or performs differently), and Cairn will store/display that fake txid as a successful, completed send. A user could believe a payment was sent (and hand a counterparty the fake txid as proof of payment) while the real transaction never reached the network and the coins remain spendable/re-spendable. This corrupts downstream logic that trusts completed status (RBF bump eligibility, coin-selection treating inputs as spent, activity/audit log integrity).

Notably, the existing test suite already encodes the vulnerable behavior rather than preventing it: src/lib/server/transactions.test.ts (~lines 183-189) mocks the broadcast call to resolve with an arbitrary unrelated txid and asserts it is accepted and stored -- this test would need to change as part of the fix.

Fix: after broadcasting, compare the Electrum-reported txid against the locally-computed one (and the multisig equivalent) and throw a clear error on mismatch, rather than trusting the server's response. Cheap, mechanical fix; the codebase already applies this exact defensive pattern elsewhere for fetched previous-tx bytes (psbt.ts ~lines 313-345, rejects the chain source if it returns inconsistent data).
close_reason: Fixed in security-audit remediation pass; see commit. All 897 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: Electrum client: silent resubscribe failure plus zero test coverage for TLS/reconnect/backoff
description: src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts:243, resubscribe(): catch body is only a comment ("Resubscription failure will surface via the next disconnect/retry") -- no log call, so a failed re-subscription to blockchain.headers.subscribe/scripthash.subscribe after a reconnect silently stops live updates until the next full disconnect cycle, with zero diagnostic trail. The whole file has no logger import at all. Additionally, the TLS certificate validation path (tls.connect, tlsInsecure option) and the reconnect/backoff/resubscribe path have zero test coverage in electrum/client.test.ts, despite this being the code that runs after every real-world network blip. Fix: add logging to resubscribe() and other client.ts failure paths; add tests for TLS cert validation and reconnect/backoff/resubscribe. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed the silent resubscribe failure: electrum/client.ts now imports childLogger('electrum') and resubscribe()'s catch logs a warn (err + server + sub count) so a post-reconnect resubscribe failure that pauses live updates leaves a diagnostic trail. Also log reconnect scheduling + failed reconnect attempts at debug. Added test coverage in client.test.ts: (1) TLS self-signed cert is rejected by default and (2) accepted with tlsInsecure:true (self-signed fixture cert embedded), (3) reconnect+resubscribe re-runs the handshake and re-subscribes an active scripthash after a socket drop, (4) no eager reconnect when nothing is subscribed. 12 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: [UX-QA f0d423f] Receive panel renders nothing when wallet unsynced/no-chain
description:
TRIAGE VERDICT: NOT a regression from this session's ux/* work. Degraded-state defect that predates today.

SYMPTOM (visual QA on merged tip f0d423f): On a wallet detail page, clicking Receive sets URL hash to #receive but renders NO receive UI (no address, no QR, no copy/rotate). Seen on a watch-only wallet with the chain backend DOWN.

ROOT CAUSE (code-confirmed, not degraded-only-cosmetic -> it's a real coupling bug):
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:316  const receive = $derived(form?.receive ?? chainData.receive ?? null);
- +page.svelte:662  {#if receive}  ... and :663  <section id="receive"> lives INSIDE that {#if}. So when receive is null, the #receive anchor target AND all receive UI are absent from the DOM. The <a href=#receive> at :565 sets the hash but there is nothing to scroll to and nothing to render -> exactly the QA symptom.
- receive comes from chainData.receive. chainData = data.chainData = cached snapshot ?? EMPTY_WALLET_SNAPSHOT (+page.server.ts:62). EMPTY_WALLET_SNAPSHOT.receive === null (src/lib/server/walletSync.ts:116-123). So a wallet that has NEVER synced (or first load with chain down) has receive === null -> empty panel.

WHY IT IS A REAL BUG, NOT JUST DEGRADED COSMETICS: the next receive address is derivable from the wallet xpub/descriptor ALONE and does not require the chain (gap-scan for next-unused index is an optimization, not a hard dependency). Coupling the receive address purely to the chain snapshot means an unsynced/offline wallet cannot show ANY address to receive to -- breaking the 2-tap receive flow in exactly the offline scenario where a user most wants a static address.

GIT EVIDENCE it is NOT ours:
- {#if receive} gate introduced 5595b82 (2026-07-03), long before this session.
- chainData.receive coupling introduced 367a826 (Release v0.2.1, 2026-07-07, streaming SSR / chain TTL cache).
- refined chainData?.receive -> chainData.receive at b4c4acd (2026-07-09 SWR).
- Today's commits: 0a9d445 (double-spend cairn-a2p1) and 7fcb734 (ux error-copy Wave B) did NOT touch the receive rendering (verified via git show -- +page.svelte; 7fcb734 does not modify the file at all).

PROPOSED FIX (owned by the double-spend track -- this file is off-limits to the ux/receive-fix session, so COORDINATION NEEDED):
Option A (preferred): populate a chain-independent receive fallback. In +page.server.ts load(), when chainData.receive is null, derive the next receive address from the wallet xpub directly (nextReceiveAddress already exists in $lib/server/wallets and is used by the ?/receive action) and hand it to the page so the panel always has an address. QR is cheap (QRCode.toDataURL). Guard the gap-scan-dependent index behind the chain but never leave receive null.
Option B (minimum): render the #receive <section> unconditionally; inside it, if receive is null show a plain-language 'still syncing / node unreachable -- your address will appear once we can reach the chain' state so the anchor target exists and the tab is never a dead click.

COORDINATION NOTE: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte is owned by the double-spend/watcher track (cairn-a2p1). The ux/receive-fix session did not touch it. Assign to whoever owns that file next.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 73a4a1b: the #receive section in src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte is no longer gated behind {#if receive} (which hid the section, including the #receive anchor, whenever chainData.receive was null e.g. never-synced wallet or node unreachable on first load). The section now always renders; when receive is null it shows a plain-language 'Still connecting to your node' message instead of address/QR (no offline address derivation attempted -- nextReceiveAddress/peekReceiveAddress both require an Electrum gap-scan via findNextUnusedIndex, which would violate this page's zero-Electrum-in-load() cache-first architecture). npm run check: 0 errors in the touched file (6 pre-existing unrelated errors in recoverySetupFlow.test.ts from a concurrent session).

title: mining engine start aborts with recordFatal('Invalid checksum') when a payout wallet xpub is unparseable — one bad wallet row can stop the pool
description: Observed on QA instance (dev server, regtest, 2026-07-19 09:26-09:29): 11+ 'mining engine fatal' ERROR entries with message 'Invalid checksum', stack parseXpub (src/lib/server/bitcoin/xpub.ts:226) reached from refreshAuthTable (authTable.ts:84) / peekReceiveAddress, and also via shared ScanCache promises created by portfolioWarm.ts:102 and addressWatcher.ts:191 (stack shows creation site, engine awaited the shared rejected promise). recordFatal() is only called from startMiningEngine's doStart catch (mining/index.ts), so these represent engine start attempts ABORTING — even though refreshAuthTable wraps per-miner failures in try/catch with the explicit comment 'One user's failure never aborts the rebuild' (the same error ALSO appears as the intended WARN 'authTable refresh: skipping miner (build failed)' for userId 3). Some escape path defeats the per-miner isolation — suspect the shared ScanCache in gapLimitScanner.ts:366 rethrowing outside the guarded loop, or a second unguarded await in doStart. REPRO: seed a wallet row with a checksum-corrupt xpub (scripts/qa/seed-miningqa-wave3.mjs walletB does exactly this), enable that user's mining prefs, boot with mining_enabled=true, watch startup logs. IMPACT: a single corrupt/legacy wallet row (or any wallet-scan error, e.g. transient Electrum failure, since the engine start awaits shared scan promises) can abort mining engine start for ALL users, and 'fatal' entries storm the admin status view (fatalErrors list caps at 50). EXPECTED: engine start must tolerate any individual wallet scan failure; corrupt-xpub wallets should be quarantined/flagged once, not error-storm every background pass (walletScan + portfolioWarm + addressWatcher + txwatch all retrip on it).

title: Self-disable and self-demote are only blocked client-side; unlike self-delete, server actions don't check id === admin.id
description: src/routes/(app)/admin/users/+page.svelte disables the Disable/Demote buttons via disabled={user.id === me?.id} - a client-side-only guard. The server side (src/routes/(app)/admin/users/+page.server.ts actions.disable/actions.demote, and src/routes/api/admin/users/+server.ts POST) call setUserDisabled/setUserAdmin directly with no id===admin.id check; src/lib/server/admin.ts only blocks disabling/demoting when adminCount()<=1 (the last admin), not self specifically. Compare the DELETE handler in src/routes/api/admin/users/+server.ts, which explicitly checks if (body.id === admin.id) return error You cannot delete your own account - the codebase clearly considers self-action guards worth enforcing server-side for destructive-to-self operations (see also the resetInstance comment: the admin layout already gates this route, but a factory reset deserves a second explicit check), but disable/demote didn't get the same treatment. With 2+ admins, an admin can self-disable via a raw POST to /api/admin/users (devtools, curl, or a stale/tampered DOM) and setUserDisabled immediately calls destroyUserSessions(id), killing their own session instantly with no confirmation, recoverable only by asking another admin to re-enable them. Fix: mirror the DELETE handler's self-id check in the disable/demote actions and API route, at minimum for disable.
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Multisig wallet detail page 500s (unhandled MultisigError) after chain network setting changes underneath an existing wallet
description:
Repro (regtest qa2 worktree, port 5192): Created multisig wallet id=1 ("Wave2M 2-of-3") while the live server process's defaultNetwork (src/lib/server/bitcoin/xpub.ts) was 'mainnet' (see cairn-0tvez for how that mismatch with the DB's chain_network=regtest setting arose). Successfully funded, drafted, cosigned 2-of-3, broadcast, and confirmed a spend from it (txid 793bf879556dcac5963974d326cf2d766436876372dcf636b351323f937d2825).

Partway through the session, the settings table gained core_rpc_url/core_rpc_user rows (another party completed the admin Bitcoin-connection setup, which calls reconfigureChain() -- src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts:86), which correctly re-synced defaultNetwork to 'regtest' to match the existing chain_network=regtest row.

After that reconfiguration, GET /wallets/multisig/1 started 500ing instead of loading:

  [error] status=500 path=/wallets/multisig/1 userId=1 errorId=3ea874b3
  MultisigError: Wave2M Key A: Mainnet extended keys are not supported on a regtest backend -- import the testnet/regtest (tpub/upub/vpub) version instead
      at resolveKey (src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisig.ts:225:9)
      at Array.map (<anonymous>)
      at resolveMultisig (src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisig.ts:568:24)
      at multisigToDescriptor (src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisig.ts:840:19)
      at load (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts:98:42)

+page.server.ts's load() calls multisigToDescriptor() unconditionally (line 98, building the `descriptor` field for the page), which throws MultisigError instead of returning a value -- SvelteKit's load() doesn't catch it, so it becomes an unhandled 500 instead of a graceful in-page notice. Also confirmed the same failure via GET /api/wallets/multisig/1 (json {"error":"Wave2M Key A: Mainnet extended keys are not supported on a regtest backend..."}), so this isn't page-load-specific.

Impact: an operator who changes their Bitcoin connection's network (mainnet<->testnet/regtest, e.g. switching from the public Electrum default to a self-hosted regtest/testnet node, or vice versa) permanently loses access to the detail page of any ALREADY-CREATED multisig wallet whose keys were encoded for the old network -- hard 500, not a friendly "this wallet's keys don't match your current network" message, and no migration/re-encode path offered. Single-sig wallets may have the same exposure (parseXpub's network-aware validation is generic); not verified here -- worth a follow-up check.

Not root-caused how far this blast radius extends (e.g. whether /wallets list page, backups, or the send flow also 500 the same way) -- flagging with the one confirmed repro given QA session time budget.
close_reason: Part (b): +page.server.ts load() (wallets/multisig/[id]) now catches MultisigError from multisigToDescriptor(toMultisigConfig(multisig)) (line ~98, confirmed via git blame/trace) and returns descriptor:null + a plain-language descriptorError instead of letting it escape as a raw 500 -- surfaced in +page.svelte as a warning Banner near the top of the page. Regression test added (page.server.test.ts): creates a multisig while defaultNetwork='mainnet' (keys encode mainnet), then flips setDefaultNetwork('regtest') and asserts load() returns descriptorError (not a throw) instead of rejecting; a control case asserts descriptor is populated + descriptorError null while the network still matches. resolveKey (src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisig.ts:219) needs NO further network threading: it calls parseXpub(toStandardXpub(key.xpub)) with no explicit network arg, which already defaults to the CURRENT getDefaultNetwork()/setDefaultNetwork() value (src/lib/server/bitcoin/xpub.ts:94-104, wired since cairn-10ox, predating xqnn7) -- that pre-existing default-parameter mechanism is exactly why the original repro correctly detected the mainnet-key-on-regtest-backend mismatch and threw a specific, correct MultisigError in the first place. eaaf0b2 (cairn-xqnn7) only threaded network through ADDRESS-ENCODING call sites (deriveMultisigAddress/assembleMultisigAddress/createMultisigDeriver in bitcoin/multisig.ts) which resolveKey doesn't touch; it left resolveKey's xpub-parsing network defaulting untouched because it was already correct. npm run check: 0 errors. Tests: page.server.test.ts 4/4 passing.

comments.author: wave2m-qa
comments.text: Also observed same-window log line at 01:44:06: ERROR notify:txwatch failed to enumerate multisigs -- the background address-watcher's periodic multisig enumeration (addressWatcher.ts) appears to hit the same MultisigError for the whole instance, not just this wallet's own page, once defaultNetwork no longer matches a stored wallet's key encoding. Collateral: could silently stop background tx-watch/notify for ALL multisigs on the instance, not just the affected one. Not independently confirmed beyond this one log line given QA time budget.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Part (a) fixed: src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts multisigAddresses() now wraps createMultisigDeriver(config) in its own try/catch (previously outside any per-wallet guard), logging and skipping just that multisig instead of throwing out through enumerateAll()'s per-multisig loop and aborting watch enumeration for every multisig after the bad one. Regression test added in addressWatcher.test.ts (cairn-zltwz part (a) describe block). Part (b) (+page.server.ts descriptor-null handling, multisig.ts resolveKey network threading) intentionally NOT touched here — that is cairn-xqnn7's area. Leaving this bead OPEN pending part (b).

title: Snapshot-backed explorer pages (home, mempool) hard-fail when Electrum is down even though Core RPC is up
description:
Post-Esplora, the mempool page's summary is deliberately Core-RPC-sourced (getmempoolinfo, ChainService.getMempoolSummary, cairn-zoz8), but the page never shows it when Electrum is down — it shows 'Can't reach chain data sources. Retry' instead. The Core-RPC-only sovereignty config the Esplora removal targets gets a broken explorer home + mempool.

REPRO (single-sig-full-wallet @ d632f64):
1. Configure Core RPC to a reachable node (regtest http://127.0.0.1:18543), Electrum on the (unreachable here) public default.
2. Enable explorer flag, open /explorer/mempool. Regtest mempool has 3 txs (getmempoolinfo returns size=3).
3. Page shows 'Can't reach chain data sources. Retry' — NOT the 3-tx summary. (Direct-Core pages /explorer/block/<hash> and /explorer/tx/<id> DO work — verified — because they do live Core RPC calls in their own load; only the snapshot-backed pages fail.)
4. Client POST /api/chain/refresh returns 502 {"error":"Electrum connection closed (electrum.blockstream.info:50002)"}.

ROOT CAUSE:
- /explorer/mempool/+page.server.ts and the explorer home read purely from readChainSnapshot() (SWR cache), no live call.
- The snapshot is built by chainSync.ts doRefresh(). Of its parallel fetches, getMempoolSummary/getFeeEstimates/getTip/histogram/trend all carry .catch(()=>null), BUT chain.getRecentBlocks() (line ~90) is the ONE REQUIRED fetch with NO catch — its rejection throws the whole doRefresh to the outer catch, so writeChainSnapshot() never runs and NOTHING is persisted (not even the Core-sourced mempool summary that resolved fine).
- getRecentBlocks() is Electrum-sourced (base block headers via Electrum, then Core-enriched with getblockstats), so an Electrum outage aborts the entire snapshot even when Core RPC could supply recent blocks (getblockcount+getblockhash+getblock) and the mempool summary.
- /api/chain/refresh/+server.ts returns 502 on any throw with no prior snapshot.

IMPACT: A Core-RPC-only instance (or any Electrum-down + Core-up state, incl. the transient window at boot before the first Electrum handshake) cannot see the explorer home or mempool, despite Core RPC being reachable and serving block/tx pages. Contradicts the settings copy 'point it at your own node for full sovereignty' and the Core-sourced mempool design.

NOTE: the failure IS honest (clear 'Can't reach chain data sources. Retry' error, no infinite spinner, no fake zeros) — so this is a capability gap, not a dishonest-degrade bug.

FIX DIRECTION: let the snapshot build partially succeed — either give getRecentBlocks its own .catch so a null recent-blocks list still writes a snapshot carrying the Core-sourced mempoolSummary, or have getRecentBlocks fall back to Core RPC (getblockcount/getblockhash/getblock) when Electrum is unavailable and Core is configured. The mempool page should render the Core summary with the Electrum-derived fee histogram/projected-blocks honestly null.

Filed by READ-ONLY live-verification agent; not fixing per house rule.
close_reason: chainSync.ts doRefresh: every snapshot field now .catch(()=>null) incl getRecentBlocks; recent blocks carried forward from last-good snapshot or [] (chainSync.ts:106); total-outage (no field reachable) still preserves stale/throws without bumping freshness (chainSync.ts:110-121). Core mempool summary persists when Electrum down. Left Core-only recentBlocks fallback OUT (getTip/getRecentBlocks are Electrum-first; option-1 .catch fully fixes the reported mempool break per bead). Tests: chainSync.test.ts 'partial success persists' (2) + updated total-outage tests. Full suite green.

title: chain/ (Esplora) facade has zero logging anywhere
description: src/lib/server/chain/esplora.ts and chain/index.ts have no logger import at all; every optional-endpoint failure (RBF, CPFP, mempool stats, difficulty, hashrate, price, v1-probe -- roughly 9-10 catch sites) is invisible to the operator. This is the facade behind api/mempool/*, api/tx/*, api/address/*, api/blocks/* routes. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Added tagged loggers to the Esplora facade: childLogger('esplora') in chain/esplora.ts and childLogger('chain') in chain/index.ts. Every optional-endpoint degradation now logs at debug with err + context: v1 probe, prices, v1/blocks, RBF, CPFP, mempool-blocks, statistics, difficulty-adjustment, difficulty-history, hashrate, fees-recommended fallback (esplora.ts) plus public price, block extras, outspends, epoch-pace projection, hashrate fallback (index.ts). Operators can now see why optional data is missing. Typecheck clean.

title: Unconfirmed tx detail shows 'Total in 0.00 BTC' while inputs show unknown (—)
description:
On an unconfirmed (mempool) tx detail page, the per-input values are honestly shown as '—' (prevout amounts not resolved), but the 'Total in' row sums them to '0.00 BTC' — a misleading zero implying the tx has no input value.

REPRO (single-sig-full-wallet @ d632f64, Core RPC up at regtest 127.0.0.1:18543):
1. /explorer/tx/5ee8ce67cca3bdeae624b7b0fcb4ee4f616879017aabe46ae5ee02d4bca27361 (a mempool tx).
2. Header: 'no rings yet / broadcast but not yet in a block'. FEE shows '—' (correct, honest).
3. Under INPUTS: each input row shows '—' for value; 'Total in' shows '0.00 BTC'.
4. Outputs render correctly (48.74997192 + 0.75 = 49.49997192 BTC).

Because prevout values aren't fetched for mempool txs, the individual inputs correctly show '—', but 'Total in' should also show '—' (or be omitted) rather than summing unknowns to 0.00 BTC. Fee already degrades honestly to '—'; the Total-in should match.

Minor/polish (P3). Confirmed txs are unaffected (their prevouts resolve). Found during Core-RPC/post-Esplora live verification; READ-ONLY agent, not fixing.
close_reason: txTotalIn() helper (explorer/tx/[txid]/txTotals.ts) returns known:false when any input value unknown; +page.svelte renders '—' for Total in instead of 0.00 BTC (mempool tx). Merged BlockContext verified honest (blockContext.ts drops null fee/vsize clauses). Test: txTotals.test.ts (4). Full suite green.

title: Nostr relay URLs bypass SSRF guard — WebSocket SSRF oracle
description: channels/nostr.ts:263, api/notifications/channels/[channel]/+server.ts:226-232 — nostr case has no scheme check or SSRF check on relay URLs (webhook/ntfy both enforce HTTP_RE). publishEncryptedDM hands URLs to nostr-tools SimplePool.publish which opens raw WebSocket connections. An authenticated user setting relays: ['ws://127.0.0.1:6379'] and triggering test endpoint gets a blind internal port scanner against loopback/LAN/tailnet. Fix: adapt checkTargetUrl to accept ws:/wss: and apply at both save and publish time.
close_reason: Merged 4418db9: Nostr relay URLs now validated via checkRelayUrl (ws/wss mode) before connecting and at save time. Note: WebSocket connection pinning not achievable with nostr-tools' SimplePool (validates-before-connect only, documented in ssrf.ts header) — full DNS-rebinding closure would need a transport swap, left as a known limitation.

title: QR signer's non-camera fallback is hidden until after a failed camera attempt
description: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/_components/QrSigner.svelte: the manual paste fallback for scanning a signed PSBT back is gated behind !showManual, which only becomes available after the user attempts to start the camera and it fails/is denied. A user who knows upfront they have no camera access (no camera, permissions locked down, non-camera browser) has no way to discover the paste option without first going through a failed camera attempt. Surface the manual paste/upload option as a visible, always-available alternative alongside the camera option from the start, not only as an error-recovery path.
