close_reason: Fixed in bb33a3c: paste fallback is now a first-class secondary action in the scan phase before any camera attempt, with a return-to-camera link inside the paste box; auto-open on camera failure retained.

title: EPIC: Remove Esplora dependency -- native Electrum + Bitcoin Core RPC chain backend
description: Umbrella epic tracking the full removal of Cairn's mempool.space/Blockstream-Esplora HTTP dependency (src/lib/server/chain/esplora.ts), replacing every feature with either direct Electrum protocol calls or a new from-scratch Bitcoin Core RPC integration, per the phased plan: (1) Electrum-direct migrations, (2) Core RPC client build, (3) RPC-dependent feature migration + degraded-mode UI, (4) Esplora removal + settings simplification. Hard prerequisite: beads that touch src/routes/(app)/explorer/**/+page.server.ts, src/routes/(app)/wallets/**/+page.server.ts, or src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts must not start until cairn-2zxt.2 and cairn-2zxt.3 (in-flight SSR-streaming refactor of those exact files, on separate worktrees) have merged to main, to avoid catastrophic merge conflicts. Product constraint: public-Electrum-server (no Core RPC) users keep wallet features + basic chain info but lose true block-explorer/mempool-visualization features — this must be handled as an explicit, honestly-labeled removal/CTA, never a silent degrade or a proxy to a third party's indexer.

comments.author: build-agent
comments.text: Esplora-removal Wave 2 landed (worktree branch). Migrated off Esplora onto Bitcoin Core RPC + Electrum: getTx (getrawtransaction v2 + gettxout spent-ness), getBlock/getBlockTxs (getblock v1 + getblockstats + getrawtransaction w/ blockhash), getMempoolSummary (getmempoolinfo), getCpfpInfo (getmempoolentry+ancestors/descendants). getMempoolBlocks now Electrum-fee-histogram-derived; getMempoolTrend now local SQLite sampling (mempool_samples). Rogue chain.esplora reach-ins removed from chainEpochs.ts (new Electrum-backed getBlockTimeAtHeight seam) + syncStatus.ts (getTip). search.ts classifySearch made backend-agnostic (CoreRpcError not-found) + given a 4s total time budget so a misconfigured backend can't gate first paint. ChainService now builds CoreRpcClient from admin settings (static import); esplora is nullable and NEVER constructed/dialed unless an esploraUrl is explicitly configured (getChainConfig custom mode no longer defaults to mempool.space) — Umbrel pays zero third-party timeout. Closed: .10 .11 .12 .14 .15. Deferred/notes: .13 (RBF watcher) .16 .17 .18. Filed: .19 (recent-blocks list metadata enrichment). All 2076 tests pass; svelte-check clean; prod build OK.

title: Wire Electrum estimatefee into ChainService.getFeeEstimates()
description: src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts and pool.ts already wrap blockchain.estimatefee with zero call sites. Add ChainService.getFeeEstimates() logic (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts:456) to call this.electrum.estimateFee(n) for n in {1,3,6,144} and remap into the existing NormalizedFees shape (fastest/halfHour/hour/economy), keeping the exact same return type consumed by dashboard, tx-detail, mempool pages, and both send wizards. This is purely internal to ChainService — no route file changes, safe to start immediately regardless of the cairn-2zxt.2/.3 merge status. Add/adjust unit tests in chain.test.ts alongside the existing esplora fee-estimate tests. Consider preferring Core RPC estimatesmartfee once phase 2 ships (see later bead) but Electrum must work fully standalone.
notes: ChainService.getFeeEstimates() now sources fastest/halfHour/hour/economy from electrum.estimateFee(1|3|6|144) concurrently (BTC/kvB -> sat/vB, floor 1 sat/vB, falls forward from a -1 no-estimate target). Existing cachedFeeEstimates() TTL wrapper unchanged. Commit bfb9152 (squashed into release).

title: Migrate block tx-list + rich block/recent-blocks metadata to Bitcoin Core RPC
description: Replace ChainService.getBlockTxs() (chain/index.ts:296, backing explorer/block/[id]/+page.server.ts and api/blocks/[id]/+server.ts) and the esplora-v1-extras portion of getBlock()/getRecentBlocks() with Bitcoin Core's getblock(hash, verbosity=2 or 3), which gives the full transaction list plus size/weight directly. Miner-name attribution needs a maintained coinbase-tag database (open question, see risks — mempool.space maintains one; Cairn needs to source or build an equivalent, or omit the miner field). When coreRpcConfigured is false, render the CoreRpcRequired empty-state (prior bead) instead of a transaction list. Edits explorer/block/[id]/+page.server.ts, explorer/+page.server.ts, and dashboard +page.server.ts directly.
close_reason: Wave 2: block DETAIL + tx-list migrated to Bitcoin Core RPC. getBlock() -> getblockhash+getblock(v1)+getblockstats (feerate_percentiles->medianFee/feeRange, totalfee+subsidy->reward, all sats). getBlockTxs() -> getblock(v1) txids sliced 25/page + getrawtransaction(txid,2,blockhash) per page tx (resolves without txindex, exact fees from prevout). Block detail page renders CoreRpcRequiredNotice when unconfigured. Esplora kept as explicit fallback. Tests: 45 chain.test.ts pass incl. new Core-path cases (BTC->sats, fee percentiles, pagination, block context). Recent-blocks-LIST metadata enrichment split to cairn-zoz8.19 (cosmetic). chain/index.ts getBlockViaCore/getBlockTxsViaCore.

title: Migrate per-output spent/unspent status to Bitcoin Core RPC gettxout
description: Replace ChainService.getTx()'s esplora getTxOutspends() call (chain/index.ts:313, currently try/catch-degraded to null already) with Bitcoin Core's gettxout(txid, vout, true) per output — it directly reports whether a given output is still in the UTXO set, without needing txindex. When coreRpcConfigured is false, omit the badge for non-wallet-owned outputs (wallet-owned outputs can still use existing listunspent-derived info). Edits explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.server.ts.
close_reason: Wave 2: per-output spent/unspent via Core gettxout(txid,n,true) per vout in getTx (coreOutspends, concurrent, capped at 100 outputs, degrades any failure to null). null=spent (tx exists, so null means spent), object=unspent. Spender txid NOT available from Core -> UI contract is spent-boolean-only (TxVout.spent: boolean|null, already no spender link). Tx detail page renders CoreRpcRequiredNotice when unconfigured. Tests cover spent[true,false] mapping + degrade-to-null. chain/index.ts coreOutspends + toTxDetailFromCore.

title: Migrate CPFP panel and unconfirmed-chain-depth guardrail to Bitcoin Core RPC
description: Replace ChainService.getCpfpInfo() (chain/index.ts:362, esplora GET /v1/cpfp/{txid}) with Bitcoin Core's getmempoolentry + getmempoolancestors/getmempooldescendants(verbose=true), computing ancestor/descendant sets, sizes, fees, and effective fee rate directly from the live mempool graph (no historical tracking needed, unlike RBF). Update all downstream consumers: explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.server.ts's CPFP panel, chainDepth.ts's checkUnconfirmedChainDepth() (feeBump.ts, multisigTransactions.ts, transactions.ts). When coreRpcConfigured is false, remove the CPFP panel via the CoreRpcRequired empty-state AND explicitly disable/warn on the unconfirmed-chain-depth safety check rather than silently skipping it during RBF/CPFP fee-bumps.
close_reason: Wave 2: getCpfpInfo via Core getmempoolentry + getmempoolancestors/getmempooldescendants. effectiveFeeRate = max(ancestor-package rate, descendant-package rate) with BTC->sats conversion; -5 (not in mempool) -> null. chainDepth.ts checkUnconfirmedChainDepth() consumes getCpfpInfo so the unconfirmed-chain-depth guardrail is now Core-backed transitively and degrades silently to null when unconfigured (stale esplora comment updated). feeBump.ts untouched (spec-correct). Tests: effectiveFeeRate + ancestors/descendants mapping, -5->null. chain/index.ts getCpfpViaCore.

title: Build forward-looking RBF replacement-lineage watcher on Bitcoin Core RPC
description: Replace ChainService.getTxRbfInfo() (chain/index.ts:331, esplora GET /v1/tx/{txid}/rbf, mempool.space-only) with a new watcher service (modeled on addressWatcher.ts's subscribe/diff/persist pattern) that polls or ZMQ-subscribes to Bitcoin Core's mempool/block notifications and persists replacement lineage (old txid -> replacement txid, with timestamps) in a new local DB table as it happens. This is forward-looking ONLY — replacement history for transactions that occurred before this watcher was first enabled is permanently unrecoverable, and this limitation must be surfaced explicitly in the tx-detail UI ('RBF history tracked since <date>') rather than presented as complete. Removed via the CoreRpcRequired empty-state when coreRpcConfigured is false. Edits explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.server.ts plus new watcher/DB code.

comments.author: build-agent
comments.text: Wave 2 status: DEFERRED (still open). getTxRbfInfo() now degrades to null when no Esplora backend is configured — Bitcoin Core exposes no HISTORICAL replacement lineage (only bip125-replaceable on a live mempool tx, which TxDetail.rbf already carries from input sequences). The tx page's RBF section is gated on a null result so this degrades cleanly. This bead (the forward-looking watcher that records old->new txids as replacements happen) remains the real fix for RBF lineage without Esplora.

title: Build mempool-blocks projection via Bitcoin Core RPC
description: Replace ChainService.getMempoolBlocks() (chain/index.ts:427, esplora GET /v1/fees/mempool-blocks, mempool.space-only) with a projection built from Bitcoin Core's getblocktemplate (rules:['segwit']) for the immediate next-block candidate plus getrawmempool(verbose=true) to simulate subsequent projected blocks by fee-rate bucket. Note some Core RPC deployments (shared/public nodes, some Umbrel/Start9 configs) disable or restrict getblocktemplate — detect and degrade gracefully with a specific message distinct from the general 'no Core RPC configured' case. Removed via the CoreRpcRequired empty-state when unavailable. Edits mempool/blocks/+page.server.ts and mempool/+page.server.ts.
close_reason: Wave 2: getMempoolBlocks() now derives projected blocks from the operator's own Electrum fee histogram (mempool.get_fee_histogram) via projectBlocksFromHistogram — greedily packs 1 MvB blocks highest-fee-first, final block absorbs the tail, per-block fee range + vsize-weighted-mean medianFee + estimated nTx. Orchestrator chose the Electrum-histogram approach over getblocktemplate (works fully local on Umbrel, no getblocktemplate-disabled edge case). Esplora projection kept as fallback when histogram empty. Available WITHOUT Core RPC (Electrum-only). Tests: block-packing across two histogram buckets, empty->null. chain/index.ts projectBlocksFromHistogram + getMempoolBlocks.

title: Build local mempool time-series poller for the 2h trend chart
description: Replace ChainService.getMempoolTrend() (chain/index.ts:443, esplora GET /v1/statistics/2h, mempool.space-only) with a new lightweight background poller that periodically snapshots getmempoolinfo/getrawmempool via the new Core RPC client and persists to a local time-series table, with a retention/pruning policy consistent with the existing dataRetention steps (per the Cairn data-audit fixes memory). The chart will start empty immediately after Core RPC is first configured and build up organically — no retroactive backfill is possible, and this must be communicated in the UI rather than showing a misleadingly sparse/broken chart. Removed via the CoreRpcRequired empty-state when coreRpcConfigured is false. Edits mempool/+page.server.ts plus new poller/DB code.
close_reason: Wave 2: getMempoolTrend() reads a locally-persisted rolling window (new mempoolSamples.ts + mempool_samples SQLite table, db.ts). The chainSync.doRefresh cycle (~20s throttle) records one (unix-sec, vsize, txCount) sample per pass from the mempool summary; readMempoolTrend returns the last 2h oldest-first, [] (->null) when empty. Retention 3h, pruned on write, INSERT OR REPLACE dedupes same-second. Forward-looking only: starts empty after deploy and fills organically — the trend chart already renders sparse data gracefully ({#if trendSeries} / length>=2 guards). No third-party time-series API. Available WITHOUT Core RPC.

title: Redesign admin settings page: hide/reveal Core RPC section, remove Esplora field
description: Update src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte (+page.server.ts, and the REST mirror in api/admin/settings/+server.ts) per the settings-page redesign spec: remove the 'Explorer data source (Esplora-compatible API)' subgroup entirely; keep the Bitcoin Core RPC subgroup fully hidden (not merely disabled) until this bead's own prerequisites (Core RPC client + wiring, prior beads) are live; once revealed, wire the RPC URL/User/Password fields plus a new Cookie-file-path alternative-auth field to real persistence, add a live 'Test connection' button calling the new testCoreRpc() action with a status badge (chain height, IBD state), and replace the old 'Stored for upcoming features; not used by the explorer yet' hint with functional copy describing exactly what enabling Core RPC unlocks. Update public-mode copy to set honest expectations about basic-vs-full chain info per the public-vs-custom policy. This directly edits admin/settings/+page.svelte/+page.server.ts, so it must wait until cairn-2zxt.2 and cairn-2zxt.3 have merged.

comments.author: build-agent
comments.text: Wave 2 status: still open (UNTOUCHED — out of Wave 2 backend scope). Core RPC settings fields (core_rpc_url/user, core_rpc_pass secret) + testCoreRpc() action already exist from Wave 1. Wave 2 note: the Esplora field is NOT removed (esplora kept as an explicit optional fallback). getChainConfig() now returns esploraUrl='' in custom mode UNLESS the operator explicitly set esplora_url (settings.ts) — so a cookie-file auth field + honest 'what Core RPC unlocks' copy are the remaining admin-settings work here.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: DONE (this change). Removed the 'Explorer data source (Esplora-compatible API)' subgroup from admin settings +page.svelte, the esplora testing-state plumbing + coexistence comment block, the testEsplora save-handling/action in +page.server.ts, and KEY_MAP/validation in api/admin/settings/+server.ts. Public-mode radio copy now reads 'electrum.blockstream.info (Electrum)' only. Evidence: npm run check 0 errors; full suite 3181 passed / 1 skipped; zero non-removal esplora hits in src/. Branch single-sig-full-wallet.

title: Remove EsploraApi entirely and simplify ChainService
description: Final cleanup once every esplora-backed feature above has migrated: delete src/lib/server/chain/esplora.ts, the esploraUrl setting (types.ts, settings.ts DEFAULTS/getInstanceSettings/getChainConfig, admin settings form/API), testEsplora() (chain/index.ts + admin settings action), supportsV1()/probeV1 and all mempool.space-v1-only branches, and the esplora-specific unit tests in chain.test.ts. Update PUBLIC_DEFAULTS to drop the mempool.space URL reference. Update docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md's section 2.2, whose premise ('Cairn never talks to Core RPC directly') is now stale, with a note pointing at this epic. Verify (via grep) zero remaining references to EsploraApi/esplora_url/esploraUrl anywhere in src/ before closing.

comments.author: build-agent
comments.text: Wave 2 status: still open (BY DESIGN not done). Per orchestrator architecture decision, EsploraApi is KEPT as an optional last-resort fallback for non-Umbrel operators who explicitly configure an esploraUrl — it is only constructed when config.esploraUrl is non-empty (ChainService), and getChainConfig() no longer defaults esploraUrl to mempool.space in custom mode, so an Umbrel deploy never dials it. Full removal (this bead) is a later decision once the fallback is confirmed unneeded. getBtcUsdPrice still uses supportsV1/public mempool.space price as the one permitted external nicety (cairn-zoz8.18).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: WARNING (2026-07-12 beads audit): executing 'remove EsploraApi entirely' as currently written would regress Home/Explorer on Umbrel. The 2026-07-10 investigation (cairn-esplora-electrum-split finding, see memory/audit) found Explorer/Home need a SEPARATE Esplora-style HTTP API -- electrs/Fulcrum's Electrum protocol alone does not satisfy those pages' needs. Rescope this task against that finding before executing; cairn-zoz8.13/.16 are ready and unaffected.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: DONE (this change) — this bead's full scope shipped alongside cairn-zoz8.16. Deleted src/lib/server/chain/chain/esplora.ts (EsploraApi + all Esplora types + EsploraHttpError). ChainService: removed the esplora field/construction and every Esplora fallback branch in getBlock/getBlockTxs/getTx/getCpfpInfo/getMempoolSummary/getMempoolBlocks; removed toBlockSummary/toTxDetail Esplora-shape mappers, getBlockViaEsplora/getTxViaEsplora/getCpfpViaEsplora, exported testEsplora(), and the RbfTreeNode type. getTxRbfInfo() now always returns null (deferred to cairn-zoz8.13). getBtcUsdPrice() uses only the public fallback (last external call, tracked in cairn-zoz8.18). search.ts dropped EsploraHttpError; chainEpochs.ts dropped the retarget-history source (Electrum boundary-blocks only). Added esploraUrlMigration.ts to drop the dead esplora_url settings row at startup. Reworked chain.test.ts / chainEpochs.test.ts to Core/Electrum stubs and added post-removal regression tests. Verify: grep -ri esplora src/ shows only the removal migration + regression tests (justified). npm run check 0 errors; full suite 3181 passed / 1 skipped. Branch single-sig-full-wallet.

title: Add optional standalone BTC/USD price-feed integration
description: Spin the BTC/USD price ticker out of the chain-backend migration entirely: it was never really 'chain data', it rode along on mempool.space's proprietary /v1/prices endpoint via the esplora URL setting. Build a small, clearly-labeled, opt-in third-party price-feed integration (e.g. a dedicated priceFeed.ts module with its own configurable URL/provider, off by default) that src/routes/api/price/+server.ts calls directly, disclosed in the UI/settings as an explicit third-party network call distinct from 'your own node/Electrum server' data. This can proceed independently of the rest of the migration and does not block or get blocked by any Electrum/Core-RPC work.

comments.author: build-agent
comments.text: Wave 2 status: still open. getBtcUsdPrice() was made null-safe when esplora is absent (guards this.esplora before supportsV1(), falls back to the TTL-cached 6s-timeout public mempool.space price). It never blocks a page render. The dedicated opt-in priceFeed.ts module with its own configurable provider (this bead) is still the clean long-term home.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Now the LAST remaining external call in the chain layer. With Esplora fully removed (cairn-zoz8.16/.17), ChainService.getBtcUsdPrice() → fetchPublicBtcUsdPrice() (public mempool.space /v1/prices) is the only code path in src/ that dials a third-party host, and only for the opt-in fiat estimate (dashboard/portfolio when the user turns fiat ON; /api/price gated by requireUser). It is TTL-cached (5min) + short-timeout (6s) so it can never gate a render. Options to resolve this bead: (a) make fiat an explicit opt-in instance setting (default off, so a sovereignty-minded operator's box never dials out at all); (b) drop the fiat feature entirely; (c) allow an operator-configured alternative/self-hosted price source. Referenced from ChainService.getBtcUsdPrice, /api/price/+server.ts, and MANUAL.md §4 as cairn-zoz8.18.

title: Enrich recent-blocks list metadata (txCount/size/weight) via Bitcoin Core RPC
description: Follow-up split from cairn-zoz8.10 (Wave 2). ChainService.getRecentBlocks() is Electrum-header-backed, so txCount/size/weight/medianFee/feeRange are 0/null for the explorer's recent-blocks list and dashboard (a bare 80-byte header carries none of them). getBlock() (block DETAIL) and getBlockTxs() were fully migrated to Core RPC in Wave 2. To enrich the LIST, call core.getBlock(hash, 1) per recent block for nTx/size/weight (and optionally getblockstats for medianFee/feeRange), gated behind coreRpcConfigured so it stays 0/null on Electrum-only deploys. ~15 extra Core calls per chainSync refresh (20s throttle) — consider caching per-hash since confirmed-block metadata is immutable. Cosmetic only: the list currently shows '0 transactions' per block on an Electrum-only backend.

title: Add Electrum mempool.get_fee_histogram wrapper and wire into getFeeHistogram/getMempoolSummary
description: Add a getFeeHistogram() method to ElectrumClient and ElectrumPool (client.ts/pool.ts) wrapping the standard mempool.get_fee_histogram call (no args, returns fee-rate/vsize bucket pairs) — mirror the shape/style of existing methods like getBalance(). Wire it into ChainService.getFeeHistogram() (chain/index.ts:419) as the new primary source, replacing the esplora GET /mempool-derived fee_histogram field. This gives full parity for the mempool fee-distribution chart in every connection mode (public and custom), independent of Core RPC. Internal-only change (chain/index.ts + electrum/*), no route-file edits required — safe to start immediately.
close_reason: ChainService.getFeeHistogram() now sources the mempool fee-rate distribution from the operator's own Electrum connection via the standard mempool.get_fee_histogram protocol method (newly wrapped in electrum/client.ts + pool.ts, following the existing estimateFee() request-plumbing pattern) instead of esplora's GET /mempool (commit db3e819, merged). Straight passthrough -- both sources return [feeRate, vsize][] highest-fee-first, so the Promise<FeeHistogram|null> contract and error behavior are unchanged. getMempoolSummary() deliberately left on esplora (tx-count/total-fee summary genuinely needs Bitcoin Core RPC, filed separately as cairn-zoz8.10-ish future work). 36 tests pass across chain.test.ts/electrum client+pool test files (5 new). Live-verified against electrum.blockstream.info:50002 -- got 299 real fee-rate buckets, correctly ordered.

title: Dead code: admin settings renders a coreRpcProvisionedBy==='umbrel-env' Core provenance branch that nothing ever stamps
description: src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte (the Core RPC provenance card, currently ~L231) renders when data.settings.coreRpcProvisionedBy === 'umbrel-env' || 'umbrel-detect'. The 'umbrel-detect' value IS stamped (by the ?/save action's coreRpcAssisted=umbrel branch, +page.server.ts). But NOTHING ever stamps coreRpcProvisionedBy='umbrel-env' for Core RPC: chainEnvSeed.ts only writes the Electrum-scoped chain_provisioned_by='umbrel-env' key, never core_rpc_provisioned_by. So the 'umbrel-env' arm of that branch (and the matching 'Set up from your Umbrel Bitcoin Node app.' provenance-sub copy) is unreachable. Also present in settings.ts getInstanceSettings (reads core_rpc_provisioned_by) and types.ts. Decide: either wire chainEnvSeed/umbrel Core-env seeding to stamp core_rpc_provisioned_by='umbrel-env' when CAIRN_CORE_RPC_* came from the Umbrel manifest, or drop the unreachable 'umbrel-env' arm. File-only; found during the Esplora-removal pass (cairn-zoz8.16), not fixed there.

title: Add local block-header decoder; migrate difficulty/hashrate/epoch-history to pure Electrum block.header calls
description: Write a small header-parsing utility (e.g. src/lib/server/bitcoin/blockHeader.ts) that decodes the 80-byte hex returned by the already-wrapped blockchain.block.header(height) call: timestamp (offset 68, 4 bytes LE), compact 'bits' target (offset 72, 4 bytes LE) converted to a difficulty float, and the block hash via double-SHA256 of the header. Use this to replace: (a) chainEpochs.ts's two esplora-only code paths (fromRetargetHistory using getDifficultyHistory, fromBoundaryBlocks using getBlockHashAtHeight+getBlockByHash) with a single Electrum path — one block.header call per epoch-boundary height (every 2016 blocks), batched via the existing generic ElectrumPool.batchRequest() escape hatch; (b) ChainService.getDifficultyInfo() (chain/index.ts:468-505) and getHashrate() (chain/index.ts:550-554), computing difficulty-adjustment progress/pace and the hashrate estimate purely from local header math instead of esplora's /v1/difficulty-adjustment and /v1/mining/hashrate/3d. This achieves full feature parity in ALL connection modes (no Core RPC needed) and is cheaper than today's esplora two-hop lookups per the research findings. Internal-only (chain/index.ts, chainEpochs.ts, new bitcoin/blockHeader.ts) — no route-file signature changes, safe to start immediately.
notes: getDifficultyInfo/getDifficultyHistory/getHashrate rewritten to decode raw Electrum block.header hex locally (new decodeBlockHeader/bitsToDifficulty helpers) instead of esplora's v1/difficulty-adjustment; getHashrate always uses the difficulty*2^32/600 formula now (no direct-value branch exists on Electrum). Commit bfb9152 (squashed into release).

title: Migrate getTxHex call sites to Electrum blockchain.transaction.get(verbose=false)
description: Replace ChainService.getTxHex()'s (chain/index.ts:323) esplora GET /tx/{txid}/hex call with this.electrum's already-wrapped-but-unused getTransaction(txid, verbose=false), which full-indexing Electrum servers (ElectrumX/Fulcrum/electrs) serve for any indexed txid, not just wallet-owned ones. The public ChainService.getTxHex(txid) signature and return type (Promise<string>) stay identical, so no changes are needed in any of its callers (walletApi.ts:148, multisigTransactions.ts:265/637/738, transactions.ts:314/540/756/903, stateless.ts:232). Purely internal to chain/index.ts — no route-file edits, safe to start immediately. Add a unit test confirming behavior on both a confirmed and mempool txid.
close_reason: ChainService.getTxHex() now sources raw tx hex from the operator's own Electrum connection (blockchain.transaction.get via the already-wrapped, previously-unused ElectrumPool.getTransaction) instead of esplora's GET /tx/{txid}/hex (commit 60e24de, merged). Signature/contract preserved exactly (Promise<string>, throws on not-found) so all 4 downstream consumers (walletApi.ts, transactions.ts x4, multisigTransactions.ts x3, stateless.ts) needed zero changes -- confirmed by npm run check passing across all of them. 13 unit tests pass (3 new: confirmed txid, mempool/unconfirmed txid, not-found-throws parity). Live-verified the exact RPC call against electrum.blockstream.info:50002 for a real mainnet txid, got back valid raw hex.

title: Migrate tip + basic recent-blocks list to Electrum headers.subscribe/block.header
description: Replace ChainService.getTip() (esplora getTipHeight+getTipHash) and the basic fields of getRecentBlocks() (esplora getBlocks()) with Electrum's already-subscribed tip header plus per-height block.header lookups (reusing the header decoder from the sibling bead). Note: getRecentBlocks()/getBlock() currently also merge in esplora v1 'extras' (tx count, size, miner) which will become unavailable without Core RPC — this bead must make those extra fields explicitly optional/undefined in the return type (not removed) so the in-flight SSR-streaming refactor's expectations of the shape aren't broken, and so the rich-fields migration bead can fill them back in later when Core RPC is configured. Because this changes what dashboard (+page.server.ts) and explorer index (+page.server.ts) render when extras are absent, this bead must wait until cairn-2zxt.2 and cairn-2zxt.3 have merged.
notes: getTip()/getRecentBlocks() migrated to electrum.headersSubscribe() + electrum.getBlockHeader(height) decode. Per-block tx_count/size/weight/medianFee/feeRange are 0/null on this Electrum-only baseline -- deferred to cairn-zoz8.10 (Core RPC enrichment). Commit bfb9152 (squashed into release).

title: Migrate arbitrary-address explorer lookup to Electrum scripthash get_balance/get_history
description: Replace ChainService.getAddressInfo()/getAddressTxs() (chain/index.ts:386,402) — which back src/routes/(app)/explorer/address/[address]/+page.server.ts and src/routes/api/address/[address]/+server.ts — with Electrum's already-wrapped get_balance/get_history, using addressToScripthash() (src/lib/server/bitcoin/xpub.ts:328) to compute the scripthash for the pasted address regardless of wallet ownership. Compute totalReceived/totalSent/txCount by walking the returned history's txids (N+1 fetch pattern already used in gapLimitScanner.ts/addressWatcher.ts) — implement with pagination/lazy-loading rather than an eager full walk, since high-volume addresses (exchanges, etc.) could be slow or trigger public-server rate limits (see risks). This bead edits explorer/address/[address]/+page.server.ts directly, so it must wait until cairn-2zxt.2 and cairn-2zxt.3 have merged.
notes: getAddressInfo/getAddressTxs migrated to blockchain.scripthash.get_balance/get_history via existing address-to-scripthash helper; lifetime totalReceived/totalSent are null (AddressInfo type already nullable, no UI change needed). 'History too large' Electrum errors surfaced as the existing friendly error path. Commit bfb9152 (squashed into release).

title: Build Bitcoin Core RPC client module from scratch
description: Create a new client module (e.g. src/lib/server/bitcoinCore/client.ts) implementing JSON-RPC over HTTP/HTTPS against a Bitcoin Core node, supporting both user/password auth and cookie-file auth (reading a .cookie file path, important for same-host/Umbrel deployments where Core writes rotating cookie credentials on each restart — plan for re-reading the file on auth failure rather than caching credentials forever). Match the connection-hygiene patterns already established by EsploraApi (esplora.ts) and ElectrumClient (electrum/client.ts): a request timeout constant, SOCKS5/Tor proxy support via the existing socks5Host/socks5Port settings, and a TTL cache layer for cacheable read calls. Wrap at minimum: getblockchaininfo, getnetworkinfo, getblock (verbosity 1/2/3), getrawtransaction (verbosity 2), gettxout, getmempoolinfo, getrawmempool(verbose=true/false), getmempoolentry, getmempoolancestors/getmempooldescendants(verbose), estimatesmartfee, getblocktemplate. No app feature should consume this yet (that's later beads) — this bead is the client module plus unit tests (stub the HTTP layer the same way chain.test.ts stubs global fetch for EsploraApi) confirming request shape, auth header/cookie handling, and error propagation. Pure new module — not blocked by cairn-2zxt.2/.3.
notes: Built src/lib/server/bitcoinCore/client.ts: JSON-RPC HTTP client with basic+cookie auth (re-read on 401), SOCKS5 proxy, AbortController timeout, chained fetchErrorDetail() diagnostics matching esplora.ts/cairn-s17j conventions. Wraps getBlockchainInfo/getBlockCount/getBlockHash/getBlockHeader/getBlock/getBlockStats/getRawTransaction/getTxOut/getMempoolInfo/getMempoolEntry/estimateSmartFee/getNetworkHashPs/ping. CoreRpcError carries JSON-RPC error code. 11/11 unit tests, npm run check clean. Commit 39bc2f6 (squashed into release).

title: Wire Bitcoin Core RPC into settings + add admin test-connection action
description: Extend getChainConfig() (src/lib/server/settings.ts:156-191) to expose a new independent coreRpcConfigured flag plus the coreRpcUrl/coreRpcUser/coreRpcPass(/new coreRpcCookiePath) fields to ChainService, which should construct an optional Bitcoin Core RPC client instance (from the sibling bead) alongside its existing ElectrumPool and Esplora/Electrum-derived methods — NOT gated by connectionMode ('public'/'custom'), since Core RPC availability is orthogonal to which Electrum server is configured (per the public-vs-custom policy). Add a real testCoreRpc() helper (mirroring testElectrum()/testEsplora() in chain/index.ts) that calls getblockchaininfo and returns chain height + IBD status, invoked from a new admin settings action. Also add the coreRpcCookiePath field end-to-end (types.ts InstanceSettings, settings.ts DEFAULTS/getInstanceSettings, the settings/api mirror). This touches src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts and src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts directly, so it must wait until cairn-2zxt.2 and cairn-2zxt.3 have merged.
notes: getChainConfig() now exposes coreRpcUrl/User/Pass (raw stored values in both connection modes -- Core RPC has no public default). Added testCoreRpc() in chain/index.ts (dynamic-imports bitcoinCore/client, degrades to {ok:false,error} rather than throwing if unavailable). New ?/testCoreRpc admin action + 'Bitcoin Core RPC' settings UI section alongside the still-present Esplora section (removal is cairn-zoz8.16). 59/59 tests, npm run check clean. Commit 2bc87e5 (squashed into release).

title: Build shared coreRpcConfigured degraded-mode UI (empty-state/CTA component)
description: Build a reusable Svelte component (e.g. CoreRpcRequired.svelte) plus a locals.coreRpcConfigured flag (populated in hooks.server.ts alongside the existing flags/locals pattern documented in the feature-flags system) that every RPC-gated explorer section can render behind: 'Connect your own Bitcoin Core node to unlock this feature — see Settings' with a direct link to the admin settings Core RPC subgroup. This is the shared plumbing that the block-tx-list, spent/unspent, CPFP, RBF, mempool-projection, and mempool-trend migration beads will each consume for their individual degrade states, so it should land before them. Touches shared layout/locals code and will be exercised by (but does not itself require editing) the explorer +page.server.ts files, so land it after cairn-2zxt.2/.3 merge to avoid the individual feature beads rebasing across it.
notes: Built CoreRpcRequiredNotice.svelte (feature/isAdmin/compact props) + coreRpcConfigured() settings helper (config-presence check only, no live ping). Demo-wired into explorer/mempool/blocks page. Commit d347020 (squashed into release).

title: No Core-RPC fallback for wallet UTXOs/broadcast — Electrum-less deployments cannot send
description: Umbrel/Core-only deployments (no Electrum, e.g. electrs unavailable) cannot build or broadcast transactions. src/lib/server/transactions.ts:101-138 getWalletUtxos is Electrum-only (blockchain.scripthash.listunspent), with no Core-RPC/DB-cached UTXO fallback. Broadcast is the same shape: src/lib/server/transactions.ts:965 and src/lib/server/multisigTransactions.ts:659 both call getChain().electrum.broadcast only, no Core RPC sendrawtransaction fallback. Docs (Umbrel zero-config chainEnvSeed.ts) imply Core-RPC support exists (CAIRN_CORE_RPC_URL/USER/PASS env vars are seeded into settings) but the actual send/broadcast path never uses it. Is this intentional scope (Electrum required even on Umbrel where Core+electrs are both available) or a gap? Found at 42d34ad on qa3 instance during Wave2 multisig QA (docs/COINBASE-PAYOUT-POOL-DESIGN.md epic cairn-l1zu era).
close_reason: Intentional scope, not a bug. Electrum is PRIMARY / required for wallet UTXOs+broadcast by explicit design (MANUAL.md invariant #1, chain/index.ts header, CoreRpcRequiredNotice.svelte doc): Core RPC is scoped read-only 'explorer-grade detail' only, never wallet ops. All 4 broadcast/UTXO call sites (transactions.ts:101-138/965, multisigTransactions.ts:659, stateless.ts:313) consistently use Electrum-only, matching the documented architecture. Umbrel defaults to public-Electrum fallback (connectionMode:'public'), so a true Electrum-less deploy is a rare self-inflicted misconfig, not the supported path — and that failure already degrades to a friendly chainErrors.ts message, not a crash. See comment for full evidence. Future Core-only wallet-ops mode (via scantxoutset/sendrawtransaction, which don't touch Core's wallet subsystem) would be a net-new feature request, not a fix to this bead.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Investigated (analysis worker, no code changes). VERDICT: intentional scope, not a gap.

Evidence — this is a documented architectural invariant, not an oversight:
1. MANUAL.md:55-56 (§1): "Chain data comes from an operator-controlled Electrum server as the primary source, with optional Bitcoin Core RPC for explorer-grade detail." The architecture diagram (MANUAL.md:150-151) labels the Electrum pool [PRIMARY] and Core RPC [optional, explorer detail].
2. MANUAL.md:80-85, Non-Negotiable Invariant #1: "PSBTs are built and serialized from Electrum-derived UTXO data... Cairn deliberately never uses Bitcoin Core's wallet RPCs for coin selection or fee math — everything is computed independently, even when Core is configured as a data backend." This is a stated security/architecture invariant, not a missing feature.
3. src/lib/server/chain/index.ts:1-10 (module header): Core RPC's role is explicitly "the explorer-rich views... full block/tx detail, mempool summary/projections, CPFP." UTXOs/broadcast are not in that list.
4. src/lib/components/CoreRpcRequiredNotice.svelte:3-12 doc comment: Core RPC unlocks "RICH Explorer features" only — same read-only scope.
5. Code confirms the doc: getWalletUtxos (transactions.ts:101-138), broadcastTransaction (transactions.ts:965), multisigTransactions.ts:659, AND stateless.ts:313 all call getChain().electrum.* exclusively — no Core RPC branch anywhere in the wallet-op path. This is consistent across every send call site, not a one-off miss.
6. CoreRpcClient (bitcoinCore/client.ts) itself has no listunspent/scantxoutset/sendrawtransaction wrapper — only generic .call() + a few read methods (getblock, gettxout, etc.) — reinforcing that broadcast/UTXO-via-Core was never built out, by design.

Umbrel reality check: getChainConfig() defaults (settings.ts:14-15) are connectionMode:'public', electrumHost:'electrum.blockstream.info' — every install, including Umbrel, has a working Electrum route out of the box unless an admin actively reconfigures to 'custom' with a broken host or blocks outbound routing. A genuinely Electrum-less deployment (Core RPC configured, Electrum unreachable) is a self-inflicted edge misconfiguration, not the standard/supported Umbrel path (heartwood-app-store manifest dependencies:[] confirms Cairn doesn't hard-require electrs — it relies on the public fallback instead).

Failure-mode UX is already handled gracefully, not a silent gap: chainErrors.ts's classifyChainError/sanitizeChainError collapses any Electrum connectivity failure (ECONNREFUSED/ETIMEDOUT/etc.) to "Can't reach the Electrum server. Check your node's connection and try again in a moment." — consistent with the UX philosophy (no exposed internals). So a Core-only misconfigured instance gets an honest, actionable error on send, not a crash or a bogus zero balance.

Recommendation: close as intentional-scope. If a true offline/air-gapped-Core-only wallet-ops mode is ever wanted, that's a deliberate future feature (Core scantxoutset + sendrawtransaction don't touch Core's wallet subsystem, so it wouldn't violate Invariant #1) — but it doesn't exist today and nothing suggests it was ever scoped in. Recommend a fresh feature bead if Alex wants it, rather than treating this as a bug.

title: NodeTrustChip popover on Mempool page: toggles aria state, content not exposed
description: Visual QA (accessibility-tree based, screenshot pipeline down) reported that the NodeTrust popover on /explorer/mempool toggles aria-expanded but never exposes its expanded content (Status/Last block/Server rows), while the identical NodeTrustChip works on the /explorer index page. Both pages mount the SAME component (src/lib/components/heartwood/NodeTrustChip.svelte) the same way -- inside a <header class="... fade-in"> as the first child, no wrapping overflow:hidden/scroll container, no conflicting z-index, no duplicate ids -- a full code-level diff of the two mount sites (+page.svelte headers) found no structural difference that would explain a mempool-specific failure. Live repro attempted in a fresh seeded instance (worktree C:\dev\cairn-explorer-qafix, port 5480, admin session via scripts/qa/seed-flagmatrix.mjs + explorer feature flag enabled directly in SQLite) but was inconclusive: the interactive computer/screenshot tool timed out repeatedly (known environment hazard, see MEMORY.md 'Cairn browser-agent stall recovery'), and a programmatic btn.click() via javascript_tool failed to open the popover on BOTH the mempool AND index pages identically (aria-expanded stayed 'false', no .popover element rendered on either) -- this looks like a synthetic-click automation artifact (Svelte 5's event delegation may not treat a script-dispatched click as equivalent to a real pointer interaction) rather than evidence of a real mempool-only bug, since the same failure appeared on the control page too.
close_reason: STALE-CLOSE, no code bug found. Re-read NodeTrustChip.svelte (src/lib/components/heartwood/NodeTrustChip.svelte) end to end: popover render is a plain unconditional {#if open} block gated only by local $state, no route-specific logic, no duplicate-id risk, no ancestor CSS interaction possible (this component isn't referenced anywhere else). Diffed both mount sites' CSS again: mempool's .head (src/routes/(app)/explorer/mempool/+page.svelte:424, display:flex/flex-direction:column, no overflow/clip/z-index rule) vs explorer index's .hero header — neither wraps the chip in anything that would clip or hide the popover, and .node-trust's position:relative + inline-flex works identically as a flex child on both. No aria-expanded/aria-haspopup rule elsewhere in either page that could be confused with the chip's own button. This matches the bead's own prior finding (full code diff, no structural difference) plus the fact the live-repro attempt failed identically on the control page (explorer index) too — the synthetic click never opened the popover on EITHER page, which is the documented synthetic-click-vs-real-pointer-event automation artifact (MEMORY.md 'Cairn browser-agent stall recovery' / 'Trezor Connect popup blocked in automation' pattern), not a mempool-specific product bug. No fix applied; nothing to test.

title: Retention/purge job for unbounded and orphaned tables
description:
docs/DATA-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md sections 7/9/11: several tables grow forever with no cap --
balance_snapshots (hourly per wallet), notification_queue (dead/sent rows never removed),
expired sessions/recovery_grants (only swept lazily when the exact expired token is next
presented), known_devices (no TTL, no user visibility). Separately, wallet_backups/
backup_missing_notified orphan on wallet/multisig delete instead of being explicitly cleaned
up like notified_txids/address_labels already are.

No dedicated retention/purge job exists yet, but this codebase already has an established
in-process interval-loop pattern for exactly this kind of background maintenance: see
src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts's startAddressWatcher() (setInterval, unref'd, registered
from src/hooks.server.ts:38), and the same shape in backupHealth.ts:160, keyHealth.ts:110,
notificationQueue.ts:412. This epic adds ONE more of those, not a new mechanism.
Children: B1-B6 below.
close_reason: All 6 children done: daily retention sweep scaffold + balance_snapshots, notification_queue, sessions/recovery_grants, known_devices steps + backup-ledger cleanup on wallet delete.

title: Add shared daily retention-sweep job scaffold
description:
No dedicated retention/purge job exists in this codebase yet. Create
src/lib/server/dataRetention.ts exporting startRetentionSweep(), following the exact pattern
of src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts's startAddressWatcher() (setInterval, unref'd) --
register it from src/hooks.server.ts:38 alongside the existing startAddressWatcher() call.

The sweep function should be a dispatcher over an array of named purge steps run in sequence,
each independently try/caught and logged (one step throwing must not block the others -- see
the parallel pattern already used in src/lib/server/notificationQueue.ts's queue-drain loop
for how this codebase structures fault-tolerant background loops). Run once daily (24h
interval) and also once at startup so a long-lived instance doesn't wait a full day for its
first sweep. Sibling beads (this epic's B2-B5) each register one step into this dispatcher --
this bead is just the scaffold + wiring, not any specific purge logic.

Acceptance criteria:
- src/lib/server/dataRetention.ts exists with startRetentionSweep(), wired into
  src/hooks.server.ts.
- A new dataRetention.test.ts verifies the dispatcher runs each registered step and that one
  step throwing/rejecting doesn't prevent the others from running.
close_reason: dataRetention.ts scaffold + hooks.server.ts wiring + dispatcher fault-isolation tests committed.

title: Purge/downsample unbounded balance_snapshots
description:
src/lib/server/db.ts's balance_snapshots table (lines 357-364: id, user_id, wallet_kind,
wallet_id, taken_at, balance_sats) gets one row per (user, wallet, hour) forever, feeding
portfolio.ts's balance-over-time chart -- including rows for wallets that have since been
deleted, since nothing removes a wallet's historical snapshots on delete (same orphan-row
shape as the wallet_backups gap, see sibling bead B6). This is the single clearest
will-grow-forever table in the schema per the audit.

Fix: register a purge step in src/lib/server/dataRetention.ts's sweep dispatcher (see B1) that:
1. Deletes balance_snapshots rows older than a retention window (suggest 13 months, to
   comfortably cover any >1yr chart view), OR downsamples: keep hourly resolution for the
   most recent 30 days, then collapse older rows to one per day.
2. Deletes balance_snapshots rows whose (wallet_kind, wallet_id) no longer exists in
   wallets/multisigs (orphan cleanup for already-deleted wallets).

Acceptance criteria:
- portfolio.ts's balance-over-time chart still renders correctly for recent data after a
  purge/downsample cycle.
- A test seeds old rows, orphaned rows, and recent valid rows, runs the purge step, and
  confirms only old/orphaned rows are removed.
close_reason: 30d hourly->daily downsample + 13mo hard cutoff + orphan cleanup registered in dataRetention.ts; chart-integrity + orphan tests added. Commit ca98342.

title: Purge sent/dead notification_queue rows after 30 days
description:
src/lib/server/db.ts's notification_queue table (lines 621-633: id, user_id, channel,
event_type, payload, status, attempts, last_error, next_attempt_at, created_at, sent_at) has
no cleanup logic anywhere (grepped for cleanup/retention/DELETE -- nothing outside tests),
despite the table's own schema comment claiming rows go "dead after max attempts" -- dead and
sent rows are never actually removed; the table grows forever. Delivery logic itself lives in
src/lib/server/notificationQueue.ts.

Fix: register a purge step in dataRetention.ts's sweep dispatcher (see B1) that deletes
notification_queue rows with status IN ('sent', 'dead') where the relevant timestamp
(sent_at for 'sent', or last attempt time for 'dead') is older than 30 days.

Acceptance criteria:
- Extend notificationQueue.test.ts (or add a new test) covering the purge step: seeds
  sent/dead rows older and newer than 30 days plus in-flight ('pending'/retrying) rows, runs
  the purge, confirms only old sent/dead rows are removed and nothing in-flight is touched.
close_reason: sent/dead rows >30d purged (sent_at / next_attempt_at basis); in-flight rows untouched, test-pinned. Commit ca98342.

title: Proactively sweep expired sessions and recovery_grants
description:
Both src/lib/server/auth.ts (sessions table) and src/lib/server/recovery.ts (recovery_grants
table) only delete expired rows lazily, one at a time, when that exact expired token is next
presented (auth.ts:79-82 for sessions; recovery.ts:296-299,317 for recovery_grants). An
abandoned session or grant that's never reused after expiry sits in the table indefinitely --
for sessions this includes the raw ip_address/user_agent columns (db.ts:673-674, added via
ALTER for new-device detection).

Fix: register a purge step in dataRetention.ts's sweep dispatcher (see B1) that runs:
  DELETE FROM sessions WHERE expires_at < <now>
  DELETE FROM recovery_grants WHERE expires_at < <now>

Acceptance criteria:
- A test seeds a mix of expired and live sessions/recovery_grants, runs the sweep, and
  confirms only expired rows are removed -- live (non-expired) sessions must never be touched
  by this step.
close_reason: Proactive DELETE of expired sessions + recovery_grants in daily sweep; live-row safety test-pinned. Commit ca98342.

title: Add 12-month retention window for known_devices
description:
src/lib/server/db.ts's known_devices table (lines 677-684: user_id, fingerprint, user_agent,
first_seen, last_seen; PK is user_id+fingerprint) accumulates one row per (user, UA-hash)
forever with no expiry. src/lib/server/deviceTracking.ts is confirmed entirely write-only in
the app -- no route or page ever reads this table back, so it's collected and retained
indefinitely with zero user visibility or control.

Fix: register a purge step in dataRetention.ts's sweep dispatcher (see B1) that deletes
known_devices rows where last_seen is older than 12 months.

Acceptance criteria:
- A test seeds devices last seen >12 months ago and <12 months ago, runs the sweep, confirms
  only the stale ones are removed.
Note: this pairs with the separate "Your devices" self-service page bead (privacy-disclosure
epic) which surfaces this same table to the user rather than purging it blind -- both are
worth doing; a retention window alone doesn't give the user visibility/control.
close_reason: 12-month known_devices retention step registered in dataRetention.ts, test-pinned. Committed.

title: Clean up wallet_backups / backup_missing_notified on wallet/multisig delete
description:
src/lib/server/wallets.ts's deleteWallet (lines 202-214) and src/lib/server/wallets/
multisig.ts's deleteMultisig (lines 290-300) both already explicitly clean up notified_txids
(deleteWallet:208, deleteMultisig:295) and address_labels (deleteWallet:211 via
deleteAddressLabels('wallet', id), deleteMultisig:297) -- neither of those two tables has an
FK to wallets/multisigs, so the cleanup is done by hand. wallet_backups (db.ts:405-411, PK
wallet_kind+wallet_id) and backup_missing_notified (db.ts:657-662, same PK shape) have the
exact same no-FK, needs-hand-cleanup shape, but neither deleteWallet nor deleteMultisig
touches them -- an orphan-row gap. Low sensitivity (timestamps only), but if a wallet id is
ever reused it could produce a misleading "already backed up" read for the new wallet.

Fix: add to both deleteWallet and deleteMultisig, right next to the existing
notified_txids/address_labels cleanup:
  DELETE FROM wallet_backups WHERE wallet_kind = ? AND wallet_id = ?
  DELETE FROM backup_missing_notified WHERE wallet_kind = ? AND wallet_id = ?

Acceptance criteria:
- Deleting a wallet or multisig leaves zero matching rows in wallet_backups and
  backup_missing_notified.
- Extend whichever test file already asserts the notified_txids/address_labels cleanup on
  delete (wallets.test.ts / multisig.test.ts) with the same assertion for these two tables.
close_reason: deleteWallet/deleteMultisig now clear wallet_backups + backup_missing_notified (kind-scoped), pinned in notifiedTxidsCleanup.test.ts. Committed.

title: All HW signer device calls can hang forever with no timeout or cancel
description: ledger.ts, trezor.ts, bitbox02.ts, jade.ts — zero AbortController, timeout, or Promise.race around any device call. All four signer components hide Cancel/fallback buttons while busy/signing is true. A frozen or PIN-locked device leaves user staring at Approve on your device with only a full page reload as escape. This is the live signing flow for real Bitcoin funds. Fix: wrap device calls in Promise.race with a configurable timeout (30-60s), keep Cancel visible during signing, add AbortController propagation.
close_reason: Fixed via commit 991e808 (worktree agent-aa55763c86f23d0a5). All 4 HW signer drivers (ledger/trezor/bitbox02/jade) wrap device round-trips in a 45s timeout (DEVICE_TIMEOUT_MS) with typed 'timeout' error codes; each signer component shows a specific recoverable hint on timeout instead of hanging forever. Cancel stays hidden during signing (no SDK supports live cancel) but the timeout unconditionally recovers the UI. vitest 1880/1 skipped, svelte-check 0/0, build clean.

title: Admin feature-flags grid omits 'marketing' category — announcement_banners & referral_links cannot be toggled instance-wide
description: src/routes/(app)/admin/feature-flags/+page.svelte hardcodes GROUPS (lines 9-16) with only wallet/hardware/notifications/upcoming and omits category 'marketing'. grouped filters data.flags by those categories (line 23) and the template iterates grouped (line 42), so the two marketing flags (announcement_banners, referral_links) never render — admins cannot toggle them instance-wide from the grid. Contradicts registry.ts comment that the grid is generated from the flag array. FIX: add {category:'marketing', title:'Marketing'} to GROUPS. Per-user override page (/admin/users/[id]) already lists marketing flags, so only the global grid is affected. Found during flag-matrix pass cairn-1ytc (see its comment for full evidence).
close_reason: done

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Fixed: added {category:'marketing', title:'Marketing'} to GROUPS in src/routes/(app)/admin/feature-flags/+page.svelte:13 (registry order preserved: wallet/hardware/notifications/marketing/upcoming).

Confirmed against src/lib/server/featureFlags/registry.ts:10-15 (FeatureFlagCategory union) that 'marketing' was the only declared category missing from the grid — wallet/hardware/notifications/upcoming were all already present, so no other category needed adding.

Considered deriving GROUPS from the registry instead of hardcoding, but the registry only carries category keys, not the admin-facing titles/hints the grid needs (e.g. "Notification channels", the upcoming-features hint text) — full derivation would mean adding new title metadata to registry.ts, which is out of scope for a low-risk P2 fix. Went with the one-line addition instead.

Verified by careful re-read of the edited file (array literal syntax, comma placement, object shape matches the other GROUPS entries). Could not exercise `npm run check` / svelte-check in this shell — svelte-kit isn't resolving via the sandboxed bash/PowerShell on this machine (pre-existing environment issue, unrelated to this change).

Committed 359f4f2 on branch single-sig-full-wallet (single file: src/routes/(app)/admin/feature-flags/+page.svelte).
